description:
The players take turns to roll and move. There are two tracks to chose from: one for sleds and one for skiers. One some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to arrive at space 50 wins the game.

—user summary

Skirminion is a fast, brutal, abstract wargame played on a chessboard, where every unit is a health‑tracked die and every decision is life or death. Position your troops, fortify with pawns, grind down the enemy through attrition, and outmaneuver them with tactical retreats, flanking, and siege. No randomness in combat — just pure battlefield strategy.

Kittens are obsessed with their toys! They're on a mission to collect as many different ones as possible. But watch out: if players get too greedy and keep collecting the same toy over and over, they'll crash—and the others will pounce on their toys. Who has the cunning of a cat and knows when to hold back?


     Play any card—no need to follow suit.
     Play the highest card or a trump to win the trick.
     Collect 5 different colors—but don’t take more than 4 of any one!


—description from the publisher

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach space 100 wins the game.

This game was published as a promotional giveaway for the "Torpedo-Dreigangschaltung" from Fichel & Sachs.

—user summary

Players team up to organize their number cards into a shared pile—sometimes in ascending, sometimes in descending order. Can you master the timing, or will you be stuck with your numbers forever? Action cards spice up the game and might save you at the last second.


     Play a number card in the predetermined direction.
     Use an action card when you get stuck. 
     Can your team beat the deck before it beats you? 


—description from the publisher

As the floodwaters rise, Noah’s Ark teeters under the weight of its passengers. Players must help balance the animals boarding the ark so it doesn’t tip to one side. Elephants, giraffes, penguins, and other creatures need to be placed carefully, for the fate of this floating zoo depends on your sense of balance and timing.

Balance the Ark is a trick-taking game for 3–4 players. Players play animal cards to tricks, with each card showing a species and weight that influence the ark’s balance. The winner and loser of each trick must place their played cards on either side of a physical ark board, causing it to tilt toward heavier sides. Across multiple rounds, players manage both card play and spatial placement to maintain equilibrium while collecting points based on balance and animal sets. After several rounds, the player with the most points from balanced placement and fulfilled ark conditions wins.
publishers: 卸売センターサンエイ (Oroshiuri Sentaa San'ei)

description:
Be the master of your own luck! Draw cards from the pile and predict: will the next card be a panda or a clover? Will the value be higher or lower than the last one? Easy… but do you know when to stop and  to score points?


     Predict the top card before revealing it and placing it on any discard pile. 
     Got it right? Keep playing or score points. Got it wrong? Next!
     The player who collects the most cards wins!


—description from the publisher

In Tap Jam you reveal and choose cards from a common pool to get triplets of same number or consecutive numbers in your display to score the highest score in the End. Each card holds two numbers, though. TAPE JAM is a tiny numbers puzzle to get your head around with some competitive elements.

Tap Jam: Off the record! delivers a handful of new action cards that give players more agency to untangle their favorite cassette tapes. Hit the perfect moment to activate one of the action cards to score the most points. But don't wait too long or another player might pick the action you wanted just before you could!

—description from the designer

Mysterious “Domino Aliens” have landed, and they love chaining together for explosive combos! Players link these colorful alien tiles to trigger dazzling cascades of reactions. Each careful placement may spark another burst, so plan your connections wisely to unleash the ultimate domino chain reaction and watch the aliens dance across your puzzle board.

Comboron is a trick-taking and puzzle-combo game for 2–4 players. Players first engage in trick-taking rounds to collect “Domino Alien Cards,” then strategically place them into their personal puzzle area. By aligning same-colored numbers that sum to 10 or more, players score points and trigger chain reactions, clearing cards and setting up new combos. Victory requires timing, foresight, and the thrill of watching your final move ignite a cascade of scoring chains.

Chosen by the goddess herself, the players take on the roles of priests conducting sacred rituals to return wandering souls to heaven. Each ceremony is a battle of faith and foresight, where divine crystals and soul cards channel celestial power. As the ritual unfolds, only those who interpret the goddess’s will and manage their spiritual energy wisely will ascend to divine favor.

Oracle is a game for 3–6 players in which players, acting as priests and deacons, perform consecutive “rituals” to earn the goddess’s blessing. Each ritual alternates between a Declaration Phase, where players wager crystals to join the ceremony, and an Ascension Phase, where they play increasingly powerful Soul Cards to claim control. The priest may play multiple cards of matching value, while special cards such as 6 (Reverse), 8 (Cut), and the Oracle (Reset) dramatically shift the flow of play—reversing strength, ending rounds, or nullifying numbers in play. Players who exhaust their crystals are eliminated, and after several rituals, the priest who ascends with the highest score is declared the most devout servant of the goddess.

For a whole decade, Bandido has outsmarted everyone and become a true legend. Now, it's time to celebrate his anniversary! Help Bandido make his daring escape from the legendary prison of Alcatraz. Dig tunnels, gather equipment and try to avoid the coming tide around the island. If the tunnels are flooded, you can’t escape! Work together and build a single exit to make it off the Rock.


     Build tunnels and collect equipment. 
     Stop the incoming water by covering it. 
     Create dead ends and keep a single exit route. 


—description from the publisher

boardgame: 宇宙亀の方程式 (Uchū Kame no Hōteishiki)
description:
In the distant reaches of the cosmos, brilliant astronomers and researchers study the mysterious “space turtle,” a celestial being whose movements seem to obey an unknown cosmic equation. Players compete to predict the turtle’s course through logic, intuition, and observation—seeking to uncover the formula that defines the universe’s most enigmatic creature.

Uchū Kame no Hōteishiki (The Equation of the Space Turtle) is a two-player trick-taking game where players alternately act as the astronomer and the turtle. Each round, both play a card simultaneously, comparing celestial numbers and symbols to resolve “tricks” that represent the turtle’s motion through space. Over a sequence of ten rounds, players aim to align or disrupt the mysterious formula X + Y = Z, represented by unique equation cards. Observation cards introduce special conditions that can alter outcomes and provide strategic depth. The player who best deduces or manipulates the cosmic relationship emerges victorious.

boardgame: 恥の多い生涯を送って来ました (Haji no Ōi Shōgai o Okutte Kimashita)
description:
Life is full of unavoidable “edges” or perhaps “shame.” In this clever card game, every move brings a new decision: will you take the edge and risk drawing more cards, or play it safe and wait for a better moment? As players build and break the shared sequence of cards, timing and psychology intertwine. Beneath its simple rules lies a tense and witty battle of nerve, planning, and just a bit of luck.

Haji no Ōi Shōgai o Okutte Kimashita (I Have Lived a Life Full of Shame) is a hand management and climbing-style card game for multiple players using a deck of 32 cards numbered 1–32 in eight colors. Players compete to empty their hands by playing cards sequentially into a shared row. Cards must be placed in numerical order, and if a card cannot be placed at an edge, the player must “edge out” taking cards from the table so their card becomes an edge.

Collected cards return to the player’s hand, potentially delaying their victory. However, forming sequences or color sets of three or more cards allows players to lay them down for points. The round ends when a player empties their hand, and scoring balances positive points for laid sets against penalties for leftover cards.

boardgame: 白と黒でトリテ 明朝＆ゴシック (Trick-taking in Black and White: Serif & Sans-serif)
description:
In a world divided by sharp contrasts, two forces: light and shadow, order and chaos, serif and sans-serif—vie for harmony through clever play. Each hand becomes a battle of balance and prediction, as players use monochrome cards to outwit one another in a duel of design and deduction.

Trick-taking in Black and White: Serif & Sans-serif is a trick-taking card game for 3–4 players that uses dual-colored numeric cards to create a unique bidding and prediction challenge. Players must forecast their success and manage both “white” and “black” cards, with tricks determined by clever number play and careful observation. The game adds variety with “Serif” and “Sans-serif” decks, as well as variant rules that change how balance and contrast influence victory.

The Scientific Apocalypse (Научный Апокалипсис) is Nigh.

Sorcerer-scientists are driving the world toward moral and spiritual decay, engineering horrific GMOs and vaccines while shunning the wisdom of traditional homeopathy. The World Government is plotting a global digital prison, and the robot uprising is imminent. Only you can stop them!

14 Factions From the Coalition of Good to the Legion of Battle Psychics.

Tactical Combat Assemble a squad of healers, soothsayers, and true researchers.

Co-op Mode 1-5 players. Band together with allies or treacherously stab them in the back.

Modern Threats Destroy 5G towers, microwave ovens, and robot vacuum cleaners.

Humor & Satire Packed with jokes, narrative storytelling, and pop-culture references.

A Rich Game World 200+ unique cards, featuring agents and missions.

—description from the publisher (translated)
Monopoly type game focusing on a town in central Massachusetts. Sold originally for fund raiser.

Players gather around to manipulate numbers with cards and dice, aiming to build combinations that outmatch their rivals. Each round becomes a tension-filled contest of intuition, risk-taking, and clever arithmetic where a single die roll can flip the balance of power.

Piplus is a climbing and dice-manipulation game for 2–6 players, where participants play numbered cards and augment their values with dice. Each card from 1 to 10 can be played singly or in sets of matching numbers, climbing over previous plays with equal-length sets of higher values. Dice can be rolled to either enhance card totals or replace card values entirely, adding an element of probability and timing to the standard climbing structure. Players continue rounds until one empties their hand; rankings determine how many dice each player receives in the next round. After three rounds, the player with the highest cumulative score wins.

As the president of a competitive construction firm, you’ll oversee the auctioning, building, and expansion of high-value skyscrapers worth billions. Deciding when to invest, build, or expand your headquarters will determine the future of your company’s success.

Construction Auction is an auction and building strategy game for 3–5 players lasting about 90 minutes. Players compete to bid on construction rights for luxurious high-rise projects across five colors, then decide how and when to build them to maximize reputation and profit. Each round, players secretly manage resources (labor, machinery, distribution, and technology) to outbid rivals or strategically withdraw to recruit workers and start new projects. Timing construction, managing limited chips, and reacting to market saturation are key. The game ends once the parking lot project and the final flag cards appear, with players scoring based on completed projects, company reputation, and efficiency.

In the competitive world of whisky production, investors seek profit through shrewd trading and bold speculation. As distilleries rise and fall with changing alcohol values, timing your investments and trades becomes the key to building your fortune in this spirited market.

Cask & Capital is a card-driven economic and market manipulation game for 3–5 players. Each round, players manage hands of alcohol-type cards representing different spirits and values. By playing and trading these cards, they influence fluctuating alcohol indices, acquire assets, and score profits based on timing and market trends. Strategic speculation and interaction with opponents’ market moves drive the tension, with the richest investor at the end declared the winner.

Roll the dice to find matches of all Pooh's friends! Be on the lookout for honey and bees but beware of Woozles!

This portable game, originally produced exclusively for the Walt Disney theme parks, offers players three different games.

Hunny Pot Dice Game includes 7 dice, instructions detailing three different dice games and 'Tally' sheets, all of which stores neatly in a high-quality plastic 'Hunny Pot.'

The aliens have destroyed their beautiful planet; the exodus is inevitable. Best organize your fleet of rocket-pyramids to bring some order to the chaos.

In this competitive card-placement game, optimize your layout to guide your fleeing alien fleet. Arrange your ships in a pyramid to trigger powerful synergies, while strategically flipping your cards — and those of your opponents — at the perfect moment. Earn points for every upright ship in your pyramid-rocket and for securing the alien majority. The player with the highest total wins!

—description from the publisher

Data is a competitive abstract strategy game about cyber-warfare and data transfer. Two players race to upload their tokens from their offline Reserve to the Cloud. The game combines deterministic movement with calculated risk, played on a unique console board where coordinates are relative: your "A-1" is your opponent's "F-6."

The Core Loop: Stream & Input

The game is defined by a strict "Move-then-Roll" loop.

     Stream (Move): On every turn, you are forced to move. You pick one piece (even if it is part of a stack) and advance it 1 Step Forward. If you march into an enemy, you "Ram" them, crashing their piece back to their start while taking the square. If you march onto your own piece, you create a Stack.
     Input (Roll): After moving, if you still have data in your Reserve, you roll coordinate dice. This allows you to deploy new pieces or "Overwrite" the enemy. If you roll the coordinates of an enemy piece, it is a Critical Crash: their piece (or entire stack) is deleted from the board, and you instantly spawn your own piece in that square to replace them.


Tactical Stacking
You can stack tokens on a single square (by rolling that coordinate or moving onto yourself). However, bandwidth is limited: you can only move one piece off a stack at a time. Stacking allows you to buffer multiple units on a safe square, but beware—if a stack is crashed by the enemy, all pieces in it are reset to the start.

The Reserve Mechanic
The game features a dynamic pacing mechanic. When your Reserve is empty, you stop rolling dice and enter a high-speed "Upload Mode," moving purely by strategy. However, if an opponent crashes you, your pieces return to your Reserve, forcing you back into the slower "Loading Mode" where you must rely on dice to respawn.
The first player to successfully upload all their data tokens to the cloud wins the race.

—description from the designer
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Hex Factor is a fast-paced abstract puzzle game for 1-4 players in which you are racing to claim territory by solving puzzles. You use colorful, wooden hexagonal tiles as puzzle pieces to try to replicate the black and white clusters of hexes — the puzzles —  on shared game boards. The tiles and the game board hexes have the same symbols in the middle (circle, star, hex) — that’s what you’re trying to match. But each tile is bi-colored (half violet, half yellow, for example), and you can only put the colorful tiles alongside each other WITH LIKE-COLORS TOUCHING (blue against blue, etc.)

There is no turn order. All players work at the same time. Each player starts with 10 tiles randomly pulled from the box lid. You will try to find a puzzle cluster on one of the game boards that you think you can replicate using the tiles you have. 
Once you have a puzzle cluster solved,  hurry up and claim that cluster (before one of your opponents does) by placing one of your 5 personal Marker-Cubes onto it. That puzzle can’t be solved again now that you’ve claimed it.

Now replace the tiles you just used with new ones from the box lid, so that you always have 10 tiles. 
Game ends when any player has used all 5 of her player markers. Then add up points: 1 point for an easy hex, 2 for a medium, 4 for a tricky one.

There are various bonus point opportunities to incentivize focusing on certain puzzles. These opportunities involve the mechanics of Area Majority and Set-Collection. The optional Attack Cards add a take-that dimension.

—description from the publisher

Color Jam is puzzley coloring game of happy accidents.

Pick 6 colors to cooperatively color your picture with, pick some goals, then start coloring from two star spaces.  You can only color spaces touching a colored space, and you can only use the crayon in the slot matching the space's number.  When you're done coloring, you place your crayon back in the 6th slot and move all other crayons up.  The result is a challenging puzzle that'll have you coloring off the rails and into a color jam.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Kittens of KITAN is a lighthearted strategy card game where players assemble a litter of adorable kittens to gather resources—wood, sand, metal, and cloth. Use those resources to build kitten essentials like scratching posts and litter boxes, then upgrade them into deluxe towers, houses, and high-tech litter boxes to earn victory points. Along the way, players can play PURRfect cards to unleash mischievous kitten antics or score bonus points. Plan smart, sabotage playfully, and be the first to reach 11 victory points to win.

A pop-up narrative puzzle adventure. Examine five pop-up chapters full of documents, artifacts and puzzles that lead towards a cursed pirate treasure.

The story:
A chilling discovery inside the walls of a centuries-old tavern sparks both fear and hope in the heart of Abigail Walsh, daughter of notorious treasure hunter Orson Peters. Recognizing the morbid artifact may represent the first and last clue to uncovering the fate of her missing father, she solicits your help to decipher its meaning.

So begins a harrowing voyage across oceans and ages, to a remote island in pursuit of riches. Was Orson's obsession a tragic delusion, or was his folly anchored in truth? One thing is certain: death and despair follow in the wake of that terrible, old man.

Explore five puzzle-packed pop-up scenes that transport you to key locations along your perilous journey. Scour documents and ephemera to piece together the enigma of Orson's fate, and chart your course across a mysterious island in search of an infamous legend.

Will you survive the perilous journey inspired by the writings of H.P. Lovecraft, or will you be lured to your doom by the siren song of cursed gold?

In December 1944, a massive German attack struck American lines in the Ardennes forest of eastern Belgium. Achieving local surprise, the Germans pushed forward in what became known as the “Battle of the Bulge” until the Americans rallied and drove them back. The battle became known as a tremendous feat of American arms.

Snowstorm of Steel is a double-sized Campaign Study, with 23 scenarios that extend and expand the story told in Panzer Grenadier: Elsenborn Ridge. You will need our Elsenborn Ridge, Dragon’s Teeth and 1940: The Fall of France games to play them all. They follow our story-arc structure so they tell the story of this epic battle through their flow, and you get battle games tying them together so you can play them with a mind toward broader operational goals.

—description from the publisher

This game is an expansion and does not include tokens. Compatible with all versions of Hitster.

Add 154 new French-language songs to your favorite Hitster game with this expansion featuring hits from the Rouge FM catalog! Listen to the songs, place them in chronological order on your musical timeline. The first player to correctly position 10 hits will be crowned Hitster.

Dive into chaotic fun with F*cked Up Opoly – a wild spin on Monopoly! Trade quirky items like your mum’s rabbit and dodge unpredictable 'You F*cked Up' cards. Amass cash while avoiding rehab spots. Ideal for parties with friends who enjoy dark humour.

—description from the publisher

Even more f*cked up-opoly!

This game is for those people who have an inappropriate sense of humour! Probably not suitable for the church social party.

Hours of inappropriate fun but be warned, some of these cards are just WRONG..... this game is not for most people!

Travel the board buying FUCKED UP things, catching fucked up diseases, visiting fucked up places and collecting welfare money and generally fucking things up so much you lose your dignity and your money! the winner is the one with the most cash and fucked up assets at the end (your nan's anal beads and your brothers cum sock are examples of "the assets" you can acquire..)

Whoever came up with some of these cards needs therapy!

Fucked Up Opoly, F*ckedup-opoly

Features:

Adult party game
For those with an even more F*cked Up sense of humour!
Earn the most cash and try to stay out of Rehab
Ideal for 2-6 players

Dragons breathe fiery judgment upon the dwarfs. As a lowly but mighty golem of the dwarfs, you must help reunite the shattered Houses, reclaim lost arcana, and spark a rebirth of civilization. Or erode into history with all of Dwarfdom.

The game includes 5 Missions leading to 7 unique victories (and dozens of endings where you're destroyed).

Structured as a branching narrative, this solo RPG gamebook invites you to direct the plot – but only if you master a unique combination of game mechanisms.


     Level up your character: Upgrade your attributes and skills
     Calculated gambits: Roll and gather dice to beat target thresholds
     Intellectual puzzles: Manipulate dice using limited actions
     Dexterity tests (optional): Slam dice at targets in battle


—description from the publisher

Kyoto no Koneko features superb illustrations by Jérémie Fleury and artistic direction by Maxime Erceau, who has created a fun object that will be hard to resist.

On the mechanical side, its creator, Kevin Gauvin, has created a competitive memory game to which he has added a personal touch that makes it so charming.

				
				
					GameplayPlayers will face a 4x4 square of cards, with only pairs of eyes visible, which are supposed to indicate the number of felines they will discover if they decide to turn them over.

On their turn, a player reveals a Mission (cards shaped like cat heads) which indicates the exact number of kittens they must reveal.

The cards in the game depict Pairs of Eyes in the Night on their front (between 1 and 3 pairs of eyes) and cute kittens on their back.

				
				
					The trick?The number of pairs of eyes on the front does not necessarily correspond to the same number of kittens on the back.

Some kittens close their eyes, or two kittens blink and stand side by side, forming a single pair of eyes!

This will trap the players around the table, who won't always be able to trust what they see on the front of the cards, and there will be many surprises when the chosen card is revealed!

You'll have to use your memory to complete your mission by remembering, for example, that behind that single pair of eyes are actually four mischievous kittens!

---description from the publisher---

boardgame: Sagrada: Promo 29 – Tantrum House 2025 Window Pattern Card
description:
Promotional card offered by the "Tantrum House Season 12 Kickstarter". It has the code FGG-SA01-PR29.

It's a new double sided player card to build the stained glass window on. Shuffle into the deck with the other window cards during setup and proceed as normal.

Campfire contains two decks of beautiful playing cards and a rule book detailing how to play our family's version of Rummy, enjoyed countless nights around the campfire in the beautiful Pacific Northwest.

Campfire is played over 12 rounds. In each round the players try to make a set of at least three and/or a run of at least 4. In round one each play is dealt 3 cards, in each subsequent round players are dealt 1 more card. The first player in each round to use all the cards in their hand lays them down face up and calls "Campfire". Each other player then has one turn to try to use as many cards as possible. All players then move on to the next round.

A Score sheet is provided and a C is used to denote a Campfire. All other players sum the value of the cards left in their hand (1-9 face value, 10 through King = 10, Ace = 15). A 0 is possible as only one person in each round can Campfire.

At the end of 12 rounds, the each player totals their points and the player with the least points wins. Ties are broken by the most Campfires.

—description from the publisher

Bastards in Bascinets is a medieval skirmish miniature game where each player controls a single miniature, representing a robber baron or outlaw knight. 
Then they squabble and try to murder each other in a Monty Python and the Holy Grail fashion of humour and vulgarity.

Best played with up to 5-6 players for more chaos.

Enjoy!

—description from the designer

Evil Rising is an app-driven cooperative horror board game about entering sealed locations where something has gone wrong, pushing deeper into the unknown, and confronting a darkness that grows with every decision. Players take the role of investigators drawn into abandoned sites, failed experiments, and forgotten rituals that were never meant to be disturbed.

Scenarios are self-contained but designed to connect into a broader campaign. Decisions made in one game can carry consequences into future scenarios, altering available options, difficulty, and outcomes. Multiple endings are possible, and success rarely comes without loss.

—description from the publisher

PVNKZ! is a little foldable microzine of rebellion and cyberpvnk streetviolence against a relentless System. 
Grab a few dice, kitbash some pvnkz and pigz, do a crime on the streets of ravaged Neo-Buda and Mega Pest. Quick, easy, fast and fun. Battlejackets on, get into the pit and mosh!

KERO-ZINE! is an add-on of sorts, providing rules for vehicles and vehicle combat including kamikaze charges and lots of explosions. Build a pvnk rig, run over a pig barricade and explode into a ball of flame!

These rules are meant to be used with 28 mm models mainly, but you are welcome to play at whatever scale you want. These rules are yours, mix them, change them, hack them, add new ones, its fairly modular and easy to modify.

Have fun, pvnkz!

—description from the designer

boardgame: War of the Three Kingdoms: Collector's Edition
description:
Set against the magnificent backdrop of the Three Kingdoms era, War of the Three Kingdoms centers on classic identity-based gameplay. Players take on the roles of famous generals from the Three Kingdoms, relying on wisdom and strategy to fulfill their unique identity missions. It is a card-based board game that combines historical depth with competitive fun.

I. Background Setting: Famous Generals of the Three Kingdoms, Skills Recreating History

The game features nearly a hundred general cards modeled after renowned figures from the Three Kingdoms, each equipped with exclusive skills that reflect their characteristics—Zhuge Liang's "Star Gazing" showcases his foresight, Cao Cao's "Traitorous Hero" embodies his ambition. The skill mechanics are deeply integrated with historical allusions, allowing players to experience the unique charm of Three Kingdoms heroes during the game.

II. Core Gameplay: Identity-Based Gameplay with Distinct Objectives

During the game, players randomly receive one of four identities: Lord, Loyalist, Rebel, or Spy, each with distinct victory objectives. The Lord must eliminate all Rebels and Spies while protecting themselves; Loyalists focus on safeguarding the Lord and cooperating to wipe out hostile identities; Rebels win by focusing fire to eliminate the Lord; Spies need to hide their identity first, and only after all Rebels and Loyalists are eliminated can they defeat the Lord in a one-on-one duel to win.
The game proceeds in turns, with players going through draw, play, and discard phases in sequence. They can use basic cards like "Slash", "Dodge", and "Peach" for attack, defense, and healing, or use trick cards and equipment cards to turn the tide of the battle. Furthermore, they can combine general skills for strategic synergy. The core fun lies in the secrecy and gameplay of identities. Players need to judge others' identities by observing their actions, rationally allocate card resources, and formulate attack or defense strategies. Every decision is crucial to victory.

III. Exclusive accessories for collector's edition

Four new Western faction generals have been added, combining classic identity game gameplay with European historical background stories, and featuring a brand new worldview setting.

—description from the publisher (translated)

《三国杀》以波澜壮阔的三国历史为背景，核心玩法聚焦经典身份博弈，玩家化身三国名将，凭智谋与策略完成专属身份使命，是兼具历史感与竞技性的卡牌桌游。

一、背景设定：三国名将，技能复刻历史

游戏以近百名三国名将为原型设计武将牌，每张卡牌均搭载贴合人物特质的专属技能——诸葛亮“观星”再现智谋，曹操“奸雄”复刻雄才，技能机制与历史典故深度融合，让玩家在对局中感受三国英雄的独特魅力。

二、核心玩法：身份博弈，目标鲜明

对局中玩家随机获得主公、忠臣、反贼、内奸四种身份，胜利目标截然不同：主公需肃清反贼与内奸，保全自身；忠臣以保护主公为核心，协同剿灭敌对身份；反贼需集中火力消灭主公即可获胜；内奸需先隐匿身份，待反贼、忠臣全灭后，与主公单挑取胜。
游戏按回合制推进，玩家依次经历摸牌、出牌、弃牌阶段：可使用“杀”“闪”“桃”等基本牌攻防续航，或借锦囊牌、装备牌扭转局势，更可搭配武将技能实现策略联动。核心乐趣在于身份的隐秘性与博弈性，玩家需通过观察他人行动判断身份，合理分配手牌资源，制定进攻或防守策略，每一步决策都关乎胜负。

三、珍藏版专属配件

新增4个西势力武将，经典身份局玩法与欧洲历史背景故事结合，全新世界观设定。

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

description:
Our galaxy is on the brink of collapse, overpopulation is depleting resources, and water is becoming scarce, making it the most precious commodity in the galaxy. The four most powerful races in our system have embarked on a battle to secure their supplies and will fight on countless planets to secure every last drop of water.

Water Wars is a confrontation game in which each player takes a 12-card army deck, draws 6 cards per round, and each turn plays a card from their hand on a planet, trying to end the round with the most power among the armies fighting for it to obtain rewards.

The armies are asymmetrical, with different distributions of strength and abilities that differentiate them from each other. Some use shields, others set fire traps, others throw acid when damaged, others repair themselves, etc.
Each round, we will improve our deck with elite cards and face off on different planets. We will end the game after the third round and count the water points obtained. The winner of the game will be the player with the most water points.

The base game has 4 armies, but it can be expanded with 2 expansions (Invasion and The Ancestors), each providing 3 armies and planets with new abilities.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Django Wanted: Wild West Skirmish Rules
description:
The sun beats down on tin roofs. A saloon door creaks. Somewhere, a dog barks. Then nothing. The silence of high noon...

Django Wanted is a skirmish game set in the Wild West, designed to capture the tension and brutality of classic western showdowns. Whether you're introducing friends to miniature wargaming or looking for a fast-paced alternative to more complex rulesets, Django Wanted delivers intense gunfights in under an hour.
Why Django Wanted?

Simple to Learn, Hard to Master No hit points. No endless modifiers. A well-placed bullet sends a man to the ground — the next one finishes him off. New players grasp the rules in minutes, but the tactical depth keeps experienced gamers engaged.

The Bloodlust Mechanic Kill an enemy and you activate again. Immediately. Chain eliminations to turn a desperate last stand into a legendary comeback. Every activation matters. Every risk could pay off — or end in disaster.

Six Distinct Archetypes Build your gang from six character types, each with unique strengths:

Sharpshooter – Patient and deadly at range
    Gunslinger – Lightning-fast on the draw
    Bounty Hunter – Ruthless in close combat
    Scout – Elusive and hard to pin down
    Outlaw – A brutal brawler who never backs down
    Desperado – Survives on instinct and sheer luck

45-60 Minute Games Long enough for stories to emerge, short enough to play multiple rounds in an evening or fit into a club night.

7 Ready-to-Play Scenarios From bank heists to hangings, manhunts to train robberies — the rulebook includes scenario ideas to get you started right away.
What's Included?

Complete rules (11 pages)
    Character profiles and stats
    Weapon tables
    Quick reference sheet
    Scenario ideas
    Atmospheric illustrations throughout

Specifications

Scale: 28mm (easily adaptable to 15mm or 32mm)
    Players: 2 (expandable for multiplayer)
    Miniatures: 3-6 per player
    Table size: 90×90 cm (3×3 ft)
    Play time: 45-60 minutes

License

Django Wanted is released under Creative Commons BY-NC-SA 4.0. Share it, adapt it, translate it — just give credit and keep it non-commercial.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Something unheard-of happened in Fruit City: sly fast-food villains broke into the bank during the night and stole all the savings of honest fruit citizens! Community is in turmoil, but one thing is for sure: the robbers managed to escape by disguising themselves in fruit costumes.

Investigate and figure out which disguises the Sly Fruit and its two accomplices used to get away unnoticed. Coconut, the faithful police mutt, will help you verify witness statements and catch the culprits!

Question the fruit witnesses to find out which ones are actually fast-foods in disguise. Uncover as many groups of sly fruits as possible over the course of the game.

Players act as one team of detectives. Each turn the Active player token is passed clockwise. A turn consists of the following steps:

     Insert a Fruit card into the Coconut board and lay out a Witness card from the pile.
     Each player chooses and plays a card.
     Reveal all played cards and then select two cards to check them for red marks.
     Move Fruit tokens based on the result and take new cards. 
     Discuss the results of the investigation and decide whether to solve the case or start a new turn.


The game ends in one of two cases:

     The active player names at least one of the Sly Fruits incorrectly. In this case, the whole team loses!
     The Witness deck runs out of cards. When this happens, count how many Sly Fruit cards you have put aside and check your result. Solve as many cases as you can!


—description from the publisher

In FinMaster, players are investors building a portfolio across multiple asset classes, from savings and retirement products to property, stocks, businesses, and alternative investments. The market is always moving, and each round brings new events that can boost one part of the economy while hurting another.

In your turn, you play an Event that affects specific asset classes, then roll the dice and assign one result to the “+” side and one to the “-” side to determine how the market shifts. If you choose, you can also activate an extra Power die by explaining the financial jargon on your Event card within a time limit, giving you more influence over the outcome. All players with affected assets either gain or pay cash based on the dice results, then you can optionally buy, sell, or trade assets before everyone receives income from the bank.

When the final Event cards have been played, the game ends with an Armageddon phase where each player reveals and resolves one final face-down Event. After that, everyone calculates their net worth by adding their cash to the return values of their assets. The player with the highest net worth becomes the FinMaster.

—description from the designer

Set in Prohibition-era America, players take on the role of rival crime bosses battling for control of major U.S. cities. Players expand their reach by deploying mobsters, seizing opportunities, and undermining rivals through direct conflict and clever plays.

Each player fights to acquire as much money as possible. Money is earned by controlling cities. Control is volatile as cities can change hands every few minutes. Each turn, players recruit new mobsters to help seize territories, and use those mobsters to 'take-out' their opponents. Once things get too hot, they can lay low to get new resources, and then wait for another moment to strike. Once the pool of money has runs out, the player with the most is deemed the Supreme Mob Boss.

This is a face-paced, king of the hill, territory control board game. The game is centred around a few core principles:

     No turtling - a big gripe I have with territory control is a slow build up and then sudden chaos. You play for an hour to have 10 minutes of fighting. These games should have constant conflict.
     No dice - it is quite frustrating to get to the final moment of a game, and then dice let you down. We wanted to focus on strategising, instead of luck.
     Multiple roads to victory - there is no one best strategy where players are reliant on getting the best cards to achieve success. Identify weaknesses and time your aggression to be victorious.


All these principles focus the game towards having a great flow, good fun, and constantly evolving gameplay that shifts every turn.

—description from the designer

It Can't Be! is a drawing game that is all about testing your originality, creativity, and speed. So, can you think beyond the obvious? And do it quick? Flip open a Game Card and set the timer to 1 minute! Players must race the timer to draw 3 things using the shape on the card, except the object mentioned on the card! Remember to think beyond the obvious, because if another player draws the same thing as you, neither of you get the point! At the end of a minute, everyone shares their drawings, all unique drawings score one point each! The player with the most points at the end of the game, wins!

Devinaigrette is an original French wordplay and riddle game designed to liven up your evenings. Whether playing solo or in teams, try to solve the 600 riddles included in this box. Pascal Morrissette and his team have cooked up some delightful content for you!

Stack War(locally known as Jangi Kule) is a high-interaction tile game designed for the lively atmosphere of coffee and tea shops. It reimagines traditional set-collection mechanics into a cutthroat war for resources, playable with custom tiles or a standard Okey/Rummy set.
The game is driven by greed and risk management. Players hold a hand of numbered tiles (1-13). On their turn, they must play one tile to perform one of three actions:

     Harvest: Match a tile in the central Market to start or grow a stack.
     Cap: Play a matching tile on their own stack to "bank" the points, securing them permanently.
     Raid: Match the top tile of an opponent's stack to steal their entire tower of tiles.


The game features unique social mechanics, such as the "Mala" (Sweep) bonus, where players must shout "Mala!" when clearing the table to claim bonus points—if they forget to shout, they lose the bonus. The game ends when the draw pile is exhausted and hands are empty. The player with the highest score (total tiles captured + bonuses for Jokers and Sweeps) wins.

—description from the designer

Race  classic SuperTed characters around the board. The 1st to the finish is the winner. The villains have a head start on the main track. There's a mini track that SuperTed has to follow first. The winner is the 1st to get an exact dice roll to land on the final yellow telescreen marker.

boardgame: PragerU: Celebrate America – Race to The White House
description:
MADE IN AMERICA & PROMOTES PATRIOTISM: Have fun celebrating our beautiful country! Helps players learn and appreciate history while having fun.

EDUCATIONAL TRIVIA GAME: Test your knowledge with over 200 questions covering U.S. history, civics, geography, innovation, and culture, making it perfect for family nights, classrooms, or homeschool learning sessions.

RACE TO THE WHITE HOUSE: Advance forward by answering correctly, ride a rocket ahead on lucky boosts, or risk falling back on railroads. It's a game of knowledge, patriotism, and chance. Foster national pride!

INCLUDES FULL GAME SET: Comes with a folding board, 200 trivia cards (6 categories), 8 unique player pieces, a sand timer, and instructions, providing everything needed for hours of entertaining and educational gameplay.

QUESTIONS FOR KIDS & ADULTS! Each trivia card has 2 questions on it: 1 for kids and 1 for adults, allowing you to cater play to your group. Ideal for families, teachers, and civic-minded groups. For 2+ players, ages 8 to adult.

250TH ANNIVERSARY: Celebrate America’s milestones with a game that educates, entertains, and unites.

boardgame: Kellogg's story book of games: book number one
description:
A book, the first of a series of four, containing four traditional children's stories or fairy tales. The story is printed on the left hand page of each spread, and the right contains a game board themed to the story. Rules are contained at the front of the book, and the inside back cover contains a flap with a built-in spinner and 6 tokens to punch out and use in the games.

Each game is a simple race game using the same rules, with various penalties or bonuses on the spaces along its track.

Book one contains the following stories and games:
Cinderella
The three little pigs
Hansel and Gretel
Little Black Sambo

Children could send in the top of any one Kellogg's cereal packet, and 10c in coin to receive any of the other three titles in the series.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Hansel and Gretel
Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Three Little Pigs

description:
Full Moon is a new take on the classic Werewolf game, where there is no moderator, and nobody ever has to close their eyes.

Combining the tabletop style of One Night Ultimate Werewolf and the knowledge and lying mechanics of Coup creates a game where Werewolves kill in plain sight, and players still aren't sure who to lynch.

Every player has two roles in the village, but can claim to have any role since nobody else has seen their cards.  If nobody challenges them, they can take the action whether they actually had a card that let them do it or not.

If both cards in front of a player are on the Village Team, then the player is on the Village Team.  But, if any of their cards are on the Werewolf Team, then they are on the Werewolf Team.  This means that sometimes you end up with a Werewolf Seer or a Minion Doppelganger.

With so many possible combinations, every game feels completely different.  You never know what to expect or who to trust in a game of Full Moon!

—description from the designer

Sumons is a tactical, faction-based skirmish board game played on a grid, where players summon units from a customized, self-built deck of cards and maneuver them on the battlefield using precise movement patterns, resource timing, and positional combat.

Before play, each player constructs a deck aligned to a chosen faction, selecting units and effects that create internal synergies and reinforce a specific playstyle. During the game, cards from this deck are used to summon units onto the board and activate abilities, making both deck composition and in-game tactics critical to success.

Each faction in Sumons is defined not only by theme and aesthetics, but by distinct mechanical identities that influence how they move, fight, and control space. Units are represented by cards and tokens, each with specific statistics for attack, health, range, and movement, as well as abilities that interact with a fixed, turn-based energy system.

Movement in Sumons is deliberate and expressive. Instead of generic “move X spaces,” units use defined movement profiles—such as omnidirectional steps, forward advances, diagonal shifts, or specialized patterns—placing a strong emphasis on positioning, board control, and spatial planning. Combat is deterministic and tactical, rewarding foresight and threat assessment over randomness.

Energy increases by a set amount each turn and does not carry over between rounds, requiring players to make efficient, turn-by-turn decisions about how to deploy their available resources. This structure emphasizes tempo, timing, and tactical sequencing rather than long-term resource hoarding.

Victory is achieved by outmaneuvering the opponent, leveraging deck synergies, and executing efficient tactical decisions over the course of the game. Sumons is designed to reward adaptability, mastery of spatial play, and thoughtful deck construction.

—description from the designer

My First Deck Building: Welcome to the grand tournament of the fantasy kingdom!

Dwarves, elves, chameleon-men, goblins, humans, and dragons are all here! It's a grand festive event that only happens once every hundred years, and you can't wait to see if your team will win the most stars!

Ready? Let's go!


     Reveal cards from the draw pile and your deck.
     Compare your teams and earn stars.
     Upgrade your team with new cards and continue the adventure!

Card Kickers is a card based Soccer Match simulation game. You build your own team with 11 player cards, each with individual stats like speed, passing skills or defense.

Each round you choose your player, then you roll for different actions like passing or shooting. If you roll a higher value than your opponent, your action was successful. But beware of fail cards, if you roll too low, that can have devastating effects. The team that scored more goals in 10 rounds wins.

Teeny Tusslers - Teeny Expansion is an expansion to Teeny Tusslers, the solo-only print-and-play tabletop game about the tiniest goblins picking the biggest fights over the pettiest things!

The Teeny Expansion brings two new goblin chiefs, that bring new conflict cards to the tussle! Explosives, traps, arrows and obstacles to name but a few. Everything a proper tussle needs!

—description from the designer

Teeny Tusslers - Teeny Puzzlers Expansion is an 18 cards standalone expansion that you can play in your hand to Teeny Tussler, the game about the tiniest goblins picking the biggest fights over the pettiest things!

The Teeny Puzzlers Expansion has a limited set of movement pattern cards that you use to navigate a tricky grid, collect objectives while returning to your starting point.

Plan smart, puzzle hard, and tussle your way to victory!

Tag Team is back with 6 new Fighters inspired by the legends of King Arthur!

Arthurian legends come to life in the Tag Team arena! Play as Morgan, Merlin, The Dragon, Perceval, The Green Knight... and even Excalibur! In Tag Team, we don’t sit around telling stories, though, we FIGHT!

Who will dominate the arena as these legendary characters engage in epic battles?

Tag Team is a cross between a tactical auto battler and a deckbuilder, inspired by the greatest classic arcade games, featuring awesome and charismatic Fighters, each with their own fighting styles and signature moves. Build your team of 2 and put together the ultimate synergies by combining their 2 unique decks!

Round 2… FIGHT!

-- All Tag Team expansions are compatible with the base game and each other. --

In more detail, in Tag Team, you start with a deck of only two cards, and the fight unfolds automatically: Flip your cards one at a time and apply their effects. At the end of each round, you get to turn up the heat on the opposing team by strategically adding new cards to program your deck — but without reordering the cards already present. Determine what makes your tag team tick, play devastating combos, and dominate your opponent by deftly blocking their attacks. Timing is everything if you want to emerge victorious and knock out your foe!

Tag Team: Arthur's Legacy is coming in 2026.

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
Boston. Present Day. An evil has taken over the city, spreading fear and driving  people to the very brink of insanity.  You feel how it manifests, catch fleeting glances of shadowy devotees, and speculate about its mysterious origin.

You and your associate are descendants of two architects of the science fiction genre: Edgar Allan Poe and HP Lovecraft.  Two incredibly different men, but each with their share of darkness. Were they just wildly creative or did they see things no one else did?

The answer is: Both. And you can sense evil too. What’s more: You can conquer it by eliminating all three of its aspects.

This is Lovecraft & Poe. Each game you face a new evil. Each evil has different powers based on its origin, manifestation and devotee, and you will pull three evil cards to reflect that. Each time you face evil, you must do so in your current condition; which may be sickly or drunk or…

On your turn, you will first roll to see how many actions you can take. From there, it's up to you how to spend those actions: gaining the knowledge you will need to remove fear, conquer devotees, and switch alley tiles.  You will move around the twisting streets of Boston, shifting the alley tiles into a puzzle that best suits your purpose.  And you'll secure rooms and alleys to make sure evil can twist them no more!

To win, you'll have to meet the conquer conditions unique to the 3 evil cards you have pulled for the game. But watch out - because each evil card also has a unique lose condition - and if you meet even one of those, the evil takes over and you lose!

Will you have the fortitude to root out the evil and conquer it? Or will you succumb to it, as your ancestors did?

Let’s find out!

UMS: Invasion is the newest module for UMS: Modern Age.

Steel robots, shrieking Insects, cold-blooded Aliens! 
Do you have what it takes to fight those monstruosities?

Three new Sci-fi armies that you can play against the Earth armies from all the UMS modules published until now.

Available in English and Italian.

Essence of the Arcane is an easy to learn, abstract strategy game for 2 players that takes roughly 5-10 minutes to play, in which you control motes of magical energy, fighting for dominion.

The goal of the game is to eliminate all of you opponent’s motes. This is done through a variety of actions, which allow your motes to move, attack, heal, split, and grow in power. All the while, your movement is hindered by the void. An area in the middle of the board where magic can not exist.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Five 28mm skirmish scenarios to pit the forces of evil against the forces of just trying to make it through the night.

Written for our flagship (free) rules, QILS. The scenarios include designer's notes to port to the system of your choice.  But, really, give QILS a shot.

Includes:

     five scenarios with multiple variants for lots of replay value
     2D terrain templates to support all the scenarios
     120 flat paper miniatures that cover all the forces in the scenarios


In the half-forgotten valleys north of the old trading road, there lies a village where winter comes early and lingers late. The people there speak softly once the first snows fall, for they know that the season belongs to more than frost and shadow. Their elders tell the young that names carry weight in the cold months, and that some things wake when spoken of too clearly. So they whisper only of “the Taker,” and even then only with a prayer tucked behind their teeth.

Each year, when the long night stretches thin as a blade, footprints appear where no one walks. Doors are found ajar though barred the evening before. And sometimes a childÕs bed stands empty, its blankets drawn back as if by gentle hands. The villagers know better than to wail or chase the trail; the forest would swallow them whole for such boldness. Instead they set candles in their windows and hope the flame endures until dawn.

boardgame: Kaiju on the Earth LEGENDS Gamera
description:
A what-if battle royale is brought to life with the four monsters from the Heisei Gamera trilogy!
If the four monsters from the Heisei Gamera trilogy, "Gamera", "Gyaos", "Legion" and "Iris", were to fight, which monster would win? This game itself is set as a "simulator left behind by a certain mana researcher".

Using a card deck that reproduces the characteristics of each monster, we have made a board game of the dream battle royale where you fight with monster pieces while positioning yourself in relation to buildings and other monsters.

There are four types of monsters that players can choose from: "Gamera", "Gyaos", "Legion" and "Iris".
The game rules are reflected so that each monster can act by making use of the characteristics seen in the movie.
In Kaiju on the Earth LEGENDS Gamera, you use a deck (card) to instruct the monster you are in charge of how to act. You can reduce the stamina of the enemy monster by making full use of attacks, movements, special actions, etc. The reduced stamina is points, so you attack more monsters to earn points and aim to win.

Catharsis is an adventure board game where captivating narratives combines with strategic dice rolling, offering players unique choices. Adventure through traps and events, engage in epic monster battles, and roll to activate your abilities. Play it as a true solo experience or cooperative with friends. Each character offers a diverse playstyle, while the unique boss decks ensure variety. If you own other Catharsis games, you can merge content, expanding your options for solo or coop play with friends. Catharsis combines tabletop RPG elements with awesome monster fight mechanics.

Catharsis Holidays has two playable characters and two bosses to battle, with goals for more characters.

CHRISTMAS
The Ice Elves call upon the mortal races to help vanquish a powerful Lich known as Santa. Created by the deranged wizard Mortimer to spread joy in ancient times, Santa has risen again through the phenomenon of death resonance, reemerging in the arctic as a lich after Mortimer's demise.

EASTER
Mr. Steelpaws was meant to be Mortimer's masterpiece of his new holiday, Easter. However, his eccentric personality and creepy looks left children terrified, leading Mortimer to seal him away in an enchanted egg prison. After Mortimer's death, Steelpaws is back, as cheerful as ever. Obsessed with cute and idol women, he's now on a mission to win over the heart of the Easter Idol, Honey Bunny, and is seeking help from his heroic friends.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: It's Sushi O'Clock (時すでにお寿司)
description:
A psychological duel of sushi in your hands!
Become the savvy sushi chef and serve sushi without letting the hidden wasabi be detected!

Confuse your opponent with exchanges like,

"Trust me, this salmon is wasabi-free" or "I know you like shrimp, right?"

If you eat three pieces of wasabi sushi, the game is over - instant defeat...! But you can only reject the chef's dishes three times. Decisions, decisions...

Easy rules can be taught in less than a minute!

A true independent game: printed on business cards and with no box!

Grapplin is a two-player climbing and shedding game where players compete to empty their 18-card hands first.

Players take turns playing combinations (singles, pairs, triples, runs) or bombs (quads or suited runs of 4 singles) to beat each other's attacks. When you match a rank in your opponent's combination, you can take cards from a communal row, add a card to transform their combination, or remove a card to weaken it. But you can only use these special actions if you can still beat their attack afterwards.

Each player has a joker that can be flipped once per round to reverse the rank order. Victory comes from reading when to use your special actions, when to flip the rank order, and when to play a bomb. First to shed all cards wins the round. First to three rounds wins the game.

—description from the designer

Players choose to play as Janet, Rita, Gladys, or Madge and race across the backyard board to be the first to reach the verandah steps. A turn consists of flipping one of the twelve face-down tiles on the sandpit board.  If it matches the next spot in their path, the player gets to progress one more space closer to the other end of the board.  Flipping over a Nana Nap or Grouchy Granny tile stops the player's progress and causes all of the tiles in the sandbox to be reshuffled. The first player to reach the last space on their path gets to call Nana Chris and teach her how to floss.

Hitster CZ/SK is a lively music party game that turns your favorite tunes into a fun and competitive challenge. This special Czech & Slovak edition features over 300 hit songs - 130 Czech and Slovak tracks alongside 178 international favorites from the last 100 years. Using the free Hitster app, players scan a music card to play a snippet of the song, then take turns guessing the release year, artist, and title to build their personal timeline. The first player to correctly place 10 songs wins! With simple rules, quick rounds (about 30 minutes), and dynamic music-driven gameplay, Hitster CZ/SK is perfect for parties, gatherings, and music lovers of all kinds.

55 new cards dive deeper into the artistic life of Paris, full of inspiration and hard choices.

New Muses have arrived… and many of them have fur!
Meet Fritz the secretive mouse, Constance the patient cat, and Tulsi the wandering dog — animal companions who may quietly (or not so quietly) influence your creative journey.
Hardship returns, darker than before.

New cards like Depression, Paranoia, and Panic Attack remind you that artistic life isn’t just cafés and applause — sometimes it’s about surviving the day.

A whole new twist: Events!
At the start of every round, one Event card is revealed and shapes the entire round for all players. From A Sunny Day in Montmartre to Riots in the Streets or The World’s Fair 1889, each round brings new opportunities, challenges, and stories.

Paw Paw is an illustrated memory game where observation, emotion, and playful actions come together.

Players take turns flipping cards to find matching pairs of dogs, each one expressing a different emotion or personality. Alongside the dog pairs, the game includes four special action cards that add surprise and interaction to the classic memory gameplay:

Sleeping bed – you lose your turn.

Change Direction – the order of play is reversed.

A treat (secret peek) – secretly look at one card without showing it to the others.

Balls! – all cards are shuffled and rearranged face down, without anyone seeing their new positions.

Designed for both children and adults, Paw Paw encourages concentration, emotional awareness, and shared moments away from screens. The hand-illustrated artwork and compact format make it a playful experience and a collectible object.

Simple to learn and joyful to play, Paw Paw celebrates attention, empathy, and the pleasure of playing together.

—description from the designer

Walls & Guards

This expansion is not just "more of the same"—it fundamentally changes how life in the desert city works. Based on Stefan Feld’s latest designs, we have packed this box with exciting new challenges.

Build & Defend

The desert is becoming a dangerous place. For the first time, you will need to construct a mighty City Wall to protect your achievements from the looming threat of Desert Raiders.

A New Use for the Tower

You know and love the Keshi Tower for distributing resources. But in Walls & Guards, the tower becomes the arena for your defense! You will be throwing your Guards into the tower to fend off attackers. Will they stand their ground, or will the raiders break through?

Farewell, Sahara Tiles?

Yes, you heard that right. This expansion shakes up the familiar board layout. The classic Sahara tiles are making way for a completely new player board section dedicated to your fortifications. This also brings a fascinating twist to the Green and Brown districts, allowing for fresh strategies and mixed placement of Keshis.

New Layers of Strategy

On top of that, we are introducing new modular elements like Private Objectives and powerful Edicts from the Sultan and Imam, giving you even more ways to score and adapt your playstyle.

Get your guards ready—Marrakesh needs you.

—description from the publisher
Monopoly type game focusing on a hamlet in Islip, NY. It was produced as a fund raiser.

Wordys: The Pachanga of Letters is a tabletop word-guessing game inspired by the classic hangman activity, redesigned as a non-elimination game based on points, turn structure, and controlled uncertainty. Players score by correctly guessing letters under changing turn conditions defined by dice and a VSD card deck. The game is played in rounds (or until a target score is reached, depending on the chosen variant). The winner is the player (or team) with the highest total score.

Components and What They Do

Wordys typically includes:

Word Board / Word Lines: one or more rows of blank spaces that represent each hidden word.
Each blank space equals one letter. Word length is always visible from the start so players know the size of each word and can plan their guesses.

Letter markers / writing method: any method to reveal correct letters in their positions (markers, tiles, dry-erase, etc.).

Blue Die: defines the type of attempt a player can make on their turn (how the guess is performed and whether special conditions may be triggered).

Red Die (optional): adds extra conditions for the current turn. It is used only when triggered by the Blue Die or enabled by the chosen variant.

VSD Deck (Value & Social Dynamics), 30–40 cards: the main deck that determines scoring values and turn modifiers.

Pachanga Cards (about 5% of the VSD deck): defensive cards that can be held (max 1) and used to avoid losing points from a negative VSD effect.

NOPE! Cards: one per player (or per team), used to cancel a VSD card effect entirely (single-use per game).

Tokens (3 per player recommended): a limited resource used to avoid risk/uncertainty by skipping the dice step and choosing a safer/cleaner attempt structure (see Turn Flow).

Mini abacus (one per player): physical score tracking. Players move beads as they gain/lose points. Score is public and always visible.

Setup (Core)

1. Shuffle the VSD deck and place it face down.

2. Give each player:

3 tokens

1 NOPE! card

1 mini abacus

3. Choose a play mode:

Classic Mode: one shared word per round, provided by a rotating player.

Pachanga Mode: multiple words are active at the same time (typically one per player), and players guess on words that are not their own.

4. For each active word, create a row of blank spaces equal to its length (maximum length can be set by the group). The blanks remain visible throughout the round.

Turn Flow (Core Sequence)

On your turn, you follow the same sequence:

1) Choose Control (Token or Dice)

You must choose one:

Spend 1 token to skip the dice step and take a “clean” attempt (the game’s safest option).
Tokens are limited and do not refresh unless a variant explicitly allows it.

OR

Roll the Blue Die to determine the attempt type for this turn.
Some results are simple (normal attempt). Some results may impose constraints (e.g., attempt type changes) or trigger the Red Die if that option is enabled.

2) Optional: Red Die Condition (Only if Triggered / Enabled)

If the Blue Die triggers it (or if the chosen variant uses it), roll the Red Die and apply a temporary condition that affects this turn.
Examples of condition types (general categories, not fixed faces):

letter-type restrictions (vowel-only / consonant-only)

turn-flow changes (direction/sequence changes)

scope changes (how many words are affected by the guess)

3) Make the Letter Attempt

Based on the dice conditions (or the token “clean attempt”), the player chooses the letter(s) they are allowed to attempt.

The chosen letter is checked against the active word(s).

Any matches are revealed in the correct positions.

The game does not eliminate players for misses.

4) Draw and Resolve 1 VSD Card

After the attempt, the player draws one VSD card and applies its effect.

The VSD deck is designed as three functional groups:

A) Scoring Cards (about 50%)
These cards mainly define how many points are awarded on a successful turn, commonly in a simple range such as +1 to +5, where higher values are less frequent.

B) Pachanga Cards (about 5%)
Defensive cards that can be kept (maximum 1 at a time). They are used later to avoid losing points from a negative VSD effect. If you already have one and draw another, the extra card is not kept (commonly placed at the bottom of the deck).

C) Action/Modifier Cards (the rest of the deck)
These add small turn modifiers that change the context of guessing without breaking the flow. Examples of effect categories:

change turn direction / order

change the scope of a guess (applies to 1 word, 2 words, or all active words)

allow an extra letter attempt

impose temporary restrictions (vowels-only / consonants-only)

5) Scoring and Protection (Pachanga / NOPE)

If the turn produced at least one correct letter, the player applies the scoring outcome from the VSD card (and any modifiers).

If the VSD card causes a negative scoring effect, the player may:

use a Pachanga Card (if they have one) to avoid losing those points (then discard/recycle it), or

use their NOPE! Card to cancel the VSD card entirely (single-use for the whole game).

6) Update the Mini Abacus

The player updates their score on the mini abacus immediately. Scores remain visible to all players.

End of turn → next player.

End of Game and Winning

The game ends using the chosen variant:

after a fixed number of rounds, or

when a player reaches a target score.

Winner: the player (or team) with the highest total score at the end of the game.

—description from the designer

Every player starts with a hand of 13 cards, with values from 2 to Ace, just like in a poker deck. You choose a card to play simultaneously. The highest card wins and gets to be placed in the 3x3 arena. In case there are ties, all tied cards automatically lose. Some cards have special effects which affect the game situation. To win the game, you need to form three in a row in the arena using your cards.

This game is a free gift which comes with the Little Fighter 2 novel deluxe edition.

The shaping and laying game from China! Although over 2,000 years old, the classic tangram is more up-to-date than ever. You only need 7 tiles in basic geometric shapes to lay countless different figures. Our enclosed 200 templates show the versatility of this game and guarantee fun and puzzles.

But be careful: patience is a virtue - and it will be put to the test. With these sometimes very tricky tasks, you must not throw in the towel too quickly and look in the solution booklet. Even completing the tangram square without a solution is a challenge!

—description from the publisher

The Hamptons is the most eastern part of New York, but there’s no better way to get to grips with the area than by playing Hamptons MONOPOLY! Buy up houses and hotels, grab Chance and Community Chest cards for unexpected prizes and penalties, and charge your opponents outrageous prices to send them into bankruptcy. With iconic locations including Shelter Island, the Long Island Rail Road and Shadmoor State Park, there’s never been a better time to play out your real estate dreams.

—description from the publisher

Survival Chess: Tartan Grid is a complete, stand-alone abstract strategy game built upon the timeless mechanics of chess. It strips away the concepts of checkmate and royal pieces, reducing the conflict to its purest, most brutal form: a war of total annihilation under a single, unforgiving law — capture is mandatory. The iconic "Tartan Grid" starting position transforms the board into a dense, interwoven battlefield, creating a unique tactical puzzle of wave-like chain reactions and resource management that is distinct from any traditional chess opening.

Components:

     A standard chessboard (8x8 squares).
     48 Pawns (24 light and 24 dark) to set up the special "Tartan Grid" starting position.
     8 Kings, 8 Queen, 8 Rooks, 8 Bishops, 8 Knights for potential promotions for each side.


Setup:
In the initial position each side has 24 pawns:
Pawns are arranged in a checkerboard pattern across six central ranks, forming an extremely dense, intertwined design reminiscent of Scottish tartan. The board is almost completely filled.
White (24 pawns in total): Pawns on squares a7, c7, e7, g7 (first line), b6, d6, f6, h6 (second line), a5, c5, e5, g5 (third line), b4, d4, f4, h4 (fourth line), a3, c3, e3, g3 (fifth line), and b2, d2, f2, h2 (sixth line).
Black (24 pawns in total): Pawns on squares a2, c2, e2, g2 (first line) and b3, d3, f3, h3 (second line), a4, c4, e4, g4 (third line), b5, d5, f5, h5 (fourth line), a6, c6, e6, g6 (fifth line), and b7, d7, f7, h7 (sixth line).
The 1st and 8th ranks are empty.

Rules:
The game follows standard FIDE chess rules, except for the following:
The Main Rule: Capture is mandatory. If you have a legal opportunity to capture an opponent's piece, you must do so. If multiple captures are available, you may choose any one of them.
The King is an ordinary piece. It has no special status: it is not given check, it is not the objective of the game, and it can be captured like any other piece.
There are no concepts of "check" or "mate."
There is no castling.
Pawn Promotion: A pawn that reaches the last rank (8th for White, 1st for Black) must be promoted immediately. The player may choose a king, queen, rook, bishop, or knight.
Pawn's Initial Double-Step: Any pawn on its starting rank (the 2nd rank for White or the 7th rank for Black) has the right to move forward two squares on its first move, provided the path is clear (a tribute to classical chess). 
The en passant capture rule is in effect.
Stalemate is a draw, as in standard chess.
Objective: Eliminate the opponent's last piece. The player who captures the final piece wins.

Gameplay:
The game begins with White's first move, which must be a pawn promotion. White can promote any of their four pawns that are already on the 7th rank.
From this moment, The Main Rule (Capture is mandatory) is in full effect. The extreme density of pieces means that any move will trigger vast, cascading chains of forced captures. Calculation becomes a matter of predicting wave-like reactions through the entire grid.
Players use their pawns from the lower ranks as a strategic reserve, advancing them to promote into new pieces and support attacks.

—description from the designer

Keevorn is a solo dungeon crawler played with a standard deck of cards and two Jokers. Each run is a journey through a dungeon where every room feels like a small problem to solve, and runs can vary in length.

Along the way you fight monsters and manage your inventory, using weapons, skills, and relics to survive. Scrolls force you to face your fortune, and the deeper you go the more the pressure rises. Play revolves around uncovering useful interactions and choosing how and when to use what you find.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Invasion of Sicily: Part 1 – Operation Husky
description:
Focusing on the British 8th Army's initial phase of the Invasion of Sicily in 1943. This packet covers the two major airborne landings and the drive up the eastern coast of Sicily during the first week of the invasion.

Montgomery and Patton led their forces across the island to Messina but not without controversy. 
Montgomery called for Operation Ladbroke, a glider based assault and capture of the Ponte Grande bridge, which spread gliders over a wide area. A small band of troops from these gliders did manage to take the bridge but ran out of ammunition before relief could arrive. 
The second airborne assault, Operation Fustian, was intended to capture the Primosole bridge. Again, these airborne forces fought hard and well but couldn't hold the bridge until relief arrived. Monty's penchant for capturing bridges too far was seen during these trying days. 
In both cases, the 8th Army did ultimately arrive and did finally capture the bridges but the airborne forces making the initial attempts paid a heavy toll.

Rules for glider assaults and parachute drops are provided for the scenarios that need them. The other battles included two SAS missions and good ground based combined arms battles. 
All in this fun package! 
This battalion level scenario pack chronicles the battles of Operation Husky's first week for the Brits and contains 11 exciting miniatures war game scenarios written in campaign form and playable by 2-4 players.

The scenarios include data sheets for vehicles and uses Fist Full of TOWs 3 rules. Scenarios can be easily converted to your favorite game system.

The eleven scenarios can be played as a campaign. A campaign score sheet is included, enabling players to track the campaign progress and final score.

These scenarios put you in the Gasoline Cowboy seat, making you and your gaming partner(s) the tank drivers as you play through The Invasion of Sicily - Operation Husky.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Children of Morta: The Board Game – Family Traits
description:
The Family Traits expansion adds a new hero and gameplay elements to Children of Morta: The Board Game:

     New Hero – Joey: The son of Ben, Joey is a playable character with his own Hero Board, Talent, Bond, and Level-up cards, special tokens, and XP dial tracker. His unique ability allows him to deal more damage as his health decreases, offering higher rewards at greater risk.
     Family Traits Mini-Expansion: Adds teamwork-focused abilities to each hero’s talent cards, encouraging cooperation and strategic synergy.
     Shrine Mini-Expansion: Shrine cards provide one-time bonuses to all players, best used by the leader at a crucial moment.


—description from the publisher

Celebrate Founder’s Day in the grand city of Obsidian. Traditionally, residents will hand out “thrices” - three small symbolic gifts. One thrice for someone influential to your past, your present, and your future. However, this year many of the thrices seem to have gone missing! Will you be able to uncover the culprit and prove you’re worthy enough to join the Hunter’s Guild (the protectors of your home town, Obsidian)?

Scenario Zero is a stand alone prequel story leading up to the events of Harbor of Blight: Act I. You will explore the city of Obsidian as you uncover clues in the Exploration Phase, test out your combat prowess in the Combat Phase, discover additional lore and hopefully save Founder’s Day in the Decision Phase! Will you make it to the True Ending?

Welcome to Seoul Journey, where you embark on a day trip to Seoul, South Korea and plan your days wisely to visit iconic locations, indulge in festivals and food, shop for souvenirs, and summon mystical guardians for bonuses.

This is a heartfelt, strategic card game inspired by the neighborhoods, food, and hidden corners of Seoul. You’ll travel across the city, collect experiences, and build your own story of discovery through clever card play and tableau-building choices. It’s designed to feel cozy, meaningful, and smart—something you’ll want to play again and again.

Seoul Journey blends strategy, set collection, and market manipulation in a colorful, thematic package that captures the essence of Hallyu (Korean wave) with vibrant artwork inspired by Seoul's blend of tradition and modernity.

Bring the whole family together with Hey Cow!, a fun and original card game inspired by an Idaho road trip. With its innovative game mechanics and memorable characters, Hey Cow! delivers bales and bales of family fun.

In this easy-to-learn game, players compete for cows' attention, using strategy and a little luck to out-manure their opponents. Hey Cow! has something for players of all ages -- from fun characters and art for little kids to endless replayability for adults.


     Easy to learn: Can be learned in just a few minutes.
     Replayability: Kids and adults alike will want to play it over and over again. 
     Engaging and strategic gameplay: Things don't move slow on this farm! Memorize, collect, and outmaneuver other players to be crowned the ultimate winner. Pay close attention to the cards you and your opponents play -- they can bolster your collection or cut your turn short!
     High Quality: Hilarious design and grade A game components mean it will hold up for your family for years to come.
     Family Time: Turn off the screens and laugh 'til your sides hurt. You're guaranteed to have outbursts of both excitement and exasperation.


—description from the publisher

Correctly answer Australian Football League related questions to score points. After each correct question make a choice to keep one guaranteed point or risk it to score six points by flicking the football through the goal posts.

Build your own pictures and guess what it is in this fun-filled game of shapes and creativity. Children take turns choosing colorful photographs and recreating the picture using multi-colored shapes. Did you choose the photo of butterfly? Use the shapes to build a butterfly like the one in the picture. When the “artist” is done building, other players try and guess what it is. Artists and guessers both win stars and the player with the most stars wins.

Pay a visit to the bustling Main Line in this stunning new edition of Monopoly! The moniker "Main Line" is a reference to the Pennsylvania Railroad Train Line that was created in the area around the 1850s. But the Main Line is in itself an area full of pride outside of Philadelphia and known for some of its mansions and historic homes. The area represents a ton of different towns, depending on who you ask, as everyone has a different definition of what the "Main Line" is. Now you can buy up iconic locations and own a piece of this historic Pennsylvania region, like the Dixon Meadow, the General Anthony Wayne monument, and Bryn Mawr College. Take in the night life by enjoying a cold drink at Cornerstone or a signature sandwich at Nick Filet in Paoli.
