In this new expansion of Water Wars, the three oldest races in the universe have decided not to remain on the sidelines of this war and intend to join the fray to put an end to it.
These new races incorporate new abilities that will allow you to explore new ways of playing, such as moving your units between planets, summoning your race's prophet, or gaining strength when in a group.
In addition, four new planets are added to this expansion. These planets have special bays where you can obtain direct benefits when you place your first unit.
A few elite cards are also included.
This new expansion uses the rules from the Water Wars rulebook. The game setup and endgame remain the same as in the base game. Players choose their armies and battle for three rounds, with the player who has accumulated the most water winning the game.

—description from the publisher

This is the adult edition of the quick-thinking word game P for Pizza. Players flip cards revealing a letter and a category, then race to shout a word that matches both (e.g., a word starting with "D" for the category "Things you ride"). The first correct answer wins the card as a pizza slice. The goal is to collect enough slices to build a full pizza. Difficulty increases individually for each player as they complete more of their pizza. This version features saucier, more innuendo-filled categories for mature audiences, making it spicier than the family-friendly original. Portable and fast-paced, it's ideal for parties, holidays, or travel.
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

description:
Brink is a fast, fun, family friendly game for 2 players. It is carefully crafted using high quality birch plywood. The piece of wood itself is sourced from the locally produced Carrooka game. Brink is a unique, eco-friendly game handmade by a Manchester-based games designer.

The goal of Brink is to flick discs across the board to land in your opponents half. The closer to the edge you get, the more points you get. If you get the disc to land so it hangs off the edge, you've scored a BRINK! Maximum points! This is an easy to learn fast-paced exciting game for two.

—description from the designer

A party game for any number of players (3+ recommended), in which the goal is to correctly identify the correct lyrics from a hint of misheard lyrics; bonus points are awarded if you can also name the song and/or artist - up to 3 points per round.
Each player takes a turn as "host", reading off the incorrect lyrics, while the other players guess.  Play continues until someone reaches 20 points (or however long you want to play, it's a very loosely defined set of rules).
Examples of misheard lyrics from the outside of the box:
"On a dark desert highway, cool whip in my hair"
"I've got two chickens to paralyze"
"A year has passed since I broke my nose"
"Keep spending most of our lives giving other gangsters hair advice"

Shootout is a 2 player game based on the Sport of Hockey where players win puck battles, pass to other positions and take shots at the opposing Goaltender.

It is an exciting, fast-paced game with All Star players, big saves and of course, Shootouts!

The player who scores the most goals by the end of the third period wins the game.  If the game is tied after 3 periods, then it goes to sudden death overtime!

—description from the designer

The ancient, godlike serpents have awakened from their deep slumber. Hungry for magic, they seek out relics of the past, consuming them to infuse themselves with immense power. Each predator sees the others only as prey, and all will fight relentlessly. Only the most cunning and fierce serpent will survive this trial of power.

Snake Clash is a tile-laying fighting game for 2 to 4 players. Players take on the role of ancient Aztec serpents, slithering across the game board in pursuit of powerful relics and engaging in deadly stand-offs with opponent snakes.

On each turn, players play a Move card to extend their serpent. Every card triggers one of five special effects:

Lunge – move an additional tile

Trick – swap your head with your tail end

Smash – create an obstacle near your tail

Shrink – shorten your snake

Attack – bite an opponent’s snake

The game ends when a player completes the Quest by collecting enough Relics and reaching the Altar, or when only one serpent remains alive. A snake is destroyed if it cannot perform a legal move (all paths are blocked) or if its Weakpoint—the tile attached directly to its head—is attacked.

Once players have mastered the basic rules, they can explore Incarnation Mode, which introduces 14 unique serpent powers that dramatically reshape the game. An additional 2v2 Team Mode is also available, allowing players to form alliances and face off in coordinated battles.

—description from the designer

Contact Unknown is a high-intensity, modern naval warfare simulation for 1 player (or 2 in co-op). You take command of an modern destroyer — the technological backbone of the modern Navy. Your mission takes you into the most volatile flashpoints on Earth, where the line between a routine provocation and all-out war is razor-thin.

The Gameplay Concept:
Forget traditional naval games where every enemy is immediately visible. In Contact Unknown, your greatest enemy is uncertainty. A glowing blip on your radar could be a harmless trawler, an aggressive maritime militia, or a lethal hypersonic missile.

Identification is Mandatory: You are prohibited from opening fire until you have a verified firing solution. Use your radar, shipborne helicopters, and passive sensors to pierce the "Fog of War."

As the commander of a single destroyer, you are on your own. Manage your Action Points (AP) wisely: Will you spend them on a precision scan, a high-stakes evasive maneuver, or a massive missile strike from your VLS cells?

High-Tech Defense: When the missiles fly, every second counts. Rely on your SAM interceptors for long-range defense, or pray for the signature roar of your CIWS as a final line of protection.

Key Highlights:
Global Hotspots: Operate in the "hottest waters in the world"—from the South China Sea to the Red Sea. Every region offers unique tactical challenges and political stakes.

Lethal Asymmetry: Face more than just enemy warships. Contend with drone swarms, maritime insurgencies, and silent, predatory submarines.

Why It Thrills

Contact Unknown captures the crushing pressure on the bridge of a modern warship. It is a game of nerves: Do you wait one more second to positively identify the contact, or do you fire before the enemy reaches you? Every decision matters; every missile is precious.

—description from the designer

In Sentinel Rush, duel your opponent by commanding a tag team of 2 Sentinels and 2 Pilots to battle for glory. Combine cards, dice, and strategy to outmaneuver your opponent, unleash devastating attacks, and dominate the arena. Win by knocking out your opponent's Sentinels (reduce them to 0 HP) before they knock out yours!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Möckeföttche: Das rheinische Wortspiel um Liebe, Lust und Leidenschaft
description:
To begin the game: Shuffle all 40 cards and lay them face down on the table, word side down. Make sure the cards are stacked on top of each other, not overlapping.

The game: The starting player turns over two cards at a time. Only one "MÖCKE" card with the back of one "FÖTTCHE" card can be revealed. Turning over two MÖCKE cards or two FÖTTCHE cards would be incorrect, as these combinations don't form a word. If the two revealed cards form a pair, the player may turn over two more cards. This continues until the player reveals two unrelated word parts. The revealed cards are then turned face down again, and the next player takes their turn. Play proceeds clockwise. Matching word pairs are identified by the correct suit, the matching heart symbol, and the corresponding standard German explanation.

Objective of the game: The player who has collected the most word pairs at the end of the game wins.

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Spielbeginn: Alle 40 Karten werden durchmischt und verdeckt - mit der Wortseite nach unten - auf dem Tisch ausgelegt. Dabei ist darauf zu achten, dass die Karten nicht übereinander, sondern nebeneinander liegen.

Das Spiel: Der beginnende Spieler deckt nacheinander zwei Karten auf. Dabei ist zu beachten, dass immer nur eine "MÖCKE"- in Verbindung mit einer "FÖTTCHE"-Kartenrückseite aufgedeckt wird. MÖCKE und MÖCKE oder FÖTTCHE und FÖTTCHE aufzudecken wäre falsch, weil diese aufgedeckten Wortkombinationen keinen Sinn bzw. keine richtigen Wörter ergeben. Bilden die beiden aufgedeckten Karten ein Paar und damit ein richtiges Wort, darf der Spieler noch einmal zwei Karten aufdecken. Dies geschieht so lange, bis er zwei nicht passende Wortbestandteile aufdeckt. Die aufgedeckten Karten sind dann wieder umzudrehen und der nächste Spieler ist am Zug. Gespielt wird im Uhrzeigersinn. Richtige Wortpaare sind an der richtigen Farbe, dem passenden Herzen und der passenden hochdeutschen Erklärung zu erkennen.

Ziel des Spiels: Gewonnen hat der Spieler, der am Ende des Spiels die meisten Wortpaare gesammelt hat.

—description from the back of the box

Regicide Inferno is a standalone Regicide game. It's Regicide with a fifth suit: Flames. Flames allow you to combo with a card from your deck, making for some crazy high-powered plays. Flames also have a downside though - as you "flame on" you'll chew through your deck and banish cards in the process, never to be seen again!

New Flames enemies bring additional challenges to the game. There's also brand new rules that both help and hinder your progress through the game.

Regicide Inferno is a fun, cooperative challenge for both Regicide fans and Regicide newbies.

—description from the publisher

Crown Duels is 2-player duel game, where players assume control of the corrupt royalty from Regicide. Like Regicide, it’s played with a standard 52-card deck. Don armour and weapons, cast spells and empower your equipment as you slowly become corrupt and unleash an epic beatdown on your opponent.
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Viking Rush is a real-time, hectic, co-operative game for 2-5 players. Collect resources, craft tools and build your shelter before winter comes! If you managed to collect enough food and complete your shelters you can advance to the next year. In each year new upgrades and fresh challenges are introduced. Viking Rush is a fast-paced game where careful planning and teamwork is needed to survive!

—description from the publisher

Shuffle all 24 Wish Cards face down to form a deck. Place it in the center of the table.

Each player takes 1 Gift Card and lays it flat in front of them. Do not touch the Gift Card before the game starts.

Each player draws 1 Wish Card from the deck and places it in front of them face down without looking. When everyone is ready, flip all Wish Cards face up at the same time to start the game.

Follow the gift order shown on your Wish Card. Fold your Gift Card to create the same square combination. Keep the card as flat and neat as possible while folding.

When a player finishes, they must call “Stop!” The game pauses and the other players check the result.

If correct, the player keeps that Wish Card as a point.

Then all players draw a new Wish Card (face down), get ready, and flip together to continue.

When the Wish Card deck runs out, the game ends. The player with the most completed Wish Cards wins.

—description from the publisher

The war to save humanity has begun!

Near the end of the 20th century, the Aberrants appeared. These mutant humans possessed terrifying psionic powers and caused unmatched devastation across the globe before finally being banished to the depths of space. 
Now, in 2120, they have returned in new and twisted forms to take revenge on Earth and her nascent space colonies. However, humanity is not defenceless! Psions, disciplined and courageous heroes endowed with their own psychic powers, stand as a last line of defence. 
Choose your side as the monstrous Aberrants or heroic Psions and join the war to determine humanity's future!

About the Game:
Trinity: Battleground is a miniature wargame where players build a small force of Aberrants or Psions and fight through a range of exciting scenarios on the tabletop. 
The game was originally released in 1998 by White Wolf Publishing, and is based on the Storyteller game system used in the linked Trinity RPG. 
With character creation rules, included players can create their own custom units for truly unlimited gameplay.

After more than a quarter century being out of print, we are thrilled to resurrect the game through a license with its current owners, Onyx Path Publishing. 
We have scanned the original game materials and miniatures to create as close a recreation of as possible. 
For players who want to expand their game with additional, experimental, rules, Onyx Path offers the Trinity: Battleground Player's Guide for free.

The miniatures for Trinity: Battleground were sculpted in 28mm heroic scale by the legendary Bob Naismith.

This is a digital product, not a physical playset, and includes everything needed to play, except miniatures and a set of 10-sided dice:

     Trinity: Battleground rulebook
     Unit sheets for Aberrants and Psions
     Cut and fold terrain, including explosive barrels, barricades, a bunker, a watchtower, and a landing pad
     Templates for hills
     Unit sheets for Aberrants and Psions
     Other game components, including cards, rulers, blast templates, and tokens


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
A fast, clever competition along a beautiful historic road

Step onto Perry Road, a picturesque riverside promenade in Shimoda, Shizuoka, Japan — a road where history still lingers, once walked by Commodore Perry during treaty negotiations.
In Shimoda: Perry Road, players take on the role of merchant leaders, strategically placing shops to attract tourists and energize the street. With rules learned in under one minute and a playtime of just 15 minutes, the game blends smart decision-making with a touch of luck, offering a fast, engaging two-player experience that anyone can enjoy.

—description from the designer

Friends with Beverages is a light strategy card game where each player hosts an international gathering and tries to serve custom liqueurs to their invited guests. Each Guest card represents a Guest from a specific country, showing their fictional signature drink, the ingredients and equipment needed to make it, and the victory points that drink is worth. Players draw and manage Resource cards to collect ingredients and install equipment, while the guest list remains fluid as new potential friends appear in the central Guest Market and old guests sometimes cancel unexpectedly.
​
On a turn, players spend “draw points” to take cards either blind from the Resource deck or face-up from the shared Resource Market, balancing quick card draw against grabbing powerful but more expensive equipment. Matching sets of resource or equipment cards can then be discarded to swap out one of your personal Guests with another from the Guest Market or from the top of the Guest deck, allowing players to chase recipes that better fit their current collection. Installed equipment stays in front of a player and can be reused across multiple drinks, but hand size is limited, forcing constant decisions about which ingredients and tools to keep or let go.
​
Once a player has the full set of required ingredient cards in hand and the necessary equipment installed in their “shed,” they can complete that Guest’s drink recipe, discarding the ingredients and turning the Guest card to show it has been served. Players may finish multiple recipes in a single turn if they can afford the costs, after which the markets are refreshed to reveal new opportunities. Recipes do not score immediately, so timing which guests to serve and when to pivot to a different recipe is a key part of the tactical puzzle.
​
Throughout the game, Interrupt and Event cards periodically shake things up by refreshing markets, cancelling guests, stealing or recalling equipment, or temporarily making swaps cheaper, rewarding players who stay flexible rather than locking into a single plan. The game ends when a player completes their third recipe in the standard game (or fourth in the advanced variant), or when the Guest deck runs out. At that point, the player with the highest total points from completed liqueur recipes wins, with simple nose-size-based tiebreakers providing a final bit of party-table humour.

—description from the designer

Otani is an abstract, two-player strategy game of positioning, momentum, and strategy played on a hexagonal board. Each player commands a small set of unique pieces, each embodying a philosophical virtue—such as Strength, Balance, Wisdom, and Spirit—granting powerful spatial abilities that push, pull, swap, spin, or teleport pieces across the board

Players take turns performing two actions with different pieces, carefully combining movement and special abilities to manipulate the battlefield. Victory is achieved by either escorting the Rock of Ruin into the opponent’s village or safely returning both Rocks of Salvation to your own, creating constant tension between offense and defense

With no randomness and fully open information, Otani emphasizes foresight, precise positioning, and creative use of cascading interactions. Simple rules support deep, emergent tactics, making each match a focused duel of minds where a single move can shift the balance of the entire board.

—description from the designer

A fast-paced, competitive flicking game with simple rules. Great for playing with your friends! The game plays like Bocce, but with a twist on scoring. First, flick out the Gold, Silver, Bronze, and Black scoring discs. Each player will then alternate flicking their own color discs, until all players have flicked out all 4 of their discs. Player with the closest disc to each scoring disc scores points, +3 for Gold, +2 for Silver, +1 for Bronze, and -1 for Black. If you have multiple discs that are the closest to a scoring disc, score more than once. Play to 35, 28, or 21 for 2, 3, or 4-6 players respectively.

It fits in an Altoid tin, so take this game on the go and have fun with 2-6 players!

Hikadyat is driven by stories told across many generations, from various cultures and times. We are very ambitious for this competitive Collectable Card Game (CCG) because of its nature of being highly collectable for collectors and strongly competitive for TCG players.

Gameplay: A shared tableau-building game where you only score at the end. You will only score cards that match the suit of the two cards in your hand. This game will satisfy players’ itch for clever card play and spatial scoring.
Players can enjoy mastering one deck at a time, or mix and match decks for even more competitiveness and replayability with multiple decks sold separately.

The objective is to accumulate the highest score. Players will gain points from the cards in the shared tableau that match the suit of their hand cards at the game's conclusion. This not only requires strategic thinking, but also provides the ultimate proving ground for players to demonstrate their adaptability and foresight through card placement and hand management.

Features Chinese folklore such as The Cowherd and the Weaver Girl, The White Snake, The Great Wall of China, The Butterfly Lovers, Journey to the West, and Lady of the Moon.
This deck introduces Form: a card-turning mechanic that scores differently in each form. The cards are also packed with interesting scoring systems for more seasoned players.
A great choice for players who enjoy flexible strategies, unexpected scoring twists, and themes inspired by legendary tales passed down through generations

1/1024  千分一機率 百分百同頻 - 香港原創桌遊：十條二選一難題，搵到同你最同頻既靈魂Buddy～ (或者發現自己係Kam L ？)

- This Ultimate Social Deduction Party Game is a revolutionary party game designed to foster genuine human connection, spark engaging conversations, and reveal surprising insights about your friends.

At its core, 1/1024 is a game of prediction and resonance. Players tackle 10 intriguing “this or that” questions, then predict whether their answer will be in the majority or the minority. But the true magic begins when you search for your BUDDY—another player whose answers align with yours for each question(or strategically differ, depending on your play!). After 10 rounds, the highest scorer is crowned the ultimate 'Resonator'. Yet the most thrilling quest is to find your one-in-a-thousand match, your soul-buddy, who shares all your answers. With odds of exactly 1/1024, that’s where the game gets its name… and its spark.

boardgame: Checker Tobi: Rund um die Welt
description: Hey everyone! Tobi takes players on a trip around the world. Everyone can test their knowledge with quiz questions or actively score points with interactive challenges. Along the way, they learn fun and interesting facts about our planet.

In Ipso, players build a pyramid of number cards and try to form rows of ascending numbers to score the most points. On your turn, take one of the two face-up cards in the center and use it to replace a face-down card in your pyramid, then place the replaced card face up in the center. When all pyramids are revealed, you can either keep your Star card for 3 bonus points or discard it to draw one last card and improve your pyramid. You score only for ascending rows: mixed colors give 1 point per card, single-color rows give 2 points per card, plus 1 point per star and 3 extra if you kept your Star card. The player with the highest total wins.

—description from the publisher

In Vingolf, you must retrieve your CHARACTER'S ARTIFACTS from a shared deck using ACTION cards. The only problem is that you don't know if your artifacts are in the deck or in the hand of another player.

Each of your characters has three artifacts that have been lost in the deck.
Game turn:

The game is played clockwise.

Each player performs one action per turn and ends by drawing cards to complete their hand up to 5 cards. You do not discard at the end of your turn if you have more than 5 cards.

The possible actions in a turn are:


     Play an action card of any sovereignty;
     Place one of your sovereignty's artifacts (this does not trigger its effect);
     Use the effect of your character card or one of your previously placed artifacts. To do this, turn your artifact card or character card over, which means that the effect has been used and can no longer be used.


There are 3 game modes: competitive, cooperative and team.

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Brimstone / Marsh Cinquefoil Promo Card
description:
Promo card (P028)

Brimstone
Scores: between 0 to 80 points depending how many butteflies

Marsh Cinquefoil
Scores: 15 Points or 7 points or 3 points depending of how many Trees

Dwarf Emberforge is a competitive strategy game for 2-4 players where you take on the role of an apprentice to a legendary Forge Master. Your goal is to prove your skill and claim glory at the anvil before your rivals do.

The game is played over a series of rounds divided into placement and activation phases. On your turn, you will strategically place your Embers into four distinct zones:

Ignar: Recycle cards from your hand to gather raw materials like Iron, Bronze, or Mithril.

Durgaz: Use the heat of your embers to forge weapon cards from your hand.

Runarium: Activate the special magical abilities of your already forged weapons.

Gildar: Acquire new blueprints or complete Guild Commissions for prestige.

Key Mechanic: Overheat Players must be careful not to be too greedy. If a zone contains more Embers than the number of players at the end of the placement phase, it Overheats. An overheated zone causes a Total Lockdown, preventing anyone from activating that zone's action for the round.

Victory Conditions The game features a "Sudden Death" ending. You win immediately if you achieve one of two conditions during your turn:

Forge Mastery: Forge 6 weapons from different collections (colors).

Guild Prestige: Complete 3 Commission tokens.

Will you balance your resources and heat management to become the next Forge Master?

—description from the publisher

Go where you want, when you want, in what you want! No schedule, no timeline, no plan... Travel and explore in your RV, Campervan, with your Trailer or simply in your Truck Camper.

Choose and manage your resources through the most efficient dice selection. Then choose to visit selected sites or upgrade your mode of travel.

Be the first to assemble the best combination of visited sites to become the ultimate Boondocker.

Beautiful sites displayed with gorgeous artwork by Tristam Rossin.

—description from the publisher

Chroma is a true blaze of colors: Turn your opponents green with envy by playing your cards cleverly into the grid, creating gradients and using their special abilities at the right time. Keep your eyes on the prize though and don't get caught red-handed, or you might soon be feeling blue… so prove your tactical prowess and pass with flying colors by being the first one to get rid of all your cards!

Order, memorize, pay - and don't make any mistakes, or you'll soon end up with the most expensive dishes on your bill.
In clockwise order you reveal Order cards and try to memorize them as best you can. The player with the best memory and a pinch of luck will spend the least and win the game!

Players lay their tiles, so that as much colors as possible match the neighbour tiles to score points.

After all 100 tiles were placed by the players, the players with most points win the game.

Become the most powerful magnate family in the Wild Fields of Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth borderland!
Euro, deck building, and area control.
One to six players play cards and expand their deck of cards, but they have to be careful, because if their family head dies, they will lose the cards drawn to the deck!
Found new villages, cities, and forts and protect them from enemies.
Players will be forced to face Tatars, Muscovites, Cossack rebels, and every magnate's greatest enemy—his neighbor!
There are almost no dice rolls in a game. Strategy, planning wealth and decption rules the Wild Fields.

The round consists of:
Season One.
All players decide which cards they want to play this season, placing them in front of themselves.
Then everyone reveals them simultaneously. Each card has its own initiative, which we calculate for each player. The player with the lowest initiative will play cards first (all cards in chosen order).
If players have played an auction card, we bid on yellow cards – one-time powerful cards: building a city, fortress, new estate, bringing in settlers, buying artillery, sending instigators, etc.

Season Two – the same as in Season One.

Harvest Season.
Players collect taxes from their subjects and generate prestige (titles held generate prestige).
Then we play world cards – historical events that occurred in a given scenario. To avoid repetitive gameplay, these cards are shuffled and revealed in random order (1-5 cards per round). The final element of the round is to determine whether the heads of the player families have lost their health and consequently died. Since titles are held for life, this may mean that the position has become vacant and a new official must be elected.

Voivode, castellan, bishop, or hetman increase prestige but also impose on players the associated responsibilities. The voivode should not break the law and must punish criminals. Failure to do so results in loss of prestige.
The castellan is his deputy.
The hetman must fight enemies of the Commonwealth and prevent peasants from being kidnapped by the Tatars. If they escape with them, he lose prestige.
The bishop simply needs to avoid shedding the blood of the king's loyal subjects.

Each time a magnate plays a yellow card, commands a battle, or commits a crime, they receive experience points.
Each level of experience earned allows them to gain one card for their playing or tactics decks (for combat).
Unfortunately, each subsequent level of experience requires more experience points, and they are gained more slowly. Therefore, it is sometimes beneficial to have the head of the family die to gain cards that are more relevant at that moment in the game. Additionally, once the head of the family rests in their sarcophagus, they receive victory points for each level of experience they gain.

During the round, players recruit troops, plunder neighboring estates, participate in royal expeditions to other countries or provinces, and expand their own estates.

The player with the most victory points wins the game.
Players receive victory points for: income, experience level of the family head, happiness of their subjects, building fortresses and destroying units of Commonwealth enemies.

—description from the publisher

Goal : Collect 4 stars before your opponents.

Each player has a character who accumulates weaknesses throughout their life, until their inevitable death.

The character is then "depleted": the player retrieves their cards and places them in their graveyard (their reserve).

By collecting the correct symbols, they can exchange them for an Objective card to earn 1 star.

Aventura Z: Volume 3 – Paradox plunges players into a survival-horror story where reality itself has been fractured. From a sabotaged Antarctic base to a silent city lost in deep time, the game’s theme revolves around mystery, danger, and temporal anomalies that threaten humanity’s survival. Players step into the role of a protagonist caught in these events, making difficult choices that shape the unfolding narrative and lead to multiple possible endings.

The experience centers on tense exploration and decision-making, as players investigate locations, confront hostile threats, and manage dwindling time and resources. Dice-based challenges tied to character attributes determine success or failure, while constant pressure ensures that every action has consequences. Rather than competing for points, players push forward to survive and uncover the truth, with strong replayability driven by branching paths and alternate conclusions.

—description from the publisher

Konspira is a fast-paced game where you work together… but not with everyone. Taking on the roles of opposition activists, players work together to carry out secret missions for underground anti-communist organizations of the 1980s. Beware! Amongst you are the traitors: Unofficial collaborators of the Polish People's Republic Security Service (SB).

While the oppositionists gather the resources necessary to complete their missions, the collaborators strive to sabotage their efforts keeping their true identity in secret. Will the oppositionists manage to avoid being caught?

At the start of the game, each player draws a card with a secret identity — either an oppositionist or a SB collaborator — which remains secret until the end of the game. The other players don't know the identity of fellow players. During the game three rounds the opposition members attempts to collect the resources required to complete the mission. In the same time the collaborators work to undermine these efforts while carefully concealing their identity. If the mission succeeds, the opposition wins. If it fails, the collaborators claim victory.

The game rulebook contains an introduction to the activities of the anti-communist opposition in 1980s Poland, highlighting the ruthless tactics used by the State Security Service against those fighting for Poland’s independence and sovereignty.

The illustrations, created by comic book artist Hubert Ronek, are inspired by real events from the 1980s.

—description from the publisher

The game is the winner of the BookGame Award 2026 for the Best Party Game.

Déjà Vu is a fast, tense memory game where players step into a mental duel shaped by false memories and strange coincidences. The theme revolves around the unsettling feeling of familiarity, as you try to recognize patterns that seem known—but may never have existed.

Players take turns secretly looking at cards and then revealing them, pushing their luck to uncover repeating numbers without making a mistake. Careful memory, intuition, and timing are key, as one slip can give your opponent the advantage. You win by collecting all the hunch tokens or by ending the game at the right moment and scoring the most points.

Fake Zebra is a lively party game of hidden roles, memory, and deception set during an over-the-top zebra party that must finish before midnight. Most players are zebras, working together to complete all the party activities, but one or two lions are secretly disguised among them, trying to sabotage the night without being discovered. The theme mixes chaos, humor, and suspicion, as everyone pretends to help while questioning each other’s intentions.

Gameplay is simple and fast-paced: players take turns flipping cards to complete matching activity sets, relying on memory to avoid mistakes. Completing activities brings the zebras closer to victory, while errors advance the clock toward midnight. All discussion is allowed, and bluffing is encouraged—especially by the lions. The game ends when either all activities are completed (zebras win), or the clock hits twelve, triggering a vote to identify the lion. If a lion is correctly exposed, the zebras win; if not, the lion (or lions) claim victory.

—description from the publisher

The Infinite Library is crumbling. You and your opponent are the last Archivariums, desperate keepers of disintegrating knowledge. The dice are Scrolls of Primal Knowledge, their faces representing six esoteric Arcanas (Alchemy, Chronicles, Astronomy, Poetry, Mechanics, Insight).

On your turn, you attempt to order the chaos by rolling Scrolls and gathering identical ones into stable Archives. Any lone Scroll becomes a volatile Key, hungry to unlock and absorb your rival's corresponding Archives. If you manage to order all your Scrolls perfectly, you can impose The Seal of Silence, protecting your hoard for a brief moment.

The game is a struggle for control over the last fragments of reality. Will you absorb your opponent's knowledge, or dissolve into the whisper of forgotten pages when your turn comes and you have nothing left?

ARCHIVARIUM
Players: 2
Playtime: 5-20 min
Components: 12 standard six-sided dice (d6)

OBJECTIVE
Leave your opponent with no dice at the start of their turn.

SETUP
Each player takes 6 dice and places them in front of themselves.

GAMEPLAY
On your turn, take all dice in front of you and begin a roll series.

1. Roll all your dice.

2. You must group dice. You must set aside all possible groups (pairs, triples, etc.) of matching numbers from this roll.

3. Decide: Re-roll or Stop?

— If you have ungrouped dice, you may take only them and re-roll (return to step 1).

— If you choose to stop, or if a roll yields no new groups, your turn ends. Any remaining ungrouped dice become Keys.

KEY PHASE (If you have Keys)
For each Key (look at its number):

If your opponent has a group with the same number, take that entire group. The Key joins it.

If not, give the Key to your opponent.

PROTECTION (The Seal)
If you end your turn with no Keys (all dice grouped), your groups gain The Seal and are safe from the opponent's Keys during their next turn.

SPECIAL: HARMONY OF SPHERES
If a roll of all six of your dice shows 1-2-3-4-5-6, you immediately gain The Seal and your turn ends.

WINNING
A player loses a round at the start of their turn if they have no dice. A full match is two rounds, with players swapping the first turn. Win both rounds to win the match (1:1 is a draw).

—description from the publisher

Come together to create the soundtrack of your life in this party game for 3-8+ players.  Use song cards to choose the best song to fit the theme each turn. What was the theme of your awkward middle school years? What song do you hear when you’re in a job interview? What would your theme song be if you were a professional wrestler? Pick the songs you think fit best!

What Is My Soundtrack?

My Soundtrack is a fun, fast-paced party game where you and your friends create the soundtrack of your lives. Choose a theme, play a song card, and see who picks the perfect track for the moment. Every round brings laughter, memories, and music!

How It Works
1. Pick a Theme

On your turn, draw two Theme cards.
    Choose one and read it aloud (example: “The song in my head when I drive past a cop”).

2. Play a Song

Each player picks one Song card from their hand that best matches the theme.
    Choices can be based on title, lyrics, or personal connection.

3. Choose the Winner

The theme-setter judges which Song card fits best.
    The chosen player earns a guitar pick!

4. Build Your Soundtrack

Play continues until everyone has shared three Themes.
    At the end, everyone reveals their full “life soundtrack.”

Winning the Game

The player with the most guitar picks is the official winner… but the real fun comes from building the best soundtrack to your life!

Optional Variations

This Theme’s for You
Pass your unused Theme card to another player instead of discarding it. They must play it on their turn!

Mash-Up Madness
Combine two Song cards to make a hilarious or epic new track. For example: “The Power of Love” + “Unholy” = “Unholy Power.”

—description from the publisher

There are no kings in this forest... there are animals whose bravery—or fear—depends on the situation.

In every encounter, ONE ANIMAL GETS SCARED.

The dog scares the cat, who scares the mouse, who scares the elephant, who scares the dog, who scares the cat...

HURRY AND FIND THE SCARED ANIMAL! If you're the first, you get it! But if none of them get scared, you'll have to do it yourself!

The sounds to scare them are: WOOF - MEOW - CHIK CHIK - BOO

Practice the animal sounds and imitations; get ready for a FAST, NOISY, AND THRILL-FULL game!

—description from the publisher

A family game to get started and discover the essence of board games:

     decision-making
     the awakening of strategic thinking
     the surprise of chance


With your cards, choose a spot on the carousel! Watch out that others don't take your place. The carousel starts spinning! With each turn, you'll win tokens, and if you pull the ring, it's another turn!!!

—description from the publisher

New game from the Shiki Universe by Maldón.

This game is perfect for the youngest members of the family. Who can resist a snowball fight? We all face off at the same time in a snowball fight.

A simple, family-friendly game of battles where you have to collect the most special cards to win. These special cards are GRANDMA'S CARDS...they're always there and so important in our lives!

Watch out, if there's a tie, you'll play with your opponent's cards!

—description from the publisher

Inelul Timpului is an immersive educational board game developed by the Cu Alte Cuvinte Foundation, specifically designed to bring Romany history and culture to the forefront. Developed with the support of the National Cultural Fund Administration (AFCN), the game serves as a bridge between generations, offering a nuanced perspective on the heritage and contributions of the Romany community.

Designed to foster empathy and fight stereotypes, players are invited to travel through time, discovering key historical figures, cultural milestones, and the vibrant traditions of the Romany people. It is more than just a game; it is a tool for social inclusion and representative education.

In Inelul Timpului, players navigate different historical eras, encountering diverse personalities and pivotal moments. The game blends history with strategic movement, requiring players to became CUAC ambassadors as a team and travel on the map to place embassies, while eliminating existing threats.  
Players will win if they clear all existing threats or reach the end of the Timeline Track.

The first of its kind to focus specifically on the rich and often-overlooked history of the Romany community, was created to be delivered to 800 Romanian schools as a game based learning experience.

—description from the designer

Qwirkle Super-Powers. These Super-Power tokens and cards give you new ways to win. Breathe new life into your Qwirkle games.

Start by drawing 3 tokens to indicate the powers available for the game. On your turn, you can then activate these powers once per game! Choose the right power at the right time: a guaranteed turnaround!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Qwirkle Super-Pouvoirs. Ces jetons et cartes Super-pouvoir vous proposent de nouvelles façons de gagner. Donnez un nouveau souffle à vos parties de Qwirkle.

Commencez par piocher 3 jetons qui indiqueront les pouvoirs disponibles pour la partie. A votre tour de jeu, vous pourrez ensuite activer ces pouvoirs une fois par partie ! Choisissez le bon pouvoir au bon moment : retournements de situation garantis !

—description from the publisher

Double Bluff is a trick taking game played with dominoes. The goal of the game is to capture more doubles than your opponent each round. Each time a player wins a trick, their hand size will be reduced by one, limiting their options. When one player runs out of tiles in their hand, the round ends.

In addition to typical must-follow trick taking, tiles can be played face down. When playing a tile face down, a player must declare that it is one of the 7 doubles. By successfully bluffing, and double bluffing, a player can have more control over the hand they are dealt. If, however, they are caught, they risk giving more control to their opponent.

—description from the designer

boardgame: ハコベルバルーン Hakobell Balloon
description:
Hakobell Balloon is a new type of balance game with a chicken race element, where players take turns placing bells on the floor, and the last person remaining must attempt to carry the bells. Adults and children alike can have fun while enjoying the sound of the bells. In addition to bells, you can use cubes and rings to freely change the difficulty level! Team battles with 4 or 6 players are also fun ♪ This is especially recommended for fans of action games, cute games, and those looking for a game that shows their child's growth! *The colors of the string, bell, handle, and cubes may vary! *Delivery will be via Letter Pack Plus and will generally be hand-delivered. If you have a preferred location, such as a delivery box, we will write this in the notes, so please let us know in a message.

•••

『ハコベルバルーン』は、順番に鈴を乗せていき最後に残った一人が鈴運びに挑戦する、チキンレース要素もある新感覚バランスゲームです。
鈴の音を感じながら、大人も子どもワイワイ楽しめますよ。
鈴のほかにキューブやわっかを使って難易度を自由に変えられます！4人や6人でチーム戦も楽しいですよ♪
アクションゲームが好きな方やかわいいゲームが好きな方、お子さんの成長が感じられるゲームをお探しの方に特におすすめです！

※ひも・鈴・持ち手・キューブの色は物によって異なります！
※レターパックプラスでの発送で基本手渡しでの配達となります。宅配ボックス等置いて欲しい場所があれば備考に書いておくので、メッセージでお伝えくださると幸いです。

A sip of courage can bring victory, but a sip of carelessness can bring about flames!
Story of Dangerous　
Sweets

Sweet tooth challengers from all over the world gather for the annual sweets festival!
Cream puffs, macarons, taiyaki, sakura mochi... they all look like the most exquisite sweets.
But among them is a dangerous item containing a chili pepper bomb...?!

Will you enjoy the delicious sweets and safely leave, or will you continue eating knowing the danger?
One bite of courage will bring victory, and one bite of carelessness will bring flames.

The hilarious Sweet Russian Roulette is about to begin!

What kind of game
?

Each round (day) a sweets card will be placed on the table.
On your turn, you flip over one and eat it.

If it's delicious, you'll get points and be happy...
but if it's a chili pepper bomb, you'll just go home in shame and get zero points.

You can also pass and go home, so come back at a good moment and score!

Recommended
Points!

There's a catch to these delicious sweets...

Each of the four sweets will have a different score if it is delicious.
For example, a cream puff will be worth 1 point, and a sakura mochi will be worth 6 points!

So should you eat sakura mochi instead?
It's not that simple.
There's a higher chance that sakura mochi contains a chili pepper bomb...

Would you still eat sakura mochi?
Or would you rather have a safe cream puff?

The pepper bomb that ruins everything

On your turn, you either flip a card (eat a sweet) or go home (pass).
If you go home, you get points for the sweets you've eaten up to that point!

If you're greedy and the sweets you eat are delicious, your score will go up, but
if you eat a chili pepper bomb, your score for that day (round) will be 0 points.

Let's go home without eating the chili bomb...
but if we leave too early, the other members will have free access to the sweets on the table...

•••

ひと口の勇気が勝利を呼び、ひと口の油断が炎を呼ぶ!?
Story of Dangerous Sweets　
​デンジャラスイーツのストーリー

年に一度のスイーツフェスティバルに、世界中から甘党チャレンジャーたちが集結!
シュークリーム、マカロン、たい焼き、桜餅・・・見た目はすべて極上のスイーツ。
しかし、その中には唐辛子爆弾入りの危険な一品が…⁉

「おいしい」スイーツを堪能して安全に抜けるか、それとも危険を承知で食べ進めるか。
一口の勇気が勝利を呼び、一口の油断が炎を呼ぶ。

​大笑い必至のスイーツロシアンルーレット、いざ開幕!

What kind of game
どんなゲーム?

1ラウンド(1日)ごとに、テーブルにスイーツカードが並びます。
手番では、一枚めくってパクっと食べます。

​おいしければ得点になって嬉しい…
​ですが、唐辛子爆弾ならすごすごとお家に帰って0点です。

途中でパスしてお家に帰ることもできるので、おいしいところで帰って得点化しましょう♪

Recommended Points​!
​おすすめポイント!

​おいしいスイーツには罠がある…

4種類のスイーツは、それぞれ美味しかった時の得点が異なります。
例えば、シュークリームは1点で、桜餅は6点!!

だったら桜餅を食べた方がいい⁇
​そんなカンタンな話ではありません。
桜餅の方が、唐辛子爆弾が入っている確率が高いのです…。

それでも桜餅を食べますか？
​無難にシュークリームを食べますか⁇

​すべてを台無しにする唐辛子爆弾

手番では、1枚カードをめくる(スイーツを食べる)か、お家に帰る(パスする)か。
お家に帰るとそれまでに食べたスイーツの得点をGet!!

よくばって、食べたスイーツがおいしければ、得点は上がっていきますが、
唐辛子爆弾を食べたら、その日(ラウンド)の得点は0点に。

唐辛子爆弾を食べることなく、お家に帰りましょう…
​ただし、あまりに早く帰ってしまうと、テーブルのスイーツは他のメンバーが食べ放題…
artists: おーつぼじゅうじん (Jyujin Ohtsubo)
publishers: ぶれけけゲームズ (Brekeke Games)

boardgame: Blitzkrieg in the West 1940: 20 Wargame Scenarios – Small Battles
description:
These 20 wargame scenarios are aimed at those players who might  be constrained with space available for a table, just starting and have few troops for this period, or might not have the time nor the inclination for a larger game. Or maybe all three factors are relevant to you. 
So I have trawled through my 192 scenarios and chosen these 20 with these factors in mind. 
These scenarios are the smallest games with the fewest troops and therefore reduced playing time. 15 require an area only 1m x 2m (3’ x 6’), representing 2km x 4km, and the other 5 need 2m x 2m (6’ x 6’) representing 4km x 4km. Being drawn from the original volumes they are all to the same standard. I've also shown in red under each scenario the size of force required. These are original scenarios with detailed maps and organisations for battles. 
These scenarios are designed for wargamers who enjoy their history as much as their gaming.

All scenarios include the actual map from 1940, accurate forces and real battles. 
These are highly detailed and well researched games.

The PDF preview details the scenarios included. 
In short, there are seven featuring the French, seven the BEF, three the Dutch and three the Belgians.

—description from the publisher

Roll the game dice to pick your word mode, then roll the letter dice to unleash your inner wordsmith! You have just one minute to write the most words and claim literary victory!

—description from the publisher

The Kingdom has fractured. The Royals of all four realms—Hearts, Clubs, Spades, and Diamonds—have been abducted.
The Kings have been thrown into the Dungeons.

The Queens are being held captive by ancient Beasts.

You are the Jack of your Realm. You must race against the Jacks of the opposing suits. You must battle through the levels to gain strength, find the specific Ace Key to unlock your King, slay the Monster guarding your Queen, and escort them both back to your Palace.

Who will restore their Monarchy first?

1-4 Players: Competitive Faction Control.

The Components: One Game Board. Two to Four Deck of Cards. Coins.

The Strategy: Manage your hand, calculated risks, and race to the finish.

—description from the designer

Happy holidays! 
All the princesses have gathered at a comfortable country castle for the summer. As is proper and beneficial for healthy slumber, each princess has brought her own set of 3 favorite mattresses. But an evil witch has crept into the castle and mixed up all the bedding, bewitching some of it beyond recognition! Now, as soon as the lights are off, the princesses bounce around on their beds all night long and won’t calm down.

It is your task as a team of tutors to sort out all the mattresses and return them to their rightful owners. Summon magical helpers and use the magic looking glass or your sense of touch to lull the mischievous princesses to their majestic dreams.

All the players are one team of tutors. By touch, you will determine what kind of mattresses are on the beds. Your goal is to sort out all the mattresses on the beds so that each princess has only her set of 3 matching mattresses. If you succeed, the girls will fall sweetly asleep. At the end of the game, check the result of your work: the more princesses you have lulled to sleep, the better!

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Princess and the Pea

description:
The goal is to be the player with the most animals in their farm by the end of the game.

This is a "push-your-luck" game. The active player starts by revealing a hidden card from the herd and placing it above their Farm card. They then decide to continue or stop.

Continuing: The player draws another card (either hidden or already revealed).

Conflict (Bust): If the player has two of the same animal above their Farm card during a turn, the animals fight! The collected cards are returned to the herd (face up), and the turn ends immediately with no gain.

Success: If the new animal is different, the player can choose to continue again or stop.

Stopping: If the player stops voluntarily (after 1, 2, or 3 cards), they secure the cards by sliding them under their Farm card. Each animal counts as 1 point.

The game ends when there are no cards left in the herd, or only revealed cards remain.

Players line up all the animals they secured under their farm. The player with the longest line of animals wins the game.

The goal is to collect the best Treasures to earn the most victory points.

On their turn, the active player picks one card from the pile in the center of the table.

Availability: A card can only be taken if it is partially covered by another card.

Card Placement & Effects:

Special Items (Fishing Rod, Shovel): Their effect is triggered immediately.

Chests: Placed face-down (Sand side up) in front of the player. The contents remain hidden until the end.

Treasures: Placed face-up in front of the player.

The game ends when every player has collected their 8th card. (Note: The Sandcastle card does not count toward this limit).

Players reveal their face-down Chest cards.

Everyone tallies their points from visible treasures and revealed chests. The player with the highest score wins.

Breaking news! A real Yeti has been spotted in the mountains! And they say it’s got a really big sweet tooth. You, as young zoologists, cannot miss this once-in-a-lifetime opportunity — you simply must become the first to tame this fantastic creature. So, you pack up your gear and set out on an adventure. However, you are not the only ones who heard about this sighting: a zookeeper is following the Yeti’s tracks, intent on catching the beast and putting it on display at the local zoo. You will not stand for this — the Yeti belongs in the wild! As for the Yeti, it’s not going to be very happy with all these uninvited guests... Unless sweets are involved, of course!

How to play in three steps:

     Choose by how many spaces you would like to move, but avoid matching other players. 
     Set your opponents back with the snowballs.
     Race not only against other player, but the game as well!


The first to reach the top of the mountain will win!

—description from the publisher

The goal is to unmask the culprit of the investigation by correctly identifying the suspect who matches the 3 specific clues provided.

The Search: All players play at the same time. They scan the suspects on the table to find the one character that matches the 3 active clues.

Identification: As soon as a player thinks they have found the suspect, they point their finger at the card.

Validation & Progression: The group verifies the choice together.

If they agree, the card is taken and flipped over.

The back of this card reveals 3 new clues for the next step of the investigation.

The search continues immediately with these new clues.

The round ends when there is only one suspect card left on the table.

The players reveal the secret Investigation card (hidden at the start).

Verdict:

     If the remaining suspect matches the culprit on the Investigation card: Bravo! You have solved the crime.
     If they are different: Failed! The culprit escaped.

Expansion of 55 double-sided cards (i.e., 110 new cards to guess) introducing new themes, including cooking, to add to your base game.

Try to make people guess your classified document by crossing out superfluous lines directly on your card. Read what's left out loud, but be careful to leave enough information for your teammates to guess!

To do this, you will first have to cross out as many lines as possible directly on the card because each piece of information left visible generates suspicion about your group and too much suspicion will cause you to lose the game. Read your text out loud, and hope your team understands the message.

Novice or advanced mode, in cooperation or in teams, adjust the difficulty as you wish for games that are always just as interesting regardless of the number of players or the gaming habits of the participants around the table.

Expansion of 55 double-sided cards (i.e. 110 new cards to guess) introducing new themes, including Comics, to add to your base game.

Try to make people guess your classified document by crossing out superfluous lines directly on your card. Read what's left out loud, but be careful to leave enough information for your teammates to guess!

To do this, you will first have to cross out as many lines as possible directly on the card because each piece of information left visible generates suspicion about your group and too much suspicion will cause you to lose the game. Read your text out loud, and hope your team understands the message.

Novice or advanced mode, in cooperation or in teams, adjust the difficulty as you wish for games that are always just as interesting regardless of the number of players or the gaming habits of the participants around the table.

The 5 Second Rule Mini Family Tube is a portable, travel-sized version of the popular party game where players name three items in a category within 5 seconds, leading to hilarious, pressured answers like "Name 3 things mom always says" or "3 types of food". It's designed for families, featuring age-appropriate (8+) questions and a compact tube for on-the-go fun, containing cards and the iconic ZOOOOOP timer, perfect for road trips and family game nights.

—description from the publisher

The fun of mahjong, condensed!
The definitive mahjong × card game that beginners and mahjong lovers can enjoy together.

Mahjong rules are explained in a clear and simple way—while keeping all the fun intact! This is a mahjong card game that both beginners and experienced players can get excited about together.

The main game, “Chi-Pon,” is played with an 8-card hand, aiming to complete three sets of three cards. By skillfully using “chi” and “pon”—stealing cards discarded by other players—you create combinations of hands and aim for high scores.

For those who want to learn mahjong, you don’t need to know complicated rules. You can learn how to aim for hands and develop strategies, making it easy to experience the appeal of mahjong in a simple and accessible way.

As a ROVE (Results-Oriented Versatile Explorer) you're used to surveying the fringes of space, but now you've crash-landed on an unfamiliar planet. You'll need to reconfigure your reusable modules to get to safety!

ROVE is a solo spatial puzzle game. In each game of ROVE, the player will face a series of mishaps that can only be solved by arranging the 6 module cards in a specific pattern. Moving a module requires Movement Points gained by discarding cards from hand for the listed amount. Players may gain additional Points if their current module layout matches the pattern shown on the discarded card.

Each module costs 1 Movement Point per activation and uses its own unique movement style, such as only moving diagonally or pushing other modules a single space. Modules also have powerful single-use abilities that can help get players out of a tight spot. Properly timing these abilities -- as well as effectively using the limited number of Movement cards -- will help the player complete ROVE’s mission successfully.

—description from the publisher

Tatu ja Patu - 200 kummaa kuvaa contains 200 different Tatu and Patu-themed images. Players set all picture buttons face up in the center of the table. Each round each player gets three cards face down, which the flip face up simultaneously. Continued simultaneously, players try to find all the picture buttons with exactly same pictures that appear in the pictures of their cards during one minute timer. If a player finds all corresponding picture buttons of one card, they score points denoted on that card. A player gets one minus point in case if they did not find all the corresponding picture buttons depicted on a card. The game ends after the round when some player succeeds to cross the goal line of 50 points, and a player with most points wins.

Mythical Tricks of Ancient Civilisations is a fast, tactical two-player trick-taking duel set in 1870, during the height of archaeological exploration.

Rival scholars compete across a series of expeditions, presenting ancient artifacts to outshine one another and earn academic glory. The game uses a compact 20-card deck, where players capture Gold and Silver Stars by winning tricks, or by combining matching halves of ancient treasure maps into major discoveries.

All cards belong to an ancient civilizations and must match to win a trick, but Atlantean artifacts overshadow all others, acting as a powerful, ever-present threat. Early in an expedition, players may act freely; as resources run low, the rules tighten.

Inspired by the traditional game Schnapsen, Mythical Tricks transitions from may-follow to must-follow rules during play, adding structure and tension as each expedition progresses.
—description from the designer

Puzzled Words is a hybrid, fast‑paced, competitive word‑building game that blends tabletop strategy with app‑driven challenges.

COMPONENTS:
100 Lasercut letter tiles as jigsaw pieces
Web app.

FEATURES

     100 laser‑cut wooden tiles with raised letters and point values
     Scrabble‑style scoring with faster, more competitive gameplay
     Interlocking jigsaw tile design for a tactile, puzzle‑like experience
     Companion app generates round‑by‑round challenges
     Required letters or categories (colors, animals, foods, etc.)
     Later rounds add constraints (points, vowels, patterns, adjacency rules)
     No long waits — everyone builds words at the same time
     Perfect for game nights, families, educators, and word‑game lovers
     Handmade, durable, and gift‑ready


Puzzled Words is a fast‑paced, competitive word game inspired by the best parts of Scrabble—without the long, slow turns many players dislike.
This handcrafted set includes 100 laser‑cut tactile puzzle tiles that interlock like a jigsaw and score like Scrabble tiles. Use the companion app to generate rapid‑fire challenges, then race to build and place your word before anyone else claims the spot.

Love Scrabble but hate waiting forever for everyone’s turn?
Puzzled Words keeps the strategy you love and removes the downtime. The app generates quick, competitive challenges so every round stays active and exciting.

Puzzled Words blends the familiar feel of Scrabble tiles with a fast, modern gameplay style that keeps everyone engaged.
No long turns. No waiting. Just quick challenges, clever words, and 100 beautifully laser‑cut tactile tiles that snap together like a puzzle.

Game mode 1: follow the category

Use the companion app to generate round‑by‑round challenges, then place your words onto the shared puzzle board before someone else steals your spot. The app throws new constraints at you every round. Build words, attach them to the shared board, and outplay everyone at the table.

How Categorical Constraints Affect Game Play:

     Players must think more deeply


Words aren’t just about meaning — they’re about structure, scoring, and tile economy.


     The board becomes more interconnected


Because every word must attach to the existing puzzle, constraints force players to find clever placements.


     The app becomes the “game master”


It drives variety, difficulty, and replayability without interfering with the tactile tabletop experience.

A unique hybrid word game that’s perfect for families, game nights, and puzzle lovers.
Easy to learn, endlessly replayable, and crafted to last.

Game mode 2: word association chain

This mode transforms Puzzled Words into a fast, creative, brain‑twisting chain‑reaction game. Instead of matching scores or constraints, players must connect concepts — building a branching network of related words on the puzzle board.

Objective

Create a continuous chain of meaningfully connected words, each one linking to the previous word on the board. The puzzle grows like a mind map, and the challenge is keeping your associations clever, valid, and attachable.

How it works

1. Start With a Seed Category

The app chooses a starting theme, such as: VEHICLES
Example seed: VEHICLES
Place the seed word tile in the center of the table.
2. Players Take Turns Adding Related Words
Each new word must be:

     Conceptually related to the word it attaches to
     Physically connected to the puzzle board
     A valid association (the group can challenge questionable links)


Example Chain
Seed: VEHICLES
Player 1: connects CAR (a type of vehicle)
Player 2: connects TIRE (a part of a car)
Player 1: connects ROUND (a property of a tire)
Player 2: connects PLATE (a round plate)
Player 1: connects DINNER (you eat from a plate)
Player 2: connects FORK (used at dinner)
And so on…
The chain can drift far from the original category — as long as each step is logically linked.

What counts as a valid connection?

A word is valid if it has a clear, explainable relationship to the word it touches.
Examples:

Category → Example (VEHICLE → CAR)
Object → Part (CAR → TIRE)
Object → Property (TIRE → ROUND)
Property → Example (ROUND → PLATE)
Function → Tool (DINNER → FORK)
Synonym / antonym
Cause / effect
Material / object
Action / object
If players disagree, they vote.

Why this mode is awesome


     Encourages creativity and lateral thinking
     Creates hilarious, surprising word chains
     Works beautifully with the interlocking puzzle tiles
     No two games ever follow the same path
     Great for families, parties, and warm‑up rounds


—description from the designer

Claim and connect to win!

On each turn, roll a die and place walls trying to build 4-sided "sectors."  Block your opponent from claiming sectors or get lucky and draw special tiles that give you extra actions.  Claim 5 sectors in a row and you've won Sectori

boardgame: Top Ten Adventures: Adventures and Discoveries
description: This is a promotional card for Top Ten Adventures featuring a Jumanji-inspired adventure.

boardgame: Murder Mystery in a Box: The Ski Lodge Murder
description:
The Assignment Letter from the FBI states that the goals for your investigation are to identify the killer and uncover the motive of the murderer. It also advises that you should observe the following protocols during your investigation: (1) solve the case by developing the most likely theory that is supported by the evidence, (2) accept statements made by witnesses or other sources of information as true unless they are contradicted by other evidence, and (3) if an inconsistency arises during your investigation, be sure to check it against the other available evidence.

​THE MURDER
The body of Leah Patterson was found lying in the snow at 9:55 pm.  She was stabbed multiple times. The murder weapon was never recovered. Patterson was wearing winter clothing including a long coat, sweater, gloves and a scarf.

Wispwood: Schräge Stämme is a Promo Card providing an alternative scoring mode.

Schräge Stämme rules text:
3 stars for each tree on a diagonal without will-o'-the-wisps

In Christmas Window Wonderland, players take on the role of department store window designers.

Within a limited space, they must arrange products, decorations, and display props to create the most beautiful, complete, and structurally pleasing Christmas window.

This is a family-friendly board game centered around shape composition.

Players don’t need to calculate complex effects—just focus on:

Connecting same-type items into visually appealing shapes

Placing key display props in the right spots

At the end of the game, the player with the highest score wins.

Fuse: Build Your Heat is an intimate card-and-dice game for couples designed to deepen connection, build anticipation, and gradually turn up the intensity. Players move through three escalating levels of play: Connection, Intimacy, and Passion.

At the beginning of the game, players agree on a prize for the winner and a playful forfeit for the loser, based on their comfort level and preferences.

On each turn, players draw cards that prompt conversation, physical touch, playful challenges, or creative role-play scenarios. A unique dice mechanic adds variety by randomly selecting body parts or actions, making each playthrough feel different. Profession and situation cards introduce light improvisation and fantasy, keeping the experience spontaneous and engaging.

Players earn coins by successfully completing tasks and answering questions. If a player chooses not to perform a task or answer a question, they must give up the number of coins indicated on the card. As an optional alternative, players may choose to take a small drinking penalty instead of losing coins.

The game follows a fixed progression on the board and ends immediately when one player reaches the final red flame space, which typically occurs after approximately 30 turns per player.

When the game ends, both players count their coins. The player with the most coins wins and claims the agreed prize. The other player completes the agreed forfeit.

While Fuse emphasizes consent, customization, and shared enjoyment, it has a clear end condition and a defined winner based on in-game scoring. Replayability comes from customizable intensity levels, randomized cards, and dice outcomes.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Cyber Pet Quest: Spray Bottle Promo Card
description:
Spend 4 (3 on Level 2) Luck to add 1 Impact to ALL CyMS within the same Space, once per Round.

An exclusive double sided promo card was provided as part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound Campaign 'Pack B.'

Tony Pawk Purr Skater is a roll-and-write game of feline skaters landing tricks in various maps. Each player chooses a skate legend and put them to the test across a variety of challenging maps, pulling off the most radical tricks to earn as many points as possible. But beware! Harder tricks can knock them off-balance, and maintaining balance is crucial to avoid falling and losing points.

Three dice are rolled for all the players every turn. Each player chooses which die or dice will be used for one of the three actions: move, maintain balance or perform a trick. Players score points for successfully landing tricks, avoiding falls, and finishing tasks. The player with the highest score wins or, if playing solo, earn a title according to the total points scored.

—description from the publisher

Phase 10 Express is a faster, more portable version of the classic Phase 10 rummy-style card game, featuring updated phases and special action cards like Wilds and Skips, designed for quicker rounds with a smaller deck, keeping the core strategy of completing sets and runs but in a speedy, convenient format. It's great for travel or when you want that familiar Phase 10 fun without the longer playtime, emphasizing quick strategic plays.

Key Features:
    Faster Gameplay: Designed to complete rounds and the game much quicker than the original.
    Portable: Comes with a smaller, travel-friendly deck.
    Updated Phases: Offers new or modified phases (like Two Sets of 3, One Run of 4, 4 Odd Cards) for quicker completion.
    Action Cards: Includes "Wild" and "Skip" cards for added excitement and strategy.

How It Plays (Basic Idea):
    Players try to be the first to lay down their hand to complete a specific "Phase" (e.g., 3 of a kind, a run of 4).
    Once you complete a phase, you discard your remaining cards and try to get rid of them by adding to others' melds or your own.
    The game ends when someone completes all 10 phases, with the winner being the one with the lowest score (least points in hand).

Inspired by the 2019 anti-government protests in Lebanon, Wasta is a satirical card game about everyday life inside a dysfunctional political system. Each card represents a familiar force in Lebanese life, such as the bank, the judge, the police officer, the nosy neighbour, and the sectarian thug.

The game is played over a series of rounds. At the beginning of each round, players are dealt a single card that they keep hidden. On a turn, a player may draw a card from the deck or use the ability of the card in their hand. The round ends either when only one player remains or when the deck runs out, in which case the player with the highest remaining card wins. That player receives a Tarboush token. The first player to collect 5 Tarboushes (or 3 in the short game) wins.

Black Dice mini-expansion pack contains five special “level up” cards to beef up your adventuring abilities, two powerful black attack dice, and five new special treasure cards to put those black dice to the test.

—description from the publisher

Are you ready for an exciting journey with KMB and LWB?  In this board game, you can travel around Hong Kong, learning various KMB payment methods and passengers' rules, making your journey more enjoyable.

This is a spin-and-move game for children to learn about riding the busses around Hong Kong.  Players use various payment methods to move around the board and collect cards that represent various attractions in the city.  The player who collects the best of attractions is the winner.

A criminal mastermind is retiring. Over seven jobs, players compete to prove their crew can deliver results and earn the reputation needed to take over the syndicate.

The Job is a 2–5 player trick-taking game built around wagering, prediction, and follow-through. Each round, players commit Crew, secretly decide how many tricks they plan to win, and then play out a seven-trick hand. Hitting your Promise is rewarded. Missing it carries consequences that can affect future rounds.

At its core, the game uses familiar trick-taking rules, but nearly everything around that core is shaped by theme. Suits are not abstract colors. Each represents a different part of a criminal operation and behaves differently at the table. Authority functions as the dominant suit, but its power reflects control rather than brute strength, and it can be weakened, replaced, or removed entirely depending on the conditions of the job.

Every round begins with a revealed Job card that defines how that round plays. Some Jobs change how tricks resolve, others restrict leadership or promises, and some alter the usual power structure. Players also draft Specialists at the start of the game, which provide persistent abilities used on a fixed schedule across the seven rounds. These choices influence how players approach risk, timing, and long-term planning.

After the first round, hands are dealt from the shared discard pile, so the card pool evolves as the game progresses. Paying attention to what has already been played becomes increasingly important.

While the theme and mechanics are tightly integrated, the system itself is highly modular. Depending on how the game is set up, The Job can play as a relatively straightforward trick-taking game with wagering and bidding, or scale into a more deliberate, strategic experience with layered interactions, asymmetric powers, and shifting rules. Players can adjust complexity by choosing which suits, Jobs, and systems to include.

Players who fall behind gain access to Contingencies, single-use tools that can bend the rules at key moments without permanently resetting the game.

After seven jobs, the player with the most Reputation wins and takes control of the syndicate.

The Job is a trick-taking game at its foundation, but one designed to feel closer to a board game in structure and decision-making. The theme is not layered on top of the mechanics; it drives how the game changes from round to round.

—description from the designer

In this fast, head-to-head dungeon duel for 2 players, each of you takes on the role of a rival Gamemaster, competing to control a dungeon made up of three mysterious rooms. With hidden treasures and limited resources, every decision counts as you fight to outwit your opponent and claim the richest spoils.
Each player starts with a hand of 8 cards drawn from a compact deck of Characters, Monsters, Spells, and Magic Items. Characters are used to attack rooms, Monsters to defend them, and Magic can be added to either side to shift the balance. Every card has a unique effect, creating clever combos and tense tactical choices. Characters and Monsters can never fight side by side, but they can battle their own kind—Characters versus Characters, Monsters versus Monsters.
Once all cards and modifiers are resolved, the strongest side wins the room. The room is then flipped to reveal its treasure: it might be completely empty or packed with riches worth up to 6 points. After all three rooms have been claimed, the player with the most treasure points is crowned the ultimate Gamemaster.
With 12 different rooms (only 3 used each game) and a deck where only 16 of the 24 cards are dealt and no cards are drawn during play, every match feels fresh and unpredictable. Quick to learn and lightning fast to play, this is the perfect filler game, warm-up, or tactical showdown while another game is being set up—or when someone briefly leaves the table.

—description from the publisher

A mind game using drafting and simultaneous action selection. The cards you can play are pure numbers, no special abilities. The number on the card has two purposes: trigger instant win & count as score. A game lasts 4~6 rounds. If no player wins suddenly in any round, then the payer who has the most score claims victory at the end of the game.

boardgame: Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Legions Imperialis: The Devastation of Tallarn
description:
During the Horus Heresy the world of Tallarn, once a vibrant and verdant green world, was subject to utter devastation at the hands of the Iron Warriors. Virus Bombed to replace the atmosphere with a toxic fog of sickly brown rot, the world was soon engulfed in mass combat with entire detachments of tanks and titans striking out against each other. Soon the world would be littered with the corpses of blown out vehicles, as the virulent fog slowly choked out the world.

In Legions Imperialis: The Devastation of Tallarn, players are able to not only recreate the Tallarn campaign with some awesome new campaign rules and missions, but are also able to expand their Legions Imperialis armies with new formations and detachments for both the Legiones Astartes and the Solar Auxilia.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Adeptus Titanicus – The Horus Heresy (Games Workshop)
Game: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness

boardgame: Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Legions Imperialis: The Rise of the Dark Mechanicum
description:
During the Age of Darkness, the Mechanicum of Mars split in two, just as the Astartes did. Fueled by righteous fury on both sides, they tore into each other with the most devastating weaponry unleashed during those bleak times, holding nothing back.

Contained within this 128-page hardback book you will find a comprehensive history of the rift that tore the Mechanicum apart, detailing the corrupt technologies employed by the Dark Mechanicum, as they came to be known. You'll learn of the Doom of Magma City, the largest conflict of this internecine war, and the forces that took part. Also included are rules for both the loyal Mechanicum, and the traitorous Dark Mechanicum, plus their Formations, and Detachments.

This supplement book presents a full campaign system for linking Legions Imperialis battles together, including an Escalation Campaign that sees the forces ramping up to a final apocalyptic battle. You'll also find rules for three Formations of Legend, with their own unique special rules, plus army lists for Collegia Titanica and Questoris Familia forces.

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Adeptus Titanicus – The Horus Heresy (Games Workshop)
Game: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness

boardgame: Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Legions Imperialis: Liber Strategia
description:
Across the battlefields of the 31st Millennium, Humanity fights for its very survival. The Warmaster, Horus Lupercal, has turned against the Emperor, along with half of the armies of the Imperium. They march relentlessly for Terra, as vast cohorts of titanic war machines clash against each other in a brutal and bloody civil war. This is the Age of Darkness.

This essential gaming supplement for Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Legions Imperialis collates the army lists for Legiones Astartes, Solar Auxilia, Mechanicum Taghmata, Dark Mechanicum, Collegia Titanica, and Knight Households. Having the Detachments and Formations for each force in one place makes army building easy, and keeping track of your rules in the heat of battle straightforward. The book also contains Iconic Formations and Bonded Cybernetica Support Formations to add even more narrative flavour to your armies.

Inside this 240-page hardback book, you will find:

     Army Lists: Exhaustive Formations and Detachments for Legiones Astartes, Solar Auxilia, Mechanicum Taghmata, Dark Mechanicum, Collegia Titanica, and Knight Households.
     Miniatures Showcases: Painted Legions Imperialis miniatures to inspire you.
     Iconic Formations: Legendary Formations of warriors you can unleash on the battlefield.
     Bonded Cybernetica Support Formations: Cohorts of Legio Cybernetica constructs you can add to your force.


—description from the publisher
family: Game: Adeptus Titanicus – The Horus Heresy (Games Workshop)
Game: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness

boardgame: Warhammer: The Horus Heresy – Legions Imperialis: Journal Strategia – The Ruin of the Salamanders
description:
