On Isstvan V, the Salamanders had been broken. Their warriors had been slaughtered, their Primarch lost and presumed dead. Their wargear, abandoned during the battle, had been claimed as spoils of war by the Traitors and turned against the Loyalist cause. It was a defeat so utterly complete that most forces would never have recovered, becoming just one more laurel for Horus to fix upon his standard as he marched upon Terra. Yet, the sons of Vulkan did not succumb to such a fate.

This narrative gaming supplement for Legions Imperialis takes you to the black sands of Isstvan V, where you can play out your own version of the Dropsite Massacre. Can you lead the Salamanders to a breakout, or will you crush them with the Iron Warriors in a historical mission? There are also fresh ways to play the Legiones Astartes with additional Detachments and Formations, as well as Iconic Formations.

Inside this 48-page softcover book, you will find:

     Death on the Black Sands: A detailed account of the annihilation of the Salamanders Legion during the Dropsite Massacre on Isstvan V.
     Historical Mission: Refight the catastrophic slaughter at the Crucible, where the retreating Salamanders attempt to break out of a trap laid by the Iron Warriors.
     Additional Formations: Discover extra ways to field the Legiones Astartes, along with Iconic Formations for the Legiones Astartes, Iron Warriors, and Loyalist Solar Auxilia.
     Extra Detachments: Unleash Legion tanks, super-heavy tanks, Saturnine Terminators, Saturnine Dreadnoughts, Saturnine Commanders, and Araknae weapons platforms.


—description from the publisher

description:
In a world full of magical colors, the legendary dancing snakes live. These mystical creatures feed on the energy of colors and transform them into spectacular light shows.

As a talented snake charmer, your task is to create the most beautiful and harmonious color combinations. Players take turns playing color cards to build their snake, strategically using special cards to gain advantages or disrupt opponents' plans.

Schlangentanz is a family-friendly card game where players compete to create the longest and most valuable snake by cleverly combining color cards. The game includes 18 color cards in 6 different colors, plus special action cards that add tactical depth to the gameplay.

—description from the designer

This is a stand-alone Unlock Case out of the Game Adventures Box. It is themed around the Mysterium boardgame.

Enter the manor and experience a dark and surreal investigation in which you must resolve the murder with the Ghost's vision cards in a dreamlike and esoteric journey filled with mysteries...

Included in Unlock!: Game Adventures but also released by itself.

WHAT SONG IS THAT?! Guess the word from the lyrical nonsense card! Think you know your music? These 350 song titles and 150 artist names have been hilariously misheard—can you decipher the real ones? A fast-paced, laugh-out-loud misunderstood songs game that puts your knowledge (and your friends) to the ultimate test.

Who's in de'nile of murder?
Egypt 1924 and archaeologist June dagger has made the most incredible discovery of the century.  Friends and luminaries have gathered to witness her findings but before  anyone can say "Tutankhamun", June is found dead, killed with a poison dart shot from a blowpipe.  This was no curse, this was murder! host your own party to find out who snuffed Junes Candle.

Sky Garden: Imitation Egg expansion adds several different birds to the Sky Garden base game: the Northern Hawk-Cuckoo, the Chestnut-winged Cuckoo, the Great spotted Cuckoo and the Black-headed Duck.

Each bird will act as an advanced game piece that will score points in specific ways.

Sky Garden: Bonded Pair expansion adds several different birds to the Sky Garden base game: the Mandarin Duck (male and female), the Great White Pelican and the Rock Ptarmigan.

Each bird will act as an advanced game piece that will score points in specific ways.

Each creature will act as an advanced game piece that will score points in specific ways.

Dino Garden: Gentle Titans expansion adds several different herbivorous to the Dino Garden base game: the Brachiosaurus, the Fukuisaurus, the Ankylosaurus and the Therizinosaurus.

Each creature will act as an advanced game piece that will score points in specific ways.

New Year's Dreams is a set collection game where you collect auspicious first dreams of the New Year.

On your turn, you play a lucky card to acquire a variety of dream cards.

Collect "Fuji the 1st" "Hawk the 2nd", and "Eggplant the 3rd" for a high score!

Or combine "Fan the 4th", "Tobaccothe 5th", and "Zatō the 6th" with lucky cards.

Are you looking for good fortune?

description:
The game is in the same spirit as the super-popular 0-100 games, but with a completely new gameplay style. Here, every player gets a number line. You don’t need to know the exact answer; it’s enough to have a rough idea—or guess well. Will you dare to take another card, or play it safe? Players can always catch up, and it’s often not the one who reaches the finish line first that wins. We guarantee lots of laughs and gaming fun!

description:
Shuffle all the cards and place them face down,forming the draw deck within reach of all players.


    Player actions:


1-Appear: draw two cards from the deck and place them in front of you vertically. You can arrange them by colour or shape to plan your strategy.

2-Hide: Draw a card from another player and place it horizontally in front of you,"hidden". "Hidden" cards cannot be drawn by other players.


    Scoring:


After performing your actions, you can score if you have three cards of the same symbol or color. These cards are placed face down in your scoring pile, and can no longer be drawn. If you are lucky, you could score two trios in ya single turn. However, putting four matching cards together is of no use, as only trios score.


    Special card:


The magic rabbit is a very special card. When it appears, it "devours" all the hidden cards (the horizontal ones held by other players), and both the rabbit and those cards go directly to your pile of points


    End of the game 


When the third magic rabbit is drawn, that player ends his turn and has one last chance to score. Then, all players pass their remaining cards (the vertical ones) to the player on their left. The player who has the most cards in his pile of points wins..

description:
Welcome Adventurer, to Modern Problems: Require Fantasy Solutions. A Medieval Party Game, where you solve everyday problems with mischievous fantasy solutions.

Players have a hand of Fantasy Solutions like "Release a bag of bees and run away." A Judge draws a Problem card, and every other player selects a Fantasy Solution from their hand, to impress the judge. The Judge decides who wins, and the winner keeps the Problem card for score. The winner is whoever has the most Problem cards when the players decide to stop. An extra Solution card from a "non-player character" can be included to increase the chaos.

Players might also draw Spells which allow them to inflict wacky effects on other players, or Curses which is an automatic effect on you for the rest of the game.

Tavern Rules are included to play the game compatible with drinking something like harmless fruit juice.

boardgame: HOMM3B: Heroes of Might and Magic 3 Board Edition
description:
HOMM3B is an unpublished, author-designed tabletop adaptation inspired by the classic computer game Heroes of Might & Magic III.

It is an adventure strategy board game focused on map exploration, resource management, and tactical combat. Players reveal and connect map tiles to explore the world, collect resources, develop their castles, recruit armies, and engage in battles with neutral monsters or other players. Heroes gain experience, learn spells, and improve their abilities through skills and artifacts.

The game is scenario-driven and allows players to learn its systems gradually. It supports multiplayer scenarios with a flexible number of players, as well as solo play. The current version includes two playable factions (Knights and Dungeon), a separate Underground map connected to the surface world, and a large set of neutral creatures.

Solo play is supported through campaigns and AI reference tables used to generate automated opponent. The game uses miniatures for units of both factions and a destructible castle miniature for siege battles played on a separate tactical combat map.

This project is a long-term personal design. The goal is to recreate the core mechanics, balance, and visual feel of the original video game while adapting them to a tabletop format. The design has been developed over more than 10 years in the designer’s free time and remains in active development.

—description from the designer

description:
In the Orlické Mountains, a local biologist named Filip Trnka fell from a cliff. Although the police ruled the fall an accident, the testimonies and circumstances suggest otherwise. That is why the case file has ended up on your desk.

Inside, you will find interrogations, photographs, reports, and other materials designed to feel like a real investigation. Your task is to go through all the documents, connect the evidence, and find out who the killer is.

This is a hysterical and satirical look at the current state of politics. In this game you are a politician campaigning for the next election. Player take turns each day of campaign season in an effort to boost polling number, but usually fail just like real politicians. Players dig up dirt on opposing campaigns,raise money and answer questions during campaign season.

Towards the end of the game it election week when players wage political warfare on each other. The dirt dug up during the game is exposed during election week and money is spent on advertising, both positive and negative ads. The game ends on election day with a roll of the dice because like the real world you never know what the voters are going to do. The player with the highest vote count at the end of election day is the winner.

Players will work hard to be their best while actively working to undercut their opponents. Many current events are mentioned in the game providing constant opportunities for players to critique the faithful and honest politicians actually running the world.

—description from the publisher

description:
Ascendant Blades: Wuxia Skirmish is a fast, cinematic tabletop miniatures game inspired by classic wuxia tales of wandering heroes, legendary sects, and explosive martial duels. Designed for 1v1 competitive play or cooperative challenges, the system blends elegant movement, dynamic techniques, and tactical decision-making into every clash on the battlefield.

Players command elite martial warriors across rooftops, forest paths, shrines, bridges, and misty courtyards — striking, countering, and unleashing Qi Techniques in tense, dice-driven exchanges. Every model is a hero. Every duel tells a story.

Cinematic Martial Combat:
Ascendant Blades uses a unique Duel Exchange System, where both attacker and defender roll simultaneously. Blades meet, sparks fly, and outcomes depend on Rank, Qi, and tactical positioning.
No passive turns — every action carries weight.

Advanced Combat Options:
Skirmishes become richer with layered mechanics such as:
• Combined Assault — unite allies to deliver a coordinated strike
• Multi-Target Volley — sweep arrows or projectiles across multiple foes
• Qi Techniques — boost attacks, defend, reposition, or break enemy stance
• Terrain Mastery — leap walls, vault railings, fight on rooftops, or control bridges

These systems create a fast, reactive battlefield where choices matter and movement becomes art.

Hero-Driven, Wuxia-Focused Gameplay:
Each warrior carries a Rank, style, and unique battlefield role.
Ascending through the ranks unlocks stronger dice, more Qi, and new tactical options.
Matches feel like scenes from classic wuxia films — swirling robes, clashing steel, sudden reversals.

Easy to Learn, Deep to Master

Designed for newcomers and veterans alike:
• Quick setup
• Simple core mechanics
• High tactical ceiling
• Beautiful synergy between heroes, Qi, and terrain

Perfect for fast competitive duels or extended campaign arcs.

A Complete Wuxia Skirmish Experience
Whether you enjoy dynamic martial combat, thematic terrain, beautifully crafted miniatures, or tactical skirmish games, Ascendant Blades: Wuxia Skirmish delivers a fresh take on tabletop action rooted in the spirit of the jianghu.

Sharpen your blade. Gather your warriors.
The battlefield awaits.

—description from the publisher

description:
Forage: The Bee Game is a strategic, tactile tabletop game where you guide a beehive through the four seasons. This is a ground-up redesign and final published version of Forage, with revised rules, mechanics, components, and game modes.

Players collect nectar and pollen, breed different types of bees, grow and defend their hive, and adapt to seasonal changes while avoiding a wandering bear. Gameplay follows the natural rhythm of a real honeybee colony, from Spring growth and Summer honey flow through to Autumn scarcity and Winter survival.

Players collect the most valuable mix of flowers and bees. Each flower type has its own score, bees in your hive add extra points, and sets of three matching flowers are doubled.  When the twelfth round ends you score your flowers and bees to see which colony prepared best for Winter.

The game features multiple modes of play, including Friendly, Fun, Competitive, Solo, and an extended two-player challenge. Each mode uses the same core systems while adjusting interaction, difficulty, and long-term strategy. Solo and 2 Player modes offer their own multi-year survival challenges!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Monopoly: How to Train Your Dragon Edition
description:
Embark on a dragon-training adventure in Monopoly: DreamWorks How to Train Your Dragon Edition, a thematic spin on the classic Monopoly game inspired by DreamWorks Animation’s beloved franchise.

Set in the vibrant Viking village of Berk, players take on the roles of iconic characters—Hiccup, Astrid, Tuffnut, or Ruffnut—each with a unique ability that adds depth to the gameplay. Your goal is to become the greatest dragon rider by training every class of dragon and collecting their shields!

Instead of buying properties, players travel the board to learn how to train iconic dragons, paying in fish (the game’s currency) to take lessons. Dragons replace traditional Monopoly properties, with each space representing a different species from the How to Train Your Dragon setting. Land on a dragon, pay for lessons, and gain expertise to claim their loyalty. The game introduces a training die, which players roll for a chance to train a dragon and earn one of eight dragon shields. If you collect all eight, you win! Along the way, the players face Viking challenges that test your skills and offer rewards or setbacks.

The board is adorned with artwork, characters, and locations from the franchise, immersing players in the world of Berk. Custom-sculpted tokens and matching character mats enhance the thematic experience, while familiar Monopoly mechanics—trading, auctions, and rent collection—are reimagined with a dragon-training twist. Strategic decisions, like leveraging character abilities or timing training rolls, add light tactical layers to the roll-and-move framework.
family: Movies: How to Train Your Dragon

description:
Shards is a fast-paced strategy board game with high-end art and 4 ways to win. Navigation, Economy, Corruption, and Lone Survivor. The idea is to corrupt the other before you yourself get corrupted. It's a game tailored to the furry and pony community.

How players take action on their turn

     Draw encounter card from either fate/ conflict 
     Sell/ buy ships - Initiate duels if applicable 
     Roll d12 to move
     Review any other activities before ending turn
     If on a " blank "space - move to the next colored space


—description from the designer

Every Captain has a few tricks up their sleeve, and now you can as well with the Captain’s Cache Expansion 1!

This expansion adds 12 new playing cards to the game (3 Tactical, 3 Comms, 3 Engineering System Cards, and 3 Crew Cards) as well as a reference card.

—description from the publisher

After years of fighting, a shortage of supplies has led to scouts searching long forgotten battlefields for useable salvage. A barely functioning Capital ship (which can be remotely piloted), the ability to overload Tactical System cards, and old war bots which allow you to save cards until your next turn, are just some of the surprises that await!

Also adds new concepts around scrapped cards. Not only can more cards be scrapped, but you can now leverage scrapped cards as an auxiliary market of sorts.

This expansion adds 12 new playing cards to the game (2 Tactical, 2 Comms, 2 Engineering System Cards, and 6 Crew Cards) as well as a reference card.

—description from the publisher

Wild World is a dynamic 1–4 player strategy game that blends worker placement, multi-use cards, engine-building, and tactical monster combat into one seamless, combo-driven experience. Set across five elemental realms — Forest, Ocean, Fire, Frost, and Magic — players gather fantastical creatures, explore vivid regions, evolve their champions, and brew powerful potions as they race to score the most points over three rounds.

What makes Wild World stand out is the freedom and flexibility baked into every turn. You may perform a number of different free actions — placing cards to strengthen your engine, traveling through realms for rewards and prestige, or discarding for resources — allowing each turn to unfold as a meaningful puzzle. Every card has multiple uses, and clever sequencing often triggers reward chains that lead to explosive, satisfying combos.

At the heart of the game are the mighty monsters guarding the world. Defeating them will give you permanent abilities, immediate bonuses, scoring conditions, new workers, and prestige, but reaching them requires smart planning: evolving creatures, upgrading your potion-brewing, unlocking stronger foes, and assembling the perfect moment to strike.

With multiple strategic paths, escalating tension, and thrilling bursts of synergy, Wild World offers a richly re-playable adventure in which every decision matters — and every turn can become something special.

—description from the publisher
The Turbulent - Starting Advantage: At the Beginning of the game, draw 2 event cards. One will apply only to you, and the other will affect everyone. You decide which is which.

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Ultimate Spider-Man, Prowler, & Spider-Man 2099
description:
Aaron Davis never shook his criminal past and donned a mask to become The Prowler. While his allegiances are unclear, his combat prowess in close quarters is certain. Miguel O’Hara, the time-traveling Spider-Man 2099, leverages superior tech and abilities to dominate anyone that crosses his path. With years of crime fighting in his webs, Miles Morales swings through New York as Ultimate Spider-Man, becoming a powerful leader in the Web Warriors.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Silk, Spider-Ham, & Spider-Man Noir
description:
This Character Pack adds 3 new Spider-Verse Affiliation characters to Marvel: Crisis Protocol. Silk uses her Spider-powers and heightened agility to strike fast and escape quickly. She is an excellent hit-and-run fighter. Spider-Ham uses his cartoon-like powers to take down foes, bringing hilarity and justice to the battlefield. Stalking the criminal underworld of 1930s New York, Spider-Man Noir packs a pistol that he uses for ranged attacks but has the durability of a street brawler.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel: Crisis Protocol – Spider-Foes Starter Set
description:
A full roster of some of Spider-Man’s most iconic villains are set to send a jolt to the Spidey-Senses as they take to the streets of New York with one goal in mind: swat down the spider! This Starter Set contains all the miniatures, Character Stat Cards, and Team Tactic Cards for the following characters: Green Goblin, Kraven the Hunter, the Lizard, Mysterio, Shocker, Sandman, Carnage, Rhino, Vulture, and Electro. Also includes Crisis Cards and all the tokens, measuring tools, and dice needed to play. Players interested in getting into Marvel: Crisis Protocol will find that Starter Sets are the perfect starting place for a single player.

—description from the designer

description:
Eggsactly Six is a small dice game published by Professor Puzzle Ltd.

Players roll dice and aim to achieve results that total exactly six, following the specific rules of the game. The game is designed as a quick, accessible dice-based experience, suitable for casual and family play.

This is a commercially published physical game purchased in retail.

This is a solo game of determination against increasingly demanding odds, building on the mechanics of Everest 1924.

You will lead the 1745 Jacobite uprising from the attempted march upon London through to the desperate retreat to Skye.

All you need to play is a copy of the game sheet, pen/pencil and six D6. Game time will be around 15 minutes.

—description from the publisher

Party & Co: Rush! Flip & Fun is a fast-paced party game where teams compete in real time to complete fun and chaotic challenges. Players must flip cards, perform actions, describe, draw, or act out clues as quickly as possible, creating a high-energy and humorous experience. Designed for groups, the game focuses on speed, teamwork, and laughter rather than complex rules.

Pressure is a family friendly, push your luck game with bidding, bluffing, and betting. The game features simultaneous play and packs big experiences into a small box. Pressure is fun, wild, and filled with suspense - it'll  take your breath away!

In Pressure, you’ll strategically add cards to a shared pool with some public and some hidden information. Use that information to bid for the honor of becoming the Dive Master. Before the dive, everyone, including the Dive Master, bets on whether the Dive Master will succeed in their reckless dive.

Then Dive Master flips over cards one at a time, trying to reach the number of cards equal to their bid. BUT, beware, if you flip three leaks, you fail. Even when your rival dives, you’re invested in the outcome, because you bet on it!

Can you stand the pressure?!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Landmarks: Promo Map Cards – The Dice Tower 2025
description:
Expansion includes the following cards:

1x Red Card - Sandwich, Actor, Tornado
1x Yellow Card - Music, Koala, Animal
1x Green Card - Delay, Gasoline, State

Four new clans and wonders to add into Spirited: Incan, Sioux, Inuit, and Mayan.

Contains 40 Clan Cards, 4 Allegiance Cards, 4 Wonders, 4 Clan Discs, and 4 Card Dividers

This economy's not big enough for the two of us. In Last Man Spending you compete with your rivals to the very bitter end. Pick up the Portfolio board, pass it around, and lay down the law. Invest in the market like there's no tomorrow, because with the DIABOLICAL Gall and Policy cards at your disposal, there might not be one. Bluff out your opponents, sabotage their progress, and CRASH the Reserve to leave them gasping for cash. At the end of the (business) day, there's going to be just one left. The Last Man Spending.

Last Man Spending encapsulates the thrilling rollercoaster of stock market trading. Using the Millionaire Marker, you and others can invest in over 21 different Shares and 6 different Businesses. Then gain IMMENSE gains from cashing out, but be careful! All money comes from the Reserve, and if that goes dry, then your stocks are no better than the paper they're printed on. Players can utilize the BALLOT BOX to push through Bluff, Gall, and Policy cards to rig the market, and the game, in their favor. Fortune favors the rich, but the rich don't always end up so fortunate in Last Man Spending with its comeback mechanics. Rags to riches and back again.

—description from the designer
Using 5 cubes with an image on each side, make the set of images show on the card, faster than anyone else. Yell match when you finish.

Rainforest Rumble -   It's an action-packed, strategic board game.  You need to start at your home base, roll the dice, and make your way around the board.  You will encounter action cards, portals, and other ways to advance or stop your opponents from reaching the end.  Whoever reaches the end first on an exact roll wins the game and is crowned the King of the Rainforest.

Made by a 9-year-old and his dad.

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #47 – Heinkel He 111P Variants
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #47 features one page of aircraft briefings and the following three updated to third edition standard Heinkel He 111P data cards (sibling of the He 111H but different):

• Heinkel He 111P-2 (First of the conical shaped all glass nose He 111s -also P-1)

• Heinkel He 111P-4 (modified for survival, more guns, more armor, more fuel)

• Heinkel He 111P-6 (final variant with improved engines, but only in limited nos.)
family: Game: Fighting Wings Aircraft Data Card (ADCs) downloadable expansions

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #48 – Ilyushin DB-3 Variants
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #48 features one page of aircraft briefings and the following three updated to third edition standard Ilyushin DB-3 long range Bomber data cards:

• Ilyushin DB-3S (First of the series, predecessor to the famous IL-4 series)

• Ilyushin DB-3B (Main production variant, some 1,500 delivered in 3B and 3M models)

• Ilyushin DB-3T (First long range torpedo bomber for Soviet Naval Aviation)
family: Game: Fighting Wings Aircraft Data Card (ADCs) downloadable expansions

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #49 – Bloch 151 & 152 Variants
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #49 features one page of aircraft briefings and the following three updated to third edition standard Bloch MB-151 and MB-152 fighter data cards:

• Bloch MB-151 (First of the series, seeing action in Phony War)

• Bloch MB-152 early (Initial production variant of 152 series with N-25 engine)

• Bloch MB-152 late (final production variant of 152, N-49 engine, better prop.)
family: Game: Fighting Wings Aircraft Data Card (ADCs) downloadable expansions

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #50 – Bloch 155, 157, 700 Fighters
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #50 features one page of aircraft briefings and the following three updated to third edition standard Bloch MB-155, MB-157 and MB-700 fighter data cards:

• Bloch MB-155 (What the MB-152 with an N-49 engine should have been)

• Bloch MB-157 (Amazing 400 mph high altitude French fighter in 1942? It flew)

• Bloch MB-700 (Marcel Bloch's light weight wooden wonder, it flew in 1940)
family: Game: Fighting Wings Aircraft Data Card (ADCs) downloadable expansions

description: Build good routes or/and buy Trains with your cards. Run your Trains and score points. After 6 rounds the player with more points win the Game.

Frontline Heroes is a solo/PvP skirmish wargame set in World War II. 
In this game, our soldiers will be members of the Allied forces battling against the Nazi army on the European front.

Players have the option to play solo, controlling one side and facing an AI system that manages the enemy, or to engage in competitive mode against a human opponent, with each player controlling one side of the conflict. Additionally, the game can be played cooperatively with multiple players acting on the same side, fighting together against the enemy controlled by the AI.

As a skirmish game with only a few miniatures (from 4 to 14 models per side), Frontline Heroes offers missions encompassing stealth assaults, direct confrontations, sabotage, and reconnaissance. Players may also engage in small segments of larger battles, determined by the outcomes of their characters' actions.

Players take on the role of a small group of soldiers fighting for the Allied cause in Europe against the Nazi army. Choose your soldiers, issue the right commands, and accomplish the objectives of each mission. Some missions may be completed without firing a single shot, while others may involve intense life-and-death combat.

Now is your chance to take part in the events that changed the course of world history forever. Stand firm in the sands of the beaches, push through the dense bocage, defend each of the rooms in the ruins where you hide, climb the heavily fortified hills, seize enemy strongholds, and eliminate your enemies one by one before it's too late. The future of the entire world depends on you.

SOLO vs COMPETITIVE MODE

Frontline Heroes primarily focuses on solitaire mode, although it also offers cooperative and competitive mode options.

Solitaire mode involves a single player battling against an Artificial Intelligence (AI) that controls the enemy forces and their decisions. This mode emphasizes narrative and storytelling. The AI controls wich enemy models activate, when and the actions they make. The player doesn´t have to take any decision in this process.

Additionally, cooperative mode allows two players to team up on the same side, facing an AI-controlled enemy.

Competitive mode follows the standard format of miniatures wargames, where two or more players form opposing sides and engage in direct conflict, aiming to defeat the enemy and eliminate as many opposing miniatures as possible. It is a purely competitive mode. In this game, you won't find exhaustive army lists to form different factions. Instead, the system for creating the opposing factions is completely original and fun.

NORMAL vs STEALTH MISSIONS
Frontline Heroes features 21 missions, each specifying the miniatures that make up each faction, the layout of the game table, the background information, possible events, and everything needed to play a game. Furthermore, each mission offers high replayability.

In the game we have normal missions, as in any type of miniature wargame, where two forces face off until one prevails over the other through sheer force; and we also have stealth missions, in which we must infiltrate a small group of soldiers into enemy territory to accomplish certain vital objectives, such as sabotaging supplies, capturing bridges or blowing up installations.

Frontline Heroes features a comprehensive rules system and 21 missions for solo or multiplayer play, presented in a nearly 200-page book.

—description from the publisher

Frontline Heroes: Modern Warfare is a miniature-agnostic asymmetric skirmish wargame set in the modern day.
 Players have the option to play solo, controlling one side and facing an AI system that manages the enemy, or to engage in competitive mode against a human opponent, with each player controlling one side of the conflict. Additionally, the game can be played cooperatively fighting together against the enemy controlled by the AI.

As a skirmish game, Frontline Heroes focuses on close, man-to-man confrontations, where a small asymmetric number of miniatures (ranging from 4 to 15 per side) are used on a tabletop with terrain pieces to represent a skirmish or a small part of a larger battle.

Modern warfare encompasses any type of small-scale confrontation or skirmish that has taken place from 1990 to the present day: Marines facing insurgents in the Iraq War, robbers battling the police during a city heist, military police clashing with cartel members in South America, SWAT teams raiding a farm controlled by an armed sect... your imagination is the limit.

SOLO vs COMPETITIVE MODE
Frontline Heroes primarily focuses on solitaire mode, although it also offers cooperative and competitive mode options.

Solitaire mode involves a single player battling against an Artificial Intelligence (AI) that controls the enemy forces and their decisions. This mode emphasizes narrative and storytelling. The AI controls wich enemy models activate, when and the actions they make. The player doesn´t have to take any decision in this process.

Additionally, cooperative mode allows two players to team up on the same side, facing an AI-controlled enemy.

Competitive mode follows the standard format of miniatures wargames, where two or more players form opposing sides and engage in direct conflict, aiming to defeat the enemy and eliminate as many opposing miniatures as possible. It is a purely competitive mode. In this game, you won't find exhaustive army lists to form different factions. Instead, the system for creating the opposing factions is completely original and fun.

NORMAL vs STEALTH MISSIONS
Frontline Heroes features a mission creation system, in which through multiple tables it will create a completely new and different scenario each time; Each mission specifying the miniatures that has each faction, equipment, possible deployments and everything needed to play a game. This system offers high replayability.

In the game we have normal missions, as in any type of miniature wargame, where two forces face off until one prevails over the other through sheer force; and we also have stealth missions, in which we must infiltrate a small group of soldiers into enemy territory to accomplish certain vital objectives, such as sabotaging supplies, capturing VIPs or blowing up installations.

Frontline Heroes features a comprehensive rules system for solo or multiplayer play, presented in a nearly 100-page book.

—description from the publisher

A trivia game for any size group in which players guess which of a pair of historical events happened first. After each pair, players who answered correctly can choose to cash in their earned points or continue guessing and risk all the points they've earned in the round. Players take turns hosting the rounds, drawing a new event card for the rest of the players to compare to the previous event card and revealing the answers.

Negative Space is a tabletop miniatures wargame with low model count, a skirmish game, one might say.

It plays on cosmic horror and retro sci-fi themes of the 70's and 80's.
Players control a crew of Nekronauts, unfortunate spacefarers who are stuck in a black hole, fighting for their survival.

The game uses a D10 system to resolve combat, eldritch witchcraft, and the technological know-how of your crew.

Environmental danger and monsters also threaten you, not just your opponent. Scouting and interactive terrain are also part of the system. Doors, Computers, Cloning Vats, Lootable Crates and many more can make the difference between survival, victory, or total annihilation.

A game of Negative Space is structured and paced as the classic Alien movies. 
Tension, scouting and careful positioning describes the first rounds. Then all hell lets loose, and the bodies pile high as firefights between crews erupt, and are suddenly interrupted by hostile lifeforms. There is a panic system to further emphasize the crew's descent into despair, as death crawls ever closer.

Scavenge. Scout. Fight. Murder. Despair. Die.

No escape from the black hole, you will die here, in the Negative Space.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Merry Men: The Robin Hood Miniatures Game
description:
With only a couple sprues worth of minis, you can play out the adventures of Robin Hood and his Merry Men vs The Sheriff of Nottingham and his soldiers.

Merry Men uses a simple d6 system - roll a target number to hit, target rolls likewise to defend.
Also; Alternating activation (no waiting around for phases to be done).

Game book includes a list of where to get Robin Hood minis (as well as sources of inspiration for your Robin Hood adventures).

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

description:
Jane Siavash is a hidden-role, deduction and reasoning game for 5 to 15 players. The game depicts a battle between Iran and Turan as players work to rescue Siavash. It is inspired by the epic story of Ferdowsi's Shahnameh.
Story: the jealousy of Garshasp, Afrasiyab’s brother, who cunningly manipulates Afrasiyab into sending an army to kill Siavash. Farangis, Siavash’s wife and Afrasiyab’s daughter, learns of this plot and sends a messenger to Iran. Upon receiving the news, King Kay Kavus of Iran, Siavash’s father, mobilizes a force of Iranian heroes to march against Afrasiyab.

As the Iranian army approaches the borders of Turan, Afrasiyab, knowing he cannot defeat the Iranians in open battle, orders his sorcerer Bazur to stop their advance. Bazur conjures a fierce snowstorm and blizzard, leaving the Iranian forces exhausted, disoriented, and barely able to recognize friend from foe. Taking advantage of the chaos, Afrasiyab attempts to secretly infiltrate the Iranian army with a few of his warriors, hoping to destroy it from within.

Siavash, aware of the Iranian army’s plight and Afrasiyab’s plot, must secretly enter the army to help them. He needs to warn the Iranians of the Turanian infiltrators without being discovered, while the Iranians, trapped in snow and cold, struggle to make clear decisions. Players must navigate deception, hidden roles, and uncertain alliances to influence the outcome of this epic conflict and determine whether Iran can successfully rescue Siavash.

—description from the publisher

Fafaru! The Ma’a Tahiti game with a Polynesian twist!

In this « poisson cru » (raw fish with coconut-milk), everyone’s smiling… except the fermented ingredients hiding in the ma’a…

Keep your role secret, play cards face down, read the reactions, accuse without mercy, or dare to eat the ma’a to win the game!
Good luck, and… « Tama’a maitai »!

—description from the designer

This game is a Japanese card game in which 2 or 3 players place traditional playing cards on the table on a circular pattern.

Then, one by one the players pick up the cards within the circle and place them in the center of the circle.

If a player gets the same suit of card as the card faced up in the center, they have to pick up all the cards in the center. This keeps going until there are no cards on the table.

Once the game ends, the player with the least amount of cards is the winner.

Pathbind: Setch is a head-to-head strategy card game inspired by the core principles of Chess, redesigned into a compact, boardless format.

Players command opposing armies using cards instead of a physical board. Positioning, timing, and long-term planning remain central, but setup time is eliminated and play focuses on decisive tactical choices rather than memorised openings.

Each match unfolds through controlled movement, calculated exchanges, and the careful management of limited options. While the rules are straightforward, mastery emerges through repeated play, adaptation to different army compositions, and anticipation of the opponent’s intent.

Setch is designed to reward foresight and positional understanding while remaining accessible to players familiar with classic abstract strategy games.

—description from the designer

Pathbind: Arena is a competitive card game built around direct combat, prediction, and commitment. Each player controls a distinct fighter with unique abilities, facing opponents in a controlled, high-tension arena environment.

Players secretly select actions and reveal them simultaneously, creating moments of anticipation and decisive clashes. Success depends on reading the opponent, managing limited options, and choosing when to commit aggressively or hold back.

There are no dice and no hidden information beyond player intent. Outcomes are determined by decisions, timing, and the interaction between fighter abilities rather than randomness.

Arena is designed for short, intense matches that reward repeated play, learning opponent tendencies, and mastering the strengths and weaknesses of different fighters.

—description from the designer

Pathbind: Throne is a strategy card game set in a dark fantasy world where every decision leaves a lasting mark. Players influence the rise and fall of factions, alter the state of the realm, and pursue victory through both direct competition and shifting alliances.

Gameplay centres on card-driven actions that modify the shared game state. Choices made earlier affect future possibilities, forcing players to weigh short-term gains against long-term consequences. Control is rarely absolute, and the balance of power can shift as players respond to emerging threats and opportunities.

Throne supports both competitive and cooperative play, allowing players to pursue individual victory or work together against shared pressures. The game emphasises foresight, adaptation, and the ability to read the evolving landscape rather than tactical optimisation alone.

Designed for repeated play, Pathbind: Throne rewards players who learn how systems interact, anticipate the impact of their actions, and navigate an environment shaped as much by other players as by the cards themselves.

—description from the designer

Pathbind: Grid is an F1-inspired competitive racing game focused on strategic decision-making rather than simulation. Players manage speed, positioning, and risk as they navigate a dynamic track shaped by changing conditions.

Each turn presents choices between pushing for faster times or preserving control as the race evolves. Track sections and environmental factors influence outcomes, forcing players to adapt their approach rather than rely on a single optimal strategy.

There are no dice-driven swings. Success comes from managing limited options, reading the state of the race, and deciding when to take calculated risks. Grid is designed for tense, repeatable races where small advantages accumulate over time.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Epic Wars Confrontation: Paladin vs Necromancer
description:
Epic Wars Confrontation, or EWC for short, is a card & dice game using pre-constructed decks. Each box contains everything you need for PvP (Player vs. Player); multiple boxes can be combined, allowing massive games with as many as six players. The last hero standing is the winner.

Epic Wars Confrontation is a fast, fun, and easy-to-learn game. The basic gameplay can be learned in five to ten minutes. Mastering each hero will take multiple games. Epic Wars Confrontation’s card and dice mechanic guarantees each game played is different.
A standalone expansion for Bezzerwizzer with questions about great cities around the world.

This deliciously educational activity lets children explore early word creation and letter learning in a fun and playful way. Add the letter dice to the soup can shaker, pour them out and see what letters you have to make a word. Save letters, or toss them back in the shaker to try again. If you can spell a word, the word card is yours. The first player to collect five cards wins! It's yummy learning fun with Alphabet Soup!

This Mastermind Expansion Pack includes 5 Mastermind cases and 4 Red Herring clues to add extra variety to your 5-Minute Mystery game.

Red Herrings: Shuffle these in with your other clues during setup to increase your challenge. These are useless 'clues' that do nothing to narrow down your suspects!

Mastermind Cases: These extra-difficult scenarios will really put your skills to the test. Are you ready to solve your toughest cases yet?

boardgame: The Great Sherlock Deck: A Set of Holmes-Inspired Card Games
description:
The game is afoot! With this six-in-one set of 60 cards plus rules book, two to six players can become the detectives as they unravel the iconic mysteries of Sherlock Holmes.

The Great Sherlock Deck turns the works of Arthur Conan Doyle into a unique 60-card deck based around thirteen well-known Sherlock Holmes mysteries. It’s family fun for ages twelve and up so anyone can play. Each of the six card games included in the rules book plays like a classic card game, so it’s easy to get started and join in on the fun.

Play Mysteries, a variant of Go Fish, as you try to collect all the clues for as many of the thirteen mysteries as you can.
    Declare It’s Elemental in a variation on crazy eights where you work to get rid of all the cards—and clues—in your hand.
    Try the Sherlock Snap, where you play the classic game of snap by matching the same type of clue from different mysteries until someone else is left holding all the cards at the end.
    Or play one of your favorite card games—the cards can be used just like a regular deck!

Everything you need to play is included in the box, and with six games to choose from there’s always something new to investigate. Join the Baker Street irregulars and get on the case!

—description from the publisher

Steel thy blade, prime your plasma rifle. War calls and you must answer!

WARSURGE is a universal miniature wargame that lets you fully customise and field miniatures from your ENTIRE collection! 
Prepare for warfare in a tabletop game where you can design and fight with any army you want.

Prepare for warfare in a tabletop game where you design and fight with any army you want. 
You can use all of the miniatures in your collection to play. With Missions, Narratives, Campaigns and several Playstyles, WARSURGE has plenty of content for both beginners and seasoned veterans alike. 
The WARSURGE App allows you to create profiles for your Units and Weapons, customize your armies. 
Alternatively, you can use Units that have been created by other players or make use of the sample Units and Weapons created by the WARSURGE Team.

The Free Rules teach you how to play a game of WARSURGE.

Game Turn: consists of four Phases

     Deploy, 
     Move, 
     Attack, 
     Dash


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: A wooden balance game.  Based on a balancing act in and of itself--refusing to make two trips to get all of the groceries inside.  What do you mean nit's impossible to carry twenty bags at one time?  Sounds like a quitter to us.

In a quiet corner of the forest, rabbits gather fruit before night falls. But beware! A clever badger is lurking to steal their food. Strawberries is a strategy game: fill your basket before anyone else!

Players take turns rolling the die and moving according to the die face. Depending on the field the end up, at, they either gather fruit or loose fruit to the hungry badger. One die face shown a moon icon, causing the clock to move further

Players try to be the first to fill their basket with the correct fruits. The first to do so wins. But if the clock reaches midnight before any player manages to win, all players loose.

Moron ou Génie 2 is a fast-paced quiz game about confidence, knowledge, and risk. Players are repeatedly confronted with multiple-choice questions that seem simple at first glance but often hide clever traps. The theme plays on a constant tension: are you truly knowledgeable, or just overly confident? The game humor comes from watching players boldly commit to answers—and occasionally fail spectacularly.

On each turn, a question is revealed with several possible answers. Players choose the answer they believe is correct and decide how much they are willing to commit to that choice. The more confident a player is, the more they can risk. Correct answers reward boldness, while incorrect ones are sharply penalized, making every decision a balance between certainty and caution. The game flows quickly, with frequent turns and immediate consequences that keep all players engaged.

Players accumulate (and sometimes lose) points through correct and incorrect answers over the course of the game. Strategic risk-taking is as important as raw knowledge. When the game ends, the player with the highest total score wins, proving they were not only smart—but also wise enough to know when to trust their instincts.

boardgame: Idiotic & Useless: Late WWI, Plan 1919, Land Ironclads, and the Martian Invasion!
description:
Idiotic & Useless allows players to fight WWI battles taking place outside of static trench lines as if the employment of armor has allowed one side to break through into "the green fields beyond," as if the Germans had manufactured large quantities of tanks of their own, as if the experimental tanks of the era were employed in quantity, or as if H.G. Wells’ fictitiously large "Land Ironclads" had been built. 
Rules are also included for the appearance of H.G. Wells’ Martian Tripods in WWI battles or for the depiction of a Martian invasion.

This game is designed to be played on a hex mat. 
The scale is such that a battalion frontage of 6 hexes corresponds to roughly 500 m. Thus 1 hex is equal to around 80 m or 90 yards. 
Figures should be mounted on square or rectangular bases so as to fit within the hexes being used. For example, 6mm figures on 1-1.25 inch or 30mm square bases work well with 1.5 inch hexes. Bases of other shapes or cardstock counters may be used, so long as the front of the base is clearly apparent.

A separate free document follows through one game turn, step-by-step, to illustrate how the game is played. The last two pages show the tables and the sequence of play.

Sequence of play:
In phases 1-5 each side rolls a d10 and the low roller moves or fires first during that phase (re-roll ties). 
In phases 2, 3 and 4 the roll also determines what each side
will do. 
In phase 6, each step is resolved simultaneously for both sides.

—description from the publisher
family: Books: The War of the Worlds

description:
A sunny day in Korkeasaari Zoo is ready to begin! Go on a zoo trip with your friends and take photos of the animals.

Play card, roll the die, and learn about conservation. The first player to collect 12 photos wins the game!

boardgame: Las Vegas Cold Case File: The Case of Angelo Angenelli
description:
In 2018, a real estate broker, Angelo Angenelli, is found dead in a hotel and casino penthouse. 
Five suspects are brought in for questioning.
But the case grows cold.
Until you, the Cold Case Detective, take another look at the evidence, interviews and files the police collected in 2018.
Can you solve the case and close it once and for all?

Squiggles is a game where players place tiles with matching lines on them on a communal grid. Tiles have different colors of lines on them and must match on the edges of tiles. The first player to place all of their tiles is the winner.

Set comes with 84 specially designed playing cards and easy to follow instructions. No numbers or letters.

This is a simple, fast-play, and free!, Ben-Hur-style chariot race game. 
The board included with these rules can be printed on 5 sheets of standard paper and assembled. 
The included board, with a sheet of tables placed at one end, will fit in an 11 x 35 frame.

The board from the 1980 Avalon Hill game Circus Maximus can also be used. Digital versions of that board are available for free online that can be printed by a copy shop onto a single sheet of much larger sizes. 
The larger boards can be used with miniatures or counters.

Paper counters are included with these rules that can be mounted.

The Finish Line:
The finish line is the end of the straightaway opposite the starting straightaway. The race is three laps long,
but without the last curve of the third lap. (Note: This is where the finish line was historically, with the
Emperor or most distinguished spectators sitting in a temple-like structure located at the finish line.)

Turn Sequence:
At the beginning of each turn the tokens representing the chariots are returned to the cup and each player
chooses the number of movement dice (d8’s) they will roll for their chariot during the current turn. The
maximum number of d8’s is either 5 or the number of Endurance points the chariot has remaining, whichever is less.
Players should consult the Movement Dice table for assistance in choosing their number of movement dice.
(Note: About 2/3 of the movement die roll outcomes will be within the “Most Likely” range of the Movement Dice table for the number of d8’s chosen. That means that around 1/6 will be above this range and 1/6 will be below.)
A token is drawn from the cup and the corresponding chariot is moved. The token is not put back into the cup until the beginning of the next turn. After the first Chariot has made its move and all attacks or other outcomes have been resolved, a second token is drawn for the next chariot. This is repeated until all the tokens have been drawn and each chariot has made its move and resolved its outcomes.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Boys From Isandlwana: The Anglo-Zulu War of 1879
description:
The Boys From iSandlwana is a simple fast-paced set of rules for gaming the Zulu war with miniatures.

The rules are only about 6 pages long. 
All the tables and charts fit on one side of one sheet of paper, though a large print double-sided version is also included. 
These rules were originally published in MWAN magazine, and this is an expanded and revised edition. 
Included are scenarios for Rorke’s Drift and Khambula, and two pages of design notes. 
While intended for figures based individually on US penny-sized bases, virtually any basing scheme will work, such as having multiple figures per base.

SEQUENCE of PLAY

     Zulu Movement
     British Missile Fire & Zulu Morale
     Zulu Melee & British Morale
     British Movement
     British Hospital
     Zulu Missile Fire
     British Melee


—description from the publisher

A society wedding at one of London’s most exclusive boutique hotels was set to be the event of the year, until the bride and groom were found dead in their private suite just hours before the ceremony. No forced entry. No struggle. Just an open bottle of wine and a trail of secrets.

Emilia Cavendish and Nathaniel Ashcroft had the world at their feet, but beneath the glamour and celebration lurked jealousy, betrayal, and a love triangle ready to implode. Four guests — each with a strained past and a hidden motive — were close enough to want the couple gone.

As a consulting detective, you will examine crime scene evidence to uncover who slipped poison into a night of romance and luxury.

Was it revenge, heartbreak, or something far more calculated?

Difficulty Rating - Medium (★★★)

—description from the publisher

boardgame: FLOE: Huleed – The Dice Tower Promo Card
description:
Huleed (Pink Monster Card)

Available from the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign (promo pack B).

Monster Huleed:

     Monster Health 8
     Base Damage 4 
     Gain one purple crystal
     Each familiar reduces damage by 1 instead

Flip Dungeon is a dungeon crawling(?) board game played with 9 cards, dice, and a few tokens. When you solve the contents of the card, you flip it over to the back. There are various changes on the back, so the dungeon is different every time.

The battle between warriors and wizards across the castle walls has begun. Roll the dice and place them according to the dice and conditions on the castle walls to win!

Game Preparation


     Place the Battle Card in the center of the table.
     Throw all the white dice and place one in each empty space on the Wall (the center area of ​​the Battle Card) alternately.
     Each player takes all 5 dice of their color and places them in front of them.
     Throw all the dice of their color and the player with the smaller sum becomes the first player.


Game Progress
On their turn, the player chooses one of the two actions and performs it.

1. Place dice in the space that meets the condition
If the condition between the white dice and the dice placement space of your color is satisfied, place one of your dice in the corresponding placement space and process the effect of the corresponding white dice.

2. Roll all unplaced dice
Roll all unplaced dice.

Game End
You win when all 5 of your dice are placed on the card.

Abstract alignment game for 2 players.

Each players has 6 balls, and the aim of the game is to line up 4 balls of your color on a straight line or a curve.

At his turn the player places one of their balls into play, move a ball, or capture a ball.

Variant: capture a ball by framing.

•••

Jeu abstrait d alignement pour 2 joueurs.

Chaque joueur possède 6 boules, et le but du jeu est d aligner 4 boules de sa couleur sur une ligne droite ou une courbe.

A son tour le joueur place une de ses boules en jeu, déplace une boule, ou effectue une prise par saute-mouton.

Variante possible: effectuer la prise par encadrement.

boardgame: Hidden Games Tatort: Schatten der Zeit
description:
In this Hidden Game, you collaborate by searching for clues and solving puzzles. It offers a narrative-driven experience where players unite to accomplish a shared objective within a designated time limit.

—user summary

Seit 84 Jahren ruhte das Wrack der Hestia in der Tiefe. Doch als bei einer Tauchfahrt eine alte Taschenuhr geborgen wird, benötigt eine Professorin eure Hilfe bei der Lösung eines Rätsels. Verschollene Wahrheiten steigen auf und werfen lange Schatten bis in die Gegenwart. Warum sank die Hestia wirklich? Folgt den Spuren, deckt jedes Geheimnis auf – und prüft genau, wem ihr vertrauen könnt!

—description from the publisher

Introduction In Talim: The Game of Mallakhamb, players take on the role of a Guru (master teacher) managing a traditional Indian gymnasium. Your goal is to restore the glory of your school by training the next generation of athletes in the ancient art of Mallakhamb—aerial yoga and wrestling performed on a wooden pole.

The Gameplay The game is played over four thrilling rounds, representing a season of intense preparation and rivalry. As a Guru, you must carefully manage your resources and students' stamina to turn raw talent into championship material.

Each round is divided into three distinct phases:

Market Phase: Visit the town to acquire essential resources. Draft new students, hire assistant trainers, and gather the supplies needed to run your Talim efficiently.

Training Phase: This is where champions are made. allocate your time and Urja (Energy) to develop your students' attributes. You must collect and strategically spend three types of skill tokens to master complex poses:

Bal (Strength): The raw power required to hold the pole.

Chapalta (Agility): The speed and flexibility needed to transition between poses.

Santulan (Balance): The focus required to maintain stability on the wooden axis.

Competition Phase (The Tournament): Put your training to the test! Enter your best students into the arena to perform against rival schools. Success in these tournaments is the only way to gain Pratishtha (Prestige).

Winning the Game The path to victory requires a balance of economic management and physical discipline. At the end of the fourth round, the Guru who has accumulated the most Pratishtha is declared the winner and their Talim becomes legendary.

Key Features

Deep Management: Balance the physical needs of your students with the economic needs of your school.

Attribute System: distinct tokens for Bal (Strength), Chapalta (Agility), and Santulan (Balance) create a rich tactical puzzle for every move.

Unique Theme: Experience the authentically detailed world of Mallakhamb with low-poly artwork that celebrates the wooden textures and geometry of the sport.

Quick Checklist for the BGG Submission:
Since you mentioned the tokens, make sure you check these Mechanisms if they are on the list:

Set Collection (Collecting Bal/Chapalta/Santulan tokens likely fits this).

Contract Fulfillment (If "Tournaments" require specific sets of tokens to win).

Drafting (For the Market phase).

—description from the designer

In this card game you are a Doubles: a street food vendor and your goal is to collect all ingredients that is required by each customer card to fullfil their order.

Each player starts with 9 ingredients cards and 1 customer card. In your turn you drawn one card and try to fullfil your customer order matching the order ingredients with the ingredients cards in your hand.

The first player to fullfil 8 customer cards or be the player with the majority of fullfiled customers wins.

Mini Charter is a two-player abstract strategy game played on a 4x4 grid divided into four 2x2 "Kingdoms". Players move their King using a constrained jump-movement system to claim territory and compete for control of each Kingdom.
This Mini Charter edition serves as a standalone demo for the full version of Cross Charter. It isolates the core movement and "overwrite" mechanics on a smaller scale to provide a fast-paced introduction to the game's positional strategy.

Gameplay Highlights:
 * Movement: Each turn, a player must jump 1 district (internal) or 2 districts (external) horizontally or vertically.
 * Claiming: Players place markers on unclaimed districts to expand their influence.
 * Overwriting: A player may seize an opponent’s district only when a Kingdom is locked in a 2-2 claim split.
 * Objective: The game ends when no further territory changes are possible. The player controlling the most Kingdoms wins.
Setup:
The game can be played with pen and paper.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Woe Hachigane and Woe Kusarigama Promo Cards
description: A set of two beta gear cards, Woe Hachigane and Woe Kusarigama, together with three Living glossary cards that explain the rules used by this gear (Clobber, Flinch, Foresight). They were included with the Woe Grimmory miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Heart of the Sword Saint Promo
description:
This is a set of promotional cards included with the Deathmas Percival miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

It consists of the Shearling Boots rare gear card, the Heart of the Sword Saint strange resource card, the Gift of Passion Atnas AI card, the Lonesome Death, and the Shockwave Cleave Atnas sword craft cards.

It also includes an install guide that explains how this new content should be added to the game.

Two teams answer questions based on chocolates and sweets. Correct answers allow them to progress around the board and the first team to answer correctly while in the centre wins the game.

Includes 58 question cards, 2 rules card, game board, 2 playing pieces.

Ahayuta is a pure, two-player abstract strategy game of deep positional play and sudden, decisive power shifts.
Played on a unique 8x8 board with its corners removed, you command six pieces (warriors). Your goal is to either capture your opponent's powerful "Ahayuta" piece or to move your own Ahayuta across the board to the opponent's home row.
The game's heart is its unique "Clamp" capture mechanism: you capture by surrounding an opponent's piece between two of your own in a straight line, not by jumping.
The twist: the mighty Ahayuta piece is not on the board at first. It is born either by sacrificing your first captured warrior or by heroically moving one of your warriors to the opponent's back row. Once present, this piece changes the entire flow of the game, moving omnidirectionally and becoming the focal point of both victory and danger.

Ahayuta
1. Components
Board: 8x8 grid, 4 corners removed. 60 playable squares.
Pieces per player: 6 Warriors (light/dark), 1 Ahayuta piece (not used at first).

2. Setup
Each player's Home Row is the closest 6-square row.
All 12 warriors start off-board in reserve.
Ahayuta pieces are aside.
Player with light pieces starts.

3. On Your Turn (Choose One)
Deploy: Place 1 warrior from your reserve onto any empty square in your Home Row.
Move: Move 1 of your warriors or your Ahayuta (if in play) according to movement rules.

4. Movement
Warrior: 1 square diagonally forward only.
Ahayuta: 1 square in any direction (horiz./vert./diag.) forward only.
All moves are to empty squares. No jumping.

5. Creating Your Ahayuta (Each player may have only one)
Path of the Hero: When your warrior reaches the opponent's Home Row, it transforms into your Ahayuta and is immediately placed on any empty square in your Home Row (as part of the same move).
Path of Sacrifice: The first warrior you lose to capture becomes your Ahayuta. On your next turn, you must place it in your Home Row (this is your only move that turn).

6. Capture (The "Clamp")
Capture only by sandwiching an opponent's piece between two of your pieces along a straight line (horiz./vert./diag.).
Capture happens immediately when the sandwich is created by your move.
Placing a newborn Ahayuta can trigger additional captures.
Multiple pieces in one line can be captured at once.
Moving between two enemy pieces is safe.

7. Winning (Immediate Victory)
Move your Ahayuta onto any square of the opponent's Home Row.
Capture the opponent's Ahayuta.
Leave the opponent with no legal move (all pieces blocked, reserve empty).

8. Draw
Mutual agreement.
Threefold repetition of the same position with the same player to move.

—description from the designer

Players answer personal questions that can be answered with a number (The example given on the box is, "How many times a week do you shower?"). One player is assigned to answer the question with a lie. Then begins an inquisition phase as the honest players try to determine the liar, while the liar tries to shift suspicion onto someone else. Guesses are tracked on a magnetic tower. Can you detect the liar?

A game book set in the Austerion world.

These adventures, playable both solo and in multiplayer mode, take the concept of books in which you're the hero to a whole new level! As the newly appointed Provost of the small town of Aubépine, you'll be able to go wherever you like in the town, interacting with its inhabitants and environment. What's more, you'll be able to enter different locations and explore them thanks to the inserts you'll uncover during your investigations.

But that's not all! Aubépine is a truly dynamic town, evolving as time passes or as a function of your actions. Elements can appear, disappear or be modified, whether it's the scenery or the inhabitants! Your fellow townspeople don't just give you missions or clues. They behave differently towards you depending on your actions, thanks to a clever prestige gauge.

The game's richness doesn't mean its complexity! The rules of Hawthorn Mysteries are simple to understand and master. A tutorial will get you started without the need for a manual. Dive straight into the action!

Equip yourself to face adversity, solve puzzles and discover artifacts that will help you unravel the dark mysteries surrounding the town! Aubépine is waiting for you to reveal all its secrets!

boardgame: Las Vegas Cold Case File: The Case of the Swinger Wives
description:
The year, 1977. Five couples meet up at Red Rock Campground for a Swingers Retreat. 
By the end of the week two of the wives are dead and five suspects have been identified
Can you figure out who killed Holly Tinson and Trinity Maclaren after almost five decades have passed?

A super simple push your luck card game.

Shuffle the deck and deal each player three cards. Randomly determine a starting player. On your turn you may FLIP or PASS.

Flip one of your face down cards face up. If a "Boom" card is revealed your points explode and your score for the round is zero. If all three of your cards are face up you may decide to push your luck. Set your face up cards aside and deal three additional face down cards.

You may pass only if all three cards in front of you are face up OR at least one of your face up cards is a "Safe" card.

Once each player has passed begin a new round starting with the player with the most points. Shuffle the discard pile if the deck is depleted. The player with most points after five rounds is the winner. Tied players share the victory.

—description from the designer

Læti is a fast, noisy party game that quickly gets everyone laughing. Players must communicate the words on the cards using only sounds and acting, without speaking any actual words. The game is played in teams and against the clock, creating an energetic atmosphere where creativity and quick thinking are key. Læti is easy to learn, works well for mixed ages, and is ideal for parties, family gatherings, and game nights where volume and laughter are encouraged.

LÆTI 2 is a lively, sound-driven party game that celebrates creativity, speed, and laughter. It’s the sequel to the best-selling Icelandic game of 2023, LÆTI, and is designed for families and mixed-age groups. The game revolves around expressing ideas through noise and simple performance, transforming everyday sounds into a playful guessing challenge.

The gameplay is straightforward and easy to learn. Players form teams, and on their turn, one player draws a card and attempts to communicate it using only sounds and head movements. Speaking words, using full-body gestures, or drawing is strictly prohibited, encouraging players to be inventive with sound alone. Each team has a limited time of one minute to guess as many cards as possible before the timer runs out.

Scoring is simple and fast-paced. Each correct guess earns one point, while saying “pass” costs the team one point. The game continues until the deck of cards is exhausted.

To emerge victorious, teams add up their points at the end of the game. The team with the highest total score is declared the winner.

BLÆTI is an adults-only party game that takes the LÆTI concept in a bolder direction. It features provocative themes and over-the-top performances designed for uninhibited party settings.

The gameplay remains the same as before. You play in teams where you either use sounds or head movements to describe a card. Correct guesses earn you one point, while passing deducts a point. The game continues until all the cards are exhausted, and the highest-scoring team wins.

The cards have an adult theme, with 73 cards covering topics like sex, crimes, and debauchery.
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

description:
KRAKKALÆTI is a child-friendly version of LÆTI with age-appropriate content. It encourages creativity, cooperation, and playful expression for younger players and families.

KRAKKALÆTI is a loud, fast-paced party game where players describe cards using only sounds and head movements. They race against the clock to score points while avoiding saying any actual words. House rules are encouraged as long as everyone agrees. The game includes 74 kid-friendly cards.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #51 – Early B-17B to B-17D
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #51 features one page of aircraft briefings and the following three updated to third edition standard early Boeing B-17 data cards:

• Boeing B-17B (First model produced for USAAC, could have fought in 1940, 1941)

• Boeing B-17C early (First combat worthy model, upgraded guns, armor, etc.)

• Boeing B-17C/D (The model the USA went to war with Dec. 1941 in the Pacific)
family: Game: Fighting Wings Aircraft Data Card (ADCs) downloadable expansions

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #52 – Macchi MC.202 Folgore
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #52 features one page of aircraft briefings and the following three updated to third edition standard Macchi MC-202 Folgore (Lightning) data cards:

• Macchi MC.202 early (initial production models, series I to V, best performing)

• Macchi MC.202 C.B. (Fighter Bomber variant with extra guns, bomb pylons under wings)
