The Legio Custodes, Sisters of Silence and Divisio Assassinorum Factions may only be used in armies of the Loyalist Allegiance, while the Solar Auxilia may be used in armies of any allegiance.

In order to use the contents of this book, you will require the Warhammer: The Horus Heresy Age of Darkness Rulebook. Additional Army List Profiles for expanded units for use alongside the core units presented in this book can be downloaded from the Games Workshop website

—description from the back of the book
family: Game: Horus Heresy – Age of Darkness

description:
Pasture Prime is a competitive strategy game in which players manage and expand cattle ranches by acquiring land, raising herds, and producing resources. Over the course of the game, players add Pasture Cards to their ranch, place different types of cattle, and collect Meat, Milk, and Meal resources based on shared dice rolls and pasture production.

Each turn, dice are rolled to determine which pastures and cattle generate resources and trigger breeding. While all players may benefit from a roll, the active player has greater control over timing, purchases, and development decisions. Players may expand their ranch by purchasing additional pastures from a shared market, stack pastures to improve production and scoring, and manage herd composition to balance growth, efficiency, and risk.

The game features multiple cattle types with different roles, light asymmetry through Barn abilities, and a shared market that creates competition for limited land opportunities. The game ends when an end-game condition is met, after which players score points from developed pastures, cattle, and bonuses. The player with the highest total score wins.

Daytime Minutes is a might-follow trick-taking game. Up to two different suits can be played into a trick, and the winner of each suit collects all cards of that suit. Players want to collect minutes on cards to try to get as close as possible to the plan limit without going over. If someone goes over, they incur overage charges and lose a point! But the player who captured the fewest minutes also loses a point. Each third trick is an evening call, and all cards played into the trick are discarded.

Das grosse Spiel der Verben ("The Big Game of Verbs") is a useful card game that focuses on recognizing and using German verbs. Players learn and practice German conjugation and sentence structure in an entertaining and engaging way using 100 verbs from the basic vocabulary and various situations.

The game consists of 100 colored cards with a picture of an action and the corresponding verb in the infinitive. There is also three folded cardboard dice: One with subjects, one  with sentence forms (affirmative, negative, and interrogative) and one with grammatical tenses (Präsens, Perfekt, Präteritum, Plusquamperfekt).

The game instructions include rules and suggestions for seven games that can be played in class or at home with friends. German names of these games are: Das 21er Spiel, Die Mimik Macht's, BIS, Memory, Der Weg, Das Schachbrett. In most of the games the winner is the player with most cards at the end of the game.

Sock it to Me! is a family card game for 2-6 players ages 7+ that plays in about 20 minutes.

Introducing your next family game night delight - Sock it to Me!  Grab the deck and gather the family - with easy rules and endless laughter - Sock it to Me! is the perfect game night pick for all ages. Players race to collect matching pairs of socks by requesting a sock of either a type or color from another player (but not both!).  While that sounds innocent enough, don’t be fooled because every turn is a chance for a bit of family-friendly mischief. Someone may give you just the sock you need.  But look out, they might slip you a holey sock and suddenly your matching pair doesn’t count. First player to pair up enough socks (holey pairs don't count!) wins the game.

Rounds are quick, so the excitement never fades, while playful strategy keeps everyone coming back for "one more game".  Get ready for big laughs, silly sabotages, and endless stories you’ll be talking about long after the cards are packed away.  A simple, fun and screen-free way to be together - Sock it to Me!

—description from the publisher

NUTS - Soviet Paratroopers Uncle Vasya's Troops, Nobody But Us!

The Soviet Union was one of the first countries to realize the potential of parachute forces, and experimented with small scale operations in 1927-1929, using parachute troops against bandits in Central Asia. 
In WW2 Soviet Paratroopers played an important role in coordinating Partisan activities, operating behind enemy lines – and supporting “Deep Battle” assaults to encircle German units...

The sourcebook includes: 

     A history of Soviet Paratroopers 
     Unit Lists in NUTS 
     How to add them to your campaign, including rules for Air Drops and Partisan operations 
     A mini-campaign for Partisan Operations 
     The Dnieper River campaign and sample scenario


—description from the publisher
family: History: Resistance during WW2 in Europe

boardgame: NUTS! War Tales! Red Army Aerosan Attack!
description:
Engines howling, the Soviet aerosans sped over the frozen ice of Lake Ilmen under the cover of darkness to scout and disorient German positions for the main attack.

NUTS - Red Army Aerosan Attack! is a campaign book for the NUTS! WW2 skirmish tabletop wargame system, intended for squad- to platoon-level games that put you in command of Soviet winter raiders mounted on aerosans—lightweight, propeller-driven sleds built to glide over deep snow where conventional vehicles struggle.

Aerosan units saw action in reconnaissance, supply runs, and rapid raids, using speed and surprise to outmaneuver the enemy. This book focuses on those operations in the harsh winter conditions of the Eastern Front—where frozen lakes, blizzards, and whiteout terrain become as dangerous as the opposing force.

Inside the Book

     A history of Soviet Aerosans
     Unit Lists in NUTS for Aerosans and Ski Troops
     Cold Weather and Ski Infantry rules
     A mini-campaign for Aerosan Raiding Operations
     The Lake Ilmen (January 1942) campaign and a sample scenario
     Sample Battle Board


—description from the publisher

boardgame: NUTS! War Tales! Alaska Scouts & The Aleutian Campaign
description:
Modern armies have never fought before on any field that was like the Aleutians…We would have to learn as we went along, how to live and fight and win in this new land, the least-known part of America - Col Dashiell Hammett, author of the Maltese Falcon

This 25-page NUTS Campaign Book focuses on the “Forgotten Front” of the Aleutian Campaign, a fight against a brutal enemy in brutal conditions that cost as many US casualties as many more well-known Pacific Island battles. 
This book provides Aleutian Island Campaign Background and Campaign Rules, the Alaska Scouts as a special unit you can play, and several battle scenarios.

Inside you'll find: 

     Background of the Campaign & Campaign Map 
     The History and Unit Organization of the Alaska Scouts 
     A complete Special Operations campaign and 6 unique maps 
     Terrain and Weather rules 
     A new Battle Board map


—description from the publisher

boardgame: NUTS! War Tales! America's First Fight: Hell Hath No Fury – Philippines
description:
As we moved up the lead tank was hit and had to get off the road and into a rice patty. The other tanks had to "beat ass out as there was no way we could get in with no place to go. Not only was there no place to go, but they were against the odds with so many antitank guns and flamethrowers..”. --Lester Tenney, 192nd Tank Battalion, US Army

Hell Hath No Fury – America’s First Fight is a campaign book for the NUTS Hell Hath no Fury tank combat game from Two Hour Wargames.

The campaign is set during the Japanese invasion of the Philippines, starting in December of 1941.

The odds are stacked against you – can you make a difference?

This book includes: 

     Campaign Background 
     Unit Lists  
     Philippines Campaign where you lead platoon or more of US Tanks and AFVs against Imperial Japan 
     Three sample scenarios 
     Tank control sheets for the US and Japanese tanks
Nopporina is a must-not-follow trick-taking game in which players invest in the colors of cards that they think will be added to a building board.

TRUTH WILL OUT
Think you know how your friends feel about the things that really matter? No, we don't mean politics or the economy; we're talking pickles, perms, people who eat from your plate—the important stuff!

FOR PEOPLE WITH STRONG OPINIONS
Say the topic and secretly rate how you feel about it. Now put on your best poker face while the others try to guess your rating. If there's a match, then things get spicy as the race is on to grab the card and win the point!

—description from the publisher

Players take turns drawing topic cards and then secretly rate their opinion of the topic; as sour, so-so or sweet, by placing a rating card face down. Everyone else must then secretly guess this players rating by placing their own rating card face down. All players then flip their rating card over, and everyone with the matching guess must try to claim the topic card by placing their hand on the lead players card first. The first player to touch it claims a point.

Four dynamite cards are dealt to each player, who take turns playing one card to a central stack, if the card is of equal or higher value than the last card played. If a player is unable to do so, they take the stack and play a new card from their hand. Play continues with four new cards dealt to each player every round until the detonate card is played, at which point everyone counts the number of dynamite sticks they have. After someone reaches 50 points, the person with the lowest score is the winner.

Grim Tactics is a streamlined, model-agnostic sci-fi wargame designed for fast play, deep strategy, and total ownership.

What is Grim Tactics?

Grim Tactics is a scalable sci-fi skirmish game played on a 4'x4' table. It uses an alternating activation system that keeps both players engaged every turn. The rules are designed to be intuitive without sacrificing tactical depth.

Key Features:


     Agnostic Miniatures: Use any models you already own.
     Alternating Activation: No sitting around waiting for your opponent to move their whole army. You react in real-time.
     Customization: Customize your units with with unique names, stats, weapons, abilities and commanders.
     Streamlined Combat: A fast, 3-step combat resolution system keeps the action moving.


—description from the designer

In Bombay, power is never given — it’s taken, defended, and paid for.
Gangs of Bombay is a competitive strategy board game for 1–4 players set in the turbulent underworld of 1960s Bombay, an era shaped by strict import controls, high taxes, and a booming black market for gold, liquor, electronics, and arms. Players step into the role of ambitious gang leaders rising from the streets, competing to build the city’s most infamous criminal empire while staying one step ahead of rivals and the ever present Bombay Police.

Gangs of Bombay blends area control and worker placement with economic engine building. Conflict is costly, alliances are temporary, and timing is critical. Players deploy thugs across key areas and locations to claim territory, extract Hafta, smuggle illicit goods, and establish networks of legal and illegal businesses. Every action carries risk: aggressive expansion invites conflict and police attention, while quiet consolidation may leave opportunities open for rivals. Managing resources, positioning, and timing is critical, as Heat from law enforcement constantly pressures players’ plans.

At the start of the game, players choose one of four unique gang leaders, each with asymmetric abilities that shape how their gang operates. While all players share the same ultimate goal, each gang offers a distinct playstyle, strategic focus, and path to victory. No two games need play the same, as players can pursue different combinations of territory control, economic dominance, and notoriety to rise to the top.

The game is played over a fixed number of rounds, after which players score Infamy. Infamy is earned through controlled territories, successful criminal operations, completing contracts, hidden wealth, and notorious actions taken throughout the game. The player with the highest Infamy at the end is crowned the undisputed ruler of the Bombay underworld.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: D.E.I Divide et Impera: Heritage Mode expansion
description:
Heritage Mode is designed for players with some experience of DEI games. It can be used for games with 2-4 players with the Factions found in the core game, as well as the Cash & Rad and Faith & Anarchy Factions.
This advanced setup mode sees each Faction start the game with units in various regions of the map, market cards and buildings. This enables players to focus on the core challenge and reduces the game time from 4 to 3 turns.
Due to the reduced number of game rounds and therefore fewer Actions available to each Faction, each Action carries greater strategic importance and must be carefully analyzed by players.
- Description from the publisher

Part of this expansion is one extra mission card. 
The rules of this expansion are Part of Ashes Magazine #12, pages 14-15.

boardgame: Haunted Lands: Special Specter Card Promo Card
description:
This card adds a fourth Specter card to the game with another effect:
If you enter a Terrain with a Specter, if you have at least one Percunia, discard 1 of it.

During setup this card may replace the first Specter card, or it can be added to the other three to increase difficulty.

This card was part of the Ashed Magazine #12.

"Are you shocked?" It appears to have the body of a fox, its eyes glowing red. You can feel its gaze piercing straight into your thoughts. You hold your breath, keeping utterly still, you realize you may have stepped into a place you were never meant to enter.

In Eastern cultures, the aversion to the number "4" stems from its pronunciation which is often associated with the word "death". For this reason, many regions have long regarded it as an unlucky number. Aware that the 4 symbolizes death and ill omens, we do not allow it to appear in our game (...?)
-description from publisher

boardgame: Requiem: Downfall of Magic – Saccripiante Adventure
description:
This is a single player adventure based on the Requiem game system. The purpose of this adventure is to give you a taste of the basics of the game, streamlining the rules and components.

—description from the publisher

This promo contains a miniature of Saccripiante, 2 equipment cards, 2 cutout sheets and a map. 
It was part of the Ashes #12 magazine, and the rules/ narrative part is on the pages 16-23.

6 Skill cards for Saccripiante are not used for this adventure but can be used to play Saccripiante in Requiem: Downfall of Magic

Exhausted by their long search for Runar on the Deathless Lisle, the clans are on the verge of giving in to despair. So the Jarls declared a day of celebration and inaugurated a legendary game: the Runar Bowl.
A replica of the Runar serves as the ball. Amidst the cheer of fans and the clanging o armor, teams of heroes compete in a fierdce yet joyful match where glory is wort as much as the teeth left on the field. 
In Campaign mode, this scenario can be plays at any time after the first game and before the fifth. Use the "Stadium" setup, and Runar is placed on the field starting from the Territory of the last player of the turn. 
- Description from the publisher

This expansion contains of 1 layout card, 1 equipment card and 2 scenario cards.

This expansion was part of Ashes Magazine #12

This expansion add a new Mode to the game. This mode involves a different setup that allows players to jump straight into the heart of the game and reduce placing time. Due to the reduced duration, each Avatars action takes on greater strategic importance and every choice must be carefully considered . Guardian-type ICEs, on the other hand, have a reduced impact, as this mode focuses on the players choices.

This expansion contains the minature of the Avatar Shroudtech, 7 Malware Cards, 2 I.C.E Desciption card and 1 Avatar Card.

It was part of the Ashes Magazine #12

This promo was part of the Ashes Magazine #12.
It adds an additional mage to the game. 
It contains the Miniature of Shroudtech, his Mage Card and 3 Personal Spells. 
He is great in converting damage cubes (on other mages and rooms)into its own color. 
He can be added to any Game of Black Rose War, Rebirth or Duel.
Creating a triangle by strategically placing rubber bands from one point to another. Claim the triangles you create with your pegs. Whoever claims the most triangles wins!

The Lion & The Unicorn is a two-player competitive card game that combines Snap with English folklore. Set in an old town square, the game pits two legendary rivals against one another in a battle for territory and titles. Players embody these fabulous beasts, utilizing a standard Rider-Waite-Smith Tarot deck to test their pattern recognition and primal reflexes.

Gameplay centers on rapid card play and keen observation. As cards are revealed, players must watch for specific symbols and matching patterns that allow them to strike. It requires a sharp eye and a quick hand to identify the turning tides of battle hidden within the tarot imagery. The goal is to seize the spoils of the conflict, claiming cards from the opponent until one beast has taken everything and the rival is effectively drummed out of town.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Funfair: Unfair – Comicbook Hacker Kaiju Ocean Expansion Promo Cards
description:
One blueprint card (named Tales of Adventure) and one staff member (named Cap'n Clank) for Funfair were included inside Unfair: Comicbook Hacker Kaiju Ocean Expansion.

The blueprint card has a primary value of 28 points and requires requires all 4 themes to be included in any theater.

The bonus value is 7 points and is awarded if you have hired the Cap'n Clank staff member. Cap'n Clank itself scores 1 point per theme icon in your park at game end

The eerie edition in DIN A4 format with 50 sheets all about the theme of fright and horror.

The ultimate game pad with 26 creative and fun categories

for a spookily wonderful evening with friends and family.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Die unheimliche Edition im DIN A4 Format mit 50 Blatt rund ums Thema Grusel und Horror.

Der ultimative Spielblock mit 26 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien

für einen schaurig-schönen Abend mit Freunden und Familie.

— description from the publisher

In 1156, during the Hōgen Rebellion, Taira no Kiyomori and Minamoto no Yoshitomo fought together on the side of Emperor Go-Shirakawa, defeating the faction of the retired Emperor Sutoku led by Fujiwara no Yorinaga.
After the victory, Go-Shirakawa’s advisor Shinzei (Fujiwara no Michinori) gained political dominance, provoking opposition from rival court factions. Go-Shirakawa abdicated in favor of Emperor Nijō but continued to rule as a retired emperor, further complicating the power struggle.

In December 1160, Fujiwara no Nobuyori, supported by Minamoto no Yoshitomo, overthrew Shinzei, seized the imperial palace, and captured both Emperor Nijō and the retired Emperor Go-Shirakawa. However, Taira no Kiyomori quickly returned to Kyoto, reorganized his forces as the official imperial army, and confronted the rebels. To avoid damage to the Imperial Palace, Kiyomori lured the enemy to Rokuhara, where the decisive battle was fought.

Rokuhara Kessen: Kiyomori vs. Yoshitomo is a two-player wargame depicting the climax of the Heiji Rebellion, the Battle of Rokuhara, with one player commanding the Minamoto forces and the other the Taira (Heike) faction.

This DIN A4 game pad is probably the most educational City-Scape-Rivers game of all time. 26 challenging categories will make every student think.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Dieser DIN-A4 Spielblock ist das wahrscheinlich lehrreichste Stadt-Land-Fluss aller Zeiten. 26 anspruchsvolle Kategorien lassen jeden Schüler ins Grübeln kommen.

—description from the publisher

This DIN-A4 game pad is probably the fluffiest city-country-river game ever. 26 creative and funny categories will get every animal lover sweating.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Dieser DIN-A4 Spielblock ist das wahrscheinlich flauschigste Stadt-Land-Fluss aller Zeiten. 26 kreative und lustige Kategorien bringen jeden Tierliebhaber ins Schwitzen.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Stadt Land: Die Schule der magischen Tiere – Der Film
description:
City Cologne, Country Canada, Animal: Tomcat Karajan! The popular city-country-river game concept with 21 fun but also tricky categories, perfectly suited to the movie world of 'The School of Magical Animals.' Search for letters, start playing, and easily score double points with real fan knowledge. A cool game pad featuring Henrietta the Turtle, Juri the Penguin, Rabbat the Fox, and other characters from the successful movies. Magical and creative guessing fun for children aged eight and up.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Stadt Köln, Land Kanada, Tier: Kater Karajan! Das beliebte Stadt-Land-Fluss-Spielprinzip mit 21 lustigen aber auch kniffligen Kategorien passend zur Filmwelt von „Die Schule der magischen Tiere“. Buchstaben suchen, losspielen und mit echtem Fanwissen ganz einfach doppelte Punkte kassieren. Ein cooler Spieleblock mit Schildkröte Henrietta, Pinguin Juri, Fuchs Rabbat und weiteren Charakteren aus den erfolgreichen Kinofilmen. Magischer und kreativer Ratespaß für Kinder ab acht Jahren.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Das Brettspiel – Christmas
description:
Christmas Edition including Classic Expansion  
Experience the festive STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN board game for an unforgettable game night with family and friends. Over 700 funny and creative categories bring every Christmas round to life. Compete in teams and collect points together with the correct terms – who thinks the fastest under the Christmas tree? But beware! Time is running, and the various action spaces constantly present new challenges. Thanks to the innovative letter dice, cheaters have no chance. Guaranteed fun!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Christmas Edition inkl. Classic Erweiterung
Erlebe das festliche STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN-Brettspiel für einen unvergesslichen Spieleabend mit Familie und Freunden. Über 700 witzige und kreative Kategorien bringen jede Weihnachtsrunde in Schwung. Tretet in Teams gegeneinander an und sammelt gemeinsam Punkte mit den richtigen Begriffen – wer denkt unter dem Tannenbaum am schnellsten? Doch Achtung! Die Zeit läuft und die verschiedenen Aktionsfelder fordern euch immer wieder heraus. Dank innovativem Buchstabenwürfel haben Schummler keine Chance. Spielspaß garantiert!

—description from the publisher

The game is encrypted! When you read the encrypted words aloud, their true meaning is revealed. How many cards can you manage before time runs out?
Try saying "Sdieb fries piets" aloud. Can you hear the encrypted phrase? If you're a little hungry right now, that's normal, because you just said: diepvries pizza!
Divide into teams or play one-on-one. Decipher as many cards as possible before time runs out. The game offers hours of fun and funny cards suitable for the whole family!

Cosmic Chronicles is an extension to the original mint tin game Existence: Life & the Universe. Cosmic Chronicles explores alternate models for the Universe and the development of species. And what if the expansion of the Universe was to be faster than expected? Or for example if some species where to become symbiotic?

In this extension, 31 cards allow for the permanent modification of the laws of Nature, the stellar objects, and for special evolutions of the species. An extra Species is also provided, allowing 5 players to enjoy the game, alongside with two extra Stars and 2 extra lifeforms per already existing Species.
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
WE ORKZ. WE MAKE DA RULEZ HERE, YOU PUNK!

ORK ROCK is an add-on for the Forbidden Psalm family of games that allows to play unruly warbands of Orks.

It adds  

     Feats and Flaws tailored for orks 
     Orkish Weapons and Items
     Rules for Junk'jines, ramshackled vehicles to ride on the battlefield


This module requires a copy of one game in the Forbidden Psalm family to be played. It is also recommended, albeit not necessary, to own the Invasion of the Space Orcs add-on, where you can find rules for orks as monsters.

—description from the publisher

A game of strategic hopping

Place your frogs around the pond and hop them to earn dragonflies. Frogs might block the space you're on, so you may have to strategize! The mud may allow you down a bit, but you'll bounce back, so don't quit!

Be the first to earn 20 dragonflies by hopping over your own frogs.

boardgame: Sengoku Folio 13: The Battle of Tedorigawa
description:
The Battle of Tedorigawa is a Japanese doujin (fan-made) game produced by Ludens Faber and is game 13 of his Sengoku Folio series. The game simulates the battle between Oda and Uesugi along the Tedori River in November 1577.

The game is a typical wargame, with movement and attack values, zones of control, a CRT for combat results, and a turn track.

The game includes a map, rules, historical guide, and mounted counters. The counters need to be cut out.
publishers: Dōjin Club Ludens Faber (同人会ルデンス・ファベル)

description:
"The Nightmare is the true hunt."

Test your hunting party with the Nightmare variants of the monsters.
This expansion introduces new behavior cards to be added to the monster decks, along with stance cards that push the difficulty to extreme levels.

By adding this expansion to your collection, you complete the roster of monsters with their Nightmare variants, unlocking the option to face every boss at the ultimate difficulty level.

—description from the publisher

A trick-taking game by Mariano Schmidt in which players try to be the second highest to win. On each turn, players play their cards simultaneously. The player who plays the second highest card scores the points of the highest card. After three rounds, the player with the second-highest number of cards wins "France".

—description from the designer

boardgame: Operation Goodtime: The Battle for the Treasury Islands October-November, 1943
description:
Operation Goodtime is a moderate complexity level solitaire play game on the battle for the Treasury Islands. The player takes on the role of the commander of the New Zealand forces seeking to wrest control of key points of Mono and Stirling Islands. These islands were key to the Allied offensive in the Solomon Islands and isolating the Japanese fortress of Rabaul. Tasked with securing these islands was the 8th New Zealand Infantry Brigade. Their amphibious assault in late October 1943 was the first conducted by that nation’s military since the 1915 Gallipoli landings in WWI. The rules govern the actions and counterattacks taken by the Japanese defenders. As the New Zealand commander, you will be challenged in taking the islands as the Japanese forces fight in terrain well suited for the defense, and as possible reinforcements gather. Can you do as well or better?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Good Face Bad Face: Sad Face Promo
description: This dark blue promo card is shuffled in the deck before the game (instead of the one of the Good Faces). A player can hide Sad Face in his/her stack to steal cards from another player's hand. If a player turns over a Sad Face, they must give all but one of the cards in their hand to the stack owner. If a Sad Face is turned over by the stack owner, they must shuffle their hand and deal out all but one of their cards to the other players.

A light-hearted game for 3-4 players about responding to tragic events. Thoughts and Prayers are worth nothing. What's really important is winning the Hearts and Minds. Except when it isn't.

Hearts and Minds is a trick taking game, where each round players will choose their scoring conditions by selecting a Goal card from their hand. Each can be used only once in the game and may have rules that change how the player must play that hand.

Two players are each given two throne cards and one king, which they place facedown in front of them in random order. Each has a defense rating of 10. Players start with a hand of five cards, taking turns to either play Attackers on an opposing castle card, or a defender on their own card. The goal is to reduce a castle card's defenses to zero, hoping that it's the king card, thereby conquering the kingdom and winning.

La Pierre du Dragon ("The Dragon Stone"), a Choose Your Own Adventure novel.

A merchant entrusts four adventurers with the mission of finding a priceless legendary stone and its guardian. Will they be able to combine their talents to climb a mountain with a fearsome reputation, outwit the ancient traps of its underground passages, and survive ferocious beasts and inhospitable natives? Only you have the answer, because YOU are the hero!

The Dragon Stone is the first illustrated Choose Your Own Adventure novel in our collection, and it's not like other Choose Your Own Adventure books. The concept remains simple: choose between four characters, each with their own unique abilities, and embark on a quest for a legendary stone, through more than 300 paragraphs and 50 magnificent full-page illustrations.

The illustrations, in addition to adding an aesthetic touch to this beautiful book, are useful for the game. They will help you find hidden objects, solve puzzles, choose the best path to take, or the most judicious action to undertake.

—description from the publisher (translated)

La Pierre du Dragon, un roman dont vous êtes le héros 
Un marchand confie à quatre aventuriers la mission de trouver une pierre légendaire inestimable, ainsi que son gardien. Sauront-ils associer leurs talents pour gravir une montagne à la réputation terrible, déjouer les pièges séculaires de ses souterrains, survivre aux bêtes féroces et aux autochtones inhospitaliers ? Vous seul avez la réponse, car VOUS êtes le héros !

La Pierre du Dragon est le premier Roman dont vous êtes le héros illustré de notre collection, et il n’est pas comme tous livres dont vous êtes le héros. Le concept reste simple : choisissez entre 4 personnages aux spécificités propres, et partez à la recherche d’une pierre légendaire, à travers plus de 300 paragraphes et 50 magnifiques illustrations pleine page.

Les illustrations, en plus d’apporter une touche esthétique à ce bel ouvrage, sont utiles pour le jeu. Elles vous serviront à trouver des objets dissimulés, à résoudre des énigmes, à choisir le meilleur chemin à emprunter ou l’action la plus judicieuse à entreprendre.

—description from the publisher

Players take turns placing a tile and moving their token deeper into a gold mine to find a vein, collecting as much gold as possible, rolling a die for any hazards, and using action cards to impede other players or alter their die roll. First player to get back to the entrance with the most gold wins.

Experience the joy and challenge of gardening in a fast-paced, family-friendly card game. The Growing Season combines strategy, chance, and a touch of nature’s unpredictability as players race to cultivate and protect their crops.

How to Play
Players take turns planting and growing crops by collecting matching cards to complete Harvests. Watch out for changing weather, blights, and pests that can threaten your progress. Defend your fields with Stopper cards and clever timing.
The first player to complete three full Harvests earns the title of Master Gardener.

Pocket edition - 100 new cards

Building on the base game, players now have 100 new cards/ situations to draw from from the pile. Each card describes an annoying/embarrissing/hurtful situation or event. Players have to rank these from "not so bad" to "unbearable". Player who is best at estimating how bad a situation is, wins the game.

A card drafting game where you draft cards to pack your bags and try to sneak candy past your mom! You will have the choice of different sets of clothing or candy to stick into your bag. Everyone will take turns being the ”Mom” and trying to catch the “kids” red-handed!

Busted! is a satirical high-school board game set inside the crowded halls, classrooms, restrooms, and stairwells of Hard Knox High.

One player may take on the role of the Faculty, while the remaining players are Students trying to survive the school day by breaking rules, looting lockers, and collecting Friends—without getting Busted! or sent to BIG TROUBLE.

Students move freely around the school board by rolling dice, navigating hallways, stairwells, and rooms in any direction. Along the way, they can land on Action Spaces, which represent risky (and often ridiculous) school-day antics—anything from looting an office to making a desperate escape. Each Action Space requires a die roll to succeed. Successful actions reward Students with Friends, which function as victory points.

The Faculty roams the board trying to catch Students-they do not roll dice, their movement and everything else is set for each figure. If they land on a student, the student must make a SLIP roll to get away - failure means they are Busted!

When a Student is busted, the Faculty takes their Friends, setting that Student back and creating constant tension between risk and reward.

The game is divided into class periods. At the end of each class, Players are graded based on the Friends they currently have. These grades accumulate over the course of the game, and the players with the highest total grade at the end wins.

Throughout the game, both Students and Faculty play cards that grant bonuses, special abilities, movement tricks, penalties, and last-second escapes. These cards add chaos, surprise, and tactical choices as players scramble to stay ahead—or bring others down.

Busted! blends strategy, push-your-luck decisions, and player interaction into a fast, chaotic experience where every hallway is dangerous, every decision is risky, and no one stays safe for long.

—description from the designer

How well do you know Harry potter? In this fast-paced game, you will soon find out! With over 300 quotes from your favourite cast of characters over 8 movies, you'd better think twice! There's even a few fake quotes that sound real!

Each player/team receives 8 cards 1 of each Character Card, 1 Professors Card, 1 Villains Card and 1 Fake Quote Card. Shuffle the quote deck and place the cards face down to form a draw pile.

Watch out for Fake Quotes that sound real! Guess the Episode name for an Optional Challenge and an extra point.

With its wide range of quotes, Who Says engages casual fans to super fans so everyone can play.
Asylum is a murder mystery game played in four chapters. Players review and study the evidence together to solve the murder of a young teacher Eleanor Whitcombe.

boardgame: Драккар: Битва за титул! (Drakkar: The battle for the title!)
description:
A simple strategy game with a fast pace of development. The clan has chosen several Vikings who will pass the test, which will determine which of the participants will become the king, the head of the clan. The winner will be the one who builds the first ship called "drakkar". Warriors will be able to exchange resources, show their skills in diplomacy and will be able to engage in fair battles! Or not completely honest ones...
To win, players must build a ship by collecting resources scattered throughout the island (wood, iron, and food). Each stage of construction requires a specific amount of resources. During the game, players can interact with each other through communication or battles. Battles involve rolling dice and using modifiers from cards to change the final value on the dice.

—user summary

Простая стратегическая игра с быстрым темпом развития. Клан выбрал нескольких викингов, которые пройдут испытание, оно определит кто из участников станет конунгом, главой клана. Победит тот кто первым построит корабль под названием "драккар". Воины смогут обмениваться ресурсами, показывая свои навыки в дипломатии и смогут вступать в честные битвы! Или в не совсем честные...

—description from the publisher

Many adventurers have braved the jungle to photograph the world’s largest crocodile. Anything goes to be the first. It’s a game of luck and strategy: good photos earn points, but blurred shots could bring it all crashing down.

Players start with a “croc tail” card, which marks the beginning of their photo.
On each turn, players draw a card showing a danger level from 1 to 3.
Without revealing it, they must choose to add it to their own photo or give it to another player, who must add it to theirs.
The higher the danger, the greater the risk of ruining your photo and being eliminated from the round, but also the better chance to extend your crocodile and add animals to your photo.

Each animal in a photo is worth 1 point, and the player who photographs the longest crocodile also scores bonus points at the end of the game.

boardgame: Body of Evidence: The Card Game – Blood in the Bayou
description:
A BRAND NEW murder mystery autopsy – Now as a card game

Another body has turned up, and this time the unfortunate victim was discovered in the shallows of an alligator-infested swamp. After examining the body and speaking to the locals, there seem to be some discrepancies between what they know, and what they’re prepared to tell you… Resolve the demise of an ill-fated banker by determining who was driven to murder, how, and why.

Difficulty: 1/3

—description from the publisher

Hvem Spiller Hvem? (Who Plays Who?) is a lively party and deduction game of role-playing, social insight, and careful bluffing.

At the start of the game, each player is secretly assigned another player as their hidden role. Throughout the game, players must answer questions as if they were that other person, trying to sound convincing without making their identity too obvious.

On a turn, a player draws a question card, reads it aloud, and chooses another player to answer. The chosen player answers from the perspective of their hidden role, and the question card is placed face up in front of them. That player then takes the next turn. Each player may answer a maximum of three questions.

Once all players have answered three questions, the guessing phase begins. One player at a time reviews their questions and answers, while all other players secretly guess who they believe that player was portraying. The role is then revealed and points are awarded.

Players score points for correctly guessing other players’ roles, and for having some — but not all — players correctly guess their own role. After all roles have been revealed, the player with the most points wins.

-description from designer

Fame & Fiction is a 20-30 minute area-control hybrid card game in which players take on the role of tactician, summoning figures drawn from myth, legend, and history to compete for influence on a shared game board.

On a player’s turn, they play cards onto hexes facing any of six directions, changing the landscape of the board. Positioning matters: cards interact with their neighbors, creating opportunities to inact combos, control valuable regions, and thwart opponents’ plans. Each decision balances risk and reward as players expand their influence through clever placement and timing.

Gameplay unfolds over a fixed number of rounds, with each player taking exactly five turns. At the end of the fifth round, players get a point for each hex they control. The player with the most points is declared the winner.

While the game is primarily designed for 2 players, there are also ways to play the game with 3 or 4.

—description from the publisher

Pacific War in 1 Hour is a strategic-level wargame simulating the Pacific Theater from early 1942 to the end of 1945. Players assume the roles of the Imperial Japanese command or the Allied command, directing operations across a simplified map of the Pacific. The game abstracts naval, air, and land operations into a monthly cycle, emphasizing the shifting balance of power during the war.

The central objective depends on the side chosen. Japan seeks either to secure key forward positions—Hawaii and Ceylon—or to maintain control of the Home Islands through the final month of 1945. The Allies win by advancing across the Pacific and capturing the Japanese Home Islands. Both sides must manage limited resources, deploy reinforcements, and time their offensives against an opponent following the same strategic calendar.

Gameplay is driven by alternating Operations Phases within each monthly turn. Players choose whether to move forces, launch attacks, or reposition fleets. Naval forces are represented by maneuverable task-force markers, while individual ships provide combat strength on separate fleet sheets. Land units advance through area-to-area combat, and naval battles unfold through carrier engagements and gunnery exchanges using a consistent 1d6-based resolution system.

Although streamlined, the game preserves the essential strategic dilemmas of the Pacific War: committing fleets across wide distances, coordinating amphibious assaults, responding to enemy breakthroughs, and balancing production against losses. The monthly turn structure creates a clear narrative of expansion, counterattack, and attrition that mirrors the historical tempo of the conflict.

Zombies Keep Out Cardgame is a cooperative game based on missions. At the start of the game the players select a mission card that dictates how the decks will be assembled and what the winning conditions are.

After assembly two decks, the house deck and the street deck, the players make alternate turns, buying cards from the street deck until they have 7 cards in hand and then they play one card for it´s effect (if any).
The problem is that the street deck is full with zombies and, if you are buying cards and reveal a zombie, the zombie go to a zombie revealed zone and the player end the turn without have a chance to play a card.
There are several types of zombies and only one or two of the same type are not a problem. But if a thirt one is revealed, the zombies make an attack.
To avoid that, the player can use ammo to kill zombies. But each ammo make only a few damage and sometimes we need the cooperation of other players to increase the amount of damage and kill a zombie. On the other hand, players that helped with ammo will buy more cards in the first phase of their turns, increasing the chance of revealling more zombies.
To avoid the risk of revealing zombies, a player can byu cards from the discard pile, assuming the top card is not a zombie.

Goal is to find the entrance of the house in the street deck and then, move through the house deck until the mission is fullfilled.

boardgame: Lock 'n Load Tactical: Day of Heroes – Battle Generator
description:
Day of Heroes Battle Generator puts the power to create scenarios and engagements into the player's hands, allowing you to get the most out of your game as you go from scenarios we built to charting your own course.  Since it’s inception, the Lock ’n Load Tactical Series has been defined by two things: its innovative rule set and its engaging scenarios.

This generator is based on a point system defined by the size of the battle you want to play and then modified up or down by the variables of the battle. This means that you get to the action fast.  The Battle Generator comes with player aid cards with all the forces in the game and the points needed to purchase them. The Battle Generator allows you to play with maps from Day of Heroes.

The Battle Generator can be used in whole or just the parts you want to play with.  Play the forces you want when you want.

As the sun rises upon your garden, the dew glistening on your cherished blooms, you pause to reflect on the labour, patience, and affection required to transform it into something truly beautiful. Still, you sense that something remains wanting: that final, ineffable touch which might elevate the garden from merely lovely to utterly magnificent. Ah, yes. Of course! What it lacks are floral dragons! Those exquisite creatures, imbued with the very essence of the flowers you so dearly treasure, would lend your garden the liveliness and wonder it so clearly deserves. The world of Ghardinn positively teems with them, and yet, for reasons most puzzling, not a single one has seen fit to take up residence within the estate you have so carefully prepared. This, plainly, will not do! It is time to set matters right. Gather your expedition supplies, assemble a capable party, and venture forth upon a journey of adventure, discovery, and, most importantly, floral dragons. But beware, the wider world is rife with many perils. Outbreaks of pleurossa corruption spread with alarming persistence, and not every floral dragon greets outsiders with kindness. Nevertheless, you have never been one to shrink from danger where flowers are concerned, and you certainly will not begin now, not when floral dragons are involved!

In Botany: Floral Dragons you will assume the role of a botanist, travelling the world in search of fame, fortune, and the coveted honor of floral dragons residing in your garden. As you travel the globe to befriend floral dragons, you will need to manage your expedition funds, experience thrilling events, and acquire support for your journey. Each player begins the game with a set of randomized goals that they then use to plot their path to victory. When players set out from their estate, they have access only to the coins they can carry with them. They can use these coins to traverse the globe and gain crew members and items to improve their odds of surviving the unknown, enhance their abilities, and increase the efficiency with which they traverse the map. However, there is danger in spending too freely, and players must ensure they have enough wealth on hand to return to England with their specimens intact. Whenever you return to your estate, you will add new floral dragons and build new garden features to gain points. At the end of the game, the player with the most points (a combination of the most magnificent garden and the most dragons residing there) will be declared the winner!

One player draws a card with a wacky question. Each player then chooses one answer from their hand. The funniest win. 
This is a card against humanity type of game but more family friendly.

•••

Une première carte est tirée avec une question farfelue. Chaque jour doit ensuite proposer une réponse de sa main. Le plus drôle remporte la manche.

Fast paced dice and card based game of basketball.
Roll the dice and cross reference the game cards for the teams and seasons you are playing to see the outcome.

Baseline Basketball is a quick-play basketball game that will give you the complete results of a game with about 24 rolls of the dice. The game will also compute the number of points scored by each player. The game requires one 50/50 decider die, three same colored d6 dice, and two same colored d6 dice, and two different colored d10 dice.

GAME PLAY:
Each team has three (3) scoring segments per quarter for the Professional game (12 total), five (5) segments per half for the College game (10 total) and four (4) segments per half (8 total) for the WNBA teams.

For each segment a team rolls the OFF/DEF decider die, plus a 3d6 for the ARC/PAINT/FT roll. In the corresponding OFF/DEF column, read the total of the 3d6 to determine the resulting category.

Once you have the result (Either Arc, Paint, or Free Throw), then you roll a 2D6 corresponding with the pre-rolled OFF/DEF and read the appropriate column to determine the number of points scored for that segment. If DEF is rolled then you would also read the points from the DEFENSE section of the opponent’s card.

That score goes in the SCORE RESULT row of the score sheet.

The Total Score Adjustment (TSA) is then added to the score roll to determine the total points for that segment. (Note: All six dice can be rolled at once if you wish).

Scoring continues through the end of the game to determine a winner.

Originally released as a reward for Splatterkins tournaments, it's also possible to get this as a "box topper," free with a purchase from Speedrobo. This is a technique that allows players to reset to their default inspiration by agreeing to shake hands.

boardgame: Get Loud: La Madre of All Word Games
description:
Brought to you by the makers of Tragos (best-selling game party card game), this new bilingual word game is the first of its kind: competitive, generation friendly, hilarious and fully interchangeable for game play in English and Spanish or both. Enjoy a unique bilingual experience that is fun for the whole family. To really get everyone crazy, you can spice things up with two difficulty modes. Players have one minute to act out or describe as many words as possible– in English or Spanish– for their teammates to guess. The team with the most words guessed at the end of 3 rounds wins. Get Loud can be played by children ages 8 and up and can be played with 2 or more players. The box includes 400 cards (800 words in total) with different categories like movies, food, celebrities etc. It’s like charades but make it Latino and way more fun. Trust us, you don’t want to miss this! Perfect for teaching and learning English or Spanish. This summer play indoor or outdoor at your family parties, carne asadas or barbeques. Bring Get Loud to the beach, the park, or any game night for a night filled with laughter.

—description from the publisher

The holiday season is here! Time to get together with family, see friends, and eat plenty of... rice with raisins!? I don't know who put raisins in the rice, but you and your cousins ​​challenged each other to see who could get more raisins than the others... Who will be the best raisin passer?

Passa as Passas (Pass the Raisins) is a roll-and-write game for 1 to 99 players, where each person walks around the bowl of rice like a rondel, drawing spaces of rice and spaces of raisins on their plate. A Christmas gift from Piquinim to the entire community!

In each round, choose 2 of 3 dice rolled to move your token around the rondel. Each time the token moves, a new polyomino shape (with rice and raisins) is selected and can be drawn in the plate grid. Raisins scores victory points, but cannot be drawn orthogonally adjacent to each other.

—description from developer

Made in honor of the host of the Escaped Alien Youtube channel, it was announced on December 26th, 2024, but I have been unable to find any information on how it was released; I'm going to assume it's another tournament promo.

Santalien is a Shimmerkin that allows you to return three cards from your discard pile, one to your hand, one to your opponent's, and one to your draw deck.

This was originally released back when Acy Faust was self-publishing through Psyterra Games, in August 2023, but was re-issued as a promo card when Speedrobo Games took over-but I have been unable to confirm when or how it was re-released. I only learned of it through Speedrobo's official card list.

Both versions are identical:
A Metallic Splatterkin, it can't be overpowered by other colorways, and can be swapped with the current splatterkin in play to take half the damage.

A deck-building and combat game based on the legendary series Saint Seiya and its characters. Each game pits between 1 and 4 Characters against a Villain. Players can control 1 or more Characters, and the Villain functions automatically (it is not necessary for one of the Players to control the Villain).

The game is played over an unspecified number of rounds and turns until the Character or Villain Victory Condition is met.
In order for the Characters to face the Villains, the Characters must be able to fight the Villains. The game
is played over an undetermined number of rounds and turns until the Victory Condition of the Characters or the Villain is achieved.

In order for the Characters to face the Villains, they will be aided by a series of basic cards that they will have at the start depending on the Characters selected, as well as different cards that they can buy during their turn in the corresponding Market by paying their cost in Cosmos.

These cards that will help our heroes will be of the following types: Allies, Lessons, Techniques (both physical and Cosmic), and cards that provide Cosmos.

These confrontations between the Characters and the Villains will take place in different locations in the Saint Seiya Universe. These locations will provide a series of advantages,
disadvantages, and limitations to the battles that take place in them.

—description from the designer

The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach the moon wins the game.

—user summary

Royalty Stacks is a medium-weight, interactive engine-building game about turning ideas into long-term revenue. Players act as innovation deal-makers, developing multiple business stacks in parallel by protecting ideas, choosing commercialization paths, launching products, and managing risk.

Each stack represents a single idea progressing through stages such as protection, development, and launch. Players must decide how broad to protect an idea, and whether to pursue a startup route or partner with external companies, with each path offering distinct risk–reward profiles. Income is generated over time through licensing and product launches, but stacks are vulnerable to interference from opponents via competitive actions and event cards.

The game emphasizes long-term planning, calculated risk-taking, portfolio management, and timing. Players balance optionality versus focus, allocate resources across multiple stacks, and adapt to shifting conditions created by shared event decks. Interaction is high, with opportunities for collaboration, disruption, and defensive play throughout the game.

Victory is achieved by building efficient, resilient revenue engines and adapting to both strategic and stochastic pressures over the course of play. The game features two directionally aligned winning conditions.

—description from the designer

¡Hora de Comer!: Fusión! is a fast-paced card game where players compete to complete their perfect lunch tray while sabotaging others. Build combinations of dishes, defend your meal, and unleash chaotic food fights in the school canteen.

Each round is short and unpredictable, filled with clever plays, take-that mechanics, and deliciously funny chaos. Perfect for families, casual gamers, and anyone who enjoys a quick and spicy battle at the table.

Includes a wide variety of cards with unique effects, food themes, and chain reactions that will make you shout “¡Hora de Comer!” again and again.

—description from the publisher

¡Limón en el ojo! is a fast-paced party and push-your-luck card game where players flip cards, avoid acid bombs, and try to be the last one standing.

Each turn, players reveal three cards from a shared deck, hoping to dodge dangerous "acid shots" while triggering hilarious combos. You might get lucky with harmless lemons... or you might end up eliminated in a citrusy explosion.

It’s quick, chaotic, and perfect for families and friends who enjoy bluffing, surprises, and explosive laughter. The last survivor wins — unless the lemons get you first!

—description from the publisher

Given to folks who came to the Game Changing Designs booth at Gen Con, they are variations of the 1, 3, and 4 cards, featuring new characters (Cat Burglar, Odd Willows, Fortune Seekers respectively), and slightly altering the abilities of the Bounty Hunter, Cultists, and Alchemist respectively.

boardgame: Inns & Outcasts: Ye Olde Vampire Promo
description: A variation of the #6 card featuring a vampire inspired by Tim Hutching's "A Thousand Year Old Vampire," it was available at GAMA Expo 2025 for attendees who purchased one copy of Inns & Outcasts. It's an altered version of the Dragon, allowing players to pay one gold to see another's card and earn a matching number of gold.

Early in 2025 via Game Changing Designs' TikTok page, a contest was opened to receive a personalized promo card, the winner getting it at GAMA Expo. The Noetzels have hinted that they're planning on having more opportunities to get personalized promos.

For this inaugural promo, it's a version of the #3 card, featuring a connoisseur dwarf hero, who can spend gold to unblock cards, then gain four gold for each card unblocked.

boardgame: Urban War: Strike Team Action – 3rd Edition
description:
Iskandria, a vast city on a small blue world. Once home to hundreds of millions, a seat of empire, of commerce and communication, the hub of a thousand planetary systems. 
Now the city is a grave, its spires shattered, its highways churned and cratered, its people in arms or in deep hiding or given over to the nightmare of alien assimilation. 
War fleets converge here, armies mobilize, creatures inimical to mankind infest the warm seas that surround Iskandria and poison the entire world toward human life.
Men die here

Urban War: Strike-Team Actions is a fast paced sci-fi skirmish game set in a brutal far-distant universe.

Players build small teams of soldiers to fight in the war-torn city of Iskandria, the scene of countless vicious conflicts between competing factions, now also threatened by aggressive alien incursions.

Urban War is a tabletop game for two or more players. 
The individual model and experience-based system enable a unique variety of tactical choices as you develop your teams to suit your own personal play-style and strategies.

In this book, you will find:

     Updated Urban War Game System (3rd edition)
     Full army lists for all seven factions
     Quick campaign rules
     Introductory Scenarios


—description from the designer

The goal is not to win tricks, but to secretly declare and collect the powers of the divine beasts.
Because both suit strength and rank strength matter, the range of valid follow plays is relatively wide.
A neutral Kirin disrupts the table, causing the balance of strength to swing wildly.

Gather round for some fish flinging fun!

In this twist on a classic, you catch sets of Colorfish and stack them on your boat.

Can't make a set of your own? Fling a Throw Fish Card to knock over and steal a set!

Play special cards to trick players, prevent a Throw Fish, or steal their cards!

Stack 4 sets of cards first, you WIN!

Tides of War is a 2-player in which generals will place commanders, units, and tactics into campaigns to claim the front-lines, capture strategic locations, and to fight with the most valor. The player that has collected the most valor at the end of the game wins!

Tides of War is played in 8 phases: 4 Commander and 4 Units & Tactics phases. In each Commander phase, players will alternate placing commanders into one of 3 available locations across 4 campaigns, claiming any immediate bonuses that the Commander grants. Then Generals will alternate playing Units & Tactics cards onto an active commander to gain ground, capture strategic locations, claim valor, or to use a special ability.

Cards played will have either immediate effects, in-game effects, and/or end-game effects.  The main focus of grey cards will be to move the front-line to control campaigns and to score valor points. Purple cards are built around capturing strategic locations, either from neutral sites or from the enemy. Orange cards grant command points so that more expensive cards can be placed. Green cards are gas attacks and are used to destroy enemy units & tactics cards and reverse the effect(s) of the destroyed cards (Detail how green cards are played to remove the top-most card of the opponent in the same campaign). Blue cards are specialized cards that have a wide range of effects that can be strategically played.

At the end of the game, if a campaign is in a General's control, then they receive all the valor and bonuses listed on the card. Any valor designated on commander, units & tactics cards are compiled. Any strategic location owned by a player is 3 points. The player with the most valor claims a strategic victory!

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Introducing IKYFL: The Official Game—straight from The Terrell Show and into your living room! This is the must-have game for your next game night, guaranteed to deliver hours of laughter, friendly competition, and wild fun for everyone.

How it works: Players take turns drawing cards, each with a unique category. The twist? Instead of simply answering, players challenge each other to see who’s more knowledgeable in each category! Once a dare is made, it's a showdown of wits and confidence, as players go head-to-head to prove they know best. Outsmart, outplay, and out-laugh your friends to claim victory!

Whether it’s a small group or a full house, this multiplayer game will have everyone shouting, scheming, and cracking up as you race against the clock. Brace yourself for surprises at every turn—IKYFL will have your crew rolling on the floor with laughter, leaving no dull moments behind.

—description from the publisher

Aqualuma "The 6 Heroes" is a free bonus expansion provided by the publisher "Spiel Das!" on their website (and shared here on BoardGameGeek).

It expands the cooperative dice-placement game Aqualuma with a narrative layer, without requiring any additional components beyond the base game.

Across six story-driven chapters, players explore the backstory of the Aqualuma heroes and learn how the crew first came together before facing the spreading darkness. Each chapter focuses on one character and introduces a thematically tailored scenario with new objectives and small rule adjustments, while using only the components of the Aqualuma base game.

This story Mode builds on the core gameplay of Aqualuma -combining magical abilities, cooperative dice placement, and the shared goal of collecting and activating the light globes across the underwater worlds — adding narrative depth and variety while remaining fully accessible and easy to integrate.

Contents:

     6 narratively integrated scenarios
     New objectives and small rule adjustments for each chapter
     No additional game components required – only the Aqualuma base game is needed


—description from the designer

boardgame: The Royal Society of Archeology: Essen Promo Card
description:
This promo adds a new archeology site called Essen.
It's a grey card with following stats:

Costs: 6 knowledge; wild card; 18 coins
Gain: 10 prestige; gain 1 knowledge in a region of your choice; activate the action publisher with the ten prestige cards

boardgame: The Royal Society of Archeology: Cannes Promo Card
description:
Gain a new destination for your expedtion called Cannes.
Cannes is a blue card.

Costs: knowledge 6, 20 coins
Gain: 14 prestige or 2 scholarship; gain 2 knowledge in blue; gain 2 to look at

boardgame: Nah und Fern: Gewürznusse und Gewürzwein
description:
Gewürznusse: if you have at least 1 prestige, you can never fall again under 1 prestige

Gewürzwein: gain 1 bonus prestige after fullfilling a task

The galaxy is ripe for the picking. You have ten eons to expand out from your home sector, exploring sectors and then exploiting their resources or trading with and conquering their inhabitants. Do you race out to the alien precursor ruins at the edge of the galaxy? Or consolidate your power in the centre? Do you focus your scientific advances on exploring and trading with the aliens you find, or build a military machine that is unstoppable?

Who can make the biggest mark on their galaxy? Back Gal4Xeon to find out!

—description from the designer

At the most sinister hour, the greed for alchemical knowledge and unlimited power burned so unbridled that an unspeakable horror saw the light of day. 
The iniquity is a mini-campaign that tells its own story in the world of Alka Sowa in two scenarios.

—description from the publisher

Santa’s Sleigh has broken down on Christmas Eve! Now, he needs your help to solve the clues left by the clever elves to match each present to the right home so they can be delivered before sunrise on Christmas Day. A jolly escape room style game, perfect for getting into the Christmas spirit and having fun with all the family.

A simple ten-puzzle game with a 45-minute time limit. Hints are included; no internet is required to play but you can use it to check your answers and to grab a 45 minute Spotify playlist for the game. This is reasonably resettable: One prop is cut into parts but that doesn’t prevent subsequent use of the prop, another one is folded which makes it obvious what you need to do to solve the puzzle (but it was always simple). Some puzzles are designed to be written on but we photographed them and then wrote on the picture.

Relation Gift from Equity & Law.
Boardgame based on the Whitbread round the world race.

Preperation fase Outer side of the board nr. 1-32 Throw dice and move forward, on justice sign you take a Card from the yellow deck of cards which contains materials and supply for the race. and has a ranking 1-5 and a class A-D

If all players reached 32 the handicap will be detiment,
With calls A throw 4 dice, B - 3 Dice, C - 2 Dice & D - 1 dice.
Handicap A = -11 point, B - 7, C - 4
Count the number thrown with the dice - Handicap and move forward on the World map equal to the result of counting.

During the race around the world there are 4 legs.
Portmouth - Capetown
Capetown - Auckland
Aucland - Punta del Este
Punta del Este
To finish a leg you have to throw the right amount with the dice otherwise you overshoot the finish and have to wait 1 turn in which you always finish.

During the race you have depending on the spot you reach you can either have luck (go forward), bad luck (go backwards) or draw a Disaster card (white deck)
When you have picked the right preperation card the disaster can be overcome, otherwase the dissaster occours and you have to go a few steps back

Máme rádi Česko ("We Love the Czech Republic" Family game is a great choice for anyone who wants to combine pleasure with usefulness. This game brings not only hours of fun, but also the opportunity to learn interesting facts about our beautiful country, all in the comfort of your home with your family or friends.

The game is based on the motif of a popular TV show that almost everyone knows. For teams of players, it offers an exciting and engaging environment in which you will practice not only your knowledge of the Czech Republic, but also your ability to cooperate and think strategically. It is ideal for family gatherings, evenings with friends or as an educational game for children and adults.

With this game, you can be sure that everyone will find something to their liking - from the youngest to the oldest, and together you will gain unforgettable experiences and new knowledge about our homeland.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: The Price of Glory: The Siege of the Alcazar, July-September, 1936
description:
“Is this the price of glory?” –General Jose Moscardó Ituarte’s comment upon learning that his two sons, Pepe and Luis, had been shot by their Republican captors for his refusal to surrender The Alcazar.

“You will find The Alcazar destroyed, but its honor intact.” –General Moscardo’s comment to General Franciso Franco, September 29, 1936.

The uprising of the Spanish “Nationalist” military against the Republic of Spain in July 1936 was met by surprisingly (at least to the Nationalists) strong resistance by those who were willing to defend the democratic government. The Alcazar, that dominated the city of Toledo and its valuable munitions factory, was held by a small Nationalist garrison made up largely of Civil Guard members and about 100 ultra-right wing Falangists. Nearly three thousand Republicans, supported by artillery, tanks and aircraft laid an increasingly bitter and costly siege to the historic fortress complex. The Alcazar was just 40 miles south of Madrid, and as the siege went on, it became an embarrassment to the Republican cause (especially as the atrocities and death toll began to mount), and a source of motivation and recruitment for the Nationalists.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Maid Ascendant: The Siege of Orleans, October 1428-May 1429
description:
“Begone, or I will make you go!” –Joan d’Arc’s command to English and Burgundian troops besieging Orleans.

The Maid Ascendant is an introductory level wargame on the siege of Orleans. The siege marked the debut of Joan of Arc as a military commander, and a campaign that would see the English eventually evicted from France and the end of the Hundred Years War.

—description from the publisher

Grimdark Future is a miniature wargame set in a war-torn sci-fi future, which is played using 28mm miniatures. 
The game mechanics are designed to be easy to learn but hard to master, bringing engaging sci-fi battles for new and experienced players alike.

This rulebook adds lots of extra content to the core rules, providing you with countless hours of epic sci-fi battles that you can customize however you like. The rules are written to be concise and to the point, and come with beautiful illustrations as well as helpful example diagrams.

Here's everything that's included in this rulebook:

     Core Rules
     Advanced Rules
         Terrain Placement
         Deployment Styles
         Extra Missions
         Side-Missions
         Extra Actions
         Solid Buildings
         Random Events
         Battlefield Conditions
         Terrain & Objective Effects
    
     Total Conversions
         Fog of War
         Brutal Damage
         Command Points
         Suppression
         Multiplayer Games
         Apocalyptic Games
         Kitchen Table Games
         Small-Scales & Multi-Basing
    
     Additional Content
         Tournament Guidelines
         Solo & Co-Op Rules
         Campaign Rules
         Mission Cards
    


Note that all of the army books are available for free on our website, and are not part of this rulebook.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: La Conquêste: The French and Indian War 1754-1763
description:
In 1754, a young George Washington blundered into an ambush in the woods of Ohio that killed a French diplomat and started a bloody six year war for control of France's vast Great Lakes empire in North America. In this exciting solitaire game by Charles S. Roberts Award winner R. Ben Madison, you sail your ships of the Line to keep supply lines open to France as the Seven Years War rages in Europe, India, and the Caribbean. Use French leaders like Governor Vaudreuil and General Montcalm to deploy your stealthy Indian allies, élite Marines, and plucky 'Canadien' militia to defeat or parry legendary generals like Wolfe and Amherst who besiege your forts on five different fronts from Nova Scotia to Ticonderoga to the Thousand Islands. King Louis XV of France orders you to stop the Thirteen Colonies from tipping the balance for Britain; it's your sacred task to deny the British Québec, Montréal, and "La Conqueste" - The Conquest of Canada in the French and Indian War!

—description from the publisher

Age of Fantasy is a miniature wargame set in a dark magical world, which is played using 28mm miniatures. 
The game mechanics are designed to be easy to learn but hard to master, bringing engaging fantasy battles for new and experienced players alike.

This rulebook adds lots of extra content to the core rules, providing you with countless hours of epic fantasy battles that you can customize however you like. The rules are written to be concise and to the point, and come with beautiful illustrations as well as helpful example diagrams.

Here's everything that's included in this rulebook:

     Core Rules
     Advanced Rules
         Terrain Placement
         Deployment Styles
         Extra Missions
         Side-Missions
         Extra Actions
         Solid Buildings
         Random Events
         Battlefield Conditions
         Terrain & Objective Effects
    
     Total Conversions
         Fog of War
         Brutal Damage
         Command Points
         Fatigue
         Multiplayer Games
         Apocalyptic Games
         Kitchen Table Games
         Small-Scales & Multi-Basing
    
     Additional Content
         Tournament Guidelines
         Solo & Co-Op Rules
         Campaign Rules
         Mission Cards
    


Note that all of the army books are available for free on our website, and are not part of this rulebook.

This rulebook is divided into 3 sections:

     Basic Rules - Everything you need to play the game,


with plenty of diagrams and examples.

     Advanced Rules - Extra rules that you can on top of


the basic rules to spice up the game.

     Total Conversions - Rules that radically modify the


base rules and provide a new experience.

—description from the publisher

A card game themed around an ordinary person finding balance between work, fun, and emotional extremes.

16 cards are laid out in a 4x4 grid.  Players take turns collecting one row or one column from the grid.  Points are scored by collecting coins, forming sets of trip cards, having the most relax cards, and avoiding mental excess cards.

—user summary

Do you get sick and can't get the job done, and your boss isn't too happy about it?  The game is all about ordinary life and how you survive the day!  The concept of the game is simple; you go to work, you make money, and you take vacation.  If you want to take more vacation, then you need to make more money! which means you have to work harder! but be careful! if you work too much! you will get condition cards! like stress or depression!  Too much of those cards could cause you serious mental health issues, so we have some good healing cards to help take some edges off like vacation card, trip card, or exercise card.  Needless to say, if you are too laid back, then you get no money to make your life happy!  so the goal is simply to balance your life and make the most of your day!

—description from the publisher

The game is a simple roll and move game which a couple extra mechanisms.  The board has 2 sides, a United Kingdom and a Scottish Highlands variant.  Rules allow for the game to either be played as a race to the finish or the budgetting traveller where the winner is the player with the most money at the end.

Players visit checkpoints along the way, and make the choice to travel on main, scenic or toll routes.  Chance and accomodation cards allow for ways for players to both gain and lose money.

boardgame: The Adventures of NitNit: The Cooperative Climbing Compendium
description:
Join NitNit, Sew-y, and their knitting obsessed pals in a cooperative climbing game filled with charm and challenge. Inspired by campaign style of The Crew and the Fellowship of the Ring the Trick-Taking Game and the climbing classic Tichu, this compendium of climbs invites your team to knit plans together and execute flawless tricks. Each new chapter is a puzzle of timing, teamwork and clever plays as your hand-drawn heroes lend their talents (and restrictions) to achieve your chapter goals. Conquer the challenges together and discover the joy of stitched-together strategy.

—description from the publisher

In each "book" (hand) of the campaign, players choose a character from those available. Some characters must be chosen, while others are optional. Each character has a specific mission (going out first, capturing specific cards, must play (if able) in certain conditions, etc). Assuming that the conditions for successfully completing the book have been fulfilled, players can move on to the next book.

It is a Colombian board game where players must open a savings account to begin playing. After opening it, they can earn money and use negative cards to freeze, hack, or close other players’ accounts.

To recover an affected account, players must roll the dice and rely on luck. The goal is to be the first to reach exactly 500 pesos; going over results in a penalty.

•••

Es un juego de mesa colombiano donde los jugadores deben abrir una cuenta de ahorros para empezar a jugar. Una vez abierta, pueden acumular dinero, pero también afectar a otros jugadores con cartas negativas que congelan, hackean o cierran cuentas.

Para recuperar una cuenta afectada, el jugador debe lanzar los dados y depender de la suerte. El objetivo es ser el primero en llegar exactamente a 500 pesos si se pasa, recibe una penalización y debe intentarlo de nuevo.

boardgame: Dr. Finn's Book of 2-Player Strategy and Word Games
description:
Dr. Finn's Book of 2-Player Strategy and Word Games contains eight two-player games that can be played in the book without cutting them out. Each game uses one sheet of paper and requires only a pen or pencil. The book contains eight unique set-ups for each of the eight games.

You can play theses game on a plane, in a cafe, or almost anywhere! Bring along on your next your vacation!

The eight games include 5 strategy games and 3 word games. The strategy games are:
Speculators
Starry-Eyed
Fish Mongers
Detectorists
Tic-Tac-Dough

The word games are:
Eloquacious
Quill Seeker
Spell Track

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Age of Fantasy: Regiments – Advanced Rules
description:
Age of Fantasy: Regiments is a miniature wargame set in a dark magical world, which is played using 28mm miniatures.
The game mechanics are designed to be easy to learn but hard to master, bringing engaging regimental fantasy battles for new and experienced players alike.

This rulebook adds lots of extra content to the core rules, providing you with countless hours of epic regimental battles that you can customize however you like. The rules are written to be concise and to the point, and come with beautiful illustrations as well as helpful example diagrams.

Here's everything that's included in this rulebook:

     Core Rules
     Advanced Rules
         Terrain Placement
         Deployment Styles
         Extra Missions
         Side-Missions
         Extra Actions
         Solid Buildings
         Random Events
         Battlefield Conditions
         Terrain & Objective Effects
    
     Total Conversions         
         Fog of War
         Brutal Damage
         Command Points
         Fatigue
         Phased Battles
         Multiplayer Games
         Kitchen Table Games
         Small-Scales & Multi-Basing
    
     Additional Content
         Tournament Guidelines
         Solo & Co-Op Rules
         Campaign Rules
         Mission Cards
    


Note that all of the army books are available for free on our website, and are not part of this rulebook.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Age of Fantasy: Skirmish – Advanced Rules
description:
Age of Fantasy: Skirmish is a skirmish miniature wargame set in a dark magical world, which is played using 28mm miniatures.
