The game mechanics are designed to be easy to learn but hard to master, bringing engaging fantasy skirmishes for new and experienced players alike.

This rulebook adds lots of extra content to the core rules, providing you with countless hours of epic fantasy skirmishes that you can customize however you like. The rules are written to be concise and to the point, and come with beautiful illustrations as well as helpful example diagrams.

Here's everything that's included in this rulebook:

     Core Rules
     Advanced Rules
         Terrain Placement
         Deployment Styles
         Extra Missions
         Side-Missions
         Extra Actions
         Random Events
         Battlefield Conditions
         Terrain & Objective Effects
    
     Total Conversions
         Fog of War
         Tactical Phase
         Command Points
         Critical Hits
         Fatigue
         City Fighting
         Multiplayer Games
         Kitchen Table Games
    
     Additional Content
         Tournament Guidelines
         Solo & Co-Op Rules
         Campaign Rules
         Mission Cards
    


Note that all of the army books are available for free on our website, and are not part of this rulebook.

—description from the publisher

The classic game of Chinese checkers on a smaller board for two players. The rules are the same as in normal Chinese checkers – get all your marbles over to your opponents side first by moving and jumping.

Caos magnético ("Magnetic Chaos") is a party game involving the placement of magnets, where your goal is to avoid placing them too close together otherwise, they will stick, and you’ll have to pick them all up.

In this fun game, roll the die and place the number of magnets indicated. The first player to get rid of all their magnets wins the game.

•••

Caos magnético es un juego party de colocación de imanes donde tu objetivo es no tratar de colocarlos muy cerca, o se van a juntar y tendrás que llevarlos todos a tu mano.

En este divertido juego, lanza el dado y coloca el número de imanes que indique el dado. El primero que se quede sin imanes será el ganador del juego.

Krash Mini is a turnless party game in which the objective is to be the first player to remove all tokens from their own field.

During gameplay, players launch tokens individually toward the opposing player’s field. The core challenge consists of ensuring that each token successfully crosses into the opponent’s play area.

•••

Krash mini es un juego party, divertido y sin turnos, en el cual el jugador que logre quedarse sin fichas en su campo será el ganador del juego.

Para ello, cada jugador va a lanzar fichas de una en una dirigidas hacia el campo del otro jugador. La complejidad se encuentra en lograr que la ficha pase al campo del oponente.

Madriguera is a game of cleverness, strategy, and luck, perfect for playing with family or friends. It allows players of different ages to interact while remaining fun and engaging for everyone. 
Throughout the game, players move across the board to find a carrot; depending on the number rolled, they may advance, discover shortcuts, or fall into traps that force them to start over. The goal is to collect a carrot and bring it back to your own burrow. The winner is the first player to successfully return their carrot to their burrow.

•••

Madriguera es un juego de astucia, estrategia y azar, ideal para jugar en familia o con amigos. Permite la participación de grupos de diferentes edades sin perder dinamismo ni emoción. 
A lo largo del juego, los jugadores avanzan por el tablero hasta encontrar su zanahoria; según el número obtenido en el turno, pueden avanzar, encontrar atajos o caer en trampas que los obligan a volver al inicio. El objetivo es conseguir una zanahoria y llevarla de regreso a la propia madriguera. Gana el primer jugador que logre completar este recorrido.

Laberinto Galaxy ("Galaxy Maze") is the new game for the whole family, combining luck, strategy, and excitement on an innovative three-dimensional board.

Move your ships from planet to planet and achieve total conquest to win the game.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Laberinto Galaxy es el nuevo juego para toda la familia, que reúne suerte, estrategia y emoción en un novedoso tablero tridimensional.

Avanza con las naves a cada uno de los planetas y logra la conquista total para ganar.

—description from the publisher

Can you find all the dinosaurs hiding in the jungle? The game board is full of ancient reptiles, but can you find them all before the other players? With a double-sided and an ever-changing game board finding may be more challenging than you think! Try to find a pair of dinosaurs from a busy board as fast as possible.

Mentaliza is a memory and strategy game perfect for ages 6 and up, designed for 2 or more players. Place the 20 cards face down in a 4x5 grid and each player rolls 2 dice trying to find the numbers on the flipped cards. If a die shows 2, the player can choose which number it represents and if they guess correctly, they keep the card. But watch out, other players can steal your cards if they remember their locations and the first player to collect 8 cards wins or the player with the most cards when there are no cards left on the table.

•••

Mentaliza es un juego de memoria y estrategia ideal para personas de 6 años en adelante, diseñado para 2 o más jugadores. Coloca las 20 cartas boca abajo en una cuadrícula de 4x5 y cada jugador lanza 2 dados tratando de encontrar los números en las cartas volteadas.
Si un dado muestra 2, el jugador puede elegir qué número representar y si acierta, se queda con la carta. Pero cuidado, otros jugadores pueden robar tus cartas si recuerdan su ubicación y gana quien consiga 8 cartas primero o quien tenga más cuando no queden cartas en la mesa.

Suertudo is a dice game in which players take risks to achieve objectives by rolling the dice.

Get consecutive pairs, roll specific numbers, and complete more objectives than the other players to win the game.

•••

Suertudo es un juego de dados, en el cual los jugadores van a arriesgarse a lograr objetivos con el lanzamiento de dados.

Consigue pares seguidos, saca cierto número y logra más objetivos que el resto de los jugadores para ganar la partida.

Ping Pong One is a one player game, where you have to make the Ping Pong bounce from one place to another, the most bounces you get, the most points you will have. The only rule is that the ping pong has to bounce from one place to another.

Ping Pong One es un juego en solitario, en donde el jugador debe acumular la mayor cantidad de saltos con la pelota Ping Pong. La única regla es que la pelota debe ir de un lado al otro.

Pulgosos is a dynamic card game recommended for both children and adults, with games lasting approximately 15 minutes or more. Each player controls their own group of puppies, and the goal is to be the first to have the required number of flea-free dogs, which depends on the number of players.

During the game, each player starts with three cards and, on their turn, can perform different actions: play a puppy card, place fleas on rival dogs of the same color, use anti-flea cards to clean their pets, or activate special cards that change the flow of the game and add strategy and interaction.

In addition to being fun, Pulgosos helps develop key skills in children such as attention, memory, decision-making, and socialization. Its fast-paced mechanics encourage respect for the rules and healthy competition, making every match an entertaining challenge for the whole family.

•••

Pulgosos es un dinámico juego de cartas recomendado tanto para niños como para adultos, con partidas de aproximadamente 15 minutos o más. Cada jugador controla su propia camada de perritos y el objetivo es ser el primero en lograr la cantidad necesaria de perros libres de pulgas, la cual varía según el número de jugadores.

Durante la partida, cada jugador comienza con tres cartas y, en su turno, puede realizar distintas acciones: sacar un perrito, colocar pulgas a los perritos rivales del mismo color, usar cartas antipulgas para limpiar a sus mascotas o activar cartas especiales que cambian el curso del juego y añaden estrategia e interacción.

Además de ser muy divertido, Pulgosos ayuda a desarrollar habilidades clave en los niños como la atención, la memoria, la toma de decisiones y la socialización. Su mecánica ágil fomenta el respeto por las reglas y la sana competencia, convirtiendo cada partida en un entretenido reto para disfrutar en familia.

Dedicate your house to the Griswold Family Christmas with the ultimate game for National Lampoon fans! Monopoly: National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation lets players buy, sell, and trade locations from the hilarious holiday classic, such as the Griswold Family Christmas Tree Site, Clark's Lighting Display, and more. Conquer the board with custom movie-inspired tokens to set up Decorations and Christmas Spirit, while dodging Chemical Toilet Storm Drain and Jelly of the Month Club cash drains. Relive the chaos of the four-alarm holiday emergency and be the last player with Griswold Bucks to win!

—description from the publisher

Which of your neighbors have your cat? In this exciting deduction game you have lost your cat and you have to ask the whole neighborhood if they have seen them.

The issue is that you don´t know any of your neighbors and you will have to ask them first some questions in order to know them better. In your turn, play a question card like: where are you from, what is your favorite food and colour and also if they have a pet. If it happens that you discover one of your neighbor´s favorite pet is a cat and they didn´t have one before, congrats you have found your cat and won the game.

•••

¿Cuál vecino tendrá el gato? en este emocionante juego de deducción hemos perdido nuestro gato y debemos preguntarles a nuestros vecinos quién lo tiene.

El problema es que no conocemos a nuestros vecinos, y debemos preguntar por su nacionalidad, mascota y comida favorita. Entre más información tengamos, más cerca estaremos de encontrar nuestro gato.

Vertigo is an action-dexterity game where you build a colorful, psychedelic tower, balance multicolored beams and "vertigoers" (human-shaped pieces that hang off the edges of beams) onto the  tower, and battle opponents in dice matches.

On a given turn, each player rolls a numbered die and performs an action based on what number is rolled. For example, if Player 1 rolls a 2, then that player picks 2 different objects (from a wall, beam, and vertigoer) to place/balance onto the tower in any order. Some outcomes lead to rolling a second "object" die that forces a given player to put specific pieces onto the tower.

If the number die lands on a 4, the player rolls the object die, places the given object onto the tower, then challenges an opponent of the player's choosing to a Battle Round. In a Battle Round, each player rolls the numbered die once, and whoever gets the highest value wins the round. After the standoff, the winner forces the loser to place a piece of the winner's choice (from ceiling, wall, beam or vertigoer) onto the top floor of the tower.

Whoever causes the tower to collapse loses, and everyone else wins. Alternatively, a player is simply eliminated from the game when they knock down the tower, and gameplay continues from the existing tower up until one player remains.

The Second Edition of the popular trivia countdown game, Kwizniac2 includes 200 new questions and sets of clues! Kwizniac 2, The Trivia Countdown Game, is a take-anywhere, twist on trivia games designed to test your knowledge. The object of Kwizniac 2 is to accumulate as many points as possible by correctly answering trivia questions with as few clues as possible. Unlike most trivia games that give you a single question about a subject, each Kwizniac 2 clue card has a set of 10 clues in decreasing order of difficulty. The more clues it takes to answer the question, the less points you receive. Kwizniac2 offers a more enjoyable trivia experience, where you’ll always get the answer eventually - now with even better NEW sets of questions! There 200 all new questions for hours of all new fun. If you enjoyed the first Kwizniac, you must add this game to your collection. Even for first time players, enjoy this easy to use and challenging educational trivia card game. It comes in one small, portable travel box for easy use on the go. Play with 2 or more players. Ages 12 and up. Try to beat your opponents by answering the trivia card questions with the least number of clues. Each clue card contains ten clues, increasingly easier to answer. Everyone has a chance to win and an opportunity to have fun! Great for vacations and evenings at home, as well as outings on the run! Don’t miss this chance for family bonding and fun travel!

—description from the publisher

Blood is a miniatures skirmish game representing the conflict of gothic monsters and innocent humans. 
It is designed to build abilities in a cinematic fashion to ensure that characters can only use their most powerful abilities as the story reaches its climax. 
It is inspired by the films of Hammer Horror and classic Universal Studios.

Blood is a gothic horror skirmish game. It needs a handful of D6, a bit of terrain and a bunch of minis representing peasants, vampires and werewolves.
It has a medium models count and with a handful of terrain and should play out in around 90 minutes.

Blood is inspired by Hammer horror and classic universal movies. Hunted humans, ravenous werewolves and sinister vampires are central to the game.

The intention is to create a system where abilities are only available once they become "powered up" in  narrative appropriate manners. Essentially the intention is to re-create the fact that in movies neither the monster nor the hero will employ their most powerful attacks at their first meeting. 
Here, Dracula can't fly in and rip off Van Helsing's head in the first five minutes, nor can the vampire's castle be fire bombed sight unseen.

Play revolves around resource management, the various forces pick up, manage and spend the resources that power their abilities in narrative different ways. For example, Vampires work in a strict hierarchy from thralls through brides to fathers while humans pick up powers for dying since only the last girl gets access to the greatest powers.

—description from the publisher

In the silly, spell-slinging party game, Spellfinder, you and your friends are student wizards at a prestigious magical university, mashing up magic words to create problem-solving spells! With thousands of possible scenarios and countless spells, you’ll have a blast coming up with zany magical solutions to the perils that plague high fantasy townsfolk—from the mundane to the mystical—and you’ll never play the same game twice!

The game contains 100 problem cards with phrases on them. Each round, a different player draws two problem cards which are combined to create a problem statement that the other student wizards must attempt to solve. The other players draw into their hand from a deck of 255 scroll cards with word fragments on them. By combining two scroll cards, they create a spell intended to address the problem, perhaps in a silly or pun-ful way. Each player presents their solution dramatically and the problem-presenting player issues a decision, or the group could vote, giving out seals of approval (points) for each solution. Play continues until someone reaches a target number of points or a set number of rounds is complete, at which point the winner is declared.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 決戦: 幕末維新 (Decisive Battle: The Meiji Restoration)
description:
Two civil war battles in Japan.

Toba-Fusimi (鳥羽伏見) battle in 1867, saw pro-Imperial clans defeat Tokugawa shogunate forces.
Tabaru-saka (田原坂) battle in 1877, opposed Imperial Japanese Army to the Satsuma rebellion.

Issue games of Game Journal #96.

boardgame: Коледни подаръци и пакости (Christmas Gifts & Mischief)
description:
Christmas Gifts & Mischief is a family-friendly holiday board game set in the magical North Pole workshop. Snowflakes drift softly as elves finish toy preparations and Santa checks his list. Suddenly, mischievous creatures emerge from the snow, intent on stealing or swapping gifts and spreading chaos among the workstations.

Players take turns moving, gathering presents, thwarting troublemakers, and even causing a little mischief of their own to gain an edge. The heart of the game lies in balancing cooperation — to keep the workshop running — with tactical play to collect the most gifts.

Each round, choices matter: protect your haul or disrupt others at just the right moment. The first to secure three gifts in their festive sack wins the game, bringing holiday cheer to the table.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Настолната игра „Коледни подаръци и пакости“ ни пренася в магическия свят на Северния полюс, където снежинките падат тихо, елфите подготвят подаръци, а Дядо Коледа проверява списъците си. Но изведнъж пакостливи създания проникват в работилницата, крадат и разменят подаръци и объркват всичко.

Играчите се редуват да събират подаръци, да спират създанията и – в някои случаи – да правят малки пакости, за да напреднат. Сърцевината на играта е в умението да балансирате между сътрудничество и тактически ходове, които ще ви донесат предимство.

Първият играч, който безопасно сложи три подаръка в своята торбичка, печели и спасява празничната магия!

—description from the publisher

Feelings Animal Welfare was created in collaboration with the association Des Pattes et des Classes (Paws and Classes), which works to raise public awareness of respect for living beings and a better understanding of animals. This game invites players to explore the emotions evoked by our relationships with animals: whether they are everyday companions or animals exploited for consumption or entertainment. It encourages reflection on the place we give them in our lives and in society, highlighting their fundamental needs as well as their biological, behavioral, psychological, and cognitive capacities.

The game uses the same mechanics as Feelings: a Situation card, related to the animal theme, is read aloud. Each person around the table chooses one of the six Emotion cards placed around the board to express what they would feel in that situation. The challenge is then to guess the emotion chosen by another person… before someone tries to guess yours. The more you are in sync with each other, the more you will progress on the path of empathy.

Feelings: Living Together explores the emotions that shape how we coexist with others, in the diverse relationships we forge: within families, at school, as couples, among friends, colleagues, neighbors, or even in our relationship with the environment. The game invites reflection on what fosters respect, cooperation, and mutual understanding in the various spheres of daily life. It's a playful and supportive way to examine our reactions to differences, conflicts, injustices… and acts of solidarity.

The game uses the same mechanics as Feelings: a Situation card, related to life in society, is read aloud. Each person around the table chooses one of the six Emotion cards laid out around the board to express how they would feel in that situation. The challenge is then to guess the emotion chosen by another person… before someone tries to guess yours. The more you are in sync with each other, the more you will progress on the path of empathy.
Cat Packs: Promo Pack 2 adds five more additional cats to your cat pack: The Bellboy, Alley McCathy, The Baroness, Don Piano and Meowrengue.

Cat Packs: Imposter Promo Card adds one additional cat to your cat pack, the Arachitty.

Arachitty
Cost to Play: -
Pawer Value: -
Ability: 3 Catnip

The card is part of the Cat Packs: Meow Resort Kickstarter.

Choose between two pairs of glasses and try to locate and arrest spies around the world. Fill your jail with a spy of each color and proudly proclaim, “Mission Accomplished!” But it won’t be that easy – only one pair of glasses will show you where the spies are hiding amongst the civilians! Watch out for sabotage and even Double Agents who lurk around every corner. The suspense is real, the stakes are high, and only you can save the world! Includes 1 Gameboard, 4 Player Boards, 21 Spy Movers, 21 Spy Mover Stands, 40 Cards, 1 Pair of Regular Glasses, 1 Pair of Spy Revealing Glasses, and Complete Instructions. Recommended for 2-4 players, ages 8 and up.

It’s up to you to arrest the spies in this world of intrigue and espionage
Use the special Spy Revealing glasses to locate spies in your city and put them in jail
Use cards to strategically move spies around the boards, make an arrest, sabotage other players or even become a Double Agent!
First player to fill their jail with a Spy of each color wins!

—description from the publisher

Take your turn, draw a card and place it in your personal board.

Assemble the pieces and characters that combine to earn the most stars.

Some cards have powers to help you with special actions!

Expansion Pack 15
Far to the of east of the empire's borders lies the temple city Amenomiya. Here dwells a dragon revered since ancient times as the Celestial Dragon. Once every hundred years, the Celestial Dragon loses its sanity and reveals its true nature as a demon beast. The battle to subdue the demon beast and restore peace to Amenomiya begins now.

In addition to the new map “Amenomiya,” the new adventurer "Onmyouji" and companion animal “Rabbit” make their debut!

Expansion Pack 16
The collaboration with the board game HacKClaD has finally come to life! Three witches from HacKClad, Flare, Mia and Luna, join the dragon-slaying campaign. The three new characters feature original illustrations by artist Akai Tera made for Dorasure.
This product also includes a collaboration bonus: three “Shot” cards usable in HacKClaD.
 In the world of Dorasure there exist mystical beings known as “Witches of Future Sight”. These witches possess the ability to foresee events yet to transpire. While the origin of this power remains shrouded in mystery, they have now come to wield this future sight to challenge the mighty dragons. Their abilities will likely prove invaluable in the fights to come.

The time for rivalries is over! At the height of their glory, the once-opposing Cities now join forces to build a grand Capital together and honor the gods.
Erect splendid Divine Altars dedicated to the protectors of the Capital, and let its aura shine throughout the ancient world.

Game Overview
The Architects’ goal is to work together to construct the Capital and achieve the highest possible score by using the best strategy. You must therefore join forces and play cooperatively.

Setup
Together, players choose one of the three available scenarios: Corinth, Sparta, or Athens, each offering an increasing level of difficulty. Each player receives a starting tile, while the remaining tiles form the heart of the Capital, placed in the center of the play area. Three Architectural Challenge tiles are revealed, along with their corresponding Divine Altars. Players also receive two Stone cubes and three City tiles, which they keep in hand like cards. Unlike the base game, there is no river.

Turn Structure
On their turn, each player may:

Add a tile to their City in order to complete an Architectural Challenge and gain a Divine Altar.
Contribute to the construction of the Capital by placing their tile in the center of the play area, allowing points to be scored at the end of the game.

At the end of the turn, draw a tile to always have three in hand.

Architectural Challenges & Divine Altars
Each completed Challenge allows you to place a Divine Altar in the Capital. In this expansion, Squares function differently: for a Square to be counted at the end of the game, an Altar must have been built there in honor of Hestia, Hermes, Ares, Zeus, or Demeter, depending on the Square’s color.

End of the Game
The game ends when all City tiles have been played. Only the Squares in the Capital adorned with a Divine Altar are valid and scored. Personal Cities do not grant any points. The final score is compared to the chosen Scenario’s target score.

Managing Stones, coordinating placements, and anticipating Challenges are essential to achieving collective victory.

Akropolis Pantheon is a cooperative expansion for the game Akropolis. The base game is required to play.

boardgame: 1878 Gotland: Railways in the Baltic Sea
description:
18xx set on the island of Gotland in the Baltic Sea off of Sweden´s east coast. 3 to 5 players invest in and manage a total of 10 railway companies with the ultimate goal of becoming the wealthiest investor.
Questionable stock manipulation, fierce rusting of trains AND tracks, and existential dread is ever present - all while laying rail on an island so small you can see your bad decisions from the other side.
Unique features of 1878 Gotland compared to most other 18xx titles is an unusual initial Private auction featuring Entanglement cards, Privates with different powers depending on ownership, a map full of "dits" which emphasizes the importance of creating lucrative routes more than ever before, both full and incremental capitalization, and more...

boardgame: Panic Stations! The Pandora Celeste Card Game
description:
The Ship’s Computer wakes you from Cryo-Sleep. There’s something Big & Nasty on board!

But you’re on this ship for a reason…
You’re trapped on a drifting death trap with one shot at survival. Race through bug-infested corridors, outwit rivals and hunt down the elusive McGuffin that will unlock your Agenda — and the access codes you need to escape before everything goes dark. Every second counts, every choice is vital. Will you get out alive?

Panic Stations! is the fast-paced card game version of the fanboy love letter to sci-fi monster movies Pandora Celeste. It’s quicker.  It’s dirtier.  And it’s a lot more bloody!
Players begin with 7 cards in hand, which they use to move around the cards that form the Ship, turning them over and interacting with what they find. Some cards reveal Tools that you can Pick Up and Use; others reveal Nasty alien monsters that bite if you try to pick them up; and others present Plot McGuffins needed to Fulfil your Hidden Agenda.

As the stock of ShipCards runs out, it reveals Big Nasty aliens seeded into the deck, which start to stalk you through the Ship. Find the McGuffin, use it to unlock the Escape Vehicles and get off this floating deathtrap before its Pandora’s Box of all the evils in the sci-fi universe overflows and swarms you all.

If Pandora Celeste is like living through a movie, then Panic Stations is the high-octane chase sequence from the trailer!

—description from the designer

Enter the island of Teru, explore its nooks and crannies and start the battle for supremacy!

On your journey to control the island of Teru, you will not only face battles with other players, but also the pitfalls of the island itself, which will constantly complicate your acquisition of resources. Sometimes you will have to rely on risky decisions and hope for the favor of fate.

Can you outwit your opponents, overcome the island's obstacles and strategically use all available resources? If so, the island of Teru and its immense riches will be your well-deserved reward.

Prepare for a challenge that will test your strategic mind and leadership skills!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Vstúp na ostrov Teru preskúmaj jeho zákutia a začni bitku o nadvládu!

Na tvojej ceste za kontrolou nad ostro- vom Teru ťa čakajú nielen súboje s os- tatnými hráčmi, ale aj nástrahy samot- ného ostrova, ktoré ti budú neustále komplikovať získavanie surovín. Niekedy sa budeš musieť spoľahnúť na riskantné rozhodnutia a dúfať v priazeň osudu.

Dokážeš prekabátiť svojich protivníkov, zdolať prekážky ostrova a strategicky využiť všetky dostupné prostriedky? Ak áno, ostrov Teru a jeho nesmierne bohat- stvá budú tvojou zaslúženou odmenou.

Priprav sa na výzvu, ktorá preverí tvoj strategický um a vodcovské schopnosti!

—description from the publisher

A cooperative game for 1-4 players.  Players play one of the main characters from the TV series.  each character has a unique ability so different combinations of character choice will drive different approaches to the game. Play is based around moving around Hawkins uncovering mysteries and confronting the monster.  Play has 4 seasons which progressively get harder for the players as restrictions are imposed and the monsters get more powerful.

To win the players have to meet the criteria for each monster once the monster token has caught up to the group’s token on the monster track.  If the criteria is met the group win, if not they all lose.

Each turn, players secretly receive their role: Honest or Liar.

A question is asked, and each player answers by placing their name tag on the magnetic vertical board. The Question card defines the scoring system, with a "Blowout Counters" zone for unexpected answers!

Each player explains their answer. This is where the Liar must excel in the art of bluffing, and where the Honest players reveal sometimes surprising truths! The questions are always numerical and personal.

Debate aloud to unmask the bluffer. Ask questions, probe, interrogate... When everyone is ready, count to three and point at the person you think is the Liar!

Reveal your Honest card! The Liar explains their lie and gives their true answer (be careful: a lie too close to the truth doesn't score any points). The Honest players score points if they identify the Liar (bonus points if they are the only ones). The Liar scores points for each person fooled, with a special bonus if he dared to give an extreme answer (the lowest or the highest)!

In Zoo Aventura, you´ll have 10 game modes you can enjoy with all the family. Play different game modes like being the fastest to grab a card while playing cards in secuence or bet your cards, and if you get a card that can fit inside a secuence of two numbers you already have you win, or you lose all your bet.

Some other game modes include, throwing a dice so you can obtain food to your animals, memory games, strategy and more.

•••

¡¡¡Un juego que contiene 5 modos de juego!!! nunca te vas a aburrir jugando tus cartas de animales y armando tu zoológico. Resuelve pruebas de memoria, juego de palabras y destreza. 
Sé el mejor cuidador de zoológico en este entretenido juego de cartas.

Cooperate to get each other to guess each other's words without ever being able to speak directly.

Using well-chosen clues and your deductions, try to guess everything together before the totems disappear!

Each player has a word to guess… but can see everyone else's!

On your turn, write a clue on a card and place it face up. Be careful: no one must know which word you're playing for!

The clues accumulate, overlap, and sometimes contradict each other… It's up to you to observe, interpret, and guess your own word at the right moment.

But beware: the number of Guess! totems is limited!

If you misuse them, the game ends immediately. Victory depends on the subtlety of the clues and the accuracy of your intuition.

Two player card game: one plays as Robin Hood and his team, the second one plays as Sheriff of Nottingham.

A game includes a set of civilians and soldiers for each of the teams. Players take turns, where one is the attacker and the second one is the defender. Next round the roles are switched.

Attack and defense is a simple guess mini-game, where both the attacker choose top or down. If they choose different side - it's a hit, if the same side - it's a miss. Player with most hits wins the duel and earn the points for the current round.

Rounds are played until all cards are used or a set threshold of points is reached.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Robin Hood

boardgame: 코드네임: 미니획장1 – 합성어 동물 (Codenames: Mini-Expansion 1 – Compound Animals

description:
Pushmajor is an abstract strategy game for two people about pushing 3 of your opponents stones off the board!

The game is played using 9 stones each of two colours on an 8x8 grid. See images for setup.

Goal
Push at least three of opponents stones off the board.

Movement:
A single stone moves like the king in chess, 1 step in any direction.

A line of stone may move in the direction of the line up to as many spaces are there are stones in the line.

Pushing:
A line may push a stone or a line of lower length for the entirety of it's movement, even off the board.

Game feel:
The game has a pleasing dynamic of pushing, and then returning to fortify a centre. Because of the swift movement, there is room for daring and surprising play. The game plays very quickly, and one might enjoy a best of three.

Optionally one may start with the pie rule. (One player makes the first move, then the other player chooses color)

—description from the designer

Zappazon is a territory making game for the 9x9 grid board.

Goal:
Be the last player to make a legal move.

Setup and material:
Board starts empty. Both players must have access to some stones for marking blocked territory, and two pawns for movement.

Phase 1:
Player start by alternating putting down their pawns on whatever space they choose until each their two pawns has been places.

Phase 2:
Players moves have two parts. First they must move their pawn the equivalent of two kings moves in chess, ending on another space than they started on. Then they shoot off a stone to land at any place visible from then in the orthogonal or diagonal directions, that is, as a queen could move in chess.

Goal and strategy
Players take turns moving until one player cannot make a legal move. Effectively one would only make moves until all opponent pawns are blocked off into separate territories, then one could simply count the remaining territory, and the one with the larger territory would be the winner.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Draw What?!: You're Going to Hell
description:
First Official Expansion Pack to the Draw What?! Game
Find Out Which of Your Friends and Family Has the Most F*cked Up Sense of Humor
Guaranteed to Offend Someone
25 Brand NEW Cards with 125 F*cked Up Words and Phrases

One Too Many is a push-your-luck game playable with a standard deck of cards about greed, timing, and reading the other players. Players collectively draw cards, trying to build up valuable piles of cards, while keeping a wary eye on the growing danger in the center of the table.

Each turn, all players simultaneously choose how many cards to take. Non-Diamond cards add to a player’s growing pile, but Diamonds are placed in the center, where they threaten everyone. If the Diamonds ever form a straight longer than the the number of players, the group busts, and the greediest player(s) that turn are punished for going too far.

Players may choose to stop at any time, banking the cards they’ve accumulated before risking it all again. As Diamonds pile up, players must judge how far the table can go, and whether taking "just one more" card is worth the risk.

—description from the designer

Crashword is a dynamic social party game that challenges players to build words and act out clues in a head-to-head style game. Players roll dice on a solid wood octagon board to generate letters and symbols, then race to create words or mime clues before opponents, using the included wooden dice, scorer board with pegs, hourglasses, and jokers. The game encourages vocabulary, spelling, and reflex skills while combining charades style play with traditional word creation.

boardgame: That's What She Said Game: First Expansion
description: That's What She Said Game – First Expansion (Adults Only) is a card expansion for the party game That's What She Said Game. It adds additional setup and response cards featuring explicit adult humor intended for mature audiences. These cards are mixed into the base game deck to increase variety and intensity of gameplay.

boardgame: That's What She Said Game: Second Expansion
description: That's What She Said Game – Second Expansion (Adults Only) is a card expansion for That's What She Said Game. It introduces additional setup and response cards featuring explicit adult humor intended for mature audiences. The cards are shuffled into the base game to increase variety and replayability.

boardgame: Имаджинариум: Это был тяжёлый год (Imaginarium: It's been a tough year)
description: An additional set of 98 cards.

In CLAC, every card counts and every placement can change everything! Build your magnetic ramp, trigger explosive chain reactions, and eject your opponents' cards. Magnets of the same color attract each other. Simple to learn, quick to play, and incredibly addictive, CLAC combines strategy, risk-taking, and instant elation. A fast-paced, interactive, and colorful card game where there's only one golden rule: never underestimate the power of attraction!

A co-op competitive game where four players play as gods, divided into two moral camps (good and evil) and determine the fate of a chosen one. By influencing moral choices, players attempt to win the hero's devotion. Players do this from the realm of the gods, placing connection tokens onto different possible actions. By managing a (good) relationship with the chosen, players are able to seize the moments to receive the most devotion points.

—description from the designer
An additional set of 98 cards.

Long sealed away realms have suddenly begun to open, introducing bygone, forgotten Monstas into the world.

You are a monsta-ology academic, and it's your time to make hypotheses on these newly discovered Monstas, test those hypotheses and have your findings written into the textbooks!

Sealed Monstas features unique deck construction (not deck building) to naturally capture this feeling of forming a hypothesis and testing it!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Scholastic Time Machine Travel Game Tin
description: In Scholastic Time Machine Travel Game Tin, 2–4 players take on the role of “time detectives,” receiving event and action cards each turn and racing to place historical events in the proper sequence. Once all players have placed their cards, the red reveal decoder is used to check answers. The game encourages learning about important dates and milestones in world history through fun, interactive play. Contents typically include event cards, action cards, a red reveal decoder, and game instructions.

boardgame: NUTS! War Tales! Lights, Camera, Action!
description:
NUTS! WAR TALES! LIGHTS, CAMERA, ACTION!

The War Like You’ve Never Played It Before

We weren’t heroes. We were just trying to make it through one more day — one more hill, one more town.
But every man in that squad? He had his story.
— Sgt. Jack “Gravel” Morgan, Able Squad, G Company, 22nd Infantry Regiment

Step behind the camera and into the chaos of World War II — where every firefight feels like an episode from a lost 1960s TV war show. NUTS! War Tales! Lights, Camera, Action! turns your tabletop battles into cinematic adventures, complete with cliffhangers, character arcs, and the grit of classic shows like Combat! and The Gallant Men.

This Campaign Book for NUTS! Frontline leans into the Cinematic Rules of the NUTS system to capture the look, feel, and drama of a vintage TV series — one mission, one episode, one story of courage at a time. Command your squad, survive the mission, and see who makes it to the next episode.

What’s Inside

     Character Creation: Combined rules from across all NUTS! books to build your own cast of heroes.
     Cinematic Rules: Drama Points, Tension Tokens, and “Commercial Break” rescues for TV-worthy twists.
     Episode Tools: It’s a Tiger!, Magic Walkie-Talkie, Here Comes the Cavalry, and more scene-saving tropes.
     Squad Campaign Play: Fight from Utah Beach through the hedgerows of Normandy, following Able Squad’s journey.
     Character Drama: Heal, rebuild, and deal with the fallout between missions — just like serialized TV storytelling.
     Able Squad Roster: Fully detailed cast with NUTS! and C&C attributes, ready to film your first “episode.”
     Episode Generator: Build and play your own TV war show with character arcs, moral choices, and recurring storylines.
     Cast & Crew System: Track your figures as recurring stars, guest appearances, or the “new guy” who might not make it past the pilot.
     Studio Mode Campaigns: Play through a whole “season” of episodes where audience approval (Fame) shapes your squad’s success.
     War Tales Crossover: Integrates seamlessly with other NUTS! War Tales books like Czechoslovakia 1938, Alamo Scouts, and The Last Knight.


Join the Cast. Grab your rifle, cue the theme music, and roll camera.
Because in NUTS! War Tales: Lights, Camera, Action! — every game is an episode worth remembering.

boardgame: NUTS! War Tales! Alamo Scouts & the Raid on Cabanatuan
description:
NUTS! Alamo Scouts & the Raid at Cabanatuan

NUTS Alamo Scouts is a campaign book for the NUTS WW2 skirmish tabletop wargame system, and is intended for squad to platoon level games that put you in command of a group of special forces units in the Pacific Theatre of War.

In the Pacific Theater of WWII, the vast jungles, hostile terrain, and fiercely entrenched Japanese forces required specialized units to conduct reconnaissance, sabotage, and rescue operations deep behind enemy lines. Among the U.S. Army’s most elite and effective units were the Alamo Scouts, a small but highly skilled group operating under the Sixth Army in General Douglas MacArthur's command.

This game supplement brings the elite Alamo Scouts—one of the most effective U.S. reconnaissance units of World War II—into your NUTS! WW2 games. Designed for use in the Pacific Theater, this book provides players with historical background, mission scenarios, and campaign rules tailored to the Scouts’ operations. You can incorporate Alamo Scout missions into existing Pacific campaigns or run a dedicated Behind Enemy Lines Recon Campaign, highlighting stealth, precision, and small-team tactics.

In this book, you’ll find:

     A timeline of the Alamo Scouts and their key operations.
     Organization details, including team compositions and mission roles.
     Information on the Scouts' weapons and tactics.
     Historical Pacific campaigns where the Scouts operated.
     Sample missions and a recon-focused campaign for your games.


This supplement is perfect for players who want to focus on reconnaissance, POW rescues, and daring raids in dense jungle terrain.

boardgame: NUTS! War Tales! Czechoslovakia 1938: The War That Never Was
description:
NUTS! Czechoslovakia 1938
This is the national program from London. Today, Czechoslovakia has stunned the world by rejecting the Munich Agreement and declaring that any move by Germany to annex the Sudetenland will be considered an act of war..
BBC, September 1938 (fictional)

NUTS Czechoslovakia 1938 is a speculative "What If" campaign book for the NUTS WW2 skirmish tabletop wargame system, and is intended for squad to platoon level games that put you in command of Czechoslovakian units fighting a German invasion of their country.

If Czechoslovakia had rejected the Munich Agreement and held onto the Sudetenland, they might have posed a significant challenge to Germany. Despite Germany's military advantage, they weren't fully prepared for a major war in 1938. Czechoslovakia's industrial capabilities and defensive terrain could have prolonged the conflict, potentially leading to international support and internal pressures within Germany.

This book includes:

     Munich Agreement & Background
     How the Czechs Could Have Fought
     Czechoslovakia 1938 Campaign
     Campaign Maps
     Three Sample Scenarios
     Units lists for Czechoslovakia, Germany, Poland, Hungary, and Soviet Expeditionary Forces

0950 hours, two trucks and trailers approached. They were ammunition trucks – a good haul. Once again a few surprise shots. They do not stop, so they ‘get it!.. e made a good job of destroying the trucks, and they were blazing merrily when we left. - -Lance Corporal Jack Davis, LRDG

The NUTS – Desert Raiders! campaign book focuses on the British and Italian special forces who dueled each other across the North African Desert. 
Desert Raiders includes rules that let you conduct desert patrols and raids against determined foes in the brutal conditions of the North African desert from 1940-1943.
This campaign book is designed for use with the NUTS WW2 skirmish game and supplements, but can also be used as a resource for other skirmish game systems.

This book includes:

     Campaign Background
     Maps Special Rules including new NUTS Pursuit and Travel Missions
     Desert Raider Campaign Rules
     Sample LRDG Deep Raid scenario
     Army and Equipment lists for the LRDG, SAS, PPA, and Axis Sahariane and Brandenburgers

Kaiju K.O. is a duel abstract tactical super sentai themed board game.

Spawn artifacts using the grid & roll mechanic.  Traverse the board to collect these artifacts before your rival, use their power to give you positions and battling advantages.

Battle is engaged with dice when two Kaiju are adjacent.

Use the Artifacts you collected to tactically turn tables. The true spirit is being able to chain these artifacts with one another.

The player that defeats all their rival’s Kaiju is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Brushwood is a peaceful yet strategic set-collection game for 2–4 players. Players take on the role of hikers navigating a vast, ever-changing forest trail to observe local wildlife, pick up berries, and forage for mushrooms.

On each turn, a player moves the shared hiker meeple one to three spaces forward along a trail of forest card stacks, then claims the top card from the trail space where they end their movement. When moving more than one space, they must place a berry token on each card they pass, making those cards more valuable for whoever lands there later. As the players collect the trail cards, they build a personal hiking route, a linear tableau which they strategically arrange to maximize their end-game points from animal sightings and mushroom foraging. When foraging, players can aim for high-value mushroom tokens by collecting enough cards of a specific type or act quickly to secure available tokens before others do.

The game ends when the forest trail begins to thin out, and players score points based on their berry majorities, mushroom tokens, animal sightings, and clever use of binoculars and birdhouses.

—description from the publisher

Challenge your friends in a game of Math and Mayhem!

In Bits Please, 3-8 players compete to be the first to grab Bit cards whose values sum up to multiples of 8. Every set captured adds to a player’s leaderboard progress, and the first player to score eight sets wins the game!

Get ready for even bigger bytes! The leaderboard progressively challenges skilled players, as the minimum sum increases the higher you are on the track. But don’t worry, even your Bit card values increase as you play!

Do you have what it takes to be the ultimate 8-Bit Champion?

A small expansion for Sacra Maioritas, consisting of a new Oath and Edict card.
The new Oath card will gain you +3 Influence if you win the last Ritual, the new Edict grants +1 Influence if you moved the marker 2+ steps.
These 2 cards provide even more variablilty to the game!

From natural wonders to amazing constructions created by man, the Earth is full of incredible views and landscapes.

Each turn the active player chooses one wonder from the wonder cards currently on display and attempts to name it. If successful, the player wins the wonder card along with up to two more cards, depending on how many leads were used.
The player then assigns each won wonder card to a continent on their personal board without looking at the answer on the back of the card and play proceeds with the next player.

At the end of the game, all players check their collected wonder cards and discard those placed on the wrong continent. The remaining cards are counted and the player with the most cards win!

Zim the squirrel, Zam the raccoon, and Zoum the cockatoo are out for a big game of hide-and-seek... and they're inviting you to find them!

In Zim Zam Zoum, each turn is a little adventure: roll the die, discover which animal you need to find, and turn over the cards one by one to find them. But watch out: if the animal on the die isn't on the card, you lose!

boardgame: The Big 80s Music, TV and Film Quiz
description: Set of 200 questions on the TV, films and music that aired throughout the 1980s

The sheep, with four different colours wool, are grazing in a circle around the table, spread across grass fields and players’ stable fields. Each player controls a stable made up of two fences placed in front of them, while the spaces between players form shared grass fields that also hold sheep.

Players aim to collect sets of sheep of the same colour in their own stable fields. By scoring sheep the player earns small bonusses to be used in future rounds.
On your turn, you place one fence on top of an existing fence anywhere around the table — either on one of your own fences or on an opponent’s. Each fence shows arrows indicating which sheep colours jump to the left and which jump to the right when that fence is activated.
After placing a fence, you immediately activate it and move the indicated sheep. Then, you also activate your own fence or fences that have not yet been activated this turn. 
There are four different sheep breeds, each with its own special behaviour. Some sheep may bring another sheep along when they move, while others will kick a sheep out of a field when they arrive. Mastering these unique behaviours — and predicting how they interact — is key to clever herding, and scoring.

Variant: The Wolf
For players seeking a tougher challenge, the Wolf can be added. Each turn, the wolf prowls the Highlands and steals sheep from the fields, leaving less to lead into the stable.

Stubborn Sheep is a tactical game of positioning, timing, and controlled chaos, where every fence placement can shift the balance — and no sheep ever goes exactly where you expect.

—description from the publisher

Catch the dream by touch! Can you guess the picture by tactile associations? In this vivid game you will exercise your sense of touch and challenge your teamwork and intuition.

Dream Catcher is a cooperative game, where you will witness whimsical stories and will try to “catch a dream” — to identify the picked story by touch.

Every round, the lead player picks a Dream card and handles specially selected Touching cards with raised pictures to the other players. The lead player’s goal is, without saying a word, to help the other players guess the picked Dream card. The core goal of the other players is to discuss the pictures they can feel on their Touching cards and to collectively choose one of the Dream cards.

You will flip Night tokens for correct answers and discard them for wrong answers. To win the game you need to have as many flipped Night tokens as possible by the end of the game.

Middle-earth: Dark Minions (MEDM), released in 1996, was the second expansion set for Middle-earth: The Wizards, the first basic set of MECCG.

MEDM adds many powerful hazard cards including more Undead and introduces the agents - a subtle group of Sauron's servants, concentrating on intrigue and influence. The set also adds an underground network of site cards, The Under-Deeps.

Number of individual cards: 180

Each round, a card shows two extremes (for example: “An unpleasant noise (0) to a pleasant noise (20)”). The player called the ‘Mentalist’ secretly picks a number between 0 and 20 and gives a clue related to their number (e.g., if they picked 18: “the sound of waves on the beach”).

Their team discusses and tries to guess the number by placing a token on the scale. The opposing team can bet on a mistake.

Once both teams have positioned their tokens, the numbers are revealed and points are awarded based on the accuracy of the placements and the success or failure of the bets.

The aim of the game is to be the first team to reach 12 points by correctly interpreting the clues, listening to your teammates, and using a pinch of strategy.

As the Elementals awaken, the fragile Order the God Pariah imposed upon ancient Chaos is threatened, and his world begins to tear itself apart.

Pariah is a dark-fantasy tactical battle game where players control opposing armies on a shared battlefield. Choose from four factions, the Humans of Cimeria, the Elementals, the Beastmen, and the Undead, each with distinct playstyles built around positioning, timing, and unit synergies. The game is designed to bring the feel of wargame-style army clashes and battlefield tactics to a non-TCG card game format.

Each player uses a 30-card unit deck, consisting of two copies of 15 unique unit cards. After drawing 10 cards, players secretly build a starting army worth up to 20 points, then deploy units alternately into the battle line, where formation and adjacency come into play. The game lasts up to 5 combat rounds, with units activating based on their Priority to attack or use abilities, while the evolving board state rewards smart trades and protecting key units.

When units are destroyed, the controlling player gains Elemental Energy equal to their point cost. Between rounds, players enter a Shop Phase to spend Energy on Relics and Evolutions that power their army. You win by Annihilation if you destroy all enemy units before the end of Round 5; otherwise, after Round 5 the winner is decided by Attrition: the player with the higher total point value of surviving units wins.

—description from the publisher

Tori Tori Trick is a cooperative trick-taking game, where the suits of the card are visible on the rear. The players are working together to make sure each player wins a specific amount of tricks.

It is a "must-follow" game, and each person will play cards from the hand of the person on their left - so the person choosing the card knows the suit, but not the specific rank.

The players succeed if they each uniquely win one of the specific bids available.

In the heart of beautiful Burgundy, you and your opponent run traditional farms that produce both wine and cheese. You’ll gather ingredients using an innovative paired-worker system, and carefully time the production, aging, and sale of your goods. Balance is key, as you’ll have to earn from both wine and cheese equally to claim victory.
Wine & Cheese is a deep, strategic game for two connoisseurs that will captivate you with its unique blend of worker placement and card management.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ぼくのくつしたどこいった  OH!MY SOCKS
description:
The goal is to collect single “sock cards” and match them into pairs.
You can hold up to three cards in your hand, so there’s a bit of strategy and bluffing involved.
The gameplay feels like enjoying the last three cards of Old Maid over and over.

Players take turns attacking another with a card. The defender must play a higher-value card or a trump card, otherwise they collect all the cards.

As long as there are cards in the draw pile, you draw again until you have 6 cards in hand at the end of the round.

When the draw pile is empty, you stop drawing. Attacks continue, and the last player with cards loses!

Sneaky Sabotage combines puzzle solving and hidden deduction, as you attempt to solve a puzzle as a group, with the possibility of a traitor.

A Saboteur may be in the group, who earns points if the answer is incorrect, and is given a hint they can use to help guide the group away from the correct answer.  An Insider may also be in the group, with that same hint they can use to try and guide the group in the correct direction.

At the end of the round, players score points based on whether the puzzle was completed or not.  Loyal players vote on who they believe the Saboteur is, while the Saboteur votes on who they believe the Insider was, earning points for correct votes.

Each round, the roles shift.  At the end of four rounds, the player with the most points wins.

Take on the role of an Earth Federation ace mobile suit pilot in the One Year War against the forces of Zeon in this deckbuidling game based on the groundbreaking 1979 anime series: Mobile Suit Gundam!

In this solo deckbuilding game, choose one of seven unique Ace mobile suits to pilot, each with it's own 50-card deck.  Opposing you are the forces of Zeon, represented by their own 50-card deck, and a selection of 14 possible Enemy Ace Mobile Suits.  This game features characters, locations and mobile suits from the Mobile Suit Gundam, Gundam 0080, 08th MS Team, and Gundam Thunderbolt anime series.

In each game, the selected Ace MS deck and enemy deck are shuffled together to form the main deck.  8 Enemy Ace MS are selected to face throughout the game.  Each turn, a 20-card timer deck represented by "Attrition" cards advances.  Your goal is to defeat all of the Enemy Ace MS cards before time runs out by building up your deck and defeating enemies on the Battlefield.

This is a fan-made, web published game.  The game rules and cards are available for download here in PDF format
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
This fourth Wingspan expansion explores the birds of Mexico, Central America, South America, and the Caribbean. This incredibly biodiverse and abundant region is home to more than 3,000 bird species, including the birds with the smallest and largest wingspans in the world (the Bee Hummingbird and Snowy Albatross, respectively).

Hummingbirds make up about one tenth of the bird species in Latin America, delighting birders with their quick movements and shimmering feathers. Designer Elizabeth Hargrave and artists Natalia Rojas, Ana Maria Martinez Jaramillo, and Martha Clare highlight them in this expansion with a new type of card that adds a twist to gameplay.

Wingspan Americas' components include 111 new bird cards plus 40 hummingbird cards (and their related boards and mats), 8 new goal cards, 5 double-sided goal tiles, and an Automa solo mode. This expansion requires the core Wingspan game (or Wingspan Asia, which is a standalone game for 1-2 players).

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The duties never end for the managers of the Candlewick Inn, a comfy country cottage serving the cute critters of the Evergreen Forest. The days hum with the arrival of new guests eager to sample the rich desserts your restaurant is famous for. You’ll fill their little bellies to the brim before sending them off to a cozy suite for a pleasant night’s slumber. Unfortunately for them, it could be their last!

You see, the inn was built over a rift where unspeakable cosmic horrors can squeeze into our world. The only thing keeping these terrifying creatures from bursting free and devouring you (and literally everyone else) is a nightly meal of fuzzy forest-dwellers stuffed with their favourite sweet treats. This is the dark side of the job that they don’t mention in innkeeping college. Can you sate your monster’s drooling hunger each night, run the restaurant inn by day, and somehow keep your guests blissfully unaware of the danger looming below?

In Killing With Kindness, you work as a manager of the Candlewick, assigning staff to different areas of the hotel to perform actions. Each action is associated with one location (Lobby, Kitchen, Dining Room, Suites, and Basement) and one staff member (Concierge, Chef, Server, Bellhop, and Housekeeper). When you assign a staff member, you’ll get to perform two actions… one for the staff, and one for the location they’re in. Over the day you will welcome new guests, serve them delicious sweet treats, and make sure they’re comfortable in a room for the night. You’ll also deal with the inevitable messes and mishaps that are part of the job when mixing hospitality and interdimensional sacrifice. Generate as much kindness as you can with your activities, because if things get too terrifying around the hotel, your staff members might get incidentally gobbled up (which is no good for anyone, especially them). When dusk arrives, direct your horror to devour specific guests that will best satisfy its dark desires. Once full, it will slink away and a new evil monstrosity will creep into your care, with fresh preferences for tomorrow’s grim feast.

After three nights of work, one anxious manager will earn the most points and the dubious honour of the Candlewick Inn Employee of the Week. Can you satisfy both the hotel’s cheery unsuspecting guests and its lurking demonic denizens better than the competition?

—description from the designer

PIDG: Predictive Intelligence & Data Governance is a strategic economic game where players build income, invest in assets, and respond to shifting market conditions. Players do not move around a track; instead, they place worker tokens on board spaces that generate income based on dice rolls.

Each round begins with a Market Condition card that alters how the system functions for that round. A die is then rolled for the table, and players collect payouts from the spaces where they have tokens.

In turn order, each player takes one action, such as placing or moving a token, purchasing an investment or card, gambling, buying a PIDGY Coin, or passing.

Players manage four worker tokens across categories including Day Job, Side Hustles, Businesses, and Investments. Tokens generate money, cards, or PIDGY Coins depending on placement and market conditions.

Players may also play Inside Scoop cards to disrupt opponents or gain advantages. Virtue cards are used for alternate endgame outcomes.

The primary goal is to be the first player to collect the required number of PIDGY Coins, which scales by player count. The game can also end through alternate conditions triggered by Market cards, in which case the winner is determined by Virtue cards.

—description from the designer

My Puzzle Adventure, the puzzle-game that tells stories!

It is a collection of puzzles in which children live thrilling adventures. They look for the pieces to turn over one by one and discover an incredible story by putting the puzzle away. Will you succeed in the adventure?

Petits Detectives is the fourth opus of My Puzzle Adventure Collection.

Discover three new investigations with Jeppy, Nouki and Zoé, the heroes of the book "Au Vol-Oeuf!".

No me toques las tumbonas is a chaotic take-that card game set in a sun-drenched resort, where players fight dirty for control of the best beach loungers.

On your turn, you’ll play action cards to grab sunbeds, block opponents, steal towels, or unleash surprise effects. But beware: your rivals will do the same — and no one plays fair under the sun.

Simple rules, explosive interactions, and lots of player sabotage make this the perfect game for summer gatherings, game nights, and vengeful vacationers. The winner is the one with the most points... or the least burnt.

—description from the publisher

Cacagedón is a tactical take-that card game where baby players face off in a messy battle of poop, defense, and survival. Each baby controls a changing mat, four poop tokens, and a hand full of dirty tricks.

You’ll throw poop at rival mats, block attacks with clean diapers, and sabotage defenses to make others collapse under the pressure. Every turn is a chance to outwit your opponents — or drag them down with you.

Score points by staying in the game and by causing others to reach full diaper overload. Fast, chaotic, and delightfully disgusting, Cacagedón is the ultimate showdown of cunning babies and bad smells.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dilemmarama the Game: You Can Only Type With Your Nose or You Always Wear a Sweater Made of Bacon
description:
A light party game where players must choose between two ridiculous and unappealing options as presented to them on “dilemma cards.” This card game is based on the concept of “Would You Rather” and there is only one real rule: you HAVE to choose…

Pairs of cards are used to create dilemmas for the players. The game can be played in two different modes:
1. In the individual competitive option, you create dilemmas for the other players and make them as difficult as possible, trying to convince players that the choices are equally horrible and thus splitting their votes. Penalty points are awarded if the other players’ choices are not split evenly. The player with the fewest penalty points wins.
2. In the team option, you guess what your teammates choose and obtain as many points as possible. This mode also has a two player option.

The game includes two rule cards, 60 dilemma cards, one “board” card, and two cards with scissor marks showing that they are to be cut into ten pieces each, to create a total of 20 two-sided (dark blue vs turquoise) voting tokens.

Manticore is a 3-player climbing-shedding game where every hand, each player is restricted to playing only one type of meld: Sets, Runs, or Flushes. To facilitate this, it uses a unique deck of 47 cards with 10 ranks, 10 suits, and 3 colours.

Each hand, players will receive a hand of 15 cards and will bid for the right to be declarer, who plays alone against the other two players. The declarer receives an extra 2 cards into their hand, and gets their pick of the three types of melds. The other two players will choose their melds in turn.

The play of the game is similar to other climbing-shedding games, except players -- instead of matching meld type and length -- always play their own particular meld type and match length, while playing a higher value rank than the previous meld that they are beating.

The first player to shed out wins points for themself (declarer) or their team (defenders), and then a new hand is played where players will again be choosing a meld type based on their cards.

—description from the designer
Stack chairs but the twist is that you need to roll 2 dice which will tell you how to place the chairs on the stack.

Ask the right questions cleverly to deduce the correct combination of three clues that identifies the hidden animal!

You'll have to move through a random grid of masks, objects, and iconic animals to figure out who's hiding.

Goal: Create runs.

Draw, discard, and create runs of 4 cards (1, 2, 3, 4) of the same suit.

Watch out for special cards (thief, heist, joker).

Spierdolada is a unique card game for adults that reflects real life: fighting your own Fuck Ups. Whoever defeats them first wins! You start the game with three Fuck Ups, and your task is to go out and get rid of them. Read the Event cards, laugh at the "Fuck Off" lines, follow instructions, and award tokens. Green traits save your ass and make you get rid of unwanted cards, red ones just make you feel worse and deepen your life of loss.

boardgame: Камера! Мотор!: Промокарты сцен (Roll Camera!: Niza Gams Edition Shot Promo Cards)
description:
Promo cards are given as a gift for pre-ordering the Russian edition from Niza Gams.

Pack of five shot cards (legendary moments from Russian TV series):

     17ПЮ (Улицы разбитых фонарей / 2 сезон / 4 серия "Визит к доктору", "-С пюрешкой, с пюрешкой!")
     112 (Катина любовь / 90 серия, вырезенная сцена, "-Что с ней? -Рожает она. Хреново ей. А ребёнку ещё хуже!")
     96Ш (Яблоневый сад, "-Да она не может любить меня! -Да я люблю тебяяяя!")
     73ТЕ (Детективы / 83 серия "Мужское бельё", "-Я не резала её эти трусы, её мужские трусы!")
     43Н (Верю не верю / 8 серия "Предсказание", "Молодец, Катюха, метко стреляешь!")

boardgame: The Battle of the Aegates: March 10th 241BC
description:
The Battle of the Aegates is a scenario for He Hemetera, Mare Nostrum in which Rome and Carthage duel in the seas off the Aegates islands in what was the climax of the First Punic War.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Battle of Noryang: December 16th, 1598 – A scenario for "Those Who Seek Death"
description:
Battle of Noryang: a scenario for Those Who Seek Death in which the allied Korean and Chinese fleets Admiral Yi Sun Sin and Admiral Chen Lin engage the Japanese fleet under Admiral Shimazu Yoshihiro in the final battle of the Imjin War, December 1598

—description from the publisher

The animals need their favorite snacks!

Help them find them by memorizing your cards and turning them over as quickly as possible.

The fastest player to turn over the animal with the matching snack will score points and win this snack hunt!

To the rhythm of the refrain "Poom Poom Crunch!", you draw cards with mushrooms you want to devour.

But if you reveal a third bear, you transform and rush to grab the wooden spoon to snatch them all! The others then try their luck with the dice to leave you with nothing... The person (or bear) who has collected the most mushrooms after 3 rounds wins!

Sally forth into a living Arthurian world, a competitive sandbox teeming with knights and courtiers, sorcerers and warlords, giants and dragons – all drawn from over five hundred years of literature and lore. Deeds of derring-do await the worthy, but winning the famed seat known as the Siege Perilous will require more than a powerful sword arm. The knight who claims the chair will be an exemplar of both skill and virtue. Gird fast your harness, gather your arms and retinue, and forge a new legend – your own!

In this expansive (and expandable) Euro-style board game, 1 to 4 players will traverse 12 kingdoms that compose Arthur’s Britain, each kingdom containing unique interactable locations, including castles, churches, training towers, ports, markets, fountains, dungeons, and the famed Forest Sauvage. The map itself changes throughout the game, as new events arise, foreign campaigns become available, and Merlin appears with a task that needs doing.

As a player, you will choose from among 6 Player Characters: Dame Britomart, daughter of King Rience; Sir Claudin, son of the Gaulish tyrant; Sir Galleron, proud son of the Northern wilds; Dame Marfisa, warrior queen of the East; Sir Morien, hailing from distant Africa; Sir Priamus, descendent of Judas Maccabeus and Hector of Troy; Sir Sadok, former vassal of the foul King Mark; Sir Wigalois, a lost son of Camelot. Each character begins with a unique set of statistics, which you will endeavour to increase throughout play, plus a starting quest unique to them. From there, embark upon your knightly career as you see fit:

At one of 6 Tournament and Politick castles, test your Skills against one of 100 Courtiers and Knights to earn rewards, allies, and perhaps enemies.

Visit Churches to increase your Virtues, earn blessings, and heal wounds. Visit Training Towers to hone your Skills, earn spurs, and shed dishonour. Pass through a Market to earn coins and purchase one of 100 combat, court, horse, and nature assets to add to your equipage.

Take ship and seek adventure and glory abroad on one of 20 Foreign Campaigns: accept a diplomatic mission to Rome, destroy a sorceress’s deadly bower in Ireland, take the pilgrim’s road in Spain.

Undertake one of 20 intrigues for Merlin: solve the mystery of the Hound Knight, investigate a ship of Solomon, explore an ancient city beneath the waves.

Pay fealty to the High King Arthur at Camelot and be granted one of 20 Quests. Should you meet with success, you’ll choose from Earthly, Divine, or Wisdom rewards. Sanctify the ill-favoured Chapel Perilous, venture into the Otherworld to rescue a kidnapped damsel, end the curse of the lady who bathes in the blood of innocents.

Drop your reins and let your fate lead you to strange adventures and deadly peril in one of 30 Forest Sauvage encounters: visit a faerie market, decide the fate of a wounded giant, track and slay the ferocious Cath Palug, and perhaps receive a test – and if ye be worthy – a mighty gift from the Lady of the Lake.

Win and wield one of 22 Treasures – magical weapons, artifacts, creatures, and conveyances: the ancient spear of Lugh, the Chariot of Morgan Mwynfawr, the lord of hounds Cavall.

End your turn by visiting a Fountain for healing and reward, a Dungeon to imprison captured villains, or a Contest Site to test your mettle against your fellow players.

Earn Arthur’s favour to win one of 40 Titles to strengthen your claim to the Siege Perilous.

And that is just the base game. Undertake one of 6 Legendary Tales that introduce new challenges, characters (including new Starting Characters), and treasures: endeavour – collaboratively – to keep Arthur’s kingdom intact during a time of rebellions and invasions, wage an epic war against the emperor of Rome, thwart the nefarious plans of Morgan Le Fay, meet the challenges of Gloriana, the Faerie Queen.

Every element of the game – each character you meet, treasure you claim, weapon, follower, mount or animal companion you acquire – has been hand-painted using traditional Mediaeval techniques, pigments, and gold leaf by renowned artist Jay Johnstone – more than 250 original paintings in all, each of which conveys additional lore in its details.

Likewise, every narrative piece is derived from extensive research of Arthurian literature and adjacent texts—from Irish myth and ancient Welsh folklore, to the historical chronicles of Geoffrey of Monmouth, the courtly romances of Chrétien de Troyes, and the work of Thomas Malory. The game’s story elements incorporate mediaeval tales from Britain, France, Germany, Italy, and the Netherlands, all the way up through the Elizabethan allegory of Edmund Spenser’s The Faerie Queene.

But, in the end, this is your tale to weave, one threaded with each decision you make, each die you roll, as you explore the realm, increase your virtues and skills, and, ultimately, write a new legend by claiming the fabled – and deadly – Siege Perilous.
Each card lists three clues about a random object, person, landmark, or animal.  The fewer clues you need to hear to correctly guess the answer, the more points you score.

boardgame: Host Your Own Murder Mystery: Murder at the Pyramids
description:
Host your own murder mystery night! This box contains all you'll ever need for a night of adventure, fun, and detective work!

Set up your home to mimic a real murder scene and then invite your friends and/or family over to solve it. The host guide has a full dress code suggestion for all characters to fit the Egyptian theme in the 1960s time period.

Players will have 3 rounds of clue reviews and will take notes to try and deduce who the culprit is. After accusations have been submitted, the sealed envelope is opened to reveal who has committed the murder and how. Awards are given out at the end for the Greatest Detective, Best Costume, and Best Acting.

A maximum of 12 hunters (2 for each player) attempt to capture three types of prey (lion, elephant, and jaguar), choosing their direction as they go through the savannah. Instead of a die, a wheel is used that rotates via a gear underneath. However, the player declares their intended direction BEFORE rolling the wheel's pointer, so the game could go awry... not the one you intended!

—user summary

boardgame: Dawn of Iron: Battle of Cherbourg Expansion
description:
This battle pack expansion covers the famous duel between the USS Kearsarge and CSS Alabama at the Battle of Cherbourg in 1864. Inside you will find:

History & Maps of the Battle (3pgs)
1x Historical Scenario
1x General Play Mission (inspired by the battle)
4x Ship Cards (Including the CSS Alambama and her predacesor CSS Sumter, USS Hatteras, which was sunk by the Alabama, and the USS Kearsarge. Contains formats for A4 and US Letter paper sizes.)
6x New Upgrade Cards (A4 and US Letter paper sizes)

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Jingle Bell Roll: PAXU Promo Card
description:
Fruitcake 
7 • At the end of the game if all other players have at least one Gift Card left in their hand.

Players take turns matching colors and numbers to play cards from their hand while each player works toward completing their own secret card. Familiar mechanics and simple rules make the game easy to learn and well suited for casual gatherings and game nights.

This micro-expansion adds 30 Providence cards and a reference Guide card to Veiled Fate to deepen strategic options in every play. Providence cards are shuffled into the main Fate deck and act as alternative fate cards that can affect outcomes during quests and other game events. Unlike standard Fate cards, many Providence cards introduce new effects or force additional interactions — for example, prompting players to play additional fate cards or altering how outcomes are resolved — adding fresh tactical layers to each Age. The Guide card helps players integrate these cards smoothly into the base game’s mechanics.

This micro-expansion introduces six quest-sized Adventure cards. At the start of each Age, these cards are placed in unused quest regions, where they create new opportunities and risks. Players who plan carefully can exploit powerful bonuses, while poor timing may result in costly penalties.

First offered during the Veiled Fate: Tribunal campaign.
12 new cards to add more variety (6 age cards and 6 city cards) that were created with the help of backers during the Veiled Fate: Tribunal Kickstarter campaign.

2 new role cards + 3 cards that support one of the role cards to expand your gameplay options when playing with the Tribunal expansion of Veiled Fate.

Originally offered during the Scales of Fate campaign.

boardgame: Scales of Fate: Community Cards Vol. 1
description: 6 additional age cards - created with help of backers during the Scales of Fate Kickstarter campaign - add game variety.

A fast-paced social dice game and race to 201 points - and all you need is five dice!

Play bold and keep rolling to score big, but don’t roll high and bust.  Wait for the right moment and steal your opponent’s big roll, but play smart or they will get you back!

Casual, strategic, and easy to learn, Danger Dice is great for families or a party.

—description from the designer

Memory Mayhem is a real-time, chaotic twist on classic Memory. Using a standard deck of cards, 2–6 players race to find matching pairs in a messy, overlapping spread. Mayhem unfolds in real time, you'll need quick hands and a sharp memory to keep up.

Players flip two cards at once, announce matches, and collect pairs in front of themselves. Unmatched cards are flipped back immediately, forcing players to remember what they’ve seen under constant pressure. The game ends when all cards are claimed, and the player with the most pairs wins.

Perfect for parties, families, or quick competitive play, Memory Mayhem turns a familiar game into a frantic test of memory, speed, and coordination.

—description from the designer

Carl Linné is a strategic board game set in the early 18th century, where players take on the roles of natural historians inspired by the work of Carl Linnaeus and his contemporaries. Through exploring distant regions, collecting and researching specimens, cultivating botanical gardens, corresponding with scientific institutions, and publishing influential books, players gradually build knowledge and reputation.
