By carefully managing actions through a unique lens system and balancing fieldwork with scholarly study, players compete to contribute most to the emerging system of taxonomy and leave a lasting mark on the history of natural science.

—description from the publisher

Operation Overlord is a deep, historically grounded strategic wargame that simulates the Normandy invasion and the critical battles that followed from June to August 1944. One player commands the Allied SHAEF forces, planning and executing the largest amphibious operation in history, while the opposing player takes the role of Oberbefehlshaber West, defending the Atlantic Wall and attempting to delay the Allied advance long enough to alter the course of the war.

Rather than focusing on tactical skirmishes, Operation Overlord operates at the operational–strategic level, where timing, logistics, intelligence, and command structure are decisive. Players maneuver divisions and army corps across a detailed map of Normandy, manage supply networks and reinforcements, execute historical and fictional operations, and influence battles through doctrine, supports, and event cards.

Each month begins with high-level planning: the Allied player secretly schedules strategic and special operations, while the German player designates key cities as Festungen, to be held at all costs. Weekly turns then unfold through intelligence gathering, supply allocation, reinforcement arrivals, and alternating unit activations that combine maneuver and combat into a tense, fluid system. Fog of war is maintained through hidden unit values and simultaneous combat card reveals, ensuring constant uncertainty and meaningful decision-making.

Victory is not measured simply by territory, but by time and consequences. The German player is unlikely to drive the Allies back into the sea—but every week gained has far-reaching implications for morale, resources, and other fronts of the war. Likewise, an Allied breakthrough ahead of schedule can dramatically reshape history. Each scenario and campaign outcome includes historically reasoned consequences that frame the result within the broader context of World War II.

With multiple scenarios (June, July, August, and a full campaign), robust asymmetry, and a strong emphasis on planning and operational art, Operation Overlord offers a demanding and rewarding experience for players seeking a serious, historically informed wargame.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The players take turns to roll and move. If a player lands on a letter space, they must guess a word that begins with the letter of that space, from the area indicated by their die roll:
On a 1, a first name beginning with the corresponding letter must be guessed.
On a 2, a profession. 
On a 3, a place (city or village).
On a 4, an animal.
On a 5, a country (or landscape).
On a 6, a plant (flower, tree, shrub, etc.).

If the player cannot name a correct word within 10 seconds, they must move their piece back to the next unlabeled space. 
If they name a correct word, they may remain on the letter space. The next player then takes their turn. 
If a player lands on the sun space numbered 1, they must find all six words beginning with the letter they were previously standing on, within one minute. If he succeeds, he may advance to the next A.
If a player lands on the sun square numbered 2, he must count backward from 20 to 1. If he makes no mistake, he may move one square in front of the first player.
The first player to reach the finish wins the game.

—user summary

In Tea & Rum, strive for victory by serving the Crown or by becoming the greatest King of Pirates!

Within 5 rounds, upgrade your resources and carefully place and un-place your workers to fulfill your ambitions.

Will you prove your allegiance to the Crown, or will you join the rebellion and rule the seas? Be careful to keep your intentions hidden: angering the Crown or the people could compromize your chances.

At the end of the game, only two players will win the game… and two will be defeated.

—description from the publisher

ATR 2 brings 100 new cards so you can keep the fun going. It's the perfect gift for those who laugh their heads off playing ATR (and it helps you pretend you don't understand a thing).

Add this expansion to your ATR game and discover new combinations, unexpected situations, and even more laughs about farts and snot to share with family or friends.

NOTE: This is an expansion; you need the original ATR to play.

Before the trick-taking phase begins, players must carefully construct and arrange their hands, as they may not rearrange the cards once play starts. During play, you can only play cards from either end of your hand—the leftmost or rightmost card—adding a clever layer of planning and strategy.

There’s no must-follow or may-follow suit rule here, setting it apart from traditional trick-taking games. Victory points are earned for winning tricks, but at the end of each round, a “deck enhancement” phase takes place: in a draft starting from the players with the fewest points, powerful new cards are added to your deck for future rounds. This creates an engaging push-and-pull between winning now and building up your strength for future success.

Key Features:


     Unique hand management by playing only the cards at either end of your hand.
     No suit-following restrictions.
     Strategic deck-building via a draft system, but with a twist: losers pick first!
     Fast and accessible gameplay, great for families and gamers alike.


—description from the designer

Forecasts is a 3–4 player trick-taking game of prediction, timing, and shared pressure.
At the start of each round, players secretly forecast whether the final temperature will end Hot or Cold. Over a series of tricks, players use skillful card play to push temperature cubes between Hot, Neutral, and Cold, gradually building pressure toward one outcome or disrupting their opponents’ plans.

Winning tricks does not score points directly. Instead, players must decide when to create uncertainty and when to lock in a forecast, using the shared temperature state to their advantage. Neutral conditions can delay scoring, while well-timed plays convert pressure into points.
After 10 tricks, players score based on how many cubes end in the bucket they forecast, with a small bonus for correctly predicting the dominant outcome. Over four rounds, the most accurate and adaptable meteorologist wins.

Forecasts rewards strong trick-taking fundamentals, careful timing, and reading both the table and your opponents. It plays in 30–45 minutes and offers high interaction with low randomness and strong recoverability.

Why Should I Play This Game?

    You should play Forecasts if you like:
    Trick-taking games where winning the right tricks matters more than winning the most
    Prediction games that reward reading the table, not guessing
    Interactive games where every player affects a shared outcome
    Tight scoring with real tension, not runaway blowouts
    Games where denial, timing, and subtle pressure matter more than luck


Forecasts is not about explosive turns or sudden reversals. It’s about building momentum, managing uncertainty, and knowing when to commit. Skilled players can recover from mistakes, outplay opponents late, and influence outcomes even when they are not winning tricks themselves.
If you enjoy games like Whist, Spades, or Hearts, but want something that adds strategic forecasting and shared-state manipulation, Forecasts offers a fresh, competitive twist.

—description from the designer

GhostStrike is a ghost-themed area control and color chain building game. It's a card game where you score points by creating color chains or stealing them from other players. You advance through levels, and whoever has the most points at the end wins. It's a game for 2 to 4 players with a variable playing time, as you can decide how long you want the game to be.

—description from the designer

Forging Dynasty is an area-control strategy war game for two to four players set in a romanticised version of China's Warring States period. Players take on the roles of powerful warlords, each commanding unique factions as they struggle to expand their territorial influence across a highly contested map.

The core gameplay focuses on the tension between military conquest, shifting alliances, and tactical deception. Throughout the game, players primarily use action selection and card play mechanisms to prevail over their enemies as they race to gain the most victory points through winning battles, completing objectives, and controlling regions.

To succeed, players must manage resources, command armies, fortify provinces, develop technologies, and execute schemes designed to outwit and outmanoeuvre their opponents. Only the warlord who can best balance martial strength with strategic wisdom will emerge as the ruler of the kingdom.

—description from the publisher

This word association game, both simple and clever, tests your ability to synchronize with your partners. The concept? Find a common word between two cards without speaking and grab the string as soon as you find an association, hoping that your partner on the other end of the string has the same word in mind! If not, start over with the two words that didn’t match! An intuitive mechanic that guarantees laughter and unique moments of connection.
With its 54 cards and endless combinations, every game is a new adventure. The game offers two modes: competitive (where the first to win 5 cards wins) or cooperative (to challenge the limits of your complicity and get the best score). Ideal for breaking the ice, developing listening skills and teamwork, or simply having a great time, Linked Mind is perfect for all audiences, starting at 8 years old."

A few ingenious villagers have come up with brand new ways to travel. Surely these will come in handy for delivering goods…and gifts!

Exotic Transports 3-card expansion is shuffled in alongside the base deck and introduces 3 new transports as well as 3 new gifts for the dragon.

By igniting the ancient beacons, you can wow the dragon with a beautiful array of dazzling flames!

Beacons 3-card expansion is shuffled in alongside the base deck and introduces beacons. Whenever you deliver through a road touching a beacon, the beacon flips between unlit and lit. Unlit beacons cost you points at the end of the game. Some beacon cards also feature blockages that prevent you from approaching from a given direction.

Set a Watch: Winterlands is a realm expansion pack to the cooperative adventure puzzle game series.

Winterlands can be mixed and matched with any standalone Set a Watch game and features the same gameplay with brand new creatures, locations and challenges.  It also has everything you need to snap it into any Doomed Run as an alternate Realm 5 - introducing some branching difficulty choices to your campaign.

Winterlands  adds a brand new creature deck, unhallowed bosses, locations, and a new adventurer—the Northman.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Set a Watch (Rock Manor Games)

description:
Food Chain is a trick-taking game for 3 or 4 players about the food chain in Japan.

The game uses a pyramid type deck (two 2s, three 3s, etc.) It is a may follow game, where the lowest number in a trick wins. The non-winning players add the card they played to their food chain. Then, the non-winning players each pass a card from their hand to the winning player to add to their food chain.

The game ends after 4 tricks.  The players then check the points they earn or lose based upon their food chains, and the person with the most points wins.
publishers: Nagoya University Simulation Game Research Laboratory (名古屋大学シミュレーションゲーム研究室)

boardgame: Artistic License: The Really BAD Drawing Game!
description: In Artistic License, two teams compete over three rounds to correctly guess the most drawings, like in Pictionary. The kicker is that as the rounds progress, the drawer's task becomes more difficult. In round two, the drawer must use their off-hand (ie, a right-handed player would draw with their left hand). In round three, the drawer must wear a mask that covers their eyes, meaning that they have to draw blind.
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description: Naja naja is a tense two-player, duel-style climbing card game inspired by the classic Japanese "Daifugo" (President) mechanic—ideal for people who want a strategic head-to-head experience. Each player aims to be the first to play all of their cards by matching numbers or playing runs of the same suit. Passing during a round allows your hand to be enhanced, setting you up for more powerful plays in future rounds—so losing a battle can set up a comeback! Unique to Naja naja, card strengths shift dynamically round-to-round according to the "trend" set by unplayed cards, keeping each duel fresh and requiring players to read both their opponent’s hand and the flow of the game. Even after a round is lost, the game continues, letting you carry over your improved hand and adapt your tactics. Outthink, outlast, and outmaneuver your rival in this evolving two-player showdown.

Frootokri is a light strategy card game where you are collecting fruits in your 'tokri' (term for basket in Hindi) to score points based on the scoring conditions of each basket, all while managing rocks being thrown at you by others.

On your turn you are performing any one of 3 types of actions.

     Take a tokri card
     Pick two fruit cards and place them in your tokri
     Gather all the rocks and either throw them in other's tokris or discard all of them for points.


The game also contains bounty cards that everyone is racing to claim first by completing the conditions.

It is a fun card game that can be enjoyed with friends and family of nearly all ages.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Invincible: The Card Game – Guardians of the Globe Exclusives Promo Pack
description: Contains all of the crowdfunding exclusives for Invincible: The Card Game. These are intended for owners of the retail edition as they were included in the Invincible: The Card Game (Kickstarter Edition).

boardgame: Tigers & Beasts: Counterattack at Staszow-Stopnica – August 1944, Poland
description:
The massive Russian offensive had arrived in Poland by August, 1944. It had advanced hundreds of miles reaching the limits of how far it could go. Units had established a bridgehead at the Vistula, but the Germans were fanatical in their defenses creating fast moving battles as their panzer units went from one location to another to stop them!

When the Russians reached Staszow, alarm bells went off for the Germans and their major counterattack began using the new King Tiger Pz VIIb "wonder" weapon of the 501st Battalion. The unit has just completed training and rushed to the front.  
It would face the mighty Russian, JS-2, "Beast killer".

At Stopnica, Russian units had penetrated, the 3rd Panzer Corps raced to meet them head-on in the crazy, see-saw battle for the city. 
It would involve hundreds of tanks.

—description from the publisher

The gang rides again! With the core game, all expansions, and promos from over 10+ years of train heisting, the Colt Express: Complete Collection is a haul of loot packaged into an all-in-one storage box for easy setup and a clean getaway.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count
Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
This train ain’t big enough for the… 8 of us!? It’s an all-out standoff aboard the Union Pacific Express, where the law’s most-wanted cross paths for the score of the century. Loot, shoot, and scramble your way through derailing train cars as you nab anything not bolted to the floor.

Bandits will use their powerful action cards to scheme against their rivals, planning their turns ahead of time before a quickdraw round of action that’s sure to send more than a few sore losers tumbling overboard and behind bars. Outwit, outmaneuver, and outgun your opponents to be the last one standing with a train full of treasure all to yourself in the Colt Express Card Game.

—description from the publisher

Anekdot is a narrative card game and questions designed to create conversations and share personal stories, strengthening family and friendly ties by reviving precious memories, often in Dutch or French, with various themes. He plays with questions that lead participants to tell anecdotes, with simple betting or bluffing mechanics to participate, even without experience.

How it works (in general):
    Question Cards: The game offers cards with questions on topics like family, past experiences, dreams, etc.
    Anecdotes share: Each player tells a personal story in response to the question drawn.
    Point System (sometimes): Some games include a betting or bluff system where you bet on the right answer or try to guess the story of others.
    Objective: Create a link, discover new things about your loved ones, and have fun.

Key Points :
    Ideal for groups: Works well for 2 to 12 players, perfect for evenings with friends or family.
    Easy to learn: Simple rules, quick parts (20-30 min).
    Various themes: Some games offer editions with more spicy or specific themes (e.g., meetings).
    Alternative to trivia games: Focuses on stories and creativity rather than general culture.

In short, if you are looking for a game to host a party and get to know the people around you better, a game like "Anekdot" is a great option.

—description from the publisher

Gridmatch is a solo puzzle card game that challenges players to build a complete 4×4 grid by placing cards according to strict symbol-matching rules.

Each card features a unique arrangement of symbols on its edges. Cards may only be placed into the grid when adjacent edges match correctly, requiring careful planning, spatial reasoning, and forward thinking. As the grid fills, available placement options narrow, forcing players to weigh short-term opportunities against long-term consequences.

With a limited deck and constantly shifting constraints, Gridmatch offers a tight and satisfying puzzle experience. No two games play out the same way, and success depends on recognising patterns, anticipating future placements, and avoiding dead ends.

Designed for quick sessions, Gridmatch can be played in approximately ten to fifteen minutes, making it ideal for travel, coffee breaks, or quiet solo play. It appeals to fans of solitaire card games, logic puzzles, and abstract strategy challenges that reward thoughtful play over luck.

boardgame: Psychonics: The Science fiction supplement to Sahipkıran
description:
THE VOID IS NOT EMPTY. IT IS ARAF.

Psychonics is a comprehensive science-fiction expansion for the Sahipkıran: Cannon & Rifle wargaming system.
It transports the "black powder" tactical lethality of the core game into a crumbling, metaphysical future where faith and firepower are the only defense against the void.

It expands the core rules into a far-future setting shaped by psychonic phenomena, alien archaeology, and decaying high technology, while preserving Sahipkıran’s fast, abstract skirmish logic. The supplement is miniature-agnostic and fully compatible with common 28–32mm sci-fi ranges, including Games Workshop miniatures.

Psychonics is designed for small to medium skirmishes, narrative play, and scenario-driven balance rather than list optimization.

What this supplement adds:

     A flexible sci-fi framework playable with any miniatures
     Distinct factions: The Genetically-Optimized Korps (GÖKs), Territorial Defence Forces, Solar Gendarmarie, Planetary Mobile Companies, the Eloi, Morlochs, Entomons, and Chitinsans
     Psychonic phenomena that influence morale, positioning, and risk
     Solar Signs: ancient xeno-archaeological sites with battlefield effects
     Solar Gates and interstellar infrastructure as objectives and hazards
     Optional rules for indoor combat, buildings, and structural damage
     Scenario generators covering raids, investigations, rebellions, and special operations
     Planetary, system, and anomaly generators for narrative play and campaigns


All additional systems are modular and optional. 
Psychonics can be used for a single one-off skirmish or as the foundation for a loose, narrative campaign without heavy bookkeeping.

Design philosophy:
Psychonics does not attempt to simulate modern or futuristic combat in detail. Instead, it treats technology, psychic powers, and alien environments as pressure systems that complicate decision-making.
Balance is achieved primarily through scenarios, objectives, time pressure, and environmental instability rather than through fine-grained unit costs or special rules bloat.

If you enjoy fast turns, clear outcomes, and narrative friction over exhaustive simulation, this supplement is written for you.

What you need to play

     Sahipkıran: Cannon & Rifle (core rules)
     A handful of sci-fi miniatures
     Dice (d6), terrain, and a standard skirmish table
     A measuring tape


No proprietary models or accessories are required.

Tactical Command Console:
Don't want to flip through tables? 
The interactive web-based generators for the game are available at the Tactical Command Console. You can access them directly via browser to instantly create mission scenarios, planets, star systems, solar signs, and solar gates.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Able, Baker, Charlie: WW2 Squad Level Combat
description:
Charlie, come in! Where are you?
A light machine gun has your squad pinned down. Two of your men have gone out of the fight and the rest are ducked down in cover. All hell’s breaking loose and everyone is looking to you for the answer. Right about now you’re wondering what the heck you’ve gotten yourself into.

Welcome to Able, Baker, Charlie!

Able, Baker, Charlie were the call signs for the soldiers when the Squad was broken down into smaller Teams. It helped identify them on the radio and helped keep them alive. Now that’s your job! 
Able, Baker, Charlie is WW2 Squad level combat at its best.
No flipping a card to see who moves first or telling you when to shoot. Instead, we use the Two Hour Wargames Reaction System, top of the line since 2005. 
How your Squad performs, if they survive, and if they achieve their Missions is up to you.

Inside you’ll find:

     140 color counters (70 double sided) representing American, British, German and Russian Squads with LMGs as well.
     Two color Battle Boards to do your fighting on.
     Rules that are quick and easy to learn yet give realistic results.
     A complete Campaign system that tie your Missions together.
     Start your tour of duty as a Private, Jr. NCO or NCO and lead your own Squad.
     Rise in Rank as you have successful Missions.
     All this and much more.


Able, Baker, Charlie is designed to build a Story, your Story!

Now pack up Soldier, you ship out today!

FYI - How is this different than our Origins Award nominated WW2 rules- NUTS?
Smoother game mechanics. 
Can be played on the tabletop or the Battle Boards with counters or minis you may already have. 
No having to track Duck Back status. 
Already built into the smoother shooting rules. 
Compatible with NUTS so you can go back and forth as desired.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Карточные битвы ПЧК: Стартовый набор (Card Battles CCD: Starter Pack)
description:
Card Battles is a collectible card game where the mighty heroes of Ponia clash for honor, justice, or their own selfish ambitions. They perform heroic feats, summon brave Allies and Companions to their side, and seize powerful artifacts.

This is the starter set "Sphinkterion vs. Larisa." Their friendship has not yet been broken by conflict, and they are ready to train in battle! The valiant prince leads his band of warriors, while Larisa the Rat commands swarms of beasts.

This set is designed to introduce you to the game, help you learn its core mechanics and rules, and play your very first matches!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: 100 single sided cards featuring one multiple choice question per card.

How many professions starting with the letter "M" can you recall in a minute? Who will come up with the longest name beginning with "U" or the shortest drink name starting with "P"? Who will be the first to remember an item costing less than 5 rubles, or something – say, beginning with "C" – that you might find in a village? Think fast, before your opponents beat you to it!

"Think It Up! Belarus" is a party and family game that will delight both children and adults alike. Each turn, you draw a card with a letter and a category, then put your quick thinking to the test by trying to be the first to recall a fitting word. To win, you don’t need to be the most knowledgeable – you just need to be the fastest!

A staple of any long road trip is stopping for spicy food along the way. And of course, when you’re eating delicious food non-stop like this, your stomach is bound to start rumbling! Haha!

In each round, players must simultaneously play one card from their hand. The deck includes "Fart Left," "Fart Right," "Breathe," and "Reflect."

If you choose to "Breathe" in a round where a friend plays a "Fart" card toward you, you’ll end up inhaling that stinky gas! If you get hit by 3 cards, you’re definitely passing out.

In this game, you must strive to be the first person to take 3 refreshing breaths of fresh air, or be the last one standing after surviving the toxic air mass from your friends' intestines inside the car.

Miracle Makers is a Christian-based card game designed to bring faith, fun, and family together. Collect the most Miracles to win, but beware - sneaky snakes are out to steal your Miracles! You will need to protect them with the help of Angels and Holy Cards.

GAME OBJECTIVE: Collect the most MIRACLES to win — but watch out for sneaky SNAKES! Protect your Miracles with ANGELS and HOLY CARDS. The player with the most Miracles at the end of the round scores 30 points. The game continues until one player reaches 300 points.

Each player has a hand of 5 cards. 4 cards from the draw pile at the beginning of the game will be known as the "On the Cross" cards. On your turn create a sum of 3 or 7 by using a single card from your hand with a single card “On the Cross” to create a Miracle. Players take the two cards that created a Miracle and make a “Miracles Pile” next to you. Continue to criss-cross your Miracles. Replenish the “On the Cross” cards and draw back up to 5 cards in your hand.

Miracles can be stolen by "Snake" cards! Players whose  Miracle is being stolen can protect it by playing "Angel" and "Holy" cards. When the game ends, count your Miracles and any Bonus Cards.

In Relic Rush, you are a wizard controlling an avatar inside a magical maze. You don't just move your avatar through the maze; you cast spells to modify the maze throughout the game. Your goal is to outplay rival wizards, collect relics, and be the first to reach the Oracle at the center of the board.

How to Play (See the full instructions, under the Files tab on this page.)

Each turn starts with a dice roll for Action Points. You spend these points to play cards from a deck of over 200 spells.

Not only does every game start with a fresh maze, it is changed by your and your rivals' spells, in your race to the next Relic tile.

These spells drive the game's progress, allowing you to traverse the maze, modify it to open a path for yourself or to block your opponents, and to interrupt your opponents in the most creative of ways. There are 2 Portal tiles and 2 Volcano tiles on the board, which can be moved around the board throughout the game, by any player who rolls a 6.

When you end a turn on a Relic tile, you flip it over to its "Enchanted" side and pick up a Relic card. This reveals a new board element — you might find a bonus like mana or extra cards, or a penalty tile that impedes your progress.

If you don't use all your action points, they can turn into mana. You need to save up mana to win, but you can also spend it during the game as bonus action points, or use it to launch direct attacks on other players, flip hidden tunnels and more.

To Win

To claim victory, you or your team must complete three tasks:


     Collect Relics: Reach a target number of Relic cards (4 to 12) based on the player count. 
     Gather Mana: Reach the maximum limit of 10 mana tokens. 
     Reach the Oracle: Once you have your relics and mana, end your turn on the Oracle tile.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Classic of Mountains and Seas: The Card Game
description:
Classic of Mountains and Seas: The Card Game is adapted from the Ming Dynasty card game Madiao Pai. Madiao Pai consists of 40 cards. Each player draws eight cards initially, while the remaining eight are placed face down in the center on the board. The game is divided into two stages: playing cards to take tricks and entering the scoring phase. The name Madiao derives from Ma (horse), referring to betting chips, and Diao (to settle), meaning the resolution of bets—hence the combination Madiao. Madiao Pai significantly influenced modern boardgames like Mahjong.

In Classic of Mountains and Seas: The Card Game, you take on the role of an ancient clan leader, guiding your tribe to survive and thrive in the fantastical world depicted in the Classic of Mountains and Seas. Through strategic cardplay, you will gather vital resources—Water, Copper, and Grain—essential for your clan's sustenance, while also enduring unavoidable Damage. At the same time, you can seek blessings from six types of deities: Clan Spirits, Nature Gods, Malevolent Beasts, Auspicious Beasts, Legends, and the Sun & Moon. At the end of the game, the leader who has guided their clan to achieve the highest score will claim ultimate victory!

—description from the publisher
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

boardgame: Landmarks: Dice Tower 2025 Promo Map Cards
description:
Originally available as part of B Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

This promo pack adds three new map cards for Landmarks.

boardgame: Vegetable Stock: Dice Tower 2025 Promo Cards
description:
Originally available as part of D Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

Adds a new set of Market Cards that can be used in place of the current Market Cards during setup.

​Shroudfall is a tabletop game that is focused on providing an exciting skirmish level battle experience for two players. The game is balanced with a competitive spirit in mind, but the core rules are still incredibly easy to pick up. ​​​​​
​​​Over five game rounds you will take control of warbands that compete for land, strategic objectives and most of all for Essence crystals. Your warband will be led by an Essence Weaver, a powerful character model which brings unique abilities and playstyle to the tabletop.

​​​​Games are played on a 36 by 36 inch battlefield, with alternating activations keeping both players involved throughout the match. What makes Shroudfall stand out is how the battlefield itself is part of your strategy.

Players bring terrain and scenario cards as part of their lists, shaping the board and objectives before the first move is even made. That means: Every game presents new challenges, and no two battles play out the same way.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The Wixárika Soñadora represents a dreaming figure inspired by the spiritual traditions of the Wixárika people, in which dreams and visions are understood as paths of knowledge, responsibility, and transformation.

Rather than embodying a specific character or myth, the Wixárika Soñadora symbolizes an initiatory state: a journey through inner trials, encounters, and thresholds that must be crossed to regain balance and awareness. Her progression is not driven by domination, but by endurance, timing, and the ability to read opposing forces.

In Oniria, this Soñadora translates these concepts into gameplay through a solitary role facing multiple adversaries, where success depends on anticipation, restraint, and a deep understanding of the path ahead.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C32 – Run For It!
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C32 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Material needed: Prison Outbreak.
A nasty surprise was waiting for you in the prison: a pair of Abominations is now running after you. Your team has to pass the security gates and the zombie-filled cells to get to the exit. What are you waiting for? RUN!

Designed by Sean McDonald

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C33 – Medical Mumbo Jumbo
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C33 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Material needed: Prison Outbreak.
Not so long ago, having appendicitis was an easy affair. Now, it's a nightmare. If you want to save your teammate, you'll have to go through a medical mumbo jumbo!

Designed by Tim White

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C34 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Material needed: Season 1
Ned's uncle used to store items of various interest, but above all he had a boat your team could use to leave the city. First, however, you have to find the keys...and why Ned's uncle didn't use it for himself!

Designed by Matt Turnbull

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C35 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Material needed: Season 1.
You found a solid, build-to-last haven for your team. Everything was going fine until...the dividing wall to the condos next door fell as you were asleep. Wake up and find a weapon, quick!

Designed by Paul Murray

boardgame: Dragon Eclipse: Dice Tower 2025 Promo Pack
description:
Originally available as part of B Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

A pack of six new Advancement B Action cards with two copies each of - 
Aura of Renewal
Mystic Barrage
Rapid Sequence

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A18 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue.

Free Mission A18

Santa Hunting
Christmas came and went. It was gloomy, clammy, and the pudding was overcooked. We decided to fight back, as post-Christmas blues were threatening to overwhelm us. We must settle the score with our bad Christmas. Santa has forgotten us? Revenge! It’s time to go Santa hunting!

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A19 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue.

Free Mission A19

Psychiatrics
The first part of our plan worked just fine, but the second, easier part of our plan became an epic failure. We left our cars near a psychiatric hospital, which seemed almost intact and ready for plunder. We thought it would be easy to find some medical stuff here. Just as we were approaching, we woke a zombie horde. We decided to face it in a place of our choosing, somewhere easy to defend: the psychiatric hospital...

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A20 – Back in Town
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A20 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Zombicide first edition.

Free Mission A20

Back in town
It’s been months since we came here. We explored the whole place, zombicided everything, then moved on. Now we're back, and I wonder what awaits us. I remember that I left two pairs of socks, almost new... somewhere. Let's explore this place!

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A16 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for prison outbreak

Free Mission A21

Hammam
I have counted. I can prove it! It's been exactly ONE YEAR! since my last hot bath. Don't misunderstand: I'm not dirty and stinking like some zombie. I wash myself, but there is some difference between a hot bath and a cold shower in a derelict bathroom. So, this time, I've decided to head for a nearby hammam. Who's with me?

Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A22 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue.

Free Mission A22

Corpse piles
This block seems untouched and full of promise. We have seen an armory, a garage, some interesting shops, and nothing has been plundered yet! And the damn hospital is why. Emergency plans required the inhabitants to gather here, and lots of people followed the plan with obvious consequences: zombies everywhere... We are going to have a hell of a day.

Finally, the young penguins can also enjoy the delicious sushi. They just have to be careful not to get any bones on their plates! In this fast-paced dice game, you try to collect the most points, but all bones result in negative points.

—description from the publisher (translated)
family: Series: The Chicken Family of Zoch
Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description:
Unicorn fans: Attention! In this game, a group of adventurous friends set off in search of unicorns. Armed with imagination and lots of joy, they dive into a colorful world of magic.

Roll both dice, then everyone searches for the card that matches the conditions shown. The first player to find the correct card keeps it. Whoever has the most cards at the end wins.

In Pick a Chicken, each chicken has a preferred seating order: Hens like to sit next to each other. The rooster won‘t put up with any competition around him, chicks only feel comfortable
in pairs, and the mother hen just wants some peace and quiet.
Your goal is to arrange the poultry in the chicken coop so they score the most points.

On your turn, take 1 face-up card from the centre of the table and swap it with 1 facedown card in your chicken coop. Place the card you removed from your chicken coop face down next to the other cards in the centre of the table. Important: Neither you nor any of the other players are allowed to look at the front of this card! Place the card you took from the centre of the table face up in the slot of your chicken coop that just became available.
Once cards are face up in your chicken coop, you can no longer move them.

The game ends when all 12 cards in all players‘ chicken coops are face up. All players count up the points for each card type in their chicken coop.

—description from the publisher

Solve a realistic murder case at home!

—description from the publisher

Plot: 
The lead actor in the blockbuster film "Shadows of Deceit" is found dead. This is the second death connected to the film, and the police suspect something mysterious is happening behind the scenes. Suspicion spreads among Hollywood's elite - is the film cursed, or is there a killer on the loose?

What's inside a case?

Each case contains a folder with around 40 different pieces of evidence in the mail. In addition to the evidence, there are five envelopes to be opened at different times as you get deeper into the investigation.

It is up to you how you resolve the matter. Search through the evidence and websites to find clues that can convict the killer.

boardgame: Salōn: a game of Tables & Chairs
description:
Salōn, a game of Tables & Chairs, is a points-based pattern-forming abstract strategy game about being in Chairs playing Games at Tables, making points by forming metaphorical “Conversations” around the Game being played.  It's meta, but really.

Smarter than Checkers, but dumber than Chess, and just a bit like Gō, Salōn refines the simplest elements from the classics to forge an entirely new strategic space of play.

Not set in fields of war, Salōn transforms the convivial yet competitive territory of the game room, a space where people are as cooperative as they need to be.

In Salōn,  all pieces stay on the board as players try to put Games onto Tables, and then try to lock as many Chairs as possible into patterns of Conversation around those Gamed Tables, thus achieving Salōn, when the score is tallied.

Salōn is like role playing yourself playing a boardgame, so bring yourself to the Table!

No Talking is Required as the player with the most points Makes Their Point in Salōn.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: あいどるの推し事 (Idol no Oshigoto)
description:
Idol no Oshigoto is a trick-taking game where the players try to increase the rankings of their favorite idols, as well as buying their merch.

The deck consists of cards in four suits, but instead of the traditional numeric ranks, the cards have letters that correspond to different idols (e.g. H, N, or K). Each player is assigned a different favorite idol during setup, and the idols are sorted into a random ranking order.

During the trick play and resolution, players may end up trading the cards they played or adding votes to an idol card to adjust its order in the ranking.

At the end of each round, players score positive points for some idols, depending on their ranking and how many cards they have, and lose points for others.

The player with the most points after several rounds wins.

boardgame: Бој на Косову (The Battle of Kosovo)
description:
Battle of Kosovo is a fast, simple and dynamic card game in which two players command Serbian and Ottoman units in the famous battle on Vidovdan in 1389. The units are represented by cards and players move them around the table to wage battle. It lasts about 30 minutes and can be played by those aged 10 and over.
One player leads Lazar and the most famous Serbian heroes, the other leads Murat and the greatest Ottoman commanders of the time. It is up to you and your strategy where you will place them and how you will use them, so carefully arrange the units to outwit the enemy. There are three types of units, cavalry, infantry and archers.
A clash of two societies, two religions. On one side, the greatest Serbian nobleman and the last major branch of the once mighty Serbian Empire, on the other, an empire on the rise, trampling everything before it. Can you change the course of history and save Moravian Serbia from its fate, or will history repeat itself?

—description from the publisher (translated)

Бој на Косову је брза, једноставна и динамична карташка игра у којој два играча командују српским и турским јединицама у чувеној бици на Видовдан 1389. године. Јединице су представљене помоћу картица и играчи их померају по столу и тако воде битку. Траје око 30 минута и могу је играти старији од 10 година.
Један играч води Лазара и најзнаменитије српске јунаке, други предводи Мурата и највеће турске војсковође тога доба. Од вас и ваше стратегије зависи где ћете их поставити и како ћете их искористити, тако да пажљиво распоредите јединице, како би надмудрили непријатеља.  Постоје три рода војске, коњица, пешадија и стрелци. 
Судар два друштва, две религије. Са једне стране највећи српски велможа и последњи већи огранак некада моћног Душановог царства, са друге стране царевина која је у успону и која гази све пред собом. Можете ли променити ток историје и спасити Моравску Србију своје судбине или ће се историја поновити?

—description from the publisher

Time’s Up! Express is a fast, accessible party game packed with laughter and adrenaline. Played in teams, the game unfolds over three rounds using the same set of words, making memory just as important as creativity!

In the first round, players can describe freely to help their team guess as many words as possible before the timer runs out. In the second round, things get trickier: only one word is allowed. And in the final round, no words at all: just gestures and frantic miming!

With every turn, the pressure rises as the sand timer slips away and the deck gets smaller. Easy to learn, quick to play, and endlessly entertaining, Time’s Up! Express is the perfect game to get everyone talking, acting, shouting, and laughing together!

Dive into a new era of Duel of Chaos with the Zoi expansion!

This new mechanical race brings a fresh dynamic to the game thanks to an exclusive keyword: Recycle. Capable of reactivating the effects of previously played cards, Recycle offers new synergies, unexpected twists, and advanced strategies. An ideal expansion to diversify your games.

Welcome to Canamunt vs. Canavall, a game inspired by the historical rivalry between two legendary factions of Palma. Instead of swords and fire, demons have decided to resolve the conflict with a water war. Manage your cards to bathe your opponents completely and take your side to victory!

Gameplay: Turn Structure and Battle
During their turn, each player must choose one of the following three gameplay options:
Option 1: Perform Actions
In this option, you can execute and repeat any of these actions using the cards in your hand:
Equipment and Demons: Play equipment cards or demon cards to activate their special effects.
Sabotage: Assign a water card to an opponent's equipment.
Water Battle: Use a water card to initiate a battle phase by declaring an opponent as a target.
Option 2: Hand Refresh
You can discard up to 3 cards from your hand to draw the same amount from the deck and end your turn immediately.
Option 3: Pass and Draw
End the turn directly and draw cards until you reach the maximum number of cards allowed in your hand.
Water Battle Mechanics
If you choose Option 1 and initiate a battle, the process follows these rules:
Attack: The attacking player plays between one and three water cards from their hand to determine the total value of their attack.
Defense: The opponent has three ways to react:
Take the attack.
Cancel it completely.
Play between one and three water cards to try to reduce, match, or exceed the attack value.
Post-battle: Once the battle is resolved, the active player can decide to continue activating other effects or, if they have enough water cards, initiate a new battle within the same turn.

•••

Bienvenidos a Canamunt vs. Canavall, un juego inspirado en la histórica rivalidad entre dos facciones legendarias de Palma. En lugar de espadas y fuego, los demonios han decidido resolver el conflicto con una guerra de agua. ¡Gestiona tus cartas para mojar por completo a tus oponentes y lleva tu bando a la victoria!

Durante su turno, cada jugador debe elegir una de las siguientes tres opciones de juego:
Opción 1: Realizar Acciones
En esta opción, puedes ejecutar y repetir cualquiera de estas acciones utilizando las cartas de tu mano:
Equipamiento y Demonios: Jugar cartas de equipamiento o cartas de demonio para activar sus efectos especiales.
Sabotaje: Asignar una carta de agua a un equipamiento de un oponente.
Batalla de Agua: Utilizar una carta de agua para iniciar una fase de combate declarando a un oponente como objetivo.
Opción 2: Renovación de Mano
Puedes descartar hasta 3 cartas de tu mano para robar la misma cantidad de la baraja y finalizar tu turno inmediatamente.
Opción 3: Pasar y Robar
Terminar el turno directamente y robar cartas hasta alcanzar el número máximo permitido en la mano.
Mecánica de la Batalla de Agua
Si eliges la Opción 1 e inicias una batalla, el proceso sigue estas reglas:
Ataque: El jugador atacante juega entre una y tres cartas de agua de su mano para determinar el valor total de su ataque.
Defensa: El oponente tiene tres formas de reaccionar:
Recibir el ataque.
Anularlo por completo.
Jugar entre una y tres cartas de agua para intentar reducir, igualar o superar el valor del ataque recibido.
Post-batalla: Una vez resuelto el enfrentamiento, el jugador activo puede decidir si continúa activando otros efectos o si, en caso de tener más cartas de agua, inicia una nueva batalla dentro del mismo turno.

Wirikuta is a postcard-sized game, compact and replayable, designed as a narrative and mechanical prologue to Oniria.

Players take part in a symbolic pilgrimage along the path leading to Wirikuta, the sacred desert of the Wixárika people. Each game action represents the advancement of groups, a step, a threshold, or a trial. Rather than seeking perfect optimization, the game invites players to read the path ahead and adapt to it.

In each game, three groups of Wixárikas are in play: those of Player A, those of Player B, and neutral groups. Players gradually move these groups along the path, advancing them step by step, and score points based on where they come to rest. Victory depends on the ability to compose with stop frequencies, sequences, and a careful occupation of space.

Inspired by Mesoamerican and Aridoamerican conceptions of pilgrimage, dreaming, and initiation, Wirikuta does not seek to represent a real ritual or location literally. Instead, the game offers a symbolic and respectful transposition of these notions, based on information transmitted by the Wixárika people themselves as part of the route’s inscription on UNESCO’s Intangible Cultural Heritage list.

Wirikuta deliberately embraces a minimal format to carry a dense and demanding ludic experience.

—description from the publisher

A murder shocks the mansion – but who is behind it? In this duel for two players, one side takes on the role of the murderer, the other that of the detective.

At the beginning, the murderer secretly decides which suspect committed the crime, which weapon was used, and through which of the eight rooms the mansion was entered. The detective tries to uncover all three secrets within eight rounds by playing cards, cleverly combining information, and ruling out false leads. 
Meanwhile, the murderer actively works against them, covering up traces and laying misleading clues. The result is a suspenseful deduction game full of bluffing and logic for two players aged 10 and up.

—description from the designer

The Chair is Empty is a card-based game about political tensions and power vacuums, for 3 or more players.

This is a much cleaned-up and streamlined version of Caudillo, a power politics game placed in a thinly disguised post-Chavez Venezuela which I first designed in 2013 (before Chavez was post-Chavez).
It is basically similar in its semi-cooperative and semi-competitive nature, and it plays up the constant tension between these urges. As players vie to create the largest and most durable personal power base (scored periodically throughout the game), the card deck delivers more and more crises that players must deal with collectively (and collect small rewards immediately), or the accumulated penalties will collapse the whole house. Coups d’etat provide another quick way to score, and the office of El Presidente has its own perks too.

The free PnP version consists of 108 cards, 88 counters, and the usual rules and play aids. Several variants are supplied, including a 2-player version and a "Gringo" variant where an invisible player piggybacks their way to power.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
AKARA is an abstract pure-strategy game of transforming elemental pieces for 2-4 players.

Players begin the game with one Aether and an army of Particle (pawn) pieces positioned opposite each other on a hexagonal board. After moving one of their pieces on the board, players may transform any one of their pieces into a different piece type. This results in an evolving board state that players must constantly anticipate and react to. Particles can transform into any of the four elements: Water, Earth, Fire, or Air. Elemental pieces can transform back into a Particle or to a different element, though they may not transform to their opposite element. The Aether piece cannot transform and other pieces may not transform into an Aether. If a player's piece lands in the same space as an opponent's piece, the opponent's piece is destroyed and permanently removed from the board (in its current form).

There are two ways to win AKARA: either be the first player to maneuver your Aether to the center of the board OR be the last player with your Aether still standing!

—description from the designer

You demonstrated your talent as a master of the ancient minotaur art of dungeon-crafting. Master Hortgully now tasks you with defending your dungeon and training a dangerous boss monster. Defeat the invading heroes, level up your monster, and prove your skill as a Master Architect.

Stonespine Architects: Invasion is an expansion to the critically acclaimed card-drafting game for 1-5 players competing to construct the most dangerous labyrinth.

Players deploy their boss monster and its minions to their dungeon at the end of each round to defend their expanding dungeon against invading heroes. The player to defeat the most heroes without letting any escape will be awarded a bonus, but all players will earn experience for their monster to unlock unique abilities and scoring opportunities.

Spend gold between rounds to customize your labyrinth with monsters, traps, treasures, and secret passages. At the end of four years, the player with the most perilous dungeon will earn the title of Master Architect!

—description from the publisher

Momo is a party game for 2 to 6 players. In this card and vocabulary game, find a word that meets the given constraints as quickly as possible. Watch out for traps and special cards that can turn the game upside down!

Ya lo ves is a fast-paced visual perception game that tests players' speed and pattern recognition skills. It is based on a deck of cards where any two cards always share exactly one matching symbol.

Overview of Play: The game features several mini-games, but the core mechanic remains the same: players act simultaneously to be the first to spot the matching symbol between two cards. Symbols may vary in size or orientation, which adds a layer of challenge to the visual search. Depending on the game mode, players might be racing to discard their hand, collect the most cards from a central pile, or pass their cards to opponents. The key is to shout the name of the matching symbol out loud before anyone else does.

How to Win: Winning conditions vary by the specific mini-game being played. Generally, the player with the fastest reflexes who manages to collect the most cards (or empty their hand first, depending on the rules) is declared the winner. It is a game of high intensity where focus and quick reaction times are essential.

Hoppsi is a small-scale capture game designed for a 5x5 grid. It’s built on a simple, high-tension constraint: pieces must always move forward.  But there’s a twist.  When your pieces hit the opponent's back row, they rebound, turning around to hunt from behind. Every move shifts the momentum, turning a safe position into a trap in a single turn.

What makes Hoppsi different is the Match Play scoring. You aren't just playing one game; you’re playing for points in a match, much like rugby or football:

Capture the enemy Captain for 1 point.
Breach the opposing home row with your own Captain for 2 points.
Capture all of your opponent's runners for 3 points. 
First to 5 points wins the match.

Whether you're playing for a quick capture or a high-scoring shutout, Hoppsi is deep enough for the serious strategist but lean enough to play on a napkin with a few spare coins. It’s small-scale strategy with big-game stakes.

—description from the designer

Actúa Rápido is a dynamic party game centered on Charades and non-verbal communication. It challenges players to express complex ideas, objects, or actions using only their bodies, all while racing against a relentless timer.

Overview of Play: Players are typically divided into teams. On their turn, one player must act out a word or phrase from a card without speaking, making any sounds, or using props. Their teammates must try to guess the answer as quickly as possible. The game’s intensity comes from its fast pace; players often have to act out multiple concepts in a row to maximize their score before time runs out. The cards often feature diverse categories, ranging from movies and famous people to everyday actions and difficult abstract concepts.

How to Win: Teams earn points for every correct guess made within the time limit. The game is played over several rounds, and the team that manages to accumulate the most points by being the fastest and most accurate at interpreting gestures wins the game.

Guess the date of your event.

Place it in the timeline.

If the date is correct, leave it there.

In Choke Point, two opposing forces clash across contested battlefields in a desperate struggle for territorial supremacy. You command a battalion of powerful units, each with unique capabilities and tactical advantages. Your mission: outmaneuver your opponent, coordinate devastating strikes, and reduce their armored divisions to smoldering wreckage.

Victory belongs to the commander who can best adapt to the chaos of war.  Hand management, timing, and battlefield awareness will determine whether your forces roll triumphantly through enemy lines or become another casualty statistic in this unforgiving conflict.

The battlefield awaits. Your units are fueled, your crews are ready, and the enemy is in range.

Choke Point is played over three rounds of team versus team combat.  Tanks come in the light, medium, and heavy variety; each having different shooting ranges, points cost, movement values, and toughness.  Move, shoot, and augment your actions by playing cards from your hand.  Complete public objectives while having your own private objectives to obtain in secret.  Win by having the most points at the end of the game or by eliminating your opponent.

Crisis: El Mundo en Juego is a strategic board game of global conquest and territorial control. Set on a world map, players compete to expand their influence, defend their borders, and fulfill secret objectives while navigating the unpredictable nature of global crises.

Overview of Play: The game is played on a map divided into several regions. During their turn, players deploy armies to their territories and initiate attacks on adjacent enemy regions using a dice-rolling combat system. A key feature of the game is the "Crisis" deck: cards that trigger events, bonuses, or penalties that can shift the balance of power at any moment. Players must balance their military expansion with the strategic use of these cards to protect their interests or sabotage their opponents.

How to Win: At the start of the game, each player receives a secret mission card. To win, a player must be the first to complete the specific objectives listed on their card—such as conquering a certain number of regions or eliminating a specific opponent. Alternatively, a player can win by achieving global domination, controlling a majority of the territories on the board.

T-Desafío is a fast-paced spatial puzzle game based on polyominoes. It challenges players' visual perception and manual dexterity as they race to solve geometric puzzles under pressure.

Overview of Play: Each player receives a set of plastic polyomino pieces (similar to Tetris shapes) and a challenge card showing a specific silhouette or grid. Once a round begins, all players compete simultaneously to be the first to perfectly fill the designated area on their card using their pieces. There are no gaps allowed, and the pieces must fit exactly within the borders. The game features multiple levels of difficulty, with increasingly complex shapes that require more pieces and better spatial planning.

How to Win: The game is played over several rounds. The first player to correctly complete their puzzle shouts "T-Desafío!" and wins the round, earning points. The player with the highest total score after a set number of rounds, or the one who completes a specific number of challenges first, is declared the winner.

boardgame: Fleece & Fortune: The Cotswold Woollen Industry in 18th-century Gloucestershire
description:
Fleece & Fortune is a medium-to-heavy economic board game set in the 18th-century Cotswold woollen industry. Players take the role of clothiers operating water-powered mills, managing the acquisition of wool, the processing of cloth, and the fulfilment of orders from regional and international markets.

On their turns, players use a worker placement / action selection system to assign workers to action spaces on the main board. These actions allow players to acquire different types of wool, send wool to spinners and weavers, process cloth in mills, improve their production capabilities, and secure new orders and status objectives. Many production steps take place in shared infrastructure, creating indirect interaction as players compete for timing and access.

Orders represent commissions for specific types of cloth and require players to sequence production efficiently. As the game progresses, orders become more demanding but also offer greater rewards. In addition to fulfilling orders, players pursue Status Cards, which represent long-term objectives such as industrial expansion, market influence, or reputation within the trade.

The game does not use dice or random movement. Instead, player interaction and variability arise from action competition, order availability, and the timing of shared production spaces. Victory is determined by Status, reflecting a player’s standing within the woollen industry, rather than by traditional victory points.

Through its systems, Fleece & Fortune aims to model the historical processes and economic decisions faced by real clothiers, combining strategic planning, optimisation, and indirect player interaction.

—description from the designer

La Nacion places the player in the role of a political dynasty, who seeks to make their mark on the country of Santa Tomas. Either through election, coup or rebellion you can seek power, (or merely begin as the newly minted leader of the country for an easier start). As a president, a guerrilla leader, or an opposition leader, you can exert control and respond to the events as they occur. 
Each action you take can have consequences for your family members. Each player's dynasty comprises a set number of figures, the game ends when your dynasty deck runs out. Through each action, you gain points and at the end of the game, you tote up the points to find your final score. Different actions and events carry different values.

The game is divided into 10 turns, where the player can spend either money or popular support to leverage as many actions as they  can afford. You can use the military or rebels to assert control as long as they don't turn on you. You can also manipulate the economy to make your money last longer, but bear in mind that that can cause the people to turn on you.

Build, and expand, or loot and steal, the choice is yours, with La Nacion in your hands!

—description from the designer

Soccer Mayhem is a fantasy wargame for up to four players, set in the chaotic world of Artrúsia, where rival supporter groups clash before, during, and after football matches. Players control gangs of orcs, humans, dwarves, or elves, each with unique abilities, morale rules, and special units, fighting across modular hex-based maps inspired by stadiums, taverns, and surrounding streets.

The game combines tactical movement, dice-driven combat, morale tests, random events, and scenario objectives, with mechanics for ranged attacks, brawls, leaders, chants, police intervention, and even magic. Matches are fast, violent, unpredictable, and filled with dark humor.

Despite its brutal tone, Soccer Mayhem is a social critique of real-world stadium violence. It does not glorify hooliganism, but instead uses satire and fantasy to explore its consequences through playful and strategic gameplay.

—description from the designer

"At Cholet, the rebels fought like tigers and our troops fought like lions"
General Kléber, commanding the french republican army

Les Tigres et les lions (Tigers and Lions) is a game that deals with three battle fought during the Vendée war, between republican forces (the Blues) and Royalist (the Whites).

On 29th June 1793, the white army failed to take the very important city of Nantes, while the loss of the city would have been a grievous loss for the stumbling french Republic.

The white army suffered another decisive defeat at Cholet, on 17th October 1793, and had to abandon Vendée to cross the Loire and try to find help in Normandy and Brittany.

Still, a few days later, the whites were able to defeat the overconfident republican army at Entrammes, on 26th October 1793.

Les Tigres et les lions is the 7th game of the series Les Soldats de la République.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Les Soldats de la République (Vae Victis)

description:
Pippins Aplenty is traditional-pack trick-taker for four players, where the hierarchy of trump suits, and the value of captured cards, changes over the course of each deal.

Playing in partnerships, trickplay follows Whist-like rules. Play initially starts without trumps.

After each trick, the lowest rank played that is higher than any existing trump-setting cards ('reaches') sets the new highest trump suit. The rank of this card also sets the scoring threshold - all cards above this are worth nothing.

Points are scored by cards won in tricks - lower-ranked trump suits are worth more points than higher ones, and ranks represented in reaches earn bonus points. Score is traditionally kept on a Cribbage board, with play as first to surpass a pre-agreed total, with 600 as standard.

Another safe is opened...this time with even more accomplices on the team.

The Gang: More Players features four new sets of bidding poker chips to allow for up to ten players in the co-operative poker game The Gang. This expansion also includes "exit chips" that simplify the game when playing with five or six people.

The Gang: Deluxe Edition is a blinged-out edition of the co-operative card game The Gang that includes the base game, the The Gang: More Players expansion that brings the player count to ten, as well as three expansion packs for more variability in game play. This edition also features ceramic poker chips, casino-quality cards, and a custom playmat.

boardgame: The Crew: Journey to the Ends of the Earth
description:
Travel around the world in a race against time on the hunt for Charlemagne's secret treasure. Follow clues that will lead you up Mount Everest, through the Amazon Rainforest, and beyond. It's up to you how your story will end. Will you make it in time, or will you be too late? The only way to find out is by playing.

The Crew: Journey to the Ends of the Earth is a co-operative trick-taking game in which players attempt to complete missions together. This game features a 25-stage journey.

Based on the classic "Celebrities" format but injected with a Maltese twist, this 17+ party game challenges teams to guess famous Maltese figures and cultural references through three increasingly difficult rounds.

It starts easy with a free-for-all description, moves into a one-word brain-teaser, and ends in a frantic round of charades where you'll have to mime your way to victory.

—description from the designer
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

boardgame: Эволюция волшебных тварей: Подарочный набор (Evolution: Another World – Gift Set)

description:
Gnomes of different clans come together to form small squads competing against one another. Select 4 gnomes from a hand of 6 to make the strongest group you can, choose their combat order, then compare each gnome's strength against their opponent after synergies and unique abilities enhance their base level. Whichever player wins the most matchups of the 4 wins the round. All gnomes are shuffled together and new hands are dealt to the players to battle again.

—description from the designer

A stand-alone game for 2, based off the hit deduction game, Corps of Discovery, this game has 2 players working together to create a map by following specific logic rules.

Players know some communal logic rules, but also each have some hidden behind their screen. Can you communicate only through your card placement what your rules are to your partner, while trying to figure out their rules?

On your turn you play one of your cards onto an ever-expanding map. If you don't like where your partner played a card because it violates one of your logic rules, then you can pay the current penalty (which is discarding cards from your deck) to remove any card in play.

If either player has no more cards to play, then the game is over and you both lose.

If at least 1 of each of the 10 terrains is in play, then either player could play the fort card - in a location that adheres to its own logic rule. Then review to see if all the cards on the map follow all the logic rules in the game - and if they do - you win!
It includes an Upgrade system as you progress from game to game, while also adding hazards that add new challenges each time you play.

—description from the designer

Everything you need to run a modern version of the game that started modern wargaming. Box has maps, counters and tracking sheets for the umpire, and 2 players (or teams).

Kriegsspiel is the original military simulation game, developed by the Prussian Army in the 1800s, designed to train officers by replicating the "fog of war" and command complexities, using an umpire to process double-blind orders on maps. It emphasizes realistic command, communication delays, and decision-making with limited information, inspiring today's modern wargames.

This version includes 10 scenarios, all using the same map.

The Market is open. Your hand is your life force. Spend it wisely—because in Shadow Market, poverty is death.

Shadow Market is a fast, closed-economy auction game for 3–4 players using a standard 52-card deck. Every card is a finite resource, and every decision leaves a scar. Your hand is your health, your currency, and your defense—run out, and you’re at the mercy of the table.

The game is driven by a brutal Winner Pays auction system. Players bid blindly on valuable Market cards. Losers return their bids to hand—but the winner must discard everything they wagered to the void. Winning feels good… until you realize you’ve permanently weakened yourself for the rest of the game.

Beyond bidding, players can burn mid-value cards to trigger powerful suit abilities—digging for better options, accelerating their economy, or stealing directly from rivals. Every turn forces a painful choice: draw to survive, spend to score, or gamble your future for immediate power.

Shadow Market is short, sharp, and unforgiving. Games end in a tense final auction with no safety net and no second chances. Victory often comes down to who managed their losses best—not who grabbed the most early rewards.

If you enjoy cutthroat auctions, meaningful player interaction, and games where every card matters, Shadow Market rewards repeat plays and hard lessons learned.

—description from the designer

Arigato: Cuentos para no dormir is a family card game where players create their ideal music band by finding musicians and matching them with the correct instruments.

During the game, players draw instrument cards that determine which type of musician they must find among the face-down musician cards on the table. By flipping musician cards and using memory, players try to collect musicians that match the required instrument colors.

The goal of the game is to be the first player to complete a band made up of four different instruments: voice, guitar, keyboard, and bass. The game combines memory, observation, and light player interaction, making it suitable for children and family play.

The game is designed as a “discogame”, combining a board game with an original music album created by the band Arigato, integrating music and storytelling into the play experience.

The number 8 is considered the luckiest number in Chinese culture. You will compete with others to accumulate the most wealth and fortune.

Place your dice on one of the 4 bet mats to steadily earn coins while blocking opponents to do the same. Change your dice fortunes by purchasing yun cards or go for the big payout by playing the jackpot.

There are limited koban coins in the pot so it's a race against time to be the richest person at the table!

-description from publisher

Mines is a fast-paced, tactical card game for 2–6 players: each turn, draw either a crystal card or a special card to build and upgrade mines, then harvest crystals based on your mine’s level. The first to collect 8 matching crystals wins! With varied strategies, tactical depth, and a dash of luck, Mines stays fresh and keeps you thinking every round.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: War of The Three Kingdoms: Endless Legends
description:
The expansion for the standard version of "Three Kingdoms Shock" adds 48 martial general cards and 53 game cards on top of the standard version. It includes martial generals from the "Wind, Forest, Fire, Mountain" series and game cards from the "Military Struggle" chapter. It must be played in conjunction with the standard version of "Three Kingdoms Shock".

—description from the publisher
family: Theme: Romance of the Three Kingdoms

boardgame: Rangers of Shadow Deep: Ashen Sky
description:
Ashen Sky is a collection of Rangers of Shadow Deep material that originally appeared on the creator’s Patreon, Games from the Grave. It includes three stand-alone scenarios set within the Shadow Deep for Rangers of level 10+, a new selection of 10-point companions that are available to all Rangers, and 52 unique magic items to be found!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #53 – Hawker Typhoon
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #53 features one page of aircraft briefings, a page of supplemental notes, a list of ADC sets available for sale and the following three updated to third edition standard Hawker Typhoon data cards:

• Typhoon Mk.IA (initial model, armed with 12 x .303 MG)

• Typhoon Mk. IB (early) (cannon armed model, adding bomb pylons under wings)

• Typhoon Mk.IB (late) (The full Montey, bubble canopy, bombs rockets, better engine etc.)
family: Game: Fighting Wings Aircraft Data Card (ADCs) downloadable expansions

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #54 – Boeing B-17 Part II
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #54 features one page of aircraft briefings and the following three updated to third edition standard Hawker Typhoon data cards:

• RAF Boeing B-17C (first version to see combat in Europe)

• USAAF B-17E (first combat worthy model, new gun turrets, tail guns, etc.)

• USAAF B-17F (initial F model, improved engines and prop and nose armament)
family: Game: Fighting Wings Aircraft Data Card (ADCs) downloadable expansions

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #55 – Early Bf 109F
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #55 features one page of aircraft briefingsand the following three updated to third edition standard Messerchmitt Bf 109F series ADCs:

• Bf 109F-1 (initial F model, suffered from weak structure, but a good performer)

• Bf 109F-2 "Galland's Special" (experimental version of F-2 adding wing guns)

• Bf 109F-3/R3 (model used to evaluate DB 601E engine, later used in F-4 series)
family: Game: Fighting Wings Aircraft Data Card (ADCs) downloadable expansions

boardgame: Fighting Wings: Data Card Set #56 – RAF Havoc I
description:
Fighting Wings ADC set #56 features one page of aircraft briefings and the following three updated to third edition standard Hawker Typhoon data cards:

• RAF DB-7 Boston / Havoc Intruder (French A/C orders taken over by RAF, applies to all below)

• RAF DB-7 Havoc Mk.I Nightfighter (better than a Blenheim, stop gap NF)

• RAF DB-7 Havoc Mk.I Turbinlite Havoc (no guns, big light in nose, paired with fighters for night intercepts - fascinating concept...difficult to use).
family: Game: Fighting Wings Aircraft Data Card (ADCs) downloadable expansions

boardgame: Junior Escape Game: Abenteuer in der Manege
description:
The players work together to solve some puzzle and logic games. If everything is solved correctly, you win all together!

The escape game trend now for the youngest!

New adventures to introduce children to puzzles, logic and lateral thinking. A great set of puzzle cards and useful items to help in finding the clues.

Behind each map is a clue for the continuation of the adventure. A mind game that is about finding all the clues to solve the puzzles while training the mind.

A game of maps and lots of puzzles waiting to be solved, just like in a real escape room!

boardgame: Sumrak srpskog carstva (The Twilight of the Serbian Empire)
description:
Сумрак Српског царства ("The Twilight of the Serbian Empire") is a 2 to 6 player wargame depicting the final years of the Serbian Empire. The game lasts for 2 to 3 hours and players age is set to 12+

After the death of Czar Dusan the Great, Serbian noble families begun to struggle for the dominance and power. Dusan’s heir, Uros the Weak was unable to prevent local wars and clashes between the families. The strength of the empire was rapidly declining, while the noblemen’s only goal was to secure their right to the throne.

Battles, negotiations, pacts, betrayals, jousting, swordsmanship…

Lead your family to glory and become the next Serbian Czar!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Сумрак Српског царства је ратна игра за 2 до 6 играча која приказује последње године Српског царства. Игра траје 2 до 3 сата, а узраст играча је 12+.

Након смрти цара Душана Великог, српске племићке породице су почеле да се боре за доминацију и моћ. Душанов наследник, Урош Нејаки, није био у стању да спречи локалне ратове и сукобе између породица. Снага царства је брзо опадала, док је једини циљ племића био да осигурају своје право на престо.

Битке, преговори, пактови, издаје, витешки турнири, мачевање…

Водите своју породицу ка слави и постаните следећи српски Цар!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Prvi srpski ustanak ("First Serbian Uprising") is a two player game covering the rebellion of Serbian people against the Ottoman Empire at the beginning of the XIX century.

Players lead Serbian and Ottoman forces on hexagon grid map of Belgrade Pashaluk, moving across different terrain, trying to capture settlements needed to achieve victory.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Гладне веверице ("Hungry Squirrels") is a fun, interesting game designed for children age 5 to 10 and the game lasts 10 to 20 minutes.
