Squirrels in the forest are hungry. Will you help them find acorns? But be aware, the Wolf and the Fox are there, too and they are waiting for their chance!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Гладне веверице је забавна и занимљива игра намењена деци узраста од 5 до 10 година, а игра траје 10 до 20 минута.

Веверице су у шуми и гладне су. Хоћете ли им помоћи да пронађу жир? Али пазите, Вук и Лисица су такође тамо и чекају своју прилику!

—description from the publisher

We need your best squad is a solo tabletop adventure wargame where you control a squad of troopers in a Science Fiction setting. Whether as a mercenary unit trying to gain a high reputation, a group of rebels trying to drive the alien invaders off your planet, a team trapped behind enemy lines after a daring raid, or one of the other seven campaigns, each of the fifteen games takes the form of a battle between some of your squad and a small number of adversaries.

The game is not aiming to be in any way a detailed simulation of armed combat, instead it concentrates on getting to the action as quickly as possible. All rolls in battle are 1D10, with a natural 1 being bad and a natural 10 being good. A single roll takes into account hitting, penetrating, and wounding. Weapons are generic. It’s not just a wargame, it’s an adventure, or that’s the intent anyway.

Every game has markers for where the adversaries might be and your team has to uncover the actual foes.  The adversaries always “React” to the team, hence true solo play rather than trying to play both sides. There are also sometimes innocent Bystanders getting in the way, and there are often objectives to deal with.

Your Squad will usually be six figures and will usually be fighting 6 to 10 enemy figures, plus there may be up to 8 bystanders wandering about.  A battle lasts 6 to 10 rounds, so you can set up your table, play through the game, and pack it down again in under 3 hours, probably 2 once you are used to it.

There are a selection of different locations (and therefore table layouts) where the action takes place, a selection of different adversaries, and a slow but steady increase in difficulty to balance the improvements and added gear your posse will get along the way.

These 104 pages include the 10 Story Arcs, full rules for gameplay, a 4 page Quick Reference for the battle tables, and an example campaign roster/tracker. You will of course need your own miniatures and terrain pieces, but the scenarios are as open as possible to enable you to use the stuff you already have, or give you an excuse to collect more.

—description from the publisher

History tells us that the Leaning Tower of Pisa was built between 1173 and 1372. Is it based on the design of Bonanno Pisano. Over the centuries, due to the soft ground, it has tilted on its foundation by almost four degress, remaining so until now.

But the real story is that, in reality, there were several crazy architects who tried to build their towers with a deliberate tilt to see if it could be done. They even had a competition to see who could build their tower first!

Whoever built their tower first, won the competition. The losers tore down their tower and were forced into exile, leaving their wonder to the glory of posterity.

Excerpt from the Encyclopedia of Ridiculous Conspiracy Stories That Have Been Hidden from You.

Each turn you will roll you dice (stones) and choose which and how you will place your dice. Use the pips of the dice to fill in spaces on the tower matching the pattern of the pips. There are some crucial spots on the tower that require precise building (copper spaces). You must use the matching die to fill in this space.

Since spaces may not match your current dice, you can chisel the die down to alter the pattern of the dice in value up to two times.

You goal is to build your tower before your competition builds theirs! You can see from a distant how quickly your competition is moving along, so you must work efficiently to get the job done. However, building a tower that leans takes time and you have to work with rocks that have to be chiseled into shape. Not an easy task. Your tower also has to be structurally sound, so you must build in accordance with the standards of the day.

If you fill in all of the spaces of your tower with a limited number of mistakes before your competition does, you win! If your competitor in the distance fills their tower, you lose.

—description from the designer

boardgame: One Card Dungeon Adventure Pack: Hell's Door
description:
In the deepest pits of the underworld, the gates of Hell creak open.
Rivers of fire surge and shadows twist, as the Lord of the Inferno rises to claim his dominion, threatening to engulf the realm in endless torment.
Only those brave enough to descend into the abyss and face him in a final battle can hope to seal Hell’s Door forever.

One Card Dungeon: Hell’s Door is a standalone adventure that follows the same setup and rules as the base game and the M’Guf-yn Returns expansion, with some unique new features.
This expansion adds a dungeon card, a boss level dungeon card, 1 new class, 5 new item cards, and the Stone of Perdiction.

—description from the publisher

Rolling Tactics: Scales of Peace is a tactical solo and cooperative roll-and-write game for 1–100 players. Lead your units to victory across varied battlefields, where terrain, objectives, and items shape every decision. Experience SRPG-inspired gameplay in a narrative 3-scenario campaign, or take on 3 challenging one-off skirmishes.

Each turn, you activate all your Player Units, with each unit moving and taking one action (attacking or healing, depending on its Weapon Type). You then roll a die to resolve an Event and end the turn by activating all Enemy Units. Complete Objectives to gain an advantage in battle and wound all Enemy Units before they wound all of yours!

Note: Each scenario and skirmish is contained on a single sheet of paper, with no crafting required. All you need to provide is 1 pen per player and 1 standard 6-sided die to play the game.

—description from the publisher

This expansion pack includes the deluxe miniature mutation attachements and the two expansions "The Plight of the People" and "The Palace Visitors" designed by Dávid Turczi and Jonny Pac.

This expansion pack is already included in the Deluxe edition of the base game.

Draw Action, Power, Spell, Kouronne, or Master cards and use them to influence the game, trap your opponent, or turn the tide of a desperate situation.

Between tension, bluffing, and the unexpected, no two duels are alike!

The game levels the playing field between the two players, for duels filled with suspense... right up to the very last move.

Inspired by the fable of the Crow and the Fox, Master Kou is a variant accessible to players aged 8 and up, faithful to the traditional movement rules of chess.

Requires a chess set to play.

Unicorn fans: Attention! In this game, a group of adventurous friends set off in search of unicorns. Armed with imagination and lots of joy, they dive into a colorful world of magic.

Roll both dice, then everyone searches for the card that matches the conditions shown. The first player to find the correct card keeps it. Whoever has the most cards at the end wins.

Grumpy Monkeys is a quick play character matching card game which will test your logic and strategy skills. Perfect for anyone who loves solving puzzles and having fun with friends and family... Luck is just a small part of this game.

Easy to learn but harder to master, the winner is the first player to solve the puzzle.

—description from the publisher

Химическая война ("Chemical War") is a historical educational board game published in 1925 by the State Publishing House of the Soviet Union.

Designed as both a strategic simulation and a civil defense teaching tool, the game reflects early 20th-century concerns surrounding chemical warfare in modern industrial cities.

Players engage in a turn-based conflict where they must manage resources, control urban areas, and respond to chemical threats while balancing offensive actions and civilian protection.

Rather than glorifying warfare, Химическая война emphasizes preparedness, awareness, and the consequences of chemical weapons, aligning with the educational and propagandistic goals of its time.

Today, the game stands as a rare example of early board games used to communicate political, military, and public safety concepts through play.

Революция ("Revolution") is a historical educational board game published in 1925 by the State Publishing House of the Soviet Union.

Set in an industrial urban environment, the game portrays revolutionary struggle during a period of political upheaval, reflecting early 20th-century revolutionary ideology and mass movements.

Players take part in a turn-based simulation where crowds, armed groups, and opposing forces clash across city districts. Control of key locations, coordination of actions, and timing are central to achieving victory.

Rather than focusing on individual characters, Revolution emphasizes collective action and social dynamics, aligning with the educational and propagandistic objectives of its era.

Today, the game serves as a historical artifact, illustrating how board games were used to convey political ideas, revolutionary narratives, and civic concepts in the early Soviet period.

This Jenga: Downfall Expansion pack includes 10 funky blocks and 30 Downfall cards to add to the Jenga game for an exciting fresh way to play! Players build an epic tower while trying to sabotage each other to get ahead. Take turns drawing cards that tell which block(s) to add to the tower. If a player topples any blocks, they add them to their stash. Certain cards let players swap stashes with others, force a player to stack more blocks, and more. The player with the fewest blocks at the end of the game wins!

This is an expansion. Requires core Jenga game to play.

COLOURFUL FUNKY BLOCKS: includes 10 bright blocks (4 solid hardwood and 6 translucent plastic) in different geometric shapes. Mix 'em in with the core Jenga game wooden blocks

BUILD EPIC TOWERS: instead of removing blocks from the Jenga tower, players take turns building an epic tower and set each other up to fail! If a player topples any blocks, they add them to their stash. The player with the fewest blocks wins

CARDS ADD CHALLENGES AND TWISTS: 30 Downfall cards tell players which block(s) to add to the tower. Some cards include game-changing actions, such as swapping stashes

MAKE MISCHIEF, SABOTAGE OPPONENTS: spice things up by placing Jenga blocks in tricky spots to make it harder for the next player to keep the tower from falling

Contents:
• 10 funky blocks (4 wooden blocks, 6 plastic blocks)
• 30 cards

Kanoodle Gravity is a spatial reasoning and logic puzzle game that challenges players to build three-dimensional structures using colorful plastic pieces.

Each challenge requires players to place pieces inside a vertical frame, stacking and balancing them while obeying the rules of gravity.

The game can be played solo as a brain-teasing puzzle or competitively, where players take turns placing a pieces on the board. When a player places a piece that does not fit, they lose the round.

With increasing levels of difficulty, Kanoodle Gravity helps develop problem-solving skills, spatial awareness, and logical thinking in an engaging, hands-on format.

The sky dims over Arborea’s prospering ecosystem as something blighted creeps across the lands. Darkness spews forth from an ancient city, corrupting everything it touches. Can your villagers work together to hold back the decay, or will this be the end of the current cycle of Arborea?

In Arborea Corruption, players send workers down new tracks to remove corruption from your creatures and ecosystems. Make offerings to the new Portal Sage, gain additional rewards for clearing corruption from the shared sages, and collect unique and ancient Majestic Creatures to bring into your ecosystems.

The Corruption expansion adds new rules, options, and scoring (including negative points). The rules of the base game have not changed and still apply unless explained differently.

boardgame: 仮面ライダーウィザード ボードゲーム (Kamen Rider Wizard Soudatu Game)
description:
Kamen Rider Wizard Soudatu Game is a Japanese children’s board game published by Bandai, based on the Kamen Rider Wizard TV series.

Players take the roles of different Kamen Riders and move across the board by spinning the included spinner. Throughout the game, players collect rings, battle enemies, and trigger special events based on the spaces they land on.

The goal is to reach the final destination and defeat the boss while using character abilities and items to gain advantages over other players.

The game is designed for young players and fans of the Kamen Rider franchise, featuring colorful components and simple rules.

boardgame: 仮面ライダーフォーゼ バトルゲーム (Kamen Rider Fourze Battle Game)
description:
Kamen Rider Fourze Battle Game is a Japanese children’s board game published by Bandai and based on the Kamen Rider Fourze television series.

Players take control of Kamen Riders and move around the board using a spinner. As they travel across different areas, players collect items, trigger events, and engage in battles against enemies using cards and character abilities.

The game combines simple movement mechanics with light combat elements, making it accessible to younger players while remaining engaging for fans of the franchise.

The objective is to defeat enemies and reach the final battle area to determine the winner.

DuvidaÊ! (Doubt It!) is a bluffing climbing game where the goal is to get rid of all the cards in your hand. Each player must play a stronger combination than the previous one – cards with higher value, or more cards. From 2 cards onwards, however, only one is played face up – all the others are played face down. This gives the next player the option to doubt. By doubting, they can trigger traps that affect the player who is wrong – the one who doubted, if the hand is as declared, or the one who bluffed, if it is not.

Play your cards on the table, look closely at the next player, declare your hand and dare them to Doubt It!

-description from designer

While this expansion is for 1-4 players and perfectly compatible with the base game, it is geared towards solo play and as such is a bit more difficult than your average game of Dungeon Rummy, should you choose to play as a team.

Goblins built Golems with tools, and now they are out of control! The Misfits must stop them! This expansion features modular bosses: a Golem with a random weapon. Luckily, Qazam, the Elementalist, joins the team to help!

News of the Misfits crossed the continents, and four new adventure-driven nuisances are now coming to join the team! Meet 4 new playable characters: Zoz, the Ranger; Ozi, the Paladin; Bernie and Claude, the two-headed Rogues; and the shamanic duo Big Bones and Lil' Seb.

Together, they'll battle the mighty Kraken in this Dungeon Rummy expansion!

The Kraken is a very special 9-card Big Boss. Each tentacle moves independently and has its own win condition!

Fulfill the Quest! The quest is not done once you beat the Kraken; you need to earn the cool points too!

Ape-Mod-X  is a classic, luck-based comparative card game with a twist in the gameplay;  The extra trait used in defence.

Ape-Mod-X is a children's/family card game. No strategy is involved - simply the ability to recognise how the cards are ranked/scored, and to follow the procedure of the game.

To play:
Shuffle the cards and split them equally  between players. If the cards don't divide evenly, place any spare cards on the table and they become a  bonus for the winner of the first round. Hold your cards in front of you so that you can only see the front(character) of the top card.
After deciding who will start, the first player chooses a trait category of the character on their top card that they believe will win them the round and they give/call out that trait category (or the colour it's backed by) and their characters' value for it.  E.g. "Wear & Hold - 6" or "Green – 6". The other players must then say their value for that trait, but then also call out and add on the value of their XTRA trait, if any. The XTRA 6th trait is a bonus & can only be used/added in defence - when you are not the 1st player that nominated the trait category called in this round. The player with the highest score wins the round and wins the other player's card(s). 
In the event of a trait score tie (when XTRA points included in defense), tied cards are 'frozen out' and left on the table (alongside any beaten cards by a 3rd or 4th player). The players that tied, repeat the round with the same player nominating the trait to play from the next card, and the winner takes all of the cards including any 'frozen'ones. The winner places cards won at the bottom of his stack and then selects/calls a trait and value from their next card on top. If a player’s last card is frozen because of a tie, they automatically lose the game as they have no active cards left. This continues until one player has won all of the cards being used in the game and they are the winner.
Shuffle and play again!

There are 3 different decks, each with 50 characters to play with: Couture, Mutants and Mecha. Decks can be combined to include more players or to have a longer game.

—description from the designer

In this card game, two to four players take turns completing progressively harder climbs to earn agility and confidence points which, in turn, can be used to take on more challenging climbs still – until they’re finally in a position to take on King’s College Chapel – the acme of the Cambridge-climbing Alps.

Along the way they have to avoid increasing police and porter presence, varying and sometimes treacherous weather conditions and keeping up their all-important academic progress – or risk losing their places at Cambridge. Advantages can be picked up too – rubber-soled shoes, a streamlined coat or extra stamina and muscles from punting, for example.

If all the dangers are avoided and the plucky climber attains the highest peak without apprehension by the authorities, then they can return to earth triumphant and claim eternal bragging rights – until the rematch, of course.

—description from the publisher

Each of the two players has 24 pieces: 16 infantry, 6 cavalry, and 2 cannons. The player who moves first places their pieces on points 1-24, then the other player places their pieces on points I-XXIV. The different troop types can be placed on these points in any grouping. Players then take turns moving.

Movement is permitted in all directions, but only along black lines (movement is not allowed across red lines or gaps). Infantry and cannons move only to the next point. Cavalry moves up to 2 points. Any piece can move up to 3 points along the railway line, even around a bend in the railway.

An undefended enemy piece must be captured. A piece is undefended if its nearest point, in the straight continuation of the enemy's line of attack, is unoccupied. The infantryman and cavalryman capture across the black and red lines, capturing the piece on the nearest point by jumping over it, moving to the unoccupied point, and removing the enemy piece. Infantrymen and cavalrymen may only capture one piece per turn. The cannon captures across the black and red lines, capturing all undefended enemy pieces on them, even if they are on points further away, by removing the undefended enemy pieces without moving its position. Captures may not be made across breaks in the playing net. Railway lines are treated as ordinary black lines for capturing purposes, and captures may not be made around curves.

A cannon is considered captured and may no longer change its position if an enemy piece manages to advance to the nearest point. If the enemy piece is undefended, it must be captured, and the cannon must also be removed by the enemy in the next turn.

Each player has one entrenchment tile and three railway tiles; in higher-quality versions, five railway tiles are required. The entrenchment tile creates a break in the game board network between the two points it is placed between. The railway tile transforms the section of line it is placed on into a railway line. Entrenchment and railway tiles may be placed on black and red lines if neither of the two points between which they are to be placed is occupied by an enemy piece. Placing or removing a tile counts as a move. If both points between which a tile is placed are occupied by the enemy, the tile becomes the enemy's property. The enemy can either leave it in place or remove it and place it again later. 
The winner is the one who first occupies points 1, 2, 3 or I, II, III in enemy Fortress, or encircles the enemy so that he has no more moves, or first destroys him down to 2 pieces.

This is eckso, a fast, two-player strategy game where magnets and gravity determine whether you win or topple.

Every move brings you closer to capturing a symbol for the win, OR toppling the board!

To play, you build the board outwards from the pedestal. Overlap or underlap one of the existing cards on each turn. Your goal is to capture at least one of your opponent’s symbols by boxing it in. As you expand the board, though, it becomes more and more unstable. Make it too delicate, and your winning move could topple the whole thing.

Placing a card changes the board significantly, because each eckso card is effectively a quadruple move. This creates tons of edge conditions to consider. It’s kind of like a Jenga-fied version of chess.

—description from the designer

It's a classic Monopoly game, but set in the world of Disney. The main objective is to be rich, very rich, as rich as Scrooge McDuck... with properties, houses, and castles... good income and lots of cash.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Genesis is a solo roll-and-write game set during the first three minutes of the Universe, when all that existed was a quantum soup of elementary particles. In this chaotic state, the player will attempt to confine quarks into protons and neutrons and gather enough electrons and energy to give rise to the fundamental constituents of matter.

The game is divided into two stages. In the first stage, the goal is to collect sufficient protons, neutrons, electrons, and photons. In the second stage, the player must trigger Primordial Nucleosynthesis—the process responsible for binding protons and neutrons together inside the very first atomic nuclei of the Universe. 
You win the first part of the game if you manage to form at least one stable proton and one stable neutron, and obtain enough electrons and photons to create atomic nuclei before the entropy of the universe reaches a certain level.

You win the second part of the game if you activate Primordial Nucleosynthesis through the exchange of gluons between nucleons.

You lose the game if you obtain too much antimatter or if the entropy of the universe reaches a certain level before you achieve your objectives.

Components:
-Game sheet
-3 six-sided dice
-Pencil and eraser

-description from designer

A kingdom is in turmoil as 2-players go head-to-head on opposing sides of the conflict.

Players will use subjects and nobles to accomplish their unique goals by sending cards to jail, manipulating the council, hiding cards from the enemy, stealing cards, killing cards, and more actions. Each turn a player will play a card, resolve its effect, discard that card, then draw 2 or more cards (based on cards played or in play) and then discard down to the hand limit. Then it's the next players turn and both players go back and forth until one of the players meets one of their win conditions.

Players take control of one of the two dueling roles, both of which have one of two unique win conditions they must accomplish in order to win the game. One side is the Conspirators, who win the game by using an Assassin to kill the Queen without getting blocked by a Knight, or by placing four Noble Conspirators on the council to overthrow the Queen. The other side is the Protectors, who win by playing all three Royal Jewels, where each Jewel gives the Protectors a larger hand size and more card draw, but they must discard their whole hand when they play a Royal Jewel potentially making themselves very vulnerable to an assassination attempt. The other way the Protectors can win is by killing all Assassins in the game and three Noble Conspirators, making a Conspirator win impossible.

Utilize a variety of strategies as either side in this easy to learn, lightly-asymmetric card game.

—description from the designer

Driftcore Racers – Rulebook

Speed is currency. Reputation is a weapon. Rules are optional.

Driftcore Racers is a fast, tabletop racing game where winning isn’t just about crossing the finish line first — it’s about manipulation, influence, and surviving a season of high-risk races in a corrupt motorsport underworld.

Players take control of ruthless racers competing in brutal circuits, upgrading their machines with questionable modifications, cutting deals with sponsors, manipulating the media, and sabotaging rivals at exactly the wrong moment. Every race is only part of the story.

This isn’t clean motorsport. It’s a gauntlet of illegal mods, backroom bets, public scandals, and high-stakes challenges where fortunes change in a single turn.

What’s in the Rules?

     2+ players, head-to-head or multiplayer racing
     Customisable racers with a range of upgrades and illegal modifications
     Four unique mission cards that drive chaos and narrative:
         Betting Syndicate – influence races and reap risky rewards
         Illegal Modding – take a chance using illegal upgrades to win races
         Spectator & Media – reputation, headlines, and public pressure
         High-Stakes Challenges – sudden opportunities and brutal twists
    
     Campaign Season Mode, linking races together into a full racing league
     10 unique Sponsors, each offering distinct playstyles, bonuses, and team tactics
     Designed for fast play, big moments, and emergent storytelling


Every decision matters. Push your luck too far and you’ll burn out — play it safe and someone else will take your place.

Out here, nobody remembers who came second.

A board game for 2-4 players that was released in Greece in the 1970s.

Each player gets a car and 10 penalty cards. The cars start from two starting points in pairs or, in the case of three players, at a location of their choice. The player who reaches the highest speed on the gear wheel starts first.

Τhe cars are moved using the gear wheel, which shows the 4 speeds as well as the words "breakdown" and "penalty." When the player rolls the numbers 1, 2, 3, or 4, they move that many squares.  When they roll the word "penalty," they draw a penalty card, and if they use up all 10 penalty cards before reaching the finish line, they are out of the game. In cases where a player is obliged to let the car behind him overtake him.

To finish, a player must reach the last square with an exact roll. Between the first and second players to finish, the winner is not the one who finishes first but the one with the fewest penalties. The same applies between the third and fourth players.

Kawanakajima 1561, the most epic battle of Sengoku Jidai, is the third volume in the Sengoku Jidai series. The game features a one-sided area map measuring 23.1 × 33.1 inches (59.4 × 84 cm), 216 beautifully illustrated counters, and a 24-page bilingual rulebook (English & French). It also includes two player aids and two scenarios: one historical and one alternative.

The scale represents 300–400 meters per area, 30 minutes per turn, and 500–1,000 men per counter. A full game lasts 2 to 4 hours and is ideally suited for two players. Kawanakajima 1561 is an area-movement wargame designed to deliver intense, fast-paced engagements.

Prepare for swift and brutal battles, where maneuver, timing, and tactical decisions are the keys to victory.

The Battle of Kawanakajima (1561)

Fought between the armies of Uesugi Kenshin and Takeda Shingen, the Fourth Battle of Kawanakajima is one of the most famous clashes of Japan’s Sengoku period. Renowned for its daring maneuvers, sudden attacks, and legendary duels, it epitomizes the art of war practiced by rival daimyo at the height of samurai warfare.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Fall Dawn is a competitive dungeon crawler card game driven by exploration, character building, and strategic bluffing. Players take on the role of Erranti, wanderers fleeing a cataclysm advancing from the East known as "The Dawn"—a phenomenon that erases reality itself. Your goal is to reach the safety of the last fortress cities, but entry is only granted to those who pay in Alaxite, the green crystals harvested from monsters that serve as the source of magic in this world.

GAMEPLAY OVERVIEW
1. Starting Gear and Strategy
At the beginning of the game, you buy your equipment. This choice defines your initial combat style: you can focus on high damage, a defensive build, a balanced approach, or a character specialized in ranged attacks. Once equipped, you begin your journey toward a kingdom in the West. Only one player will survive by securing enough Alaxite, the green crystals extracted from monsters which are the source of magic.

2. The Turn Loop
Each turn follows a specific sequence:

The Setup: Three Path cards are placed at the cardinal points: North, South, and West. The Camp always remains to the East.

The Event: An Event card is drawn from one of three chapters: The Escape, The Journey, and The Dawn. These cards modify game dynamics, affecting combat or Camp activities, providing immersion into the lore of Narwar.

Phase 1: The Promise: Starting with the first player, everyone moves their Errante card to a path they intend to explore. This is the negotiation phase where you can agree to form alliances to defeat the most threatening monsters.

Phase 2: The Choice: Every player secretly selects one of their four cardinal direction cards. This represents your actual destination. All cards are revealed simultaneously, showing who kept their promise and who betrayed.

In summary, the game revolves around the alternation of these two phases. You must read the other players' eyes, understand their intentions, and decide who can be trusted and who cannot. Unpredictability is always present. The moment of maximum tension occurs just before the simultaneous reveal of the actual choices, where surprises and betrayals are guaranteed.

3. Combat and Rewards
Winning a clash grants rewards based on a grid on each Path card. There are four types of loot: Alaxite, Gold coins, Valuable resources, and Rare resources. The grid shows that the larger the party, the smaller the individual reward.

This means that at the beginning of the game, you will be more motivated to form alliances. However, toward the end, once you are equipped with more powerful weapons, you will try to face threats alone, pushing other Erranti away with the help of Spell and Condition cards.

After exploration, rewards are assigned. Players who went to the Camp (East) earn 2 Gold coins and can buy or sell resources.

4. The Player's Hand, Spells, and Conditions
The resources you draw make up your hand. These can be Equipment cards, which are used to either complete or replace your current gear on the table. However, they can also be Spells and Conditions, which remain secret in your hand. This hidden information provides an additional element of surprise, as other players do not know what tools you have at your disposal.

Spells allow you to boost your stats, force other players to keep a promise, or even change the positioning of the Erranti. You can hold up to six cards in your hand, allowing for a high number of strategic combinations.

5. Facing the Dawn
The game consists of 15 turns. The final turns belong to "The Dawn" chapter: the difficulty increases as the cataclysm reaches you, putting your Errante’s abilities to the test. Even if you have become powerful through magical equipment and high stats, the final turns will challenge you to the limit.

Victory Condition:
The winner is the player who has obtained the most Alaxite. The game relies on calculating the intentions of others and manipulating their choices to your advantage.

In the event of a tie, the player with the most Gold wins. If a tie still persists, the winner is determined by calculating the total combined value of all Equipment and Resource cards.

Crooks love to play games and cheat. In Fia med fusk you can do both! But wait, did you say you can cheat! Oh yes! Who will win in this crooked game of Parcheeshi.

The game plays like Parcheesi with an additional cheating die that allows you to double your die roll, swap tokens on the track and even  kick other tokens off the board.

Autocards is an accessible and tactical card game where players compete to be the first to get rid of all their cards. It can be played with 2 to 7 players.
Each round, the starting player chooses how many cards to play, and the others follow by playing higher sets of the same number. Can't play? Pass. Play smart and dump your cards as fast as possible!

With fewer than 5 players, when you pass, you can draw extra cards or snag one from the table, adding extra tension. Autocards is easy to learn and delivers tons of fun with family and friends. Ideal for both small or large groups, whether you're a casual gamer or a strategic mastermind!

Experience life's mysteries through one of the greatest tales of contemporary sci-fi with the ultimate game for Dune fans! Monopoly: Dune lets players buy, sell, and trade influence with characters of different factions from the epic franchise, such as Paul, Emperor Shaddam, Princess Irulan and more. Travel the board with custom tokens based on items from the film, including a Hunter Seeker and Gom Jabbar. Outlast the consequences of Fate and Politics to be the last seeker of the spice standing to win!

—description from the publisher

Two games for children to learn about traffic safety:
1. See and be seen:
Turn over one of the tiles and discuss the potential hazards.
2. Cycling where it's safe:
While moving across the game board, players must check the depicted cycling situations for potential hazards.

The box contains a booklet with a story about the journey to school and a dice game on the theme of "Safe Routes to School":

The game depicts a school route where children can unintentionally find themselves in dangerous situations due to distractions or inattention. In the dice game, revealing cards depicting dangerous situations costs chips. Revealing cards depicting safe situations earns chips. The winner isn't the fastest, but the player who arrives at school with the most chips.

This box includes two versions to play:
1. On the sidewalk and when crossing the road:
Children learn from adults how to behave correctly on the sidewalk and how to cross the street safely.
2. Obstacles and distractions:
Adults explain to children the danger of obstacles and distractions in road traffic using an example situation.

Each player has 12 stone in their colour. On their turn, they place a stone on the 3D grid.

The first player who gets four of their stones into a winning line wins. A winning line is either four stones in horizontal, vertical or diagonal line on the same level or in a multi-level row.

ContemPLATED is a dry erase deduction game about guessing worldly license plates. It is also a geography game as players learn about global ISO-2 country codes and country locations.

Components:
2+ Dry erase cards
2+ Dry erase secret holders
 
Setup:
Give each player a game card, secret holder and marker.  Fold the secret holder such that the sides are at a 90-degree angle.
Decide upon which territory to start with, either:
Canada & USA or Worldly plates.
Decide upon the number of rounds to play: usually three.
With the secret holder facing them, each player secretly writes a Territory Code, Fragment and Number. 
 Each player then carefully passes their secret holder to an opposite player such that each player cannot see their own holder.
 
Game Play (Proceeds in Turns):
A player will ask a question.
The question will either be directed for all players to answer or one that requests an answer specific to that player only.
A player either attempts a guess at their own license plate - is my license [code+fragment+number?] or calls out one of the following questions for all players to answer:

     Code is from Canada|Americas|Europe|Africa|Asia ?
     Code is an island?
     Will adding letter [x] to the code make a 3-letter word?
     Will adding letter [x] to the fragment make a valid 4-letter word?
     What is the sum of digits of the number?
     What is the product of digits of the number?
     What is the difference of digits of the number?


All players provide an answer to their opposite player.
Players record their specific answers on their card and mark clues accordingly.

Making Deductions
For each answer given, record your answer on your dry erase card. Ask questions strategically, remembering that most questions will require all players to reveal answers. Use processes of elimination and deductions to narrow down their assumptions, to formulate appropriate questions and finally attempt to guess their entire license plate.

Tracking Questions & Answers:
Keep track of all answers on your game board. 
        
Making a Guess:
When ready, a player may ask a specific question related only to their license plate.

Winning:
The first person to correctly guess their plate will score 3 points; 2 points for the second person; 1 for the third person.
After playing the predetermined number of rounds, the winner will have the most points. In case of a tie, play again.

—description from the designer
A Monopoly-like game focusing on Leicester, Massachusetts.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Besides the base game, the Deluxe Edition also contains the miniature mutation attachements and the two expansions "The Plight of the People" and "The Palace Visitors" designed by Dávid Turczi and Jonny Pac.


As mining activities intensified in the twilight years of the industrial revolution, competition for natural resources grew fierce and industrialists burrowed themselves deeper and deeper into the sodden depths of the earth. One mining operation dug deeper than any other, and the events that followed this endeavour have become the stuff of myth and legend. What little information is known about the venture centres on the discovery of a new and powerful element, an element known simply as ‘The Great Shard’.

The Great Shard’s mystical properties not only accelerated the industrial revolution dramatically, but also altered the world around it, spawning creatures and phenomena that defied every natural law imaginable. The world would never be the same. Soon, the mad industrialist known only as ‘the Keeper’ had learned to control the power of the Great Shard. Their lust for progress extended beyond the industrial world through which they had gained their power, and morphed into an obsession with biogenetic modifications. People who indulged in this practice of self mutation became known as the Mutagen.

Now, many years later, the Keeper of the Shard is nearing the end of his unnaturally extended life, and you are one of four Mutagen in the running to take over their position of power. The Keeper will be observing your every move, and will choose only one successor before he draws his final breath.

Mutagen is a customisable worker placement game set in a 1920s biopunk universe. Each player has four workers that each react differently to the areas they are placed in. Each time a player places a worker, they will activate that location's action, plus an additional action determined by the worker's type. Players can unlock the full potential of biological upgrades, by infusing their workers with biological upgrades that will grant them a decisive edge over their opponents.

Each worker location has been forever transformed by vast bio-enhanced changes. Collect elements from the floating cities and use them in various ways. Trade them for objective cards, establish dominance over particular elements through strategic investments, or harness their energy to upgrade your airship. With each move, you accumulate points, driving you closer to victory at different stages of the game.

The player who has best harnessed the newfound powers of this biopunk universe, and used them to collect the most amount of points after four years (rounds) will win the game.

-description from the publisher

P.E.N is a shedding game, where the players aim to play all of the cards from their hand first.

The lead player chooses one of the available combinations of cards to play (e.g. single card in blue or a pair of numbers), flips over the corresponding card, and then play proceeds.

As combinations are flipped over, there are more points available to a player who plays all of their cards, and the combinations are not available again until, basically, all of them have been used once.

The person with the most points, once someone has 10 points at the end of a round, wins.
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

description:
Buildin' Tycoon is a shedding game, where in addition to playing cards, players play buildings.

At the start of the game, players can only play a single card on their turn. As the game continues, players can use cards from their hand as buildings that allow them to play additional cards, alter the value of their cards, etc. Generally, constructing these buildings require a player to add additional cards to their hand - making it harder to go out.

The first person to play all of their cards wins.
publishers: Southern Cross Games (サザンクロスゲームズ)

description:
In the cooperative game Mummy Dino (also known as Dino Mama), winning is a shared team effort. 
To win, players must work together to collect all 9 wooden eggs and safely return them to the nest (the hole in the center of the board) before the game ends. The game ends immediately in a victory once the final egg is placed in the nest.

Players take turns rolling a wooden die and performing actions to move the eggs toward the nest:
    Rolling an Egg: If the die shows an egg, you place an egg of that color into a matching circle on the rotating game board.
    Moving Mummy Dino: If you roll "Mummy Dino," you rotate the board (left or right).

Jenga version with green blocks, Tarzan figures, and cards featuring the same figures.
The classic game variant is expanded with additional rules and victory points, depending on which figure is depicted on the block that is drawn.
In each turn, the active player draws a block from the tower. If it shows one of the 6 Tarzan figures depicted on the cards, the player receives the corresponding card. If there are no more cards left, the game continues.
There are also Tarzan characters that trigger special actions, such as stealing a card from another player or skipping a turn.
Then, as usual in Jenga, the block is placed on top of the tower and it is the next player's turn.
The game ends when the tower collapses. Then each player's points are counted. Points are awarded for the cards collected, bonus points for various combinations of cards, and negative points if you were the player who caused the tower to collapse.
The player with the highest score wins.
In real time, players cooperatively play Marvel superhero cards into a grid, matching card colours, in order to gain a high enough "strike" number to beat the villain's "energy" number.

boardgame: The Zach Attack! Scratch 'n Solve Puzzle Pack
description:
The Zach Attack! Scratch 'n Solve Puzzle Pack is a collection of six different scratch-off solitaire games. Each game involves scratching off different spaces and collecting the reward or penalty shown. Each game has different mechanics, but they all have logic rules to help you deduce what might be hidden in each space, along with a push-your-luck mechanic. The games include:


     Danger! In the Temple of Malice - Navigate a dungeon to collect medallions, dodge spikes, and escape before you're trapped.



     Task Force '86 - A battleship-like game where you must sink all enemy ships. Many spaces have enemy missiles which hit your own ships, but there are logic rules for where enemy ships and missiles can be placed.



     Max Midnight: Better Dead Than Never - Infiltrate a supervillian's stronghold and scratch off spaces to collect punch cards to foil the villian's schemes. Henchmen and robots add a push-your-luck element as running into too many enemies will be game over.



     Signal to Noise - Hack your employer to steal their data before escaping. The game has a collection of grids with clues on how much game-ending ICE is in each row/column, and each uncovered ICE says how many adjacent spaces contain ICE.



     Dragons Over Dunkirk - Explore with your squad of soldiers to eliminate every dragon nest. Dragons will attack your party, but each dragon also gives a clue about nearby nests.



     Capital Offense - Collect investments as spaces to fill in a grid, and fill every space on a grid to obtain weapons for defending yourself from violent rivals.

boardgame: Dying Light: The Board Game – Stretch Goals Box
description:
Take the City to another level with all the stretch goals unlocked during the Kickstarter campaign for Dying Light: The Board Game, conveniently packed in a tuckbox.

The expansion contains all stretch goals unlocked during the Kickstarter campaign for Dying Light: The Board Game, provided in a single box. This includes the First Pilgrim, Authority, Ronin, Fire Devil, and Nimble Ninja equipment packs; new Runners: Lauri, Yaqeen, Favio, and Neyra—with included miniatures; new Infected: the Bolter, the Charger, and the Bomber; supplementary mechanics: paragliding, Runner exposure cards, event cards; Heritage Items: charms, weapons, and utility items; new structures and terrain packs: bridges, Military Area, and Bandit Camp; as well as night activation chips and UV stickers.

boardgame: Survival: le jeu dont vous êtes le héros
description: On our planet where the climate has sadly outdated, at 18h40 that day, everything collapses... You will have to survive in a story where you can become heroes! A game like the book you are heroes Welcome to Survival! Accompany the Wang family who are struggling with a terrible ecological cataclysm that may well be the last we have to know. Experience this episode of disaster with Mohamed the father of the family, Mary the mother, Sofia their little daughter and John the brother of Mary. In this story-game for two to four players, you will experience an interactive fiction in which each of your choices and each of your decisions will have a significant impact. Here, no randomness, no roll of dice, no board, only a story with 3 types of cards that will form on the game table your itinerary with your doubts and your arguments to convince all players to go to the same decision. Depending on your choices, the adventure of our four heroes will end more or less well and only 1 end out of the 6 proposed will be truly happy. Try to save them all! The game contains 160 cards including: - 8 tutorial cards with the rules of the game - 4 character cards - 118 story cards: they present the situations and choices that allow you to progress in your adventure by taking different paths. - 30 key cards: during your adventure, you will be able to choose to accumulate or unload these cards. Some paths of story cards can only be tracked if you have the corresponding key cards. Thus every choice has implications for the rest of the history.

Players take turns expanding pipe networks from a shared central water system, carefully choosing which tiles to place and where. The goal is simple—but far from easy: be the first to build an unbroken pipeline that reaches five levels out from the centre.

Along the way, players can block and disrupt their opponents, turning the race into a tense battle of timing, positioning, and nerve. Every placement matters, and a single well-timed move can bring victory—or bring everything to a sudden stop.

Quick to learn, sharp to play, and full of interaction, Pipe Down rewards smart planning and bold moves in equal measure.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Winds of Numa Sera: Forgotten Keep
description:
Five new item cards for the game where 3-4 players that shares the same world and characters as the acclaimed graphic novel from Dark Horse.

Each player controls a different asymmetric kingdom that comes with a trio of unique heroes to play as and customize. There are four distinct paths to victory, and whoever is first to achieve any 2 of them, wins the game.

Vast, beautiful, and dangerous, the world of Ethera is full of unique cultures and kingdoms all vying for control of the landscape. From the ordained bloodline of Numa Sera to the valiant knights of Dena Vale, the majestic “cat people” of Meran, and the pillaging hordes of Siaa De’e, each force will stop at nothing to advance its power.

As the leader of your people, your goal is to amass resources and create the most powerful kingdom in all of Ethera so your name will be remembered long after you are gone. There are several ways to achieve this: by conquering strongholds, slaying your
opponents’ heroes, mastering skills, or earning new class titles. You can rule benevolently, cruelly, or anything in between. The choice is yours.

Whichever path you take, you will need to acquire assets to advance your heroes and grow your kingdom. Through exploration of Ethera’s many distinct environments, you will uncover rare items and enlist new allies to assist in your campaign. But exploration also has its risks: Ethera is a dangerous place, full of ancient secrets and hidden obstacles.

So be ever vigilant…

The winds of Numa Sera are blowing.

—description from the publisher

In Cownado!!, players race to herd cows to safety while avoiding the devastating tornado! On your turn, you roll the dice to move cows around the board, using strategy and a bit of luck to keep them away from the swirling storm. Random events and changing weather conditions add chaos that can hinder your progress or disrupt opponents.

The game combines light strategy, player interaction, and unpredictable elements for an engaging family experience. It’s designed to be easy to learn with fast turns and colorful components.

boardgame: Trivial Pursuit: Parks and Recreation Edition
description: Return to Pawnee with this special travel-sized Trivial Pursuit game featuring 600 questions about the beloved comedy series. Pit yourself against your friends and family to see who is the master of recreational knowledge. See if you can answer questions in each of six categories: Citizens of Pawnee, Tour the City, Very Good Category (All Things Ron), Parks Department, Say What?, and Treat Yo'Self. This is the perfect game for a trip to the local park for a game day in the sunshine!

Features all kinds of fierce creatures to test your memory and observation skills
150 individual questions and 25 conversation starter topics
One or more players will enjoy a game in around 10 minutes with rapid turn based gameplay
Fun & educational for kids, developing the skills needed for life-long learning
Game contains: 25x game cards, 1x 6-sided die, 1x ten second timer

boardgame: Les Soeurs de l'Alliance: Les Alliés de l'Eden
description:
Les Soeurs de l'Alliance is a card game based on a deck-building system, where you can evolve your cards and acquire new ones during the game. Every choice counts, and your decisions will directly influence your progress and style of play. The extensions will bring new challenges, new bosses and new Action cards, with the ability to customize your deck before each game, in the spirit of a JCE.

The confrontation between Nethopha and Og was a titanic test. Under a sky ignited by fire and thunder, Nethopha agilantly dodged the monster’s destructive assaults. Driven by Noa's unshakable will, he eventually detects Og's weak spot: his magma heart, exposed between the stone plates in the hollow of his skull. In a final momentum, Nethopha plunged his sword into this glowing heart, triggering an explosion that marked the end of the creature. The ground vibrated under Og's roar, and a rain of sparks fell on Eden, like a blessing.

But this victory left a bitter taste. Noa, the protective spirit, contemplated the horizon with a dark resolution. Asmoth, by his greed and excess, had already inflicted too much suffering on Eden and her children. Noa understood then that defending his home was no longer enough. We had to put an end to the tyranny of this crazy king. She turned her thoughts to a legend as old as Eden himself: The Sisters of the Covenant.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Les Soeurs de l'Alliance: La Chute d'Asmoth
description:
Les Soeurs de l'Alliance is a card game based on a deck-building system, where you can evolve your cards and acquire new ones during the game. Every choice counts, and your decisions will directly influence your progress and style of play. The extensions will bring new challenges, new bosses and new Action cards, with the ability to customize your deck before each game, in the spirit of a JCE.

The confrontation between Nethopha and Og was a titanic test. Under a sky ignited by fire and thunder, Nethopha agilantly dodged the monster’s destructive assaults. Driven by Noa's unshakable will, he eventually detects Og's weak spot: his magma heart, exposed between the stone plates in the hollow of his skull. In a final momentum, Nethopha plunged his sword into this glowing heart, triggering an explosion that marked the end of the creature. The ground vibrated under Og's roar, and a rain of sparks fell on Eden, like a blessing.

But this victory left a bitter taste. Noa, the protective spirit, contemplated the horizon with a dark resolution. Asmoth, by his greed and excess, had already inflicted too much suffering on Eden and her children. Noa understood then that defending his home was no longer enough. We had to put an end to the tyranny of this crazy king. She turned her thoughts to a legend as old as Eden himself: The Sisters of the Covenant.

—description from the publisher

Relaxed medieval life, making wine together,

This game progresses through repeated "pressing phase" and "working phase." First, in the pressing phase, you convert grapes (tokens) harvested from your vineyard into wine in a press and acquire wine barrels. In the subsequent working phase, you can serve wine to people to increase your friendships and use their help to improve the quality of the wine. High-quality wine will solve someone's problems and earn you VP.

The UNO game players love features graphics inspired by favorite characters from DC Comics' Batman.
Game play matches the classic card game where players match colors and numbers to get rid of all their cards.
The special 'Bat Signal' rule forces an opponent to draw cards until they get a card with either Batman, Robin, Batgirl or Nightwing on it, and then add all the cards to their hand!
When down to one card, don't forget to yell, 'UNO!'
Designed for 2 to 10 players ages 7 years and older, the collectible card game makes a great toy for fans of Batman comics!

The two reaper households of Ashenford and Rosethorn have haunted the Mournshade graveyard for centuries.

In Mournshade, players represent their reaper houses and guide their spectrals across the gloom of the cemetery. You will expand your family’s claim by placing cards to form a shared map of graves, dahlias, groves, and dirt while dodging traps. Each player begins with a secret scoring condition, and every condition awards points to both players. As the graveyard grows still, the household with the highest tally claims the Raven’s favor.

-description from designer

Imaginarium: Horror Stories contains 98 new illustrations, each of which contains funny, beautiful and unusual monsters. It's terribly interesting to see what's inside this box!

The new themed "Imaginarium" will shock your imagination (and tickle your nerves a bit). Here you can see Baba Yaga, a multi-eyed puppy and even a friendly garden scarecrow. Try to look into the face of a child's fear of babayka and find out what fears your friends have!

The rules will be familiar to those who have already played Imaginarium. The players take turns becoming the leaders and guess the association for one of the colorful illustrations "by hand". The rest select a picture suitable for this image from their stocks, and then all the cards are put to a general vote. The task of the players is to determine which card was original. The presenter will get maximum victory points if most of the players recognize his picture, but not all. Therefore, balance between clarity and mystery in your associations.

You can play with the whole family or a group of friends of up to 7 people. It's only better to assemble a team over 6 years old in order to understand the rules exactly. The game will definitely appeal to all fans of the slightly titillating atmosphere, fans of previous versions of Imaginarium and those who love unusual illustrations.

•••

Имаджинариум: Страшилки — это 98 новых иллюстраций, на каждой из которых вы найдёте забавных, красивых и необычных монстров. Жутко интересно взглянуть, что внутри этой коробки!

Новый тематический «Имаджинариум» потрясёт ваше воображение (и немного пощекочет нервы). Здесь вы сможете увидеть Бабу Ягу, многоглазого щенка и даже дружелюбное огородное чучело. Попробуйте заглянуть в лицо детскому страху перед бабайкой и узнайте, какие страхи есть у ваших друзей!

Правила будут знакомы тем, кто уже играл в «Имаджинариум». Игроки по очереди становятся ведущими и загадывают ассоциацию на одну из красочных иллюстраций «с руки». Остальные подбирают подходящую под этот образ картинку из своих запасов, а после все карточки выкладывают на общее голосование. Задача игроков определить, какая карточка была оригинальной. Ведущий получит максимум победных очков, если его картинку узнает большинство игроков, но не все. Поэтому балансируйте между понятностью и загадочностью в своих ассоциациях.

Сыграть можно всей семьёй или компанией друзей до 7 человек. Только лучше собирать команду старше 6 лет, чтобы точно разобраться в правилах. Игра точно понравится всем любителям слегка щекочущей нервы атмосферы, фанатам предыдущих версий Имаджинариума и тем, кто любит необычные иллюстрации.

Shih Tzu is a fast-paced zoo building game that starts out calm and strategic, then gradually introduces chaos. Players compete to build the most prestigious zoo by managing animal diets, habitats, and card synergies, while adapting to twists that can disrupt even well-planned strategies.
Twist of Fate cards and the Shih Tzu add unpredictability, rewarding flexibility, timing, and smart decision making. Beneath the approachable rules is meaningful strategic depth, with layered choices, evolving combos, and multiple paths to victory.
The artwork brings the animals and their misadventures to life and supports the game’s sense of humour, making the cards easy to read and distinct. Easy to learn but rich to explore, SHIH TZU works as a family friendly gateway game that continues to engage more experienced players over repeated plays.

Shih Tzu is game from independent games publisher Average Bear Games Ltd. 
Contact: hello@average-bear.games 
To find out more information or buy direct visit average-bear.games

Enter an intense psychological duel where every word can betray you and every silence can become a trap.
Bluff, manipulate, and anticipate your opponent’s intentions to gain the upper hand. Fast-to-learn rules and tense, nerve-racking rounds, perfect for two players seeking strong sensations.

Objective: achieve three victories to win the game.

⚡ Fast to play – dynamic rounds, ideal for chaining games.

? Strategic – victory depends solely on your ability to read (or deceive) your opponent.

? Addictive – impossible to stop at just one game!

Are you ready to enter the bluffing arena?

Box contents: 17 cards + 1 Rules card.

- - - - - - - - - - - -
Rules :

WereWolf DUEL

In the hamlet of Yonder-By-Yonder, there are only three of you. While you learn that one of you is a werewolf, you receive no news from your third neighbor.
You have only five days to act… and survive.

- - -
OBJECTIVE OF THE GAME:
Be the first to achieve 3 victories.

- - -
A ROUND:

Place the 3 footprint cards (shoe/wolf) in the center of the table with the shoe side face up.

Create a pile with the 5 SUN/MOON time cards, SUN side face up.

Each player takes into their hand the 3 ACTION cards (SHIELD / TORCH / ATTACK).

Shuffle the 3 ROLE cards (Villager / Werewolf), then place them face down. Each player secretly draws one ROLE card.

- - -
THE ENCOUNTER:

Flip one SUN card to its MOON side.

This is the time to discuss, bluff, and try to read your opponent’s play. Each player chooses one ACTION card and plays it face down.

WARNING: only the Werewolf may play the ATTACK card.

During the discussion time, you may still change your card. When ready, start a countdown and reveal your cards simultaneously.

- - -
RESOLUTION:

- If the ATTACK card was played:

ATTACK vs TORCH = END OF THE ROUND: the WEREWOLF wins.

ATTACK vs SHIELD = END OF THE ROUND: the VILLAGER wins.

- If the ATTACK card was not played:

Flip as many shoe footprint cards to their wolf footprint side as there are TORCH cards played.

- If all 3 wolf footprint cards are visible:

END OF THE ROUND: the VILLAGER(S) win.

Otherwise:

If there are SUN cards remaining, start a new ENCOUNTER.

If none remain, END OF THE ROUND: the WEREWOLF wins, or the two VILLAGERS lose.

NO!SMOKING! is a must follow trick-taking game for 3 to 6 players. The deck consists of 4 suits, with different rank ranges. Each round a restriction card is revealed which shows the sum limit of cards played to the current trick. If the sum doesn't exceed the restriction, the winner receives a positive point; if it does, they receive a negative point.

Each player has a one-time power they can use, and there are item cards that can be acquired.

The person with the most points at the end of the game wins.

A cryptic notebook leads your archaeological expedition deep in the jungle to an ancient temple rumored to hold incredible artifacts. Your goal: Explore the rooms of the temple and escape before your energy runs out!

But that's easier said than done because the temple is a maze of movable walls and mysterious mechanisms! Now it's time to work together. Using your action cards, you'll navigate through this unique structure, move walls, and even rotate the entire temple!

In the action-packed co-operative game Temple Twist, players take turns choosing an action card from their hand to strategically manipulate the three-dimensional temple and move the explorer cube through the maze of rooms. Play through a series of increasingly difficult expeditions, or pick up some challenge cards and dive right in. This game combines strategy, co-operation, spatial reasoning, and hands-on action with a first-of-its-kind 3D-maze-gameboard that changes as you play.

LASTONE is a trick-taking game for 2 players that takes place on a postcard.

In general, one player will win a trick and the other will make a choice about the next trick (e.g. restrictions on what suits can be played.). There are points available after tricks 1, 6, and 7, and some at the end of a hand.

The person with the most points after 2 hands wins.

The game is simple. One player draws a Question Card, everyone else plays an Answer Card. Whoever played the funniest, most outrageous or most controversial card gets to keep it, the first player to collect 7 cards wins! We tossed some dare cards in the mix just to keep things spicy.

—description from the publisher

Crissement Sketch is a party game where players create funny and often absurd mini-scenes by filling in blanks with word cards. It is very similar in spirit to L’Osti d’jeu and Cards Against Humanity.

In each round, one player is the judge. The judge reads a short sketch or scenario out loud that contains one or more blanks. All other players choose word cards from their hand to fill in those blanks and submit them secretly.

The judge then reads all the completed sketches out loud. The results are usually ridiculous and meant to make everyone laugh.

The judge chooses the sketch they find the funniest. The player who submitted the winning card gets a point. After a set number of rounds, the player with the most points wins the game.

The Buddha sends the players on a dangerous journey to the West to collect scriptures. Along the way, they face many challenges and tests. In the end, only one player will finish the mission.
This game is inspired by the characters and setting of Journey to the West, incorporating randomized numbers and quest-based objectives centered on the pilgrimage for scriptures. Through player interaction, participants apply arithmetic thinking and strategic decision-making to complete tasks and compete for victory, offering an experience that balances learning and fun.

The Bordeaux alternative board expansion to Viticulture offers players a starting boost, 4 full worker placement seasons, a wake-up chart full of benefits, a major new “expert” action that permanently upgrades an action for you, revisions to existing actions, a trade market, face-up vine and wine order cards to choose from when drawing, and end-game conversion of grapes, wine, and coins to victory points.

--Compatibility: This expansion is compatible with most modules in other expansions (Tuscany structures and special workers, Moor Visitors, and Rhine Valley).

--Box Fit: The new board easily fits into the Viticulture box (or the Wine Crate), especially since the back side is the original Viticulture board, allowing it to permanently replace the space that board currently occupies in the box.

--Not Standalone: The Viticulture core game is required to play the Bordeaux expansion.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
GENE-SCAVVERZ is an agnostic miniature wargame where players build a warband of mutants who fight over gene material and territory. 
The objective is to be the largest dealer of gene material and the fiercest warband in the irradiated wastes.

GENE-SCAVVERZ is a wargame set in a dystopian world, one destroyed by planet wide nuclear conflict. which left a warped and scarred land in its place.
In order to survive in a nearly unhabitable world, all living things needed to adapt or die.
The consequence of this adaptation was rapid onset mutation.
The Human race, if you could call it that, would never be the same again.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Bear Marches West: 12 Scenarios for 1980s NATO vs Warsaw Pact Wargames
description:
NATO kept the Warsaw Pact behind the Berlin Wall until its dissolution - but what if it hadn't?

The Bear Marches West contains 12 wargame scenarios set during a fictional Warsaw Pact invasion of West Germany in the 1980s. All the scenarios are based on battles depicted in well-known novels, and are designed to be used with whatever rules the players wish.

Each scenario includes the battle's context, weather conditions, deployments, force lists and a colour map. 
The scenarios range in scale from small skirmishes, with a company on each side, to large engagements, with Soviet regiments bearing down on smaller but better-equipped NATO units.

In this card game players take on the role of mountain explorers, visiting high peaks, natural parks, and alpine areas. Along the way, they encounter plants and animals, upgrade their equipment, and gain experience to deal with unexpected situations that can happen in the mountains. The goal is to overcome problems and stay in the game longer than everyone else.

On each turn, a player draws a card and then discards one, choosing how to play: forming sets to face obstacles by adding up experience points, using the ability of an expedition card, playing an action card such as Alpine Rescue, or passing. Unexpected events can interfere at any moment, forcing players to manage their cards and make careful choices!

As the game goes on, players who cannot handle the challenges are eliminated. The winner is the last player still in the game, having shown the best balance between preparation, timing, and luck.

The dwarves of Gorenfrost, driven by greed, have dug too deep in their endless search for gold and ore. In their recklessness, they’ve disturbed what should have remained buried. From the darkness below, Queen Freya has awakened. Condemned for her past deeds and entombed for centuries, the undead queen now rises with horrors in her wake. Summoning the dead from their sacred rest, she seeks to reclaim the power that was once hers and nothing will stand in her way.

With this expansion to Boardquest: Tales of Liria, you'll get:

     Two new factions: Dwarves & Undead 
     New hero skills for each faction
     New units for each faction 
     New monsters to battle 
     New items to equip 
     New modular maps to explore 


—description from the publisher

Star Trek The Next Generation Interstellar Board Game:
Challenge your friends to a race through seven years of Star Trek: The Next Generation with this brand new board game. Players draw cards that send them on missions from famous episodes of TNG. Then race around the board trying to complete them first. Along the way you'll face hazards from an intron virus that de-evolves the crew, to a visit from Q.

The game is designed for up to eight players but only the best Starfleet officer will win!

Fast-paced board game where players navigate the Galaxy as the Enterprise-D crew, completing missions and strategic challenges.

Navigate through space, plan efficient routes, and complete missions before other players to prove yourself as the top Starfleet officer.

Features iconic elements from Star Trek: The Next Generation including the Enterprise-D, holodecks, and Klingon encounters.

Face various hazards during your journey, including malfunctioning holodecks, sentient computers, and the dramatic Klingon civil war.

Combines route planning, mission completion, and tactical decision-making for an engaging multiplayer experience.

—description from the publisher

Fifteen minutes. Three to Six players. Escape the Arctic or be the last one standing.

Panic Zones: Arctic Edition is a fast, take-that card game where survival means outwitting your friends before the cold, or they, eliminate you.

There are two ways to win: Collect all 3 different Rescue Cards to escape, or outlast every other player as they get eliminated.

The game is turn-based, played with two decks: Discovery and Advantage Decks.

You end your turn by drawing from the Discovery Deck: it could hand you the card you need to escape or other pleasant surprises, but it could also throw a Polar Bear or other obstacle to eliminate you, or a Panic event stirring chaos for everyone at the table.

Shift the odds to your favor by playing Advantage Cards to steal the card you need from opponents or grab it from the discard pile, weaken or disrupt your opponents, block threats or redirect threats.

With quick turns and a different story every game, rounds end fast and the rematch starts immediately.

A card game so cold, it'll freeze your friendships.

Discover and travel across every Argentine province without leaving home with Conociendo mi Argentina! This card game is perfect for sharing with family or friends while learning about our cultural and geographic diversity.

With a deck of 24 cards (two riddles and two fun facts per province), you’ll explore everything from capitals to traditions, foods, and local architecture. Whoever guesses the most riddles becomes Argentina’s ultimate expert.

Chef’s Menu is a light, fast-paced card game where players compete to create the most delicious three-course meal. Build a starter, main course, and dessert by collecting ingredients, reacting to unexpected events, and making smart choices about when to expand your menu.

Each round, players either roll a die to gather new cards or visit the market to buy exactly what they need. Ingredient cards are used to develop dishes, while event cards introduce timing, interaction, and surprise—some resolve instantly, others can be saved and played at just the right moment.

Each dish has a limited number of ingredient slots and may reward or punish certain ingredient symbols. Special bonuses can be triggered as soon as the right ingredient is added, while end-game scoring may grant extra points—or penalties—depending on how well a dish meets its criteria. Ingredient colors also matter: once a dish is started, all ingredients added to it must match the same color, encouraging careful planning and commitment.

Players may develop their menu at any time, but once a card is placed on the table it usually stays there until the end of the game. Only specific event cards allow dishes to be replaced or altered, making timing and risk assessment an important part of play.

The game ends when a player completes a full menu—starter, main course, and dessert filled with ingredients—and declares “Bon appétit!”. All other players then take one final turn before scores are tallied.

Points are awarded for ingredients, dish criteria, event bonuses, and leftover coins. The player with the highest total score has created the best menu—and wins the game.

Chef’s Menu is easy to learn, quick to play, and offers a satisfying mix of tactical choices, light interaction, and replayability for families and casual gamers alike.

A whole family of characters to build the funniest stories and scenes!

You’ll be able to express ideas and emotions while boosting creativity. With a big set of finger puppets and creative prompts, your imagination can take off to create a GREAT story.

Play in rounds, inventing stories using one or more characters, whoever puts on the best “show” wins!

The city of Druzhny holds its breath in anticipation of the celebration – the long-awaited metro will soon open! But behind the grand facade, tension reigns: Service Dogs, loyal guardians of order, scurry through the streets, sniffing out the slightest trace of threat. Their noses won't fail them, they can't fail them! However, in the shadows, among rusty pipes and abandoned warehouses, the Wolf Resistance lurks...

They've already committed a daring theft and now know the shocking truth hidden in the master plan: the stations are built, but the tunnels... well, there are no tunnels! A dangerous game begins for the wolves – they must deliver the incriminating evidence and escape the city before the bloodhounds pick up their scent.

Immerse yourself in the tense atmosphere of a secret confrontation in the alternate world of Sobakistan! Собакистан: Ищейки ("Sobakistan: Bloodhounds") is an asymmetrical game that invites you to feel like part of a detective thriller, where the success of the entire operation depends on your cunning and premonition. One player must think like a senior bloodhound, relying on logic and instinct to uncover the conspiracy, while the team of wolves operates in the shadows, using hidden signals and bluffs.

The unique "one against all" gameplay, the Soviet modernist style, and an endless variety of scenarios at each turn create a unique experience. This is more than just a board game – it's a battle of wits where you will either rise to the top of your career or execute the most daring escape in history!

How to play?
The goal of the game is to carry out an intense operation where the wolves, led by a contact, must leave incriminating evidence in a secluded location and escape the city through the exit station, working as a team. In turn, the senior tracker must catch the wolves: confiscate incriminating evidence and arrest the violators, taking their tokens!

The key element of the game is the route cards, which define three key points on the board: the starting station, where the wolves begin the game; a hiding place for leaving incriminating evidence; and an exit from the city for escape. Only the contact knows all three points, while the wolves only have the starting position at the beginning of the game.

Preparation begins with assigning roles: one player becomes the senior tracker, another the contact, and the rest become wolves. The board is assembled from random station tokens, creating a unique metro map each time. The wolves receive boards for stealth movement, and the tracker receives squad figures, a surveillance log, and markers.

The game consists of a sequence of four stages, repeated each round, creating a tension between the two sides:


     Liaison Turn: The Wolf Resistance player places one or two clue cards with associations (e.g., "Dark," "Calm") to direct the wolves toward the objectives indicated on the route map. They can combine cards with "No" to create counter-meanings, making it more difficult for the tracker to decipher. The liaison then draws up to five cards from the deck, preparing for the next round.
     Wolves' Turn: All wolves simultaneously analyze the liaison's clues and secretly write the numbers of the selected stations on their boards with markers. Movement is only permitted to adjacent stations!
     Senior Tracker's Turn: The player makes up to two squad movements, but cannot move directly between stations—this is the main tactical challenge of this role! Squads can enter stations to check, but at the end of their turn, they automatically return to their districts. The Bloodhound can use special abilities: cordon off areas and metro lines, conduct searches, and employ other operational measures.
     Final phase of the round: The Wolves decide whether to leave the incriminating evidence in a secluded location or escape through the exit, guided by hidden objectives on the route map. Hunt cards allow both sides to perform special actions during the round.


An important feature: the liaison and the Wolves can comment on the game, providing clarification and discussing strategy, but without direct instructions or specific hints. All comments are audible to the Bloodhound, creating additional tension and requiring careful wording.

The game continues until one of the following conditions is met:

     All Wolves are arrested
     All Wolves have escaped the city
     All spaces on the Wolf boards are filled


Who wins?
After the game, the sides count their tokens. Each Wolf checks whether they correctly identified the secluded location and exit station. For each correctly guessed target, the wolf team receives a corresponding token. The senior tracker takes tokens from the wolves for each mistake and for each team member arrested.

The side with the most tokens wins. In the event of a tie, the senior tracker wins – law and order prevail!

—description from the publisher (translated)

•••

Город Дружный затаил дыхание в предвкушении праздника – скоро откроют долгожданное метро!
