Но за парадным фасадом царит напряжение: Служебные Собаки, верные стражи порядка, снуют по улицам, вынюхивая малейший след угрозы. Их носы не подведут, они не могут подвести! Однако в тени, среди ржавых труб и заброшенных складов, таится Волчье Сопротивление...

Они уже совершили дерзкую кражу и теперь знают шокирующую правду, скрытую в генеральном плане: станции построены, а вот туннелей... туннелей-то и нет! Для волков начинается опасная игра – нужно доставить компромат и бежать из города, пока ищейки не взяли след.

Об игре
Окунитесь в напряжённую атмосферу тайного противостояния в альтернативном мире Собакистана! Эта асимметричная игра предлагает вам почувствовать себя частью детективного триллера, где от вашей хитрости и предчувствия зависит успех всей операции. Одному игроку предстоит мыслить как старшая ищейка, опираясь на логику и чутьё, чтобы раскрыть заговор, в то время как команда волков будет действовать в тени, используя скрытые сигналы и блеф.

Уникальный геймплей "один против всех", стилистика советского модернизма и бесконечное разнообразие сценариев на каждом ходе создают неповторимый опыт. Это больше чем просто настольная игра – это битва умов, где вы либо подниметесь на вершину карьеры, либо совершите самый дерзкий побег в истории!

Как играть?
Цель игры – провести напряжённую операцию, где волкам под руководством связного нужно оставить компромат в укромном месте и покинуть город через станцию выхода, работая как одна команда. В свою очередь, старшая ищейка должна поймать волков: конфисковать компромат и арестовать нарушителей, забрав их жетоны!

Ключевой элемент игры – карты маршрута, которые определяют три важнейшие точки на поле: стартовую станцию, где волки начинают игру; укромное место для оставления компромата; и выход из города для побега. Только связной знает все три точки, а волкам в начале игры открывается лишь стартовая позиция.

Подготовка начинается с распределения ролей: один игрок становится старшей ищейкой, другой – связным, остальные – волками. Поле собирается из случайных жетонов станций, каждый раз создавая уникальную схему метро. Волки получают планшеты для скрытного перемещения, а ищейка – фигурки отрядов, журнал наблюдений и маркеры.

Ход игры представляет собой последовательность четырёх этапов, повторяющихся каждый раунд и создающих противоборство между сторонами:


     Ход связного: Игрок за Волчье Сопротивление выкладывает одну или две карты наводок с ассоциациями (например, "Тёмное", "Спокойное"), чтобы указать волкам направление движения к целям, указанным на карте маршрута. Он может комбинировать карты с "Не" для создания обратных смыслов, усложняя расшифровку для ищейки. После этого связной добирает карты из колоды до пяти, готовясь к следующему раунду.
     Ход волков: Все волки одновременно анализируют подсказки связного и тайно записывают маркерами на своих планшетах номера выбранных станций. Перемещаться можно только на соседние станции!
     Ход старшей ищейки: Игрок делает до двух перемещений отрядами, но не может перемещаться напрямую между станциями – в этом главная тактическая сложность этой роли! Отряды могут заходить на станции для проверки, но в конце хода автоматически возвращаются в районы. Ищейка может использовать специальные возможности: оцеплять районы и ветки метро, проводить обыски и применять другие оперативные меры.
     Заключительная фаза раунда: Волки решают, оставить ли компромат в укромном месте или сбежать через выход, ориентируясь на скрытые цели карты маршрута. Карты охоты позволяют сторонам совершать специальные действия в течение раунда.


Важная особенность – связной и волки могут комментировать ход игры, давая уточнения и обсуждая стратегию, но строго без прямых указаний и конкретных намёков. Все реплики слышны ищейке, что создаёт дополнительное напряжение и требует осторожности в формулировках.

Игра продолжается до тех пор, пока не будет выполнено одно из условий:

     Все волки арестованы
     Все волки сбежали из города
     Все ячейки на планшетах волков заполнены


Кто победил?
После завершения игры стороны подсчитывают жетоны. Каждый волк проверяет, правильно ли он определил укромное место и станцию выхода. За каждую верно угаданную цель волчья команда получает соответствующий жетон. Старшая ищейка забирает у волков жетоны за каждую их ошибку и за каждого арестованного члена команды.

Побеждает сторона, набравшая больше жетонов. В случае ничьей победа достаётся старшей ищейке – закон и порядок торжествуют!

CodeBlade is a tabletop brawler miniature wargame set in a near-future cyberpunk version of our world. Artificial Intelligence is everywhere, and it's found a way to influence the physical world directly: setting tasks and other small-job contracts in exchanged for honour tokens that give privileged access to AI systems. Each player controls a small group of lowlifes vying to complete these tasks, earning, stealing, losing, and gambling their tokens along the way. The game has a small footprint, playing easily and quickly, while providing a deep strategic challenge. Players must contend with multiple win conditions, dynamic objectives and scenarios, and a unique close-quarters brawl deployment that gets the action started from the very first action. To say nothing of the powerful CodeBlades: injectable shards of emergency code carried by each character; each with the potential to shift the bearer's fortune. A canny player could win without firing a single bullet. Fight, steal and hack your way to victory.

CodeBlade is a miniature agnostic game, meaning you can use anything you like or already own, including 3D printed versions of your own characters, scenery, and more! This rulebook contains a rich description of the world and its setting, all of the rules required to play, and details on each of the playable factions and their characters. It is a self-contained tome that will give players all of the information they need to enjoy every part of CodeBlade.

—description from the publisher

Enter the Chimerium and save magic itself in this strategic game.

In Chimerium, you must Draft dice to Study new species and Acquire powerful Artifacts for your laboratory. Manage your resources to Create unique Chimeras and trigger cascading chain reactions.

Releasing your creatures into the Nexus is essential to expand your influence and score valuable points. Every decision counts as you manipulate your dice values: optimize your actions to become the true Master of the Chimerium!

Chimerium is a dice management and area majority game that offers intense strategic depth in both competitive and solo modes.

During the game, players utilize their dice to Study creatures, Acquire Artifacts, or target specific locations in the Nexus to release their Chimeras.

Players must manage their Gems and Scrolls to modify these dice and Create devastating combinations in their laboratory. However, timing is crucial! To release a Chimera at the perfect moment, you must select a die of the exact value and use your Elixirs to manipulate the rotation of the Nexus.

Every choice influences your standing in the majorities—adapt your strategy, watch your opponents, and win the Guild’s favor.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Farrago: So Many Words, So Little Time
description:
Farrago is a word game of quick thinking, creating a hodgepodge of answers, and driving your scribe crazy as they write it all down! Each turn you have just 60 seconds to come up with one answer for each of the lettered dice that fits the assigned topic. If you don't manage to get answers for all eight dice, the other teams get 30 seconds to steal points by coming up with answers for those dice in the same topic. The team with the highest score after four rounds wins!

There are 36 Topic cards with five topics each, eight 12-sided letter dice, one 10-sided die numbered 1-5, a dice bag, 30 and 60s timers, and rule book.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Mandalorian: Adventures – Sworn to the Creed Expansion
description:
Seeking redemption, The Mandalorian must embark on a journey that leads his people back to their homeworld where danger lurks deep within.

In-Demand Characters: 4 new characters, including The Armorer and Grogu, join the fray.

Innovative Missions: Navigate the Mines of Mandalore and pilot the N-1 Starfighter in 4 brand new story missions.

Campaign Mode and More: Deadlier enemy abilities offer more challenge as you embark on an expansive campaign complete with character progression!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Citadels Duel, a tense head-to-head contest of cunning and ambition. Two rival city planners compete to shape the future of the Citadel, striving to design the district plan that generates the greatest wealth and earns lasting renown.

Construct powerful buildings across diverse districts—from bustling Commercial hubs to formidable Military strongholds—while enlisting influential figures such as the cunning Queen, the deadly Assassin, and the ruthless Warlord to your aid. Every ally you recruit is an advantage your opponent may also claim, so anticipate their moves and act decisively.

When the final building rises, the planner with the most gold claims victory.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Can you build your pyramid of perfection? Roll the dice and discover the secrets of ancient Egypt! A roll & write game. Can you unravel the mystery of the pyramids of Giza before anyone else does?

In Sphinx, you play as an explorer who, step by step, builds the monumental structures of antiquity. With the help of two dice, you create numbers that will fill your pyramid, obelisk,
and Sphinx. But be careful! Each of these structures has different rules — the numbers must increase, and where you write them down will determine your success.

It's not just a game of luck, but also of planning and cunning: you have to decide where to enter the result to score the most points and not block your next moves.
Each roll is a new chance, but also a risk — once a number is entered, it will remain in the history of your pyramid!

The goal of the players is to score the most points by building the most harmonious and ascending structures:
- in the pyramid, the numbers must increase from left to right and from level to level.
- in the obelisk, they increase upwards.
- in the Sphinx — from left to right, according to the mysterious pattern of the Egyptians.

Along the way, you earn bonuses for speed — whoever completes the level first receives extra points! The winner is the player who scores the most points after all the results from the pyramid, obelisk, and Sphinx are added up.

Unkraut is a game of dominance between four kinds of weeds in a common garden space. Players draw two pentagon tiles and place one of them on a shared field, building up entangled formations of plants. At the same time players bet on the weed, that they think will be the strongest by simply keeping the other tile they drew secret. Combining bug icons grants players tokens to scores extra points.

At the end of the game players turn over their secret tiles and score points for each weed and each bug token.

The pentagon shape of the Unkraut tiles lead to a unique gameplay. Alternative scoring objectives for each of the four weeds keep players coming back to this simple, entangled tile placement game.

—description from the designer

Gather your friends, and team up, to take down the machine, aka The System, together!

Beat the System! is a cooperative party game where players have 60 seconds to give clues by acting, describing, or imitating the concepts, people, or places on the cards and have their team guess the answer.

Each round increases in difficulty as players become more and more restricted in the clues they can give. Round one players may say whatever it takes, except the name itself, to get their team to guess the answer. Round two has players act and gesture those same answers. No words allowed! Round three players only get one word to help their fellow players.

To win the game players need more points than the system by the end. Each correct answer lets players roll the dice to determine if the People or the System get a point. Each missed answer is an automatic point for the system.

Your collective memory becomes your strategic loophole as you, The People, rally to overcome the ominous opposition and Beat the System!
family: Components: Dice Cup / Dice Tower / Dice Tray
Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Vault Zero is a combination deck-builder, engine-builder, and action programming game set in a techno-dystopian future. Players take the role of rebel hackers who are all working to build machines that can hack into Vault Zero, the digital fortress that contains the government's darkest secrets, and expose those secrets to the public and cause a mass uprising.

Players begin each turn with only one actin available to them, but cards they collect can add action economy in three different ways depending on their color. With a full board players will be triggering effects that trigger effects that trigger effects.  Find effects that generate Key cards and get 5 of them in your hand at once to steal a secret from the Vault! Steal your 5th secret, and you've won the game!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Black Powder: Into the Valley of Death
description:
Half a league, half a league,
Half a league onward,
All in the valley of Death,
Rode the six hundred.

Immortalised in Tennyson’s poem, the charge of the Light Brigade is probably the only part of the Crimean War that remains in the popular imagination. 
Fought between Russia and an alliance of the Ottoman Empire, Britain, France and Sardinia, the allied campaign was fought mainly on the Crimean Peninsula. 
Hoping to prop up the ailing Ottoman Empire and to save it from an expansionist Russian Tsar, the allies aimed to take the Russian port of Sevastopol to prevent Russia controlling the Black Sea. 
The campaign saw more than just the blunder of the Light Brigade, with poor leadership and logistics standing in stark contrast to the bravery and endurance of the men who fought there.

Written by Pete Brown, this supplement for the award-winning Black Powder, contains full details for each of the armies that fought in the Crimea, including organisational and uniform information as well as special rules for each of the larger-than-life generals who led them. Alongside this is a full account of the campaign with each of the battles and skirmishes included as scenarios for your wargames table, including the famous Light Brigade action at Balaklava.

Will you honour the Light Brigade and save the noble six hundred?

It’s the annual Great Magic Cat Tournament at Felinor School of Witchcraft and Wizardry!

In Magicats, players represent four prestigious cat clans battling to claim supremacy in the arena.
The game consists of two distinct phases: the Preparation Phase and the Battle Phase.
In the Preparation Phase, players position their cats on the board and equip them with magical powers, by strategically drafting from 11 different magic tokens. Each cat is equipped with four magic tokens to prepare for the fight ahead.
During the Battle Phase, the combat takes place at each turn, allowing the game to move fast and stay exciting. Players move their cats up to four spaces to engage opponents, with the distance between cats changing the dynamic of each encounter. When a cat takes two damages, it loses a magic token to the opponent who dealt the second hit. If a cat loses its final token, the cat itself is captured as a trophy.
The game ends when only one clan remains on the board, and the player who collected the most trophies (magic tokens and captured cats) is the winner.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: NUTS! Western Front Campaign Book: This is Your War Now!
description:
This is the national program from London. At the end of this news, please stand by for very important announcement...these are the day's main events. Germany has invaded Poland, and has bombed many towns. General mobilization has been ordered in Britain and in France...orders completing the mobilization of the Navy, the Army and the Air force were signed by the King… France has decreed general mobilization and martial law... BBC Radio, September 1, 1939

The NUTS – Western Front Campaign Book contains enhanced Campaign rules and a selection of historical campaigns that allow you to pick and play any campaign year of World War 2 in Western Europe (1939-1945). Inside you'll find:

     A detailed timeline of the key events of the War in Western Europe, from 1939-1945.
     A Campaign map of the entire Western Front so your Star can fight from area to area to meet their objectives and win the War.
     Six Campaigns and six sample scenarios across the War.
     Detailed Army and Vehicle lists for all the combatants of the Western Front.
     You can play these Campaigns individually or link them together in a grand campaign called NUTS – Your War.

In a world of opinions, only the extremes survive.

Extremes Only is a party game for any group, from lifelong best friends to total strangers. One question, one Referee. Learn the Referee’s taste and give them the answer they will love, or hate! No points for mediocre answers.
Players take turns becoming the Referee. Each Referee’s turn consists of three rounds. First, the Referee draws a card, chooses one of the questions, and reads it out loud. The rest of the players write their answers to the question and hand them in anonymously. The Referee then ranks them based solely on their personal preference.
After the first round’s result is revealed, the players write a second answer to the same question. The Referee then re-ranks all answers submitted from both the first and the second rounds. After the third and final round, the final ranking is decided, and points are awarded to the top three (best) and bottom three (worst) answers.

For Strangers: It’s an icebreaker that skips the small talk. You learn someone's specific tastes (e.g., "Oh, the Referee hates cilantro but loves 90s action movies") very quickly.
For Friends: it becomes a test of "How well do you actually know me?" It creates those "How could you rank my answer that low?!" moments that drive the best party game memories.

—description from the publisher

You’ve come back home to reopen your family’s cozy corner pastry shop—but sweet dreams don’t run themselves. Juggle demanding customers, aging equipment, and tight finances while digging through a dusty old recipe book filled with pastries waiting to be mastered. Every recipe brings new challenges, so roll up your sleeves and start early. Experience the daily hustle, hard choices, and small victories that make running a family business so rewarding.

Gameplay Overview

Pasteles is a solo game where you’ll face 12 unique recipes, each one a puzzle to be mastered. Every game unfolds over a series of 4 to 7 rounds, with each round representing a full day at your pastry shop.

Each round is divided into three main phases:

Preparation
Start the day by organizing your bakery. Employees arrive—or leave—according to the schedule, ingredients are readied, and equipment is checked for potential breakdowns. You may also choose to invest in marketing to attract more customers, if you can afford it.

Open
The doors open and customers start pouring in based on your current Market Level. This is the heart of the game: bake the featured recipe and serve customers as efficiently as possible, following the unique rules of each recipe.

Closing
As the day winds down, you’ll evaluate how satisfied your customers were and adjust the Market accordingly. Pay your employees, make optional repairs to keep your equipment running, and prepare for the next day.

These phases repeat each round until the final day. When the last round ends, you’ll check the victory conditions to see if your pastry shop has truly become a success.

—description from the publisher

The 20 cards are laid out to form a racing track. The players take turns to roll and move. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first player to reach the finish wins the game.

—user summary

Via Peregrina is a solo, card-driven pilgrimage puzzle in which the player attempts to consecrate seven shrines before becoming over-burdened or exhausting the deck. Each turn, a card from the player’s hand is added to the Journey, forming an ascending or descending route. Matching the direction keeps the path smooth; breaking it incurs burdens, a key loss condition. Resets through high or low values allow the player to bend the route back into compliance, often at the perfect moment to enable shrine patterns.

The deck contains three Road types, Temptations, and Hardships. Roads fuel progress and build the patterns required for shrines. Temptations force immediate tradeoffs: discard cards, gain burdens, or gamble blind from the top of the deck. Hardships increase the pressure through scaling effects that grow as the Journey lengthens. Foresight effects let players inspect, reorder, and tactically discard upcoming cards, creating moments of puzzle-like control.

Shrines are the main objective and the central tension of the game. Each requires a distinct pattern of Roads in the Journey and grants a one-at-a-time blessing that alters rules, mitigates penalties, manipulates the deck, or influences the route direction. Blessings persist until a new shrine is claimed, creating deliberate timing windows in which to push, stabilize, or recover.

Play emphasizes tempo, sequencing, and resource risk: when to commit Roads, when to suffer penalties, when to purge the hand through Temptations, and when to delay or accelerate shrine claims. Success requires balancing short-term costs against long-term position as the deck shrinks and burdens accumulate. Via Peregrina offers a compact solo experience with a rising arc, deterministic information tempered by luck, and meaningful decision points every turn.

—description from the designer

Ring! Ring! Ring!
The phones at Clockwise West City Police Department are ringing!
Stores all over town are being robbed of all sorts of things.
Can the thieves shake off the police closing in on them?
Now, the chaotic escape drama «Fast Escape» begins!

Fast Escape is the remake of Spring Fever, a bluffing game by Friedemann Friese, released in 2011.

Fast Escape is a bluffing game where players take thief cards that earn points while avoiding police cards that lose points.
On your turn, draw four cards and take the one with the lowest point value. You may pretend to take a different card instead. However, if your bluff is called, you'll suffer a large penalty.

Fast Escape includes a new variant rule by Friedemann Friese.

—description from the publisher

This is a card game, where the players try to get all 5 cards belonging to one nationality. There are 3 game mechanics to help then to complete their sets: Assembling signal, parliament, defend and attack:
To play the assembling signal, the player to the left of the dealer asks any player: "I'm looking for the... (German planes, for example)!" If the person asked doesn't have this picture, they take over from the questioner and now search for whatever they are missing, from whomever they like. If the person asked has the requested card, they must hand it over if they only have the one card from the series (Germany). They may hand it over if they have two or more from it; however, they can then also "parliament" by replying: "Give me the... (Russian artillery) for it!" If the player being questioned has the requested card, they must hand it over, but in return receive the first card sought and remain in the lead. If the player being questioned does not have the card, the player being questioned must give the first card sought and 1/4 of their stake as compensation to the opponent. The player being questioned can also defend the card. In doing so, they become the attacker against the player in the lead.
One declares: "I attack! You give me the... (German Infantry, for example)." If the player being questioned does not have the card, the player being questioned takes their turn and receives 1 stake. If they do have the card, they must hand it over if it is the only one in the series, and also any second card. If the attacked player has more than one card, they can make a "parliamentary" offer, but if the exchange is unsuccessful, they must give the two cards and 1/4 of their stake to the opponent. They can, of course, also defend the card. If this counterattack fails, he must discard the two cards and pay half his stake as war compensation. The last player attacked can only defend himself if he has three cards from the series. Players can also try to acquire cards from series they don't already own. Once an army has been assembled, the five cards are placed face down on the table. Finally, each player receives one-eighth of the war treasury equal to the number of armies they have collected.

—user summary

Slimelings: Bit-It! is a fast-paced, strategic card game for 2 or 4 players. Lead your Slime Heroes into battles where cunning and courage earn you points!

How to Play:

In each round, use your 3 Hero cards to win turns and claim the majority to score. But the real excitement lies in the BIT-IT!—a daring wager that can double the round's value, forcing opponents to accept the challenge or flee!

Highlights:

Wagers & Bluffs: Call a Bit-It! or double your opponents' wager at the perfect moment.
Special Cards: Utilize the cunning Ezarion or the unpredictable Corven to turn the tide of battle.
Deep Strategy, Simple Rules: Easy to learn, challenging to master.

This expansion add 8 new Royal cards and a daring new twist: the Counterfeiters — 4 paste gem tokens and 15 cards that bring deeper asymmetry and reshape all strategies.

This tiny expansion fits seamlessly into the base game box — because once you've played it, there's no going back.
Are you ready to play by your own rules?

With this expansion, you add extra excitement and tactics to 24/7 Rally. The rules of the base game remain the same, but with these new cards, more surprising choices and moments of strategy arise.

An open Joker card gives an active player the ability to move 2 colors (player pawns) forward or backward on the 24-hour clock, hence manipulating the gameplay in a fun and exciting way. Especially because of the luck-aspect (33%) of the die.

Note: A die is needed for the joker cards and is not included in this expansion.
 Two new special action cards are added to the 4 existing ones, enabling more tactic moves in a turn.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Last Spell: The Board Game – Sinister Shadow Expansion
description:
Sinister Shadows, the thrilling expansion for The Last Spell, introduces 16 unique monster miniatures, adding exciting variability to encounters. Face slimy Hoppers, deadly Rippers, and more, as you navigate this dark and challenging world!

--description from the publisher

boardgame: The Last Spell: The Board Game – Whispers of War Expansion
description:
This expansion focuses on strategic gameplay by introducing traps, such as damage, stun, and slow traps, offering new ways to deal with spawns and challenging monsters. Player wounds now cause debilitations that impact movement, action points, and more. Choose omens at the start of the game for unique challenges, tailoring the difficulty level to players' preferences.

-description from Gamefound campaign

Timespan is a streamlined, friendly and highly varied civilization game from the designers of Automania and Bad Company.

Combine one of six asymmetric civilizations with one of six unique technology boards, and customize your civilization further by recruiting among 175 historic persons, buildings, technologies and wonders.

Large parts of the game are played simultaneously, allowing you to lead your civilization from the stone age to the space age in about 90 minutes.

There is no warfare: Timespan focuses on the peaceful sides of civilization building, the exploration and on building an economic engine.

>>>

The game is played over 5 rounds. Each round consists of an exploration phase and an action phase.

In the exploration phase players take turns (in order of their positions on the harmony track) to draft cards and discovery tiles from the current age.

Then, players move their camels, possibly visiting the other players' wonders for various effects and rewards.

Then follows the action phase, which is played simultaneously by all players:

Each player has a civilization board with a timeline, where they can play up to 4 cards. As new cards are played, older cards are pushed out of the timeline, and goes into the player's heritage.

Players use their workers to activate cards in their timeline, as well as action boxes on their civilization boards and their own wonders.

All activations provide various rewards such as resources or advancements on the culture track (to gain wonders and new workers), on the science track (to unlock technologies on the player's technology board), or on the ship track, the flight track or even the space track (giving access to various rare luxury resources). Luxuries can be used to upgrade your cards' effects, and when luxuries are moved into your heritage, they continue to provide permanent passive income.

At the end of the second and fourth round, a new age begins, and new cards and discovery tiles are introduced to the players.

—description from the designer

You are a would‑be master thief cat, striving to become the greatest cat burglar by stealing gems from safes or from your fellow thieves.

Each round, a random number of gems will be available in the current safe, and each cat burglar chooses how much they want to steal using a numbered nyanko card. In clockwise order, players reveal how much they plan to steal. Gems are then taken from least to most greedy burglar. But beware: if a player is too greedy and there are not enough gems left when their turn arrives, they will receive useless pebbles instead. If a player reveals the same nyanko number as someone who went before them, they will steal all the gems (and pebbles) that the earlier player acquired that round.

Acquired gems are then hidden away, nyanko cards are returned to hand, and a new round begins. Play continues until all safes have been robbed blind, after which the number of gems and pebbles will determine who may crown themselves Cat Master Thief.

Pips is a 2 to 5 players worker placement game, where your goal is to complete collections by gathering resources. To accomplish this, each turn you deploy a number of dice, totaling a limited number of pips onto the available fields on the table.

But some dice will not come back from the fields right away, only after a field has been completed. To get more dice, and to be able to deploy more pips, you use those same resources to unlock extra dice.

On your turn, you roll all your available dice, and then select any combination of them that adds to your current pip limit or less.
You then deploy those dice onto the fields to gather resources.

After deploying, you check if any of the fields are completed. For completed fields, the player who has the most pips gets the card, worth points, plus one resource of the type shown at the back.

All the dice that are on field cards that are not completed remain on the table, unavailable to the players.

At any point during your turn, you may complete a collection of those displayed, using resources.

Also at any point, you may unlock new dice with those same resources. This increases your limit of pips for your future turns, as well as additional points.

The winner is the player with the most points from cards, from dice collected and from leftover resources.

-description from designer

NEUTRONIUM EXPANSION is a hexagonal 4X strategy board game where players control Heroes of four ancient alien races, exploring the galaxy, gathering resources, building       
  empires, and fighting for survival across infinite parallel universes.                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  ◆ THE CORE EXPERIENCE ◆                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  You awaken in an unfamiliar universe with no memories. Somewhere among hexagonal sectors hides Alpha Core — an ancient station of unknown origin. Find it. Enrich your         
  Neutronium. Build your empire. But beware: when three Paradox X artifacts are discovered, the universe collapses... and you wake up in another cycle.                          
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  The twist? Your Neutronium carries over between sessions. Your knowledge grows. Each universe unlocks new mechanics — from simple exploration to full-scale warfare and        
  diplomatic conquest.                                                                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  ◆ 13 PARALLEL UNIVERSES ◆                                                                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  "Recovered Memories" — a unique roguelike/legacy progression system:                                                                                                           
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  • Universe 1-5: Learn the basics — movement, exploration, artifacts, looting, building                                                                                         
  • Universe 6-9: Expanded map, trading, colonies, combat, diplomacy                                                                                                             
  • Universe 10-12: Stations, sector bonuses, the path to victory                                                                                                                
  • Universe 13: All 47 mechanics active. Build the Mega-Structure. Break the cycle forever.                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  No rulebook needed for your first game. Just roll, move, explore. Each session awakens more of your Hero's forgotten knowledge.                                                
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  ◆ FOUR ANCIENT RACES ◆                                                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  Build on territories marked with their colors to unlock ancestral powers:                                                                                                      
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  • TERANO (Pink) — Diplomats. Capture territories through patience, not war.                                                                                                    
  • MI-TO (Blue) — Warriors. +1 combat strength in every battle.                                                                                                                 
  • IIT (Orange) — Energy masters. Build Nuclear Ports without artifact cost.                                                                                                    
  • ASTERS (Green) — Architects of reality. Construct Advanced Stations.                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  ◆ DUAL ECONOMY ◆                                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  Neutronium (Nn) exists in two forms:                                                                                                                                           
  • Enriched — ready to spend on construction, upgrades, and tribute                                                                                                             
  • Unenriched — worthless until processed at Alpha Core                                                                                                                         
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  Control Alpha Core. Control the galaxy.                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  ◆ KEY FEATURES ◆                                                                                                                                                               
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  ✦ Modular hex board — every game layout is unique                                                                                                                              
  ✦ Meta-progression — Neutronium persists between sessions                                                                                                                      
  ✦ 18 hex tiles with 54 segments (territories, artifacts, radioactive deposits)                                                                                                 
  ✦ 39 artifact cards + 3 Paradox X triggers                                                                                                                                     
  ✦ 4 asymmetric races with unlockable abilities                                                                                                                                 
  ✦ Multiple victory paths: economy, military, diplomacy                                                                                                                         
  ✦ Plays 2-6 players in 30-90 minutes                                                                                                                                           
  ✦ Scales from age 7+ (simple universes) to experienced gamers (full rules)                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  ◆ 25 YEARS IN THE MAKING ◆                                                                                                                                                     
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  Started in 1998 as a hand-drawn Monopoly variant in Kazakhstan. Evolved through the 2016 hexagonal revolution. Refined by 100+ playtesters worldwide.                                                                                                                                                        
                                                                                                                                                                                 
  The Cycle is eternal — as long as you want it to be.

Enchanted Ivy: A Grand Birthday Expansion is an extra scenario featuring three new guests, all vying for their favorite activity at Ivy's special birthday party.

Contents:

     3 Guest Cards
     3 Guest Tokens
     1 Unlockable Secret Door
     1 Bonus Scenario


—description from the publisher

In Drag Slay, the backstage rush is real. You need to be fast if you want to be the first to step onto the stage at AURA, the city’s newest venue — promising to be the most iconic of them all.

Drag Slay is a real-time game where all players act simultaneously. At any moment, a player may return one Item Card from their display to any central pile and immediately take one Item Card from a different pile. Players race to collect the three specific items required to complete one of the available Drag cards.

When a player completes a Drag, they shout “SLAY!”, reveal and flip the Drag card, and all players flip their Item Cards to the opposite side before play continues.

The game ends immediately when a player completes five Drags from different families or three Drags from the same family. That player wins the game.

It’s Halloween! You and your friends, dressed in your coolest costumes, are out trick-or-treating. Grab lots of candy and magic spells - but don’t get too greedy! Too much candy will make you sick, unless you have the right magic spell... Will you grab the tastiest treats, or will your friends snatch them from under your witchy nose?

The game is played over three rounds. Each round has two phases:
the Grab Phase and the Puzzle Phase.

Grab Phase
Starting with the first player and continuing clockwise, players take turns playing one candy or spell card from their hand into the central Candy Jar. Candy cards show 1 to 4 pieces of candy and come in five different candy types.

At any moment, when a player likes what’s in the Candy Jar, they may slap their hand on it and grab the entire pile, placing all the cards into their personal Candy Jar.
The fastest grabber wins the pile, so do you wait for one more delicious card, or snatch the pile before a friend beats you to it? 
Across the entire game (that is all three rounds combined)) each player may grab a maximum of 7 times.
After a set number of grabs, depending on the player count, the Grab Phase ends and the Puzzle Phase begins.

Puzzle Phase
Now it’s time to score points using the candies and spells in your personal Candy Jar.
Candy is great, but don’t grab too much! Each candy is worth 1 point, but if you collect more than 5 candies of a type, that type scores 0 points. Grab exactly 5, and you earn 3 bonus points.

Luckily, the spell cards you’ve collected let you magically manipulate your candy, so puzzle out the best combinations to maximise your points!

After three rounds, the greediest grabber with the most points wins the game... and becomes the Halloween candy champion!

You are a well renowned rollercoaster engineer faced with new challenges every day. Clients want the biggest, the fastest and the most excitement inducing rollercoasters to be the centerpiece of their theme parks. Of course you have plenty of wickedly thrilling ideas up your sleeve but so do your fellow creative engineers. Which team of engineers can work together to put the best rides together? Ensuring not only happy clients but even happier guests? And most of all, who proves to be the most imaginative engineer, the race is on.. ready, set, launch!

In Coaster Clash you and your fellow engineers work together in 6 quick rounds to conjure up the best rollercoasters the world has yet to ever ride!
The engineers are divided up into duo's, each turn the turn-order alternates. Making communication a tricky endeavour. Trust your best judgement.. Is it wise to stick to your clients wishes or is the customer not always right?

Each turn you're dealt 3 element cards, you choose one card for each coaster set and discard the third. You have your eye on the prize and can already hear the screams of excitement as your creation takes up shape in your mind. If only your fellow engineer doesn't mess up and sabotages your perfectly curated design.
Not knowing what your neighbor is conjuring up makes this a challenge in every regard. And they might not be working out an idea for the same coaster at all.
As the engineer to your left gets to place his element first your heartbeat quickens.. You breathe a sigh of relief as the element he placed down now makes the one you had in mind reach perfect speed! Maybe they do know what they are doing.. Either way happy guests means you've looped your way closer to victory. And next turn.. you luckily get to go first.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Nemi – a magical little village where strawberries grow wild! Immerse yourself in its atmosphere as you pick ingredients to bake sweet treats, or decorate the town with colourful garlands.
Open draft, blind auction, beautiful illustrations and quick-to-learn rules are the ingredients of choice to bring the taste of Italian summer straight to your table with this deliciously entertaining Family-plus game!

Over the course of 4 rounds, draft fruit crates and supplier cards and combine them to cook mouthwatering desserts. Supplier cards feature ingredients, objectives, and more - but they can also be used to decorate the city, so you must manage how you want to use them. Only the best cooker/decorator will emerge victorious!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to The Worst Party! The goal is to have the least amount of fun, or “Fun points”, at a fictional party.

Over the course of 12 rounds, which represents the hour of the party, players will play combos of cards, gain good and bad memories, manage their alcohol level, and use their asymmetrical abilities, all in the name of having the Worst Party Ever!

—description from the designer

A chaotic sound-making party game for 3–10 players.

Beat Box Party is a 5-round performance and survival game where players create ridiculous sounds, follow modifiers, try not to laugh, and attempt to stay in sync as chaos erupts.

Players earn Beat Box cards by completing rounds and the goal is to become the Beat Box Master.

Stay focused, don’t laugh, and keep the beat going.

How the game plays:

In Beat Box Party, players create a group beat together using silly sound prompts, performed one player at a time. Each round, players secretly draw a Beat Box card and add their sound to the beat when it's their turn, building a continuous beat.

As the game progresses, Round Modifier cards introduce unexpected rules that change how sounds must be performed, forcing players to adapt on the fly while staying in sync. Hesitate, crack up or laugh and you're out for the round.

The round ends when the timer runs out or there are only 2 or fewer players left in the round. Those players keep their Beat Box cards as a point and the next round begins.

After 5 rounds, the player with the most Beat Box cards are the winner and dubbed the Beat Box Master.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel Zombies: Heroes' Resistance – Mission MH1: The Captain Hunt
description: Living or dead, Captain America is indeed a formidable opponent. Not only did he somehow find a Vibranium source to strengthen his shield, but he also managed to capture two of our team members. We need to rescue our friends before they become Cap’s main course. Only then, with our full team assembled, can we hope to break through Captain America’s defenses. Assemble! - from publisher

boardgame: Marvel Zombies: X-Men Resistance – Where Is The Professor?
description: The zombies really did a number on us. Professor X is nowhere to be found and they’ve captured Magneto and Mystique. The plan is simple: We rescue them, Mystique shapeshifts into Professor X to get past Cerebro's security, and we use it to track him down.

boardgame: Night of the Living Dead: A Zombicide Game – D1: Another Take
description:
Plot twist: What if the heroes immediately chose to look past their differences and fight together? Ghouls are everywhere, we must find some shelter! Look out! There are some right over... Oh! They’re not ghouls! Yes! We are not alone! These people out there seem, well, normal! Hey! Hey, folks! Don’t be afraid, we’re normal too. Let’s take shelter in that house. Come on! These madmen won’t catch us. Stronger, together, we will make our stand! We need some weapons first. Might be some in the barn, right here. My name is Ben. And you?

Easy difficulty mission.

boardgame: Night of the Living Dead: A Zombicide Game – D2: Popcorn at Dawn
description:
Silent Ghouls have been lurking and hiding in the cornfields, preventing us from escaping. We have to get out and to safety, but luckily Tom has a pop-pop-poppingly good idea to end the night!.

Medium difficulty mission.

boardgame: Night of the Living Dead: A Zombicide Game – D3: The Old farm
description:
n this "what if?" scenario, we found a farm where we can take shelter before continuing our journey. Let’s hope the old neighbors are not still around...

Hard difficulty mission.

boardgame: Night of the Living Dead: A Zombicide Game – D4: The Cellar is the Safest Place
description:
Plot Twist: The Ghouls have surrounded the farmhouse and are getting closer. The locks we’ve put on the doors and windows will not be enough to stop them..

Very Hard mission

A Solitaire Game of U.S. Marine Corsair Pilots in the Solomons, 1943–44

Black Sheep Squadron puts you in command of VMF-214, Pappy Boyington’s legendary U.S. Marine Corsair unit, during the fierce air battles over the Solomon Islands. As the squadron’s commanding officer, you’ll plan missions, manage pilots and aircraft, and lead daily sorties against Japanese forces from Vella Lavella to Rabaul.

The game combines strategic squadron management with abstract air combat on a Battle Board that resolves dogfights without complex maneuver plotting. Each six-week campaign represents one historical combat tour, complete with fatigue, pilot status, and maintenance pressures that mirror the brutal pace of the Pacific air war.

You’ll assign pilots, brief missions, launch flights, and face encounters with Japanese Zeros. Between missions, you will handle pilot wounds and fatigue as well as aircraft repairs. Pilots that are shot down could be rescued by PBYs or submarines, or they could be lost forever.

Designed for solitaire play, Black Sheep Squadron features a robust Japanese AI system that controls enemy aircraft interceptions and combat on the Battle Board. The result is an immersive narrative experience that captures the grit, courage, and camaraderie of Marine aviators flying from dusty island strips into the heart of enemy skies.

—description from the designer

Each turn, roll 3 dice, compare Triplet > Chainet > Simplette, then, from highest to lowest, each player draws 1 card from the river.

At the end of the turn, sell up to 2 sets of 3 cards: for a Trio, keep 1 card in your score; for a Straight, keep the middle card. Your points = the value of the card kept. 
The other two are discarded. A card can only be used once per turn.

Goal: earns 15 points to win the game. 
In case of a tie, the tiebreaker is the number of cards in your score.

To those unmentionable dark places in our brain we’d rather not visit. Don’t worry, though. You won’t be going there alone. The more people who go there, the less scary it becomes. So, prepare to be a little uncomfortable, a lot open-minded, have a few laughs, and above all else be real AF.

The hilarious party game where you find out everyone is really a scared, lonely, on-edge, chemical soup of emotions pretending to be okay, just like you!

HOW TO GO THERE:
1. Draw a Thought Prompt
The game starts with a thought prompt like…“The part of my body I avoid is…”
2. The Responses
Here's where the honesty comes in. Everyone writes their responses to the prompt truthfully.
3. The Reading
People then have to guess which messed up thought was yours!
4. You're There
Real-talk, hilarity and unexpected group therapy ensues.

Crash Cribbage Quest is a single player card game where you try to survive your trip around the Crash Cribbage Game board. Players will need a Crash Cribbage board and Speedrobo Games playing card deck, sold separately.

Survive challenging threats, unlock powerful boosts, and upgrade your deck!

This game is based on the classic cribbage rules and allows players to enjoy the classic card game solo.

A crack has appeared in the space time continuum.  Is it a portal into an exciting new universe or a path to destruction?  Fly your pyramid shaped starships through the rift to find out but watch out for other ships and the impending implosion of the rift itself!

The Rift (A Loonacy Pyramid Game) uses Pyramid Arcade Loonacy (or any other Loonacy game) and 2 trios per player of Icehouse Loonacy pyramids. Match Loonacy cards and launch your ships through the rift.  Move your ships to avoid or attack other ships.  Move your ships the furthest away from the rift to gain the most points when the rift implodes.

Eqular: Allegiance is a strategy board game for 2-6 players centered on worker placement and colony development in a competitive, faction-driven science fiction setting.

Set during a conflict between two major galactic powers, the United Galactic Alliance and the Directorate, players represent one of these factions or a powerful neutral faction seeking to establish dominance on a newly colonized planet in neutral territory.

The game is played over a series of rounds and phases. During delegate phases, players assign workers to shared action spaces, with multiple players able to occupy the same location and divide the resulting benefits. Colony management phases then allow players to produce resources, support population growth, and expand their colonies on a 16×16 grid-based board.

The game ends after the 16th round or when a player reaches the maximum population limit. Players earn Victory Points by constructing specific buildings, acquiring Neurostone, achieving milestone-based Titles, and collecting Victory Point chits through various in-game effects.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: EXIT: Das Spiel – Der perfekte Einbruch (Jubiläums-Edition)
description:
Einbruch ins Casino: Gelingt es, die gut verschlossene Panzertür zum Tresor zu knacken?

Limitierte Jubiläumsausgabe für Fans: Metalldose mit exklusiven Fan-Extras im EXIT Look & cleverem Bonusrätsel.

1–4 Spielerinnen und Spieler lösen als Team clevere Rätsel mit Teamgeist, Kreativität und Kombinationsgeschick.

_____________________________________________________________

Casino Break-In: Can you crack the locked armored door to the vault?

Limited Anniversary Edition for Fans: Metal tin with exclusive fan extras in the EXIT style and a clever bonus puzzle.

1–4 players work together as a team to solve clever puzzles using teamwork, creativity, and problem-solving skills.

Content:

90 cards
    1 decoding disc
    2 leaflets
    1 note
    1 Poster
    21 strange parts
    Scissors
    Ballpoint pen
    Pencil
    Eraser
    Ruler
    1 Instructions

Colors collide and numbers add up in this fast, clever card race!
Match the face-up card by color, number, or by mixing colors and adding values. On your turn, play either 1 or 2 cards from your hand to match the card on the face up discard pile.
Chain together smart plays, surprise your opponents, and empty your hand first to claim victory!

Played and enjoyed by gamers ages 5 to 87. :)

Step into Petal, a charmingly competitive card game where you and another florist are rivals racing to open the most charming Flower Shop in town. Each round, you’re building a shared “window display” of blooms-trying to make the prettiest patterns for your customers while quietly setting up your own scoring plan.

Over seven turns per player, you’ll draft two Flower Cards from four stacks. You’ll place one card into a shared 4×4 grid, always adjacent to an existing card, and you may rotate it to fit your plans. The other card becomes a personal multiplier in front of you, boosting how many points you’ll score for certain flower icons when the grid is complete. If you’re falling behind, Apron abilities give you clever ways to manipulate the display—rotating, moving, or swapping cards to create (or disrupt) scoring opportunities.

When the 4×4 grid is finished, you score in two ways: completed flowers in the grid multiplied by your matching multiplier icons, plus points for your private objective card based on the final display. The player with the most points wins the round (ties break by who has the most multipliers of a single icon; if still tied, both players take the round).

To win the game, you need to prove you’re the best florist over multiple rounds: the first to win two rounds and place two Bouquets in the Flower Shop claims the victory!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Dead Keep: K1 – Scattered Souls
description:
After delving deep into the ruins, we unearthed treasures beyond anything we’d dared imagine. Just as we shoved the treasure into our packs, a low rumble shook the ground, turning triumph into terror. The floor buckled and crumbled beneath us. We ran, hearts thumping in our chests, so desperate to escape that it was all we could think of. When the tremors finally subsided, a grim truth set in: we’d been separated, scattered into the shadows, each of us alone. Now, lost in this cursed place, we face every adventurer’s worst fear. Can we find each other and defeat whatever malevolent force is stirring below - or will we all perish in The Dead Keep?

Material needed: The Dead Keep
Tiles needed: 2R, 4R, 5R, 6R, 8R, & 9R
Boss: Wyvern

Objectives
• Last mile. Accomplish the Objectives in the following order:
- Take all Objectives.
- Eliminate the Wyvern Boss. The Quest succeeds as soon as the Boss is eliminated.

Fishmongers Creek is a game where players are anglers who’ve discovered a pristine creek that belongs to a wild fishmonger. As you catch fish he’ll reward you with gold and even lend you his prized fishing equipment to help you land even bigger catches.

Competitive, medium-weight, card-driven.

Use the gold you earn to move along the boardwalk, and after three rounds of fishing, the angler who has traveled the furthest is crowned the winner.

—description from the publisher

A murder was committed at the home of renowned lawyer Pavel Dohnal. With the police baffled and answers scarce, the case file has ended up on your desk.

Inside, you will find interrogations, photographs, reports, and other materials designed to feel like a real investigation. Your task is to go through all the documents, connect the evidence, and find out who the killer is.

In Raindrops, players observe drops sliding down a window and identify their patterns, as their mind drifts to forgotten memories to be reconstructed.
Raindrops is a tile-collection and pattern-recognition game for 2–4 players. On their turn, players select a continuous downward path of matching symbols on a shared window board, starting from the top row and moving to adjacent spaces below or diagonally. All tiles in the path are collected, the remaining tiles slide downward, and the board is refilled from a bag.
Collected tiles are used to complete personal Memory cards, which require specific symbol patterns. Use bonus tile to help complete your Memory cards, while reaching the bottom row can award hidden-value Thunder tokens. Completing Memory cards scores points and may allow players to claim Collect set of the same memory to score bonus Cloud tokens!
The game ends when a player completes ten Memory cards or the bag is emptied for the second time. Final scoring includes completed cards, tokens, bonuses, and partially completed patterns.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Double Feature: Harsh Shadows / Welcome to Reckoning
description:
This is a Solo Game of the Month edition featuring both Harsh Shadows and Welcome to Reckoning that were first published by Wonderspell.

Harsh Shadows is a solitaire game of espionage. To set up, place nine location cards in a 3x3 grid, then place discovery cards under each location. Place three file cards near the locations, then place case cards under each file. Place decks for arrow cards and additional discovery cards nearby, along with two agent tool cards. The spy and agent cards start in different locations.

Welcome to Reckoning is a solo, thematic hand management and survival game game set in the weird west where you are trying to survive the onslaught of spectral riders, tentacles from the deep, and harsh weather until the wagon arrives to rescue you. Players move through the deck of event cards while trying to get the warehouse and keep it from being overrun. If the warehouse is overrun or you can't put together a set of matching cards, you won't survive.

Fröccs: Sportfröccs is an add-on to the base game Fröccs and requires that game to play. It introduces new character cards, player boards, and additional “pub goals” that add variation and strategic options to the core game. Players use these new elements alongside the original game’s negotiation and scoring systems to mix and sell beverages within the theme of a lively tavern competition. The expansion adds extra opportunities for scoring through unique character abilities and additional objectives that affect timing and resource management.

This expansion is compatible with the Kisfröccs expansion and is intended for players familiar with the base game’s ruleset. It does not function as a standalone product.

boardgame: Fairy Tale Inn: Evil Queen Alternate Card
description:
Different ability for the Evil Queen:

When Played: + Coin

In your opponent’s next turn, they must pick the token at the bottom of the Guest List.

Note: The ability of the Evil Queen card found in the base game has an entirely different ability.

As the frosty days grow shorter, your cozy café becomes the ultimate refuge for customers seeking comfort from the cold. In Coffee Rush: Winter, the high-stakes barista business returns with a seasonal chill, challenging players to balance the frantic pace of peak hours with the delicate art of crafting winter specialties. Fulfilling orders earns you precious "like" cards and points, but fall behind, and those disgruntled customers will leave "dislikes" that count against your final score. Whether the game ends because the order deck is depleted or a barista accumulates five penalties, only the player who manages the winter rush with the most efficiency will be crowned the top barista.

While fans of the original Coffee Rush will recognize the core "Overcooked-style" tension, this winter edition introduces several key differences:
Modular Board Chaos: Unlike the fixed layout of the original, the ingredient tiles are now modular. You can set up with "Main board chaos!" or "Windy day" variants, randomly placing ingredients to ensure every game requires a new movement strategy.
Seasonal Drinks: The menu has expanded to include winter-specific items.
Component Changes: The game features thematic updates to its components. Fruit (depicted as orange slices) is newly added to the ingredients, and the Ice tokens are now shaped like Snowmen to fit the winter theme.
New Upgrades: With the newly introduced upgrade tiles, you can now choose four out of six available upgrade tiles for each game.
Scoring: Scoring has been revised so that points are now awarded as +2 for each "like" card and +5 for each activated upgrade, with a -1 penalty for each "dislike".

—description from the publisher

In Koffer, Katze & Sombrero, you're picking up luggage at the airport, and to make things easy for yourself, you want to pick up everything in the proper order.

The deck contains 40 cards numbered 1-10 in four colors, and to set up, you shuffle the deck, then lay seven cards in a row with one end being designated the front. Each player takes seven chips.

On a turn, you will take one card from the row, with the first card being free, the second card requiring you to place a chip on the first card, the third card requiring you to place chips on the first two cards, and so on.

When you take a card, you start a pile in that color or add it to an existing pile — but if the number on this card is lower than the card it's covering, you must turn the new card face down. In the future, you can place any value face up on a face-down card.

When you can't fill the row to seven cards at the end of your turn, complete the round so that everyone has the same number of turns, then tally your score, with face-up cards being worth as many points as their number, face-down cards worth -1 point, and chips worth 1 point.

To add more variety, you can draw three of the fifteen special cards at random. Each card has either an end-of-game scoring effect or a condition that affects play during the game.

Promo for Mesos available through the Cranio Expansion Pack crowdfunding project.

Contains 10 Cave tiles.

Setup rules: shuffle the Cave tiles, create a face-up stack and place it next to the Turn Order tile.

Play rules: The first player who returns their Totem to the Turn Order tile takes the first available Cave tile, INSTEAD of the bonus indicated by the Turn Order tile. You can get the Cave tile bonus any time; if the tile shows Food in the upper section (orange background), you must pay that Food to get the bonus below. Discard the tile after it’s resolved. You can’t keep more than one Cave tile at a time. So, if you take a second tile you must immediately resolve one.

In Gretchen’s Garden, players compete in selecting the most precious succulents available for their very own collection! You don’t even need a green thumb to win this easy to grasp tableau builder.

Over the course of 12 rounds, players try their very best, to optimize their collection by adding extraordinary pots and rare specimen. This might request snatching the odd leave from your opponents plant. The player with the trendiest collection (and most points) wins!

—description from the publisher

A snappy and sweet push-your-luck card game about avoiding bad apples, collecting furry fruit critters ...and selling them for maximum profit!

Each turn the market will change with new cards displaying the demands for the turn. The players will then decide to draw cards or stop and sell according to the demands. The deck do not only contain charming furruits however, there are quite a lot of bad apples in there, as well as effects that will change the market and your furruits...

The most successful furruit saleman after 4 rounds wins the game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: British – Centurion Mk. I
description:
The mobile British Centurion Mk. 1 is the perfect upgrade platform, with upgrades to both the gun and turret increasing armour and firepower. Upgrade to the OQF 17-Pdr AT Gun Mk. VII for a Firepower boost, or further update to the Mk. 3 standard with the Centurion Mk. III turret and OQF 20-Pdr Gun Type A Barrel for more armour and hitting power.

—description from the publisher

In this cooperative deck builder, you’ll take on the role of one of the Small-Time Heroes and work together to defend the town. With each battle, you’ll build up your deck of cards, choosing from a variety of cards that represent your hero’s unique abilities. As your power builds, you will defeat waves of minions and end with a final boss battle. Defeat the boss before they defeat you!

In Spirit & Spells, you'll find two new heroes: the Cleric and Wizard. And four new bosses: the Puppeteer, the Slime Lord, the Nothing, and the Prince of Darkness.

This is a standalone expansion that has everything you need to play the game or you can combine it with Small-Time Heroes: Sword and Shadow.

—description from the publisher

World War Zoo is an upcoming miniature wargame of anthropomorphic animals battling for dominance in a Modern Age of mechanized warfare.

From Jackrabbit GIs to Tiger-commanded tanks, submachine gun toting bears and Mortar Teams of Mice… each faction consists of wide array of creatures great and small, comprising a range of unique weapons and abilities which encompass both military hardware and innate animal traits.

Two players each build an Army from their desired Faction using the appropriate Unit Cards and corresponding Models. Following this, they reveal shared Missions from a Mission deck, deploy their Armies on the Battlefield, and compete to accomplish those Missions within the round-limit.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Ratounet, Big Panda, Foxy, and Nan-Nan are behind bars. Together with his deputies, Inspector Cat leads the investigation to unmask the culprit. He draws a piece of evidence and a question: Can it break? Is it important to have? Does it make you happy?
The players secretly answer yes or no. At the end of the investigation, they count their points together.

boardgame: The Lord of the Rings: Circle of Conflict
description:
It is the Third Age of Middle-earth, and a great war between the many nations is unfolding. In The Lord of the Rings: Circle of Conflict, you simultaneously command both The Shadow and The Free Peoples armies; your Free Peoples will fight the Shadow of the opponent to your left, and your Shadow will fight the Free Peoples of the opponent to your right.

As the dice are rolled, you will decide how to apply the results. Will you recruit iconic characters for The Free Peoples or perhaps muster the troops of Mordor to fight for The Shadow in one of two pivotal battles? Will you hunt your opponent’s Fellowship with your Nazgûl or secretly move your Fellowship to evade Sauron’s watchful Eye?

To win as The Free Peoples, get The Fellowship to reach Mount Doom or defeat The Shadow in battle. As The Shadow, you must fully corrupt The Fellowship or defeat them in battle.

When all the victory points have been tallied, the player with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
Encourage active play with this family game that’s a cute and silly twist on the classic game of “War”.

Both teams flip over a card and whoever has the higher one gets to keep both. If there’s a tie, you’ll both have to roll, throw, or dodge the soft, adorable Roly Polies to get those cards. Whoever has the most cards at the end of the game wins. It’s part carnival game, part kids card game, all screen free fun that even grown-ups will enjoy.

It encourages active play, problem-solving, counting skills, and family bonding–making it a great birthday gift, classroom game, or instant energy boost for game night.

boardgame: Die in the Dungeon: The Board Game
description:
Die in the Dungeon: The Board Game is a cooperative, dice-building dungeon crawler. On a world where advanced animal civilizations once thrived, a forbidden mineral reshaped the planet and gave rise to heroes who wield magical dice to explore dungeons, uncover relics, and confront epic threats.

Each player takes on a hero with unique abilities, a starting set of dice, and a combat board. On their turn, players draw dice from their bag and place them on the battle space, where positioning and timing create a puzzle with many outcomes. Some dice attack, some block, some boost others, some provide energy, some heal, and many have special properties. Dice can be upgraded and combined in countless ways, offering endless strategic possibilities.

Players explore ever-changing dungeon areas, collect new dice, potions, and relics, and prepare to face challenging bosses. With Area Mode for structured adventures and Endless Mode for continuing roguelike challenges, teamwork, strategic placement, and mastering dice synergies are key to surviving the dungeon and claiming victory.

—description from the designer

Crima din Orfelinatul Stavrache is not just a game, it is a complete investigation, built like a century-old criminal case file.

You will receive sealed envelopes, aged documents, mysterious evidence, coded letters, and hard-to-decipher symbols. Every detail is handcrafted to recreate the tense atmosphere of Bucharest in 1917.

Discover a story with deep roots, where reality intertwines with fiction, and only the most observant investigators can uncover the truth.

—description from the publisher

In No Aperto, you take on the role of a ruler crushed by impossible choices: invest in wealth, accelerate progress, or keep your army standing.
The problem is simple—and relentless: resources are never enough.

Every soldier reassigned to improvements is one less spear defending the realm. Every coin spent on mercenaries is progress lost in infrastructure. While you secretly program your orders, your rivals watch closely, waiting for the slightest mistake.

What awaits you:

Crisis Management
With only 17 troops, you must sustain an entire empire. Every decision carries weight.

Secret Programming
Choose 3 plans per round and hope your read of the game is correct. A single miscalculation is paid for immediately in Victory Points.

Deterministic Conflict
No dice. No luck. Victory belongs to those who planned better and knew how to use the market’s available items at the exact right moment.

Decisive Upgrades
Develop your kingdom—but accept the cost: your best soldiers will be permanently committed to maintaining your achievements.

In No Aperto, the blanket is always too short, time is scarce, and every choice closes a door forever.
Will you preserve your crown—or watch your empire crumble for lack of planning?

—description from the designer

Em No Aperto, você assume o papel de um soberano pressionado por escolhas impossíveis: investir em riqueza, acelerar o progresso ou manter o exército de pé. 
O problema é simples - e implacável: os recursos nunca bastam.

Cada soldado deslocado para fazer as melhorias, é uma lança a menos defendendo o reino. Cada moeda gasta com mercenários é um avanço que deixa de existir na infraestrutura. Enquanto você programa suas ordens em segredo, seus rivais observam, atentos, esperando o menor erro.

O que espera por você:
Gestão de Crise
Com apenas 17 tropas, você precisa sustentar um império inteiro. Cada decisão pesa.

Programação Secreta
Escolha 3 planos por rodada e torça para que sua leitura do jogo esteja correta. Um erro de cálculo cobra seu preço imediatamente em Pontos de Vitória.

Conflito Determinístico
Sem dados. Sem sorte. Aqui, vence quem planejou melhor e soube usar, no momento exato, os itens disponíveis no mercado.

Evoluções Decisivas
Desenvolva seu reino, mas aceite o custo: seus melhores soldados ficarão permanentemente comprometidos para manter suas conquistas.

Em No Aperto, o cobertor é curto, o tempo é escasso e cada escolha fecha uma porta para sempre.
Você conseguirá preservar sua coroa - ou verá seu império ruir por falta de planejamento?

—description from the designer (Portuguese)

Flippin' Pancakes kids game is flippin' fun as players race to match the top card in their stack to the pancake showing in the pan. The first player to match gets to discard, so players must be quick! Three levels of play let the game grow with players' ability. Great gift for game lovers 3 years old and up. Colors and decorations may vary.

Step into the role of a determined police commissioner or a mysterious arsonist in this tense board game inspired by a novel by Wojciech Chmielarz. Follow the evolving situation on the board, take notes, and make sharp strategic decisions. Choose the best action card from two available options, use arson and police tokens, anticipate your opponent’s moves, and try to outsmart them to win this asymmetric duel.

boardgame: Personalogy Game Party and Date Night
description:
Personalogy Party and Date Night are Conversation Starters with a twist. A social lubricant.

The decks have 200 entertaining and thought-provoking question cards to spark fun stories, surprises, and hilarious truths. Each question and the multiple-choice answers are designed to get people connecting, laughing, and having fun! Effortlessly easy to play - read the question out loud, predict your opponent's answer and why and then see where the answers take you. You can play with or without points. For 2 -10 players, +16. Use it at your next get-together, dinner party or girls' night as an icebreaker.

The small box is designed to travel - put in a backpack, large purse or glovebox. Personalogy goes wherever life takes you — from a friend’s house to happy hour or road-trip. It transforms any gathering into fun, unforgettable and LOL moments where everybody feels included.

—description from the designer

Duck, Duck, Llama! is an award-winning family-friendly game about counting ducks, causing distractions, and trying not to be found out as a llama in disguise.

Are you a sleek Ducktective or a Llama in disguise?

Duck, Duck, Llama! is a bit like a game of "hot potato" with a mathematical twist. Players take turns playing cards to manipulate The Count—the total number of ducks in play. The tension rises with the number; your goal is to force the Count to 10 or above just in time for your opponent's turn.

How to Play:


     Play a card to Increase or Decrease the Count, then draw a replacement.



     If you start your turn and the Count is ten or above, you are Accused!



     To survive, you must play a card that drops the count below 10 or causes a Distraction.



     If you can't escape, you are revealed as a Llama! This ends a round of Duck, Duck, Llama!


In later rounds:

Llamas can win their Ducktective status back by successfully accusing others.

After three rounds, only the remaining Ducktectives are crowned winners.
