Key Features

     Three Distinct Hulls:
         The Wraith (Interceptor): A glass cannon with high evasion and superior hand filtering.
         The Vanguard (Fighter): A balanced workhorse that corrects pilot error and excels with guided ordinance.
         The Titan (Gunship): A flying fortress. Slow and easy to hit, but capable of regenerating shields and dealing massive damage with Heavy Weapons.
    
     Modular Loadout System: Your deck is your ship. Before the match, equip 5 Modules—from Pulse Lasers and Torpedo Pods to ECM Arrays and Tracker Units. Each module adds specific cards to your deck, allowing you to build for pure aggression, electronic warfare, or tanking.
     The "Combat Stress" Mechanic: Every pilot gets tired. Every reactor glitches. Your deck includes mandatory Combat Stress cards—unplayable dead draws that simulate the chaos of battle. You must manage your hand effectively to work around them.
     Tactical "Active Field" Gameplay: Combat isn't just instant damage. Play Active Field cards like Target Lock or Ambush to set up ongoing buffs and debuffs that pressure your opponent on their turn.


What's Inside?
This download includes print-optimized files designed for standard home printing (Landscape Layout):

     Core Rulebook: 4-page streamlined ruleset covering Setup, Deck Building, Combat Math, and Keywords.
     Ship Cards: Profile cards for the Wraith, Vanguard, and Titan.
     Module Cards: 18 distinct Weapon and System mini-cards to track your loadout.
     Game Deck: A complete suite of Action cards (Attack, Maneuver, Support, Special) to print and build your custom decks.


—description from the publisher

Talking bugs! is both a gamification tool to learn to identify the macroinvertebrates of the river, as a card game.

The goal of the game is to discard all macroinvertebrate cards before the other players. In each round, players can modify the biological quality of the river, its speed or typology in order to place their macroinvertebrates.

—description from the designer

Bestioles que parlen! és tant una eina de gamificació per aprendre a identificar els macroinvertebrats del riu, com un joc de cartes.

L'objectiu del joc és descartar-se de totes les cartes de macroinvertebrat abans que els altres jugadors. A cada ronda, els jugadors poden modificar la qualitat biològica del riu, la seva velocitat o tipologia per tal de col·locar els seus macroinvertebrats.

—description from the designer (Catalan)

Dice Football Replay is designed as a fast-playing game system allowing players to recreate an entire football season in a matter of hours. The game is primarily meant for solo players though it can be played by two players. Each individual football game is played out in a simplified, abstract format, sacrificing detail in exchange for speed and ease of play. Teams are rated for their relative strengths and weaknesses in terms of rushing and passing offense as well as rushing and passing defense, based on their actual statistical performance for the given season being replicated. Teams are also rated for their extra point and field goal kicking, as well as special teams and defensive scoring, again based on actual statistics for those teams. Using the game sequence of play below, a player should be able to complete a single game in as little as five minutes. This will make playing out complete seasons a realistic goal.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Middle Earth Strategy Battle Game Journal: The Treachery of Gollum
description:
Gollum has promised to guide Frodo and Sam through the treacherous routes into Mordor to destroy the One Ring. But he still covets his “Precious” and will do anything to get his hands on it again. Leading the Hobbits into a series of winding tunnels, he hatches a plan that involves a giant arachnid…

The Treachery of Gollum is an expansion to the Middle-earth Strategy Battle Game that provides a series of linked Narrative Scenarios and engaging hobby content. It allows you to recreate some of the most iconic scenes featuring the creature Gollum and the vile spider, Shelob. Will you guide Frodo on his arduous quest to Mordor, in an attempt to destroy The One Ring and end the tyranny of the Dark Lord once and for all, or side with the conniving Gollum and lure the Ringbearer into a carefully laid trap and claim the Precious for yourself?

This 48-page softcover expansion contains:


     Nine Narrative Scenarios: Recreate some of Gollum and Shelob’s most diabolical and evil moments.



     A Linked Campaign System: Connect each of the Narrative Scenarios so that the result of one battle will have an impact on the next.



     Full Painting Guides: Learn how to paint your Gollum and Shelob miniatures so they look just how they did in The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King.



     Step-by-step Hobby Tutorials: Create the winding tunnels of Shelob’s lair, the sewers beneath the ruined city of Osgiliath, the foetid bogs of the Dead Marshes, and more.


—description from the publisher

Literary Quicktionary is an "all-play" word game in which there are no turns. Quickly think of a literary word or phrase that meets all the conditions on the three face-up cards. The first person to shout out a valid answer earns a card. The first player to earn five cards wins!

—description from the rulebook

The game has three sets of cards: teal, orange, and purple (34 cards each). The teal cards give the broadest category: "associated with horror," "associated with romance novels," etc. The orange cards are about the words: "is exactly seven letters long," "does not contain two vowels next to each other," etc. The purple cards are about letters: "contains the letter B," begins with the letter F," etc.

boardgame: Blitz Tactics: A Modular WWII Wargame
description:
Blitz Tactics is a fast-paced, 28mm miniature wargame designed to strip away the bloat of traditional historical systems without sacrificing tactical depth. Built on the Battle Logic engine, this game focuses on decision-making, alternating activations, and narrative progression rather than endless table-referencing.

Whether you are storming the beaches of the Pacific, holding the line in a ruined Stalingrad factory, or engaging in armored duels across the sands of North Africa, Blitz Tactics puts you in the boots of the commander.

Why Play Blitz Tactics?

Dynamic Activation: Blitz Tactics uses an alternating unit activation system, keeping both players constantly engaged. React to your opponent’s maneuvers in real-time—will you Advance to cover, Assault to pin them down, or risk a desperate Charge?

Campaign System: The included "The World War" expansion turns a single battle into a war story.

     Progression: Your units earn XP, gain veteran abilities (like Hardened or Skirmish), and specialize over time.
     Logistics & Triage: Manage Requisition Points to resupply your force. Roll for Triage after battle—did your squad leader survive, or are they Scarred with a permanent injury?
     Three Theaters: Pre-built campaign tracks for North Africa, the Eastern Front, and the Pacific Theater.


Solo Mode: Fly solo with the "Artificial Enemy" (AE). This isn't just a random die roll; it is a Unified Logic system that dictates enemy behavior based on their role (Infantry, Support, or Vehicle). The enemy will flank, hold objectives, and fall back intelligently.

Digital Tools: Blitz Tactics comes with standalone HTML applications that run in any modern web browser—no installation required.

     The Forge: A fully functional, interactive Army Builder. Create rosters, calculate points automatically, equip special gear (Flamethrowers, Anti-Tank, Radios), and export/print your army list instantly.
     Solo Engine: An interactive digital assistant that handles the AI logic for you during solo play.


What's Inside?

     Core Field Manual: All the rules you need to play, including unit stats, weapon traits, terrain rules, and the 4-action combat system.
     Campaign Supplement: Rules for injuries, income, upgrades, and 4 narrative scenarios (Breakthrough, Supply Raid, Ambush, No Man's Land).
     Solo Rules: The "Unified Doctrine" flowcharts and guide for playing against the game itself.
     Universal Unit Roster: A JSON unit roster ready for immediate play.
     Blitz Forge (App): The interactive roster builder.
     Solo Engine (App): The digital AI companion.


—description from the publisher

The Nine Totems were raised to bear silent witness.
What is built cannot be undone.

Each Totem records a decision once made, a tension once settled.
Around them lie the Marks: choices not yet resolved, things that can still change.

The past is fixed. Finality is chosen.

A brutally elegant game poised between abstract strategy and quiet myth. Every decision leaves a permanent mark. You are not defeated by a single move, but by the moves you made when you could have chosen otherwise.

Nine Totems: The Third Decree is a two-player abstract strategy game of tension, restraint, and irreversible decisions.

Each action reshapes the order, and brings the final decree closer. It rewards players who delay commitment, but punish those who delay decision. Victory comes not from accumulation, but from timing: being the last player able to act, or completing the final Totem.

—description from the designer

This is the ultimate edition of Clinic Deluxe Edition.
It contains 5 giant plastic trays and 5 giant transparent cover trays to fit all Clinic Deluxe Edition components and expansions released from 2019 to 2026.

The Clinic Deluxe Big Box also contains a new big extension: Clinic Deluxe 911th expansion, the only extension that is only one big big module where you will need to build a second building next to your main Clinic!

—description from the publisher

Brändi Via is a fast-paced tactical board game for 2–4 players that blends route building, area control, and clever timing. Players compete to connect destinations across the board using their playing pieces, creating networks while blocking opponents and seizing key routes.

Each turn offers meaningful choices: expand your own connections, disrupt your rivals, or position yourself to score valuable points. The simple rules make the game easy to learn, while the constantly changing board state ensures depth and replayability. Tactical foresight and adaptability are rewarded, as no two games develop the same way.

With its clean design and engaging player interaction, Brändi Via is well suited for families and casual gamers, yet offers enough strategic tension to satisfy experienced players. Games are quick to set up and play, making it an excellent choice for short, competitive sessions with plenty of table talk and rivalry.

Players move around a board with different cities on a world map. All start at a fixed location and everyone tries to reach their target location by answering either area-specific or general knowledge questions correctly. Event cards like skipping a turn are possible as well.

Once the target location is reached, the corresponding player moves to the start location again and draws a new target location card. The goal is to accumulate 50 points, where each question card has a specific value to add on correct answers.

Cup of tea & a scone is a promotional card for the base game Coffee Rush.

This promotional card adds a new drink inspired by a traditional British combination. The promo card is treated as a standard order and follows the normal rules of the game.

Components:

1 card (44×68 mm):

• 1 UK Country Order card (Cup of tea & a scone);

Ingredients = Tea, Water, Steam

• 1 Title/Rules card

During setup, take one order card from the base game at random and return it to the box. Shuffle the Country Promo card with the remaining order cards to form the deck.

Moonstone is a tabletop miniatures skirmish game using card mechanics in battles.

This is an alternative 2 player starter set for Moonstone, contains everything needed to get started with the game.


     8 miniatures (Angerboda, Greymair, Negroli, Serif, Jackalope, Jayda, Hoff & Mr Toodles) with their stat cards
     rules pamphlet
     Moonstone game deck
     Moonstone dice set
     Psychopomp tokens
     Measuring widget
     Energy tokens

One player, the Curator, reads aloud one of the four theme on the Theme card they drew. Then secretly rank the four Art Piece cards randomly drawn from most fitting to least fitting.
The other players must match their choice to get points.
Then each player bets on a player of their choice and guess how many similar choices they both made. If the bet is correct, they get bonus points.

The game ends after everyone has been Curator once, the player(s) with the most points win(s).

A fun and quirky party game about art, where everyone's tastes and sensitivities create an atmosphere full of laughter and heated debates!

A relaxed approach to works of art, where you will have to guess your friends' preferences and bet wisely to win the maximum number of points.

boardgame: Coffee Rush: Country Promo – The Netherlands
description:
During setup, take 1 Order card from the base game at random and return it to the box. Shuffle the Country Promo card into the remaining Order cards to form the deck.

COMPONENTS 2 cards (43x67mm):

1 Netherlands Country Order card (Coffee & Stroopwafel ; Ingredients = Coffee, Water, Steam)

1 Title/Rules card

The board game Мема: Пісьменнікі (Mema: Writers) introduces you to outstanding writers of the 19th-20th centuries and their popular works, immerses you in the world of Belarusian literature, and inspires you to read famous books.

The game offers two rule variants:

“Literary Mema.” A classic version in which players search for matching pairs of cards — a writer and the work they authored.

“Chronology.” A team game where players must arrange cards with writers in chronological order according to their birth dates.

Мема: Пісьменнікі is part of the Mema series, designed to acquaint you with the literary heritage of Belarus.

The board game Мема: Славутасці ("Mema: Landmarks") introduces you to the architectural heritage of Belarus. The cards feature buildings from different eras, architectural styles, and purposes — from churches and cathedrals to railway stations and theaters.

The game offers three rule variants:

Classic. Players search for pairs of cards with identical landmarks.

Infrastructure. This variant adds diversity to the classic rules and makes your copy of the game unique.

Chronology. A team game where players must arrange the buildings in chronological order according to their construction dates.

An important addition to the game is a booklet with fascinating facts about the landmarks.

Once upon a time you and your friends came up with a golden business idea together, but things soon went sour. You’ve since gone your separate ways with all new businesses - but tensions still run high. As you try to build your business portfolio to deliver cash profit, brand love and planet points your ex-friends are your new competition. Be the first to build your empire to 5 businesses (each with at least one business extension) to win the game and become the ultimate Zucker.

Players take turns to roll dice and activate their businesses to generate cash, love and planet points which can be used to spend money on business expansions. Action cards can change the game dramatically forcing players to lose points, close down businesses or steal businesses from other players.

The board game Мема: Кузуркі ("Mema: Insects") introduces you to the bugs you can spot in your own yard or while walking in a park or forest.

Which plants do they hide among? Which birds do they fear? What makes them unique? Play and find out!

The game offers two rule variants:

Classic “Memory”. A traditional version that helps you get to know the insects in the game and remember them.

“Meadow”. This variant introduces the world of insects: where they live and which birds they fear. It builds an interaction chain between plant–insect–bird in the natural environment.

An important addition to the game is a booklet with fascinating facts about insects, plants, and birds.

In the team board game Патлумач ("Explain") you’ll have to explain words on the cards using other words. But be careful — each explanation can become a trap for the rest of the players.

Патлумач is a fun board game with simple rules. You play as one team, and to win you need to truly understand each other.

Players take turns explaining a word from their card, following one important condition: you cannot repeat the choices of other players. Each card contains six different words, and you are not allowed to pick the same numbered word as those chosen before you. To avoid mistakes, you must carefully guess all the previous words — and you’ll have limited time to do it!

The game includes 1,200 Belarusian words, divided into two groups: simpler ones for children or those less fluent in Belarusian, and more complex, unique words for language enthusiasts.

Патлумач is a wonderful opportunity to speak Belarusian and enjoy quality time with friends and family.

WILD DOMINOES - This Regal Game comes with 55 double nine dominoes plus 11 extra Wild! Dominoes for a total of 66 dominoes. This set is compatible with Spinner and Double 9 Dominoes for replacements

DURABLE BONES - The Wild Spinner Dominoes game set features durable and sturdy tiles with indented and well-painted dots that won’t fade any time soon to provide you with long term use.

TIMELESS WITH A TWIST - Be the first player to dispose of your dominoes in this classic game that comes with a Wild! twist.

PORTABLE - The reusable tin protects the dominoes, providing a durable and organized case that can be easily stored away and taken on the go.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blue's Clues and You!: Scavenger Scurry Game
description:
In Scavenger Scurry, preschooler and kids join the action with Blue's Clues & You, searching for items around the room. To play, first place the 30 Color Cards around the room, color-side up. Spin the double spinner to get a color and number, and then get up and find that number of colored cards. If you do, move your Character piece on the board path. If you land on a Challenge space, everyone plays at once!

Each Challenge Card shows a different room, color, and number and everyone rushes to that room to find the correct number of colored items. First one back gets to move the number of spaces on the Challenge card. Reach the end of the board first, and you win!

Fulcrum is a card game set in medieval times, comprised of 54 poker-sized cards, and has both single-player (patience-style) and two player (head-to-head) rules.

In solo mode, place two trebuchet cards from your hand underneath any of the falling comets. Cards vary in value, depending on whether they match either the suit or colour of the comets. If you successfully place cards with equal or higher value underneath the comet before it hits the ground, it's destroyed. Otherwise, the game is over.

In head-to-head mode, comets fly overhead, and you must compete with your neighbouring castle to knock them out of the sky using the same points system as the solo mode. The player with the highest value destroyed comets at the end of the game is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Game Inc. takes you back to the vibrant 1980s, the golden age of video games. As the owner of a studio, your mission goes far beyond simply creating games: you must also develop consoles, expand your publishing company, and conquer the market in pursuit of money and—above all—victory points, the true measure of your studio’s fame.

But beware: the market is dynamic and highly competitive, and releasing the same game as your rival can be costly. To stand out, you will need to hire talented staff, invest in research to unlock new features, upgrade your console hardware, and strengthen your publishing business to reach as many players around the world as possible.

In the end, only the most famous studio will be crowned the winner. Are you ready to write your name into the history of the video game industry?

—description from the designer

Plurality is a finite territorial game. The game is played on a 13x13, 15x15, or 19x19 board. The concept of territory exist disconnected from concepts like groups or liberty. Territories' ownership depend on the most represented color in their perimeters.

On their turn, players drop two friendly stones and one adversary stone as a single orthogonally connected group (a tromino). It is forbidden to make a 2x2 shape of stones of any color configuration.

Check complete rules and examples at the game's forums.

—description from the designer

Microlonies is an asymmetric strategy game in which each player controls a unique ant species, striving to ensure the survival and dominance of its colony.

The game is played on a modular map representing the insect micro-world: hunting grounds, strategic areas, environmental bonuses, and a highly contested central termite mound. Each player begins with their own anthill, the core from which their colony expands.

The game features 16 ant species, all fully asymmetric, each with its own rules, strengths, limitations, and playstyle. Mastering your species while understanding the behavior and threats of others is key to success.

Throughout the game, players will:

Deploy ants to claim hunting zones

Take advantage of environmental bonuses

Complete species-specific objectives

Adapt to the presence and pressure of rival colonies

The central termite mound can become a powerful strategic asset, granting significant advantages, but it comes with a critical responsibility: feeding your population. Overexpansion or poor planning may lead to starvation and collapse.

In Microlonies, every choice matters. Growth, area control, direct interaction, and resource management are tightly interwoven in a demanding game where only the most adaptive colony will prevail.

—description from the publisher

Microlonies est un jeu de stratégie asymétrique dans lequel chaque joueur incarne une espèce de fourmis cherchant à assurer la survie et la domination de sa colonie.

Le plateau est constitué d’une carte modulaire représentant le micro-monde des insectes : zones de chasse, emplacements stratégiques, bonus environnementaux et une termitière centrale convoitée. Chaque joueur commence avec sa propre fourmilière, point d’origine de son expansion.

Le jeu propose 16 espèces de fourmis, toutes entièrement asymétriques, chacune disposant de règles spécifiques, de forces uniques et de contraintes propres. Maîtriser son espèce tout en comprenant celles des autres est essentiel pour survivre dans cet écosystème hostile.

Au fil de la partie, les joueurs doivent :

Déployer leurs fourmis sur la carte pour prendre position dans les zones de chasse

Exploiter les bonus offerts par l’environnement

Accomplir des objectifs propres à leur espèce

Observer et anticiper les actions des colonies adverses

La termitière centrale peut devenir un levier stratégique majeur, offrant des avantages puissants, mais elle impose une contrainte constante : nourrir sa population. Une expansion trop rapide ou mal maîtrisée peut conduire à l’effondrement de la colonie.

Dans Microlonies, chaque décision compte. Croissance, contrôle territorial, interaction directe et gestion des ressources s’entremêlent dans un jeu exigeant où seule la colonie la plus adaptée survivra.

—description from the publisher (French)

Make your game even more wacky with two new Tricky Rules cards!

Contents:
• 2 Tricky rules cards
• 1 rules and info card

Nine Nights Siege is a solo or cooperative fortress-defense game where you manage a small keep through a repeating day-and-night cycle. By day, you assign limited “Day Dice” to crucial tasks - gathering resources, crafting potions, training your hero, repairing defenses, and advancing a siege engine project.

At night, enemy forces assault the walls. Your preparations determine how well each zone holds, as defenses, allies, and tactical choices combine to blunt the attack. Randomness comes from dice, but long-term survival depends on planning, prioritization, and smart use of scarce actions.

The game is designed for Print & Play and features scenario-driven pressure, meaningful progression, and a fast, table-friendly footprint.

—description from the designer

Chicago Plumbers Local 130-opoly is a custom, Monopoly-style board game themed around the real-world culture, history, and work life of Plumbers Local 130 in Chicago. It blends classic property-trading mechanics with union pride, jobsite humor, and city-specific flavor.

Players take on the role of union plumbers navigating Chicago’s neighborhoods and major job sites, moving around the board to claim contracts instead of properties. Rather than buying streets, players secure commercial buildings, infrastructure projects, and union-relevant locations—from high-rise downtown builds to public works and industrial plants. Once owned, these contracts can be upgraded with training certifications, foreman status, and full crews, increasing the “dues” opponents must pay when landing there.

Money is themed as union wages, and the traditional railroads are replaced with supply houses, tool depots, or dispatch halls. Utilities become essentials like water mains and gas lines, where payouts scale based on dice rolls, reflecting the unpredictability of jobsite conditions.

Chance and Community Chest are reimagined as Jobsite Events and Union Business cards, triggering scenarios such as surprise inspections, overtime pay, strike votes, injury downtime, or winning a big municipal contract. Some cards reward players with bonuses and job security, while others introduce setbacks that reflect real-world trade challenges.

The “Jail” space becomes Apprenticeship Training or Union Hall Duty, where players are temporarily held up by mandatory classes, paperwork, or disciplinary review—but can get back to work by paying fines, using a special card, or rolling their way out.

The goal of the game is to outlast and out-earn rival plumbers, building a dominant presence across Chicago’s plumbing landscape without going broke. Victory goes to the player who best balances smart investments, union strategy, and a little Chicago grit—making Chicago Plumbers Local 130-opoly both a playful parody and a tribute to the trade.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
The Frog-Mages have long presided over the Loppian System, guided by their gift of Prophecy. Their wisdom allowed for the psychic storms which once tore across the system to be tamed and even harnessed. In time, they would come to set this power aside, scattering it throughout the system to prevent its power from being misused. Yet, it has not been buried forever. Deep within the unrelenting storms of Tlatec, a world deemed cursed by the Frog-Mages themselves—lies the secret to unlocking the long hidden power of the Voidsun.

Tehaxi, a Gecko Starseer driven by vengeance, leads a rebellion against the established Frog-Mages. But he does not work unopposed; Ruyi Nonga, a Jackal Vulture, has offered her services to the Frog-Mages, bringing with her a band of Jackal scavengers eager for adventure and salvage. They will set out in the search for the Voidcrown, the artifact said act as the key to control the system’s artificial sun.

The battle is soon joined by others. Poisonzeal, a self-proclaimed healer, emerges from hiding, claiming that the crown must never be reunited. Meanwhile, the High Elven Ancient Commander Trasara Loncrinius has been driven into the system by desperation, seeking to repair her damaged fleet but she is soon pulled into the conflict by those who seek to use her as a pawn.

Tehaxi is determined to claim the power of the Voidsun for himself, even as the destruction caused by his rebellion tears through the system. It is only a matter of time before the pieces are discovered and each of the factions must choose a side; will they fight against the threat of the Voidsun or embrace its promise of power.

Alliances will be tested and betrayals will cut deep.

When the storm breaks beneath the awakened Voidsun, will you be left standing?

The Voidsun Threat is a remaster of our Maximum Threat Mission Pack for Grimdark Future, our FREE Sci-Fi Miniature Game. It includes a full narrative story in which to play the 18 missions included in the pack as well as rules for different ways to run the campaign!

The book explores the story and characters over an art-filled, 15-page section, and includes the 18 missions, which were all reworked and adapted to the narrative, so you can replay the battles that encapsulated The Voidsun Threat.

Note that whilst the missions are designed for Grimdark Future, you can play them with the rules for any of our other games (with slight adjustments). This means that you have even more missions to play if you buy multiple packs!

Here are the missions included in this pack:

         Aerial Theatre
         Blood & Snow
         Dark Cave
         High Speed Chase
         Crimson Skies
         Excavation Site
         Fire & Rubble
         Industrial City
         Secure the Drops
         Big Guns Never Tire
         Extermination
         Tattered Legions
         Storm Clash
         Psychic Nightmare
         Portal Network
         Abyssal Rifts
         The Bale Eye Opens
         Spatial Distortion
    


Note that to play these missions you’re going to need the core rules for Grimdark Future as well as some army lists, which you can find for free on our website.

The main characters of this campaign are available for purchase as 3D print files on the One Page Rules MyMiniFactory store.

Psychopoly is a Monopoly-inspired board game themed around psychology and famous figures in the field. Instead of buying streets, players acquire properties that represent renowned psychologists and psychological concepts, with each property card often including background info about its “owner.” The goal is similar to classic Monopoly: roll the dice, move around the board, buy properties, collect rent, and aim to be the wealthiest player. The game comes with themed components like hand-sculpted pewter tokens (e.g., Maslow’s pyramid, Freud’s couch, a brain, lab rat), money, dice, and special card decks such as “Community Service” and “Synchronicity” that add psychology-flavored twists to gameplay. Board spaces include things like ethics boards and famous theoretical stages, and there’s even an “asylum” space with a chance for Free Therapy, mixing humor with learning.

It’s essentially a playful, educational re-theme of Monopoly that celebrates psychology history and ideas while delivering a familiar property-trading experience.

Golf‑Opoly is a golf-themed twist on the classic property trading board game that lets 2–6 players bring the fairway indoors with strategic, family-friendly play. Instead of streets, you acquire golf-related “properties,” earn rent by collecting tees and upgrading to clubhouses, and navigate fun course challenges like getting stuck in the woods or dodging storms and caddy fees as you move around the board. The game includes themed tokens like a golf bag, golfer, club, cart, shoe, and glove, and combines familiar roll-and-trade mechanics with a playful golf vibe perfect for fans of the sport and casual gamers alike.

It’s a light, competitive game that captures the spirit of golf strategy and luck, making every round feel like a quirky day on the course without ever leaving the table.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: Grimdark Future: Edge of the Radiance – Narrative Campaign
description:
Once a hero of the Eternal Dynasty, Captain Lyao had resigned himself to a life of obscurity on the barren world of Loung We at the edge of the Radiance Exclusion Zone. This changed suddenly when Prime Warrior Druzhak arrived, seeking a lost Alien Hive clutch hidden beneath the planet's surface.

Two forces collide in this all-new narrative campaign for OPR Games' Grimdark Future. What is there to discover at the Edge of the Radiance?

This game expansion for Grimdark Future takes players on an adventure across 10 missions which are tied together by an exciting story of epic proportions. Rules for unit progression are also included, so you can watch your armies grow and evolve throughout the campaign.

Even though this narrative campaign was written with Eternal Dynasty and Alien Hives in mind, it is easy to imagine this type of clash to be happening between any of the other factions in the Galaxy, so feel free to swap them out with whatever armies you have available to play.

Note that to play this campaign you’re going to need the core rules for Grimdark Future as well as some army lists, which you can find for free on our website.

The main characters of this campaign are available for purchase as 3D print files on the One Page Rules MyMiniFactory store.

Edge of the Radiance is book 1 out of 12 in the Gateway saga for Grimdark Future. Make sure to check out the rest of the books in the saga to complete this epic narrative.

In the conflict resolution game, players move around a board or take turns drawing scenario cards that describe common disagreements (like sharing, teasing, or differing needs). Each scenario prompts you to use specific peace-building strategies such as apologizing, compromising, listening actively, respecting differences, or finding a win-win solution.

On your turn you read or discuss a situation, decide which skill best addresses it, then explain your approach. Players earn points or advance spaces when they choose thoughtful, collaborative responses. Facilitators (or the game rules) guide the group through lessons on each conflict-resolution skill before or during play.

How to Win or Succeed
Winning isn’t about beating other players in the usual competitive sense — it’s about building the strongest set of conflict-handling skills. You “win” by consistently choosing solutions that reflect empathy, communication, compromise, and creative problem-solving, and by helping the group reach peaceful outcomes. The player or team with the highest score for effective solutions or positive interactions at the end is the winner. Points are awarded by a designated judge like a teacher or parent or other player.

In essence, conflict resolution games reward thoughtful, cooperative responses rather than competition — helping players learn to resolve real conflicts with respect and understanding

Earth is but a distant memory and humanity struggles to survive in the Sirius Sector, a new galaxy filled with epic conflicts, mighty heroes, and daring adventurers. Now they join in the conflicts of many species and factions in the battle for  survival and supremacy.

Sirius Chronicles Vol. 1 collects 12 short stories set in the worlds of Grimdark Future, and gives you a glimpse of the life and conflicts of the lives and people of the Sirius Sector.

Bring these tales to your game table as well. Each story includes a custom mission for Grimdark Future or Grimdark Future: Firefight to let you play out these personal conflicts and bring more variety to your games.

Out among the stars, how will your story be remembered in this Grimdark Future?

Included Stories & Missions:

         Jackpot by Pierre Mortel
         Last to Leave by Pierre Mortel
         Faith in the Hive by Dillon Olney
         Meal Ticket by Austin McIlsley
         Old Wounds by Dillon Olney
         Instrument of Fortune by Reese Surles
         Connection by Dillon Olney
         In Command by Austin Mcilsley
         The Garden of War by Austic Mcilsley
         Hidden Daggers by Dillon Olney
         Research Interrupted by Austic Mcilsley
         Last Ride by Robbie McGuinness
    


Even though the missions are designed for Grimdark Future and Grimdark Future: Firefight, you can play them with the rules for any of our other games (with slight adjustments).

Note that to play the included you’re going to need the core rules for Grimdark Future as well as some army lists, which you can find for free on our website.

-description from publisher's website

The war of winter’s night has broken out across the northern lands of Epizon.  A dark presence is pushing all the kingdoms to battle.  Alliances will be formed and broken.  Who will survive the chaos?

Epizon Battlefields is an expandable, turn-based, strategy, tabletop board game where players take turns moving pieces across a hex tile map and make use of polyhedral dice (D4, D6, D8, D10, D12, & D20) to decide their fate.  Players command their forces to secure their enemy’s fortress, defeat the opposing commander, or be the last one standing.  Venture across snowy fields, icy waters, bridges, trees and hills to engage the opposition.

50 Set Maps +Endless Custom Combinations
Each Map Offers a Different Experience
Play Any Combination of 2-6 Players
Team Up in 2v2, 3v3, or 2v2v2 Games
Easy to Get Started & Quick to Play
Long-Lasting, High-Quality Pieces

—description from the designer

Hersheypark‑Opoly is a themed property‑trading board game in the spirit of classic Monopoly but reimagined to celebrate Hersheypark and its attractions. Instead of buying streets, players purchase areas, rides, landmarks, and experiences from the famous Pennsylvania theme park where Hershey’s chocolate heritage meets coaster thrills. As you move around the board, you collect iconic park spots, charge opponents fees when they land on your properties, and draw event cards with sweet twists inspired by the park’s candy culture and amusement fun.

It’s a playful mix of nostalgia, strategy, and theme park excitement that lets fans bring a bit of The Sweetest Place on Earth home and compete to build the most deliciously successful board position.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
Grimdark Future: Firefight is a skirmish miniature wargame set in a war-torn sci-fi future, which is played using 28mm miniatures. The game mechanics are designed to be easy to learn but hard to master, bringing engaging sci-fi skirmishes for new and experienced players alike.

This rulebook adds lots of extra content to the core rules, providing you with countless hours of epic sci-fi skirmishes that you can customize however you like. The rules are written to be concise and to the point, and come with beautiful illustrations as well as helpful example diagrams.

Here's everything that's included in this rulebook:

     Core Rules
     Advanced Rules
         Terrain Placement
         Deployment Styles
         Extra Missions
         Side-Missions
         Extra Actions
         Random Events
         Battlefield Conditions
         Terrain & Objective Effects
    
     Total Conversions
         Fog of War
         Tactical Phase
         Command Points
         Critical Hits
         Suppression
         City Fighting
         Multiplayer Games
         Kitchen Table Games
    
     Additional Content         
         Tournament Guidelines
         Solo & Co-Op Rules
         Campaign Rules
         Mission Cards
    


Note that all of the army books are available for free on our website, and are not part of this rulebook. If any updates are made to the rules, then this version of the rulebook will also be updated.

Beachbody‑Opoly is a Monopoly‑style board game that playfully reimagines the classic property‑trading experience with a fitness and Beachbody theme. Just like in Monopoly, players roll dice to move around the board, buy themed properties, collect money from opponents, and aim to build a financial lead, but spaces and cards are customized to reflect Beachbody concepts, workouts, products, and community culture. Imagine snapping up popular programs, supplements, and coaching perks instead of streets and railroads, while event cards introduce workout challenges, product launches, or team‑building twists that can help or hinder your progress. The game blends familiar economic strategy with tongue‑in‑cheek nods to fitness goals and Beachbody lifestyle elements, making it a quirky, theme‑centric experience for board game nights.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
A print and play board game.

GIVE THE players each until the Hexagons run out. The person next to the dealer starts. They place a hexagon IN THE MIDDLE. then the other players place hexagons untiL they are filled. Record the Points on the POINT LIST on the Pad. Whoever gets rid of their hexagons wins.

Save the points for the next round.
Note: Hexagons need to touch each other, but do NOT overlap.

Set out the trivia cards face down or shuffle the deck. Players take turns drawing a card and reading the question aloud. Each card typically has multiple-choice or direct questions about the topic. On your turn, you either answer the question yourself or challenge another player to answer. If the answer is correct, you keep the card or score a point; if it’s incorrect, no point is awarded and play moves on. Keep going until a set number of cards are used or a target score is reached.

How to Win
The winner is the player (or team) with the most correct answers / collected cards at the end of the game. Listen carefully to all questions, and don’t be afraid to take a guess if you think you know the answer—every correct response gets you closer to victory.

boardgame: Try Not To Lose 5 Points: 1st Expansion
description:
The first expansion of Try Not To Lose 5 Points!

There are no changes to the rules.

Customizable - customize your cards.

-5 POINTS - MOST MOST Powerful card!!!!

Swap HandS - Swap HANDS WITH THE Player next to You, You can PICK whichever.

Stand or sit around a table with your tower (collapsed) and your ball ready. On the start signal, everyone bounces their ball into the hole at the top of their tower. Each time you make a successful shot, you expand your tower one level taller and then try again. Keep bouncing the ball into your tower as it gets taller and harder to make shots. Once you finally make a shot into your fully expanded tower, slam the top down to signal you’re finished.

How to Win
Speed and consistency are key. The first player to make it through all the stages and successfully bounce the ball into their fully expanded tower and slap it down finishes first. Everyone else keeps going, and the person to finish last loses. In short: bounce accurately, expand fast, and be the quickest to complete your tower.

Setup & Goal
Set up the Flip Kick Soccer table and place the ball on the field. Each player (or team) takes a side, with one controlling their players in front of their goal. The first to score 10 goals wins the match.

Basic Play
You take turns kicking and flipping the ball toward your opponent’s net using your player controls. Rather than traditional passing or manual movement, you use a flip kick mechanic—trap the ball between two of your players (if connected) and flick forward to send it flying toward the other goal.

Rules to Keep in Mind
● You can’t touch the ball with your hands—if you do, the play stops and the other team gets a penalty kick.
● If the ball leaves the playing surface, your opponent places it back on their side to resume play.

Scoring & Penalties
Each time the ball goes into your opponent’s net, you score a goal and move your score marker forward. If a player illegally touches the ball with their hand, the other team gets a penalty shot from the center spot with some defensive restrictions on the defending team.

In Big or Bang, players race along a cosmic track by chasing a fast-moving unicorn. This push-your-luck game tasks you with rolling dice to advance the unicorn. Each successful roll pushes the unicorn further, but as the collected dice are placed on the mat and cannot be rerolled, the risk of "busting" increases.
On your turn, you must collect all jokers and may choose one number to collect all matching dice. Then, you can reroll but you must collect a number higher than what is already collected. You must decide whether to "go big" with your remaining dice or "cash out" to move your own player meeple to the unicorn’s current position.
You can “take over” to start your turn where the previous player cashed out, inheriting their progress but with fewer dice. If you push too far and bust, the unicorn resets to the start, and you receive a bust token. However, collecting two identical bust tokens allows you to advance your meeple, turning a failure into a forward leap. The first player to reach or pass space 40 triggers the end of the race, and the traveller farthest along the track wins.

—description from the publisher

Capsule Collector is a set-collection game for 2–4 players in which players compete to gather and open colourful toy capsules. Players draw cards from a deck and add them to a communal queue, then choose a colour and take all available cards of that colour to fill a 4×4 personal collection grid. Because cards are initially collected "capsule-side" up, players must strategically activate Action Tiles to flip them to their "toy-side" to score points. The game features a dynamic action system where the number of cards collected in a turn determines which tile is activated, and tiles are shifted after use to change the available actions for future turns. The game concludes when a player fills their 16-card grid, leading to a final scoring phase. Toys vary in rarity; for example, a colour set like red might contain three high-value "5-star" toys, five "3-star" toys, and ten "1-star" toys, which shifts the probability of a big score depending on the colour's total card count. To maximize points, players must use "Open" actions to flip connected groups of the same colour or spend Open Tokens for individual reveal.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 勇者が一撃でやられた！ (Our Hero Was Defeated with a Single Blow!)
description:
勇者が一撃でやられた！ ("Our Hero Was Defeated with a Single Blow!") is a chase‑and‑escape board game for four players.
The Warrior, Priest, and Wizard must use their actions wisely to flee from the mighty Demon Lord.

Each player takes on one of the four characters and moves their figure through the Demon Lord’s castle.
The three heroes run for their lives while the Demon Lord relentlessly pursues them.

Players use basic actions such as Move or Stay, along with powerful character‑specific special actions, to escape the Demon Lord—or, when playing as the Demon Lord, to hunt down the heroes.

Characters rotate each round, and the first player to reach 10 points becomes the ultimate victor.

Tier King is a party deduction game where everyone works together to create a ranking chart—a “tier list.”
The Judge chooses a ranking theme, such as “sweetness of vegetables,” but hides the key word (in this case, “sweetness”).

Each player writes one item that should appear on the tier list, and the Judge places those items on the chart according to the hidden theme.
The other players then look at the placed words and try to deduce what the theme is.

Players get two chances to guess!
If everyone guesses incorrectly on the first attempt, the group adds another word to the list and tries again.

Choosing which word to submit is crucial, and interpreting the placed words is the heart of the deduction.
If the group correctly guesses the theme on the second attempt, the Judge earns a high score—so the Judge must guide the players with just the right hints through their placements.

We ourselves decide what our Universe is made of: a sphere or a flat disc, gravity or turtles and whales. Today you must build your own Universe from wooden pieces. But, brave Creators of the Universe, be careful: from creation to destruction is only one careless step.

In the board game На трох кітах ("On Three Whales"), players are challenged to construct and maintain their personal “universe” using three components — wooden blocks, discs, and tokens. The order in which the pieces are placed will be determined by fate — a six‑sided die.

Wordz Up! is a cooperative categories-style game that has you racing to find matching words for the called categories.
In the middle of the table, there are 3 letter cards. One player draws a category card, and off you go:
Call a category and all other players need to find a word matching this category that begins with one of the three letters in the middle of the table. If they do, they cover this letter with a new one from their personal draw pile and a new category is called.
Sounds easy? Well, as a team, you’re under serious time pressure: each round gives you 5 minutes to work through all the letter and special cards (yes, there are also special cards!). After five rounds, add up your points and see how well you did!

In front of you is a cheerful and intellectual board game featuring 100 wonderful, unique Belarusian words that are often used in everyday life.

Three different rule variants make the game engaging. You will need to find letters, guess, and invent words faster than your opponents. Inside the box you’ll find 100 cards with beautiful illustrations and words, as well as a dictionary with translations into Russian and English.

Мова: Цудоўная ("Language: Wonderful") is a great opportunity to gather with family and friends on a cozy winter evening and speak Belarusian together.

Box is a roll‑and‑write game in which you gradually fill the grid on your sheet with numbers.
Your goal is to group identical numbers into rectangles or squares whenever possible, because that’s how you “box” them in and score points. Keep an eye on the special spaces as well — they can earn you plenty of bonus points.
And if you have the most points at the end, you win the game!

Rumspinnen is a roll‑and‑write game played over two phases. In the first phase, you roll to determine which patterns of thick strands you can use use to weave your own spiderweb as tightly as possible. In the second phase, you receive the sheet of the player next to you and launch insects into their web with plenty of momentum — trying, of course, to cause as many negative points as you can. Who has woven the tightest web and catches the most insects in their own net?

Kuchen is a roll‑and‑write game all about eating cake. 
Grandma has been baking, and everyone wants a piece! But before you can shove that delicious cake into your greedy mouths, you’ll have to cut it into bite‑sized pieces. Each round, you decide anew: cut or eat? The more pieces you gobble up, the happier Grandma becomes. But beware — if you butcher the cake too much, there will be trouble …

Cooked Pirates invites you to become captain of a ridiculously incompetent pirate crew in a manga-inspired card battler. Fight with bizarre characters, like a vetetable wielding swordsman, each desperate to prove their worth in a world where gold is scarce and loyalty is bought.

The gameplay revolves around high-interaction elements. Each turn has a mandatory auction with a veto twist, where players bid gold to recruit new pirates. You must carefully manage your treasury, as strong pirate come with a gold cost. In combat, you fight with your active crew-pirate, and an optional face-down pirate from your hand, allowing you to bluff powerful attacks in key moments.

To win, you must be the first captain to fulfill 3 WANTED Contracts by collecting specific sets of pirates. However, the path to victory is not passive. Combat allows you to destroy rival crews or steal random cards directly from their hands. In a final twist, you cannot submit your third and final contract peacefully; you must successfully attack a rival captain in the same turn to claim the ultimate victory.

—description from the designer

Himizu is a game of strategy and cunning where the spirits of Fire 火 (hi in Japanese) and Water 水 (mizu in Japanese) face off in an unprecedented duel that will determine the fate of a clearing in the forest.

Objective of the game:

Fire 火: Have all 16 burning/charred terrain tiles on the board.

Water 水: Have no flame figures on the board.

If no one has achieved their goal by the end of round 10, Water wins. Therefore, Water can simply play to negate the objective of Fire.

On each turn, the player can move their pieces on the board and use their spirit's abilities.

Movement: Each spirit has 3 movements per turn, that can be distributed among their pieces as they see fit. Some spirit powers add more movements per turn. Each piece has effects when moving; for instance, each time a flame piece leaves a square, you put a burnt terrai tile on that square.

Powers: Each spirit has a unique set of 4 powers, and each power has a cost in spirit stones. Once you spend spirit stones to pay for those costs, your pass them on the opposing player. Your own spirit stones have a value of 2 when performing your powers, and the other player's spirit stones are worth just 1. You can only regain your spirit stones when the other player spends them. Thus, you need to balance the need of performing powers with the need of not giving too much power to the other player.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: Michitori is an abstract game for 2 to 4 players. The rules are super simple: Players take turns placing a path tile from their hand and one of their pieces. If you sandwich an opponent's piece with your own pieces, you can convert that piece to yours. The player with the most pieces on the board at the end wins. Despite the simple rules, the game is highly strategic and deep. When placing a path tile, all paths must connect. All players receive the same set of path tiles (5 types x 2 tiles each, though 2-player games use 5 types x 4 tiles each), making the order of tile usage and observing other players' tiles critical. Corners can be taken, but also outmaneuvered. Four-player games create different board states each time, leading to unpredictable developments. It's a game anyone can easily enjoy, yet it's hard to master.

boardgame: 三省堂国語辞典かるた：令和編 (Sanseido Kokugo Jiten Karuta: Reiwa Edition)
description:
The Sanseido Japanese Dictionary (Sankoku) 8th Edition, which swiftly incorporates new words, has been transformed into a karuta game! It features a careful selection of 50 new entries from the dictionary that have primarily gained popularity during the Reiwa era. Let's have fun guessing words that represent 'now'.

Features of this product:

Many of the words and phrases reflect a modern linguistic sensibility; while frequently used by many people, their meanings are often not well understood. This game provides an opportunity to rediscover such Japanese words and experience the fun of their meanings and nuances. Why not explore the words that mirror our lives in the 'now'?

boardgame: 三省堂国語辞典かるた：昭和・平成編 (Sanseido Japanese Dictionary Karuta: Showa-Heisei Edition)
description:
This Karuta game features words from the Showa and Heisei eras that have been deleted from the Sanseido Japanese Dictionary. Players quickly grab cards while learning about language from bygone days. Based on 'The Sanseido Japanese Dictionary of Lost Words,' it includes 50 widely used words from those periods, like 'Akadensha' (red train), 'Phonosheet', 'Kogyaru' (schoolgirl fad), and 'MD'. Designed with a pop, retro Showa-Heisei feel, it includes standard rules, advanced rules for replayability.

How to Play
Listen: A designated reader reads a sentence from a Reading Card.
Search: Players look at the Grabbing Cards on the floor to find the one.
Grab: As soon as you spot it, touch or grab the card. Speed is everything!

Quest for Happily Ever After is a lighthearted, family-friendly board game designed for players ages 4 and up. Set in a whimsical fairy-tale world, players travel along a winding path toward a happily ever after. The game board is intentionally designed with rich visual details to spark imagination and storytelling in young players, while keeping the rules and gameplay straightforward.

Gameplay follows a classic roll-and-move format inspired by traditional games such as Snakes and Ladders (also known as Chutes and Ladders). On each turn, a player rolls the die and moves their piece forward along the path. Certain spaces may move a player ahead or backward, creating moments of surprise and excitement without introducing strategy or complex logic. The detailed artwork adds atmosphere and charm but does not affect gameplay.

The objective is simple: be the first player to reach the final space. By relying entirely on chance rather than planning or roles, the game offers an accessible, stress-free experience that serves as an engaging introduction to board games for young children and an enjoyable activity for families to share.

The 23.6x23.6 Inch / 60x60 cm game board is assembled in a jigsaw puzzle style of 9 pieces, allowing small players to piece it together before play begins. This adds a tactile, engaging setup experience and makes the board easier to store while encouraging hands-on interaction. Once assembled, the spacious layout gives children plenty of room to see the path clearly and follow their progress throughout the game.

—description from the publisher

Nani Dungeon is a solo game where you as an adventurer go through a dungeon by playing item cards in ascending or descending order to gain item cubes to defeat monsters. Each dungeon card has specific conditions to fulfill that corresponds to the color of the cubes. Once fulfilled, you defeat the monster and advance to the next dungeon card. You win if you reach the end of the dungeon with the treasure chest and you'll lose if the Dungeon Master or Boss is in the same location as you or reaches to the treasure chest before you.

—user summary

There are various dungeons in the world-towers, caves, underground labyrinths, floating islands, and more. But why are these called dungeons? It's because all dungeons have two things in common: a Dungeon Master and treasure. The Dungeon Master is far too powerful to confront easily, but the treasures they guard are so alluring that many adventurers still venture forth into "some(nani) dungeon" risking everything for the promise of a bright future.

—description from the publisher

The Bloomworks is a city block known for bold art and busy markets, the perfect place for an aspiring florist such as yourself. With nothing more than a patch of studio sunlight, you've given yourself three years to grow a garden, master floriculture, and cover the block with your arrangements. The florist with the greatest reputation after three years gets to quit their day job and set up shop in the Bloomworks for good.
"—description from the publisher"

The game is played over 12 rounds (4 seasons per year). Each season, players harvest flowers from their garden to deliver flower arrangements to their neighbors. Each delivery moves you up the floriculture track in the different flower families earning special perks and end-game points. You can take actions to plant new flowers in your garden and expand your garden space to generate even more flowers year after year; buy and sell at the changing flower market to fill needs; and try to impress neighbors with your expertise and claim variable milestones for additional perks and points.

boardgame: Kingdoms: An open source strategy game
description:
Kingdoms is two-player minimalist strategy game where you place counters to capture your opponent’s castle. Cards are used in the game to provide powerful extra moves, and create an additional dimension of strategic play.

Whilst cards provide an element of chance, managing the cards and when to play them gives players full control whilst reflecting the vagaries of battlefield fortune.

—description from the designer

This is an expansion to the base game of Nani Dungeon.
It adds 2 to 4 players, 5 class characters, 3 bosses and a new dungeon.

New Dungeon Bosses have different movement speeds, different number of cubes to block movement requirements and different Trial cards setup.

Class characters have unique abilities.

The new dungeon cards makes it a little harder to achieve rewards.

The rules for playing 2 to 4 players is a little different from solo play. Each player now has a hand of 3 cards to play ascending or descending order to advance in the dungeon.

boardgame: First Victories: Assault On Western Ridge Expansion
description:
The Assault on Western Ridge Expansion for Glory & Empire: First Victories expands the battlefield with new scenarios, units, and tactical challenges set during the early campaigns of the Napoleonic Wars. As the British, French, and their allies clash for dominance, this expansion introduces fresh strategic depth, challenging players to outmaneuver, engage, and claim victory in critical engagements.

This expansion brings historically inspired scenarios focusing on the fierce engagements along the Western Ridge, testing your ability to coordinate infantry, cavalry, and artillery in the heat of battle. Experience new battlefield conditions and objectives that will push your tactical prowess to the limit.

In Wildscape, you will restore barren lands in Africa with rich habitats -- woodlands, wetlands, savanna, deserts, and rivers -- to create areas for a diverse animal ecosystem to return and to thrive.

Each player must expand their land and fill it with specific habitat tiles based on the needs of both common (but variable) objective cards and personal goal cards acquired throughout the game. Your habitats' size and type will attract various herbivores that grant end-game points and will support predators and primates that give powerful bonuses. You will hire specialists for their on-going effects to accelerate your revitalization effort in unique ways. The backbone of all of this is an action selection system on the community roundel where each space contains a unique combination of possible actions. Driven by this choice, action tokens cascade around the roundel creating an ever-changing strength for these actions throughout the game.

The winner will be the one who develops the most valuable ecosystem of herbivores, predators, primates, birds, and indigenous fauna.

—description from the publisher

A Totally Rad 1980's Murder. It's 1985 and shooting is about to begin on the latest blockbuster movie from the studio of Mr. Honcho L. Stree: Blastdance: The Explosive Teen Musical. The cast of teens (and would-be teens) are assembled, the crew are ready, and visitors to the set are hushed in anticipation. Suddenly, there's an explosion from the next soundstage, and it blows the body of Mr. Stree himself.

It's obvious it was murder--but whodunit? Was it Keanu Heaves, the teen star looking for another excellent adventure? Was it Leggy Varmer, the aerobics queen making her first movie? Or was it Martie McFry, a time traveler from the year 2024, who just buzzed onto the set in her custom Tesla? Who knows, dude? Maybe it was--YOU!

boardgame: デブリウム -オタカラ- (Debrium: Treasure)
description:
デブリウム -オタカラ- ("Debrium: Treasure") is a competitive mining board game for 3–4 players, played in about 30 minutes.

Players take the role of space corporations sent to a dangerous sector around an abandoned orbital station. Space debris constantly rains down in this area, making mining a high-risk operation. Hidden among the debris, however, are valuable treasures that companies are eager to recover.

Each round begins with a bid to determine turn order. Paying more grants earlier access to actions, but the player who acts last must take on debt. In turn order, players choose whether to mine debris or withdraw to gain additional funds, forcing constant decisions between risk and recovery.

During mining, a Debris Fall occurs, causing debris to crash onto the board at random locations. Workers caught by falling debris—or by burst effects that affect entire rows or columns—are removed, resulting in failed mining attempts. At the same time, newly fallen debris may contain even more valuable treasures.

At the end of the game, players total the points from collected debris, remaining funds, achievements, and penalties from debt. The player with the highest total score wins.

With fast-paced play, tense bidding, risk management, and unpredictable events, the game delivers a lively and dramatic strategic party experience.

—description from the designer

Designed by a long-distance hiker for both hikers and gamers alike. In the role of backpackers, players follow white blazes from Georgia to Maine as they visit Appalachian Trail locations, use Gear, encounter Wildlife, and live through Experiences and Weather on their path to reaching the opposite Terminus.

Blazing Thru the Appalachian Trail is played by going through a Trail deck one State at a time, each player taking two turns per State. In a turn, they plan their hike (draw a card from the Planner) and then hike (play a card from their hand, score it, and take its action). Gear cards may be used at any time during a player's turn; Gear helps draw or play extra cards or avoid difficulties during a hike.

The object of the game is to be the hiker with the most valuable experience (determined by points) at the end of the game, awarded during the game by Trail & Wildlife cards and State Bonuses. After the final State, add points from Premium Gear to determine the winner!

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Managerz From Hell: Let’s Rock is a competitive card game for 2–5 players that plays in about 30 minutes, putting you in the shoes of an aspiring rock band manager. It pays tribute through parody to real rock bands, musicians, and known rock situations, letting you assemble tongue-in-cheek lineups like AB/CD with Federico Mercurio on vocals, Splash on guitars, The Bug on bass, and The Beast Bunham on drums. Players draw from two shared decks: a Band deck and a Backstage deck (musicians and effect cards). Collect the required musician roles to form bands; bigger bands score more, and bonus cards can act as flexible substitutes when completing a band. The first player to reach 15 points wins.

On your turn you draw, may negotiate a one-to-one trade, play an effect as your action, and try to form a band. Effects come in three types: common effects (played as your action), bonus cards (used to help form bands), and free effects that can be played outside your turn to disrupt opponents. Effects include stealing cards or musicians, forcing discards, revealing hands, drawing extra cards, recovering from discard, cancelling effects, disrupting trades, and hindering band formation.

Smart trades, tight hand management, and well-timed free effects can swing the race to 15 points in a single turn, turning every gig into a tactical battle for the best lineup.

—description from the designer

Backyard Blackjack is a cooperative, take on the classic casino staple blackjack. 2–5 players work together to try have more chips than the casino by the end of 10 hands.

During those 10 hands, the team must navigate unique casinos and unpredictable dealer events. You win by taking the casino's chip reserve or finishing the final round with the larger stack. But be warned: the house always wins a tie.

Core Gameplay:
While the goal remains the classic "get to 21," the way you get there is entirely cooperative:

Shared Resource Management: Your chips are a shared pool. You can spend them to trigger powerful Special Actions like Peeking at the deck, Splitting hands, or Exchanging cards with teammates to save a hand from busting.

Themed Casinos: Choose from different casinos each with their own unique rules and twists.

Dealer Events: Each of the 10 hands features a unique Rule/Event card. These modifiers will give the players special objectives or rules to follow during a hand.

—description from the designer

Players are invited to a grand party at the Schlossberg Tower to celebrate Freiburg's 900th anniversary. However, they've mistakenly ended up at the Seepark Tower, and now they must quickly cross the city to reach the correct tower in time for the party. Those with a lucky roll of the dice can take shortcuts via bike paths, trams, or even an elevator inside the mountain. Those who are unlucky might slip in one of Freiburg's characteristic little streams (Bächle) and have to run for it to be the first to reach the finish line. Along the way, you'll also encounter some historical figures who have left a significant mark on Freiburg throughout its history.

Dino Racer is a fast-paced card game where five wacky dinos compete in a prehistoric race to the finish.

Across three races, the announcer flips Race cards to advance the dinos forward along the track. Players then draft those cards as betting tickets, scoring points based on which dinos finish 1st, 2nd, or 3rd. Faster dinosaurs award fewer points, while underdogs offer higher rewards.

As the race unfolds, checkpoints may trigger unexpected events that can dramatically shift the standings.

After three races, the player with the highest total score wins.

—description from the publisher

Collect the most pairs of cards to win. Pairs have special powers.  Peter Pan and Tinkerbell sprinkle pixie dust to take a card from the deck, Captain Hook and Croc say “Tick Tock” and steal a pair from another player. Wendy and brothers are a pair, and Lost Boys and Neverland are a pair.
In your turn: Draw a card (or more until you have 4 cards in your hand) from the deck or discard pile
Play a pair, or discard a card. Game ends when draw pile is depleted.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Peter Pan

description:
Checkpoint Charlie is a solitaire or cooperative game of British Secret Intelligence Service (SIS) espionage missions in Berlin in the early 1960s.

West Berlin is an isolated outpost of the Western Powers in the center of the German Democratic Republic (East Germany). East Berlin, on the other side of the Berlin Wall, is a base of operations for Soviet KGB agents and the Stasi secret police. This is a city of spies, a focal point of worldwide espionage in the growing Cold War. In this game, you send your assets (agents) on missions and use your influence and foresight to help them complete objectives before they are detected and compromised by KGB agents. As you play through these missions, you will:


     Ensure that an important defector gets safely out of the city.
     Make contact with a dissident Russian scientist on the other side of the Berlin Wall.
     Gather intelligence in East Berlin and return safely to the West
     Entrap a troublesome KGB agent with tempting intelligence.
     Sow distrust between KGB and Stasi agents.
     Identify a Soviet mole among your SIS agents and wait for them to reveal themselves.


Can you accomplish all of this in secret, as the very public events of the Cold War change the political landscape of Berlin itself?

—description from the publisher

Become a Mystic who seeks to master the four elements: Fire, Water, Earth and Air. Enlist your Warriors, summon the power of the Arcana and prepare to make sacrifices at just the right moment.

In this game you will gather cards and build your field using  Warriors that allow you to obtain the elements, or Arcana, which introduce effects that alter the course of the game: some will help you progress, others will directly affect your opponents. The first player to obtain and maintain all four elements is the winner. However, keeping the elements in your field requires constant sacrifice: so be prepared to lose cards from your hand.

With an aesthetic inspired by Tarot symbolism, “Místicos y Arcanos” combines art, tension and tactics in a game where nothing is guaranteed.

—description from the publisher

The moon is rising over Turtle Beach… and the hatchlings are waiting.

Beneath the sand, Hawksbill, Leatherback, and Flatback turtles begin to hatch - but the path to the sea is dangerous. Foxes prowl the dunes, rubbish and nets wash ashore, and only the moon can guide turtles safely home.

On your turn, draw cards and build turtles from four puzzle pieces, showing them hatching beneath the sand. Until a Moon card is placed on top, turtles remain vulnerable. Foxes and rubbish threaten your beach, while Rangers remove dangers and protect your hatchlings.

Each turn brings tense choices - build, defend, or disrupt another player. Be the first player (or team) to hatch and save the required number of turtles.

—description from the publisher
Played in teams. Players quickly memorize a hand of cards, then switch hands with their partners. Then each player chooses a card that he thinks will match one of his partner's cards.

Battleship: Black Panther Wakanda Forever game: This game for kids ages 7+, brings together strategy, competition, and excitement as players choose sides and go head-to-head on 3D platforms.

Deploy, defend, defeat: Black Panther fans will love this strategy game! It's the ultimate search-and-destroy mission as Wakanda and Talokan face off in a battle at sea on 3 different stacked grids.

Wakandan ships and talokanil structures: Players call out a sector and a row or column to make their hits. If their opponent's tokens occupy any part of that location it's a hit and gets an orange peg.

Includes vibranium pegs: If an opponent hits a location with a vibranium peg, that whole row or column is protected. That shot then deflects to the opponent's same location on their grid

2-Player game for kids: Land a hit and earn a special bonus to use on the next turn. The player who defeats their opponent's entire fleet before theirs is destroyed, wins the game

—description from the publisher

How to Play
Set up the game table with the backboard and trays, then hand out the two launchers and balls. Players stand on opposite sides and load their launcher with balls. On “go,” everyone fires balls into the game frame, trying to land them into the scoring areas by launching them quickly and accurately. It’s a mix of strategy, speed, and skill, where timing and aim matter as much as how fast you can shoot each ball.

How to Win
The core objective is similar to a rapid Connect-4-style challenge: be the first to get four of your shots lined up in a row (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) through fast, well-placed launches. Land your shots smartly so you block your opponent while building your own sequence. The first player who connects four in a row — or reaches the agreed target score if you play with rounds — wins the game.

In this strategic, bag building game, players will draw hexagon shaped tokens from their own bags each round trying to connect them to their own honeycomb which will earn points to acquire more honey and nectar tokens.

The player with the most honey tokens at the end of the game wins. However, honey tokens have only a single connector while other tokens have more connectors. You must balance your desire for buying honey tokens versus your ability to construct a large honeycomb.

Every round players will have the opportunity to draft a new token to help build their honeycomb or buy nectar and honey to get them closer to victory.

—description from the publisher

Draft cards to build streets of bird communities. Every player builds three roads by placing overlapping cards. Each game is played with four different scoring criteria. You must strategically place your drafted cards to maximize your score based on the four variable criteria for that game.

Along with drafting a row of cards you will gain a bird to place in its corresponding birdhouse at the end of each of the three rounds.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dominion of Rice and Napalm: NAM – Fight Battles of the Vietnam War: Solo Game
description:
This is the Dominion of Rice and Napalm Fight Battles of the Vietnam War Solo Game. 
This standalone set of rules is built on the Dominion of the Spear solo game system with appropriate rule changes for the Vietnam War. 
These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-7 units and a solo battle can be fought in 10-15 minutes. 
An example play through is included. 
There is a Quick Reference Sheet on the back cover. 
Use paper and pen (or model figures of any scale) as there is no measuring (grid used). 
Uses 2 six sided dice.

24 historical battles and campaigns are included ready to play, each with a brief background, army lists, deployment, and brief account of what happened historically. Together they provide a simple overview of the Vietnam War.

The battles are:

     Battle of Ap Bac 2nd January 1963 
     Battle of Binh Gia 28th December 1964-1st January 1965  
     Battle of Pleiku (Camp Holloway attack) 6-7th February 1965 
     Battle of Dong Xoai 9-13th June 1965  
     Battle of Van Tuong (Operation Starlite) 18-24th August 1965
     Battle of Ia Drang 14-18th November 1965  
     Battle of Cu Nghi (Operation Masher) January 28-31st 1966 
     Battle of Hill 488 15-16th June 1966
     Battle of Long Tan 18th August 1966
     Battle of Hill 875 (Battle of Dak To) 3-22nd November 1967
     Battle of Khe Sanh 21st January-9th July 1968
     Battle of Saigon (Tet Offensive) 31st January-7th March 1968
     Battle of Hue City (Tet Offensive) 30th January-2nd March 1968  
     Battle of Hamburger Hill 13-20th May 1969
     Battle of Fire Support Base Ripcord 1–23rd July 1970
     Cambodian Incursion 29th April-22nd July 1970 
     Battle of Firebase Mary Ann 28th March 1971
     Operation Lam Son 719 8th February-25th March 1971
     First Battle of Quang Tri (Easter Offensive) 30th March-1st May 1972  
     Battle of An Loc (Easter Offensive) 13th April-20th July 1972  
     Battle of Kontum 14th May-18th June 1972 
     Battle of Ban Me Thuot (Spring Offensive) 10-18th March 1975
     Battle of Xuan Loc 9-21st April 1975
     Fall of Saigon 27-30th April 1975


This is easily read on a tablet or mobile phone and can be printed as an A5 booklet. 44 A5 pages.

—description from the publisher

Counting Cats is a cute pocket-sized game with that involves two things: counting and cats!

On a turn, players will reveal cards one at a time from the deck, forming a full panoramic picture of a bedroom. Once it is formed, it is all about speed; all players immediately start counting the number of cats (real cats!) in the bedroom that matches the target cat type, which is visible as the backcard of the card on top of the deck (e.g., Orange cats).

Only one answer matters: Say it out loud. Everyone get to chance; the answer is wrong. If it is correct, the player scores 2 PAWnts. If not, all other players score 1 PAWnts.

Game end when a player scored 5 points.

—description from the designer

No Chance! is a category game that challenges your knowledge against other players, but it is not just about what you know. It is also about how well you bluff and how well you know your friends.

The goal is to prove to other players that you can name a specific number of items in a category within the time limit. At the same time, you should challenge other players who you think have No Chance! of naming that many. Completing a challenge earns you points, while failing one costs you points or, more likely, a big sip of your drink!

The round begins with the player on turn playing a category card from their hand, such as "Fast Food Restaurants," and making a claim about how many items they can name.
