The next player has two options: they can doubt the claim by calling out No Chance! and issuing a challenge, or they can believe the player and play a card from their hand, making their own claim. Cards typically increase the number of items required in the current category, making the task more difficult as the round progresses until, eventually, a player calls out No Chance! At that point, the challenged player must complete the task within a specific timeframe.

While this is the basic gameplay, special cards add new dynamics and an extra layer of excitement, making the rounds even more interesting and fun.

In a world where corporate drones are sacrificing their life at the alter of quarterly earnings, you have opted for a more... exciting... life path.

You are a merc that has opted to make rent by performing dangerous gigs for cash. Maybe one day you'll save up enough credits to buy that flat and escape your landlord's tyranny!

Cy//Korg is a solo cyberpunk game that fits in a small easy to carry around zine. You never know when you'll be stuck somewhere with some time to burn and the itch to roll some dice.

Imitori Karuta is a game where you listen to a meaning and then quickly take the card with the corresponding word. Accurately knowing the meaning of words is a great opportunity for both children and adults. Take turns as the reader and the taker, becoming familiar with various words and their meanings. It's also recommended to play leisurely with a dictionary in hand, as dictionaries become more enjoyable the more you flip through them. Explaining words from their meanings and guessing words from their meanings are quite different experiences, both important for language familiarity.

How to Play: The reader draws one reading card from the deck and slowly reads its explanation aloud for everyone to hear. Other players quickly grab the taking card that matches the explanation on the reading card. Players who successfully take the correct card acquire it. Players who take an incorrect card sit out for that round.

The cards come in four types: 'Beginner,' 'Advanced,' 'Development,' and 'DIY,' to suit players and levels. Beginner and Advanced cards are selected from 7,700 basic words that elementary school students should learn. The reading cards indicate the recommended grade level in the upper right. 'Beginner' cards are chosen from basic words for grades 1-3, including simple words playable by 5-year-olds, with 30 relatively easy words. 'Advanced' cards are selected from basic words for grades 4-6, focusing on 30 slightly more difficult words that even adults might struggle to define precisely. 'Development' cards are chosen from approximately 40,000 words beyond the basic set, focusing on 30 fun words for play, Katakana words, and buzzwords. 'DIY' cards are blank cards, allowing players to create their own important words. 30 blank cards are provided so you can make cards with words significant to you. Try flipping through a dictionary to find words and create your unique karuta set. It will surely be fun to create it together with friends, parents, or grandparents.

—description from the designer

Get out into nature – or bring adventure into your living room! Together, you can build an extraordinary island, piece by piece, using leaves, twigs, stones and much more. Everyone has secret missions and transforms the collected materials into boats, treasures or even volcanoes. Indoors, Freiluftwelten provides creative fun – outdoors, it becomes an experience like never before: an adventure that takes place right in nature!

—description from the publisher

Raus in die Natur – oder holt das Abenteuer ins Wohnzimmer! Gemeinsam baut ihr eine außergewöhnliche Insel, Stück für Stück, aus Blättern, Zweigen, Steinen und vielem mehr. Jeder hat geheime Aufträge und verwandelt die gesammelten Materialien in Boote, Schätze oder sogar Vulkane. Drinnen sorgt Freiluftwelten für kreativen Spielspaß – draußen wird es zu einem Erlebnis, das es so noch nie gab: ein Abenteuer, das direkt in der Natur entsteht!

—description from the publisher (German)

boardgame: Happy Double: No Risk No Run!
description:
Roll two twelve-sided dice, record the higher value and decide when you want to stop. The player who skilfully weighs up the risks, draws action cards with doubles and collects the most points wins in the end. The game promotes quick decision-making, courage and judgement. Every roll brings excitement, and the balance between risk and safety keeps all players actively involved. Ideal for entertaining rounds full of thrills.

—description from the publisher

Würfle zwei zwölfseitige Würfel, trage den höheren Wert ein und entscheide, wann du stoppen willst. Wer geschickt sein Risiko abwägt, mit Paschen Aktionskarten zieht und die meisten Punkte sammelt, gewinnt am Ende. Das Spiel fördert schnelles Entscheiden, Mut und Einschätzungsvermögen. Jeder Wurf bringt Spannung, und die Balance zwischen Risiko und Sicherheit hält alle Spieler aktiv dabei. Ideal für kurzweilige Runden voller Nervenkitzel.

—description from the publisher (German)

The Green Star is a tactile, narrative-driven escape room game designed for home printing. Set at a remote observatory in 1953, players take on the role of agents conducting surveillance on a disgraced astronomer obsessed with a mysterious celestial signal known as the Green Star.

Gameplay centers on a printable mission dossier. Players must physically manipulate these documents by cutting, folding, and aligning paper components to decode hidden messages and solve nine interrelated puzzles to advance the story.

A browser-based digital companion app supports the experience by simulating the Bancroft Telescope Control Panel. This interface provides verification for puzzle solutions and offers hints as players focus on the physical investigation of the dossier.

boardgame: Rat Schemes: A Survival Card Game
description:
Rat Schemes is a tactical survival card game for 4–10 players set in medieval times. Players take on the roles of selfish rats competing to be the last one standing while avoiding capture by the Hunter. The game emphasizes direct player interaction through betrayal, deception, and strategic backstabbing.

The game consists of two distinct phases. In Phase 1 (Schemes and Intrigues), players draw and play cards to attack opponents, steal resources, and accumulate Cheese — the game’s survival currency. Attack cards include Bite (forcing players to lose Cheese or skip turns), Rat Trap (stunning players for a turn), and Cage (forcing extended turn skips). Players defend using cards like Luck (canceling any action), Hide in Shadow (avoiding the Hunter), or Slippery Rat (redirecting attacks to the next player). The deck continuously cycles as used cards are reshuffled back into play.

Phase 2 begins when any player draws The Hunter card. The deck locks (players can no longer draw cards except through specific card effects), and the Hunter begins moving clockwise around the table. On the Hunter’s turn, the active player must either discard Cheese to pay him off, use defense cards to escape, or go to the cage and be eliminated. Meanwhile, players continue attacking each other — they can play Mark to teleport the Hunter to a chosen victim, use Pull from Shadow to drag hidden opponents directly to the Hunter, or play Poisoned Cheese to reverse an opponent’s card effects.

Victory is achieved by being the last rat not in the cage. The game features 23 unique card types (89 cards total) with mechanics that encourage temporary alliances, sudden betrayals, and constant tension, as players never know when the Hunter will reappear in the reshuffled deck.

Rat Schemes is currently raising funds on Kickstarter and is not yet in retail.
If you enjoy high-interaction card games filled with betrayal and chaos, please consider supporting the project on Kickstarter and help bring Rat Schemes to life.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Snorkers! (Good oh!): Rules for Fast Paced Naval Actions 1939-1945
description: Snorkers is a set of miniature wargames rules focussing on actions between smaller ships (light cruisers and destroyers) during WW2. It features a turn structure that stresses the friction of naval battles, a force morale system, a quick mechanism for resolving gunfire and a unique torpedo system. It also includes rules for air attacks and off-table covering forces of battleships, unit stats and a number of scenarios.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Drużyna do Zadań Specjalnych is a cooperative adventure board game based on Marcin Mortka’s humorous fantasy novels, where players take on the roles of iconic heroes and work together to overcome enemies and challenges from the story. Throughout the game, players manage their hands of cards, choosing whether to use each card for its numerical value or for its special effect. Using a card for its value helps the active player face challenges and defeat enemies, while using its special effect allows players to support their teammates at critical moments.

Victory depends on cooperation and smart timing. Players must constantly decide whether to focus on their own actions or sacrifice power to assist others. With five different bosses and their accompanying minions standing in the way, only a well-coordinated team that uses its cards wisely will be able to overcome the challenges and win the game.

—description from the publisher

Monopoly Junior KOSMOS board game is a fascinating journey into the unknown, taking young players straight into outer space. Rockets, shimmering asteroids, mysterious planets, and orbital stations await to be discovered. This cosmic edition of the classic game stimulates imagination, teaches strategic thinking, and guarantees great fun for the whole family.
Key features of the game:


     Adventurous journey between planets – traverse galaxies, collect minerals, and visit distant corners of space.
     New challenges and cosmic events – space storms, rocket malfunctions, and unpredictable occurrences add excitement to every game.
     Kid-friendly rules – simplified mechanics allow children to quickly grasp the rules and start playing.
     Interactive, educational fun – children learn planning, counting, and decision-making while exploring space-related topics.
     Attractive design and colorful board – futuristic spaces, shiny graphics, and a unique atmosphere encourage long gameplay sessions.
     For 2–4 players – an ideal family game that engages both children and adults.


Rules of the KOSMOS edition:


     Choose an astronaut or helper robot token, take MONOPOLY money, and prepare for launch.
     A dice roll decides the first player.
     Landing on planet and space station spaces allows you to purchase them, and if they belong to another player, you pay a fee for stopping there.
     Acquire all spaces of one color, place a token, and increase your cosmic earnings.
     The winner is the player who gains dominance on the board and drives opponents to bankruptcy.
     Once you've mastered the basics, try additional rules to create even more exciting gameplay!

Players cooperate as heroes—the Bard, the Shaman, and the Magician—each with special abilities that can modify dice, creatures, ... Battles are resolved through dice rolls, but clever use of abilities and optimal placement of heroes allows the team to maximize their chances of success.

The game features a variety of creatures, each represented by a combination of two cards—such as Weak Goblin, Corrupted Dragon, or Legendary Skeleton. Heroes must adapt their tactics to the creatures’ strengths and weaknesses while managing their dice and abilities.

Victory is achieved by winning five turns before the creatures do. To win a turn, the total power of the defeated creatures must exceed the total power of the remaining, undefeated creatures for that round. Strategic cooperation, thoughtful use of hero abilities, and smart dice management are key to defeating the monsters and claiming victory in this fast-paced, tactical adventure.

Combo-bliterate! is a solo card shedding game using a standard 52 deck of cards. This game is about finding clever ways to create Chain Reactions (similarly to games like Candy Crush, or Potion Explosion) to clear your hand efficiently and to score points. The game ends once the entire deck has run out and you have no cards left in hand. The aim of the game is to have a final score of at least 1 point.

When you perform a Remove action to shed a card, you place the discarded card into your penalty pile. Each card in your penalty pile will lose you 2 points. However, a Remove action may cause a Chain Reaction allowing cards to get Scored and discarded to your scoring pile either faceup or facedown depending on certain criteria: Create a run of 3 or more to discard those faceup to your scoring pile, or create a pair of matching suit or rank to discard those facedown in your scoring pile. Faceup cards in your scoring pile will be worth 1 point each, and facedown cards will only be worth 1 point for every 2.

Score more points than you lose from your Remove actions and you will win.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Souls to Waste: The Battle of An Bao, May 5, 1968
description:
“I see light at the end of the tunnel.” –National Security Advisor Walt Rostow, December 1967.

Souls to Waste portrays the epic fight waged between the 1st Battalion, 50th Infantry Regiment of the 173rd Airborne Brigade and tanks and the 1/69th Tank Battalion against three Battalions of the 22nd Regiment, 3rd PAVN (People’s Army of Vietnam) Infantry Division.

The NVA laid a trap in the hills to the west of three firebases maintained by the 173rd Infantry Brigade. The NVA’s 22nd Infantry Regiment was recently deployed to the area and well-armed with the latest Soviet weaponry, especially new Rocket-Propelled Grenades (RPGs) that could easily penetrate the armor of the American’s M-113 Armored Personnel Carriers (APCs), as well as mortars and heavy machine guns. The goal was to lure one of the US troop companies into an ambush, and then either inflict more casualties on any relief force that would come to their aid, or fall back and do it again at a time and place of their choosing.

Each turn represents 30 minutes of time. An inch on the map corresponds to about 100 yards in actual distance. Infantry type units are platoons, and armored units represent 1 or 2 vehicles.

—description from the publisher

TETRA FLIP is a solo card game using 2 standard 52 decks of cards. The game is about placing cards into a grid by sliding them down from the top of the columns to the bottom (like the game Tetris), trying to create sets so you can remove them from the grid and score points. A set is a pattern of 4 or more of the same suit, rank, or ascending/descending sequential runs.

The deck is shuffled with half the cards faceup and half facedown. Use the Flip action wisely to flip all cards in your hand to the opposite face at the best time.

The game is played in rounds and at the start of each round a market of cards is available for you to purchase pairs of cards from. These pairs are "attached" to each other and must be placed into the grid adjacent to one another. You use facedown cards from your hand to pay for these cards in the market. If you leave cards unpurchased in the market at the end of a round, they will enter your grid facedown, causing a hindrance.

The game ends in 1 of 3 ways: If you are forced to place a card outside of your 4 by 4 grid, you immediately lose the game. If you end a round with no cards left in your grid, you immediately win the game. If all 8 rounds are complete, you score 1 point per card in your score pile, however, you lose 1 point per remaining faceup card and 2 points per remaining facedown card in your grid. If you score at least 1 point, you win.

—description from the designer

Infinitune is a Modular music Card-game that turns music theory into a game for all. Match different notes to create music intervals, scales and chords, and win more fans than the other musicians in the concert. There is NO need to know anything about music, the game will teach you everything you need to know.

Each gameplay mode offers a unique way to get more fans. In the Dis-chordia game mode you will be assembling chords by drawing and discarding cards until  you have at least the 3 basic notes. Each note will grant you +10 new fans. But if you want a challenge level up the game to be able to use complex interactions called "Accompaniments" as others play their chords to get ahead in the game.

In the Intervals game mode you will be counting notes to be the first one to play the one that is at the distance described by the interval deck. Play the next note to earn +10 fans, or chain multiple notes in the same turn to gain a "sequence" multiplier.

Before each game, no matter the game mode chosen, players pick a venue, and each venue has a capacity limit. First "musician" to fill the venue with their fans wins.

Higher Worlds is a civilization board game, where you lead one of four ancient alien empires that are adrift in the alternate universe of the Firmament.

Blends Euro-style resource management and tableau building, with combat and area control.

Lead Your Empire: Start with a homeworld and basic units. Build settlements and reclamators to harvest resources like Aethe gas, Shade oil, Shine plasma, and Teara ore. Transform Populace into specialized Heroes.

Discover Worlds: Find and claim the dreamlike wildworlds spread across the eight realms. Build and unlock development bonuses, but keep an eye on your rivals.

Develop and Adapt: Draw from your Construct and Tactic decks to queue and play cards on your player board. Acquire Lore cards to gain arcane advantages. Use Life actions to generate wealth and build culture.

Battle and Encounters: Escort forces with Captains to invade worlds. Survive monsters and events spawned on the Encounter track.

Win the Game: Deliver Keys to the Great Door to become the most cultured of all empires.
 Solo Play, Scenarios, and Narrative Depth: Immerse yourself in a highly developed fictional universe that pays homage to the unbridled creativity of mid-20th century sci-fi, fantasy, and art.

—description from the designer

A very special Encyclopedia!

Hello Globetrotters! In this cooperative memory game, you will travel around the world in search of the monsters, spirits, and legendary creatures that inhabit it.

The Encyclopedia of Monsters comes in 5 colourful Volumes - like a real encyclopedia - that represent the 5 Continents. Each Volume contains all the necessary components to visit that Continent’s 3 Countries. Each Country offers you new ways to play, with its own rules, challenges, and monsters.

Find all the Monsters! On your turn, play a Monster card and use its Special Rule to try to find the corresponding token. If you successfully find that Monster, it stays visible; otherwise, a Candle is blown out, all the Monsters flip face-down again... and you start over, and try to remember where each one is hiding!

This is not just a game! Each page you turn in your encyclopedia teaches you something about the customs or creatures of each Country you are visiting, sometimes even in the words of the people who live there.

Become the world’s greatest Monster Expert!
 —description from the publisher

The expansion adds four Spices to the game, one of which will be part of the players pie. Add the cards to the normal ingredient cards, rolls an additional 1d4 to determine which spice you get, and play the game over six rounds instead of five.

Spices are: Cinnamon, Garlic, Saffron and Ginger.

—description from the publisher

A secret military base beneath the Nevada desert just wen sideways fast. After a failed experiment, an alien mutagen now spreads through the corridors, absorbing and rewriting everything it touches. Few humans remain, every minute counts, and the nightmare learns as you play. Will you escape... or be assimilated?

Survive the fall.
Hoxfall is a cooperative survival horror dungeon-crawler set in a secret military base where an alien mutagen is corrupting all organic life in its path. Outnumbered and unprepared, team up with other unlikely survivors and fight your way through hordes of alien mutants before your luck, and time, runs out. The mutagen spreads unchecked as the story moves on with or without you, in this real-time narrative campaign.

Find a way out!
You're trapped in the underground facility and each section of the base is a different scenario. Choose which section to explore, impacting how you experience the story. While the ever-mutating organism grow in strength... your ammo and energy are limited. Is up to you ti decide when and how to utilize them, in a desperate attempt to survive.

Thanks to the Teburu System, experience a living narrative that reacts to your actions, creating suspense, surprises, and memorable moments every session. Through the digital app, and physical Smart miniatures and dice, the overrun military base feels alive... because it truly is! The mutagen learns during play, spreading unchecked throughout the facility as the story moves on with or without player intervention. Survival is the only focus!

—description from the publisher

Fantasy Zombicide is back, and this time it’s a pirate invasion! Dead Men Tales brings the popular Zombicide gameplay to paradisiacal pirate islands, where gold is plentiful and grom flows freely. Command crews of humans, orcs, elves, and more as you face off against relentless zombie hordes. Fire cannons, swing from ropes, and fight for survival as the undead attack from every direction, including the sea itself!

Gamefound Campaign: https://gamefound.com/en/projects/asmodee/dead-men-tales

—description from the publisher

Freak Ball is a 1 vs 1 exciting football (soccer) game that fits in your pocket.

With just 52 minieuro-sized cards you can recreate a tense and thrilling final where you´ll be represented by one of 8 different players, with their unique special ability.

During the match, you will both play with the same six starting cards, with different effects on attack and defense, so it will be crucial to get inside your opponent's mind and choose appropriately to overcome them and take the lead.

In addition, you will also have to deal with fatigue, which means you will have fewer and fewer cards and will have to choose wisely which ones to keep, until you have none left at the end of the game.

In case of a tie, or if you want to play with the little ones at home, take the "Penalty Shootout" cards and settle it, because we definitely need a champion!

—description from the designer

After a successful first year building your Conservas business, it’s time to venture into uncharted waters. A new season awaits, and your customers are hungry for bold new flavors from the sea. But chasing new tides also brings fresh challenges—ones that will truly test your fishing skills.

This expansion opens up new opportunities with additional markets to explore, a wider variety of boats and upgrades, and new ways to experience the base game. It includes three modular additions:


     New Upgrade and Boat cards
     A new Market Book
     Locations


You may play with any combination of these modules—including all three—tailoring the experience to your preferred level of depth. The expansion also introduces new token types: Eels, Tuna, and Predators, adding variety and new strategic considerations to every game.

—description from the publisher

Games Room: Action Movie Trivia is a high-energy trivia game themed around classic and modern action movies. Players answer questions covering famous films, characters, quotes, and unforgettable moments from the action genre, celebrating everything from explosive blockbusters to cult favorites.

Gameplay is straightforward and fast-moving. Players take turns answering action-movie–themed questions, earning points for correct answers while racing against their opponents to build a lead. The game is designed for quick rounds and accessible play, making it easy for both casual players and movie buffs to jump in.

The goal is to score the most points by answering the most questions correctly before the game ends. The player with the highest score proves they have the ultimate action movie knowledge and wins the game.

boardgame: Entre Linhas Superinteressante: Mais Palavras Superinteressantes (2)
description:
Mais Palavras Superinteressantes ("More Super Interesting Words") are individual mini-expansions for the game Entre Linhas Superinteressante, with themes that delve into the universe of science, history, and technology—all with Super's distinctive creative touch.

Players can shuffle these cards along with the others and let fate decide, or play them directly to make the game even more interesting!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Mais Palavras Superinteressantes são mini-expansões individuais para o jogo Entre Linhas Superinteressante, com temas que mergulham no universo da ciência, história e tecnologia — tudo isso com o toque criativo e marcante da Super.

Os jogadores podem embaralhar essas cartas junto às demais e deixar o destino escolher, ou colocá-las diretamente em jogo para deixar a partida ainda mais interessante!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Entre Linhas Superinteressante: Mais Palavras Superinteressantes (4)
description:
Mais Palavras Superinteressantes ("More Super Interesting Words") are individual mini-expansions for the game Entre Linhas Superinteressante, with themes that delve into the universe of science, history, and technology—all with Super's distinctive creative touch.

Players can shuffle these cards along with the others and let fate decide, or play them directly to make the game even more interesting!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Mais Palavras Superinteressantes são mini-expansões individuais para o jogo Entre Linhas Superinteressante, com temas que mergulham no universo da ciência, história e tecnologia — tudo isso com o toque criativo e marcante da Super.

Os jogadores podem embaralhar essas cartas junto às demais e deixar o destino escolher, ou colocá-las diretamente em jogo para deixar a partida ainda mais interessante!

—description from the publisher

Spin Cycle is the playful, suspense-filled card game of heroic washing machines battling evil laundry. Compete for glory, defeat laundry, and collect relics hidden in pockets. Read your rivals and play your cards right to be crowned the most legendary LaundroBot!

Asymmetric player characters, lane battling, set collecting, and loads of puns all tumble together to form a snappy lighthearted game that will leave you asking for just one more spin.

—description from the designer

Extend your path by connecting tiles that depict straight, crossroads, and curved paths.
However, since paths are stacked in a single pile, you can't see the path you were on a moment ago. You'll need to draw a map in your head.
Eventually, one player may accidentally collide with another and you can declare "end" to win.
You can also push the limits by placing "double tiles" to cause confusion and collide paths, or by intentionally creating routes that seem like they'll collide.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: War of the Ring: Sieges of Middle-earth
description:
During the War of the Ring, the strongholds of the Free Peoples faced their darkest hour, under assault by the relentless armies of the Shadow. Siege towers advanced against mighty walls; catapults battered the defenders day after day. Yet these assaults – those that took place, and those that might have – were decided not only by swords and engines of war, but by the endurance and courage of the brave souls standing against overwhelming odds.

Sieges Of Middle-Earth is the fourth expansion for the War of The Ring: Second Edition board game, focusing on the siege battles against the main strongholds of the Free Peoples. New game mechanisms bring those desperate battles to the forefront of the conflict: the Shadow player can field powerful siege engines to smash fortifications and spread despair among the defenders, while the Free Peoples player may attempt heroic feats, lead bold sorties, and inspire last stands that can turn the tide of battle.

New components include plastic figures, special dice, tokens, and cards to add to the core game, expanding the strategic options of both players and enhancing storytelling possibilities.

—description from the publisher
family: Series: War of the Ring (Nexus/Ares Games)

description:
Shadow of Accomplice is an expansion of Dying Message, which is an expansion of the card that can be played for another fun with the addition of a new identity card.

The addition of identification cards of 13 suspects, 13 tools, 9 motives, 9 adjectives, and 9 nouns will enable more diverse reasoning, increasing the possibility of playing the game.

If there are more than 4 people, you can play the game with a newly added expansion rule. Players have to deduce a total of 4 cards, from the existing 3 cards to the accomplice. One player is now an accomplice to the killer. The accomplice knows the culprit, the tools to kill, and the motive for the killing. The accomplice must guide the reasoning of other players in the wrong direction.

With a total of 27 new cards and 20 tiles, including newly added suspects, tools, murder motives, nouns, and adjective tiles, you can now deduce more colorfully.

A larger box can store all the expansions together with the main edition.

Players collectively build the mountain using L-shaped blocks and race their 3 mountain climbers to the summit of the mountain using action points allocated by dice-rolling. Players may draw Positive and Negative cards and play them on future turns to help themselves or hinder an opponent. The player who gets all three of their mountain climbers to the summit first wins!

The Herbalist’s goal is to gather rare ingredients from the surrounding forests and craft powerful potions for the citizens of Mythwind Valley. The Herbalist explores the forests, researches new recipes, and refines her craft through careful study and experimentation. Her work provides medicine, elixirs, and remedies that strengthen the town and its people.

The Forest
The Herbalist ventures into a vast forest, divided into three distinct depths: Grove, Woodland, and Deepwood. Each level contains unique opportunities and dangers. The deeper she travels, the rarer and more valuable the ingredients become.

The Greenhouse
After returning from the forest, the Herbalist brings her gathered ingredients to the greenhouse. There she researches new potions, clears spaces for future ingredients, and brews her creations to earn coins and unlock special abilities that improve her craft.

Tools and Upgrades
The Herbalist begins with simple gear for travel and study. As her knowledge grows, she can enhance her equipment to move farther, carry more, and manipulate ingredients with greater precision.

—description from the publisher

The Shepherd’s goal is to nurture the pasture, guide a growing flock, and provide the town with wool, milk, and other valuable goods. Each day, the Shepherd works the land, tending crops, growing apple trees, herding sheep, and shaping the rhythm of Mythwind Valley’s fields. Careful planning, smart movement, and a steady hand are essential as the Shepherd manages the flock and responds to the changing weather.

The Pasture
The Shepherd oversees six connected regions of open pasture. Weather determines how quickly pasture grows, when apples ripen, and how the land shifts from day to day. Each region offers opportunities to plant new crops, harvest the land, or expand the flock. Managing space, growth, and grazing is key to building a healthy and productive pasture.

The Flock
The Shepherd guides up to three sheep across the regions to graze, produce, and stay safe from predators. A well-fed flock thrives and grows, but stray sheep or poor grazing patterns can be dangerous. Keeping the flock together, creating new sheep, and protecting them during Dusk is central to the Shepherd’s strategy.

Companions
Dogs and hired workers support the Shepherd with movement, foraging, and daily tasks. Dogs help manage the flock, while workers help tend, cultivate, produce, and trade. The Shepherd also develops new skills that improve their mastery over the land.

—description from the publisher

Discover the beauty of the desert along meandering trails during the height of springtime blooms. Set out from the entrance to find plants and appreciate sweeping views across the landscape. Enjoy a peaceful walk and a few moments to pause and admire the wonder of nature.

Explore the desert by placing tiles and bench goal tokens. Find ways to earn points by arranging natural features, connecting walking trails, and setting up benches among the blooms to meet your bench objective.

Over the course of Blooms Across Desert, you expand the desert around the entrance by placing tiles, using tool cards, and fulfilling bench goals. Your bench objective card, bench goals, and walking trail will help guide your decisions. Once all available tiles have been played and / or all bench goals have been fulfilled, the game ends and you score based on your bench objective card, placed benches, rocks, leftover desert tiles, incomplete bench goal penalties, and disconnected trail penalties.

—description from the publisher

Nestled in a cozy corner of town is your bakery, where you pair handmade creations with friendly smiles. Your coworkers and you wake up early every morning to prepare baked breads destined to brighten someone’s day. Add a dash of your love to the ingredients as you create warm, delicious flavors that fill your customers with joy and delight!

Earn the most points by gathering ingredients to bake different types of breads to accomplish your chosen tasks. Gain bonuses from your baked breads and goods, along with completed daily specials, over the course of 5 rounds.

—description from the publisher

On a warm, sunny summer afternoon, the branches and flowers across a hillside meadow lazily sway in the gentle breeze. Suddenly, a gust sweeps through and lifts leaves and petals into the air, creating Windblooms. Capture the magic of the experience as you collect sprightly blooms floating by on a late summer day!

Players will play cards of different suits and values into each round’s breeze. Earn points by winning breezes and creating beautiful, flowing gusts. Rounds continue until any player(s) has collected 25+ victory points. Whoever earns the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

The goal is to complete various tasks under time pressure (using an hourglass) to collect raspberries. These tasks include building challenges, memory games, and concentration exercises, aiming to become the fastest food gatherer in the forest. It's a family-friendly game that promotes speed, concentration, and motor skills, and is also suitable for parties or birthdays.

In this abstract game players remove colored balls from the central board by a simple rule: all of the balls of a row or column should be pushed in one way till no one can move further. After each ball in the queue have found their place tge current player cab remove all items which have the same color next to themself. The game ends if no one can remove balls.

The player with the most collected balls win.

Drunagor has fallen. The Apocalypse consumed the land, leaving behind a scarred realm haunted by the corruption that nearly destroyed everything. But something stirs deep beneath the ruins. Hope. Defiance. A spark that refuses to die.

Chronicles of Drunagor: Aftermath is a cooperative dungeon crawler for 1–5 players and a new standalone entry into the Drunagor universe. Built on the foundations of CoD: Age of Darkness and its Apocalypse expansion, the game features engaging tactical combat driven by its signature action-cube system, deep character progression, and a branching narrative campaign full of meaningful choices. Shaped by nearly a decade of development and direct community feedback, Aftermath represents the most refined evolution of a system praised for its depth, tension, and replayability.

Aftermath unfolds through 12+ scenarios with branching paths that push the lore of Drunagor further than ever before. Explore forgotten cities, confront ancient forces awakened by the Apocalypse, and rebuild the strongholds of a broken world. Every scenario is designed with cleaner, updated mechanics and a sharper focus on storytelling, allowing you to decide whether Drunagor rebuilds, adapts, or crumbles under your influence.

—description from the publisher

Big Chain of Command 2 is a complete tool box for creating and playing larger games of Chain of Command, be that with more figures, more player or both.  
Big Chain of Command introduces some revolutionary new concepts to Chain of Command which allow larger games and the introduction of a higher level of command. 
The HQ now runs the battle using a system of HQ Orders. 
The HQ also has control over the reserves which can be fed into the action as required.  
The addition of operational Phase Lines allows the action to develop over a deeper battlefield.

All in all, the 51 page PDF of Big Chain of Command 2 allow you to expand your games of Chain of Command with ease to Company size actions and even multiple companies of a whole battalion if you are mad enough.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Hercules Hero Pack
description:
He is a man known by many names. Born Alcaeus, renamed Herakles, then—to distance himself from Hera—he was renamed again to Hercules. Now, his friends just call him Herc. This son of Zeus has had a tumultuous relationship with Olympus throughout his long life; sometimes he is their glory, other times their disgrace. But no matter what, there are few problems that he doesn’t first try to solve with his immense, godlike strength. Now, the mighty hero Hercules muscles into your games of Marvel Champions: The Card Game! With his immense strength and his ability to take on herculean tasks in exchange for mighty rewards, Hercules is a unique and powerful hero worthy of the fame his name brings. In this hero pack, you’ll find Hercules, his fifteen signature cards, his special side decks of gifts and labors, and a full assortment of Leadership cards inviting you to lead his fellow Avengers into battle.

Anyone looking to fight for their ideals will want to add the Hercules Hero Pack to their collection! This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving you the chance to start playing from the moment you open the box.

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

boardgame: Marvel Champions: The Card Game – Wonder Man Hero Pack
description:
Simon Williams, the former industrial rival of Tony Stark turned superpowered movie star, first got his powers by agreeing to undergo an experiment crafted by the Masters of Evil. After meeting and subsequently being defeated by the Avengers, he changed his ways and became a consistent, part-time member of the team. Now, the flashy hero Wonder Man blasts into your games of Marvel Champions: The Card Game! With his ionic physiology allowing him to manipulate energy in incredible ways, Wonder Man is a unique hero and a fantastic addition to any team. In this hero pack, you will find Wonder Man, his sixteen signature cards, and an assortment of Justice cards—with a mix of other aspects—inviting you to thwart the enemy’s schemes.

Anyone looking to fight for their ideals will want to add the Wonder Man Hero Pack to their collection! This expansion comes with a 40-card pre-built deck, giving you the chance to start playing from the moment you open the box.

—description from the publisher
family: Collectible: Living Card Game (Fantasy Flight Games)

description:
In Lemonade Wars, you play as neighborhood kids competing to be the most successful lemonade magnate. In the process, you’ll employ questionable business tactics, throw tantrums, and involve the adults when necessary.

Each player starts with some parental seed money, then they are off to the streets to peddle some sweet, sweet lemonade. Players receive victory points by earning titles and bragging rights via their accomplishments and sales during the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dossiers Criminels Pocket: Qui a tué Lord Ravary II ?
description:
NEW CONCEPT: No rules to read, just open and investigate!
 The ideal game to embark on your first investigation or continue challenging yourself.

PLAY AS A DETECTIVE: Lord Ravary II has died before the most important horse race of the season. 
Who benefits from the crime? 
Determine the cause of his death and who is responsible!

CREATE YOUR OWN INVESTIGATION BOARD: Sort, match, and assemble more than 40 pieces of evidence at your disposal to solve the mystery.

     Surveillance footage
     Maps
     Witness statements
     Photos
     Letters
     and much more...


A FUN, NON-DIGITAL GAME: Observe, cooperate, and discuss with each other to successfully complete the investigation. 
Also playable solo.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A volatile horse-racing card game. Place bets, sway race outcomes and meddle with your opponents to see who comes out in front.

Horse races in the future are built DIFFERENT. Horses are super-charged on various forms of energy and you’re more than just an ordinary gambler. You’re playing with cards up your sleeve that let you control the race, but so do your opponents! Players take turns rolling the dice and playing cards. The horses will move ahead on the track and players will rack up bets for - and against - different horses. Once all horses move past a card on the track, that card will be played and the track will shorten ensuring nail-biting finishes every time. As soon as a horse crosses the finish line, players will tally up their bets that hit and subtract their losses. Whoever has the most points after 3 races is the champion!

-description from designer

Evilution is a solo, dice-rolling game of mathematical thinking.  You play as an unseen force striving to facilitate the successful evolution of a woman recently injected with a scientifically modified alien organism.

Strategically apply addition, subtraction, multiplication and division to the values of your rolled dice to connect DNA Strands while also blocking Mutagen Cells.

Fail, and the mutagens will instead run rampant, causing the host to burst and release dozens of lower, seed carrying life-forms...

—description from the publisher

Thirty Years of Misery is an easy to play game on the Thirty Years War of 1618-1648 for up to 4 players. 
Players on the same side must coordinate and work together to win the war, but the victory conditions only allow a single faction within the victorious alliance to win the game.

Carddriven mechanics with lots of events, random chit pull based turn order and combat resolution using custom dice ensures a strong narrative and lots of thematic unpredictability.

As befitting a bitter religious war, players are striving not only to secure military control over the various political entities of Germany but also to enforce their religious policies in them.

—description from the publisher

Deep within the land of Hallbrack lies an element of tremendous power, called Ferment Crystal. This rare and stunning substance has long been a source of wonder, capable of performing miracles in the right hands. Yet, when consumed in excess, it turns you volatile, unpredictable… and deadly.

For years, the ruthless tyrant Tronad has scoured other lands in search of this elusive crystal. Obsessed with this power, he has left nothing but ashes and sorrow in his wake, seizing every fragment he could find. Yet, none large enough to satisfy his desire. Now, whispers speak of a massive crystal, hidden somewhere within Hallbrack’s uncharted depths.

This crystal must be found before Tronad claims it and brings ruin to the realm. Players work together to explore diverse locations, uncover clues to the crystal's whereabouts, and track Tronad's movements. As the timeline steadily counts down, heroes must gather new gear, make smart choices, and race against time! Fail this mission, and Hallbrack may be lost forever.

DESCRIPTION: How to play

Goal: The game is won if you manage to find the large ferment crystal, before Tronad - and hide it well. Then fight him.

Choose one hero from the 9 available characters.

You begin your journey by building paths on the large map. The direction of these paths is shaped by your actions and may change as the adventure unfolds. The type of map tile you can place is determined by luck, as tiles are drawn at random from a mixed bag.

You move together along these paths using two standard D6 dice.

The large map represents the land of Hallbrack and reveals all locations you may enter as the story progresses. All discovered locations can be explored to uncover clues, find items, and encounter enemies. Your choices determine the order in which locations appear, and some may be visited more often than others.

Inside each location, players take turns moving independently with their hero by rolling that character’s designated dice. Some heroes move more easily than others, affecting movement options each turn and making hero selection an important early decision.

Item icons within locations indicate which item cards you may view. These cards can provide gear, but more often drive the story forward.

Both inside and outside locations, the Fightboard and Pointboard are used on many occasions.

Your main goal is to find the large ferment crystal and defeat Tronad, though many optional activities can aid you on your adventure.

A funny family game where you roll dice with letters on them and write down the funniest sentence you can think of in 30 seconds, using different letter for each word in the sentence.
You don't have to make a logical sentence, only grametically correct one!
You get points according to the length of sentence you manage to come up with each round.

boardgame: War of the Ring: The Card Game – Hope and Despair
description:
The heart of Middle-earth beats within its lore, its moods, its ethos. Tolkien's words immerse the readers into his wondrous creation, weaving beautiful and powerful stories.

Hope and Despair is an expansion for War of the Ring: The Card Game that adds a new type of card: the story card. Inspired by key events and turning points of The Lord of the Rings, story cards represent natural and supernatural influences on the events of Middle-earth, providing a powerful way to modify your playing decks.

This expansion includes 54 story cards, 40 story tokens, and rules for adding story cards to your game, both in multi-player and solo scenarios.

Hope and Despair can be used in conjunction with Fire and Swords, or just with the base game alone. A set of Shadow "bot" cards are provided for use with Against the Shadow to play solo or co-operatively.
family: Series: War of the Ring (Nexus/Ares Games)

description:
Kettō is a quick-fire card game of snap decisions, hand management and pushing advantage.

Master the way of the warrior and strike when your enemy least expects. Keep your calm, focus your blade and claim victory.

Two players face off, drawing from a shared deck and using their cards to engage in battle. Winning a clash requires preparation, observation, and an understanding of your opponent’s rhythm. A clash may be swift and brutal or prolonged and elegant, with outcomes that vary depending on the cards played.

The aim of the game is to reduce your opponent’s life cards to zero. Offensive cards such as Slash, Thrust, and Strike drive relentless attacks and help secure victory. Defensive play is equally important and cards like Feint, Parry, and Retreat allow you to rebuild your defences and prepare a counterattack.

At its best, Kettō is a deadly dance, with both players acting in harmony. Though in the end, only one can emerge victorious, and the outcome depends entirely on your mastery of the cards.

—description from the publiser

Unleash the shadowy Dark Gnomes in your VÁLKA battles! Small but deadly, these gnomes bring fierce warriors, shadow priests, and dark magic to the battlefield.

What's Inside:

     7 Fighter Cards: 5 warriors, 1 Legendary Warrior, and 1 Familiar.
     Event Cards: Game-changing effects like Magic Bag, Mercy!, Inspired Strike, and more.
     Buffs & Relics: Wield powerful items like Rapier of Pain (+2 damage) and Brïshtal’s Cursed Fang (heal to full, but take double damage).


Command the shadows and add a new layer of strategy to your VÁLKA games!

Doot-doot 6-7. It's now a Goal Promo Card you can use in Math Fluxx! You may have heard this nonsensical phrase a time or two in 2025 which is why it became the Dictionary.com Word of the Year.  We don't get it either... but every kid is saying it. The meaning is quite ambiguous, despite 67 being the 19th prime number and also a lucky prime. Still, with all that going for it, why not add this silly and playful phrase as a Goal to Math Fluxx? Yeah, we couldn't think of a reason not to either. Six-Seeeeven!

-description from publisher's website

Quartermaster General: West Front is the third entry in the Quartermaster General two-player line. It is played over 23 seasonal game rounds, from Fall 1939 through Spring 1945, with one player controlling the Allies' forces — United Kingdom, United States, and France — and the other playing the Axis: Germany, Italy, and other associated countries (the "Pact", so called after the Tripartite Pact).

West Front also includes components and rules to create massive, combined games with Quartermaster General: East Front and South Front.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Vilnius, a city of alchemy and magic, where steam-powered carriages rush through the streets, automatons serve in taverns, and magnificent airships fly over the Cathedral. However, the Russian Empire is not prepared to accept the changed political situation: its agents are stirring up chaos in the free cities. They are not alone – pirates disturb the peace in the skies, and one of the most mysterious organizations – the Revivers – is building an unstoppable war machine, the Iron Wolf, in the catacombs of Vilnius.

Playing Vilko Valanda' ("The Hour of the Wolf"), you become powerful Personas in Andrius Tapinas' world of Stone and Steam cities: wizards, secret agents, politicians, guardians of Vilnius. Your goal: to gather as many influence points as possible by unraveling intrigues, recruiting agents, and accumulating weapons.

boardgame: Stars & Lasers!!: Where The Wild Things Are...
description:
Stars & Lasers - Where The Wild Things Are...

Something is stirring in a distant sector of space, the galaxy whispers of an ancient power awakening. Until now, no one knew of their existence.

The Hive Empire—an ancient and relentless insectoid race, far older than humanity—was first discovered when their strange, alien ships encountered a human fleet in a far distant sector on the outskirts of known space.

Known by humanity as the Hive 'Infestation,' these chitin-armoured beings have roamed the stars for millennia, their hive mind guiding their every action, the Hivers are emerging from obscurity.

The Hive Empire brings unique mechanics, tactics, and challenges to the tabletop, forcing players to adapt or perish against their brutal efficiency.

The question is... will humanity stand united against this alien threat, or will the Hive Empire consume all in their path?

This new supplement covers a new alien race for Stars & Lasers and all the rules for their ships and their weapons.

Note: this is not a stand-alone game, you will need a copy of Stars & Lasers to use it fully.

Hotzone's core game is a 1v0 or 1v1 board builder where a defending team is protecting a building from an attacking team trying to rob it. Defending teams consist of a single Security Chief and a hand full of Response cards granting them increased influence on the board, and Attacking teams consist of three individual Operatives working together, controlled by one person.

Setup is cooperative, with each team taking turns placing a tile to make a 4x4 map grid. Defenders place tiles face down to hide their contents while Attackers place them face up. During gameplay, the Security Chief can move swiftly over un-flipped tiles, while the Attackers flip the tiles over as they move to reveal their contents. Both Defense and Attack teams select their Operatives from a deck of characters, equip them in a draft format from a deck of equipment, and augment them from a deck of Masterminds serving as the "leader" of the team. Attacker teams are all kept hidden until the defender discovers them during the game.

Gameplay itself is a back and forth, with each character activating, moving, and responding to things they encounter. Attacking teams infiltrate through stairs, windows, or an Airlock tile, and spread out over the board in search of Bounty Caches which hold the Bounty credits they need to live. The Security Chief tries to stop them, either by using guards they assigned to tiles earlier, their own abilities, or the Response cards they have.

Once the Attacking team feels they've collected enough they extract through similar extraction points. Gameplay becomes a careful balance between scouting fast, staying safe, getting out quick, but maintaining enough board presence to collect what you need to win. When all the attackers are either dead, collected, or escape, points are tallied based on who survived, who was captured, and what was taken. Highest point value wins!

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Last Spell: The Board Game – Carnage Unleashed Expansion
description:
Carnage Unleashed intensifies the challenge by empowering monsters with elite cards, creating complex gameplay dynamics. As you confront tactical scenarios, keep track of the auras of powerful elites and strategically scavenge the bone piles left behind. Unleash your heroes, seize the rewards, and navigate the perilous nights in this epic battle for survival!

-description from Gamefound campaign

boardgame: The Last Spell: The Board Game – Lt. Lamasse
description:
Lt. Lamasse is an additional Hero miniature that brings versatility and variability to the game. As an alternative character option, it offers a fresh experience in character selection providing unique starting stats.

-description from Gamefound campaign

boardgame: The Last Spell: The Board Game – The Twins Expansion
description:
The Twins expansion introduces two powerful Goddesses who appear early in the game, adding a new gameplay scenario. With four progressively difficult phases, they leave behind dangerous modifications and employ dirty tricks like stunning Heroes and depleting mana. Prepare for a true tactical and endurance challenge that impacts gameplay experience!

-description from Gamefound campaign

Redirection is a probability-based engine-building game played on a shared game board.  Roll the dice to select a board cell.  Use money to build tiles, which benefit you no matter who rolls them.  Use white dice to change your rolls and hit stars to gain victory points and win.

—description from the designer

Onward: Legendary Expansion with 1 alternate outsider (standee), 5 legendary monsters (standees) and 16 legendary items.
These new monsters grant you an epic item upon defeat.

Get 8 more heroes, one from each class, from the Taulot faction in the Onward: Taulot Expansion.
You can freely mix factions but this one is known for disruption, pushing lanes, and dealing an annoying amount of Disarm…

Get 8 more heroes, one from each class, from the Nupten faction in the Onward: Nupten Expansion.
You can freely mix factions but this one is known for speed, creating illusions, and dealing an annoying amount of Silence…

Astra Titanus Deluxe is a solitaire game of space ship fleet battles against a titanic alien AI controlled death machine. You command fleets of warships against a lone Titan or two. The Titan is controlled by a deck of AI cards that drives the Titan unerringly towards its objectives. Defeat the Titan before the objectives are destroyed to achieve victory.

What's New in Astra Titanus Deluxe:

All components are upgraded - including a stunning sturdy full color box, hard mounted game board, large thick counters with rounded corners, high quality poker size cards, counter tray, and more!
Astra Titanus Deluxe can be played by two or more players cooperatively against the Titan or with one player taking control of the Titan.
Astra Titanus Deluxe comes with 12 scenarios as well as a random scenario generator.

-description from publisher

Dropzone Commander: Operation Artemis is a game of fast combat in the far future. Speed is key as brave PHR pilots attempt to capture or destroy Bioficer beacons whilst fending off waves of mighty Megafauna.

Seize control of the battlefield by eliminating your foes with devastating attacks or breaking them down with constant waves of damage over the game's 6 rounds. Predicting your opponent's next move will be critical to victory, whilst powerful command cards could turn the battle on its head.

This box contains everything that two players will need to start playing games of Dropzone Commander: Operation Artemis. Each box contains opposing forces of PHR and Megafauna with a variety of miniatures for both.

As combat intensifies, the Noise and Threat go up. Many actions will increase the Noise, which will in turn increase the Threat. The higher the Threat Level, the more aggressive the fauna will be. PHR Commanders will have to choose wisely when to go loud during combat and when discretion is needed.

A 20-page rulebook contains all the rules required to jump straight into the action and includes 5 unique scenarios. Also included are dice, noise meter, command cards, and fastplay cards to help you get stuck in quickly.

This set includes:

Plastic PHR & Megafauna: These multi-part hard plastic miniatures come on sprues for ease of construction. With easy-to-assemble pieces, you’ll have your forces ready in no time at all.

Mat & Buildings: Sturdy and easy to assemble, this box set includes 6 sturdy buildings with 3 different styles available. Also included is the 3x3 gaming mat, which quickly folds away for easy storage between games.

20-Page Rulebook, Dice, Noise meter & 54 Command Cards,and Fastplay Cards:
Fastplay Cards for all units in the box help you to get playing, whilst both factions have unique command card decks which unlocks a variety game-altering effects.

-- description from publisher website

boardgame: Battle of New Market: Field of Lost Shoes May 15, 1864
description:
Battle of New Market is a low complexity game of hex and counters. The same system Battle of Bentonville: March 19th-21st. Playing time is 1-3 hours. The counters are single sided and the CRT does not have a lot of ELM and no Exchanges. The players need to maneuver their units to cut off paths of retreat.

In the Spring of 1864 commander of all Union forces, Lt. General Ulysses S. Grant, designed a strategy to attack the Confederacy on all fronts. This included a surge up the Shenandoah Valley in Virginia. Assigned to this task was Major General Franz Sigel. Although skeptical of Sigel’s prowess as a general, Grant was hopeful that Sigel could advance quickly enough to capture Staunton and threaten the flank of Robert E. Lee’s Army of Northern Virginia opposing Grant in the central part of the state.

Opposing Sigel was Confederate Major General John C. Breckenridge. Breckenridge a former Vice President of the United States put together a rag tag force of veteran soldiers and students from the Virginia Military Institute (VMI). On May 15, 1864, in a driving rainstorm the two sides clashed at the Town of New Market. The battle was long and furious. A hole in the Confederate line compelled General Breckenridge to “Put the Boys in,” referencing the VMI cadets whom he had held in reserve. Using a fence line for cover, the veteran Confederate troops and VMI cadets traded unyielding fire with Sigel’s forces occupying Bushong’s Hill. With the rain falling hard and, in the smoke, and chaos of battle the entire Confederate line surged forward. As they ran across Bushong’s farm field many of their shoes were pulled off their feet by the thick Virginia mud. The union artillery was captured, including a gun by the cadets themselves. The field they charged across would forever be known as, “The Field of Lost Shoes.” Sigel’s forces were soundly defeated and forced to retreat. This would mark the only time in American history that a school’s student body would participate in an active battle.

The game system is very similar to the Battle of Bentonville hex a counter game with playability similar to SPI style with a bit more detail.

—description from the publisher

Open the doors to game night fun with Disney Doorables Unlocked. This imaginative board game adds a new way for Doorables fans to peek behind every door – where surprises are in store! The game pieces are exclusive Doorables figures: Disney Mickey Mouse, Stitch, Jack Skellington, and a mystery figure. Each is designed with Doorables’ signature style, glittery eyes, and a special iridescent finish. The 4-piece interlocking game board panels assemble to create a 2-story house with 8 rooms to explore. At each turn, players amass gems, play Action cards, and collect keys to unlock (and flip over) Door cards. On the back of the card, find a Doorables character to add to a party of pals. The more Doorables characters, the more advantages the player earns. Try both Classic and Strategic versions of the game for challenging play at every skill level.

Includes: 4 Doorables figures, 4 game board panels, 68 Door cards, 4 Starting Color cards, 40 Action cards, 60 keys, 25 gems, 2 blank dice, sticker sheet.

Secret Role Cards - Detective or Killer (5 for Detectives, 1 for Killer)

Weapon Cards - Various weapons that the killer might've used (5 cards)

Person card - the person killed. (3 cards)

You are the killer! - If the killer is found, play this card (1 card)

How the person was killed - put how the person was killed. (5 cards)

HOW TO PLAY

Before starting the game, place the secret role cards in envelopes, randomly mixed. Start the game by you giving the envelopes. Do NOT share your roles with anyone. Place the other cards separately in the table face down. Start the game by giving envelopes to each player. The killer can pick a player to betray the detectives by looking at them shortly. Then everyone closes their eyes, and the killer and the betrayer opening their eyes, then the players picking a player they suspect is killer. They play 2 weapon cards from the deck. Then players look at the "how person was killed" cards and by a vote, they pick 2 cards. Then the players nominate a person of interest. That person of interest, if the actual killer, must choose to admit, testify or lie. If he chooses to testify, then says another player is a killer, they can start a vote if it is true. The player that was said to be the killer can lie or admit it. If the suspected killer admits it (they need to say if the description in the "how the person was killed card") and the weapon card if it is right, the detectives win. The detectives get 5 points each for every right thing in the investigation.

Idea by Simone Pinto / Rules written by Aeross INIGO T. Manuel.

-description from designer

Shuffle the deck and give one random card each. When they are ready, they take cards immediately until the deck runs out. then they make sandwiches out of it.

You can get points by using this:

Every sandwich made.

1PT

Grilled cheese (Burnt toast and cheese) -2PTS

PB&J SANDWICH (Jam and Peanut Butter card or the PB&J Card) - 2PTS

Whoever has the most sandwiches and has a high point list wins.
-description from designer

boardgame: Super Giant Monster Showdown: Kaiju Attack!
description:
The classic tabletop game of giant monster action is reimagined and renovated with an all new robust kaiju creation system and gameplay options. Kaiju Attack is the core set (1st Volume) in the all new Super Giant Monster Showdown series of expanding sets.

This base game includes enough Bio Cards, Physical Modifier Cards, Power Cards and Powersource Cards for players to use to generate their own custom behemoths! Map Cards are used to create the battlefield, and Events Cards spice things up!

Players' kaiju can engage in wanton property destruction, engage in tactical maneuvers and battle with an endless variety of abilities.

Like its predecessor, the game can be played in a freeform mode, encouraging story beats and epic moments. A new Dynamic Scoring is also included for more point-based play.

—description from the designer

Oh My Word! is a very simple word-making game.  Every player is dealt a hand of vowel and consonant cards. They make words with the cards in their hand and score them. First player to 100 points wins.

- Every time a player makes a word, it becomes the active word and the previous word is discarded. You are awarded bonus points for making longer words than the active word and also penalized for making shorter words. 
- At the end of your turn you draw two cards (from the vowel and consonant piles).
- Every player starts the game with 3 power cards which they can use at any point in the game, but only once and by themselves.

-description from designer

boardgame: トゥルーマリンショーミニ拡張2025 (True Marine Show Mini Expansion 2025)
description:
This is the 2025 Expansion for True Marine Show, including 2 Red Cards, 5 Green Cards, and 2 Yellow Cards (including a Minato Aqua errata card).


    This cannot be played on its own. At least one of True Marine Show 1-3, X, or R is required.


New vTubers from Nijisanji and hololive Overseas have entered the fray! These powerful new effects are sure to bring more excitement to your matches. The idea behind these cards are unprecedented meta-effects that can reshape the competitive landscape. It's guaranteed to be a small, yet spicy addition to your games!
artists: Karakara shin (空々心) (からからしん)

description:
The British Mandate got drawn up by the great colonial empires, when ordinary people caught between them had to make impossible calculations about their futures. Most failed, but some prevailed. This is their story—and yours to set right.

Mandate is a solo-native, 3-player game covering 1914 through 1950, when incompatible visions for the same land collided repeatedly. You control one of three asymmetric sides: Allied factions pursuing Zionist ambitions, Axis factions representing Ottoman and Arab interests, or Proxy factions wielding colonial British, French, and American influence. History resists permanent teams—and Mandate rewards players who understand why.

Two resources drive every decision: Funds let you act, while Influentials measure your position. But Influentials can be spent to survive the current crisis—converting civilians into militias, blocking an opponent's critical advance. Every expenditure closes doors. Every gamble you've been building toward either pays off spectacularly or collapses because you had to burn it for survival. Event cards representing actual historical events serve as campaign turns, with low-severity situations escalating if you lose ground on key issues. New factions and leaders emerge across twelve Events; others fall into exile.

Victory flows through eighteen Political Issues, drawing inspiration from GMT's Churchill and Dune: Imperium. Advance issues for bonus Influentials, Table them to remove distractions, or Challenge opponents at a cost. But Wedge Issues—real intrigues that operated in the background—can't be resolved. They drain resources, handing victory to whomever survives despite their impacts. Sometimes the distractions shaped history as much as the main events.

Why "Mandate"? When the League of Nations handed Britain this region in 1920, this geopolitical framework tried to satisfy three incompatible promises yet satisfied none of them. Mandate's campaign features over 100 leaders—from military figures to future heads of state—across 64 events spanning 32 difficult years. At its core, eighteen separate issues have to be resolved across three sides, forcing difficult priorities and demanding intelligent strategies. Looming Wedge Issues and Intrigues stymie progress for everyone—just as they did in the interwar period and beyond. It's up to you to turn Britain's colonial past into thriving modern nation-states. Can you do it? Or will you fall asunder?

—description from the designer

Fox Flip is a quick, easy-to-learn 2-player game of skill, luck, and timely flips. Play timely fox cards to outfox your opponent and win piles of cards!

To start, deal 7 cards to each player. The player opposite the dealer goes first. Alternate turns playing cards of equal or greater value, or by following the Fox card rules.

At some point, someone won't be able to play. When that happens, you have one chance to stay in the game by flipping the top card of the deck onto the play pile. Flipping a card acts as your turn. If the flipped card satisfies the current rule in play, you survived and normal play resumes starting with the other player. If the flipped card doesn't satisfy the current rule in play, the other player wins the pile.

Once a pile is won, alternate turns drawing 1 card per turn up to a maximum of 7 cards. Once a player has 7 cards, they must play a card on their next turn, which restarts the play phase.

Continue playing until the deck is empty and all cards have been won.

When all cards have been won, count your cards. 1 card = 1 point. The first player to 100 points wins. A typical game of Fox Flip takes 3-4 rounds.

—description from the designer

Challenge your wits and reflexes in this thrilling word-making game! Players race to play out their hand by combining them and forming words. Using your cards and quick thinking, race to form valid words before your opponents. Use Red vowel cards strategically to unlock endless possibilities and outplay your rivals.

Gameplay:
Players race to play out their hand by combining them and forming words.
The last played card must be used to form the next word. Players may also use the additional vowel cards that are always open and available on the table in forming their word.
The player who plays out their hand first wins the game!

Whether you’re playing with friends or family, ONE WORD guarantees endless fun, laughter, and moments of triumph!

-description from publisher

See it, do it, say it – and make it snappy!

In Snaps!, Players take turns drawing and revealing cards from the deck until there are matching colored cards, either between players or a match in one player’s own card pile. When a match happens, the fastest to name a thing in the category starting with the letter on the card will win points. But that’s not all – you will also have to be quick on your feet as you have to strike a pose on the card as you blurt out your answer! At the end of the game, the player with the most points wins!

It will take brains AND brawn to be the champion, do you have the quick tongue and fancy feet to do it?
Get ready for the hilarity that ensues – you’ll never guess what you have to say or do next!

—description from the publisher

The surface world, once a vibrant cradle of civilization, has become a relentless, uninhabitable wasteland. Decades of unmitigated global warming have culminated in a terrifying cascade of ecological disasters. Escalating severe weather—hypercanes, perpetual dust storms, and flash-flooding of biblical proportions—has made life above ground a fragile, momentary existence. With long-distance, interstellar space travel still a distant, theoretical possibility, humanity has been forced to confront the only viable solution for survival: an ambitious, monumental endeavor to burrow deep beneath the Earth's surface.

The entire colossal undertaking is orchestrated and overseen by a singular, monolithic global entity known simply as The Corp. This organization holds absolute authority, acting as the ultimate governing body for the next era of human civilization.

The Corp has chosen four highly-skilled leaders for the immediate mission to explore, excavate, and rapidly establish new, self-sufficient underground societies. It is the ultimate trial. The victor will not be determined by size or speed alone, but by the metric The Corp values most: Reputation.

The prize is not merely the greatest distinction; it is the apex of power and influence in the new era. The corporation that successfully earns the highest reputation will inherit the Earth's future. The stakes could not be higher: the survival and legacy of humanity rest on the success of this underground trial.

Game Type: Worker placement and engine-building game for 2-4 players, ages 14 & up.

Setting: Near-future Earth, deep beneath the surface.

The Premise:
In a bid to safeguard humanity from the harsh surface environment, specialized corporations, controlled by the players, are utilizing advanced technology to drill deep into the Earth's core.

Your task as a Corporate Leader is to establish and manage a thriving subterranean society.
Dig underground, find suitable terrain to build up your ideal society based on your strengths & unique abilities. Players must secure the favor of The Corp by diligently completing assigned tasks and delivering necessary goods and resources. The Corp's valuable perspective is crucial for the society's success.

Failure to meet The Corp's demands will result in irreparable damage to the player's reputation.

Core Gameplay & Objectives:
Corporate Specialization: Each corporation possesses unique expertise in certain environments, as well as unique abilities. Partial collaboration is essential; you may need to hire the specialized skills of rival teams to successfully build some buildings.

Resource Management: You must efficiently manage your resources to fund your workforce, keep them fed, and maintain high morale to build the most successful society.
Executing Orders: Each turn, The Corp issues a set of orders for you to select from, often accompanied by small bonuses. Success depends on your ability to strategically prioritize these tasks within your managing console.

The Corp Relationship: Occasionally, you'll need to "send gifts" to The Corp. Some call them bribes, but we prefer to view it as a demonstration of your exemplary leadership, designed to gain extra resources or boost your reputation.

Hidden Agenda & Victory: The Corp has given each team some secret, specific objectives to complete. In the end, only one corporation will emerge victorious, earning the exclusive rights to all future subterranean development. Will your leadership prevail?

-description from designer

Red Storm: Southern Flank includes two full expansion modules — Red Storm: Bavaria covering southern Germany and Red Storm: Adriatic covering northern Italy and Yugoslavia. These two modules greatly expand the original Red Storm game, providing new air forces, with unique aircraft types and variants, and adding aircraft to air forces already depicted in the original game.

Players deploy their forces on new maps across 50+ scenarios, including solo and campaign scenarios, confronted by new ground and naval forces — all based on the same detailed operational air warfare rules pioneered by Lee Brimmicombe-Wood’s acclaimed Downtown game system.

The Red Storm: Bavaria expansion adds the air forces of France, Czechoslovakia, Hungary, and Italy to the game system. Featuring a two-map play area encompassing southern Germany and parts of western Czechoslovakia and Austria, Red Storm: Bavaria includes 22 new core scenarios, one new campaign scenario, and four new solo scenarios. From air superiority sweeps, close air support missions, deep strike raids, helicopter operations, airborne drops, and nuclear weapon attacks, this expansion delivers true “meat and potatoes” Red Storm action, with the new combatants and forces fighting over the rugged Bavarian battlefield.

The Red Storm: Adriatic expansion takes the air war to a unique and rarely simulated theater. While the main combatants are the air forces of Italy, the United States, and the Soviet Union, the expansion also adds the air forces of Spain and Yugoslavia to the Red Storm game system. As a mixed air and maritime theater, the expansion provides all the rules and counters needed for scenarios involving NATO and Yugoslav naval forces operating in the northern Adriatic, including the naval air elements of the Italian, French, Yugoslavian, and Spanish navies. Red Storm: Adriatic provides another 22 core scenarios, a campaign scenario, and four solo scenarios covering the operations in this area, including air superiority sweeps, long-range strikes, airborne operations, convoy raids, amphibious assault support, close air support missions, and more.

Important: Play of the expansions requires ownership of the original Red Storm: The Air War over Central Germany, 1987 game. All other rules, counters, charts, and materials needed for play are provided, including the full set of naval rules and counters first introduced in Red Storm: Baltic Approaches. In total, Red Storm: Southern Flank adds 54 new scenarios, four maps, 560 new counters, and many new aircraft and data charts to further explore a potential air war in 1987 between NATO and the Warsaw Pact.

Components:
    Two 34” x 22” Maps, printed front (RS: Bavaria) and back (RS: Adriatic)
    Two Countersheets
    Two 8.5” x 11” Small Cards
    Four 11” x 17” Large Cards
    Four Booklets
    One 8.5” x 11” Naval Log Sheet Pad
    Two 10-sided Dice
    One 6-sided Die
    One 2" Box

-description from publisher's website

Loot the World is a tile ownership, connect-the-board, & push-your-luck strategy game for 3 to 4 players. Take control of Victorian era trading companies in a rabbid race to convert land into cold hard cash over a series of rounds where each player takes their turn using a Progressive Turn Order mechanism.

There are two ways to win the game: either be the first player to connect opposite cardinal ends (i.e. North to South or East to West) with a continuous chain of owned tiles, or be the first to amass 40 or more gold bars in wealth.

To achieve victory, players will purchase tiles, upgrade those tiles with buildings, and or expand their territory by capturing other players' tiles with an army. Of course, players may also choose to work with each other, until such a time where it may be beneficial to break that partnership in order to win.

Arkontia is a medium-sized Euro game. Strategy and resource management game.
