The board shows a world divided into regions with natural resources that are connected to each other. Players choose a capital city from among their territories and, at the beginning of the turn, obtain the resources from the territories connected to the capital. 
They then use these natural resources to develop their empire or expand their army, enabling them to conquer other territories by rolling dice.
 The OBJECTIVE is to obtain 10 victory points, which are earned by achieving different milestones that are dynamically added or subtracted from the player who obtains them. 
The ways to earn points are: conquering continents, developing cities, obtaining the longest trade route, obtaining the longest sea route, the largest army, and the greatest number of strategic enclaves.

Resource trading is done with the other players and the bank, and it is important not only to control your strategic depth but also to negotiate.

There are three phases in each turn:

     First, we obtain the resources for the turn and decide how to use them (buying cards, ports, cities, or armies).
     Second, we attack with our armies to conquer nearby territories (by rolling dice).
     Third, we mobilize troops to advance our strategy or protect remote territories.


The game is very easy to understand but at the same time achieves a deep complexity.

—description from the designer

Arkontia es un Euro-medio. Juego de estrategia y control de recursos.

En el tablero se presenta un mundo divido en regiones con recursos naturales y conectados entre si. Los jugadores eligen una capital entre sus territorios y obtienen al inicio del turno, los recursos de los territorios conectados a la capital. 
Posteriormente emplean esos recursos naturales para desarrollar su imperio o ampliar su ejército, pudiendo con ellos conquistar otros territorios mediante lanzamiento de dados.
 El OBJETIVO es conseguir 10 puntos de victoria que obtendremos consiguiendo diferentes hitos que de manera dinámica se van sumando o restando al jugador que los obtenga. 
Las formas de obtener puntos son: conquistando continentes, desarrollando ciudades, obteniendo la ruta comercial más larga, obteniendo la ruta marítima más larga, el mayor ejército y el mayor número de enclaves estratégico.

El comercio de recursos se hace con el resto de jugadores y con la banca siendo importante, no solo controlar su profundidad estratégica si no también la negociación.

En el turno tendríamos tres fases: 

     Primero obtenemos los recursos del turno, y decidimos como emplearlos (comprando cartas, puertos, ciudades o ejércitos)
     Segundo atacamos con nuestros ejércitos para conquistar territorios cercanos (mediante tirada de dados)
     Tercero, movilización de tropas para avanzar nuestra estrategia o proteger territorios remotos.


El juego es muy sencillo de entender pero a la vez consigue una profunda complejidad estratégica, siendo cada partida un reto.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Explore more of the magical landscapes of the southern England.

Just a stone’s throw from Dartmoor lies the equally impressive Exmoor National Park, where heather and grass moors meet coastal cliffs and mystical, man-made traces.

With Forest Shuffle – Dartmoor: Exmoor, the second standalone game in the Forest Shuffle universe receives its first official expansion. The 55 beautifully illustrated cards celebrate the unique flora and fauna of Exmoor National Park in Devon, located further north from Dartmoor.

The new species intergrade seamlessly into the base game, and some of them introduce a brand-new effect. Instead of letting you to play cards from your hand for free, as usual, they allow you to rummage through the clearing. If there is nothing suitable there, you can test your luck by revealing a certain number of cards from the draw pile. In addition, five new asymmetrical caves also provide variety.

Like all Forest Shuffle games, Forest Shuffle – Dartmoor: Exmoor is part of the Lookout GreenLine, 100% eco-friendly manufactured.

Immerse yourself in the mystical landscapes of the Exmoor National Park!

—description from the publisher

Each astronaut must reach the final lunar base, gradually moving on to other space locations. Two types of dice are used depending on the position on the board. Along the way, you can intercept and capture UFOs, or they can bounce you back to the previous space station. The game ends when ALL astronauts have reached the lunar base. The player with the most captured UFOs wins (you continue to capture them even after reaching the lunar base).

QYRSQYRS (Kirskirs) is a fast-paced, tactical trick-taking card game for three to four players, focused on bold decisions, risk management, and reading your opponents. Each player plays individually, all against all. The goal is to score as few points as possible, as all card values collected in tricks count as penalty points at the end of the round.

The game consists of 32 cards, featuring number cards from 2 to 8 in multiple suits, as well as four Queen cards, which act as permanent trump cards.

In a four-player game, each player receives eight cards; in a three-player game, each player receives ten cards, and the dealer places two cards face down into the Treasure Chest. Additionally, each player secretly places one card face down into the Treasure Chest. These cards remain hidden until the end of the round.

The Treasure Chest is the central mechanic of the game. Before play begins, players must make an irreversible hidden decision by contributing a card to the chest, creating tension and unpredictability from the very start. The dual role of the Queen cards as powerful trumps—either held in hand or hidden in the chest—can decisively influence the outcome of the round.

The player to the left of the dealer leads the first trick by playing any card face up. All other players must follow suit if possible or play a trump card. If a player cannot follow suit or trump, they may discard any card. Trump cards beat all other suits. The winner of a trick leads the next one.

The winner of the final trick also collects all cards from the Treasure Chest.

Special Achievements:
Flawless: A player wins no tricks. All opponents double their penalty points.
Grandiose: A player wins all tricks. All opponents receive 500 penalty points.

The game emphasizes timing, bluffing, and anticipating opponents’ decisions. Players constantly react to played cards, adjust their strategies, and decide when to intervene or hold back. The rules are easy to learn, yet each round offers meaningful tactical choices.
The game ends after the final trick is resolved and the Treasure Chest is claimed. Players then count the values of all cards they have won. The player with the fewest points is the winner.

-description from designer

Once upon a time, there was a veeery tired wizard...

In Adventurers Required, players can land their dream job: Who hasn't always wanted to be HR manager of a guild? Assemble a team of sneaky adventurers, tiptoe into the wizard's tower, and snatch his treasures. But beware! Alongside their skills, the team’s noise rating is cruicial. If things get too loud, they’ll wake the wizard. And that would be very bad...

At the start of each round, players have to hire one new team member. And yes, it will be tricky to choose from the recruits: A card from your hand, one from the market or will a lucky draw hire the perfect team member from the draw pile?

From bard to druid, every adventurer comes with their strengths - and also a certain noise level! But even the loudest can be silent as a shadow with the right partner. Once your dream team is ready, let the wizard snore on… send them in and leave with the biggest haul – and victory! After only five rounds, the player with the most treasure wins.

Adventurers required is a beginner friendly card game, plays super quick and will make you crave: Again, please!

On top, it's manufactured eco-friendly as part of Lookouts GreenLine.

-description from publisher

Baby Ls is a print-and-play, dice-and-grid strategy game where players compete to have the largest "clusters" (contiguous areas filled with their colour) across all game grids. It requires two dice, a writing implement and game grid sheet for each player, and about 20 minutes to complete a match.

Throw the dice to determine the start point of your "Baby L" shape - an arrangement of three squares in a right-angle configuration - and draw it in the grid. Create larger clusters of your coloured squares by outsmarting your opponent, helped with a little luck, then pass your grid to the next player.

Continue filling in the game grids, earning points for your largest clusters on each game grid. The player with the most points wins the game!

—description from the designer

Two players take turns placing only corner jigsaw pieces to score points in this abstract strategy game.

Both sides (front and back) of the jigsaw piece can be used and the piece can be rotated.

An unoccupied square can lose its ability to become occupied by a corner jigsaw piece and the other placement rules are intuitive and simple.

At the end of the game each player will have one or more groups of connected pieces of their own color.

Players score points for each of their groups using a scoring system similar to that of the game called "Ponte del Diavolo".

Strategically good placements create potential for large groups and/or prevent the opponent from creating large groups.

If both players try to win then the board will most likely not be completely filled, hence the name of the game.

—description from the designer

Politigaffes:  The War on Error is a political party game in which each player creates humorous quotes inspired by real politicians, historical figures, and fictional characters.

You function as a candidate who is running for office, but your notes have been scrambled beyond all repairs just seconds before you walk out on national television.

In Politigaffes, each player has their own personal deck of “Talking Point” cards that they will work into the conversation.  You want a fascist regime? Go for it. Are you a conspiracy theorist? You can do that, too. There’s even stuff for you if you want to make some fan fiction for some of your favorite movies, comic books, and video games. You get the most votes during a turn; you win the state and score points equal to the amount of Electoral College votes that the state possesses.

In this crazy restaurant, players must be the first to collect 5 or more Order cards to win the game.

All players simultaneously try to correctly cook the omelette shown on their Order card as quickly as possible, placing the required Ingredient tiles into their pan from their own perspective. But be careful! You must visualize how the Ingredient tiles will look once the pan is flipped.

The Arising is a 176 page expansion book for Moonstone featuring rules expansions and examples of play, rules for linked campaign games, 9 new story scenarios, 55 new character profiles and nearly 100 pages of lore and storyline.

Set in the same world as Going Knowhere and using the same combat system, The Knowmad puts you in the role of a Knowhere defender in training, mastering a range of Builds through endless Trials.

Each run, you assemble a limited set of Abilities and attempt to collect as many Knowstones as possible, recording the result in your Training Log.

To play, you’ll need a standard pack of playing cards (including 2 Jokers), 24 printed Ability cards, a printed Training Log sheet, and a pencil.

Elseware Express is an economic stock manipulation game where players put on the hats of investors 5 years prior to an AI taking over the world. Capitalism is coming to a close and this is your last chance to cash out.

Elseware Express is played over 5 rounds, with each round divided into 2 phases. Players spend the first phase as investors, buying shares in companies and buying goods from the market. In the second phase, the player with the most stock in a company makes decisions on its behalf, building out track, connecting to various cities and then delivering goods, aiming to meet the needs of the people. If all went well, a large payout is given to all investors involved. At the end of the 5th round, the player with the most cash is the winner.

Elseware Express Vol 1 aims to be a colorful and approachable entry in the 18xx genre. Mostly eliminating the need for a calculator with lower values, a simple 2 token cash tracker instead of paper money, and a small 3D stock market that is easier to manipulate. There is a solo mode.

—description from the designer

Democratix is a Slovak board game for 3 to 5 players that shows how democracy works – from campaigns and elections to coalitions and governing. Players get to experience what it's like to make political decisions, seek compromises, and win the trust of voters.
Each player represents a certain part of the social spectrum, according to economic aspects — from left to right and social aspects — from conservative to progressive.

In the first part of the game, players take turns playing activity and promise cards. For these cards, they gain, take over, or remove voters on the board. Once 15 voters appear on the board, the first part of the game ends and the election results are evaluated.
Players gain the number of representatives won in the election and, through discussion and value intersections, a coalition is negotiated. Once the players have agreed and divided into a coalition (with a selected prime minister) and an opposition, the second part of the game begins, which is a 4-year election period.

The elected representatives are assigned to individual promises, which are then voted on with yes/no cards (according to the number of representatives - a majority is required for approval). The opposition uses its MPs for constructive criticism, which can weaken other promises, or leads an anti-campaign and gains political points.

There is a discussion about individual proposals, and the prime minister determines the order in which they will be voted on. Players then gain/lose political points for approved laws based on how they align with their political orientation, which they drew at the beginning of the game. After 4 rounds (years) full of unexpected votes, heated discussions, and betrayals, the game ends and the player with the most political points wins.

The Witch of 1918 will transport you to the somber aftermath of the Great War. While the conflict has officially ended, its echoes haunt the world in the form of "Battle Fairies"—girls magically modified into living weapons of war. As a Witch Physician, you have opened a specialized clinic to treat the indelible psychological scars left upon their spirits.

In this strategic card-management and tile-placement game, you will manage your clinic over eight days (rounds). Each round, you must navigate through several critical phases to save those broken by war:
Auction phase: You must manage your Weight cards carefully, placing them face-down to secretly allocate your influence between neighboring towns. Once all players have passed, the cards are revealed, and the total influence determines the drafting order for new patients and essential herb tiles.

Harvest phase: Arrange your acquired herb tiles into your personal garden to form specific patterns. Creating a "Garden" or a "Trail" triggers powerful production combos, allowing you to harvest the magical resources needed for your medicine.

Healing phase: Strategically assign your harvested herbs to your patients. You can aim for a "Perfect Cure" to receive maximum rewards and reputation, or choose an "Emergency Cure" at a critical moment to stabilize a patient with fewer resources.

Clinic Expansion phase: Cured patients don't just leave; they stay to assist your clinic. Based on their unique abilities—whether one-time, ongoing, or end-game—they will provide the leverage you need to stay ahead of the competition .

The goal is to accumulate the most Credit through successful treatments, strategic set collection of patient symptoms, and overall clinic performance .

—description from the publisher

The game is free in the official Discord!
https://discord.gg/cbuzhgh9FM

A solo quick roll&write game about governing an old egyptian land.
You have been appointed as overseer of Wawat (1871BC) under the Pharaoh Senusret III.
You must manage your land, defend against Nubian attacks, and withstand the harsh desert environment. Can you succeed?

The game endures exactly 5 turns (~20 mins in total).

During each turn, you roll 6 dice and you will use them (choosing the order) performing actions to improve your settlement and to avoid natural calamities.

The game is played on a writable sheet, and your actions adds
(or kill) elements written in the board.

At the end of the 5 turns (if you didn't lose), you will calculate the winning score and get your score "title"!

The Pharaoh has spoken!

Space Commander is a science-fiction roll & write game in which players take on the role of commanders exploring and developing their own galaxy. By establishing connections between planets and stars, players gradually build an interstellar network while managing limited resources and growing spatial constraints.

The game is played over six rounds. Each round begins with a shared dice roll that all players must use on their individual galaxy sheets. Dice values are assigned to planets to create connections, secure stars, close zones, or unlock one-time bonuses that modify future actions. All placements must follow strict rules, and once a value is placed, it cannot be changed, making planning and timing critical.

As the game progresses, the available dice decrease, increasing tension and limiting options. Players must decide how to best use each value, whether to spend powerful bonuses, and when to accept penalties if no legal placement is possible. Optional modules and a solo mode add additional layers of interaction and challenge.

The game ends after the sixth round. Players score points for secured stars, closed zones, and the size of their largest closed area, while losing points for unfinished or invalid elements. The player with the highest total score is declared the Grand Space Commander

Peanut Punch Bacchanal is a lighthearted deduction and recipe-building game set in Trinidad and Tobago, inspired by the flavors, rhythms, and playful Trinidad carnival culture. At the center of the game is a beloved peanut punch recipe—rich, creamy, and carefully guarded. Players step into a festive setting where ingredients, guesses, and a bit of friendly bacchanal collide.

The goal of the game is to uncover the hidden peanut punch recipe before the other players do. Each game features four ingredient categories—nuts, milk, sweetener, and accent—with one card from each category secretly chosen at the start. These four cards form the unique recipe for that round, creating 256 possible combinations and ensuring that every game plays differently.

Players take turns gathering information, eliminating possibilities, and using logic and deduction to narrow down the hidden ingredients. Through structured actions and shared clues, players learn which ingredients are in play and which are not, all while keeping their own progress partially concealed. Strategy matters, as players must decide when to observe, when to test assumptions, and when to make a final guess.

The game ends when a player correctly identifies the full recipe, claiming both victory and bragging rights as the one who cracked the punch. With simple rules, quick playtime, and endlessly variable outcomes, Peanut Punch Bacchanal offers a social, replayable experience that blends reasoning, tradition, and celebration.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Run From the Dark: Forbidden Dark
description:
Forbidden Dark is a Stand alone book that brings the world of Run from the Dark to the Miniatures game of Forbidden Psalm.

The book is stand alone and does not require either the Forbidden Psalm rule book or the Core Run from the Dark RPG. Forbidden Dark however does include a full 15 session long story campaign that can be used in Run from the Dark! 
It includes many of the monsters from Run from the Dark stated up for Forbidden Psalm, new equipment, magic items, new magic, new backgrounds and classes for your Forbidden psalm Warbands and so much more.

This includes a full campaign for SOLO PLAY, COOP and VS play modes. 
Take the world of Run from the Dark and play using the Forbidden Psalm ruleset. 
A full narrative campaign for GM-less gaming.

Forbidden Psalm is a 28mm miniatures agnostic game. 
You can use any miniatures you already own that fit the bill, or build custom minis for this game (with random tables to help you determine how to build them). 
Solo Play and Coop rules are included.

All content can also be used in the MÖRK BORG TTRPG. 
So this means that Forbidden Dark also brings with it rules for using Run from the Dark monsters for your games of MÖRK BORG TTRPG

Players take on the roles of food delivery workers tasked with bringing food to space aliens. First shuffle the Orders deck and place three cards face up, which players will be competing to fill. Each player gets twelve cards and take turns matching food items to an order card, and claim it if they can provide a vehicle, taking a Tip card as a reward. Play continues until all Order cards are claimed, with the winner being the person who got the most tips.

boardgame: Big Bluff Quizz: Le supplément spontané
description: An expansion of BBQ of 110 new cards (as many as in base game) to renew your game.

Players take turns drawing a card from the Prompt deck, each with a picture and a partial sentence, secretly writing on a scrap of paper the most demented thing they can think of to finish it, then vote on which is the funniest. After four rounds the person who got the most votes wins.

On a 5x5 grid, the player draws one card at a time, placing it face up on the grid. If two cards match they're removed from the board, with these two restrictions: a card sandwiched between two cards is "stuck," and no card can be removed if it leaves other cards unconnected to the rest. The goal is to match all pairs, leaving the grid empty; a full grid means you've lost.

For the tenth anniversary of Claim! Search for the Golden Nugget, Speedrobo made a set of promo cards available with any order of at least $50 between January 19th and Junes 1st, 2026, for three of their games:

Pickaxe for Junk World - On attack, destroy this weapon and discard cards from your hand equal to your draw stat. If you do, draw cards equal to your draw stat.

Wake-up Juice for Claim: Mine Rush - You may discard this card at the end of your turn. If you do, take another turn after this one.

Rocky and Rockopher for Claim! - When you discard (play) a card on your turn, you may choose to not resolve its effect. If you do, look at the bottom card of the deck. Put that card on the top or bottom of the deck.

DEER! is a slap-jack style game that simulates driving on a Colorado Highway and reacting to the various wildlife that crosses their path. On each card flip, players must react and determine how to respond to the situation on the card!

Players set out the stop, slow, and go cards in the middle of the table. If there is a deer, elk, or moose in the road, players race to slap STOP. If the deer, elk, or moose are on the side of the road, slap SLOW. And if the road is clear, slap GO.

The first player to slap the correct reaction claims the card. At the end of the game, the player with the most cards wins.

-description from designer

Fluxx is a card game in which the cards themselves determine the rules! Each player starts the game with three cards in their hand. On their turn, player draws one card, then play one card. By playing cards, you can put new rules into play that change numerous aspects of the game: how many cards to draw or play, how many cards you can hold in hand or keep on the table in front of you, and even (most importantly) how to win the game!

Dog Fluxx features canines and familiar Fluxx gameplay, with the game featuring eleven Dog breeds, The Human, a Fire Hydrant, new Actions such as "Bark At It Until It Goes Away" and "Did You Bring a Plastic Bag?", and new Rules such as "Live Dog Bonus" (which has you draw an extra card each turn while a dog is in the room).

Aetherium is a 15-20 minute abstract strategy game for 2 players. This forgotten duel was once a sacred rite used by ancient civilizations to maintain harmony through the four elements. Lost empires fell and centuries passed, its knowledge faded into myth and silence. Now, the game has resurfaced, calling for new duelist looking to find immortality.

Goal
Either have all stones of an element at the top of each column by the end of your turn, or bring the Aether token to your side of the track as far as you can.

Gameplay
The game is played in three rounds. Each round, players alternate taking turns using one of their five element pawns to perform an action on their side. Once all pawns have been used, move the Aether token and prepare for the next round.

On your turn, choose one of your element pawns that you haven't yet used this round, place it on any of the circular spaces on your side of the mat and resolve the corresponding action.

Every action must follow these rules:
You can only move stones that match the element pawn played for the action. When you play your wild pawn, declare which element it will count as for that turn.
You must be able to do the entire action (if you cannot, choose another action or pawn).

—description from the designer

The tree decorating contest has begun! Be the first to decorate your tree with a star, tree skirt, and lights or ornaments. Decorating is a breeze when you have helpful elves, but watch out for the naughty side of tree trimming. Cats might knock off decorations, squirrels
love to swap items, and naughty elves will steal anything they can. To top it all off, a playful Yeti family enjoys shaking trees for fun!

Each player receives a tree board with spaces for a skirt, decorations/lights, and a star. The first player draws a Decoration card and place it on their tree. Next they draw a card from the Naughty or Nice deck - these cards are either helpful or harmful. Play then passes to the next player. The first player to decorate their tree with a skirt, star, and all lights or all decorations wins.

25 Years of Kicking Down Doors and Backstabbing Your Buddies!

After 25 years of kicking down doors and battling monsters, a Second Edition of Munchkin is here! We kept your favorite jokes and monsters – and added more!

This edition feels the same, but it plays faster, and the setup is simpler. A new game mechanic – Gold Pieces – allows players to get change when selling Items and to micro-bribe their friends. Reference cards for each player have been added, so no more fighting for the rulebook – unless you just want to.

All the illustrations – including the box, gameboard, and cards – have been updated by Munchkin’s original artist, John Kovalic.

And the best part? It’s backward-compatible, and guidelines for using cards from your First Edition collection are included!

Grab your friends (or enemies), because it’s time to kick down some doors!

What's new in Second Edition
Not only is there a ton of new art, Second Edition has redesigned cards, actual coins, fast setup rules, and more!

     New Illustrations and Card Designs



     New Icons


Icons! Icons! Icons!
You told us that icons would make the cards easier to read. We listened. Here's the chart from the rulebook.


     Updated Card Backs


Full-color card backs! 
The card backs echo the background color of the card types - blue for Doors, yellow for Treasures -, making them easier to sort no matter which side is up.


     Spiffy Gameboard


A Fresh Coat of Paint!
John Kovalic redrew the gameboard, where you'll start at the top and progress down toward that pile of loot at Level 10!


     Player Reference Cards


Munchkin Second Edition includes six player reference cards - one for each player color - so you can look across the table and see who is playing which color. This also means you won't have to open the rulebook as often.


     New Mechanic: Coins


Real (fake) Gold Pieces
Now you can get change when you sell an Item (which lets you level up a bit faster, which speeds the game). Coins can also be used to bribe other players - and even to level up. Add some coins to your next trade deal. Or hoard them. We know you want to!


     New Setup Rules


Set up the game and Kick Down The Door faster than ever before!
After choosing your player color, shuffle the marked Starting Cards. Deal one Door and two Treasures to each player. Shuffle the leftover starting cards into their respective decks, and give five 100 Gold Piece coins to each player. Everyone starts off at comparable strengths, ready to kill (small) monsters right away.


     New & Updated Phases


Listening just got more lucrative!
When you Listen at the door at the start of your turn, take one coin (100 Gold Pieces) and Draw one card from either the Door or Treasure deck, face down, and put it in your hand before you Kick Down The Door. After the hard work is done, it’s time to Loot The Room. And rather than drawing a card from the Treasure deck, a munchkin may roll the die and take that many Coins. Queue up that Loaded Die to maximize your return!


     Updated Win Condition


Who said you had to do it alone?
Munchkin Second Edition introduces an updated way to win! If you're going for Level 10, you may ask any one player to help you. If you win the combat, you share the victory – regardless of the helper's level, Role, or anything else. (But you don't HAVE to ask for help, if you think you can beat everyone else at once.)
This set is packed with rules tweaks to make it faster, clearer, and more exciting, with even more mayhem in the race to win!

CONTENTS

     150 Cards
     6 Double-Sized Reference Cards
     48 Coins
     1 Custom Die
     Gameboard
     6 Standees w/ bases
     Rules
     Upgrade Guide

Starship Combat is a game of interstellar combat circa 2700 A.D. Each hexagon on the plane sheet represents a trillon miles of an imaginary sector of space, yet each unit counter represents just one starship or inner stellar base. The starships move in hyperspace mode at speeds greatly faster than light (I will not make any pretense on knowing how it is done, since present-day physics does not grant the possibility of such speeds), and they are equipped with energy-beam weapons, and sensors which operate at speeds even faster than the ship's speeds. Interstellar bases, although immobile, also exist in hyper-space mode, so as to allow safe contact with starships. An object is in hyperspace mode, however, has a disadvantage of being easily discernible by enemy sensors; and against units in hyperspace, the weapons possess a maximum effective range of 6 rillion miles.miles.

Vigil Games are a class of strategy games in which players must maintain an uninterrupted line of sight between their pieces and a designated reference point or region of the board. The first vigil game on record is 1892's Kastellet.

Sentinel is a vigil game where players always need to have at least one line-of-sight to the board's center. Sentinel is the second abstract game of this genre (after more than 130 years!), mixing typical movement and capture ludemes with permanent line-of-sight control of the central square.

The game mixes forward movement with sowing stacks which are the only way for armies to increase their size.

Complete rules at the game's forums.

—description from the designer

Behind every great medieval story are unseen hands shaping its fate. In Medieval Madness, you take on the role of a hidden puppet master, guiding kings, knights, and troubadours as their stories unfold. Each game brings a different mix of dramatic events and turning points, ensuring every tale is unique and every play tells a new story.

Across multiple rounds, you’ll weave strategy and foresight into every trick. Will you carefully build your deck to shape events, or take bold risks for powerful plays? In Medieval Madness, each decision pulls you deeper into chaos and only the cleverest puppet masters will shape the final story.

Medieval Madness is a trick-taking game in which, during the trick-taking phase, you build your deck for future rounds. The cards you gather allow you to score points regardless of the revealed scoring cards.

—description from the publisher

Cardgame with pubquizstyle questions about the 90s. With over 200 questions about the 1990s and 2000s, the Pubquiz De Jaren '90 tests your knowledge of these years.

The game is played in 4 rounds, which each will contain 4 to 12 questions about specific categories (sport, entertainment, news and estimation).

The players or teams can score points by answering questions correctly. Once you played through all the cards, you total up the points scored. The team/player with the most points wins the game.

Mutamorphosis ist ein Kartenspiel zum Thema Mut, das dich auf eine Reise zu dir selbst schickt. Du wirst herausgefordert, deine Ängste zu überwinden und mutige Entscheidungen zu treffen.

Die Reise beginnt mit einfachen Aufgaben. Doch je weiter du kommst, desto mehr Mut und Überwindung wirst du brauchen. Unterwegs tauchen alte Freunde oder längst vergessene Erinnerungen wieder auf. Möglicherweise musst du dich auch unbequemen Wahrheiten stellen. Oder Herausforderungen, die dein Herz höherschlagen lassen. Was auch immer dir auf deinem Weg begegnet: Es ist eine Chance, über dich selbst hinauszuwachsen, Neues zu entdecken und vielleicht sogar eine Seite an dir kennenzulernen, von der du bisher gar nicht wusstest, dass sie existiert.

Bist du mutig genug, diesen Weg zu gehen?


Mutamorphosis is a card game about courage that sends you on a journey to discover yourself. You will be challenged to overcome your fears and make courageous decisions.

The journey begins with simple tasks. But the further you go, the more courage and determination you will need. Along the way, old friends and long-forgotten memories will resurface. You may also have to face uncomfortable truths. Or challenges that make your heart beat faster. Whatever you encounter on your way, it is a chance to surpass yourself, discover new things and perhaps even get to know a side of yourself that you didn't know existed.

Are you brave enough to take this path?

Features

A game concept that allows you to grow at your own pace, packaged in a handy, sturdy box – lovingly produced so that you can enjoy it for a long time to come.

- High-quality, lovingly illustrated game board
- 50 playing cards with varied and psychologically sound challenges as well as 7 valuable impulses
- Quick feet stickers to leave your traces of courage
- Treasure chest (in the form of a drawer) for valuable memories
- Accompanying booklet with suggestions for self-reflection
- 2 game modes: alone or in pairs

This game is for people who want to reflect, grow and make decisions. It is ideal for anyone who feels that there is still room for improvement.

Mutamorphosis® is an adventure that entertains you and helps you understand yourself a little better. It boosts your self-confidence, gives you the courage to make small and big decisions, and shows you that you can do much more than you think. Maybe you will not only become braver, but also clearer, freer and more determined?

It is not a typical game and not something for a classic game night.

It is a well-thought-out experience that accompanies you step by step, card by card, in a playful way – to more courage in life. With depth, but without heaviness. With structure, but without pressure.

What makes it special is that you play at your own pace. Courage knows no time or competition. What is a piece of cake for one person may take real effort for another – and that's perfectly fine. You decide when to take the next step and how big it will be.

A new continent awaits in the Forest Shuffle universe: After exploring Central Europe in Forest Shuffle and the wilds of southern England in Forest Shuffle – Dartmoor, this new standalone game invites players on a journey to North America.

As the third standalone game in the Forest Shuffle series, Forest Shuffle - Smoky Mountains takes us to this popular North American National Park. Building on the familiar gameplay of the series, it introduces new species and clever twists in mechanics. This keeping the experience as fresh as the bubbling waters on the new terrain cards!

Here’s what Forest Shuffle – Smoky Mountains is all about:

     New terrain types centered around water, playable on their own or tucked as universal waters under trees.
     New synergies between flora and fauna, all revolving around the element of water
     In addition to a variety of species around the water, the game is also home to various large animals, such as black bears or bobcats.


Once again, the player who collected the most points, wins.

Forest Shuffle - Smoky Mountains is part of the Lookout GreenLine, manufactured 100% eco-friendly.

—description from the publisher

The True King of Monsters is finally here!

Toho’s legendary Kaijus clash in an epic battle to decide who will be the definitive King of Tokyo. Play as Godzilla, King Ghidorah, Hedorah, Rodan, Mothra, and Mechagodzilla.

For those familiar with the original game, discover a new mechanic: “Event cards” that affect all monsters. And for fans, yes, evolution cards are included!

King of Tokyo: Godzilla is a family game with dice, cards, and smashes, inspired by the yams system.

In King of Tokyo Godzilla, you play the legendary ToHo's monsters of whom are destroying Tokyo and whacking each other in order to become the one and only King of Tokyo.

At the start of each turn, you roll six dice, which show the following six symbols: 1, 2, or 3 Victory Points, Energy, Heal, and Attack. Over three successive throws, choose whether to keep or discard each die in order to win victory points, gain energy, restore health, or attack other players into understanding that Tokyo is YOUR territory.

The fiercest player will occupy Tokyo, and earn extra victory points, but that player can't heal and must face all the other monsters alone!

Top this off with special cards purchased with energy that have a permanent or temporary effect, such as the growing of a second head which grants you an additional die, body armor, nova death ray, and more.... and it's one of the most explosive games of the year!

In order to win the game, one must either destroy Tokyo by accumulating 20 victory points, or be the only surviving monster once the fighting has ended.

-description from publisher

Prepare the deck of 25 room tiles by setting aside the Hall, which is placed in the center of the table. Shuffle the remaining room tiles and stack them face down.

Choose the character you wish to play and take the corresponding Sanity, Health, and Item Card tokens, selecting the number of starting Item Cards as specified by your character.

Shuffle the 30 Event Cards and place the deck face down on the table.

Shuffle the Monster Cards. Draw one card at random without looking at it and place it on the table.

Shuffle the 12 Item Cards and place the deck face down on the table.

Place your character's standee in the Hall. Reveal the first room tile… place it on an available connection point, and draw an Event Card. The exploration now begins…

Game Objective and Victory Conditions

The goal of the game is to collect enough Clues to escape the house and, if possible, save the girl. The escape combinations and victory points are as follows:

1 Clue + Girl: 10 Victory Points

2 Clues + Girl: 20 Victory Points

3 Clues: 35 Victory Points

3 Clues + Girl: 50 Victory Points

+  any bonus points from Research Cards

Important: The only way to escape without the girl is by having 3 Clues.

During your investigation, you must survive two types of horror:
Physical Horrors that cause Damage (reducing your Health Points).
Mental Horrors that erode your sanity (reducing your Sanity Points).
If either your Health or Sanity reaches zero, your character meets a grim end: they either die or go insane, remaining trapped in the house... forever!

boardgame: Was passiert wenn ich dran lecke?
description: In this guessing game, players try to identify a hidden term using quirky questions and humorous clues. On each turn, one player chooses a question card, and the clue master reads the answer aloud. All players may then secretly write down a guess, which is checked by the clue master. Players have two guessing attempts per round and score points for correct answers. If no one guesses correctly, the clue master scores instead. The game continues until all question cards have been played, keeping everyone engaged until the end.

Players launch rockets from the Great Blue Planet in a dazzling race through space. Tiles are arranged in a circular path, and each player receives a rocket and a supply of fuel. On their turn, players place fuel on the starting tile to move forward, then decide whether to end their turn to collect more fuel or spend additional fuel to continue flying. The first player to orbit the universe three times wins the galactic race.

Part 8 of the successful puzzle escape series takes players behind the scenes of a variety show. Solve a mix of word, picture, and number puzzles while following an intriguing story. Players start by responding to a job posting for a performer, which leads them into an old storage hall. Locked inside, they must find a way out using cryptic clues that guide them through abandoned trailers. All game materials remain intact, and helpful tips and solutions are included.

boardgame: Klick Klack Klug Starterset ab 5 Jahren
description:
This magnetic learning game is designed for preschoolers to develop concentration and logical thinking. Created specifically for school readiness and educationally tested, it features a variety of colorful themes – from early math, ABCs, and telling time to animals and more.

On each turn, a child draws a card and places all magnetic pieces on the table. Tasks on the front of the card can be completed by sorting or matching. The child selects the correct colored magnet first, then attaches the remaining magnets according to the task, building a tower of six solutions. The back of the card allows checking whether the tower is correctly assembled.

This exciting card-laying game in Rummy style is ready to play immediately using familiar Rummy and poker cards. Players collect clever card combinations, secure rows and columns, and score points while a 5×5 grid gradually fills in the center. On their turn, a player places a card into the grid and can reserve a row or column for themselves. The goal is to collect high-scoring Rummy combinations in your reserved lines, while opponents can block you by placing disruptive cards. Perfect for travel or family game nights.

Get ready for a fun and ewciting race between Chase Marshall, Everest and Skye! Which one of the furry friends will reach the finish line first? With a little luck of the dice, you will have the best chance to win! 
You will roll the dice, move your favorite puppy and the first who arrive to the tower is the winner! Perfect starting boardgame for very young kids!

One player acts as the storyteller, reading the riddle on the front of the card. The other players try to guess what happened, asking questions that can only be answered with yes or no. The goal is to solve the mysterious scenario by combining clues and reasoning together. This simple yet clever format keeps everyone engaged and challenges deductive thinking.

This set includes 50 dark and macabre riddles centered on spectacular selfies, pranks, and online challenges. Players must piece together the stories behind shocking fails – some ending with an exclusive trip to the emergency room – by asking yes/no questions and reasoning carefully. Perfect for fans of twisted mysteries and clever deduction.

Mix and match your favorite Cyberpunk characters to create an unstoppable edge running crew. Over come your opponent not just by doing damage, but by completing gigs, gaining eddies, and increasing your rep as Night City Legends.

Your crew of three legends start in play face down as Eddies. You can sell 1 additional card from your hand to place it face down as an Eddie each turn.

Play units and gear using Eddies, or flip one of your legends, unlocking its unique abilities.

At the start of each turn, you'll roll one of your gig die and add it to your pool. When you attack another player with one of your units, if your attack isn't blocked, you steal one of their gigs.

The first player to begin their turn with 6 gigs wins!

In Racket Dice, each player has one special skill die for each of three abilities: hitting, agility, and stamina. The number of sides on the die indicates how good they are in five increments (d4, d6, d8, d10, and d12 is the best). Players start low but upgrade these skills during the game. Upgrades involve sacrifice, requiring players to choose the right moment to advance a skill. The three skills are unique and lead to different playstyles. A specialist in hitting makes more hand-winning plays. The agility player builds their energy stockpile for later advantage. The stamina specialist wins long points with very high numbers.

—description from the publisher

Roll, bid, and bluff your way to victory in this two-player dice game about racket sports.

It's two games in one. Play tennis or squash, on opposite sides of the board.

Your dice indicate the talents of your athlete. You have one die for each skill. The more sides your die has, the greater your level of ability. If you have a 12-sided hitting die, you'll be sending a lot of winners. If you have a 4-sided agility die, expect to make some positional errors.

Your dice can be upgraded during the game. Pursue a unique promotion strategy. With different skills come different playstyles.

The serving player sets the stakes. Set them high if you have a great roll – or bluff your opponent into thinking you do.

Gather energy and spend it at the right moments. Use extra energy to win this hand – but be careful you aren't too tired to win the next one.

Promote your dice, bluff your opponent, and spend your energy wisely.

Soon you'll be lifting the cup!

—description from the box

Crab vs. Octopus is a discard game for 2 players.

A game is played in several rounds. In each round, players take turns until one of them passes, either because they can no longer play or because they choose to stop.
At the beginning of each round, players draw cards to fill their hands to 5 cards. Starting with the first player, each player plays a card and applies any effects. The card played must have a value strictly greater than the card previously played by the opponent.
When a player cannot or does not want to play anymore, the round ends. The last player to have played a card wins the round and becomes the first player in the next round. All cards played are discarded, then a new round begins.
The game ends when a player plays, discards, or has the last card in their deck stolen (empty hand and deck); they then win the game.

2 game modes included:

Symmetrical: Crabs and Octopuses each have their own specific powers. Adapt your strategy!

Asymmetrical: Both decks have identical powers. Fight on equal terms and try to come out on top!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Cute and Aggressive Capybaras: Capysutra NSFW Expansion
description:
Feeling adventurous?
Indulge in more than just the usual game night—step into the most provocative and daring expansion yet, where not all fruits are sweet—some just leave you hanging!

Don't miss the opportunity to embrace your inner mistress and cruise through the forest looking… for fruits, or maybe you’re just here to watch the action unfold.

20 new gameplay cards

—description from the publisher

Shadows and Steel is a concept development card game developed by the United States Army War College with the intention of crowd-sourcing force design  through player focused competition. Players build a customized military forces and engage rival players, using cards that represent military capabilities, bases, political events, and strategic maneuvers. Currently in Alpha Stage, Shadows and Steel is undergoing active playtesting to refine balance, clarity, and enhance its value as a professional analytical and educational tool.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Step into the beloved, brutal world of Invincible and put your life on the line to defend the people of Earth from merciless supervillains and otherworldly invaders in this cooperative race against the clock that'll push you to your limits as you zoom from Chicago to Atlantis and back again to save lives before it's too late.

In Tiny Epic Invincible, players will assemble a team of 1-4 superheroes, each with their own unique powers, and face off against a randomly selected supervillain seeking world domination.

The game plays across five different locations, with players beginning at the Guardians of the Globe HQ and traveling out to four unique locations pulled from the location deck at the start of the game. The ultimate goal of the players is to rescue and secure civilians at each location before they're caught up in the villain's warpath.

More foes will pile onto these locations as the game progresses, taking advantage of the chaos, and players will need to effectively use the push-your-luck action system to keep up with their opponents. Recruit iconic, non-player allies to station around the world and creatively combine your actions to keep the villains at bay, save lives, and prevent the conquest of Earth!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A27 – The Red Cap's Lair
description:
Designed by the original studio, A missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

A27 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for Rue Morgue & Angry Neighbours

Red Cap Ben was already a survivor before the zombie apocalypse. The lone and moody guy now needs your help as most of his precious tools are locked in his shelter, with many zombies roaming around. Will you gain the Red Cap’s trust, and survive? Let’s find out!

Charters: Profit Through Public Works is an economic strategy game about auctions, shared incentives, and indirect control. Players do not build projects themselves. Instead, they invest in public works companies that expand infrastructure, generate dividends, and reshape the value of the entire system.

Each round, players participate in a rotating auction to determine which company acts and how. When a company is activated, all players may benefit - or suffer - based on their shareholdings and the timing of prior investments. New infrastructure increases future payouts, but also opens opportunities for rivals. Because ownership is fragmented and control is temporary, success comes from reading incentives, anticipating others’ interests, and acting at the right moment rather than optimizing a personal engine.

The game emphasizes valuation growth, dividend timing, and scarcity of opportunities over direct conflict. Information is limited, outcomes are intertwined, and every decision leaves a lasting impact on the table state. No player ever has full control, and alliances form and dissolve naturally through shared stakes.
Charters plays in 30 to 60 minutes for 1 to 4 players and supports both competitive and solo play. A complete Print and Play edition is available, with a small handcrafted physical edition planned.

—description from the designer

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C36 is a hard difficulty exploration mission for the original zombicide.

Material needed: Season 1. Toxic City Mall. Prison Outbreak.
From the overture to the grand finale, lead your own zombie musical! Featuring Ultrared Weapons, a trapped Toxic Abomination, a taxi, lots of zombies...and you!

Designed by Hervé Fortin

In Under the Sand, players are members of an expedition. The role of lead explorer will shift around as much as the dunes surrounding your dig sites. Each turn a new leader will assign explorers to take a turn, alliances will rise and fall depending on who is selected when. Cards will shift around the deck and between players. Hold onto cards too long and you may be cursed!

This worker-placement style game plays in just 18 cards and with up to 4 players. Play by putting your thumb on the action to show it’s been taken. With only 4 actions per tablet card competition will be fierce! Each new tablet changes the game and the actions available.

The game ends after the round when the 4th Tablet is revealed. Players reveal their hands and score points based on Treasure collected. Any player who has 1 or 2 Curses discards 1 Treasure for each Curse. Players then score the points printed on their remaining Treasure cards, the player with the most points wins.

Dig in and explore the game as the players seek to keep themselves from falling for traps not only from the deck, but by other players as well. Pyramids may rise and fall under the onslaught of wind and sand, but who knows what depths the players may find as they search.

—description from the publisher

The fast reaction, push to answer, category game!

There are 6 game modes to choose from. The object of all game modes is to buzz in and give an answer from the category that starts with the letter shown before the timer runs out.

For example, "MADE OF METAL" and "T", could be "TOOL" or "TRAIN". The letters and/or categories will change during the game.

Scar Sworn is a fast-paced, brutal skirmish wargame designed for 2 players. Played on a compact 3'x3' battlefield with warbands of just 5-10 models, it strips away the bloat of mass-battle games to focus on the visceral reality of individual combat. In Scar Sworn, survival is not guaranteed, and every die roll counts.

Key Features
The Triumph System: Momentum is everything. In Scar Sworn, success breeds power. Every time a model eliminates an enemy, they gain a Triumph. These tokens represent the surge of adrenaline and morale in the heat of battle. Spend them at any time to push your warriors beyond their limits.
Brutal Combat: There are no safe attacks. Combat is simultaneous and deadly. When an attacker strikes, if the defender is not eliminated, they immediately retaliate. This makes every charge a calculated risk—will you crush your foe, or just step into their blade?
Dynamic Initiative: The tide of battle turns quickly. While the first turn is decided by a roll-off, in subsequent rounds, the player with the fewer models on the battlefield seizes the Initiative.

Interactive Army Builder App
Scar Sworn comes with a standalone, browser-based Army Builder that works on any device—no internet connection required.

     Automatic Point Calculation: Instantly tallies your warband points and warns you if you exceed the limit for Small, Standard, or Large battles.
     Rules Validation: Prevents illegal lists by tracking unit limits and upgrade restrictions automatically.
     Print-Ready Unit Cards: Once your list is built, the app generates a clean, printer-friendly layout of unit cards for use at the table.
     Save & Load: Export your rosters to JSON files to share with friends or tweak later.


What's Inside

     Core Rules PDF: The complete ruleset, including movement, combat, and terrain rules.
     Scar Sworn Builder (App): The fully interactive roster building application.
     3 Scenarios: Control, Clash, and Plunder—offering distinct tactical challenges from holding ground to seizing treasure.

Runecircle is a tactical hex-based collectible card game where ancient Druids clash across a living battlefield of elemental power and shifting terrain. Command your Druid, awaken your Kin, and bend the forces of nature to your will — or watch your enemies do the same.
Players build custom decks around one of four elemental factions, weaving together spells (Awens), warriors (Kin), and devastating Druid transformations (Morphs) to outmaneuver, outlast, and outplay their opponents. When the tide turns against you, your Druid can Morph into a powerful elemental force — shifting the battlefield in an instant. Every hex matters. Every decision counts. Victory belongs to the Druid still standing when the dust settles.
Set 1 introduces four factions:
Pyr (Fire) — Destruction incarnate. Pyr Druids burn their enemies from the inside out, wielding direct damage and self-sacrifice to unleash devastating bursts of power. They don't just win — they leave nothing behind.
Hydor (Water) — Patient and suffocating. Hydor Druids manipulate the battlefield, drowning enemy Kin to keep them at bay while slowly tightening their grip on every hex.
Verdant (Earth) — Raw animalistic power. The Verdant Druid leads a ferocious pack, overwhelming opponents through sheer force and coordinated aggression.
Drunken — A band of misfits with tricks up their sleeve. Their Druid commands through a network of pubs — sources of power, energy, and a supernatural teleportation system. And don't sleep on the Amberfangs. The vampires are always watching.
Combat uses d12 dice rolls modified by card abilities, with a Reaction Window system that keeps both players engaged on every single turn — even when it's not yours.
Runecircle fuses the deck-building depth of collectible card games with the spatial tactics of hex-grid wargames into a fierce, replayable competitive experience.
—description from publisher

boardgame: Grimdark Future: Firefight – Assault on Malhadra: Mission Pack
description:
Hidden from the rest of the galaxy, many have tried to claim Malhadra as their own, only to fade from memory, swallowed by its shifting sands.

While seeking new prospects in distant systems, the Dwarf Guild Champion, Hadrund oth Irnwuld, stumbled upon the elusive world once more. This discovery did not remain secret for long, however. The Robot Legion Technomancer, Echo-3G01, initially dismissed his mission as a routine patrol, but rumours of Robots abandoned on Malhadra by the Elves sparked his interest.

Both were caught by surprise when a mysterious distress signal hailed out from the planet’s surface. Vanguard Superior Constance of the Blessed Sisters responded, entering into the fray to aid anyone trapped on the isolated world. Finally, Swiftclaw the Unbidden Hand made a stylish entrance, intent upon slipping through the shield in order to perform his most magnificent heist yet.

Strike teams have been dispatched to Malhadra, where they will have to survive the harsh deserts, treacherous underworld, and frozen poles of this unforgiving world. Beyond these dangers, they also must contend with each other.

Will you be the one to claim the mysterious technology which has hidden Malhadra for centuries?

Here's all the missions included in this pack:

     Failed Negotiations
     Cargo Bay
     Weightless
     The Dark Night
     Ash Storm
     Hit & Run
     Escort
     Road War
     Spec-Ops
     Fungal Jungle
     Rations Refinery
     Cash-Grab Back-Stab
     Class Purge
     Waves of War
     Frozen Wasteland
     Lab Raid
     Feral Machines
     Console Overload


Even though the missions are designed for Grimdark Future: Firefight, you can play them with the rules for any of our other games (with slight adjustments).

Note that to play these missions you’re going to need the core rules for Grimdark Future: Firefight as well as some army lists.

The main characters of this campaign are available for purchase as 3D print files on the One Page Rules MyMiniFactory store.

When a huge pile of B-roll footage is slated for the dumpster, a savvy producer has an epiphany: hire talented but hungry directors to edit it into new, exciting movies...and you are one of those directors.

This is your chance to land your dream job and enter Hollywood's big leagues. In this town, that's measured by one thing: popcorn sales.

Play cards simultaneously to cut and arrange your perfect movie, while meeting Studio Requirements for extra points. Be the director who sells the most popcorn, and claim your spot in Hollywood's Hall of Fame!

—description from the publisher

Cuando una enorme pila de material de archivo está a punto de acabar en la basura, un productor avispado tiene una idea brillante: contratar a directores y directoras con talento pero sin recursos para que la editen y lo conviertan en películas nuevas y emocionantes... y ese es tu trabajo!
Esta es tu oportunidad de entrar en la liga de las grandes estrellas de Hollywood. En esta ciudad, eso se mide por una sola cosa: las ventas de pochoclo.
Juega en simultáneo para montar tu película perfecta, mientras cumples los requisitos del estudio para obtener puntos extra. ¡Sé quien venda más pochoclo y reclamá tu puesto en el Salón de la Fama de Hollywood!

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

International powers balance is in constant evolution, while newcomers change the map of geopolitical arena. It is time for you to lead your country in this global struggle !

Diplomacy & Dominance add a new strategic layer to World Order with new challenges and game modes which will test your skills. Use new ability, growth and strategic assets cards to spread your influence; fulfill your superpower objective cards which reflect main strategic views of our time; and challenge one or several automas to assert your domination on other superpowers, no matter their number.

Automa rules
New rules allow you to play solo against one or several automas or to include them into your multiplayer games. With two difficulty levels, they will adapt their challenge to new players and seasoned ones.

New strategic assets
Discover new tactical possibilities with these 12 new cards.

Superpower objectives
Your objectives now reflect strategic orientations of real-world superpowers.

New ability cards
4 new ability cards enhance the game and give more interactions between players.

New growth cards
2 new growth cards sets renew your game experience. Replace or combine with growth cards from base game.

This expansion for World Order includes 91 cards, one rulebook, and one double-layered automa board.

This game is a Murder Mystery consisting of three consecutive scenarios.

The first part (the first scenario) is set in 1980 in a rural town where rumors of "a serial killer who escaped from a sanatorium" are whispered. A mysterious pair of people present, the children who were "hunting for the killer" and a local constable unravel the murder at an abandoned station after the storm has gone.

The second part is set 10 years ago in 1970 at a sanatorium where serial killers are quarantined. Two arson attacks followed by the death of the director. The mystery is challenged by doctors, medical students in training, detectives visiting staff, patients, residents, and children raised in the facility for some reason.

The third part will be set in 1990 in the entire rural town that encompasses abandoned stations and sanatoriums drawn in the first and second parts. The first murder in 10 years and the signature of a serial killer left on the scene... Can those involved in the past prevent the next murder and obtain soul salvation?
family: Series: Mystery Party in the Box

boardgame: Crimson Skies: The Black Swans Squadron Pack
description:
The Black Swans are one of the most successful and dangerous raider groups in the skies. Formed in 1934, this ruthless squad was recruited by the enigmatic Black Swan herself. Composed of highly skilled combatants, the Swans do their best to direct their machine gun fire toward legitimate targets, avoiding collateral damage. The Black Swans currently fly in and around the Empire State area.

A set of four aircraft miniatures for the Crimson Skies base game's aerial dogfight rules. The pack includes two J2 Fury miniatures (aircraft #23 and #24) and two M210 Raven miniatures (aircraft #25 and #26).

All four miniatures are painted in a style common to the squadron with different details on each plane.

boardgame: Cyber Savage: The Laser-Sorcery Miniature Game
description:
Cyber Savage: The Laser-Sorcery Miniatures Game is a fast and tactical skirmish game played with 3-12 miniatures.

Designed to be flexible and expansive enough to accurately but simply reflect any character that could be brought to the table.
Played on a 2'x2' area, the game easily supports up to 4 players with 8 different scenarios.

Force building is simple and can be done in our Force Builder web app!

Full color rulebook packed with art and photos.

One of a kind resolution mechanic, no combat equations, no feast or famine rolls. Every decision is important and both players participate!

A passion project by two brothers. 
We're so excited to bring our world to you! 
All miniature sculpting and rules writing done by us! 
As well as most of the art along with some collaborations with a few of our favorite artists.

We hope this game helps you build new characters and exciting stories just like it has for us!

It’s harvest time on Mallorca. In La Cosecha (Spanish for “the harvest”), you run your farm in the idyllic hamlet of Alpich near the town of Esporles. Each round, you harvest goods such as olives, grapes, and grain to mark boxes in different areas of your farm sheet. Fields need tending, the barn roof needs covering, and market carts want loading—your farm is full of opportunities!

Often, you’ll need upgraded goods such as food and wine, which you must first produce from harvested goods. You’ll have to balance short-term revenue against long-term planning. In addition, you can acquire tools and farm extensions with special abilities that may give you the decisive edge.

The player who manages to use their revenue most effectively, combine the strongest bonuses, and keep an eye on the competition will earn the greatest renown and win the game.

La Cosecha is set in the world of La Granja. It draws on elements you may already know from La Granja (Spielworxx, 2014), No Siesta (ADC Blackfire, 2016), and El Burro (Spielworxx, 2024), while still being a completely standalone game with fresh ideas and challenges.

The game includes two different farm sheets:
• La Fiesta, where you host a banquet, and
• El Camino, whose long road leads you to the Port de Palma.

Both share the same core rules, but each sheet offers its own challenges and level of difficulty.

You can also play La Cosecha solo. Face the imaginary opponent Leon and try to beat your own high scores.

-description from publisher
4 new totem cards with different, more complex effects, inspired by the Czech mountains and the legend of Krakonoš.

You're retired and you want to create memorable moments, enjoy good times, and do so in good company. You'll have the chance to participate in new activities and make wonderful connections while exploring the challenges of retirement.

Taking turns, you'll have the opportunity to participate in activities and leisure pursuits and accumulate memory points. When all the events are over, the game ends and all players count their points.

The player with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

Vous êtes à la retraite et vous avez le désir de créer des 
souvenirs mémorables, de profiter de bons moments, 
et ce, en bonne compagnie. Vous aurez la chance de 
participer à de nouvelles activités et de faire de belles 
rencontres tout en explorant les défis de la retraite.

À tour de rôle, vous aurez la chance de participer 
à des activités et des loisirs et d’accumuler des 
points de souvenirs. Lorsque les événements 
sont tous terminés, la partie prend fin et tous les 
joueurs* comptent leurs points.

Celui ou celle qui a le plus de points remporte la partie!

—description from the designer (French)

The Spell Pack for Wandering Towers contains all 6 spells from the Mini-Spell expansion packs in one box!

The wizards have more spells at their disposal to reach Ravenskeep first!

Retrieve a Card allows you to put the top card of the discard pile back in your hand. Movement Swap allows you to swap the movement values on the card you play, including moving something that is not shown on your card, like moving a wizard if only a tower is shown.

Have you ever wanted to Close Ravenskeep and prevent any wizards from going there? Well, now you can for one turn. Drawing 2 cards allows you to interrupt your turn and gain additional card options.

Fill a Potion Bottle gives you the ability to flip a bottle without covering any wizards. Finally, Extra Die Roll gives you the chance to get a new movement value, replacing the one printed on a card.

Mix and match these wondrous spells with those from the base game. These spells offer exciting strategies, increasing player choice and tactical depth when combined with the original magic of the award-winning game Wandering Towers!

—description from the publisher

Fight to dominate the Mediterranean world and crush the rise of Rome in its cradle with The Pyrrhic Wars!

This splendid tome contains everything you need to fight the campaigns of Pyrrhus of Epirus in the 3rd century BC to life on the Hail Caesar tabletop.

Inside its covers you'll find a wealth of historical background information covering Pyrrhus, ruler of Epirus, and his clashes against the nascent Roman Republic, as well as his allies, enemies, and battles, all accompanied by a plethora of gorgeous miniature photography.

With this supplement for the Hail Caesar game, you can command the army of Pyrrhus or one of his multitude of enemies using twelve detailed army lists, and battle through twelve new scenarios that encompass Pyrrhus' exploits, ranging from his engagements against his fellow Successor Kings, his costly victories over the Romans, to his adventures in Sicily, and finally his doomed bid to dominate all Greece.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Hail Caesar: Massed Battle Wargaming from the Biblical to Medieval Eras (Second Edition)
description:
War has been a constant throughout the history of mankind. From the first civilisations of Babylon and Egypt to the classical eras of Greece and Rome, and beyond to the medieval wars between England and France, ancient civilisations have massed thousands of soldiers to march against their enemies to clash for dominance and conquest. On the battlefields of history, only the sinew of men and the cunning of generals will prove victorious.

With Hail Caesar, you can raise an army of ancient warriors and take them into battle against your friends, recreating some of the most famous and legendary battles ever fought.

Hail Caesar is a set of massed-battle wargame rules that allow you to recreate any number of battles from throughout ancient history, including all the rules necessary for open conflict and siege assaults.

This 236-page hardback book written by renowned games designer, Rick Priestley updates the Hail Caesar game with amended rules and all new features such as rules for sieges and the extension of the timeline to include such conflicts as the Hundred Years War and the War of the Roses.
family: History: Greek & Persian conflicts of the 4th century BC
History: Wars in the Ancient Near East

description:
At the dawn of existence, the primordial spirit Gaia awakens and entrusts the players—known as Biomancers with a sacred mission: to spark life in empty, dormant biomes. By shaping proto-ecosystems and channeling Gaia’s essence, the Biomancers awaken tiny elemental beings called Bloomlings, whose presence generates the forces that sustain life itself. Towering Natural Golems, ancient servants of Gaia, help activate these biomes, turning barren lands into thriving worlds.

Players place tiles and develop biomes within their ecosystems, using Bloomlings to generate elemental resources. These resources are spent to acquire new biomes, activate abilities, and maintain balance across different environments. Strategic choices revolve around where and when to activate biomes, how to combine their effects, and how to efficiently use the limited resources Gaia provides. Although all players serve the same divine purpose, they compete for optimal growth and harmony.

The game is won by the Biomancer who achieves the greatest overall prosperity and balance by the end of the game. Points are earned by creating flourishing biomes, efficiently managing resources, and maintaining equilibrium among the elemental forces. The player who best fulfills Gaia’s vision is granted her ultimate favor and ascends to the Undying Lands, securing victory.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Siberian Manhunt: Ejection Zone Mini Expansion
description: Jack MacKenzie bails out of his U2 before it crashes, randomizing the starting conditions for Siberian manhunt and jumpstarting the action.

In GalletaMix, each player becomes a true cookie lover, competing to devour as many cookies as possible. How? By forming lines of three or more matching cookies on the table!

Turn by turn, you’ll move a delicious cookie on the shared board, trying to connect shapes and decorations to create a perfect line of three or more. If you succeed… you eat them and score points!
But beware: the other players are hungry too and will try to block your moves or create their own sweet combinations.

A tasty mix of strategy, fun, and friendly competition—no crumbs left behind!

—description from the designer

En GalletaMix, cada jugador se convierte en un amante de las galletas que compite por devorar la mayor cantidad posible. ¿Cómo? Formando líneas de tres o más galletas iguales en la mesa.

Turno a turno, moverás una deliciosa galleta en el tablero común, buscando conectar formas y decoraciones para crear una línea perfecta de tres o más. Si lo logras… ¡te las comes y ganas puntos!
Pero cuidado: los demás jugadores también están hambrientos y tratarán de bloquear tus jugadas o formar sus propias combinaciones dulces.

Una deliciosa mezcla de estrategia, diversión y competencia amistosa.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

In this fun board game, the main thing is not who gets to the finish line first, but who helps the Moomins collect the trash that Stinky has thrown in the forest. With the game, you will learn to keep the forest tidy.

START

Each player chooses a piece and places it on the starting square. All trash cards are placed face down in piles on the table.

PLAY

Roll the dice and move forward on the game board according to the number shown on the dice. If you move to a square with picture, follow the game instructions. If you move to an empty square, the turn passes to the next player.

The first player to the goal gets three extra trash cards.

WINNING THE GAME

You can win in two ways.


     When the first player reaches the finish square, the winner is the one with the most trash cards. 
     Each piece of trash has a different point value depending on how long it takes for the trash to decompose. You can find the points for the trash in the game instructions. At the end of the game, add up all your points and the one with the most trash points wins the game.


•••

Tässä hauskassa lautapelissä pääasiana ei ole kuka pääsee ensimmäisenä maaliin, vaan kuka auttaa muumeja keräämäänroskat, jotka Haisuli on heittänyt metsää. Pelin avulla opit pitämään metsän siistinä.

Grand Spin is a fast-paced 1-on-1 dueling card game where players build 20-card decks from over 120 unique champions and  duel to reduce their opponents life total to 0.

With a unique resource system, fast paced duels, card synergies and battles decided by the spin of a top. It is easy to learn but difficult to master.

Players take turns sending champions to battle from their bench, activating their unique abilities. If a champion in battle shares a type with a card on their bench they can also activate their type bonus.

Once both players have selected their champions and have activated all abilities and type bonuses, players spin their champions on a magnetic top to battle. Each side of the card has an initiative number, when the cards stop spinning the initiative numbers facing each other determine what card gets to attack. This repeats until one card is destroyed, the winning card deals its attack to their opponents life total. After battle the winning player refreshes their bench with champions from their deck and starts the next round.

Two teams face each other and the goal is to make your partner guess what you are drawing before the timer runs out. The team that guesses the most drawings wins.

—user summary

Le jeu idéal pour animer vos soirées! Tentez de faire deviner à vos coéquipiers les mots en les dessinant. Grâce aux 4 thèmes proposés (Québec, Général, Jeunesse, Rétro), créez vos parties sur mesure pour viser l’univers des joueurs de toutes les générations.

—description from the publisher

Set in the middle of the Second World War, Fliegerkorps is a solo PNP wargame of operational air command during the Second World War. You take control of a German air corps (Fliegerkorps) and attempt to sustain effective air operations across one of three historical campaigns: the Battle of Britain (1940), Operation Barbarossa (1941), or the Mediterranean Theater (1942).

Before the campaign begins, you assemble your Fliegerkorps by selecting a small force of aircraft and a commanding officer. Each turn generates a Mission that demands an immediate response, from intercepts and ground strikes to naval patrols, while rare Campaign Missions represent major operations that can decisively shift the course of the war at significant cost. Aircraft launch, take losses, and cycle through refit as fuel, time, and maintenance run short. Missions and random events force constant adaptation, while Air, Land, and Sea campaign tracks quietly reshape the war if neglected. Each turn reshapes the balance of the campaign. Victory is earned by sustaining operations, absorbing losses intelligently, and maintaining momentum until the campaign breaks in your favor, or breaks you.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Flames of War: Maginot Line – Complete WWII Starter Set: German vs French
description:
French and German armoured forces clashed across the lush countryside and cobbled villages of France and Belgium, in epic battles that pitted two entirely different approaches to modern warfare against each other.

In this box you will find all you need to test your mettle as either a French or German tank force commander, fighting to win the Battle for France!

—description from the publisher

A game of strategy and chance! Play as one of three intrepid explorers on a mission to spot the elusive Sasquatch - or play as Bigfoot, who doesn't want to be found! Each explorer has their own unique motive and skill in tracking the elusive cryptid through a range of territories with various properties. Multiple dice and an asymmetrical game-board make for a complex game of hide and seek.
