Once an explorer gathers some evidence of the gentle beast, either through catching a photo or setting a trap, the tables are turned and the explorer must make a dash back camp with the evidence before Bigfoot catches up with them. The explorers’ goal - to get evidence of the Bigfoot back to camp before being destroyed (running out of hearts). Bigfoot’s goal - last one standing - not to be caught or destroyed (by running out of hearts). The game can be played using teamwork or individual strategy - or a combination of both. Game ends when either an explorer returns to camp with evidence, when Bigfoot runs out of hearts or when the explorers all run out of hearts. This game is simply delightful! Crafted in a claymation style pacific northwest setting - the game resonates with cheerful, humorous, natural adventure.

—description from the designer

Monopoly Jaws style. In this version of Monopoly players must buy up property and build homes in the Jaws universe.

Everybody out of the water! The classic 1975 movie is now available as a new edition of the classic MONOPOLY game. Travel around the board as one of six special tokens from the film (Compressed Air, Orca Boat, Machete, Lilo, Fin, and Barrel), collect moments, and pick up Repairs and Reward cards featuring dialogue from the script, for additional prizes and penalties. With officially-licensed imagery from the cinematic classic, will you collect the events on Amity Beach, the grisly discovery of the first victim, or Hooper's shark hunting gear? Will you land on the Shark Attack space or collect the Chum Line and Sonar? Buy up classic Jaws characters like Hooper, Quint and Brody, then build boats and workshops to charge your opponents more rent and drive them into bankruptcy

Darkness looms on the horizon – A ruthless Overlord gathers power and followers, set on bending all to their will.  As their influence grows, Adventurers gather to oppose them, seeking fame, glory, and wealth. Can these champions put an end to the Overlord’s tyranny? Or will they be crushed like so many before them by the wrath of the Overlord?

In this asymmetric deck-building game, players take on the role of either heroic Adventurers or the evil Overlord. Adventurers work as a team to survive waves of enemies, level-up their decks, and slay the Overlord’s bosses before time runs out. Meanwhile, the Overlord raises an army of sinister minions and assigns them hidden orders to exterminate the Adventurers.

Every player take a colored pawn
There are three scoreboards: the Olympic stadium with the 5-lane track on which you can compete in athletics competitions, the central section with the platforms for the jumps (high jump and pole vault) and the platforms for throws (javelin and shot put), and the last area for the swimming, diving, and rowing competitions.
Cards are needed to move the pieces.  
Every player take 5 cards.
The cards are divided into number cards and "snap" cards  
Every cards show various numbers: These numbers are the spaces your piece travels during the competition:  100 meter dash, the 110 meter hurdles, in the swimming, springboard diving, long jump and pole vault.
The goal is add the value of the cards to make the longest throw, get to the finish line first, or make the best dive.
The winner of single competition win a medal.
The Olympic winner is who wins more.

The players take command of various type of ships: 1 carrier with aiplanes carried, 1 Battleship, 1 Cruiser, 1 Destroyer and 1 atomic submarine.
The game board is a representation of the sea divided into squares.
Each turn, the miniatures moves on the game board one or two squares forward, backward, right or left.
On the games board it is possible to put island that block ship movement and using coastal batteries to sink the enemy fleet.
Your task: eliminate the enemy fleet.
You can do this using only ships, using Carrier Planes or missiles carried on submarines.
The winner is who destroy first the enemy fleet.

Articulate! Fame is a fast-talking party game focused entirely on famous people from across entertainment, history, sport, and popular culture. Designed as a compact, high-energy spin-off of Articulate!, the game challenges players to rely on shared knowledge, memory, and quick thinking as they race to identify well-known figures under increasing pressure.

Players split into two teams and play through three timed rounds using the same set of name cards. In the first round, a team member describes as many famous people as possible within the time limit using any words they like, except the name itself. Only successfully guessed cards are kept and used again in later rounds. In the second round, those same names must be described using only two words, and in the final round, each name must be conveyed with just a single word, making careful listening and recall essential.

Teams score a point for every correctly guessed name. After the third round, the team with the highest total score wins. With its diminishing descriptions and escalating tension, Articulate! Fame rewards teamwork, creativity, and memory, while delivering a lively and often hilarious party-game experience.

Heat: Legends takes your races to the next level! This expansion brings all six expansion cars onto the track as Legend automated drivers. But these Legends aren’t just extra cars—they’re smarter, faster, and tougher. They’ll slipstream, draft, clear corners with precision, and unleash powerful new abilities that keep every race unpredictable.

With multiple difficulty settings, you can fine-tune just how fierce the competition gets.

–description from publisher

Contents:

36 Legend cards
36 Power Up cards
12 Rival cards
21 Sponsorship cards
1 Rules booklet
family: Game: Heat – Pedal to the Metal

description:
You are an Aztec warrior, the strongest of your tribe. You have the honor of competing in the prestigious Fire Ceremony. Fight your way up the pyramid earning resources, fighting opponents, and earning the favor of the Gods. Beware, you only have 12 months to complete the ceremony. Get to the top of the pyramid, earn the favor of the six ancient gods, and save the world… or else.

GAMEPLAY:
To play, there are three phases: 
1. The Action Phase (where player have two action points to spend on various things, acquiring/trading resources, praying to Gods, moving up the pyramid, etc.) This is also where players may turn in sets of attack cards to gain extra action points to perform more actions. Some actions cost more than 1 action point.
2. The Attack Phase: Players (besides the Chief) must pay resources to enter an exclusive battle mechanic where cards are played in a Rock, Paper, Scissors style attack. Unlike RPS, this battle is a free for all and the attack card your choose may save your life as well as earn you a victory against your target. There are also special cards that add a layer of interest to the game. The shield blocks all incoming attacks, and there is a mask card which utilizes a special sneak mechanic, earning them valuable steps up the pyramid. Winning attacks with weapons (staff, tomahawk, or sword) will allow players to effect each other, stealing resources, bringing them down the pyramid, or wounding them)
3. The Gods' Phase: This is where the Chief will take the form of the Gods, beginning with a die roll. This roll will determine the God of the Month, resulting in all players gaining the favor of that God for the rest of the round. Then they will pull a random event card which will effect all players. Each God Card will show anywhere from zero to five Gods on it. Match any one God on your board with any listed on the cards and you will reap the rewards of a Blessing. If you don't match any Gods, they will punish you with a Wrath. Event cards result in players gaining/losing resources, steps, or life blood.

At the end of these three phases, players will return any temporary Gods to their inventories and the Chief will rotate clockwise to the next player. The calendar will click down one month closer to the end of the game and the phases will begin again with the new Chief beginning with their Action Phase.

Life blood is important. Every player begins with 5 life blood, but it is also used as a valuable resource where you can buy permanent favor of Gods. by doing so, players who buy permanent favor will fill the shrine slot for that particular God at the top of the pyrmaid. There are other resources include Crops, Gold, Attack Cards, and God Cards. All of the resources may be traded at the market, so gaining as many resources as possible is highly beneficial to players so that they may acquire more Blood Tokens, Attack Cards, or God Cards to suit their play style.

Also important to note: Each time the game is played, Faction pieces will be distributed randomly into the corners of the board giving each player two powers and two starting bonuses for the rest of the game.

WIN CONDITIONS:
To win, a player must be standing on top of the pyramid and acquire all six of the permanent favor of the Gods before the final month of the round ends. This results in saving the world. If no players achieve the win conditions by the end of the 12th month, everyone loses and the world is plunges into darkness. If there is a tie, players will count up their resources and determine who has more left over and the player with more will be determined the winner.

PLAYER COUNT & PLAY TIME:
Ontleco plays well with 2 - 4 players, but plays BEST with a full table of 4. Play rotagtes clockwise around the board completing each phase in stages before turning the month down 1 notch on the calendar. The "Chief: begins each round and after each month, the Chief will rotate to the next player in clockwise order.

Playtime is varied depending on player familiarity with games. Learning the rules is time consuming. With 4 new players, the average play through is roughly 2.5 hrs, but with four experienced players, play time can last under 1 hour even with a full table.

EXTRA INFO:
The best part about the game is the social aspect. Players are able to effect eachother. It isn't a heavy strategy game, although strategy certainly plays a big part of the decision making. Players often remark that they enjoy the replayability with the rotating factions.

We have plans for expansion packs that include more factions to increase player rivalry, strategy, and replayability. We want to design a solo mode and think about how to add in a 5th and 6th player option!

—description from the designer

Bad Deals is a trading and hand management game where players trade soul and related contract cards so that the ones in their hand would be fulfilled.

During their turn the active player proposes a partially hidden set of cards to trade, for example "2 Hearts" or "1 Monk". The other players do the same then the active player chooses who they trade with.

At the beginning of their turn a player with at least one contract and only currently fulfilled contracts (such as "You have a Jester, a Keeper, a Weaver and a Monk" or "You have exactly 9 cards") in their hand wins.

The twist is that Contracts feature a suit like any other card so they can be traded the same way (for example some of the 2 hearts above could be contract cards).

—description from the publisher

Storm through the city gates and cast down the tyrant king in this game of wizardry and revolution!

In this game, players will roll and manipulate their mana dice and spend them as a resources to recruit citizens, cast spells, and defeat enemies.

Crashing gates unfold new city levels until you reach the palace, where the tyrant king awaits for the final challenge.

Once the last stage of the king is defeated, the player with the most victory points wins.

—description from the designer

A children's game released by the now-defunct Brazilian department store Mesbla, part of a line featuring its mascot (the bear Sbryfs).

It involves spinning a roulette wheel to try and buy, trade, or even find stickers to complete your album first.

Jogo infantil lançado pela finada loja brasileira Mesbla, parte da linha do seu mascote (o urso Sbryfs).

Consiste em girar uma roleta para tentar comprar, trocar ou até mesmo achar cromos para completar primeiro o seu álbum.

By means of this game, children can learn to read and to recognize letters and sounds. 
Therefore all letter cards are put face down on the table. Then every child gets 3 boards with an image of an object. The youngest player starts and takes one of the letter cards. If the letter is in his object, he puts the card face up on the table and reads the letter. 
The first child who manages to have all letters for his object  wins.

By means of this game, children get to know the letters. 
Therefore all the cards are distributed evenly to the children. The cards are put face up on the table. The youngest player starts and checks for cards which match in the first letter and the image: For example, if there is an image with a mouse and the letter 'M', the child has one pair. If the player has no matching cards, he asks another player to exchange cards. 
The child with the most pairs wins.

Star Trader 7 is a 7 systems variant to the Original Star Trader game from Ares magazine set in SPI’s “Universe” Role Playing context. The 7 systems trade web  is coherent with the original map and Features Mu Herculis a common denominator.

The game uses the original rules except as modified in the expansion. It adds 2 commodities, new chits, agents and corporations. It changes some rules like initiative determination, ship combat or sabotage and adds optional Anarchist Non Player faction.

Kapyle is a two-player strategy card game focused on timing, prediction, and limited information. Players attempt to score the highest before either player runs out of cards.

At the start of the game, each player selects a faction, which defines how cards function and what abilities are available.

The deck contains only five unique card types.

During play, players choose to play cards face up to activate abilities or face down to conceal intent. Hidden cards may later be revealed to trigger effects. Resolution is interactive, with abilities that can interrupt, absorb, block, or modify plays as they occur. Successful play relies on reading the opponent’s intentions and choosing the right moment to commit or reveal cards.

Scoring is based on the number of scoring cards multiplied by an active multiplier. The game ends when a player can no longer make a valid play, and the highest single scoring action determines the outcome.

In Final Chance, up to four players join a football league before the final three weeks of matches are played. Based on the current standings and remaining fixtures, players must predict which of the eight teams will win the league and place bets on the outcome of each game.

Matches are played by drawing cards from a shared deck, which players help build by contributing their own cards. Players can use their cards to make additional predictions or influence the results during stoppage time. The player who earns the most rewards is declared the winner.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Spielend lernen: Erstes Zählen mit Tierkindern
description:
By means of this game, children can learn to count up to 12. 
Therefore all cards are distributed evenly to the children. Every player looks at his cards. If he finds a matching pair, he puts it face up on the table. The youngest player starts and asks another child for a card with a specific animal. If the player has this card, he hands it over and the player can put another matching pair on the table. Then it's the next player's turn.
The child with the most matching pairs wins.

boardgame: Tales of Derring Do: Supplement 3 – Tales of the Old West
description: Old West expansion rules for the core Derring Do set.  Takes the action into the Wild West.

To learn English, the kids can play a memory and a bingo variant with these cards.
For memory, they put all the cards face down on the table. The youngest player starts and puts 2 cards face up on the table and reads the English words. If the cards match, they can take it. Ohterwise, the next player continues. 
For bingo. all players get a board with 9 images. The youngest player starts and takes a card. He reads the English word and shows the card to all players. If a player has this image, he puts a token on the image on his board. The first player who has a row or a column, says bingo. The player who has the whole card with tokens wins the game.

boardgame: Enemies of Gotham City: Knight on the Town Expansion
description:
Gotham City is in turmoil as Batman’s fiercest foes fight for supremacy. In the Enemies of Gotham City – Knight on the Town expansion, make your bid for dominion by harnessing the abilities of unique and powerful villains! Place deadly riddles across Gotham City as The Riddler and put the hurt on the other villains as Killer Croc. Single-handedly take on your rivals as one of the two new Lone Villains by unleashing vengeance as The Phantasm or concocting secret formulas as Man-Bat!

Enemies of Gotham City Knight on The Town Expansion introduces a new Villain type, Lone Villains! This expansion adds 55 new cards including 4 new Villains: The Riddler, Man-Bat, The Phantasm, and Killer Croc.

—description from the publisher

Monopoly Jaws 50th Anniversary style. In this version of Monopoly players must buy up property and build homes in the Jaws universe, with a 50th Anniversary design.

Add Jaws 50th Anniversary Monopoly to your game collection and enjoy a legendary twist on the classic Monopoly experience. A perfect choice for unforgettable game nights or as a standout gift for collectors.

Contains:
Game Board
6 Collectible Tokens
28 Title Deed Cards
16 Orders Cards
16 Commands Cards
32 Tents
12 Barracks
Money
Dice
Rules

boardgame: Spaceship Raiders: Scum of the univers
description:
The quick story:
Spaceship Raiders is a easy & fast strategy card game for 2 players, where Raiders fight to gaining control of a abandoned spaceship first, while it is falling apart !!

A turn overview:
Players takes turns placing 2 cards (soldiers, officers, leaders) on rooms, in the spaceship.

Cards take effect/shoot when placed, but energy shields work all the time.

With the ability on the left bottom side, a card can shoot one or more cards adjacent to it. Cards has a protective energy shield other cards can`t shoot through. Cards also have an advanced ability, on the right side.

Hand size can vary, and you may also use cards to play/draw more than 2 cards in a turn.

Then ending their turn by drawing 2 cards, and it is the other players turn.

Two ways to win:

You win instantly by occupying all purple, or all orange rooms.

If all rooms in the spaceship are full or you run out of cards, the player with most points win. (Point for enemy cards shot + room points, for the rooms your own cards are placed in)

—description from the designer

boardgame: Legends of Time and Space: Into Chaos
description:
Eden Colony. The surface hides under a thick cloud blanket, thrown up by the terminal war. The aliens came out of nowhere and their invasion spread so rapidly that nobody knew how to stall it. The off-world relief forces had no time to form, and in the end, the only weapons that could destroy the aliens destroyed civilization as well. But that was a long time ago. Probes report that the planet is no inhabitable, and there are powers that have vesting interest in returning...

---

Into Chaos is a programmed adventure using Dark City Games Legends of Time and Space rule set. These rules provide game play very similar to that of Melee and Wizard from Metagaming.

Battles are fought using counters on a hex map, which are included with the game.
family: Series: Legends of Time and Space (Dark City Games)
Series: The Fantasy Trip (Steve Jackson Games)

description:
Three races are run on three different tracks. Six riders can participate, each leading one horse. One rider acts as the referee. Only three riders can participate; in that case, each rider leads two horses, and with only two riders, each leads three horses, one after the other. The riders roll dice; the rider with the highest roll receives number 1 in the tournament, and the remaining numbers follow according to the rolled number. On some sections, events hinder the players or they receive penalty points.


     Flat Race
     Obstacle Race
     Final


If a horse receives more than three penalty points, it is eliminated from the race. The scoring of all horses that cross the finish line is determined by the number of penalty points and their times.

—user summary

A group of friends are attending the Fuzzballs Pizza Party and are going to try and predict how many pizza slices there will be at Party Time.
First some initial hidden value cards are played, then use your cards to either raise or lower your prediction, or play abilities that modify the pizza count or other predictions.
Each action card will tick the clock closer to Party Time, at which point the player who has the closest prediction is declared the winner.

Welcome to the dungeon! Four Against Darkness is a best-selling solo dungeon-delving game. It may also be played cooperatively or with a GM. All you need is this book, a pencil, two dice, and grid paper. Choose four characters from a list of 20 classes, equip them, and venture into dungeons created by dice rolls and your choices. Fight monsters, manage resources, grab treasure, dodge traps, find clues, reveal secrets, and even accept quests from the monsters themselves. Your heroes will become more powerful with each game... IF THEY SURVIVE. This expanded edition brings in the game new classes, spells, foes, equipment, and environments. It includes two short adventures and all the tables required. Compatible with all the published supplements. 208 pages PDF

—description from the publisher

Will You Survive the Labile Labyrinth? Four hundred gold pieces for escorting a caravan across a valley populated by butterfly people. Who could refuse that? Of course, it won’t be that simple . The moth folk fiercely defend their valley with treacherous traps and bruising brigades. Not to mention the menacing mushroom men, weird temples, shape-changers, and the ominous Tower of the Screaming Virgins. Things wouldn’t be so bad were it not for the quarrelsome, alcoholic, lustful and dishonest crew members who you will have to keep an eye on. Add the bad roads, broken wheels, stolen cargo, tactical ambushes… and the llamas. Oh dear. Requires only the core Four Against Darkness book. This book contains adult humor, flamingos, and lecherous llamas.

—description from the publisher

Foes Like You Have Never Seen Before This book includes statistics, background info, tactics,and secrets of twelve of the most unusual creatures of Norindaal, suited for parties of all experience levels. Each monster has an illustration, unique reaction tables, loot, and story hooks to bring them into your games of Four Against Darkness. You will meet zombie wereshark hare-men, cactus men, forest guardians (like the Yadals, Kabuk, and the Chabuma), elemental minions like the fire-and-ice wielding Coal-Hearted Men, CandleWax Golems, undead terrors like Lila the hellfire girl and Neriya the handless fiddler, or the haunting Maze-Maker.

—description from the publisher

As the Demon Lord, you harness the power of Demon Essence to create and unleash your own demonic creatures, seeking to possess hapless villagers and establish dominance over the realm.

Objective: 
Possess as many villagers as possible to gain a high score. 
Gameplay: 
The gameplay consists of 7 rounds. Each round consists of the following:

1- draw and add villager cards
2- roll all of your minion dice
3- assign your minions
4- produce demon essence 
5- resolve villager cards

The game ends after completing 7 rounds or when your HP reaches 0.

At the end of the game, calculate your score as well as any bonuses collected.

The game was part of the 2023 solo, print and play contest.

Squad Cards is a team-based party game for 4 or more players that evolves the traditional "Fishbowl" experience. Designed for ages 17 and up due to its suggestive content, the game challenges two teams to communicate phrases through three increasingly difficult rounds using a shared, player-curated pool of cards.

The game begins with a unique preparation phase where each player is dealt a set number of cards from the deck. Players review these cards—familiarizing themselves with a portion of the total phrases that will be used throughout the game—before all selected cards are shuffled together into a single "Squad" draw pile. This creates a closed loop of information where every word or phrase in play has been seen by at least one person at the table.

The game is played over three distinct rounds: verbal description, one word, and charades. In each round, teams alternate turns. On a player's turn, they have one minute to get their teammates to guess as many cards as possible from the draw pile.

When the draw pile is exhausted, the round ends. All cards are then collected and reshuffled to form the draw pile for the next round. Each card correctly guessed earns the team one point. The game concludes immediately after the draw pile is exhausted for the third and final time. The team with the highest cumulative score across all three rounds is declared the winner.

boardgame: Escape Game Party: Le Manoir Hanté
description:
Escape Game Party: Le Manoir Hanté: Play an adventure in 45 minutes!

Having recently moved into a shared house, you thought you'd found the perfect place: an old building full of charm with incredibly low rent. 
A few weeks later, the truth becomes clear... 
You're living in a haunted mansion! 
Luckily, the ghosts aren't trying to harm you: they need your help.
Explore the premises, collect clues, unravel the mansion's mysteries, and bring peace to the wandering spirits, all in 45 minutes or less!

—description from the publisher

You are a member of I.M.P. - The Immediate Murder Professionals: A crew of ruthless, ridiculous, and surprisingly resourceful killers who assassinate Targets to pay the rent. Compete with your rival I.M.P.s to claim the most valuable souls.

Your Asmodean Crystal will get you up close and personal with the mortal realm via the Portal, but the Portal is fickle and can be manipulated by Spells and IMP-Terruption cards. At the end of each turn you will draw a card to replenish your hand, but beware! Members of the Hellaverse will randomly make an appearance and force players to claim the Target at their Crystal, and cause other unpredictable nonsense! Do whatever you’ve got to do to win. Friendship is a thing of the past! The only way to win is to look out for yourself, because when five Summon Cards are triggered, the Portal slams shut and everyone is sucked back to Hell!

This game is about winning, but a better way to think about it is making everyone else lose. Try to end your turn in the best possible position so that if you draw a character card, you are more likely to claim the Target in front of your Crystal. Use your Spells while you can and play your IMP-Terruptions at the most opportune moment for maximum chaos!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Escape Game party: Traque au Far West
description:
Escape Game party: Traque au Far West: play an adventure in 60 minutes.

The notorious criminal Lucie June has just been arrested by the sheriff after years on the run!

You must escort her to the courthouse. However, the journey is going to be eventful…

In this adventure, you will have to observe, manipulate, and solve puzzles, just like in a real escape game, to deliver the fugitive in a maximum of 60 minutes!

—description from the publisher

NPC Rivals is a deckbuilder. Play Hero, Loot, Monster, and Action cards from your growing deck to complete quests that require specific combinations. Each quest is added to your quest log to score big XP, but you’ll need to outplay your rivals in a constantly shifting marketplace to stay ahead. Cycle through quests to find the ones that suit your build, grab powerful cards from the market, and complete your quests before everyone else, but only one NPC will be crowned the real "main character."

In Picnic de la Reina, players take on the roles of ants from the same colony who must fulfill various supply orders to win their queen's favor. Divided into teams, these ants infiltrate a picnic in search of valuable resources.

Each player controls a different group of ants, and through the strategic use of cards, you'll explore the expansive picnic blanket to collect food that will grant you victory points. You must also meet the queen's demands to increase your chances of winning.

But beware of wasps, which will constantly try to bother you and steal your food.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Escape Game Party: Le Labyrinthe des Oubliés
description:
The Labyrinth of the Forgotten, an adventure to be played in 60 minutes!

A labyrinth, a multitude of possible paths, and no idea how you got there...

In this adventure, you'll have to observe, manipulate objects, and solve puzzles like in a real escape game, to escape this endless maze in a maximum of 60 minutes!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fokker Fodder: Rules for Dogfights with World War One Model Aeroplanes
description:
FOKKER FODDER: Rules for dogfights with World War One model aeroplanes.

Original rules created by Les Ward, developed by Chris Nicole, demonstrated and played by Humberside Wargames Society.

When we wrote these rules we wanted to produce a simple World War One air combat game to use as a participation game at conventions, and to run as a campaign game at the club. So the rules had to be simple to explain and play, but also give the feel of Western Front dogfights. As this was going to be a participation game we had already decided to use 1/72 scale aircraft on an 8 foot by 6 foot board. The game has ended up being very playable.

The rules are a bit deadly, as we intended that a participation game shouldn’t go on too long.
With four to six players it is possible to set up, play the game and clear away in a hour. We recommend the game to any wargames club as an easy one to introduce to new players, something to interest the members who have modelling skills, and a popular game to play at the start of a wargames session, while club members are waiting for others to turn up. We also suggest how you can run a campaign.

Feel free to experiment with these rules, ignore bits that you don't like, add extra rules and options. 
But remember, we've spent a lot of time getting the balance right with long sessions of playtesting. 
So have fun, and here's hoping that your ace pilot never spins into the ground!

Sequence Of Play For Each Round:
1 Initiative
Each player adds together their plane’s height band, current speed and the score of 1D6. Aces add +1 to the dice score, superaces add +2. If two or more players have the same total score, they each dice again to determine order
of movement between them, lowest score going first. Note that a plane’s acceleration for the round is added after
initiative has been determined during that plane's movement phase.
2 Spinning aircraft move first
These stay in the same square and lose 4 height bands. If they fall to height band 0 (or below!) then they have spun
into the ground and are destroyed. A spinning aircraft may not fire at any other aircraft (the crew have other things
to worry about.)
After you have made your spin move, you can attempt to recover from the spin. Throw 1D6 and if you score 4+ (aces 3+, superaces 2+) you have recovered from the spin. If you recover from a spin, your plane can face any direction you choose. If you fail to recover from a spin, your plane continues spinning next turn.
3 Gliding aircraft move
Gliding planes have 5 mp and turn C (or worse). They must lose at least 1 height band each round (this gives them a minimum of 6 mp.) If there are two or more gliders, then they dice between themselves to determine order of movement.
4 Other aircraft move
All remaining planes move and manoeuvre, in the order of their initiative total, lowest scores moving first.
Exception; If one aircraft is tailing another, then the tailing aircraft moves immediately after the aircraft being tailed.
See tailing.
5 Shooting
After all aircraft have moved, planes with a valid target in their arc of fire may attempt to fire any available guns at
the target. See shooting. The effects of shooting are simultaneous. So, even if you destroy an enemy aircraft, he
may still have fired back at you!
6 Control panel
Surviving planes record any changes in height, adjust their current speed and note damage status and ammunition
used on their 'control panel'.
7 Start the next round.

Each round 2 cards are pitted against each other and the players must choose: This/That. The card with the most votes "wins" and anyone that didn't vote for it got it "wrong" and takes a strike.

In round 2 the stakes are raised.  All of the winning cards from the previous round return to be pitted against each other. This time you take 2 strike for a "wrong" answer.

Rounds continue ramping up until a single winning card is selected and the player with the fewest strikes wins the game!

LEGACY:
In each game the top 4 cards are set aside in a special tuckbox labeled "THESE." Whenever you have enough cards in there from previous games you're able to play a game with the creme de la creme - the very best of the best.

—description from the designer
family: Organizations: Game Designers of North Carolina

description:
Uncharted Skies is a 2-4 player tile flipper where players compete to control a shared and changing night sky.

On a given turn, players will have three flips to match up constellations from the 5x5 board with the cards in their hand. As the game goes on, the board will change and players will make discoveries leading to points and bonuses. The astronomer with the most points by the end, wins.

boardgame: Penjami ja Jepulis maakunnassa: Päijät-Häme pursuit
description:
Welcome to the Finnish region of Päijät-Häme! In this game, players are traveling with the cartoon characters Penjami and Jepulis, answering trivia in this region of eleven municipalities - Asikkala, Hartola, Heinola, Hollola, Hämeenkoski, Kärkölä, Nastola, Lahti, Orimattila, Padasjoki and Sysmä - grouped around the southern end of lake Päijänne. Players start in the largest city and regional capital, Lahti, all questions are multiple choice with three possibilities, and a right answer nets you the card of the municipality with the coat-of-arms, motto, and some basic facts about the place. The player with the most cards after all have been distributed wins!

This game suitable for all ages was published by the regional council of Päijät-Häme as part of an EU-supported project in a print run of 2000 copies. A Christmas book, "Penjami ja Jepulis Joulukylässä" that ties in to the theme and has the same two main characters was published for the preceding Christmas of 2011 by the same designer-artist duo.

Note: As the game is from 2012 the list of municipalities may seem a bit odd to the modern viewer; there are currenly only ten municipalities in the region, namely Asikkala, Hartola, Heinola, Hollola, Iitti, Kärkölä, Lahti, Orimattila, Padasjoki and Sysmä. Iitti does not appear in the game as it was transferred to the Päijät-Häme region from the Kymenlaakso region in 2021, and since the publication of the game Hämeenkoski has been absorbed by Hollola, while Nastola is now a part of Lahti.

Note2: The somewhat odd names of the main characters (who have no actual role in the game, except for box and board art) are a reference to a famous and eccentric sewage collector in early 20th century Lahti, nicknamed "Jepulis-Penjami". There are many great stories still circulating about him in the city!

The 90s cliché teen horror flick board game!

There is a KILLER on the loose in the town of CREEPERTIN, and the town has been evacuated.
All except for a handful of determined, teenage high-school stereotypes.

These teens have one objective: SURVIVE. The KILLER is here to KILL (duh.)

The TEENS must put aside their differences and work together if they want to escape with their lives.

This is a game of hunt or be hunted.
Kill or be killed.
Life or death.

This is Horror High.

High school seniors face an unrelenting and murderous killer in a desperate gambit for survival in this nostalgic
asymmetrical survival horror board game.

Base Game Includes:

     8 playable Teens with unique abilities and accessories.
     4 playable Killers with terrifying abilities and ultimate attacks.
     6 Escape Routes to power up and use to survive!
     100 Twist Cards to shake up the game!


—description from the designer

Dark Cases: Feuerfest is a cooperative mystery deduction game for 1 to 6 players set in the fictional German town of Hagenkirchen. The narrative begins with a town in crisis: the mayor has been missing for 48 hours, a forest barn has burned to the ground, and an unidentified body with severe burns has been discovered among the ruins. Amidst the town's annual Midsummer bonfire celebrations, players take on the role of an expert investigative team called in to assist the local police in solving these interconnected mysteries.
The primary goal of the game is to solve the crime by correctly identifying the perpetrator and reconstructing the events leading to the fire and disappearance. To achieve this, players must answer four specific final questions through a digital evaluation system, which then provides a concluding audio summary of the findings.
Gameplay is immersive and non-linear, utilizing a hybrid of physical and digital components. Players examine roughly 15 to 20 realistic "analog" documents, including autopsy reports, crime scene photos, witness statements, newspaper clippings, and local maps. These clues are supplemented by digital research, requiring players to use an internet-enabled device to access emails, social media articles, and audio files. Unlike many escape-room-style games, Feuerfest focuses on deduction and combining information rather than linear logic puzzles, and no materials are destroyed during play, making the game fully reusable.

First part of the game is that its a turned based two player card game where you customize your deck like Magic: the Gathering. you build a base with your cards placing down economic and production buildings. you create units from the production buildings and send them to conquer three relic sites. first player to conquer all three sites wins the game. there is more to it like spells and building upgrades.

Second part of the game are the battles which are a game in itself using the "Chaotic Combat System" Units have individual stats and you use dice to determine battles like Warhammer 40k. There are five factions - Humans, Goblins, Dwarves, Elves, and Undead. Each faction has unique units.

Game is replayable and no two games are the same due to the variety of base configurations, unit combos, spells and movement strategy like risk. I'm thinking a game like chess that has stood the test of time, so wont get boring.

—description from the designer

Each player gets a deck of pictures.

The decks are identical, and the players split up into teams of two.

Your goal is to match with your teammate by choosing and arranging the same pics that illustrate an announced theme:

Same pic + same position = +3 spaces
Same pic + different positions = +2 spaces
No match: 0 spaces

The first team to reach the final space wins and is declared the most matchy matchy duo!

The Twisted expansion introduces a complete solo mode for Onward: The MOBA Game. Face 32 Twisted Heroes and the Outsider as it joins the fight in the lanes. Then add a Twist or Wild Twist to push the experience further with unique rules and alternate setups.

Flipping Fortune is a quirky push-your-luck deck-building game about tarot reading. Tell customers their fortunes by flipping tarot cards from your deck. The better you meet the fortunes the customers seek, the happier they leave. But don‘t overdo it and stay realistic or your tarot reading busts.
Serving customers gains you fortune cookies, that you can spend to improve your deck with more tarot cards and strong effects. Play five rounds and advance your tarot deck to win the game.

—description from the designer

Wild West skirmish rules, for use on a 3' x 3' table.  One figure represents one character.  Run a small band and try to out-do the other team.  You win by robbing the bank, stealing cattle, capturing your foe or winning the shootout.  Each character has their own skills and weapons which they bring to the fight that can help you succeed.

A simple and fast-play set of rules using d6 for most activities.

Comes with 7 scenarios to get you started.

A simple memory and matching game for young children. Players have a shopping basket card showing different groceries to collect. In turns, a player flips one face-down tile from the market and shows it to everyone.

If the revealed market tile matches an item still missing in the player’s basket, the tile is placed onto the corresponding spot in that basket. If it does not match, the tile is returned face down to the market. The first player to fill their basket wins.

The market also contains one-time action tiles that add small twists (e.g., secretly looking at tiles, shuffling the market, removing a tile from another player’s basket, or using a bread tile as a temporary wildcard).

In a galaxy of constant warfare and exploitation, there's one thing everyone can agree on: DreadBall All-Stars is the most brutal, spectacular sport ever created.

Billions tune in every week to watch legends clash on the neodurium pitch. Corporations spend fortunes sponsoring teams (mostly for tax breaks), while fans mortgage their futures for a chance to see their heroes in action.

Command a team of All-Stars in head-to-head games across the galaxy’s most hi-tech arenas. Will you choose the sleek speed of the Jets? Or dominate with the sheer power of the Juggernauts? Outscore your rival in a fast, brutal sport of tactics, chaos, and glory.

Coming to Kickstarter: https://www.kickstarter.com/projects/manticgames/dreadball-all-stars-the-sci-fi-sports-game

Комитет ("Committee") is a cooperative narrative-driven board game set in an alternate-history Soviet Union during the late 1950s and early 1960s. Players take on the roles of agents working for a secret organization tasked with investigating unexplained phenomena and maintaining stability across the country.

Each game is played as a scenario in which players collectively manage action points, travel between cities on the map, resolve incidents, and complete objectives while dealing with ongoing routine responsibilities. Decisions are made cooperatively, and success depends on careful planning, prioritization, and teamwork.

The game emphasizes story progression, thematic events, and shared decision-making, with each scenario presenting new challenges and narrative developments.

Pálinka is a thematic board game that immerses players in the world of traditional Hungarian fruit brandy making. Players take on the role of master distillers, competing to create the finest and most prestigious pálinkas.

During the game, players collect various fruits, guide them through the processes of fermentation and distillation, and refine their products through meaningful decisions. The game’s mechanics are tightly integrated with its theme, ensuring that every action has a clear purpose.

Pálinka features easy-to-learn rules paired with engaging strategic choices. The game is enjoyable for both beginners and experienced players, bringing a rarely explored and distinctly Hungarian theme to the table — seasoned with a pinch of humor, meaningful decisions, and an authentic pálinka atmosphere.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ants & Crumbles: Battle at the Beehive
description:
Battle in the Beehive is built on the same core gameplay as Ants & Crumbles, but the experience feels entirely different. With its new board and object system, it plays like a complete game in its own right rather than a simple expansion.

Victory can be claimed in two ways:

     Dominate the picnic by eliminating all rival colonies and being the last one standing.
     Feast in glory by collecting enough crumbles before anyone else.


This expansion replaces the temporary items on the board with two new bee standees that stay on the board and affect movement until relocated. They are relocated with an additional bee die.
Besides that a new board with hexagonal fields replaces the original board.

boardgame: Ants & Crumbles: A Table Full of Perks & Hazards
description:
A Table Full of Perks & Hazards introduces a fresh layer of Perk and Hazard effects that sit on top of the existing gameplay. Designed to work seamlessly with both the Base Game and the Expansion, without changing the core rules.

Candy Tokens are dropped onto the board, tempting your ants with a sweet sugar boost they simply can’t resist. But every treat comes with a risk: these tokens can trigger powerful perks or nasty hazards.

Biotopia is a family-friendly board game in which players create diverse natural habitats to attract animals. By placing terrain tiles and carefully managing water, players build interconnected ecosystems where wildlife can thrive.

Throughout the game, players work toward both shared and individual objectives, encouraging interaction and flexible strategies. Each decision influences the value of the habitats and the animals that settle there. The winner is the player who creates the most valuable and well-balanced biotopia by the end of the game.

Biotopia offers accessible rules, meaningful choices, and a calm yet engaging experience for families and casual gamers alike.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Monopoly: Ozzy – Back to the Beginning
description: Monopoly: Ozzy is a Monopoly game with theme around the artist Ozzy Osbourne. In this version of Monopoly players must buy up property and build homes in the Ozzy universe. This games has  six unique custom-molded game tokens and a themed game board with artwork and song titles.

Verona is a highly interactive strategy board game set in the city of Verona between the 14th and 16th centuries. Players develop their own districts by constructing buildings, managing workers, and competing fiercely for limited action spaces.

Each player controls workers with values from 1 to 5, which are placed on action spaces through a bidding-like system. Higher-value workers can displace others, creating constant tension and confrontation. The game features four main action spaces: a rondel for acquiring buildings at varying costs, a space for gaining money, one for recruiting assistants, and another for constructing powerful monuments.

Buildings are placed into a personal 5×5 grid, where players aim to create diverse and efficient districts. Citizens can also be settled into districts, providing ongoing benefits and strategic advantages. Victory comes from clever planning, tactical placement, and mastering direct competition on the action spaces.

—description from the publisher

All You Can Chili is a competitive card game focused on bluffing, risk management, and deduction.

Each player is secretly associated with a character who has a randomly assigned spice tolerance threshold.

During each turn, players play cards that add spice points to the characters in play. 
If the total spice value on a character exceeds its hidden tolerance threshold, that character loses control.

Players must observe opponents’ actions, estimate hidden thresholds, and decide when to push the heat and when to hold back.

—description from the publisher

Banner Blood is a semi-cooperative candlepunk fantasy adventure game set in a dangerous fictional world where powerful technologies are fueled by your blood. Blood acts as both your health and the fuel for an equipment set that gets completely chosen by the player. The world is hostile, with each province in play chosen by a different player.

Gameplay is simultaneous. Each round, players move across the modular world to each resolve a random individual quest while  drawing Journey cards. Each Journey is either an event, resolved by using innate abilities on equipped devices, or combat where you must strategically allocate blood cubes to your devices to attack enemy groups. Meanwhile, each quest introduces a terrible World Event that adds obstacles to the map for all players.

Although players must ultimately work together to defeat a Final Boss, real victory is not shared. Throughout the game, players collect banners by achieving feats to build individual notoriety. When the Final Boss is defeated, the heroes return to the capital for a Hero Ceremony where banners determine which adventurer is awarded the Banner Medal and declared the true hero in a performance by the other players.

Banner Blood emphasizes shared danger, asymmetric goals, and dynamic load-out choices, creating a tense experience where cooperation is suggested, selfishness is constant, and player choice merged with random events create a unique experience each session.

—description from the publisher

Scripta is a strategic card game for 2 to 5 players where you take on the role of a scholar deciphering ancient texts to forge your arcane mastery. Over 20-minute sessions, players explore secret libraries, managing their hand and sets of "Grimoire" cards.

In each turn, you will study by drawing cards from public libraries or a private collection, learn by revealing combinations (sets or runs) to add to your "Arcane Knowledge," and contribute back to the libraries. Each card added to your knowledge activates powerful effects that allow you to manipulate the deck or copy abilities. Victory is achieved by being the first to complete a specific sequence of five numbers, collecting four of a kind, or reaching a total of 35 points.

boardgame: Paws and Portals: The Deck of Destiny
description:
Paws & Portals: The Deck of Destiny started with a simple question: what if the game didn’t pretend to be fair?

Most tabletop games carefully balance turns, probabilities, and outcomes so that control feels evenly shared. This game does the opposite. It hands authority to a deck of cards and asks players to react, adapt, and occasionally laugh when plans collapse. The goal was not randomness for its own sake, but visible momentum — moments where pressure builds, breaks, and resets in ways players can feel immediately at the table.

Removing dice was a deliberate choice. Cards are familiar, readable, and dramatic. 
When a high card wins a fight, everyone sees it. When Overdrive spirals, it’s exciting because it might end at any moment. When it does end, it ends cleanly. 
That rhythm — surge, pause, reshuffle — is the engine of the game.

Cats and Dogs were chosen not just for charm, but because their personalities naturally support asymmetry. 
Cats reward speed, timing, and audacity. 
Dogs reward durability, cooperation, and commitment. 
Neither side is “better”; they simply thrive under different kinds of pressure. 
The campaign scenarios were written to explore those pressures in different ways: speed, control, and endurance.

Finally, the setting — a house turned battlefield — exists to lower the barrier to play. Furniture becomes terrain. Cushions become objectives. The game works just as well on a kitchen table as it does on a carefully built board.

If this game does its job, you won’t remember who won. You’ll remember why things went wrong, when the deck betrayed you, and the exact moment everyone realised it was already too late.

That’s not a flaw.

That’s the point

Gameplay:
There are no turns.
Everything in this game is controlled by one standard 52-card deck plus two Jokers.
Activations. Combat. Reactions. Magic. All of it. If a card is drawn, it came from this deck. When the card is done causing trouble, discard it.
When the round ends, discard everything, reshuffle, and pretend this was the plan.
Red cards activate Cats.
Black cards activate Dogs.
Jokers activate whoever the deck feels like, which is usually the worst possible option.
Jokers never end rounds. They simply arrive, ruin the mood, and leave.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Slay the Spire: The Board Game – Exclusives
description:
Slay the Spire: The Board Game – Exclusives contains all stretch goals from the Kickstarter campaign.

Contains:

     1 Merchant Playmat (3 mm neoprene with stitched edges 219x156 mm)
     3 Heart Keys (acrylic)
     32 Foil cards (duplicates of cards from the base game)
     10 Blank cards (for your creative ideas)
     1 Claw Pack (9 cards)
     1 Claw Die

Machinations is a modular expansion for Age of Quarrel. It keeps the base game’s structure intact, but changes how power is expressed. Some modules add direct disruption. Others deepen the economy, or sharpen Middelalderen’s influence.

Subterfuge: Indirect Warfare.
Win leverage without marching an army. Subterfuge rewards timing and targeting weak seams in a rival’s situation. Used well, it breaks momentum, forces defensive attention, and punishes plans that rely on being left alone.

Mills: Engines of Commerce.
Mills turn parts of the map into long-term economic engines. They encourage specialization and commitment, which raises the stakes of territory control. Taking a clearing can mean taking a working machine, not just denying space.

Roles: Instruments of Influence.
Roles add a political current to Middelalderen. Staying connected matters more, and the controller gains a new kind of leverage that isn’t just military. Roles create moments where power is granted, traded, or resented, all driven by board position.

Barbarian Accords: Tribute & Pacts.
Barbarian Accords adds a Rapport sideboard and Parley rules that let players negotiate with the Barbarian Tribes. Players can build Rapport, pay Tribute to avert attacks, and secure temporary Pacts that alter Barbarian targeting while easing movement and In Supply pressure.

Into the Unknown:
Fifteen additional Exploration cards.

Machinations is for groups who want conflict that reaches beyond direct battle. It makes infrastructure feel riskier to expose, strengthens non-military pressure, and gives players more ways to shape what the table will be dealing with next Season.

-description from designer

Idlewild is a card game designed by Oddlab Games, featuring original illustrations by Tina Nawrocki.

Gameplay has players take turns stacking colour cards on the field to match recipes of flower cards. Each flower card is worth a given number of points, which are placed into a player's pot and scored at the end of each round. Points can be used to pay for special Shed cards, which can advance or hinder players' game plans.

Each player's turn has a weather forecast, dictated by a revealed weather card. Three of the same weather cards in a row (rainy or sunny) or a wild card (frosty, windy, or lucky) triggers a weather event, which may destroy a player's flowers or impact them in other ways.

A game consists of four rounds of five to seven turns, depending on the number of players. At the end of the four rounds, the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher

The board game Loupežníci 2 is designed for a minimum of 2 and a maximum of 5 players. It consists of a three-piece game board depicting a path that represents the journey from the dragoon’s headquarters to the robbers’ castle. The path is made up of yellow spaces, with 31 red spaces placed among them.
The goal of the game is to guide your dragoon (soldier) as quickly as possible to the robbers’ stronghold and back, overcoming all obstacles along the way and capturing as many robbers as you can.

Who will crown themselves as the master thief of Artifox?
Step into the bustling city of Artifox Famous for it's beautifull Artifacts. As a fox living on the street, life is tough, but your cunning and courage can take you far! You dream of more, and you know that by perfecting the art of stealing, you can amass great wealth. Do you dare to take on the challenge?

Artifox is a strategic and interactive card game for 2 players that takes you on a whirlwind adventure full of cunning and deception. Start with a  base deck and traverse the city to steal from various shops. Each card stolen will be added to your hand and eventually your deck. Use these tools, tricks or traps to manipulate the field and steal valuable loot. Guards are on the lookout so be carefull!

However, you are not the only one eyeing the city’s treasures. Your opponent is after the same loot and will do everything possible to outsmart you and steal your findings!

Have you gathered a Trick, Tool & Trap? Then it’s time to steal Mayor Silverfang's treasures! These valuable artifacts are added to your deck and can be stolen by your Opponnent. When no Artifacts are left at Silverfang Manor, the fox with the most Artifacts wins!

—description from the publisher

Returning from the game Keep the Heroes Out are 8 unlikely allies to add to your game of Flame & Forge!

Included are the following:

8x Allies
8x Special Ability Tokens
16x  Monster Cards
1x Vortex Card

boardgame: Keep the Heroes Out!: Dungeons Below
description:
This expansion includes the following:

6x New Scenarios
3x Boss Battles
3x Unlockable Monsters
14x Meeples
60+ Cards

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Spidiculeg Boil Recipe Promo
description:
Additional recipe that may be used with the Cooking Story Event found in the Core Game

Spidiculeg Boil

1 Exoskeleton
1 Venom Sack
1 Crab Spider
1 Fresh Acanthus

Benefit: reduce damage for every hit suffered by 1, to a minimum of 1 (record this)

HOW DO YOU MAKE A GAME ABOUT THE GREAT CITY OF GOOSE CREEK, SOUTH CAROLINA?

While it’s impossible to include everything, we jammed all the Goose Creek we could into this box. It's full of outdoor adventures, family attractions, rich history and of course... the COOPER RIVER - a little something for everyone! Looking for a fun way to experience Goose Creek?

HERE’S A QUICK IDEA OF HOW TO PLAY!

Buy your favorite Goose Creek Properties, increase your rent by collecting City Blocks and trading them in for Keys to the City. Sound easy? Well, add in some traffic jams, property tax and the IRS and it gets a little more difficult and a lot more fun!

GET YOUR GAME ON!

Choose your token and advance to Go Goose Creek! You may soon be the Mayor... or out of the game for eating too many Apple Fritters from Donut Connection!

THIS IS GOOSE CREEK... AND IT'S YOUR ROLL!

—description from the back of the box
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: O Bom, o Mau e o Bode: Carta Promo Chefe
description:
The Chefe (Chief) Promo Card, distributed at the 2024 Diversão Offline (DOFF) board game fair by the publisher Grok.

---

Rule Changes:
During Stage 4 of Preparation, shuffle the Chief along with the other Character Cards to form the draw deck.

In Phase 1: Supply, when removing Character Cards from the deck, remove three cards instead of two; two of them are face down and one is face up.

Remember: if the face-up card is the Goat, it must be replaced with another card from the deck.

New Character Effect
The Chief includes a new type of effect, the Delayed Effect. Delayed Effects are indicated by the hourglass icon and are activated at the end of the Saloon Phase, after all Characters in the Saloon have had their effects resolved.

Chief
Unlike other Characters, the Chief does not have a corresponding Location. He is positioned next to a Location chosen by the player controlling him at the time he is revealed.

Delayed Effect: Collect all Belonging Tokens OR Coins from the location where the Chief is.

If the location has no Coins or Belonging Tokens, too bad, you receive nothing.

The party game Ma se fossi... involves players asking ridiculous questions and pointing fingers to win! There are no judges in this game. The majority wins! The active player draws a question card and reads it aloud. Then all players vote. Each player in the majority gets one point and can brag to the other players!

With the death of the King of Tessera, turmoil stirs in the land. Champions must rise from throughout the realm to establish the next reign over the kingdom.

Regascent: Contest of Champions, captures the Adventure these Champions will embark on and the Arena they will compete in.

Players choose between four Champions, various draft cards, and allies to begin their epic journey to ascend the throne and claim the crown for the good of Tessera.

Complete Quests, skirmish bandits, collect gear, gain Renown, and finally, stand your ground against your opponents in a daunting Arena battle.

The last Champion standing wins the crown and the right to rule. Tessera will enjoy another reign of a worthy king. Do you have the wit and might to rule Tessera?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Flames of War: Dunkirk – Complete WWII Starter Set: German vs British
description:
Flames of War: Dunkirk: Both the British and Germans excelled in combined arms fighting, coordinating armour and infantry, with each arm bringing their complementary skills to the battle.

In this set, you can command fast moving British Cruiser tanks and well-drilled riflemen against well-armoured German StuG assault guns and veteran infantry platoons.

Can the British push the Germans back out of France, or will the onslaught of Germans prevail?

—description from the publisher

A premium big box for Kingdom Builder and all its Queenies released thru 2025 which includes Kingdom Builder: Queenie 4 – The Emperor's Choice that was not included in the last big box release (Kingdom Builder: Big Box (Second Edition)).

Tiles are about 20% smaller in size compared to the previous release. Comes with a Folded Space Insert.

The graphics will match those found in Kingdom Builder: Family & Friends Compact Edition.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
This tile-placement game is themed around town development in the Bitchū Fukiya district (Hiroshima City, Okayama Prefecture).

The red pigment “Bengara,” accidentally created in Fukiya, brought immense wealth to this land, leading the town to peak prosperity.
The wealthy merchants who amassed fortunes through the local Bengara trade painted their residences and various structures red, creating a distinctive townscape resembling a crimson corridor.
Today, this nostalgic and beautiful red landscape, along with the progressive ideas that shaped it, continues to deeply impress visitors.

System Features
BENGARA is a head-to-head game for two players.
Each player acts as a wealthy merchant establishing themselves in Fukiya, placing tiles featuring various buildings onto a shared board to develop the town.
Players secure Bengara pigment and funds needed for construction while fulfilling the diverse building requirements listed on Request Cards to earn victory points.

One key system feature is the simultaneous action selection and turn order auction.
Players decide whether to prioritize turn order for the next round or take a more powerful action this round, all while observing each other's situations.
Additionally, the main action of tile placement includes elements like bonus resources gained by fulfilling conditions, various benefits, and the ‘Completion of an Area’ feature that yields victory points.

BENGARA is a game for two players that offers deep, strategic gameplay.
Furthermore, by the end of the game, a beautiful landscape of various red buildings will be created on the table.

boardgame: Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Premium Big Box
description: A premium big box for Escape: The Curse of the Temple and all its Queenies released through 2025 which includes Queenies 12–17 that weren't included in the last big box release (Escape: The Curse of the Temple – Big Box 2nd Edition).
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: 時空旅行の時代 (Era of Time Travel)
description:
The Era of Time Travel is a worker placement game where players move across world cards to gather resources. By using resources to build bases across time and space, players can obtain resources more efficiently.

Players use their pieces to acquire resources while moving across randomly selected world cards, expanding their personal boards and placing base pieces. Carrying on the professor's legacy of enabling time travel, players restore the world using hourly-shift robots.

Capture beautiful landscapes inside countless frames. Draw the shape shown on the revealed Block card, and create an empty space that matches the shape on the specified Frame card. Make the most of the abilities you gain, collect various bonuses, and aim for a high score.

Spikers! is a high-tension tactical volleyball duel for two players, designed as a solo project under the SteamWhale studio. In this game, players step into the shoes of coaches managing a six-player squad, where every inch of the court and every point of stamina counts.

The game features a unique Inertia and Speed system: as the rally intensifies, the speed of the ball dictates how many actions players can take, forcing tough decisions between positioning, resting, or committing to a powerful strike. With a dice-pool mechanic that includes "Exploding Criticals," coaches must balance raw power with control—pushing too hard might lead to "Uncontrolled Force," handing a point to the opponent. From managing player fatigue to executing advanced rotations and blocks, SPIKERS! brings the authentic, fast-paced strategy of professional volleyball to the tabletop.

—description from the publisher

Neologism is a fast and playful party game where words don’t exist… until you invent them.

Each round, players create a completely new word and then try to predict which absurd definition the majority of the table will associate with it. Creativity helps, but intuition and group psychology matter even more: the goal isn’t to be right, it’s to think like everyone else.

Quick to learn and easy to play, Neologism turns language into a shared joke and guarantees surprising interpretations, unexpected votes, and lots of laughter.

—description from the publisher

The quick folding quiz cleverly combines trivia questions and origami. Do you know the answer? Then fold your paper, that only the answer is visible! Hurry up – the fastest player wins. But be careful: thanks to slanted lines, bright colours and a jumble of numbers, you'll be wrinkling your forehead faster than you can say ‘paper aeroplane’.

—description from the publisher

Das schnelle Knick-Quiz mischt clever Wissensfragen und Origami. Weißt du die Antwort? Dann falte dein Papier so, dass nur noch sie zu sehen ist! Beeil dich – der schnellste Spieler gewinnt. Aber Vorsicht: Dank schräger Linien, leuchtender Farben und Zahlenwirrwarr legst du flotter die Stirn in Falten, als du „Papierflieger“ sagen kannst.

—description from the publisher (German)

boardgame: Hasiči, hadice a žebříky: Pat a Mat
description:
Pat & Mat: Firefighters – Hoses and Ladders (Czech: Hasiči, hadice a žebříky) is a thematic racing game for children based on the classic "Snakes and Ladders" mechanic. The game features characters from the popular Czech stop-motion animated series Pat & Mat.

In this version, the two handymen take on the roles of firefighters. Players move their character pieces across the game board based on the roll of a six-sided die, attempting to reach the final square to extinguish a fire.

Gameplay Mechanisms:
- Ladders: If a player's piece lands at the base of a ladder, the player moves their piece to the square at the top of the ladder.
- Hoses: If a player's piece lands on a square featuring a tangled hose, the player must move their piece down to a designated lower square.

The game is language-independent and designed for young children and families.

In Jatra, you and your fellow players take on the role of siblings dropped off at a fair, each eager to make the most of the day in your own way. Your goal is to complete sets - eating food, going on rides, watching spectacles, and winning toys, all before the others do. But childhood never follows a plan. Memories resurface at unexpected times, disrupting your progress, changing how you play, and forcing you to adapt.

Designed for 3–5 players, Jatra unfolds over five distinct stages, each altering what you’re trying to achieve and how memory cards affect you. What feels overwhelming early on slowly becomes manageable, even familiar. Strategies that worked before may no longer apply. Just like growing up, the game changes while you’re busy playing it.

Every turn is a small decision with emotional weight. Do you push ahead and complete a set, or slow down to deal with a memory that could trip you up later? Do you take a risk now, or play it safe and watch someone else surge ahead? Jatra balances competition with co-operation; there’s always tension at the table, but never without warmth.

—description from the publisher

Fruit Mix is a clever combination card game for 2 or 4 players. Play alone or in teams. Add to your partner's stacks and complete your own fruit sequences or fruit groups. You build the stacks with number sequences or rows of different juicy fruits. If you have jokers or honey, you can use them tactically to win the game with the highest score. A classic card game with a fruity new twist.

—description from the back of the box

What is Fruit Mix about?

Collect as many points as possible by forming sequences or groups and completing them. Sequences are at least 3 consecutive cards of the same colour. Groups are at least 3 cards with the same number, regardless of colour. To score points, complete the sequences/groups with at least 6 cards or get even more points by completing them with 10 cards. The game consists of 3 rounds. The team with the highest total score wins.

How do you win the game?
The game consists of 3 rounds. The aim of each round is to score as many points as possible and either finish the game quickly to prevent your opponents from scoring, or continue playing until there are no more cards left. Add up the total score from all rounds. The team with the highest score wins the game.

—description from the publisher

A stifling day in the big city. a lone P.I. prowls the mean streets, trying to keep one step ahead of the cops and rival shamuses. The case had seemed so simple at first, but considering the recovered documents, footprints, and that lone key... it’s clear that this was merely the entrance to a complex labyrinth. All you can do is keep asking questions. Search the harbor, chinatown, and main street. Watch the central station, civic center and cafes. Keep trying to piece it together, keep asking questions...

P.I. (Second Edition) is a new remake of the legendary deduction classic by Martin Wallace.

This second edition, released after 15 years, features brand-new artwork and introduces a dedicated two-player head-to-head mode. In addition, by scanning the QR code in the rulebook and downloading the companion app, players can enjoy a solo time-attack mode in which an App hides the clues for you to uncover.

In more detail..
In the original edition, scoring was calculated at the end of each round by summing points based on the success or failure of deductions made during that round.

In the new edition, points are tracked in real time for each individual deduction attempt, according to the result. In the first edition, points were awarded in descending order based on the order in which players successfully solved the case. In the second edition, however, once the first player successfully makes a deduction, the maximum number of points available begins to decrease with each subsequent round. This adjustment was made to allow for a faster pace of play in line with current design trends.

In the two-player mode, the game is no longer decided by accumulated points. Instead, each round is determined by whether a player’s deduction attempt succeeds, and the first player to win two out of three rounds wins the game. However, winning a round requires returning a hint token, which creates a penalty in the following round. For this purpose, the reverse side of the board is used, along with a dedicated scoreboard for determining round victories.

The solo mode represents the most significant change compared to the previous version. It introduces a real-time deduction format, where players score based on how quickly and accurately they solve the clues. Results can be uploaded to a server, allowing players to compete against others through a leader board system.

boardgame: Zoo Tycoon: The Board Game – Dinosaur Digs
description:
Zoo Tycoon: The Board Game - Dinosaur Digs is a new standalone board game set in the world of Zoo Tycoon. In this highly thematic dinosaur park simulation, each player builds their own dinosaur park in front of them and must keep an eye on animal welfare, revenue, popularity, science, and the danger level. Dinosaur Digs uses familiar mechanics such as set collection, worker placement, and tile placement, but also introduces completely new mechanics.

The game is played over several years (rounds), during which players perform different actions depending on the season. Players breed dinosaurs in the laboratory, obtain new dinosaurs/fossils from various dig sites, expand their zoos with new enclosures and buildings, improve the security of their fences, and must always keep an eye on their finances.

The player who does this best and maintains a balance between science and popularity wins the game.

-description from the publisher

Beneath the fallen stones of an ancient cathedral, rival rat clans wage an endless struggle for control of the ruins.

In this dueling card game, players build rat clans by equipping their rats with powerful relics. 
The core mechanic revolves around combining rat and relic cards to form unique units, each with its own abilities and synergies.

On a turn, a player either draws cards from the rat and relic decks or plays a pair of cards from their hand, gradually expanding their presence on the table.
As the game progresses, each player controls an increasing number of rats, which begin to form an engine shaped by their chosen strategy. Different card combinations offer varied tactical approaches, creating multiple viable paths to victory in every match

To win the game, a player must successfully play one of several powerful relics while meeting its specific conditions. Relics are divided into Weapons, Arcane, and Trinkets, and each category represents a distinct victory condition, encouraging players to commit to a strategy while adapting to their opponent’s plans.

—description from the designer

Are you looking to develop your dungeon crawling skills and to gain access to even more dangerous monsters and devious traps? In the Lairs: Adventurer’s Pack, you’ll add all sorts of challenging new content to your Lairs base game. Unique attribute cubes unlock powerful abilities, and you’ll need them to overcome the original monsters and traps your opponent will have at their disposal. Add the new content all at once or unlock the boxes and envelopes in stages, introducing it gradually. The Adventure is up to you!

Bigger Dungeons! Weird Walls! BOSS MONSTERS! Do you want to take your dungeon delving to the next level (down, that is)? Lairs: Deeper Dungeons features all the fun ideas we just couldn’t fit in the Lairs base box. Add Characters with their own unique abilities. Play with three and even four players in multi-player mode. Work your way through difficult Terrain and fresh Challenges. There is so much more to discover in Lairs: Deeper Dungeons.

boardgame: Mischwald: Rotkäppchen / Großer, böser Wolf Promo Card
description:
Little Red Riding Hood / Big Bad Wolf Promo Card
PX25 - Christmas 2025 German Promo Card

Little Red Riding Hood
Scores: 10 Points for each Mushroom symbol.

Big Bad Wolf
Effect: Draw a card for each Boot symbol from the deck.
Bonus: Extra Turn
Scores: 5 Points for each Boot symbol.
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
One Last Fight is a card-driven roguelike, GM-less roleplaying game where 1 to 4 players take on the role of doomed adventurers on theit final quest to defeat their ultimate Nemesis or die trying.

Players create their own adventurer's backstories, establish the story of their Nemesis, and work together to overcome a gauntlet of Challenges 8both physical and personal) until they find themselves in their Nemesis' Lair. There the adventurers meet their fate and battle their nemesis head to head.

The players take turns to roll and move. The first to reach space 50 wins the game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

The Game was published as a supplement to the magazine "Der Hausfreund".

—user summary

boardgame: Gold Country: Bona Fide Bonanza Promo Pack
description:
Competition between the mines is relentless, and further riches can be gained by helping them achieve their ambitions … It’s a bona-fide bonanza! This mini expansion adds asymmetric company goals to the experience! Players can commit to one or more company goals (or even double down on the same company goal) which function like a bet of sorts. If a goal is achieved — major payouts are in store for those who committed to the goal. The first players to commit to a goal will also receive a higher payout. If the goal is failed, then a penalty must be payed!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mythwind: Sprites & Sprouts Event Card Pack
description: A pack of event cards for from the Sprites & Sprouts Kickstarter.

Shajarat is a solo tile-laying game, where you have to place magical trees (a walking olive tree, a floating baobab...) next to each other, while respecting their requirements (the walking tree needs one free space next to it, the flying baobab needs to be next to non-floating trees, etc...).

In each game, you'll play with only 4 of the 8 included species of trees, so you'll have no less than 70 different set-up possibilities, each one with its own (re-playable) challenge.

A relaxing brain-burner, set in an enchanted eastern setting (Shajarat means tree in Arabic).

As the valley evolves, unique foliage and undiscovered ores emerge! How will your town adjust to the price of success?

LINKED GAMEPLAY
Experience a cascading story engine where Adventure Cards introduce Goal Cards, which then unlock new Event Cards.

EVOLVING VALLEY
Your choices matter. From mining rushes to culinary festivals, the events of the valley unfold based on how you guide the town.

SEAMLESS INTEGRATION 
Shuffle the new Adventure cards into your deck at any time!

—description from the publisher

Hell of a Deal is a cooperative poker game against The Devil Himself & all the minions of Hell. Of course, they all cheat! To compete, you’ll have to cheat like Hell...  and the more you do, the more doomed you all become.

Welcome to The Last Resort & Casino… and winning IS your last resort. To get to the Big Table, you’ll face off against diabolical bosses like Dedly Aloor, Jack Potts, The Brothers Dreadful, & Roul-ette, each of whom twists the game in unique ways.
