It’s real poker - as far as playing poker in Hell goes, with you all trying individually to beat the house. Every Boss changes how you will approach the hand and has a bare minimum hand to beat, should their dealt cards not pan out.

It's a Texas Hold'em style game - and players get not two but three cards dealt to them (one up their sleeve for more options). You may earn powerful Favor cards that let you cheat, like making a four-card Flush, but at the cost of a Curse or suffering Temptation.

Your chips will earn you Souls (the coin of the realm) which you'll need at the Big Table against The Devil Himself - or betting one of your five Power chips will gain you immediate abilities, like revealing one of the Boss's cards, or taking a face-up discard into your hand. But lose the hand and your wagered chips flip over to deliver harsh penalties. Suffer too much Temptation and the game ends in a loss. Manage to win against The Devil Himself and you might just earn your way back to the living.

It has the feel of high stakes poker, with no cash on the table. And delivers a whole Poker Night in just 90 minutes.

The retail edition of the game features 52 custom 11.5g poker chips and six neoprene play mats inside the box. The Community board mats alone fill a 28"x20" space at the center of the table making it feel like a full poker table.

Hell of a Deal is made for board gamers who love poker - and even if you are relatively new to poker, its cooperative nature and in-game guides make it approachable enough for anyone to play.

Poker is 100% skill and 50% luck. 
Being a good cheat helps too.
Have a Lucky Day!

—description from the publisher

A gigantic black hole is about to swallow your space station and, in the panic of the evacuation, the fleet is now in complete disarray. Will you manage to re-organize all ships before they reach the space jump rendez-vous point?

Each turn, you roll 5 dice (one for each color of ship), and have to choose wisely which one to use to move on elf the ships of this color.

At the end of each turn, the black hole will swallow one of the tiles making your path shorter. Will you manage to reach your destination with a re-organized fleet?

Defuse the Bomb is a solo print & play dice game played with only five six-sided dice.

Each turn represents a critical moment in the defusal process. You must decide whether to secure your progress or push your luck, knowing that every additional roll increases the pressure—and one bad decision could end everything.

The game focuses on:

Meaningful decisions under pressure

Push-your-luck mechanics

Risk management and timing

Fast setup and minimal components

With simple rules but deep tension, Defuse the Bomb is designed for solo players who enjoy compact games where every roll matters.

What you need to play
5 six-sided dice (5d6)

Printed player sheet (or use it digitally)

Pencil or pen

Every turn counts.
Not everyone who rushes wins—only those who know when to stop.

Fries is a 3-4 player climbing-shedding game where the goal is to be either the first player to shed out, or the last player with cards still in hand! Those caught in the middle will lose a precious fry.

During each trick, players must play a meld if they have one that beats the previous meld, and the first player who cannot beat a previous meld is then given the choice to create a bomb, giving each round its own special flavor. In addition, each player's chosen wild card inverts the ranking of the cards, making it harder and harder to shed out - or stay in!

Players who achieve one of the two special feats (winning every trick as they shed out 1st, or winning NO tricks as they become last in) double the penalties for their opponents.

Monopoly: Godzilla - 70th Anniversary is a Monopoly game with theme around the movie Godzilla. In this version of Monopoly players must buy up property and build homes in the world of kaiju. The game has six collectible tokens, anniversary artwork and themed cards.

boardgame: NUTS! War Tales! The Silent Knife: Captain Nieves & The Filipino Resistance
description:
When Japan invaded the Philippines in 1941, the fall of Bataan and Corregidor marked the beginning of a brutal occupation. But across the islands, resistance was never crushed—it only grew stronger. In Leyte, one of the most formidable guerrilla leaders was Captain Nieves Fernandez, a schoolteacher turned warrior, leading her band of freedom fighters in a relentless war against the Japanese.

Inside you’ll find:

     A Brief History of the Filipino Resistance
     Guerrilla Warfare Rules & Organization – Build and command a resistance cell, balancing combat operations and civilian support.
     Weapons & Tactics – Use captured Japanese rifles, improvised shotguns, and ambush strategies to outmaneuver the enemy.
     Dynamic Resistance Campaign
     Solo & Group Missions


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Oasis: New Life on A Distant World
description:
Cultivate a vibrant biome where the circle of life fuels the ecosystem's survival!

In Oasis: New Life on a Distant World, you embody the forces of nature on this primal planet, shaping the planet’s biodiversity by guiding the life cycles of its many bizarre species. Your goal is to cultivate a balanced, vibrant biome where the circle of life fuels the ecosystem’s survival

Timing is key, however, as the annual floods will wash away plants and animals below the floodline at the end of each round. You must manage your hand of cards and maximize your actions to outplay your opponents and build the most dynamic and highest-scoring biome.

On a turn, play a card from your hand face-down into one of the Pools in the middle of the table. Each card modifies the water value in that Pool, and if the combined water value ever meets or exceeds 10, the Pool spills over and chaos ensues. Try to predict the cards your opponents will play, while outwitting them with your own cards.

Each card you play has some amount of influence and when a Pool spills over, the player with the most influence in that Pool wins a Score card that is worth points at the end of the game.

Some cards also have actions that might bump adjacent cards into different Pools, or let you swap a card from your hand and steal the Score card at the last second! After the Score cards run out, players add up their points and the player with the most points from their Score cards wins.

EvenGlow is an light-strategy puzzle game that’s all about balance. Players are planting magical gardens with a sunlight and moonlight side. Turns are simultaneous with the active player choosing two plants for themselves and two that all other players will receive.

Pairs of plants must then be placed in mirror opposite positions of the garden, one in sunlight and the other in moonlight. Evenglow features gorgeous watercolor art from Clèmentine Campardou (Wyrmspan, Origin Story).

boardgame: Holy Pickles Card Game: The Brinewood Forest
description:
Holy Pickles is a strategic deck-building and worker placement game where your deck becomes your workforce.

In this charming but deeply tactical forest world, players recruit animal allies, harvest rare ingredients, manage divine Faith, and fulfill sacred recipes to craft legendary pickle jars.
With meaningful player interaction, evolving board states, and endlessly replayable engine-building, Holy Pickles delivers a fresh twist on two beloved genres in one elegant system. Easy to learn, rich to master—this is a game about smart planning, clever combos, and becoming the forest’s ultimate pickle master.

Thessaloniki, 4th century: the sun ascended, illuminating the Forum and the harbor's masts, bathing a city vibrant with ambition in a golden glow. Its bustling markets teemed with merchants from every corner of the empire: Greeks, Romans, Syrians, and Illyrians, all engaged in trade, intrigue, and the pursuit of wealth. Amidst the clamor, rumors of imperial favor and trade monopolies permeate the agora. Ships, laden with grains, oil, grapes, and meat, creak under their bounty, destined for distant ports or the vast granaries of powerful patricians. You are one of these patricians, competing for riches and influence. Yet only the shrewdest will rise above the chaos. In a city where commerce and politics are inseparable, your eloquence and strategic acumen forge your legacy in the annals of history.

Thessaloniki is a game set during the vibrant merchant era of the Tetrarchy in the 4th century Roman Empire. Players take on the roles of ambitious traders fulfilling contracts, acquiring commodities, and trading with allied cities.

Compete to sell your wares at the best prices or secure shipments to high-scoring destinations. As the game unfolds, you can climb the Influence track to sway the popularity of destinations, reaping extra rewards for aligning your shipments accordingly. Resolving contracts that offer powerful bonuses is key to becoming the most influential merchant in Thessaloniki!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: NUTS! War Tales! The Chindits: War Behind the Lines against Japan
description:
We moved through the jungle in silence, boots sinking into mud, sweat trickling down under our slouch hats. No supply lines, no reinforcements—just us and the enemy. We weren’t here to hold ground. We were here to raise hell behind theirs.

CHINDITS: WAR BEHIND THE LINES drops you deep into the jungles of Burma, where Brigadier Orde Wingate’s long-range penetration groups waged guerrilla warfare against the Japanese. Fighting through dense terrain, with air-dropped supplies and ruthless improvisation, the Chindits struck key railways, bridges, and garrisons in a brutal war of endurance.

Inside you'll find:

     A historical overview of the 1943 and 1944 Chindit campaigns
     Unit Lists for Chindits, Japanese, and Kachin Partisans
     Jungle Warfare rules, air resupply, and movement fatigue
     Rules for Demolition, Ambush, and Deep Infiltration
     A full Behind the Lines campaign system with ready-to-play Missions based on real operations
     The daring Operation Longcloth Campaign


No backup. No easy choices. Just you, your squad, and the jungle. Ready to fight behind the lines?

—description from the publisher

Monument Card, originally issued as a promo during the PickPocket Games’ Kickstarter campaign for Lodge (2026).

“4 points. Once per game, after constructing, you may remove all resources from squares in 1 row in your town.”

boardgame: Dominion of the Sherman and T-34 WWII: Fight Battles of the late 2nd World War – Solo Game
description:
This is the Dominion of the Sherman and T-34 Fight Battles of the late 2nd World War Solo Game. 
This standalone set of rules is built on the Dominion of the Spear solo game system with appropriate rule changes for mid to late 2nd World War. 
These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-7 units and a solo battle can be fought in 10-15 minutes. An example play through is included. 
There is a Quick Reference Sheet on the back cover. 
Use paper and pen (or model figures of any scale) as there is no measuring (grid used). 
Uses 2 six sided dice.

24 historical battles and campaigns are included ready to play, each with a brief background, army lists, deployment, and brief account of what happened historically. Together they provide a simple overview of the second half of the 2nd World War, with the first half covered in Dominion of the Blitzkrieg. The battles are:

1. Battle of Voronezh (German Summer Offensive) 28th June-24th July 1942
2. First Battle of El Alamein 1-27th July 1942 
3. Raid on Dieppe 19th August 1942
4. Battle of Stalingrad 23rd August 1942-2nd February 1943
5. Battle for Henderson Field (Guadalcanal Campaign) 23-26th October 1942
6. Second Battle of El Alamein 23rd October-11th November 1942
7. Battle of Kasserine Pass 19-25th February 1943
8. Third Battle of Kharkov 19th February-15th March 1943
9. Battle of Tunis 19th April-13th May 1943
10. Battle of Kursk 5-16th July 1943
11. Battle of Sicily (Operation Husky) 9th July-17th August 1943
12. Battle of Salerno (Operation Avalanche) 9-16th September 1943
13. Battle of Tarawa 20-23rd November 1943
14. Battle of Monte Cassino 17th January-18th May 1944
15. Battle of Anzio (Operation Shingle) 22nd January-5th June 1944
16. D-Day (Operation Overlord) 6th June 1944
17. Operation Bagration 22nd June-19th August 1944
18. Operation Cobra 25-31st July 1944 
19. Operation Market Garden 17-25th September 1944
20. Battle of Leyte (Philippines) 20th October-31st December 1944
21. Battle of the Bulge 16th December 1944-25th January 1945
22. Battle of Iwo Jima 19th February-26th March 1945
23. Battle of Okinawa 1st April-22nd June 1945
24. Fall of Berlin 16th April-2nd May 1945

This is easily read on a tablet or mobile phone and can be printed as an A5 booklet - click booklet (and fit to page in printing options depending the program used). 44 A5 pages.

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: Augustus to Aurelius – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
On Bloody Ground: Augustus to Aurelius: A 64 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.

On Bloody Ground - Augustus to Aurelius, includes nine complete army lists to enable players to build Imperial Roman, Germanic Tribes, Ancient Britons, Caledonians, Dacians, Parthians, Sarmations, Jewish and Iberian.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

boardgame: 柱の智慧 (The Wisdom of Pillars)
description:
From the publisher:

This is a two-player competitive game where players arrange blocks in a three-dimensional space and deduce the color and content combination of the "scoring condition" set by their opponent.

Players aim to place blocks to fulfill their own scoring conditions and earn points. However, whenever you score points, you must give your opponent a hint about "which color block you scored with."

Victory is achieved through this exchange of hints. You win immediately if you quickly figure out your opponent's scoring conditions or if you place blocks to fulfill all your own scoring conditions at once. If no winner is decided after four rounds, victory goes to the player with the higher total score.

Additional notes:

The basic game uses five colors of block and five scoring conditions (e.g. a color may score if it is standing, if it is on top of other blocks, etc.) The advanced game adds a sixth color and a sixth scoring condition.

Cocci Wars is a turn based game played on a hexagon map. Each player control a coccus bacterium with the purpose of creating a bacteria culture. How? Each player can move one bacterium per turn, to eat or reposition. A bacteria that feed will reproduce a identical individual that will receive a mutation. The mutation is chosen by the player from a total of 6 mutations, no dice or cards are used, and a bacterium can receive up to 7 mutations, which creates a huge combination possibilities. The eating process is when a bacterium moves on a hexagon with a food item, remove that item and your bacterium is feed. As some of your bacteria reproduces and some die you will observe the process of evolution, where only the fittest will survive and reproduce. In some case you will find yourself controlling 2 separate species of bacterium when the middle chain went extinct.

The forest fights back! This expansion transforms Boktor into a vertical, reactive and evolving battlefield, introducing the Guardian Trees, formerly peaceful keepers of the forest, now powerful foes, corrupted by the Tyrant. To purify them, hero players have to find relics dispatched across the map and to bring them at the summit of the trees.

Climb a tree can be dangerous but this action also allows new abilities : the line of sight is wider, monsters can be pushed or pulled off the tree, and hero players can perform a jump attack to surprise enemies on an adjacent tile!

boardgame: Stadt Land Vollpfosten: Köln Edition – Kölle Alaaf!
description:
The game pad with 26 creative and fun categories for an unforgettable gaming experience. Each player receives one page of the A4 pad and places it in front of them. A time limit is set for each round (we recommend 2 minutes). The innovative letter bar helps to determine the letter. Then all terms must be entered as quickly as possible. There are 8 terms to find per round.

—description from the publisher

Die kölsche Edition des Kultspiels STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN rund ums Leben in Köln. Der Spielblock mit 26 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien für ein unvergessliches Spielerlebnis unterrheinischen Frohnaturen. Jeder Spieler erhält eine Seite des DIN-A4 Blockes und legt diese vor sich hin. Es wird eine Zeit pro Durchgang ausgemacht (wir empfehlen 2 Minuten als Begrenzung). Die innovative Buchstabenleiste hilft bei der Ermittlung des Buchstabens. Dann gilt es so schnell wie möglich alle Begriffe einzutragen. Pro Runde sind 8 Begriffe gesucht.

—description from the publisher (German)

Invite your friends and enter the football world of Voll Verschätzt – the most exciting guessing game ever. Here you will find 350 sporting questions on the subject of football that will make even the best guessers sweat! Whether you are a walking encyclopaedia or rather clueless: what counts here is fun – knowledge is secondary!

—description from the publisher

Schnapp dir deine Freunde und begebt euch in die Fußball-Welt von Voll Verschätzt – dem verrücktesten Schätzspiel aller Zeiten. Hier erwarten dich 350 sportliche Fragen rund um das Thema Fußball, die selbst die besten Schätzer ins Schwitzen bringen! Egal, ob du ein wandelndes Lexikon oder eher der Nichtswisser bist: Hier zählt der Spaß – Wissen ist Nebensache!

—description from the publisher (German)

Necromunda exists on the brink of complete societal collapse and revolution. The teeming masses outnumber their noble masters many times over, and even the staunchest defence wouldn't be enough to hold them back if they ever united. Enter the Palanite Enforcers – loyal to the spires, ruthless, and well-equipped, they suppress and subjugate the lawless, protecting their patrons by ensuring the status quo remains firmly in place. No depths of the underhive are truly safe from their domineering presence.

This book peels back the veneer to uncover the brutal truth of the Palanite Enforcers, merciless soldiers whose remit is to impose law and order on the chaotic underhives in service to their noble masters. You'll find rules for different precincts, an exhaustive armoury, new scenarios to get them into the action, a fresh set of Hangers-on and Hired Guns, and loads more.

This 128-page hardback book contains:


     Background of the Palanite Enforcers: In-depth background material for the callous lawmen who patrol Necromunda with impunity, including their organisation and hierarchy.



     Palanite Enforcer Patrols: Rules for running your own patrol, including an extensive list of weapons and Wargear.



     Hangers-on, Hired Guns, and Dramatis Personae: Add everything from psychic Haunts to the Scrutinator-Primus Servalen to your gang.



     Additional Rules: Palanite Drill skills, rules for different Prefectures, and forming alliances with the Imperial House.



     Palanite Enforcer Scenarios: Two scenarios involving the Enforcers – Asset Seizure and The Getaway.



     Badzone Enforcer Gangs: Rules for creating Enforcers from isolated or abandoned fortress-precincts.


This is an expansion to Necromunda – you'll need a copy of the Necromunda: Core Rulebook, which is available separately, to use the contents of this book.

— Description from the publisher

Today, Nono and his family plan to find something in the veggie patch to make a delicious pie.

While preparing for this busy day, Nono put 3 yummy carrots in
his pocket. But his neighbors, the birds, plan to follow him on his adventures and steal his precious veggies!

Discover 5 different adventures and search for the important details of the story on the cubes. Check your choices by looking in the holes, insides the cubes. We hope you found a rabbit and not a magpie! If so, it means you can continue & Finish the puzzle before the magpies take all your carrots!

This story-based seek & find will develop many skills for children and progressive supports will help a lot to accompany them.

Pantheon of Nations is a strategic conflict board game for 2 to 4 players, in which each player controls a nation guided by a powerful god-avatar. Each nation is associated with distinct elements, units, and abilities that define its strategic identity and available options.

Players issue orders by spending a limited shared resource called Faith. During the order phase, players secretly assign commands to their units, committing to a plan without knowing the choices of their opponents. These orders are later revealed and resolved in sequence. Orders allow players to move units across a hex-based map, recruit forces, attack enemies, activate special abilities, or protect their god.

A central element of the game is hidden planning and anticipation. Because players must commit to actions in advance, incorrect predictions can cause players to restrict their own options, misposition units, or expose critical pieces at the wrong time. In contrast, successfully anticipating opponents’ intentions is strongly rewarded, often resulting in highly efficient turns and decisive advantages on the battlefield. As a result, players are required to think not only about their own plans, but also to continuously evaluate and predict the strategies of one or multiple opponents.

The game also features Blessing cards, which are granted to the player who wins a bidding contest. These cards introduce temporary rule changes or powerful effects for the current round, allowing the winning player to shift the balance of the game and capitalize on successful anticipation and risk-taking.

Military forces consist of infantry and cavalry units equipped with different weapon types, including swords, spears, and bows. Each weapon functions differently in combat, with distinct ranges and effectiveness. Gods themselves play a critical role in the conflict, providing unique elemental abilities that influence the battlefield. If a player’s god is destroyed, that player is immediately eliminated from the game. Victory is achieved by being the last player whose god remains in play.

With asymmetric factions, variable player powers, secret order programming, and high levels of player interaction, Pantheon of Nations emphasizes strategic foresight, commitment to decisions, and the consequences of both successful and failed predictions.

—description from the designer

Barbarian (Adventure deck) consists of overcoming game stages (stage cards). Each card will ask for one or more checks to continue advancing. If you succeed in the check you will move on to the next card and if not, you will lose stamina. This way you will reach the last card: the boss. If you beat him you will have beaten the video game.
Checks are made with the hero deck. The hero deck contains cards ranging from 1 to 6. But be careful because if you fail a check you will lose a card and with it the possibility of having a greater chance of success in subsequent tests. This deck also symbolize the stamina of a hero. When you run out of cards the game is over.

Enneatrix is an abstract strategy game of sublime geometry and gravitational manipulation for two players. You are an Architect of Reality, competing to impose a new order upon the Enneatrix—a ninth-order triangular mosaic that serves as a spatio-gravitational construct. Command your Celestial Spheres and use tactical placement to activate powerful gravitational pulls across the board's dual network of Stable and Structural cells. The first to assemble a perfect triangle of nine spheres—a microcosm of the board itself—rewrites reality and wins. Elegant rules give way to deep, emergent tactics where every move can trigger chain reactions and set up devastating victories.

The Board: A 9th-order triangular mosaic (81 cells) with two cell types: Stable (blue) — the majority, and Structural (magenta/red) — the load-bearing framework of the network.

Goal: Be the first to create a triangle of 9 adjacent cells occupied by stones of any color (an exact, scaled-down copy of the entire board).

Turn: Place one of your stones on any empty cell.

Activation: If after a move any cell becomes surrounded by three occupied cells of the opposite color, it activates.

Effect (The Pull): The stone in the activated cell creates an instantaneous gravitational pull along the six directions of its color network. In each such direction, the nearest visible stone (of any color) instantly moves into the cell of its own color adjacent to the activated center, provided that cell is empty.

Victory: Checked after the turn is fully completed and all Pull effects have resolved. If anywhere on the board there exists a filled triangle of 9 adjacent cells (a 3rd-order triangle), the player who made the move wins.

—description from the designer

Little Red's Pizza Express is a cooperative family game where all work together to achieve a common goal.
You probably know the tale of Little Red Riding Hood and the Wolf. Help Little Red Riding Hood deliver the pizza to Grandma before the Wolf arrives!

Collect food, dodge wolves, and ask hunter for help.
All players work together to help Little Red Riding Hood deliver her pizza to Grandma’s house before the Wolf arrives. You’ll need to flip tiles, match food pairs, and avoid advancing the Wolf. Memorization and teamwork are critical for victory!

—description from the publisher
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: Little Red Riding Hood

description:
In a distant forest lies a small village, well hidden from view. This village is called Saint-Rondin-sur-Rivière.

Every five years, the village beavers take part in a unique competition: build the most dams to become the next king!

Stack your log cards in ascending order to build your dams. Use action and bonus cards to help you or slow down your opponents. Each character has a unique power to give you an advantage!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Dans une forêt lointaine, se trouve un petit village bien caché à l'abri des regards. Ce village se nomme Saint-Rondin-sur-Rivière.

Tous les cinq ans, les castors du village participent à un concours unique : construire le plus de barrages pour devenir le prochain roi !

Empile tes cartes rondins dans l'ordre croissant pour construire tes barrages. Utilise des cartes action et bonus pour t'aider ou freiner tes adversaires. Chaque personnage possède un pouvoir unique pour t'avantager !

—description from the publisher

A game of Batalha aims to depict a clash between cultures competing to outdo each other. Each Player brings a deck representing that player’s culture, their kingdom, their empire, their tribe... Within the deck, you will find cards of different types, they are the weapons, the architecture, the ingenuity and the people of that culture.

To win, a player must accumulate 20 Glory. Glory is granted by completing tasks that will make history, be it sieging settlements, killing powerful enemies, or building a great wonder.

—description from the designer

A comedic, ribald & risqué town adventure for Four Against Darkness, for levels 3-4 Shamelessly mocking Lysistrata and Romeo and Juliet, Kingly Knights & Knifing Knaves is a hilarious and lewd commedia dell’arte town adventure where the townswomen hold a sex strike to convince their bellicose husbands to lay town their arms, to no avail. Will you restore peace, take a side, or just kill everyone and loot their gold? Featuring new classes (Cavalier, Flamingelf, Flamingo Folk, Harlot Skunk Folk & Swain), Craft skills for all classes, new Character Traits, magic flamingo retainers, new rules to play in town and very, very naughty events you shouldn’t tell your mother about. Contains lewd art, silliness, flamingos and adult stuff.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pacific Fleet: Rise & Fall of the Japanese Empire
description:
From the surprise attack on Pearl Harbor to the final Allied offensives of 1945, Pacific Fleet is a grand-strategic simulation of the Second World War in the Pacific. Players command the full spectrum of naval, air, and ground forces, coordinating carrier task forces, long-range air power, amphibious invasions, and complex logistics across the largest theater of war ever fought.

Gameplay is turn-based, with each turn representing roughly three months and divided into seven operational phases. Players form and maneuver task forces, conduct air strikes, surface battles, amphibious assaults, ground combat, and manage supply. Fog of war is central: task forces and ports can be hidden, forcing decisions under uncertainty. Aircraft carriers, land-based air power, submarines, and logistics are decisive tools.

Victory depends on the scenario. Short scenarios focus on key battles like Guadalcanal or Midway; campaign games use victory points gained from sinking enemy ships, holding or capturing strategic locations, and maintaining operational momentum. Japan must gain land and key islands to form a solid wall early and decisively, or the Allies will survive, counterattack, and methodically dismantle Japanese power. Time is the ultimate enemy—especially for Japan.

Designed for experienced wargamers, Pacific Fleet emphasizes operational realism without sacrificing playability. Hidden naval movement, limited sorties, supply constraints, and national doctrine ensure that every decision carries long-term consequences. Victory is never achieved through brute force alone, but through timing, intelligence, and strategic foresight.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: TUSC: The Universal Skirmish Combat Engine
description:
TUSC: The Universal Skirmish Combat Engine is a modular, setting agnostic skirmish wargame designed to allow you to create custom stat lines for any models you may desire.

It is the debut title of indie tabletop games studio Sword & Board Games, and includes a supplement for playing games in Sword & Board Games' in-house sci-fi setting Tetrarchy to show how you may adapt the game to fit your own custom setting or any pre-established setting.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Poetic Platitudes of the Potato People: A humorous adventure for Four Against Darkness
description:
Not With a Bang But a Whimper Ever since the potato fields have been quoting T.S Eliot’s monotonous poems, the orc hordes of the Withering Warlord have been depressed, unwilling to rise arms against the bigoted human empire of Dorantia. Worse still, the Warlord’s trifling lieutenants have been quarreling, blaming each other in their petty attempts to grab more power for their tribe, leaving the agricultural orc town of Kartoffelheim incapable of feeding the greater war effort. It sucks to be an orc in these times. Tartaglia, the Warlord’s herald and buffoon, seeks true heroes to investigate the matter, find culprits or scapegoats and be done with them. Will you stand to save the nascent Orc Republics against the evil of humankind and their elfish toadies? At long last, here’s a chance to play from the Other Side’s perspective. Features the new bugbear, giant, orc, kobold, kobelf, goblinogre, hobgoblin, ling, potato people, potatomancer & zombingo classes and plenty of occasions to slaughter those wimpy elfs and humans.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ravagers of Ruins: Rules for ruined cities, their monsters and custom Beastfolk
description:
More Things To Kill

This book is a fully illustrated bestiary of 34 new monsters dwelling in ruined cities, for parties of level 1 or higher. Also featuring:

     Rules for creating your own classes for animal folk characters! * Sample beastfolk: Cat Folk, Crocodile Folk, Hare Folk, Hound Folk, Mouse Folk, Mosquito Folk, Sheep Folk, Spider Folk and the endearing Swine Folk.
     Rules to generate maps and content for Ruined Cities.
     Tables for special features, treasure and magic rings.
     New spells to be used in game-mastered games under the “RPG-Lite Option”.


—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Bone Cult of Tha'ar Tala: An Adventure for Four Against Darkness
description:
The Lives of Children Hang in the Balance The outlawed Bone Cult of Tha’ar Tala has kidnapped children from the streets of Paraxi, to use them in their dark rites. Your party is hired to save the children and clean out the cult. Rumor has it that they are hiding in a ruined temple of Elidra, near a forest a few miles from town. This is a four-chapter adventure for a Four Against Darkness for characters of all levels.

—description from the publisher

The Next Installment in the Drinking Quest Series!

Like a typical Role Playing Game you have a character sheet, roll for saving throws, fight monsters and find treasure BUT when your character dies in the game you have to chug your drink in real life! It adds a real level of tension and excitement to the battles. Everything matters.

This new installment brings back the sidekick mechanics
and 4 new quests. a Skyrim Parody, an alchemy gone wrong parody, a public drunkeness quest and a cthulhu thirst trap quest.

The battles are a series of one-on-one fights to the death. You either kill the monster earning coins and XP or your character dies and you must chug your drink!

The cards act as the GM (So everyone can drink) and the heroes go through four themed quests in each game - each getting progressively difficult and more likely to drink. However as this is a light RPG and a light Drinking Game there is a one chug per quest maximum which prevents Drinking Quest from being a "Pass out in 10 minutes" kind of drinking game. Over the course of 2 or 3 hours, a typical game averages 2 or 3 chugs.

Whoever gets the most XP wins! Whoever loses had to chug the most so it's sort of like everybody wins.

—description from the publisher

Shape It is a cooperative game in which players move shapes to try to guess a word. What word is your group trying to illustrate? That’s the game: You don’t know for sure... But it has to be on one of the 5 cards on the table. When it comes time to guess, will you all see the same word in the shapes you’ve placed? Good luck!

If your whole group sees the same Word in the shapes you’ve placed, you win the round and collect a Word card. If you don’t all see the same thing, you lose a Life.

→ If you collect 5 cards, you win!

→ If you lose your 3 Lives... you’ve lost the game.

boardgame: Monopoly: Jimmy Buffett – Trop Rock edition
description: Monopoly: Jimmy Buffett - Trop Rock edition is a Monopoly game with theme around the artist Jimmy Buffett. In this version of Monopoly players must buy up property and build homes in the Buffett universe. This games has six unique custom-molded game tokens and a themed game board with artwork, illustrated cards and unique tropical property spaces.

Investigate a chilling true crime–inspired case where a rideshare trip goes tragically wrong and the back of a car becomes the crime scene. This immersive murder mystery game challenges you to trace a killer’s digital footprint and uncover what really happened.

Examine realistic evidence like in-car video recordings, passenger logs, reports, and physical clues as you piece together the truth. Designed for adult game nights, date nights, or group play, this mystery-in-a-box delivers suspense, strategy, and hands-on investigation.


     Immersive Detective Game: Step into the driver's seat as a detective, sift through realistic evidence and testimonies. Piece together the complex connections in this unique murder investigation game.
     Realistic Evidence & Clues: Decipher in-car video recordings, reports, passenger logs, and realistic clues like a keepsake bobblehead. Every detail matters to solve this case game!
     Unsolved Mystery in a Box: Contains everything you need for a complete cold case investigation and hours of challenging puzzle board strategy. Can you put the killer behind bars?


Difficulty: MEDIUM

—description from the publisher

Four new clans and wonders to add into Spirited: Carthaginian, Phoenician, Etruscan, and Minoan.

Contains 40 Clan Cards, 4 Allegiance Cards, 4 Wonders, 4 Clan Discs, and 4 Card Dividers

—description from the publisher

Every country has its unique festivals. Vietnam has many of its own. Those days are called "Tết". Days of Tết (also Gà & Nếp) is an Augmented-Reality powered board game where players can immerse in those festive days.

Roll your D12 and move along a calendar of the festival days.  Each day can be scanned with your phone, bringing up stories, pictures, and videos.  Offer the traditional boiled chicken along the way.  The first player to reach the last festival day is the winner.

Storyboard Heroes is a card game in which players create one or two comic strips by building a sequence of connected scenes. Each card represents a moment in an action-packed story featuring heroes, villains, and unexpected twists, and players connect cards using matching edge icons to ensure a coherent narrative flow.

During the game, players draft Scene cards from their hand or from a shared display, expanding their personal strips while adapting to changing opportunities. Reaction cards can be played to score points and introduce sudden turns in the story, sometimes even affecting opponents’ strips. As the comic strips grow longer, the narrative naturally moves toward its conclusion.

The final phase of the game introduces Finale cards, which can only be played once the last round begins and often require a complete narrative arc to be present in a strip. These cards represent the ending of the story and provide significant scoring opportunities.

At the end of the game, players score points from the Reaction and Finale cards in their strips, with possible penalties for unresolved elements. The player with the highest total score wins, creating a different and replayable comic-book story each time the game is played.

—description from the designer

VIDRO is a variant tic-tac-toe–style game.

The victory condition is to line up three or more of your own pieces in a row—vertically, horizontally, or diagonally!
However, you may not place a new piece adjacent to any of your pieces already on the board.

Use the ‘Flick’ action to trigger chain reactions between pieces and aim for victory!

Wisdom of Numbers is a game in which players collect number chips from 1 to 100 and fulfill various mathematically themed objective cards, such as “four prime numbers,” “three consecutive numbers,” or “three Fibonacci numbers.”

Players reveal chips in the market to search for the numbers they want, or claim objective cards to gain face-down chips, while allocating the numbers in their hand across multiple objectives.
publishers: iop games (イオピーゲームズ)

description:
In this fast paced football card game, you must coach your team to victory. Recruit 5 players of different colored shirts to win or keep an eye out for the Captain card which activates a different alternative win for everyone in play!

Make sure to score goals and earn trophies to prevent other coaches from stealing, swapping or punishing your players. Be quick to play as in football there is no time to think, if you snooze you lose!

—description from the publisher

The Vikings, Become the Legend core game is a competitive solo to two player wealth building game. Each game turn clans competing to settle, develop their settlements and earn resources.

A key part of the game is planning and then successfully executing your actions. You decide when to build and what to build from warriors to ships and settlement structures.  You decide where to send your Raiding or Long Ships and when & how to “Trade”, “Raid & Pillage” or “Raid & Settle” with each of these ships and their warriors.  The opposing clans may also be your targets from time to time. But, use this option with caution, a blood feud does neither clan much good.

Battles are fought using the Battle Board.  Raid Cards and the Attacking and Defending forces.  Are placed on the board and the number of attacking rounds determined.  Raid cards are simultaneously declared for the battle then unique battle dice are rolled to determine casualties.  Shields defend, archers are resolved followed by berserkers (double axes) and then single axe-men.

As a solitaire war game, the game system "A.I." called “Automa” handles an enemy clan which may thwart or interfere with your mission plans. You will have to respond to the unexpected events and resistance within your new settlements. When you incorporate one or more expansions you may choose to have more than one Automa opponent and or multiple live opponents.

The game ends when one player has 15 Silver on the Silver Tracker and has in play three settlements, one with a Market, one with a Ring Fort and one with a Mead Hall.  Clan leaders then score their achievements with the high point total winning the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kingdoms Forlorn: Princess Slawia and Little Ser
description:
Princess Slawia is a new ally that can accompany you on your adventures, while Little Ser is simply adorable! (also replaces Ser Sonch's Little Ser token)

Includes:
Miniatures for Princess Slawia and Little Ser
Ally card for Princess Slawia

Escape Game Puzzle - Les Catacombes: A UNIQUE ADVENTURE!

Paris, 1782. 
You are tasked with reinforcing old stone quarries.
Unfortunately, on your very first day, you get lost in the tunnels and stumble upon a chamber filled with bones: the catacombs! 
A rockfall blocks your exit. 
Your only clue is a mysterious notebook.
Your only chance: carefully observe your surroundings and solve the riddles to find a way out!

—description from the publisher

Overheat is a competitive game for 2 to 6 players that blends team management, betting, and arena combat between customizable mechas. Each round, two or more players face off while the rest watch, bet, and interfere using cards. Between fights, everyone manages their team: upgrading robots, recruiting crew, negotiating with other players and preparing their next big entrance into the arena. The player who manages to win events and stand out in the grand finals of the league, is the player who wins.

boardgame: Escape Game Party: Alice au Pays des Merveilles
description:
Will you be able to escape this adventure?

Following a mysterious white rabbit, you entered a burrow that has now collapsed: you're trapped!

Explore three wondrous and unsettling worlds to find the path that will lead you out before you run out of oxygen… 
Discover a thrilling adventure in which you'll have to observe, manipulate, and solve puzzles (encrypted alphabet, maze, logical deduction, etc.), just like in a real escape room.

All the materials you need to play like in a real escape room!

50 detachable objects, 4 envelopes, 5 sheets, 33 cards, 1 book, and 1 soundtrack!

—description from the publisher

Lock and load! Assemble a team of convicts. Travel through deep space and eliminate monstrous threats to earn your freedom!

Cloud Jumpers is an asymmetric turn-based combat strategy game set in an original deep space sci-fi landscape.

In this "one vs. many" shooter, players will gather and manage resources, upgrade weapons and equipment, and play utility cards to fight off hoards of unknown and deadly threats controlled by a single player. Strategy and synergy are needed to build a combat effective team, and to stay prepared for any enemies lurking in the clouds!

—description from the designer

Capture the vibrant, chaotic energy of Hồ Chí Minh City in Sài Gòn, an open drafting and set collection game where players compete to develop the most prosperous city block. In a city famous for its unique "tube houses"—tall, thin architecture that rises rapidly over months or years—you must balance vertical expansion with bustling street life to outshine your rivals.

Gameplay Overview
The game is played in two distinct phases that blend strategic planning with competitive interaction:


     The Drafting Grid (Competing for Permits): Players take turns adding tiles to a shared central grid, expanding the market depending on player count. The twist comes when you commit your colored die to a specific corner of the grid, staking a claim on the four surrounding tiles. Timing is everything: lower value dice draft first, but placing your die early restricts your options. Be careful—if your die is surrounded before you're ready, you might be forced to draft immediately.



     The Construction Phase: Back at your player board, you must arrange your drafted tiles to build your city block.
         Build Up: Stack matching Building Tiles to create the tallest skyscrapers in the district.
         Street Level: Manage the ground floor with essential upgrades like Restaurants and Housing, or suffer the penalties of Abandoned Buildings and Construction zones.
         The Street: Fill your lower row with vibrant activities—from Markets and Food Stalls to Dragon Dancing—to earn set collection bonuses.
    


Key Features

     Vertical Strategy: Score points by constructing the tallest building of each of the 6 distinct types, but remember that you cannot mix building types as you build upwards.
     Interactive Economy: Utilize "Street Vendor" meeples to invade opponents' tiles for points, but beware of the Police marker, which can kick your vendors off a rival's block.
     Risk Management: While Construction tiles offer resources for Unique Buildings, they clutter your street with negative points.
     Unique Architecture: Unlock massive, multi-tile Unique Buildings like the Pagoda or Park by collecting Architect Tiles to cover up unsightly abandoned areas.


Will you build the tallest skyline, or will your streets come alive with the most culture and commerce? The spirit of Sài Gòn awaits.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Batalha Das Ardenas: A Ultima Cartada De Hitler
description: After the resounding defeat suffered in Normandy, the German Army (or what remained of it) retreated, being intensely pursued by the Allies to the German border. Then, the increase in German resistance, the excessive extension of supply lines, and the attrition of their units caused the Allies to lose heart in the pursuit. Taking advantage of this, the Germans reorganized their forces and brought in new reinforcements. Adolf Hitler then ordered the planning of a major attack: the Ardennes Offensive. The plan consisted of attacking, with three armies, the weakly defended Ardennes region. The Panzer armies (5th Army of General Hasso von Manteuffel and 6th Army of General Josef "Sepp" Dietrich) would advance to the Meuse River, cross it between Dinant and Liège, and reach Antwerp, isolating the entire British 21st Army Group north of the penetration, while the southern flank would be covered by the 7th Army of General Erich Brandenberger. These armies would be under the command of Army Group "B", of Marshal Walter Model. On the American side, the Ardennes were defended by two Army Corps: V (General Leonard T. Gerow) and VIII (General Troy H. Middleton), both belonging to the American 1st Army (General Courtney H. Hodges). The offensive began on 12/16/44 and caught the Americans by surprise. However, the rapid movement of reinforcements, the stubborn American defense, and the improvement in weather (allowing Allied planes to return to the sky) enabled the Allies to contain and then repel the German attack, with heavy and irreparable losses for Germany. Hitler's last gamble had failed.

Players are invited to Level Up Their Financial Class through the excitement of earning income from the Culinary Business, Freelance Work, and Gold Investment. Their goal is to accumulate Happiness Points from Essential Needs, Donations, and Retirement Funds, while achieving financial goals through Savings and Gold Ownership.

The objective is to collect Happiness Points gained from Essential Needs, Donations, and Retirement Funds, and to successfully reach financial goals through Savings and Gold Ownership.

In addition, to maximize profits, players can expand their culinary business using Sharia-compliant financing. However, beware of Life Risks. Prepare Emergency Funds and Insurance to protect players from unexpected events.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Happiest Cat In The World
description:
Compete with others to create the coziest, happiest space for your  cat and win their eternal contentment – or at least their grudging  approval. 
Buy new tiles for your deck to expand your cat's apartment and make attractive combos. Move your cat to take advantage of attractions, collect points for doing so, and use the best combinations between attractions. Manage your deck wisely.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Ludicurrus!

The vibrant, fast-paced chariot racing board game that hurls you straight into the chaos of the Circus Maximus!

Every turn is a gamble in the hands of the gods - will they bless your ambition, or curse you back to your last checkpoint? With push-your-luck mechanics, daring manoeuvres, and a healthy dash of absurdity, no two races are ever the same.

Win ambrosia to unlock divine powers, unleash gladiators to sabotage rivals, or send wild beasts charging onto the track. So grab the reins, embrace the madness, and prepare for a ludicrous battle of speed and wit!

—description from the publisher

Every battle has two sides… literally.

This set can be played standalone or combined with any other FlipWars pack.

Claim your ground, flip the tiles, and outthink your opponent in a fast, tactical 1-on-1 duel.

With multiple game modes - from sacred strongholds to ruined keeps- no two clashes play out the same way.

Easy to learn, quick to play, and dangerously addictive.

Flip with honor. Think in shadows. Control the board. Claim victory.

—description from the publisher

Are you ready for the most chaotic race of your life? Trouble Racer is the high-octane board game where speed is nothing without strategy, and the lead is never safe! This game brings the raw adrenaline of the racing track directly to your game night table.

Step into the world of professional racing! Take full control and fine-tune your racing car setup to get the maximum out for this race weekend. On your personal Setup Board, you hold the power: Will you push for legendary Top Speed to blast down the straights, or master the corners with superior Downforce?

We’ve designed the experience to evolve with you through three immersive modes: Start in Rookie Mode to learn how to feel the car, make smart setup decisions, and lay the foundation for a successful race weekend. Once you're ready, the Challenger Mode puts you into wheel-to-wheel battles where you must test your skills under pressure, master car control including damage management, and prove you've got what it takes to fight in the pack. For those who seek the ultimate test, the Champion Mode is where legends are made: manage your own race strategy, handle tire wear like a pro, and adapt to ever-changing weather conditions to claim your victory.

But beware—racing is a contact sport! Deploy devastating Action Cards to gain the ultimate advantage and disrupt your rivals' race. Sabotage the competition at every turn, but watch your back, because Revenge is just one card away! From the nail-biting tension of Qualifying to diving into a chaotic three-car Battle for the lead, Trouble Racer delivers an adrenaline-fueled experience that will keep your gaming group on the edge of their seats!

—description from the designer

A puzzle-style game with 6 animals to assemble. Each player chooses an animal image to complete. They then take a card from their neighbor, and if it matches their image, they place it in front of them to begin their puzzle. The first player to finish their puzzle wins!

In a few months, the Thing will take place, where major decisions are made and judgments rendered. If you want your voice to count at this great assembly, you will need to increase your influence by expanding your village and participating in raids!

Be the first to complete your cat mission!
A hilarious game for the whole family!
The aim of the game: step into the shoes of Patapon, Frimousse, Elvis, Tigrou or Pilou and get up to all sorts of mischief to complete your cat mission!

Eat Bubulle the fish, treat yourself to a personal spa day, ruin your owner's morning, plan your escape, prevent the old aunt from coming home... 25 missions, each one funnier than the last, to complete on an obstacle course throughout the house.

boardgame: キャッチ イン サークル (CIC)
description:
Catch in Circle is an action board game with a unique tactile experience, where players use a compass to enclose tokens by drawing circles.

The game’s key rule is that exactly four tokens must be moved every time a circle is drawn.
Because the outcome changes dramatically depending on where you place the center of the circle and how wide you open the compass, the game demands both intuition and analytical thinking.

From author Nathan E. Doverspike, comes the pocket-sized dungeon crawler that allows you to explore the Sci-Fi Fantasy Aetherial War universe from within a mint tin! Featuring a retro black/white Sci-Fi feel and designed explicitly for solo play, the Aetherial War: Untold Adventures Complete Edition gives you, the choice between five unique species, and sixteen different classes, each with their own stats, starting equipment, strengths, and weaknesses.

As you progress through each quest, every subsequent level gets more and more difficult. Since the room layout changes every time, you'll have to stay on your toes and be ready for anything that comes your way: from forks in the road, to traps, to deadly enemies! Stock up on valuable items and use them to your advantage when in a tough spot in order to sieze victory.

Combat is carried out over a series of rounds, during which your character attacks, followed by the enemy attacking you. Attacks are done by rolling the dice, and calculating if the attack misses, hits, or scores a Critical Hit (one extra damage). Certain classes and/or items may be able to influence your rolls, so finding them and holding onto them for the right time will be crucial to your survival.

—description from the designer

New characters have been invited to the Queen’s Table. Diplomats and confidants will upset the balance of power, but will also allow for new alliances to be made.
With this expansion, you will be able to play Courtisans with up to 6 players. Two new roles, the Diplomat and the Confidant, have arrived with new special effects.
This will also allow you to play in teams of 4 or 6 players.

—description from the publisher

Catena is a territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal grid of hexagons (board).

				
				
					DefinitionsA clump is a stone along with all stones one can reach from it through a series of steps onto adjacent stones.

A ribbon is a clump with no stone adjacent to more than two stones and at least one stone adjacent to less than two stones. A bounded ribbon is one that cannot grow without becoming a non-ribbon.

To claim a ribbon is to replace all enemy stones in it with friendly stones.

				
				
					PlayBlack plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, place a stone of your color on an empty cell without creating a non-ribbon, then let every newly bounded ribbon be claimed by the player with more stones in it. Tied ribbons of this kind are claimed by your opponent. When no more stones can be placed, the player with more stones on the board (plus komi and button where applicable) wins.

Komi is the whole number of points that is added to White's score at the end of the game as compensation for playing second. Before the game starts, one player chooses the value of komi, then the other chooses sides.

The button is a special token that is placed next to the board at the start of the game. On your turn, if neither player has taken the button yet, you may take it instead of making a board play. At the end of the game, a half point is added to the score of the player who has taken the button.

				
				
					VariantsFor buttonless ribbons, demand that two stones be placed on every turn after Black's first. Some alternative scoring rules are:


     Longest ribbon: When no more stones can be placed, remove pairs of same-length, opposite-colored ribbons until no more such pairs remain. The owner of the longest ribbon left over wins.
     Square score: Each ribbon contributes the square of its length to its owner's score. The player with the higher score wins.


—description from the designer

Jenga Bob's Burgers Edition is a fun, themed twist on the classic block-stacking game that brings everyone’s favorite burger-slinging family to your game night. Instead of just plain wooden blocks, this edition features 54 hardwood blocks customized with Bob’s Burgers-style flair, plus character pieces like Burger Bosses and Jimmy Pesto and a custom spinner to add playful mechanics to each turn. Players take turns carefully removing blocks from the tower and placing them on top, just like in traditional Jenga, but in this version you’re also moving your characters up the tower “levels” to beat Jimmy Pesto’s high score and prove Bob doesn’t sux.

Extrusion is a drawless territory game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the spaces (cells) of an initially empty hexagonal grid of hexagons (board).

A clump is a stone along with all stones one can reach from it through a series of steps onto adjacent stones. A checkered ribbon is a clump with no stone adjacent to a like-colored stone, no stone adjacent to more than two stones and exactly one or two stones, both of the same color, adjacent to less than two stones.

Black starts by placing a black stone on any cell, then players take turns placing exactly two stones of their color on empty cells. At the end of your turn, all clumps must be checkered ribbons. Passing is not allowed, but, if you cannot place two stones, your turn is skipped.

When neither player can place two stones, the player with the higher score wins. Your score is the total number of stones in all checkered ribbons with more friendly than enemy stones.

				
				
					Variants
     Free: Ribbons are not required to be checkered, i.e. stones of the same color can be adjacent to each other. This is still drawless because the total number of stones on the board is always odd.
     Odd: Ribbons are not required to be checkered, but they are required to have an odd number of stones.
     Palindromist: Ribbons are not required to be checkered, but their first stone must be the same color as their last, their second stone must be the same color as their second-last, etc. This variant was suggested by Dale Walton.


—description from the designer

boardgame: Kittens Love Sushi: Feeding Frenzy – the Expansion
description: This ten-card expansion adds six new kittens, two sushi, an action card, and a pupper card, allowing for a fifth and sixth player.

Each player is given one half of the deck, from which they initially draw five cards for a starter hand, and take turns playing cards to a center stack, by either matching the bird, or the colored border of the card. The person who runs out of cards first wins.

This Netrunner themed mini-expansion for Burgle Bros 3 expands the game with the following:

5 new characters to play pulled right from the Netrunner universe. Each one comes with a two unique thematic powers - one for their disguised mode, and one for their geared mode on the back side of their card. Each side has custom artwork. The also have a special tool card to use when the time is right. They each have a custom wood token, heat transfer printed on both sides!

2 new AI bosses - these are the evil computer systems running the whole building that you have to defeat to win the game. They introduce a new set of trap tiles to the tile bag that make your mission extra risky!

—description from the publisher

You control bacteria on a Petri dish trying to out-compete your rivals. Your bacteria navigate a hex grid representing a Petri dish with either straight runs or with chaotic tumbles determined by dice. Knock your rivals out of the way on runs. Collect food to divide or release toxins. Dodge deadly antibiotics or grab the antibiotic resistance trait. Eat the most food, remove the most antibiotics, and divide into the most bacteria to win!

At the end of 4, 6, or 8 rounds, players score points for the size of their bacterial colony, with bonus points for bacteria that possess antibiotic resistance, as well as for food eaten, antibiotics removed, and toxins released.

The Alchemist steps into the Mad King’s arena—ready to transmute chaos into victory! Each year, champions battle for the ultimate prize: The Throne. Will your mastery of science and sorcery be enough to claim it?

Alchemical tinctures and bubbling brews are the building blocks of battle. Through genuine genius or malicious madness, she fills her flasks with elixirs of strength to over power, concoctions to cause corrosion, and bottles that bolster defenses.

The Alchemist is the first in The Dice Throne Universe single characters.

A heart-pumping, fast-playing game of skilled card play and dice manipulation. The game is played over a series of rounds where you will be rolling your hero's dice up to 3 times. You will use the combination of dice symbols or numbers to activate your hero's abilities to attack your opponent(s).

Dice Throne is a game of intriguing dice, tactical card play, powerful heroes, and unique abilities.

It's a fast-paced 2-6 player combat game (1v1, 2v2, 3v3, 2v2v2, or free-for-all). Select from a variety of heroes that play and feel completely distinct from one another. Attack opponents and activate abilities by rolling your hero's unique set of five dice. Accumulate combat points and spend them on cards that have a large range of effects, such as granting permanent hero upgrades, applying status effects, and manipulating dice directly (yours, your teammate's, or even your opponent's).

All heroes are compatible with any heroes from any other season of Dice Throne
Each hero has their own beautiful set of custom dice
Use upgrade cards to improve your hero's abilities as the game progresses
Play action cards strategically to manipulate dice and surprise your foe

In Much Too Much?! players try to guess the correct answers to as many bizarre, fascinating, and utterly crazy questions as they can, but using a limited and random set of numbers requires strategic planning.

Each row has a question with a numerical answer, that's hard to guess exactly, so players are trying to guess a number as close as possible without going over. Players place a number tile on a row to increase the current number. Starts with a preset of "0000". Number tiles can be placed on top of a zero, or other previously placed numbers, and 6s can be 9s. Players then place a marker to claim that row – for now.

Once out of markers, it's time to score. If the answer is equal or greater than the highest guess, players receive points. If the answer is lower, players bust.

After three rounds whoever has the most points wins!

Much Too Much?! is a varied, funny, and surprising party game for anyone looking for both a challenge and some entertainment.

—description from the publisher

You're never too young to have a senior moment or to play Senior Moments, a memory game for anyone who might need the practice.

Senior Moments Travel Edition is a compact, quick-play memory challenge card game themed around classic “senior moment” jokes—with a friendly, pop culture twist. The oldest player starts as the active player. The person to their right is the reader and draws from the deck. If a Special Card appears, the reader keeps it to use later and draws again until a Memory Card is pulled. The reader then reads the Memory Card twice, slowly and clearly. The active player must repeat the list of items—or perform the listed actions and will score 1 point for each item remembered or action performed correctly.

Play continues clockwise until someone reaches the agreed point goal. After the first player hits the goal, everyone else gets one final turn to try to match or beat it. If there’s a tie, those players go head-to-head, taking turns reading lists to each other until one misses—last one standing wins.

—description from the publisher
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
Across the Greensea, as far as the edges of the Outer Rim, no airship is cheered louder than a bloomhunter when it comes into port. From the bustling capital of Ash Aris to the starry sky islands and distant glaciers, these adventurous captains voyage far and wide to gather the finest flowers, then ride the wind home in time to sell them to commoners and aristocrats alike.

The most important day of the year for a bloomhunter is the Spring Festival of Ash Aris, when the five noble families who rule the capital island will select their official florist for the year to come. Bloomhunting companies vie to outdo each other with grand displays, generous donations, and partnerships forged throughout the archipelago. The Greensea is a vibrant culture in the prime of its history, brought to life through the vivid illustrations of Tom McGrath and Brad Alston.

Bloomhunter is a game about exploring and building for two to four players, featuring a novel tile-laying mechanic. In under two hours, players create a new archipelago for each session, then survey the modular map to gather flowers. Take care when you place a tile: you'll need to match colors to secure new sources of flowers, but matching weather patterns is also vital, since you can only move freely along tiles of the same weather pattern.

By carefully choosing your actions each turn, you'll be able to discover new islands, chart their terrain, and win over local allies, represented by a deck of over 30 unique cards. Gain points by turning in bouquets, giving lavish gifts to the nobles, and spreading your influence to new lands. The winning captain will be the one who uses their actions most efficiently and marshals their resources to deploy in high-scoring turns. Can you defeat your rivals to win the day?

The Last Council is a board game for 3-6 players.

Each round, players secretly vote and bid power on difficult dilemmas, using influence, timing, and unique hero abilities to shape the outcome. Easy to learn yet rich in consequence, the game thrives on hidden intent, shifting alliances, and unforgettable moral choices.

The city is divided. The king grows weaker with each passing dawn, and whispers say his strength will not last another full moon. In the shadow of the throne, the council has gathered power piece by piece, its influence seeping through every hall, every gate, every prayer.

With no heir to claim the crown, the question has become inevitable: which of you will rule when the last royal breath fades? Some rise on the faith of the people; others on gold, leverage, and secrets. Some bind themselves to law and order; others learn to rule from the dark.

Once, the city ruled the realm through unity and will. Now it fractures—forced to rely on uneasy alliances, desperate bargains, and aid that never comes without cost. Each passing day brings a new trial, and every choice reshapes not only the city’s future, but the fate of the kingdom it commands.

In this realm, crisis is constant—and within every disaster lies an opportunity for greatness.

—description from the publisher

Monopoly: Predator is a Monopoly game with theme around the movie Predator. In this version of Monopoly players must buy up property and build homes in the Predator universe. This game has six exclusive collectible tokens, thematic color palette and custom board artwork.

Collect cards to earn points (by color or shape), but be careful: You get only one action per turn. And your options change suddenly, when the game switches into the endgame.

The game features NSFW photography (at varying states of undress) by the game-maker, with a line of faceup cards and a line of facedown cards set up in the middle of the table. Facedown, the cards define what sets you can make (and how they score at the end). Faceup, they can be used to make those sets.

You may get the chance to turn facedown cards faceup, but not the other way around, and not before endgame is triggered, when tensions really mount!

boardgame: Smoking Gun: Murder at Gelp Manor
description:
In Smoking Gun, players are junior detectives competing for promotion at the Fahler Police Department by investigating a 1985 murder at Gelp Wellness Resort. Victory goes not to whoever solves the case first, but to whoever proves they contributed the most through Credibility Points.

Players navigate six locations within Gelp Manor, gathering Investigation Clues to verify evidence across four categories: Suspect, Weapon, Time of Death, and Motive. Two matching clues verify evidence—single clues mean nothing. Detectives question suspects, examine Physical Evidence, and strategically position the Detective marker to control information flow.

What sets Smoking Gun apart is its unique scoring through narrative style. Players earn Credibility by presenting Case Updates—playing up to four Case cards while weaving 1980s cultural references into their theories. Accuse Tina Turnin of poisoning Printz while mentioning Lettuce Patch Dolls or MTV? That's bonus points. This rewards creative storytelling alongside deductive reasoning.

The innovative Red Herring mechanic adds strategic bluffing—play your token to discard a card face-down, but opponents never know if it's legitimate or a bluff. Players can advance their cases with unverified evidence, creating lasting uncertainty.

With over 38,000 unique murder combinations and solo mode included, Smoking Gun blends competitive deduction with theatrical 80s storytelling for fresh experiences every game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ace of Spades: Ace of Base and Ace of Space
description:
The Ace of Base is a new scene 10 encounter. This enemy does not have a life value. In order to defeat it, you must play a hand that includes an Ace and Queen of the same suit. You cannot use the Jammed Gun rule.

The Ace of Space, if added to the deck, requires that you remove the Ace of Hearts, Diamonds, or Clubs from the Poker deck. The Ace of Space plays in any Hand just like any other Ace in your deck, and it adds 3 extra damage points as normal. Since it is it's own suit, you cannot include it in Flush or Straight Flush Hands.

Once per game, if you have the Ace of Space in your hand, you may remove it completely from the game to immediately banish the Enemy that you're facing. This works with any Enemy except for the Final Nemesis. If used to banish enemy, return the Ace of Space and that enemy to the box, you do not benefit from any bonus the banished enemy may provide.

The city never sleeps, and neither do the crews looking to take it for everything it's worth.

You and your rivals all have eyes on the same prize—vaults full of cash, high-end loot, and a chance to cement your crew’s reputation as the best in the business.

But there’s a problem: the more jobs each crew pulls, the more attention they attract. Sooner or later, the cops will be breathing down someone’s neck.

Play it smart or play it fast—every job adds Heat, and when things get too hot, the whole operation could go up in smoke.

The crew that scores big without getting busted takes the glory.

The others? Let’s just say orange jumpsuits aren’t a good look.

The scene is set. The clock is ticking. Can your crew outplay, outsmart, and outlast the competition and the law?

The game includes 62 cards, featuring a mix of Character, Item, and Action cards.

It takes only a few minutes to learn… but mastering it? That’s another job entirely.

Gameplay supports 2–4 players.

The PDF includes all printable 62 cards required to play DUELYN: Vault Breakers, along with Heat and Loot tokens. Tokens are optional—you may substitute the tokens with coins, tiddlywinks, D10’s, or pen and paper.

—description from the publisher

"Test your heart and your smarts! Sweethearts Love & Romance Trivia celebrates love and romance with charming questions perfect for couples, friends, and everyone in between." - Description from box

Sweethearts Love & Romance Trivia is your basic trivia game, 100 cards and rules for 2-4 players or teams. Players decide how many points they want to play for to win, and answer questions to get there. Once they reach that point goal, they win!

Intended to be gifted for Valentine's Day, and released alongside two other similar gift items (ask your date questions, and date night scratch-offs).
This expansion contains 130 new cards with 260 new words, plus 4 replacement pens for Krakel-Orakel.
This expansion contains 130 new cards with 260 new words for adults, plus 4 replacement pens for Krakel-Orakel.

This is the first title in a new series of stand-alone sword-and-sorcery solo adventures using the 2DSAS (Two Dice Solo Adventure System), written by Andrea Sfiligoi and published by Ganesha Games. If you played our zine game Demonslayer, you already know the system. We plan to publish four titles per year. Each booklet will be around 48–64 pages. You build a character using a very simple point system (you have 15 points to allocate among five Attributes) and then begin a procedural hexcrawl. If your character survives, you can keep any items found and any experience points, and replay this booklet or move on to another title in the series. However, every book is fully stand-alone. The second title, The Temple of Death, will be available in about 40 days. SPIDER HUNT Refugees speak of clicking sounds in the night, of cattle vanishing into the undergrowth, and of webs thick as ropes strangling the trees. Thousands of spiders of all sizes swarm across the land. You must find the Brood Queen and end this infestation. Grab a pencil, paper, and two dice. They will be your sole companions for this adventure. NO OTHER PURCHASE NECESSARY. NOT RECOMMENDED FOR ARACHNOPHOBES. The system All rolls in the game are “roll under the relevant Attribute on 2d6.” Want to wound a foe? Roll Might or less. Want to spot a clue? Roll Intelligence or less. Want to dodge an attack or climb a tree? Roll Dexterity or less. Want to get information or a good deal from an NPC? Roll Charisma or less. Maps are generated randomly on a blank hex sheet.

—description from the publisher

A box containing everything you need to organise a girl's party : a book, a card game and a dice, badges and a booklet to fill in.
In the card game, each player plays a character and has to roll the dice, take a card and answer the question or get a penalty. They also have 1 joker and 2 objection cards to expose other players as liars. The winner is the player who has received the fewest penalty cards.

Warlocks & Warlords is luck and skill based battle card game where 2 players test their hand at strategy with the added thrill of a 'luck of the draw' card game. The game consists of 1 deck shared by both players. The aim is to clear your opponents' board of cards and 'Attack' the player(s) to eliminate them and win the game.

The cards for the game are separated into 2 types:

     Units
     Assists


Units are the core focus of the battle themed gameplay. Each 'normal' Unit has a value between 1 and 10. any Unit of any value can be played onto the board. but on their own they do nothing. Stacking them up in numerical order (1,2,3 or 7,8 for example) converts them into attacking Warriors with their Value being the top number.
Or alternatively, Units of the same numerical value can be stacked up to form Walls that blanket protect your brigade of Warriors.

There are also Special Units with Unique Abilities to help you push forward.

Secondly we have Assists. Cards which are discarded from your hand to aid you in your conquest to victory by having abilities such as drawing cards from the deck, 'destroying' your opponent's Units, or stealing cards from their hand.

The deck is shuffled, decide whose going first, then both players are dealt 5 cards.

Each turn of gameplay follows these 3 Steps or 'Phases':

     Preparation - This phase is where players play their Units, use Assists or use the Abilities of on board Special Units.
     Warfare - Your typical 'Battle Step'. This Phases is where one player attacks the other to gain control of the board and try to push for the win.
     Respite - The turn player draws cards from the deck units they are holding 5 cards then it becomes the next players turn.


A winner is decided when one player clears the other players' board of cards and attacks the player for the Win, or leaves them in a position where they're forced to concede.

—description from the designer

Boom! An apple has fallen in the garden. Who will find it and eat it first – the colorful Worm or the slow Snail? Let’s all help the Worm together. Go, Worm, go! Faster!
Help the Worm reach the apple before the Snail! Players must work together. On your turn, roll the dice. Turn over as many cards as the number you rolled. You must place at least one worm card on the board – this is how the Worm moves! If you don’t place a worm card, the Snail moves one step forward.

The player draws as many cards from the top of the pile as the number of dots rolled and places them face up on the table. The player then checks which cards can be added to the board. Each correctly placed card allows the Worm to move one step forward. During their turn, the player may place all suitable cards on the board.
Any cards that cannot be placed must be turned face down and put into the used cards pile beside the draw pile.

     If one or more “Move the Snail one step backward” cards appear, the player decides whether to use them. If they do, the Snail is moved backward by as many steps as there are such cards. In this case, the player cannot use any Worm cards during that turn. After completing the move, all revealed cards are placed into the used pile.
     If the player cannot place any Worm cards, the Snail moves one step forward.


—description from the publisher

Chartbreak is a music trivia game. In each round, one person is a moderator and draws a card with six numbered questions. They roll a d6 and read the question with the number they rolled. Then they have to pick one player to answer the question. If correct, both the moderator and the player who answered get 2 points; if wrong, both lose 1 point. Afterwards, the moderator asks one of the remaining questions of their choice to each of the other players, gaining points for correct answers, but not losing points for wrong ones. So the goal of the moderator is to guess which of the other players might be most capable of answering a given question.
