After a certain number of rounds (dependent on the number of players), whoever has the most points, wins. 
The six questions on each card come in six different formats. Some require scanning a QR code and listening to a song on Spotify.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: White Death: Velikiye Luki, the Stalingrad of the North
description:
White Death: Velikiye Luki, The Stalingrad of the North is a game of the battle of Velikiye Luki in the winter of 1942-1943. The game begins with the massive Soviet offensive that led to the encirclement of the city of Velikiye Luki by the forces of the Red Army’s Kalinin Front against the Wehrmacht’s 3rd Panzer Army during the Winter Campaign of 1942–1943 with the objective of liberating the Russian city of Velikiye Luki as part of the northern pincer of the Rzhev-Sychevka Strategic Offensive Operation (Operation Mars).

White Death: Velikiye Luki, The Stalingrad of the North is a 2-player Frank Chadwick game covering the battle of Velikiye Luki in the winter of 1942-1943. Originally published by Game Designers’ Workshop (GDW) in 1979, this new edition preserves the innovative original design, while significantly improving the play experience by incorporating errata, clarifying ambiguities, updating graphics, and adding new informational markers and map features to help players.

Product Information:

Complexity: Medium
Playing time: 4+ Hours
Solitaire Suitability: Medium
Players: 2
Map Scale: 1 Mile Per Hex
Time Scale: 5 Days Per Turn
Unit Scale: Company/Regiment
Game Designer: Frank Chadwick
Game Artist: Phoebe Hinkle
New Edition Developer: Mathew Hinkle
Project Director: Julian Thomas

Game Components:
2 Countersheets
1 22”x34” Map
1 Expanded Velikiye Luki Map
4 Order of Battle Displays
4 Order of Arrival Displays
1 Order of Arrival Card
2 Player Aid Cards
1 Game Rules Booklet
2 6-sided Dice
I Game Box

-description from publisher's website

You are the commander of a Soviet army combat group in WW2. You will take command in the hardest fought campaigns from 1941 Operation Barbarossa to 1945 Battle of Berlin!

Each of your Campaigns involve both operational and tactical decisions. At the start of a Campaign, you select the Units and Commanders to make up your force. During each Week of the Campaign, you decide which Enemy Battalions to attack, which of your forces to allocate, and then resolve each Battle using the Tactical Battlefield.

Your Commanders gain Experience with every Battle, but they also suffer Stress. Each Week, you must decide how hard to push your men to achieve Victory.

T-34 Leader builds on the core mechanics of Tiger Leader and Sherman Leader while introducing new Units (Snipers, Partisans, Command Groups and Cossacks), various Difficulty Levels, rules for Elevation and Urban Combat, and an Opportunity Fire step that allows Infantry and Snipers to break cover and make a surprise attack!

T-34 Leader is a Solitaire wargame, so you can play at your own pace.

Expansion packs will offer Mud/Snow Terrain tiles (and Special Rules)}, Expansion packs that provide variable Units, and three full-blown Battle Packs covering The Winter War (Soviet v. Finland), Manchuria (Soviet v. Japan) and Prokhorovka (Soviet v. Germans in the wider Battle of Kursk).

boardgame: The Last in the Woods: Raven
description:
This card represents a special playable character available exclusively to Gamefound backers from the campaign that ran from January 8 to February 8, 2026.

Raven: Once per turn during exploration choose 1 of 2 resources. Put the other on the bottom of the deck.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Similar to Tic-Tac-Toe, but where the 3 pieces of each player shrink until they're gone (first-in first-out order).
Two players alternate their turns and the game is over either when one player wins or when both players agree they cannot win.

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission C37 – Defend The Mall, Save The Baby
description:
Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C37 is a medium difficulty exploration mission for the Toxic City Mall

Material needed: Toxic City Mall. 
Death is everywhere but life is invincible. Your team is entrenched in a mall, helping a woman to give birth. Today, you are heroes!

Designed by Scott Hill

Designed by players for the Kyoko mission contest, and later published by the original studio, C missions are supplementary missions released online for print and play.

C38 is a Hard difficulty exploration mission for Prison Outbreak

Material needed: Prison Outbreak 
The army used the prison as a stronghold before being overrun with zombies. There has to be interesting stuff here!

Designed by Simone Burgio

First or Last is a 2p only climbing/shedding game.

At the start of each hand, after cards are dealt, one player must decide if they will shed out first or shed out last. The rules of card play change based on which one they choose.

A deal consists of players having cards in their hand (hidden from the other player) as well as a strawman pile (a face up pile of cards on the table in a 2-3-2 formation).
Cards in the strawman pile are part of your hand for scoring, but not part of your hand for shedding out. However, if you pass you must draw one card from your strawman pile, adding it to your hand as another card you must play to shed out. 
Lastly, each player has 1 wild card, but it is stuck in the back row of their strawman pile. If you want to gain access to it, you need to work your way through your strawman pile.

The game is played over 4 hands (short game) or 8 hands (long game). The player with the most points after 4/8 hands wins!

There are additional rules that can turn the game into a compendium game.

—description from the designer

It’s a sunny day and Emma and Ben meet at the playground. Joining them are Emma’s fluffy dog Sammy and Ben’s curious cat Mimmi. The four friends are looking forward to a joyful afternoon on their favourite playground attraction: the big seesaw.
However, an unpleasant surprise awaits them: the seesaw is broken! Its colourful boards are scattered everywhere, and nothing moves anymore. Together, they decide to fix the seesaw so that all children can enjoy it once again. A real team adventure begins!
What begins as an ordinary day turns into a cheerful construction project with lots of colours, first counting, and friendship. And in the end, the seesaw rocks again – and the afternoon at the playground is saved!

Roll the dice and jump forward the corresponding number of tiles. It's up to you to decide which tiles to jump on. Find the tiles that match the colors on the seesaw boards and put them back together.

—description from the publisher

Book your accommodation, take the ski lifts and hit the slopes, stop off at the restaurant and enjoy unlimited après-ski!

But be careful...

Of other skiers and their attacks: falls, lost credit cards...
Of your own excesses: fondue overdose, génépi doping...

And what if your desires change!

Will you be able to keep up the pace throughout your stay? Will you become the resort's new legend?

Skiiks, simple rules for an immersive, tactical, and interactive game.

Aim of the game: A game is played over a stay consisting of several days. Each day begins with a roll of the dice to determine the objective in terms of activities to be completed by all players. But this objective can change at any time with a new roll of the dice.

Points are only counted at the end of the day. The winner is the player who has scored the most points after the trip.

Ωmegas is a Greek collectible card game focused on strategic deck building and competitive battles.

Each player builds a deck consisting of 22 different Ωmegas character cards. Duplicate characters are not allowed within the same deck, making every deck truly unique and emphasizing thoughtful composition and long-term strategy.

The game is built around a structured progression system with four distinct stages, allowing characters of different power levels to interact in a balanced way. No single card is inherently dominant or weak, as success depends on how players combine characters, manage progression, and adapt to each stage of the game.

Ωmegas is designed as a collectible experience with randomized card distribution and a strong focus on tactical decision-making. This first edition of the game is currently available in Greek.

—description from the designer

Welcome to New Odessa. A city which was once safe and prosperous. Now the New Odessa Mob has its tentacles in every neighborhood and alley of the city, ruling with violence, corruption, and fear.

Each player will step into the shoes of an everyday hero,  who has a reason to seek justice, uncover a mystery, or exact revenge on the mob. Players will navigate the map of New Odessa, choosing Jobs ranging from attacking mob strongholds to protecting endangered 
citizens. The game uses a blend of action-selection, multi use cards, and resource management mechanics.

Players will gain reputation points throughout the game by completing jobs, winning battles against the mob, stashing away cash and gems, and gaining favor with the local citizens. In addition to this, each player's character has their own unique specialty and scoring opportunities. The player who accumulated the most reputation when the end game triggers is the winner of the game.

-description from designer

Embark on a journey to the mystical realm of Eldronia with "Crystalline Wars: Arcane Battlegrounds."

This game invites players to immerse themselves in a world where wizards wield immense power, and the mastery of elemental crystals determines the fate of all.

Game Overview
Crystalline Wars: Arcane Battlegrounds is a tabletop skirmish game that captures the essence of its card game predecessor, Crystalline Wars, which is set against the backdrop of the tumultuous Crystalline Wars era in the Fyndoria Universe, players will find themselves navigating the strife-torn lands of Eldronia, engaging in battles marked by the brutal use of fused magical elemental crystals.

Gameplay Mechanics
The game operates on a point-based system, allowing players to assemble their team of wizards with allocated points for spells and items. Elemental Crystal Tokens play a crucial role, as they are required for casting spells during the game.

Players draw these tokens each turn, introducing an element of unpredictability and strategic depth. The game also features a character activation system using coloured dice or counters, which adds a layer of excitement and surprise to each turn.

Our vision was to create a game experience that relies minimally on dice, using them solely for initial turn order determination and initiative draws from a bag (unless you use counters). The focus is on strategic play rather than dice-dependent combat outcomes.

What's Inside
"Crystalline Wars: Arcane Battlegrounds" includes detailed rules for setting up the game, character data sheets, and an array of spells and items to enhance your wizards' capabilities. The game's print-out section offers Elemental Crystal tokens, other tokens, pre-built wizard character sheets and forces, and blank wizard sheets to build your own small force of wizards, allowing for a fully customizable experience.

The Fellowship of the Ring is a game for two players using a standard deck of 52 cards. Try to keep a ring of cards balanced between red and black until one of you secretly becomes Boromir and betrays the Fellowship. At that point, Boromir plays black and the other player red. Can black subtly tilt the balance in their favor before red notices and defends?

—description from the designer

The Maelstrom is a dynamic map region tile, churning at the center of the map. Attune to the maelstrom when you activate your character, visit it to deepen your understanding, and use these resources to trigger Maelstrom aberrations to bend the rules of the game, flipping through this sets’ aberration cards like a book to access different effects. This two-pack mini-expansion introduces significant gameplay impact with very few components.

Includes 16 cards, 5 tokens, and the Maelstrom region tile.

—description from the publisher

Introducing Jump Masters, a cutthroat battler inspired by Chinese checkers! This easy-to-jump, hard-to-master game from the talented Manny Trembley is the colorful caper of your tabletop dreams.

In Jump Masters, players command a drafted team of heroes in a skirmish. Taking turns, players jump their heroes around the arena to defeat their opponents and gain victory points! When you jump over a friendly hero, you gain energy! When you jump over an opposing hero, you attack, reducing the two heroes’ energy until one is defeated! Chain multiple jumps together to pull off more powerful turns. Each hero has its own unique ability; Drafting the right combination of heroes and using their abilities to your advantage are key to success! Terrain will block you (or empower you!), so you’ll need to plan accordingly. Be the first to collect the required amount of victory points by defeating your opponents, and you’ll be declared the Jump Master!

-description from publisher

Sixteen passengers set sail near a secluded tropical island—but as the voyage unfolds, a series of mishaps sends them tumbling overboard.

On their turn, players roll dice to move boats and determine the fate of the passengers. Dice choices are critical, granting powers to throw passengers overboard, swap their seats, or even change the positions of the boats themselves—all in pursuit of valuable adventure points.

As the chaos escalates, players may play cards when specific conditions are met to score additional points. Staying alert is essential, as adventure points can be earned at any time—even when it’s not your turn.

The race ends when the first boat reaches shore. When the dust settles, the player with the most adventure points emerges victorious.

-description from publisher

Welcome to Responsible Advancement of Transgenic Science - RATS

Engineering rats is our game.
Competing for cheese is theirs.
Six million dollars later, our rats are better, stronger, faster.
They are no longer challenged by mazes.

Instead, we have designed special cars that have bumpers to keep them safe, a mouse ball for them to run in for power and control, and a rubber pellet shooter to make each other lose control. We put them all on an arena and see who gets out with the cheese.

In The Rat Race, players control the rat driven cars to try to get the cheese and escape the area. They shoot and ram their opponents to force them to drop the cheese and pick up powerful power ups along the way! A platform racing and bumper car board game! The objective is to drive to the center of the arena, pick up the cheese, and escape with it!

The game comes with six different cars, each with their own limits of Speed, Steering, Power Ups, and Shots. Cubes are used to track the how many of each a car has remaining. Each car also has its own special ability. There are eight different drivers, each with their own special ability as well.

On your turn, you gain an extra Steering cube, and may choose to slow down or speed up by adding or removing an existing Speed cube. You can used any or all of your Steering, Power Up, and Shot cubes during your turn.

You must use all of your Speed every turn, which is tracked by sliding the cubes down into the Used position. Speed cubes stay on your car from turn to turn and are moved back up into the available position at the beginning of your turn.

To turn your car one "click" left or right requires you to return a Steering cube to the pool. You must roll to see if you maintained control during your turn. If you fail, you drift the rest of your movement. Steering is your most important commodity!

When you shoot at another car, you return a Shot cube to the pool and roll to see if you hit. Each car has a limited number of Shots for the game, so they must manage them carefully as they do not come back.

What's a platform racing style game without Power Ups? For each Power Up cube a car has, they have a matching Power Up card. These cards give additional cubes, bonuses on die rolls, and can even change the positions of walls in the game to make an easier escape route, or block an opponent from escaping. When a Power Up is used, its cube is dropped back onto the board so it can be picked up again!

Let's not forget the cheese. When you pick it up, you must then escape with it. Whenever you are shot, rammed, or run into a wall or another car you drop it. It then bounces away randomly waiting for another rat to pick it up and try to escape!

For those unfamiliar with the first edition, Future War Commander 2 is a set of miniature wargame rules allowing you to play combined-arms games to represent futuristic and science fiction based battles.

Each player takes charge of a Battlegroup, which could be anything from a platoon right up to a division. The rules attempt to anticipate the warfare of the future to provide a game that is playable, easy to use and fast to play, based on sound military principles that have been projected into a believable future. 
Games can be played solo, one-on-one or with multiple players.

You can use any scale of miniatures to play the game, from 2mm right up to 28mm. Your miniatures can be based or unbased as this makes no difference when playing the game. One stand of figures represents an infantry squad and one model represents one item in real-life, whether that be an armoured fighting vehicle, a dropship or a Massive walker. Alternatively, one stand of miniatures can represent a platoon when playing at a higher level of command.

So whether you want to fight it out in a galaxy far, far away, or take control of the vital compounds on a desert planet, Future War Commander contains everything you need.

The book also includes 17 scenarios for you to try, as well as two example army lists.

Song of the Wildkin is a cooperative adventure for 1 to 4 players. Find your missing mentor, Hammer Wildmane, and safeguard your community from the mysterious forces turning peaceful woodbeasts into frenzied threats.

The campaign unfolds across a series of Chapters, each carrying your party to a distinct region of the world. Your journey begins in the Giggling Glade, a sanctuary of art and song tended by fairies, and will lead you to places few Wildkin ever dream of.

Within a chapter, you will brave the wilds through a variety of Scenarios, featuring unique objectives, challenges, and discoveries. Each scenario takes about 1 hour to play, or you can finish a chapter in a single afternoon.

Chapters culminate in climactic Boss Battles. Defeating these powerful adversaries opens new paths forward, unlocks potent abilities, rewards rare items, and may even bring strange Unlockable Characters to your cause.

Only by facing these trials and uncovering the truth behind the woodbeasts’ corruption can balance be restored. Be as one, Wildkin.

—description from the publisher

Onni-peli is a two player game that's kind of Tic-Tac-Toe with goal cards. In addition there're so cute hand made figures in this game. Both players have three figures that players try to set in the shape shown in the goal card. This adds some deduction about the shape another player is aming with their figures.

Onni means luck in Finnish but there's not only the luck that matters when playing this Onni-peli. May the player with the best strategy win the game!

Cuan Cat Cafe is a strategic business management game where players step into the paws of savvy entrepreneurs. Your goal: maximize happiness by turning a tidy profit.

Manage your resources by purchasing premium ingredients to fulfill customer orders. While simple drinks are easy to make, high-end beverages require more ingredients but yield significantly higher returns. Reinvest your hard-earned "Cuan" into shop upgrades and decorative items to boost your prestige. In this cafe, the fluffiest feline with the most successful business wins!

—description from the designer

Monopoly: Red Bull Racing is a Monopoly game with a Formula One and the Red Bull Racing team theme. The game includes six exclusive racing tokens, race-track inspired properties, and the board and cards have a Red Bull branding.

Office Chaos is a competitive take-that board game set inside a chaotic modern workplace where players race to clear their task list and reach the Boss before anyone can sabotage them.

Each turn, players navigate departments, manage tasks, and use mischievous tactics to dump work on rivals, block their progress, or protect themselves from the Boss’s unpredictable gaze.

The first employee to end a turn with zero tasks and successfully report to the Boss wins, but in this office, even the cleanest desk can get messy in seconds.

boardgame: Major League Baseball Pop-up Dice Board Game
description:
How to Play
Players choose their favorite MLB team and place their game piece at the start of the board. On your turn, you press the pop-up dice dome, then move your piece forward based on the roll. As you advance, you land on baseball-themed spaces that represent game situations like hits, outs, steals, and special plays. Some spaces let you move ahead, others send you back, and a few can dramatically change the momentum—just like a real baseball game. Turns rotate quickly, keeping everyone engaged and the pace moving.

How to Win
The objective is to be the first player to reach the finish and score the winning run. Victory comes down to smart movement, good timing, and a little bit of baseball luck. While you can’t control the dice, paying attention to special spaces and capitalizing when opportunities arise can give you the edge. The first player to safely make it home wins the game.

Easy to learn and quick to play, the MLB Pop-Up Dice Game is perfect for baseball fans, kids, and casual game nights where everyone wants a light, competitive experience with a big league theme.

1809: Talavera is the second volume in the"Fix Bayonets!" series of Napoleonic tactical wargames. It covers, at the battalion level, the two days of the battle with one player in charge of the Anglo-Spanish armies and the other leading the French forces. It can be played solo, playing both sides.

Components:

    One (2-panel) 55 1/2" x 22" mounted map.
    6 double-sided color counter sheets with 18 "Fix Bayonets!" markers, 168 3/8x3/4" markers, 220 1/2x1/2" markers, 372 3/4x3/4" unit counters.
    One 24-pages Battle booklet.
    One 56-pages Rules booklet.
    Two identical 17x11" player aid cards.
    One 17x11" Turn & Tracks sheet (similar to the one in Albuera) 
     two custom 10-sided dice, one for each side.
    One 11x8.5" + two 17x11" Order of Battle sheets
    47 Leader and Special Event cards

boardgame: The Barons' War Second Edition: Starter Set
description:
Lead Your Retinue to Glory on the Medieval Battlefield!

The medieval world was shaped by power struggles, shifting alliances, and brutal skirmishes.

From duelling lords and rebellious barons to castle sieges and battlefield clashes, warriors fought for honour, power, and survival. Now, it's your turn to take command.

This game brings dynamic, narrative-driven tabletop battles to life, allowing you to lead a medieval retinue into tactical skirmishes filled with cunning strategy and brutal combat. While inspired by the First Barons' War, this versatile system enables you to recreate historical engagements or craft your own medieval conflicts, whether defending a stronghold, leading a daring raid, or fighting for the crown itself.

This box contains the essentials you need to play The Barons' War, including enough 28mm miniatures to build two 500-point starter retinues or a single 1000-point retinue. Also come with The Barons' War (Second Edition): Core Rulebook.

Gather your forces, rally your warriors, and write your name into history!
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: The Kittens of New Orleans: Pits and Platforms
description:
Use memory, strategy, and abilities of your kittens or vermin pieces to have 3 of them cross the game board and off the other side (your opponent’s side of the board), all the while casting  your opponent’s pieces from the ramshackle platforms down into the swamp below.

In the game of Platforms & Pits, your Cats and Rats will battle across a network of rickety wooden platforms built across the alligator infested swamp of the New Orleans bayou.

In combat, any piece thrown from the safety of a platform into a pit becomes food for the gators and is lost from play. The game ends when one player has crossed the opponent’s end of the game board 3 times or all of their pieces have been cast into the bayou.

—description from the designers

This is a childhood-themed Cards Against Humanity clone. Follows the standard Cards Against Humanity rules.

Turn your childhood nostalgic moments, iconic characters, heroes, and villains into a full-blown comedy session

Presenting you the entertaining, highly anticipated, and hilariously funny Game Against Your Childhood- a perfect game to turn any gathering into an instantly legendary game night

Despite the name, Cards Against Childhood is NOT made for kids. If you’ve played any Cards Against Humanity games, you know what I’m talking about. The game is supposed to be played by ages 17 or older, for 4 to 20 or more players.

Gameplay:

     Pick one player to be the Genie "Card Reader" for one round, who deals everyone 10 white cards;
     The Card Reader reads one black card, and everyone submits their funniest white card;
     The player who played the funniest white card gets to keep the black card of the round as an awesome point. The Winner for the round will be the New card Reader.


Winning:
If you win one round, you get one awesome point.
players can agree to stop when someone hits 5, 7, or 10 Points or more

boardgame: The Silver Bayonet: Venice – The Alchemist's Legacy
The Silver Bayonet: The Barbary Coast – A Rising Tide
description:
Sailors know well to be on their guard when sailing near the Barbary states. Corsairs and pirates may be rife in these waters, but for all their menace, they are still just mortal. In hidden coves and beneath sand-swept ruins on these rocky shores lie the secrets of ancient civilisations – and those who protect them. While rumours of lost treasure may tempt the bold to brave these perils, the legend of an ancient curse that dooms those who seek its power keeps the wise away.

A Rising Tide explores the Barbary states on land and sea as Napoleon’s wars and the battle against the Harvestmen continue to rage. From port towns and isolated islands to the ruins of ancient Carthage, a competitive campaign and five solo scenarios present players with a host of new challenges, including powerful pirate fleets and creatures lost to myth, as they seek to uncover a plot whose roots reach deep into the past…

Separate the 2 decks  •  Shuffle each deck and place on the table.

Flip the top Ice Breaker deck card face up.

Flip the top Penguin Panic deck card face up! THE PANIC BEGINS!

Players compete to come up with a qualifying word first. The first player to finish saying a qualifying word is the winner of that round and receives the flipped “Penguin Panic” card to record a point.

To determine the letter in play, reference the color of the circle on the face down “Penguin Panic” playing deck. If the circle is red then the red letter is in play. If the circle is blue then the blue letter is in play.

To be a qualifying word, the word must start with the correct letter. The word cannot be the word on the face up card in play, cannot be a name (Michelle, Lonnie, Wanda etc.), cannot be a number (three, seven, fifty, four etc.) and cannot be a word that has been used before to win a card.

When a PENGUIN card is flipped, the face up card on the “ICE BREAKER” deck comes into play.

The player who collects the most cards/points when the deck is finished is the winner!

Be the first to come up with a word that matches both the letter tiles on the table and the question being read aloud to score points. Choose between 100 trivia and 100 party questions, flip the hourglass, and let the game begin!

boardgame: Your Magnificent 7: Western Gunfight Rules with a Cinematic Flair
description:
A set of wild west rules that pit your 'cast' of seven characters against your opponent's.  Design your team, ensure they have the skills you want, and set out down Main Street for the final cinematic showdown.  The winner is the side left standing at the end.  During the battle you may also progress as you gain credit for all your good (or bad) deeds and exploits.

A set of wargame rules designed for 28mm figures and a suitable wild west-themed tabletop. What you’ll need besides these rules are a friendly opponent, seven 25/28mm miniatures that represent your magnificent 7 frontier group, a tabletop with Wild West terrain, an imperial tape measure and two six-sided dice.

Next Day! is a 3–6 player cooperative party game where players attempt to deliver packages and complete challenges!

As a team, players must deliver 100 points' worth of packages by going to their respective addresses on the board.
Individually, players must get the most amount of D.I.K. points. This is achieved by secretly completing challenges.
Players take turns driving the van to achieve these objectives.

A turn (driving the van) consists of picking up 4 time tokens and spending those tokens on actions: moving the van on the board, drawing a D.I.K card, repairing the van, or collecting a new package.

Each package has one or more events that correspond to the package; when covered by a time token, the driver fetches that event and reads it aloud. 3 options are presented, and the driver must choose one. Then the outcome is read aloud, and played out (lose time, damage the van, etc.).

D.I.K cards are challenges that players must do secretly during the game. These can include singing, dancing, insulting each other, etc. If a player notices another player doing a D.I.K, they can wager one of their own D.I.Ks and try to guess it. If successful, they claim the other players' D.I.K and keep the points; if not, they lose their wagered D.I.K.

The game ends when the team either reaches 100 points, destroys the van, or runs out of time on four packages.

—description from the designer

A quick "Snap!"-style party game about spanking pigs, declaring doubles, evading errors and becoming the Pig Spanking Champion.

Play starts with everyone having a deck of at least 10 cards each (depending on player count) on which are a variety of animals: pigs, pugs, walruses, cows, hippos, zebracorns and beans. Simultaneously, everyone turns over one card each.

Any pig turned up must be "spanked" (players cover it with their hand, or put their hand on top of a quicker hand!) as quickly as possible. If any other animal shows up more than once, everyone must shout its name. And if there's two pigs, spank twice - two pairs, shout twice.

Last player to spank or shout gets a Whammy (negative point). But be careful! The first player to spank or shout incorrectly also gets a Whammy.

Play continues with the next card in the players' decks until all cards have been played.

The winner is the player with the least Whammies and becomes Pig Spanking Champion!

boardgame: Monopoly: The Lord of the Rings – Hobbiton edition
description: Monopoly: The Lord of the Rings - Hobbiton edition is a Monopoly game that takes place in The shire. Each property space is replaced with Hobbiton landmarks. The playing tokens are made of metal, shaped into items such as Gandalf’s pipe, a hobbit foot, the One Ring, and a Green Dragon beer mug from J.R.R Tolkiens Middle Earth.

Meet Dave, the world’s friendliest Grim Reaper. He’s clocked out early for game night — and he’s bringing chaos with him. Dave's been bored lately, so he's made some duplicates of himself to make Game Night fun.

Grim Rummy is a quick, quirky twist on the classic card game, featuring cute reapers, spooky suits, and plenty of dark laughs. Match suits, play Wild Daves, and laugh in the face of mortality as you outwit your friends.

Fast-paced, easy to learn, and wickedly fun for 2–5 players.

Grim Rummy — because even Death deserves game night.

On their turn, each player will have the option to play a card from their hand into the draft pool to reap a card into their plot (or potentially play a card from their hand directly to their plot). Our Little Grim, Dave, also may come along from time to time to give a player a power up to reap additional cards.

At the end of the game, players score points for linked cards in their plot. Cards are linked by sequence within a suit or by a matching number to another suit. There are bonus points for groups more than 3 cards and the player with the most points after all the cards have been played wins the game.

-description from designer

This book is designed for two players: one player interacts directly with the book, while the other uses printable sheets downloaded from the website.

It's impossible to escape from Alcatraz prison... Or almost! 
The guard you bribed has given you one hour to reach the shore and find the canoe that will allow you to escape. 
But beware, you're not alone in your quest for freedom... 
In this adventure, you'll have to observe, manipulate objects, and solve puzzles just like in a real escape room.

Thanks to these riddles (encrypted alphabet, maze, logical deduction, etc.), you'll progress through the investigation and the book, aiming to find the solution in a maximum of 60 minutes!

Farmed is a quick and simple drafting game.

Each year, reveal one weather card and then each player draws five crop cards. Players then draft and play one crop, and then reveal another weather card. Crops can also be played face down under an existing crop to count as one weather of your choice, while weather draws can result in events or natural disasters.

After four cards have been drafted, the year ends and crops that met their weather conditions are scored. Bonus points can be earned for a variety of crop types. Previously planted crops will carry over to future years, and after four years the game ends.

The advanced rules add improvements to your fields and bonus points for producing certain crops.

The game consists of 2-4 players, each tying to move men around the game base and back to the life boat.  A shark moves around the base, randomly disrupting the play with his jaws and tail.  The first player to complete the trip around the base with at least one man, wins the game.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Seed Pattern Survivor: Ingama
description:
Seed Pattern - Ingama is packaged in a graphic box that includes:

1 x Seed Pattern Survivor - Ingama hard plastic miniature
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 30mm base and insert
1 x Feral Zanbato seed pattern card
1 x Feral Zanbato seed pattern gear card
1 x Living rules glossary card
1 x Large matte art print

The broken sword sits atop the memorial of her twin. The ever-present reminder is a bilious sickness in her churning guts. Pouring maddening drink over the incandescent grief in the pit of her stomach brings momentary relief. In the moment of inspired madness, Ingama's fingers tremble, forging a new weapon of vengeance.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Furnace Lantern Promo Card
description:
The Furnace Lantern is a gear card that was included with the Frozen Survivor - Cyclamen miniature, which itself is not part of the game.

It has a crafting cost of one endeavor and one broken lantern, the following ability:

Whenever you move on the hunt board, gain +1 survival if you are Insulated.

If the survivor has a red affinity, the lantern also gives them +2 strength tokens on arrival, if paired with heavy armor in the right way.

A set of four spell cards to be added to the base game.

Quake allows a player to shift all tiles in a single row or column one space.

Tower Attack is a fantasy card game where players control champions with unique attack powers and abilities in a strategic duel. The first player to knock down two enemy towers wins. A 1v1 game, 15–30 minutes, for ages 10 and up.

This version flows well and is easy to understand.

Each player drafts 2 of the 6 champions, takes all of their cards, and shuffles them together with a base deck that is identical for both players.

During each turn, players play cards from their hand—featuring attack values and abilities—into one of the enemy tower lanes. If the total attack equals or exceeds the tower’s integrity, the tower is destroyed.

The first player to destroy two of their opponent's towers wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Tower Attack es un juego de cartas de fantasía en el que los jugadores controlan campeones con poderes de ataque y habilidades únicas en un duelo táctico. El primer jugador en derribar dos torres enemigas gana. Un juego 1 vs 1 de 15 a 30 minutos para 10 años o más.

Cada jugador elige 2 de los 6 campeones mediante un draft, toma todas sus cartas y las mezcla junto con un mazo base que es idéntico para ambos jugadores.

Durante cada turno, los jugadores juegan cartas de su mano —que presentan valores de ataque y habilidades— en uno de los carriles de torres enemigas. Si el ataque total iguala o supera la integridad de la torre, la torre es destruida.

El primer jugador en destruir dos torres de su oponente gana la partida.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

boardgame: Rolling Thunder! Rules for Epic Scale Fleet Actions, 1630-1720
description:
Recreate the grandest naval battles of the Age of Sail on the dining room table or your local club with Rolling Thunder!

The rules draw on the core principles of Mad for War and allow players to recreate entire battles with up to 100 ships per side through innovative and easy to understand game design.
family: History: War of the Spanish Succession

description:
Napoleonic Battles, Waterloo (NBW) is a hyper-realistic tactical-operational wargame that crosses the line between game and military simulator. It combines accessible mechanics with exceptional tactical depth and an immersive narrative typical of a professional command simulator.
Designed to be played solo or with up to 15 players, NBW always maintains historical balance and narrative coherence, enhancing the experience the greater the number of participants.
It includes four pre-designed modes (2, 3, 4 and 5 hours), with adjustable difficulty by narrative layers that allow you to modulate the intensity, realism and pace of the game.

Under the ‘Videos’ tab, there are three videos showing the prototype’s development and a fourth showing its current components, which are still under development. The fifth video has not been uploaded and covers basic mechanics; it is reserved for interested publishers and the board game rules as well.

The rules are compatible with Napoleonic modelling landscapes and minifigures, further enhancing the realism of the game and making it a gem for clubs with these interests.

—description from the designer

Footy Dice is a head-to-head soccer game that blends dice rolling with tactical decision-making. Choose your formation, then resolve dribbles, passes, shots, and tackles using dice and influence tokens that let you push your luck or play it safe. With gameplay that mirrors the flow and rules of real soccer, Footy Dice appeals to both board gamers and soccer fans — and stands strong as a tight abstract strategy game.

Players begin the game by secretly selecting a formation, then revealing it and setting up their teams on the field.
The field (or “pitch,” if you prefer) is divided into 15 distinct zones: each side’s 18-yard box, defense, midfield, and forwards, with each area split into a central zone and two wing zones.

Players are represented by round tokens numbered 6, 8, 10, or 12, corresponding to 6-, 8-, 10-, and 12-sided dice. In Footy Dice, the team in possession can dribble, pass, or shoot – just like in real soccer. Whenever a player is involved in an action, the manager (you) rolls that player’s corresponding die. Each action is resolved by rolling and comparing combinations of dice from different zones, depending on the action being attempted.

Dice are compared from highest to lowest, counting only dice that can be matched against an opponent’s rolls. If both players win an equal number of comparisons, the total sum of all dice rolled is used as a tiebreaker. If the offensive player wins, the action succeeds and they retain possession. If the defensive player wins, the ball transfers to their player with the highest roll, and the game clock advances by ten minutes.

Between each action is a movement phase. First, the team in possession may move a player, followed by one defensive movement, then another offensive movement (if that team passed initially). When a player with the ball is close enough to the goal, they may attempt a shot, with bonuses or penalties depending on their zone on the field. A successful roll results in a goal. After ninety minutes of play and two rounds of stoppage time, the game ends. The player with the most goals wins, or the match ends in a draw.

Footy Dice also offers players some additional tactical options and ways to mitigate their worse dice rolls via three types of action tokens: skill, teamwork, and tactics tokens. Skill tokens may be traded in before rolling to add +1 to any single roll for that action. Teamwork tokens may be played after rolling for an action to re-roll one dice involved – either yours or your opponent’s. Tactics tokens may be traded in for one of three different options: (1) skip a movement phase, (2) move one extra player during this movement phase, or (3) use your defensive move to tackle the player with the ball by rolling one-on-one against that player.

—description from the publisher

Together, these action and movement rules and token abilities combine to create a wide range of tactical and strategic possibilities. Flexible starting formations encourage replayability and creative play, while dice-rolling creates exciting and unpredictable moments that will have players on the edge of their seats.
This ten-card expansion as two wild cards, two nest cards that can be used to start new stacks, and a six-card cardinal suit.

Compete in 5 unique challenges that will reward luck, strategy, and bold choices in the expandalone sequel to Survivor: The Tribe Has Spoken. Win the Immunity Idol and vote out your competition to be the last player standing in standalone mode, then combine Let's Go To Rocks with the original game for the ultimate Survivor experience.

Think you can rise to the challenge? Prove it with Survivor: Let's Go To Rocks.

-description from designer

An adventure to be completed in 60 minutes!

It's impossible to escape from Alcatraz prison... Or almost!

The guard you bribed has given you one hour to do it, so hurry!

In this adventure, you'll have to collaborate, observe, manipulate objects, and solve puzzles, just like in a real escape room, to escape from this legendary prison in 60 minutes or less!

An easy card game for up to 4 players that utilizes memory and strategy.  Over 5 rounds, players attempt to swap out their cards to achieve the lowest value amongst their hand before calling out 'Boo' to end the round.  Each round has a different condition on how many cards you may peek at prior to the start or double value for round 5.  Lowest score wins the game.  There are action cards that allow you to look at your cards, other player cards, and more.

Smoczy Kryształ is a light, family-friendly fantasy adventure game for 2–4 players. Each player is a hero (warrior or mage) traveling across the board to reach the dragon Drakoniusz and claim the legendary Dragon Crystal. Along the way, heroes explore the land, fight monsters, and collect magical gems and artefacts.

On their turn, players roll two six-sided dice to move their pawn across the board. Landing on certain spaces triggers events such as drawing monster or adventure cards, which are resolved with simple dice-based tests combined with the hero’s attributes (Strength, Intelligence, Magic). Players can gain artefacts that improve abilities or interfere with opponents, and if two players meet on the same space, they may fight each other in a quick, non-eliminating duel.

To win, a player must be the first to fulfill all victory conditions at once: reach the dragon’s lair, collect a required number of gem tokens, and defeat a set number of monsters. Reaching the dragon alone isn’t enough — only the hero who arrives fully prepared claims the Dragon Crystal and wins the game.

boardgame: Detective Box: Le Tueur au Tarot
description:
Detective Box is a game that lets you experience a realistic and immersive murder mystery at home. You play as a detective who has to solve a complex case by examining clues, interrogating suspects, and following leads.
The game is divided into three episodes, each one delivered in a box that contains various elements of the investigation, such as police reports, evidence, testimonies, etc. You also have access to an app that helps you navigate the case and interact with the characters.
The goal of the game is to find out who killed the victim, why, and how, by using your logic, deduction, and intuition.

Detective Box is a game for one to four players, and each episode takes about three hours to complete. The game is suitable for anyone who loves crime stories, puzzles, and mysteries.
The investigation is structured via a web-based app that guides your progress, offer you tools and help if needed.
Research on the Internet is also required during play.

In Le Tueur au Tarot, you play as investigators helped by a Cold Cases Unit, assembled after the murder of a woman, killed in the signature style of a serial killer jailed many years ago.

Gold Rush 1850 takes players to the heart of the Wild West, where the gold rush has just begun. Each player takes on the role of a prospector seeking fortune deep within the mountains. By strategically placing tunnel cards, players extract valuable gold. With more than 70 different tunnel cards, each game offers a new and varied experience.

Players can spend their hard-earned coins in the saloon, meeting unique characters who provide useful abilities and help speed up tunnel construction.

Throughout the game, players must balance risk and reward, manage their resources wisely, and seize opportunities that arise from dynamic interactions.

At the end of the game, the player with the most victory points—earned from gold, successful mining jobs and completed side missions—wins the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Borderlands: Mister Torgue's Arena of Badassery – Loot Chest
description:
Designed to hold all of the expansion content from our previous campaign, the Loot Chest is more than just a pretty box. Packed within is a trove of new gameplay content to update and expand your games.

—description from the publisher
The players take turns to roll and move. The first to reach Space 54 wins the game. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players.

Shepherd’s Meadow is a light and accessible family board game where players build their own meadow by placing sheep on personal player boards.

Each round, players take turns selecting dice that allow them to place sheep of different colors, adjust their herd, or reposition animals already on the board. By arranging sheep into specific patterns, players claim goal tiles that grant points and advantages as the game progresses.

Once all goal tiles have been claimed, the game ends and players score points based on patterns formed in rows and columns according to a shared scoring card. Clever placement, timely adjustments, and smart dice choices are key to creating the most valuable meadow.

Simple rules combined with meaningful tactical decisions make Shepherd’s Meadow an engaging experience for both children and adults, offering quick playtime with plenty of replayability.

The player with the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher

Players earn chips by knowing the correct French translation of German words.

On their turn, the player rolls the dice and moves the pawn on the board accordingly. If they reach any empty field, their move is over. If they reach a city field, they may translate a word. The left neighbouring player takes a card from the pile and covers the French translation. If the player translates the German word correctly, they earn a chip.

The first player who earns 5 chips wins.

Science Trivia Challenge lets players of all ages explore their knowledge of interesting science facts. Do you know which moves the fastest: lightning, spacecrafts or light? How about the number of taste buds on a human adult’s tongue? Fascinated reactions await when you play this trivia game! Fun for family game night or classroom indoor recess, players win by amassing the most observation tokens, which are collected through avoiding setbacks on the game board and answering questions correctly. Each question card is double-sided with an advanced question on one side and a beginner on the other, so the whole family can play together, regardless of age or skill level. Players choose a level to play throughout, which helps keep an even playing field when different ages are playing against each other. Each card represents a different color and corresponds to the spaces on the board. "Know It" cards have multiple choice and true/false questions. "Name It" cards require players to identify the object in the picture shown. "Rank It" cards challenge players to put a list of scientific items in order based on criteria like size, distance and speed. Make your way around the board, watching out for hazards such as spilling solution or falling out of orbit!

Yokai Forest Duel is a lightweight and fast-playing tabletop dueling tournament card game. It supports 2–8 players, with the ability to scale up to 16 players if two copies of the game are used, and is designed to be learned in just a few minutes thanks to its streamlined, language-neutral ruleset. The core premise places each participant in a series of head-to-head duels using a small hand of cards representing actions like attacks, defenses, ammunition management, and permanent enhancements, with the goal of outthinking opponents.

Gameplay centers around strategic decision-making and open information play, meaning most cards are revealed during duels and players must anticipate opponents’ choices in real time. Duels are conducted in a best-of-three format, and your choices in each confrontation—whether to build up your capabilities or press the attack—affect later rounds because improvements to your tableau carry over through a tournament. This creates tension between long-term planning and immediate tactical actions, encouraging players to balance upgrades with aggression.

—description from the publisher

On their turn, the players roll the dice and move their pawn accordingly. On question fields, they answer questions about etiquette. If the answer is correct, the player may move forward.

The first player to reach the finish line wins and is "etiquette king".

Bobo Siebenschläfer plays hide and seek with his family. Help him find the best hiding place or help the family members find him.

The start player hides Bobo under one of the cards, e.g. the curtain card (Note: the box is designed respectively). The other players are the seeking players in this round.

At their turn, the seeking player moves the bedtime monkey to the right (time indication). Then they role the dice. Depending on the result, the hiding player is to give hints on where Bobo is hidden. 
Depending on the eyes of the dice, the seeking player may check the number of spots. If Bobo is there, all the players win a Bobo card. If Bobo is not there, the next player is on the throw.

Then, the next player is the hiding player.

The players win collectively if they gathered 4 Bobo cards. The players lose collectively if the bedtime monkey reaches bedtime before the players win 4 cards.

The world is ravaged by the Coronavirus pandemic. 
Coronarr is a jump and run game where little green viruses haunt through the streets, the shops, parcs, cinemas, and the theatres. The players try to hoard toilet paper to exchange that for vaccine doses or masks to protect themselves.  
The player who removes the last token from one of the other players wins. 
All players calculate their points and the one with the most points will get the title LOO PAPER KING resp. LOO PAPER QUEEN.

The kids let their penguins slide down to the finish line to score victory points. But be careful, if your penguin is too fast, it may fall in the ice hole and you don't get any points!
The kid who is the first one with 500 victory points wins the game.

boardgame: League of the Lexicon: Slang Edition
description:
Where is the WOP-WOPS?
What would a BUNG-NIPPER do?
How do you know if you've been PHUBBED?

Find out in League of the Lexicon's Slang Edition, an expansion to the bestselling game about words and language. Features questions on slang, cant, swearing and everything pertaining to the vulgar tongue.

Contains 500 questions in five new categories, at by world-renowned slang expert Jonathon Green.

Players build pyramids with their monkeys.

Each player receives a jungle board and a bag. In the bag are the 10 monkeys in their player colour, plus a lilac and a white monkey (12 monkeys in total).

On their turn, the player pulls out a monkey from the bag without looking. If it is a monkey in the player's colour, they can add it to their pyramid. 
If it is the lilac monkey, all the monkeys pulled out during this turn go back into the bag. 
If it is the white monkey, all the monkeys pulled out during this turn go back into the bag. The player may out the white monkey into an opponent's bag. 
After each monkey put into the pyramid, the player may say stop and end their turn.

The first player to complete their pyramid wins.
(4-3-2-1 monkeys)

boardgame: Zealous Zouaves: Mercenary Companies, Knight Orders, Traits and Troupe Rules for Four Against Darkness
description:
Give your adventurers a common identity as members of the same mercenary band or belong to a knight’s household. Join one of 17 knight orders or 9 mercenary companies in troupe-style play or design your own. Rules for level zero characters, 39 new traits, The Pixie class and Featuring the sellswords of the Atlis Coast Guard, the Cancelors, the Iron Thunderers, Bahadur’s Buccaneers, the Living Wall, Synnova’s Simian Separatives, the Two Moons, the Death Dealers, the White Eagles, the illustrious orders of the Knights of Alliteration, the Knights of Mistrust, the Knights of Kingliness, the Knights of the Orb, the Knights of the Order of Ataxia, the Knights of Purity, the Knights of the Purple Bolide, the Knights of the Robin, the Knights of the Regal Flamingo, the Knights of the Rose Blossom, the Knights of Tezany, the Knights of the Weeping Skull, the Knights of the Wolflord, the Servant Knights of the Portal, the Sphilis School of Strumpetry, the Supreme Knights of the Dragon and the Tenacious Knights of the Slug, with their background and heraldry.

—description from the publisher

Every February 2nd, brave groundhogs leave their warm slumber to faithfully predict the weather for us sky lovers. We treat these majestic creatures with celebrations and delicious food.

Help these plucky marmots make their predictions by guiding their tunnelling paths and influencing their spring or winter prognostication with your dice rolls.

Score points by influencing weather predictions, creating large interconnected tunnels, and by ensuring the groundhogs come up holes to provide their prognostication — becoming the best groundhog wrangler.

—description from the designer

Roll dies to defeat the Final Boss. Minions are waiting to shooting you.
In your turn, roll 4 dies and asign damage to minions and let 1 dice to the final boss attack.
Use your Power Ups, Ultimate and luck to win the game.

•••

Tira los dados para derrotar al Jefe Final. Los esbirros te esperan para dispararte. En tu turno, tira 4 dados, asigna daño a los esbirros y usa 1 dado para el ataque del jefe final. Usa tus power ups, tu habilidad definitiva y tu suerte para ganar la partida.

Each card is divided: one half shows a number, the other half shows a small calculation task (plus / minus; numbers 1-20).

Each player receives 4-6 cards, depending on the number of players. A starting card is drawn from the card pile. On their turn, the player tries to put connect one of their cards to the cards on the table by matching a calculation task with the correct solution. 
If the player has no fitting card in their hand, they skip the turn and draw a card from the pile.

The first player to add all their cards to the display wins.

The game is to be played in teams.

On their turn, the player roles the dice and moves the pawn to the respective symbol. They draw the card from the pile with the respective symbol and carry out the task: either guess a term or answer a question correctly. The lightning symbol is for action cards. They are explained in a separate booklet.

If the player successfully completed the task, the field is turned over and the team receives the amount of cash indicated on the backside of the field. If the player is not successfull in completing the task, the field is not turned.

The team who owns the most cash once all the fields are turned over wins.

The game is published as part of a youth debt prevention project.

This game is designed as an addition to the geography classes in Styria.

On their turn, the player draws a card from the pile and reads out the task. They then must solve the task. The solution to the task can be uncodified by using root foil. If the player completes the task earns the points indicated on the card.

The first player to earn 10 points wins.

The players alternately add their chips to the board to complete a row of 4 chips (diagonally, vertically or horizontally). However, instead of adding a chip, they can also choose to turn the board and thus change the game situation.

The first player to complete a row of 4 chips wins.

Before the game begins, each player is handed the same amount of solution cards. Any leftover solution cards are openly displayed next to the board.

The solution cards solve any "obstacles" the players encounter on their way to "environmental paradise". At their turn, the player roles the dice and moves the meeple accordingly. If they encounter an obstacle, they have to solve it with the matching solution card. They can also swap solution cards with other players, if they agree.

The first player to reach "environmental paradise" wins.

Vorchess is a chess variant played on a circular board with all pieces moving the same as traditional chess. The most outer ring is called the "Castle" which is a safe zone. In this Castle (safe zone) no piece can capture or be captured while located in this ring and the King cannot be put in check or checkmated. They must move their piece out of the castle and into the other 4 rings in order for their piece to be able to capture or be captured. They can however move within the castle wherever they like and move in and out of the castle as much as they want. The exception is the King. Once the King leaves the Castle he cannot be moved back into the Castle. The goal of Vorchess is to either checkmate the opponent OR to move their own King safely into the centre which is called the Vortex. However they cannot safely move their king into the Vortex if the opponent has at least one piece in the most inner ring (ring 5) or a knight in rings 4 or 5. The reason the game is called Vorchess is a play on words combining Vortex and Chess. The Vortex being the centre of the board.

—description from the designer

Monopoly: Grateful Dead - 60th Anniversary is a Monopoly game with a theme around the rock band Grateful Dead. Each property space is reimagined with references to the band’s songs, venues, and cultural legacy. The game has special metal tokens and these tokens include the dancing bear, a vibrant tour bus, a classic guitar, a set of drums, a blooming rose, and the iconic skull emblem. The game celebrate the band’s 60-year legacy.

Guy of Gisborne is the final expansion for HOOD: Troubles in Sherwood and one of the most personal figures in the entire legend. He is not an official or a symbol of authority. He is a hunter, a mercenary, a man sent in when words fail. In the Robin Hood stories, he stands closest to Robin himself – not as a superior, but as an equal rival who tracks, sets traps, and strikes at the worst possible moment.

—description from the publisher

Dog Pile is a fast, press-your-luck party card game where players build a shared pile of dogs and try to claim it at just the right moment. Play dog cards, jump in out of turn with matching dogs, and decide when to stop pushing your luck and claim the pile using one of your small dogs.

On your turn, add a Large or Medium dog card to the growing central pile. When the timing feels just right, finish the pile with a Small dog to claim it and score. Each player only gets 4 Small dogs, so pick your moments wisely!

If you have the exact same dog as the top card on the pile, you can interrupt with a “Dogpile!” to snatch control and play an extra card. This real-time mechanic keeps all players constantly engaged, so don’t blink!

You’ll need good timing, a little nerve, and a lot of bark.

—description from the designer

In Septet, players are attempting to gain points through collecting sets of matching suits of cards. Once a suit has a total value of 7 among all players, then those cards are scored immediately and all other cards in play are discarded. Star cards have special abilities and can be used strategically, including Forbidden (purple) cards which can be played as any color but are worth negative points at the end of the game. Once the end of the game is triggered then the player with the most points wins!

—description from the publisher

Grim Jumpers is a high-octane, solo or cooperative tactical miniatures game where elite soldiers —the Jumpers— are dropped into hostile zones to fend off relentless waves of Artificial Enemies (AE). 
Designed for 1–4 players, this system prioritizes fast-paced action, cinematic survival, and tactical synergy.

Whether you are performing a daring civilian rescue or a desperate last stand, every decision counts.

The Tactical Edge

     Scalable Mission Profiles: From solo 2'x2' skirmishes to 4-player 4'x4' battlefields, the game scales its difficulty and round limits dynamically to match your squad size.
     Cinematic Combat: Manage your Kinetic Shields to soak up damage, but stay clear of the enemy to let them recharge. Once your shields are down, every hit counts against your limited Health.
     Class-Based Synergy: Choose from four distinct classes:
         Vanguard: The versatile front-liner with stim-injectors.
         Heavy: The armored wall with devastating fire-support.
         Marksman: The long-range specialist with high-precision optics.
         Breacher: The CQC expert designed to shatter enemy lines.
    


The Artificial Enemy (AE)
The AE system is designed for automated play, requiring no "human" opponent. Using a standard deck of playing cards (including Jokers), the Orbital Insertion phase dictates reinforcements.

     Dynamic Spawning: AE units enter the battlefield in a cycling pattern, keeping you on the move.
     Escalating Threat: As the rounds progress, the deck throws more at you—from swarms of Grunts to tank-like Heavies and game-changing Global Events like Solar Flares or Orbital Jamming.
     Boss Waves: Pull a Joker, and the battlefield becomes a nightmare as a full Boss force drops on your position.


Five High-Stakes Scenarios
Included in the core rules are five distinct mission types to test your squad:

     Escort Civilians: Protect non-combatants and lead them to the extraction zone.
     Missile Launch: Navigate a complex sequence of terminal hacks to launch a counter-strike.
     Stronghold: Siege a fortified AE structure that constantly spawns volatile units.
     Survival: The ultimate test—all Jumpers must survive until the clock runs out.
     Gather Intel: A high-speed recon mission to secure data and bug out.


Product Includes

     Core Rulebook: Complete rules for movement, combat, stratagems, and terrain.
     Standalone Mission Manager (App): A digital companion tool that automates Round tracking, Squad Vitals, and the Spawn Deck. Simply open it in any web browser to streamline your tabletop experience!


—description from the publisher

boardgame: King John: A Source Book for The Barons' War Second Edition
description:
Having an entire book devoted to the period has allowed to look far beyond the civil war of 1215–17 and delve into the full, chaotic sweep of King John’s reign from 1199 to 1216. This was a time of intrigue, betrayal, shifting loyalties and near-constant conflict, presided over by a king whose realm stretched from the Cheviots to the Pyrenees. For wargamers, it is a goldmine of inspiration.

The Barons’ War: King John presented the perfect opportunity to expand the Feudal European retinue list from the second edition rulebook and bring the king’s allies, enemies and rivals to life on the tabletop. The result is six new historical retinue lists, each with its own distinctive style of play and tactical challenges.

Included retinues:

     Feudal European (revisited)
     Flemish
     Poitevin
     Mercenary
     Scottish
     Welsh
     Outlaws


But The Barons’ War: King John is more than a collection of retinue lists. It is a full sourcebook for wargaming the period. The book opens with a historical introduction to the Angevin world our hero, or anti-hero, inhabited, charting the dramatic events of his reign. It is a story overflowing with violence, vindictiveness and derring-do, all crying out to be recreated on the tabletop. To help bring that history to life, the book includes thirteen historical scenarios that allow you to refight key moments from John’s turbulent rule.

—description from the publisher

It's the year 3,000

A planet on the edge of our galaxy. For centuries, it was misused as a garbage dump—but recently an inexhaustible energy source has been discovered beneath the mountains of scrap, and now the race is on: Competing corporations are sending cyborgs to prepare their landing on the rugged surface. With nothing but probes in your luggage, you are shot onto the planet to secure dominance for your company. You explore irradiated zones, build workshops, radio towers, and refineries, and rummage through the scrap to find machine parts or weapons. And if you weld the right parts together, you can even bring an auxiliary robot to life to make your work easier or sabotage the other cyborgs.

To be faster and better than your competitors, you have to download the right programs from the cloud and learn the objectives of secret missions via radio. And then you need to occupy the relevant areas... and hold them, because be careful: you might be shot at.

Schrott is a competitive area-control game with deck & engine building mechanics, unknown missions - and Robots!

—description from the publisher

Das Jahr 3.000.

Ein Planet am Rande unserer Galaxie. Jahrhunderte wurde er als Müllhalde missbraucht - doch nun wurde eine unerschöpfliche Energiequelle unter den Schrottbergen entdeckt und das Wettrennen beginnt: Konkurrierende Unternehmen senden Cyborgs aus, um auf der zerklüfteten Oberfläche ihre Landung vorzubereiten. Mit nichts als Sonden im Gepäck werdet ihr auf den Planeten geschossen, um für euer Unternehmen hier die Vorherrschaft zu sichern. Ihr erkundet verstrahlte Zonen, errichtet Werkstätten, Funktürme und Raffinerien und wühlt euch durch den Schrott, um Maschinenteile und Waffen zu finden. Und wenn ihr die richtigen Teile zusammenschweißt, könnt ihr einen Hilfsroboter zum Leben erwecken, der euch die Arbeit vereinfacht oder andere Cyborgs sabotiert.

Um schneller und besser zu sein als die Konkurrenz, müsst ihr die richtigen Programme aus der Cloud laden und über Funk die Ziele geheimer Missionen erfahren. Und dann heißt es, die relevanten Areale besetzen … und halten, denn Vorsicht: Es darf geschossen werden.

Schrott ist ein kompetitives Area-Control-Spiel mit Deck- & Enginebuilder-Mechanik, unbekannten Missionen - und Robotern!

—description from the publisher (German)

boardgame: Storms of Kural: Two Player Box Set
description:
Arcane storms erupt from the corpses of ancient gods! At the opulent zenith of forbidden temples, bold heroes and dark champions clash for supremacy. Will you harness the languishing fury of forgotten gods, or lay low the otherworldly oppressors to free the world from their hateful designs?

The Storms of Kural is the perfect starter set.  
This set includes two full-sized warbands, and an all new campaign format for Relicblade. Cracking open this box will set events in motion that cannot be undone! Two players will face off against each other at the climax of an arcane crisis. Fateful twists will unlock new upgrades and powers in a clean, card-driven campaign. As players battle to the mountain peak, they will test their skill and revel in the heroic tactical combat that defines the best of Relicblade.

This meticulously designed box set includes everything two-players need to start playing Relicblade. New players can get the Storms of Kural box set bundled with the core rulebook for Relicblade, The Seeker’s Handbook, at a discount!

The eight champions featured in this box set represent the eight main factions in Relicblade, and each character has been carefully designed to represent that faction’s play style. If you like one character in particular, then I can practically guarantee that their associated faction will fit your style.

—description from the publisher

Disney Villainous: Come, We Fly! brings the Sanderson Sisters from Disney's Hocus Pocus to the game as a playable Villain.

On a turn, the active player moves their character to a different location on their player board, takes one or more of the actions visible on that space (often by playing cards from their hand), then refills their hand to four cards. Cards are allies, items, effects, and conditions. You need to use your cards to fulfill your unique win condition.

One of the actions allows you to choose another player, draw two cards from that player's fate deck, then play one of them on that player's board, covering two of the four action spaces on one of that player's locations. The fate deck contains heroes, items, and effects from that villain's movie, and these cards allow other players to mess with that particular villain.

Disney Villainous: Come, We Fly! is playable on its own, and its characters can also face off against those in the other Disney Villainous games.

boardgame: Disney Villainous: Success at Any Cost
description:
Disney Villainous: Success at Any Cost will introduce Ernesto de la Cruz from Disney Pixar's Coco and Prince Hans from Disney's Frozen.

On a turn, the active player moves their character to a different location on their player board, takes one or more of the actions visible on that space (often by playing cards from their hand), then refills their hand to four cards. Cards are allies, items, effects, and conditions. You need to use your cards to fulfill your unique win condition.

One of the actions allows you to choose another player, draw two cards from that player's fate deck, then play one of them on that player's board, covering two of the four action spaces on one of that player's locations. The fate deck contains heroes, items, and effects from that villain's movie, and these cards allow other players to mess with that particular villain.

Disney Villainous: Success at Any Cost is playable on its own, and its characters can also face off against those in the other Disney Villainous games.

A Moomin themed card game in which players are aming to get rid of their hand cards. When a player have only 1 card in their hand, they must remember to say "One to Go!" Or hopefully any other doesn't recognize that if a player missed saying that. Action cards   add some fun flavour to the game.

boardgame: Steamfront: Pack 2 joueurs – Luminarch vs Steamwokens
description:
Steamfront, the Steam-Fantasy Skirmish Miniatures Game.

In a steam-fantasy setting, Steamfront simulates a clash between two clans across predefined scenarios.


     Fast: Games last between 45 minutes and 1 hour.
     No downtime: You activate one miniature, then your opponent does the same.
     Tactical: Miniatures can overload with steam using their equipment and, when their leader commands, unleash the fury of steam for devastating actions.


To win, you’ll need to find the perfect timing between seizing ground, overloading your warriors, and destroying the enemy clan.
Starter set for 2 players, complete with two factions: Luminarch and Steamwoken.

This box contains 18 3D-printed miniatures along with 18 textured bases. It also includes the rules, profile cards, and all the tokens/dice/markers needed to play.

Included miniatures:

     Luminarch faction: 1 Champion – 2 Protectors – 3 Archers – 3 Sword & Shield Guards
     Steamwoken faction: 1 Coordinator – 2 Hammerers – 3 Whisperers – 3 Assemblers


—description from the publisher

DeFuZion: Wild Dice is a fast-paced, high-tension dice game designed to work as an intro game, a filler, or a high-energy game night starter.

All players begin with 100 points and compete over a shared set of five face-up cards drawn from the DeFuZion Deck. On your turn, you roll five Standard Dice and a Bonus Die, which resolves immediately and can alter dice values, grant additional rerolls, remove dice from play, or allow you to take control of the Bomb Token.

After rerolling, players assign dice to all five cards. Dice may be combined to reach a card’s value. Most cards allow partial matches with point loss equal to the difference between the card and the assigned dice. Bomb cards and FuZed cards must be matched exactly, and failing to do so results in a 40-point loss. Bombs can also FuZe other cards of the same number, spreading risk across the table.

The Bomb Token adds direct player interaction. When used, it is placed on a card of the holder’s choice, which then acts as a Bomb card and forces the next player to match that card exactly or suffer a 40-point loss. Because all cards are shared, each player starts with an even playing field.

Special outcomes create dramatic swings. Matching all five cards exactly causes a Total Lockdown, dealing damage to every opponent at once. Rolling five identical dice triggers a System Overload, ignoring all Bombs, FuZed cards, and penalties for the turn.

The game ends after the round in which any player reaches zero points or below. The player with the highest final score wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Παιχνίδι ερωτήσεων: Παγκόσμια γεωγραφία edition (Question game: World geography edition)
description: Παιχνίδι ερωτήσεων: Παγκόσμια γεωγραφία edition is a trivia and estimation board game published by ΠΟΛΥ ΚΟΥΛ, focused on questions related to global geography. Players are presented with questions that require numerical answers (such as distances, populations, heights, or quantities) and must estimate the correct value as accurately as possible. The player whose answer is closest to the correct number without exceeding it scores points, while answers that go over the correct value are eliminated for the round. The game blends knowledge, intuition, and risk management, encouraging discussion and participation even from players who may not know the exact answers.

Solder is a pure placement game of unification between two players – Black and White – played on an initially empty board – e.g. a size 6 hexhex board or a size 11 square board.


     Players only place stones of their own color, and only onto empty cells.
     On the very first turn of the game, Black places 1 stone.
     From then on – stating with White – players take turns placing 2 stones each.
     Each individual placement must be adjacent to an even number of friendly groups.
     If, and only if, you cannot make both placements, you pass your entire turn.
     When the players pass in succession, the game is over and the winner is the player with fewer groups.


—description from the publisher

This fast-paced word game challenges players to find vocabulary under pressure. The game begins by shaking a cup and tapping it to reveal three lettered dice. Players must then quickly identify and call out words that incorporate all three of the displayed letters.

The competition proceeds in rounds where memory and speed are essential. If a player is unable to think of a new word or accidentally repeats a word already mentioned during the round, they are eliminated. The last player remaining wins the round.

Additionally, the game includes a solo mode. In this version, a single player can test their vocabulary limits by attempting to find as many different word combinations as possible from a single set of three letters.

What comes to mind when you see Party, Nougat and Paris? Write it down and hope someone else thinks alike.

In this cooperative word association game you try to become one mind and earn enough gems before 6 rounds are over.

In secret every player writes down a word connecting to the previously written words. Every match with another players earns the group gems.

The words your group writes down become the words you should connect to next round. So try to narrow it down within the group to become One Mind collectively.

Do you recognize this?

You're standing at the cash register, ready to use your debit card. And suddenly, you've forgotten your pin. A feeling of tension and time pressure crawls you down. Hoping for the best, you enter a code. And, miracle of miracles, it turns out to be the right pin. What a big luck!

This game let's you relive that moment. You and your opponents will each be assigned a unique and unknown pin. Each round can reveal valuable information about your pin. To win the game, you must reveal your pin before your opponents know theirs.

You play Pincode with the interactive app, which, just like a card reader, tells you whether your pin is correct or incorrect. It's not just a race against your opponents, but also against the clock. If you fail to guess your pin within the allotted time, you're out of the game. Just like at the cash register, you have a maximum of three attempts to enter the correct pin.

Enter an incorrect code for the third time? Then, unfortunately, the game is over for you. Do you play it safe? Or do you take a gump and decide the game right away? Whatever your tactic, we wish you a lot of fun with this brainteaser

SoloWar: WWII is a solitaire card game set on the heated fronts of World War II. As an Allied commander, you will lead your forces against a relentless enemy, fighting decisive battles that will turn the tide of the conflict.
