Plan your moves, manage your troops' morale, and carefully choose when to risk everything to secure a strategic objective. Fight in Normandy, among the ruins of Stalingrad, in the dangerous Ardennes, and conquer Berlin. Every turn is a choice, every card can make a difference.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Flintlocks & Percussion Caps is a set of skirmish wargame rules where each figure represents one character.  They are aimed at the period from 1800-1860 in Texas, SW USA, Mexico etc.  Figure scale is 28mm.

Each player, and there can be multiple players on each side if desired, takes their force and pits their wits against the opposition - be it Comanche Indians, Mexicans, Texas Rangers or other groups.  Several scenarios are included, based on historical encounters.

The rules include all the types of actions and forms of fighting characters of the time would have encountered.  So saddle up and head for Texas.

boardgame: Oakspire: The Builders of the Sunleaf Grove
description:
It's time to build, Architect!

Welcome to Sunleaf Grove, where a community of animal residents work paw-in-paw/fin/hoof/claw to build a home in harmony with nature – and they need your help!

As a Grove Architect, you’ll plan projects, organize supply lines, and transform timber into beautiful, sustainable structures. Gather fallen wood from the forest floor, float it downstream, shape it into sturdy planks, and raise buildings of every size – from snug burrows to towering treetop apartments.

Collect, craft, build: it's a peaceful, puzzle-y cycle!

Oakspire is a cozy tableau-building game where you'll carefully select Project Cards that represent different stages of wood crafting. These cards can be used in a multitude of ways: Turn them into Restored Wood to fuel your production chain, play them as Company Actions to move, process, or upgrade your resources on supply lines, or blueprint them to start working on a new building! With an emphasis on an environmentally friendly approach, you'll repurpose and upgrade salvaged wood to fulfil contracts and expand your workshops.

Each completed project increases your crafting capabilities, and as the game progresses, so does our woodland city, as cozy cafés and riverside markets transform it into a bustling metropolis of nature. But you can't just focus on your own projects! When community actions are triggered each round, they may give you a helpful boost or nudge your plans in an unexpected direction - so keep an eye on the Community Board!

The race is on to be the first to build a certain number of projects for the city, securing their reputation as the best architect and carpenter in the city. Maybe you'll even get a statue made of you in the city centre...

—description from the publisher

TURM - Das Duell is a tactical card game for two players from Reinbek, in which the packaging becomes the playing field. Players work together to build towers from 0 to 12 and collect gold nuggets. The player with the most gold nuggets wins.

Build towers together and earn gold. The rules are simple, but be careful: if you're not paying attention, you'll hear the dreaded ‘TURM!’ and it will be your opponent's turn. Use your gold tactically to block your opponent or gain an advantage. In this card game, the winner is the player who has collected the most gold nuggets.

TURM - Das Duell is a tactical two-player card game about incremental tower building - simple rules, growing complexity and constant pressure from the opponent

To let their own salmon swim to the spawning grounds, players lay out picture cards so that they overlap. Everyone tries to rearrange the “rotating picture” to their advantage. Only salmon that remain visible there can get closer to their spawning grounds. At the same time, the tributaries of individual waters open and close when the picture cards are laid out. This blocks coveted swimming routes or creates new loopholes, where Bear Babbel also curiously sneaks around: Will a tasty treat jump out of the water?

Flitze Flatze Bärentatze is a clever tile-laying game in which alert eyes control whether the fish dive down where the bear appears.

—description from the publisher

Identity V: Paper Escape is a survival-themed card game for 2-6 players. Players take on the roles of Survivors, navigating the dangers of the Manor while managing their "Panic" levels. By utilizing unique character skills and item cards, players must protect themselves while hindering their rivals. As players are eliminated once their Panic becomes too high, the goal is to be the sole survivor. This game faithfully translates the original's Gothic art style into a fast-paced, competitive tabletop experience.

There are tripping hazards everywhere in the enchanted forest. The forest floor tips away there under your feet ... and ... oops ... you're lying on your nose. To avoid these insidious traps, the players memorise where it's better not to set foot. This way, their Zausel gnomes can safely reach the forest clearings where they have forgotten their beloved hats. With the hats on their heads, they then make their way back to their Zausel hut via the stumbling blocks, where each hat they bring along adds to their own hat collection. As the coolest hats have more rings, the winner is the one who brings home the hats with the most rings.

Die Zausel vom Zauberwald is a richly equipped memory and collecting adventure in which children usually show adults how never to stray from the safe path.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Old King's Crown: Songs of Home
description: The Songs of Home expansion adds new factions, neutral kingdom cards and exciting ways to help you claim the throne.

boardgame: The Frightful Dozen: Twelve Detailed Foes for Four Against Darkness
description:
This book includes game statistics, background information, tactics and secrets of twelve unusual creatures of Norindaal, with rules for encounters suited for parties of all experience levels. Each monster has an illustration, unique reactions or behavior tables, specific loot, and story hooks to bring them into your games of Four Against Darkness. You will meet the Stinkhorn, Madame Peony, the Transparent Couple, Orum the Human Spider, Matryona, the Glass-Toothed Ripper, the Kidnapping Hag, Iglar the Mosquito-fiends, the Rottlor, Nevar of the Rotting Hand, the Krabadarus and the Telltale Birds.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Entreaty of Eldritch Enchanters: Magic classes for Four Against Darkness
description: In this supplement for all levels of play, you will discover many new character classes of a magical nature: Ash Bride, Battlemage, Dilettante, Dragonkin, Elementalist, Fakir, Frost Maiden, Greater Familiar, Greater Griffin, Knight of Neutrality, Living Relic, Magman, Mudman, Oracle, Psion, Scarecrow, Specialist, Sylph, Undine. Also included are new spells and psionic skills.

boardgame: Affairs of the Heart: Adventures in Love and Passion for Four Against Darkness
description:
This book lets you add romantic and erotic content to games of Four Against Darkness, for characters of any level. The rules include: New classes (cuckquean, seductress, cupid, dominatrix), milestones, character traits, expert skills, romantic minions, romantic weird monsters, equipment, love magic items, erotic epic rewards, love and lust potions, and a new patron. This is not a stand-alone game. This supplement for characters of all levels requires the Four Against Darkness core rulebook and references many other books in the Four Against Darkness line.
This book contains explicit sexual themes and is recommended for adults only.

– blurb from publisher's website

The remote asteroid field known as Crestfell was discovered to have the richest deposits of Asteryte ore in the sector.  The Emperor is opening up mining for this valuable mineral for a short time and has proclaimed the most effective mining faction will get exclusive rights.

Through mining and conquest, you are determined to acquire the most Asteryte and become the dominant mining power of the sector.

In Crestfell, you'll need to create the best fleet to attack other players' bases while balancing defenses to protect your own.  This game was designed to be very interactive and quick to learn with a large amount of dynamic combinations and strategies.

Details:
If the individual game you will have your fortress which serves as the base for your fleet.

You have 3 bases, to which you set the amount of Asteryte being produced for that round. Each player then puts a formation of 3 cards (ships) to defend the base and that can be used to attack other players.

Every player has 2 moves each round, to which they can use to increase their fleet (draw cards) or attack other players.

Every round is made a little more interesting by events through the asteroid field that players can adjust their strategies to take advantage of.

Players then add up the Asteryte they collected for the round both through mining and conquest.  At the end of 5 turns the player with the most ore wins the game!

The team game mixes this up, where now the Factions have decided because the stakes are so high they are willing to share a victory for the Crestfall asteroid belt.  And now the aggression is full on.

Players use a combination of transport ships for direct assault and have consolidated their defense and mining to a single base.

In the team game you deploy formations for your ally to use and add to their fleet, and victory can only be achieved through teamwork and maximum attrition of your opponents.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Audacious Adversaries: A Bestiary for Four Against Darkness
description:
Audacious Adversaries is a supplement for Four Against Darkness for parties of level 5-9. It features new monsters, new retainers, special features and unique treasures. Featuring: Zombie and Silver Sigbins, Creeping Kapres, Green Genies, Sentient Floating Fireballs, Maggot Warriors, Scarred Scarecrows, Corpse Golems, Cavalier Ghouls, Werehippos, Taunting Tiyanaks, Viridian Spiders, Polychromatic Dragons, plus 6 New Trap Rooms, 6 New Special Features, 12 New Magic and Unique Items, 6 Useless Items, 10 new retainers (Veteran Bodyguard, Eye Surgeon, Treasure Expert, Decoy, Fungi Fighter, Orc Negotiator, Wizard’s Assistant, Chronicler, Curse Neutralizer and Dungeon Runner) and 8 Quick Encounters for short sessions. You need Four Against Darkness and Four Against the Abyss to use this book. While designed with expert parties in mind, Audacious Adversaries may be used for lower levels with minimal alterations.

—description from the publisher

Locked n' Loaded is a fast paced interactive game with a combination of timing and knowing when to push your luck.

Players compete to be the first to get all of their dice Locked n' Loaded, while using their own dice and convincing players to work against others.  As the rounds develop the excitement and treachery builds as players build to be the first to win!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ominous Opus of the Occult & Obscure: Cantrips for Four Against Darkness
description:
This book features nearly a hundred level zero spells (aka cantrips), new character traits and gear as well as a pre-generated adventure for a solo wizard character for Four Against Darkness. The book contains some instances of mild adult humor -- reader's discretion advised when playing with children. This is not a stand-alone game. At a minimum, a copy of Four Against Darkness is required to play. Numerous notes are given so that the book can be used in synergy with other books in the Four Against Darkness line.

—description from the publisher

On Thin Dice is a fast-paced shedding and survival game where the goal is simple: get rid of your dice before you fall through the ice.

You start on the ice with 7 dice. On your turn you have the chance to get rid of dice if you can make the match for the round. If you can’t make a match, you are forced to take another die, adding to your burden.

But watch your step - if you get to 10 dice you'll break the ice, and not in a good way!

There are two ways to play the game, with the "cold snap cards" or with the "thin ice dice".  Giving players two games in one box.

—description from the designer

You are amusement park managers.

While the game includes an element of luck through dice rolls, it also requires strategic thinking—such as deciding which facilities to build and where, and when to level up. The result is a finely tuned balance: easy for beginners to jump into, yet offering plenty of depth for experienced players to think through.

Just like the catchphrase “Roll, Build, and Earn!”, the gameplay is simple and intuitive: roll the dice, build facilities, and earn money and victory points.

Designed to be enjoyed with both family and friends, this is a lively, fun game full of excitement—so be sure to give it a try!

Trade the “Magic Wands” in your hand to obtain even more powerful ones!
The player with the highest total score of Magic Wands in their hand wins!!

Use the effects of your Magic Wands wisely and aim for efficient engine growth!!

An engine-building card game full of tough decisions and deep strategic depth.

Almost a thousand years ago prophets visited each of the six races with a warning.
    

     Do not approach the temple until the six signs have all come to pass.
     No not war with each other until the prophecy has come.
     When the signs come, choose a champion and send them to the temple before the dawn of the rise of the blood moon.  


    
If you do not succeed, the world with be lost.
    
Most scoffed at the warnings, until two races reignited an age old conflict and were washed away in a firestorm.
    
The remaining four races paid attention.  And obeyed the prophecy, each in their own ways.
    
Now, the six signs have come.  Champions for each race have been chosen.  And old enemies will have to work together to face the ancient danger in the Temple of Prophecy.
    
There are 4 games attached to this set for each of the surviving races:

     Deceivers Apothecary
     Battle to Dusk
     Fate of the Fey
     Runes of Balance 



In each of these four games players will compete to be that races champion in a contest unique to that race.  The victor of that game will be able to bring that character directly to the Temple of Prophecy.

Note: There will also be "prebuilt" characters of each race if players choose to play this game directly, or don't have that races game.
    
The Temple of Prophecy is a dungeon crawler game which players will randomly choose one of multiple scenarios for the game.
    
There are several main characteristics for players, NPC's and opponents:

     Attack - the ability to hit an opponent (roll modifier).
     Damage - how much damage is done by a default hit.
     Energy - This powers weapons and spells.
     Agility - The ability to move and dodge.
     Health - Your life points.
     Armor - The ability to absorb damage.  Heavy armor can affect agility bonuses.


Players will each have gear and powers unique to their race for their characters.

All players will have the objective to be victorious in the temple, but also will have secret quests they have each been tasked.

Players will have to work together to survive and save their world while completing the quests that will ensure their race will survive the inevitable war to come.

—description from the publisher

The solar system we live in exists thanks to a miraculous arrangement of planets.
Even for a god, recreating the same miracle twice would be impossible… right?

…Huh? You say it’s easy?

You “gods” must work together to return the scattered planets to their proper order.
The only hint is the involuntary phrase that slips from your lips: “OH MY GOD.”

A bidding & racing game where your dog’s mood rules the day!

Try to lure Daisy the Samoyed—who’s not in the mood for a walk—out for a stroll using tempting goodies!
But be careful: Daisy’s unpredictable mood might keep you from reaching the finish line!

aai’aiuun’shuun; the children of the void.

Ancient, ethereal, deadly.  They exist as both myth and legend to some races.  But those who know of them, know to be wary.

Those who do, call you Shadow.  In the Crown of Night under the blood moon, as one of the Chosen, you have gathered to choose a champion to go to the Temple of Prophecy.

You will be the champion of your kind.  You alone with be the most powerful and important aai’aiuun’shuun to ever exist.

It is your destiny.

The Shadows are adept at mixing and absorbing concoctions to give them temporary powers and allow them more direct influence in the mortal realm.  It is fitting their champion will be the one most adept at increasing their power to go to the Temple of Prophecy.

In this card game, players work to both increase their ingredients to be able to complete recipes from the limited draw pile, and to hamper their opponents without being seen by the Adjudicator.

If a player takes an action that the Adjudicator sees, or uses their special powers they will lose reputation which negatively affects their gameplay.

The play area has a place for face up ingredients, but also has three card spots where players may have face down cards to hide ingredient or action cards from other players.  But be careful, without an event you can't look at these cards and if you fail in an attempt to brew the Adjudicator will punish you.

Players will need to make decisions using their cards and timing to make potions that both give them points to help them win the game, as well as to be able to use these powers in the Temple of Prophecy where they can port their Champion.

This game can be played as a standalone game or within the Temple of Prophecy series.

—description from the designer

JADO 02-A (Joint All Domain Operation: Northeast Naval-Air) is an operational-scale modern wargame designed by Gavin Hu and published by War Drum Games. As the "Naval-Air" component of the series' Northeast theater, the game focuses on high-tech maritime and aerial warfare within the Korean Peninsula and surrounding waters. It simulates the complexities of contemporary "joint all-domain" operations, where electronic warfare, missile strikes, and information superiority are as critical as traditional firepower.

The game utilizes a "heavy techno-focus" engine on an operational scale, with 4-hour turns and hexes representing 75 kilometers. Players manage high-stakes engagements through an interactive sequence of phases, including routine patrols, air defense readiness, and ballistic missile deterrence. Combat is resolved using a d10-based system that emphasizes the interaction between a platform’s Radar Cross Section (RCS), its electronic protection modules, and the specific capabilities of modern weapon units like supersonic anti-ship missiles.

A defining feature of the JADO series is its "Informationized" counter design. Each counter is dense with data, often double-sided to represent different operational modes; for example, ship counters display general movement and air defense on the front, while their offensive strike and detection capabilities are found on the reverse. Units represent individual large ships (over 6,000 tons), groups of smaller combatants, or squadrons of 10-12 aircraft, each tracked with separate cards for formation management and ammunition expenditure.

Beyond kinetic combat, JADO 02-A deconstructs modern military theory into eight distinct simulation modules. These range from "Emergency Combat Readiness Takeoff" to "Deterrence Warning" and "Anti-Ship Strike" missions. The game can be played as a standalone Naval-Air simulation or combined with its companion land set (JADO 02-B) to provide a complete, multi-domain picture of modern conflict in the Northeast Asian theater.

This is the commercial edition of a doujin game that received high praise from many corners at Game Market. Players choose a stack of cards, inspect them, and place them face-down one by one. Only the last card is placed face-up, gradually revealing what’s in each stack.

A simple game that’s quick to play, yet full of deduction and bluffing, with an innovative system that will make you sit up and take notice.
King's Valley Hex has the same rules as King's Valley, but uses a hex board.

Dead Hand is a solitaire roguelike adventure played entirely with three standard poker decks. Each turn you draw a handful of dungeon, treasure, and monster cards, then decide the order to resolve them in a tense sequencing puzzle where every choice matters. There are no phases and no downtime — just a constant push‑your‑luck flow of tactical decisions, resource management, and escalating danger.

You begin with a small set of stats (Attack, Defense, Skill, HP, and DrawMod), and the dungeon grows more dangerous as your DrawMod increases. Monsters scale by tier, treasures provide temporary or permanent boosts, and Aces act as powerful one‑off events that can swing a run in your favor… or complicate everything.
Combat is simultaneous and driven by simple dice math, with two high‑impact mechanics:

     Perfect Block: If a monster deals 0 damage, you roll on a special table for bonus effects.
     Overkill: If your killing blow deals 3× the monster’s starting HP, you trigger a second Perfect effect.


Monsters become especially deadly at Tier 4, gaining +2 to all stats, and Ace Monsters only drop their Boss Shards when defeated at this highest tier. Collecting shards is the long‑term progression goal of the run.

Dead Hand is designed to be:

     Portable — playable anywhere with common components
     Fast — a full run takes 15–25 minutes
     Replayable — every turn is a new puzzle
     Tactical — sequencing, stat‑burn healing, and deck manipulation all matter
     Print‑and‑Play friendly — the rulebook and components are easy to produce at home


If you enjoy Talisman‑style encounters, MTG‑style combat math, or solitaire games with teeth, Dead Hand aims to scratch that itch with a compact, endlessly replayable system built from the most familiar components in gaming.

—description from the designer

When all heroes fall, the King calls for the Beastward Hunters.

Your task is to reclaim cursed lands overrun by monsters and push back the darkness spreading across the realm.

Explore a dangerous map filled with hostile regions, hidden treasures, and limited resources. Fight varied monsters, collect loot, and trade supplies in towns and remote locations. As you progress, you develop your hero through a dedicated skill tree, shaping your playstyle and growing noticeably stronger over time.

Master tactical combat to survive increasingly powerful enemies. Clear three towns of monster infestations and purify three ancient idols scattered across the map. Only then can you summon Fernobog from the Dark Tower and face the final confrontation.

—description from the designer

Each player will have 5 letter cards, 2 wild cards (double letters), and 1 challenge card. They must answer the questions placed in the center of the table as quickly as possible until they run out of cards. The first player to run out of cards wins.

The game adds chaos with the challenge cards because they offer actions that can change the pace of the game.

Furthermore, the number of answers per round is limited depending on the number of players, so you must be very quick with each answer to avoid running out of cards early.

Word Dungeon is a solo tactical puzzle game where you create words by connecting adjacent letters on a grid to navigate through a dungeon layout. The dungeon contains monsters, treasures, and an exit, all represented by letters on the grid that you must incorporate into your words.

During play, you move through the dungeon by forming words from orthogonally adjacent letters. When you encounter a monster, you engage in combat by creating words that include the monster's letter, with longer words dealing more damage. Treasures provide points when collected, and occasional magic items offer single use abilities to help with difficult encounters.

The game ends when you reach the exit. Your final score is calculated from points earned through defeating monsters, collecting treasures. Each dungeon presents a randomised puzzle with optimal solutions to discover through careful word placement and tactical decision making.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Monopoly: The Shining – Overlook Hotel edition
description: Monopoly: The Shining - Overlook Hotel edition is a Monopoly game with the theme of Stanley Kubrick’s movie. Each property square replaces the usual street names with memorable scenes and locations from the film, such as the Pantry, the Hedge Maze, and Room 237. The game pieces are crafted in metallic gold, shaped as the axe, typewriter, tricycle, Room 237 key tag, snowcat vehicle, and boiler valve. The board has the 45th anniversary branding.

boardgame: Descubre la Ciencia del Barrio Cívico
description:
Descubre la Ciencia del Barrio Cívico is a collaborative board game where players explore the city through curiosity, observation, and imagination. Guided by Pelux, a friendly magical being, and their animal companions, players discover that science is not only found in laboratories, but also in streets, buildings, parks, and institutions that make the city work every day.

During the game, one player secretly takes the role of Pelux and decides where key buildings of the Civic District should be placed. The rest of the group works together to observe clues, discuss ideas, and deduce Pelux’s choices. Each turn challenges players to think about how different institutions function, how they relate to one another, and why their location in the city matters.

To win, players must collaborate effectively: sharing ideas, listening to each other, and making thoughtful decisions. Points are earned by correctly guessing Pelux’s placements across four rounds. The goal is not competition, but collective success, learning together, improving with each turn, and achieving the highest possible score as a team while uncovering the science hidden in the city.

—description from the publisher

You are a Zombivor. Something in your reassembled brain still functions. You are not a simple walking corpse; you have instincts, you have... goals?

Your priority is singular: to Zombivive.

You must flee from the enraged humans, use the fog to your advantage to hide, and perhaps—just perhaps—establish a new "unlife" in the forgotten depths beneath the city.

Zombivor is a cooperative survival game for 1 to 4 players where collaboration (and swapping body parts) is the only way to avoid ending up permanently dismembered.

Victory Conditions: The Zombivors win the game if they manage to survive the humans, find the Lair, and defeat the Boss if necessary.

—description from the publisher

Español -

Eres un Zombivor. 
Algo en tu cerebro recompuesto aún funciona. No eres un simple cadáver andante; tienes instintos, tienes... ¿metas?

Tu prioridad es una sola: Zombivivir.

Debes huir de los humanos enfurecidos, usar la niebla a tu favor para ocultarte y, quizás, solo quizás, establecer una nueva "no-vida" en las profundidades olvidadas bajo la ciudad.

Zombivor es un juego de supervivencia cooperativa para 1 a 4 jugadores donde la colaboración (y el intercambio de partes) es la única forma de no acabar desmembrado definitivamente.

Condiciones de Victoria:
Los Zombivors ganan la partida si consiguen sobrevivir a los humanos, encontrar la Guarida y acabar con el jefe de ser necesario.

—description from the publisher

The ethereal Sprites have learned of Dragons' plan to align the cosmos and have brought the randomness of chaos to Logic & Lore.  The Variance expansion adds two new modes: Star Flight and Star Sprite! Both new modes showcase Sprites, a varied set of dice, and two new sets of logic questions.  These add a whole new layer to your deduction strategy - choices based on probability. Now you must weigh which dice will give you the best chance at helpful information on top of which cards and questions you choose for clues.  Choose dice carefully to earn dragon tokens that unlock more powerful actions.

The expansion also brings new player boards that immerse you into your Dragon persona,  Dice boards, and a new notepad and token reminder system as another way to help keep track of your clues.

-description from publisher

Earth Hegemony Marine Corps (EHMC) is a thrilling solitaire or cooperative game that plunges players into intense, high-stakes missions.

As the commander of an elite team of Hegemony Marines, your mission is to lead your squad through perilous environments, facing off against formidable enemies and overcoming daunting challenges. 
Your objectives are outlined in detailed briefing sheets that set the stage for each mission, requiring strategic thinking, quick decision-making, and teamwork. 
Customize your squad by selecting from a diverse array of marines, each with unique skills and specialized gear, to create a formidable force ready to tackle any obstacle. 
Whether playing solo or with friends, E.H.M.C. offers an immersive, action-packed experience where every choice could mean the difference between success and failure.

Game Sequence:

     Squad movement
     Tile Activation
     Report


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Steam Up: Twelve Zodiacs Card Game
description:
The Twelve Zodiacs is a standalone card game set in the world of Steam Up.

The twelve Zodiacs are on a tasty mission to enjoy a delicious dim sum feast. Knowing the meal isn’t complete without Egg Tarts at the end, they must give it their all to collect the most egg tarts in this ladder-climbing card game.



The game is played in rounds. Each player begins with 8 cards in hand, including 7 Zodiac cards and 1 Wild card.On each turn, a player takes one of two actions:

• Play: A player must play a valid card set. The set played must rank higher than the previously played set. After playing, if a player has fewer than 8 cards, draw from the Zodiac decks until they have 8 in their hand.

• Pass: A player does not play a set. Instead, draw 1 card from a Zodiac deck. The player who played the previous set wins and gains 1 (or 2) Egg Tart as a reward for playing a set that wasn’t beaten.

A round ends when one of the two Zodiac decks runs out. The player with the most Egg Tarts wins the round and collects a Win token. A new round then begins, and play continues until a player has collected 2 Win tokens, winning the game!

—description from the designer

The Weather Dice accessory set has been designed to be used with Book 6 the World Builder expansion. It adds the effects of the weather to your D100 Dungeon games and will make life in the world you're creating more realistic. The set contains:  1 rule book, 4 weather dice (1 for each season), and 1 Dice bag.

Hispania describes 1,500 years of Spanish and Portuguese history, from 320 BC to 1200 AD. During this period, many peoples and empires invaded Hispania. Many of these peoples or empires, called “Nations” in this game, attempted to colonize or conquer Hispania. But the new conquerors were unable to maintain their power for long. In turn, they were defeated by other invaders.

The game ends after the great migrations, at the dawn of the Reconquista of Muslim Hispania by its Catholic half.

In Hispania, each player controls several different Nations. During the game, the player accumulates victory points with each of their Nations. The player with the most victory points will be victorious at the end of the game!

During the game, a series of historical events unfold, which players will see represented by the various appearances on the scene of the nations that intervened in the history of Hispania, as well as numerous events that will occur over the course of the game.

—description from the designer

To win you need to build the correct sequence of DUE cards.
The only thing to be careful about while playing is to always keep only TWO cards in your hand. 
Be wise in deciding which cards to keep and which one to let go.

With TENNISmind you can play a tennis game on your table. With very simple rules players hit the ball and move on a square-grid clay court, using original mechanics:


     You start moving in secret, while the opponent chooses the ball destination, forcing both of you to double-guess each other.
     The umpire dice will decide the outcome of shots that target lines or the net.


You can thus recreate aces, passing shots, lobs, drop shots, volleys, and even ask for the Hawk-Eye! But beware, the handling of your initial energy points will let you move further just when needed, or will get you exhausted...

The game includes a solitaire mode ("Solitaire Rafa"), a variant to create special players, and alternative fast courts.

—description from the designer

(Looking for a publisher, but in the meantime the game is available as Print & Play, you will find how to build it in the News section)

You don't have to host a luau to have a blast playing Tiki Toss. In this tabletop skill game, you toss (or precisely release) a small metal hoop tied to a tower.

With the right angle and force, your ring will swing toward the base of the tower and get hooked on the attached grapple. It’s a simple concept that requires skill, focus, consistency, and the speed to do it before your competitors.

Up to four competitors can play simultaneously on this tower that requires only a flat surface to play. Give Tiki Toss a swing, and you'll be hooked!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Duel for Cardia: Guardians of the Wild
description:
The conflict over Cardia has awakened mythical guardians!
But each creature grants its power to only one of the factions. Can you use it to your advantage?

Every second round, players encounter a guardian. If a card with the matching faction is played on the guardian, they trigger their ability and can thus massively change the encounter or encounters already played. 
This adds another exciting tactical component to the game.

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Terror from Below
description:
Fueled by his ambition and desire for power, Riff Tamson brings the planet of Mon Cala into the Clone Wars. Conspiring with Quarren chieftain, Nossor Ri, these devious warriors in service to the Separatist Alliance manipulate events and thrust their unwitting foes into the fray.

Utilizing predatory power and swift speed, Riff Tamson leads the fearsome Separatist Alliance Aqua Droids and ally Nossor Ri to accomplish his dastardly goals. Exploiting the terrain to bring their savage combat prowess to bear, the Terror From Below Squad Pack joins the battlefield in Star Wars: Shatterpoint.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – I Am No Jedi Duel Pack
description:
On the bleak world of Malachor, Ahsoka confronts Darth Vader amidst the ruins of a Sith Temple! Once a padawan of Jedi Anakin Skywalker, Ashoka Tano left the Jedi Order and escaped Order 66 only to face this terrifying agent of the Empire. Ahsoka leaps into battle with the Dark Lord of the Sith, vowing to avenge her master and end Vader’s threat to the galaxy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Shatterpoint – Delta Squad, Form Up Squad Pack
description:
Deployed on the front lines of the Clone Wars, Delta Squad puts the elite skills of the clone commandos to the test. Boss, Fixer, Sev, and Scorch, unwavering in their loyalty to the Republic, execute each mission with deadly precision and overwhelming firepower.

Never ones to sit back and wait, Delta Squad takes control of the battlefield removing every obstacle on their way to securing their objectives. Armed with the customizable DC-17m ICWS blaster and their unparalleled combat training, Delta Squad can complete any mission no matter the enemy.

—description from the publisher

Date Drop is a fast paced card game about harvesting dates. Your goal is to be the first to pack all your date boxes. To harvest dates, you need to flip cards one-by-one. After each flip, you may top to ollect them, UNLESS you just flipped one of the following:

If you flipped a DUPLICATE, you keep nothing!

If you flipped a DATE DROP, everyone gets a chance to grab a flipped card!

There are also a few simple but powerful abilities to help you harvest quickly.

The first person to pack all their date boxes win!

—description from the publisher
This is a test

boardgame: Year of the Rat: Vietnam, 1972 – Deluxe Edition
description:
Year of the Rat Deluxe Edition; recreates the “Easter Offensive” of the North Vietnamese into South Vietnam on 30 March 1972.

This Deluxe Edition enhances the original, acclaimed SPI game design (published during the campaign) with a half-century of research and analysis, providing updated orders of battle and terrain analysis. Three scenarios and fifteen order of battle variants cover a wide range of game options, including operations into Laos and Cambodia.

Additional features include:


     New graphics on enlarged maps and counters
     Expanded Allied airmobile operations and units
     NVA divisional reorganization and tank regiments
     Full 1971–72 US order of battle
     Australian, Royal Thai, Cambodian, and Khmer Rouge forces
     Vietnamese and US Navy riverine units
     ARVN base camps and regional forces
     Extensive optional rules


—description from the publisher

New Campaign Book for 2GM Tactics.

Includes the European, Pacific Front, and North African campaigns. The Manual contains 50 pages.

Includes the general scenario rules and 5 new campaigns, for a total of 31 scenarios.

1940: Battle of France
1941: South Pacific - Hong Kong
1941: South Pacific - Malaya
1943: Battle of Tunisia - Operation Torch
1945: Battle of Germany (United Kingdom)
1945: Battle of Germany (United States)
1945: Battle of Germany (Soviet Union)

---

Nuevo libro de Campañas para 2GM Tactics.

Incluye las campañas de Europa, Frente del Pacífico y Norte de África. El Manual contiene 50 páginas.

Incluye las reglas generales de escenarios y 5 nuevas campañas, para un total de 31 escenarios.

1940: Batalla de Francia
1941: Pacífico Sur - Hong Kong
1941: Pacífico Sur - Malasia
1943: batalla de Túnez - Operación Torch
1945: batalla de Alemania (Reino Unido)
1945: batalla de Alemania (Estados Unidos)
1945: batalla de Alemania (Unión Soviética)

Moscow, 1571. The city is ablaze. Only the Kremlin has been spared. 100,000 to 120,000 Muscovites perished in the flames. The Crimean Khan's Tatar army returns to its capital with over 100,000 captives, both men and women, destined for the slave markets of Constantinople or the Sultan's harem.

Ivan IV, Tsar of all the Russias, saw it as divine punishment. He abolished the Oprichnina and its reign of terror. He restored the boyar families to their privileges. He decided to open peace negotiations to end the Livonian Wars, which were draining his state's resources. He even went so far as to offer the Crimean Khan the return of Astrakhan, a gateway to the Caspian Sea. The Russian state was on the verge of collapse.

1572. The Crimean Khan, Devlet Ghirai, decides to raise an army to deliver the final blow to the moribund Russian state. The effort is immense: over 100,000 steppe cavalry, janissaries supplied by the Sublime Porte, and siege artillery. The ultimate goal is to restore the power of the Golden Horde over Russian lands.

Ivan IV the Terrible must face a war of conquest. But still entangled in the Livonian Wars, he can only muster 60,000 men. The heart of this army is the Streltsy, the professional arquebusiers who form the embryo of a standing army. The fate of Russia will be decided on the battlefield of Molodi, south of Moscow. 
Will you be able to prevent the burning of Moscow in 1571? 
Or will you restore the Golden Horde and bring about the end of the Russian state?

Two scenarios are offered, one in 1571, the other in 1572. 
The rules are those of the Gold and Steel series.

After the final throes of the Hundred Years' War, the Italian Wars, the conquest of America, and the Ottoman Wars, this new game allows you to explore another arena of conflict: the Russian steppes.

Armies largely composed of cavalry and mounted archers dominate the battlefield. But the firepower lies with the Russians (Streltsy and Cossacks). 
Special rules simulate certain aspects of warfare among the descendants of the Mongol conquerors (captive raids, pillaging, etc.). 
Some events take into account the burning of Moscow, one of the very first "maskirovska" operations in Russian military history. The Russian army must crush the invaders, and the Crimean Khan must establish himself in Moscow to rule the Russian lands.

Published in Vae Victis 184


     A game turn = a month
     A counter = 250-500 infantry, 1500-2000 cavalry, 50 cannons
     Complexity = 6/10
     Solitaire game = 5/10
     Duration of the game = 2-3 hours

description:
Players use card and dice combinations to create calculations that end in the number 9. Once you make a calculation, the cards used move into your card discard pile. The game offers beginner, intermediate, and advanced level options, including variations to increase difficulty. All cards are face up, and players have an opportunity to steal.

The game ends when either one player no longer has any cards available or the deck has been used up. The player with the most cards in the card discard pile wins.

Players can play as individuals or in team.

The year is 1378. Europe is on the verge of chaos. The Emperor is dead and storm clouds gather over the Holy Roman Empire. Ambitious princes across Germany and Italy plot to seize power for themselves. At stake is the fate of Europe for centuries to come.

In Grey Eminence: Heart of Europe players relive the epic struggle for power in late-Medieval Central Europe. In the sprawling Holy Roman Empire, which spanned today’s Germany, the Netherlands, Czechia and Northern Italy, hundreds of states competed against each other for territory and influence. While all were nominally vassals of the Emperor, in reality they all served their own interests. In 1378, when the crown fell to a young boy, chaos and war ensued.

Each player takes control of a distinct country within the Holy Roman Empire, vying for influence and prestige. During the game, you will send your emissaries to provinces across Central Europe on missions of diplomacy, intrigue, military, development and religion. Since every country’s starting position is unique, the path to victory will look different every time - but it will always require strategic cunning, timely betrayals and at least a little bit of bloodshed.

				
				
					Uniquely Replayable Setup
     Play as any one of 8 countries within the Holy Roman Empire: Austria, Bohemia, Brandenburg, Burgundy, Holland, Milan, the Palatinate of the Rhine and Saxony.
     Each country starts in a unique position on the map, with different resources and capabilities.
     Choose a political role at the start of the game: Chancellor, Marshal, Cardinal, Steward or Spymaster.
     Start each game with a unique deck depending on your country and role.


				
				
					Fast-paced & Innovative Strategic Gameplay
     Take turns sending out your emissaries on missions of diplomacy, intrigue, military, development or religion.
     Progressive turn order ensures minimal downtime and fast-paced gameplay with plenty of opportunities for reaction.
     Each type of mission has a unique card back, so at all times you will have an idea of what your opponents are planning - as will they about you. Spies are everywhere.
     Interact with other players through various cards, roles & locations to bribe, cheat or force your way into victory.


				
				
					Multiple Thrilling Win Conditions
     Maintain the balance of power and win by amassing the most prestige (victory points).
     Win a sudden death victory by securing control over 4 of the 7 electoral provinces.
     Betray the Empire and assist France or Hungary in dominating Europe - become the final boss, but make everyone your enemy.


Grey Eminence: Heart of Europe combines deck-building with card-driven worker placement. The game is played over up to 10 rounds, with each player taking turns to send out one of their emissaries by playing a card from their hand. Cards and provinces allow players to perform a variety of actions to expand their influence and conquer new lands for victory points. Securing 4 of the 7 electors is one path to victory, as is assisting France or Hungary in taking over the Holy Roman Empire. Otherwise, the player with the most victory points at the end of the final round is declared winner.

—description from the designer

In Drakaina, the spirit world of nature, you will help plant and gather flowers for the Garden Festival.  As a Dragon and Familiar, take part in this two-player abstract strategy game of token placement and collection in order to be the first player to score 15 points.

Players will take turns moving either their Dragon or Familiar and placing one of their flowers on the board in any unoccupied adjacent space.  At the beginning of their turn, players will collect any unblocked rows of four or more flowers.  Players will then move either their Dragon or Familiar and then place a flower.  Dragons and Familiars are able to move onto spaces occupied by flowers and block collection, so plan your moves carefully!

Collect your flowers and score points to be the best represented at the Garden Festival!

—description from the designer

Anticipate and move your cards to make combos of elements, animals or numbers, and be the first to get rid of all your cards.
Use your special cards wisely to knock your opponents out.
This universal game stimulates thinking and anticipation.

boardgame: Prelude to War: Marco Polo Bridge
description: An Historical Advanced Squad Leader (HASL) module depicting the battle outside the walled town on Wanping, China on July 8, 1937

Fractured Sky: Awakening is a collection of four modular expansions that deepen the tactical intensity of Fractured Sky, introducing powerful new ways to increase airship power, control unique abilities on the board, and race for new bounties that award starfalls.

Return to the skies and discover new horizons

All these expansions can be played together or separately (along with Rift micro-expansion, not included). With all four, Fractured Sky evolves into a much crunchier battlefield of timing, bluffing, and long-term planning.

Payloads adds a thrilling push-your-luck layer by letting players sacrifice immediate rewards to secretly stockpile massive power for future reveals.

Flagships introduce a rotating draft of powerful, asymmetric ships that reshape the board every round and rewards clever timing and positioning.

Guardians bring roaming, board-changing entities that players can manipulate turning the map itself into a dynamic weapon.

Bounties replace the hidden objective system with five always-visible, race-to-complete goals that drive open conflict, constant tension, and clear strategic direction every round.

—description from the publisher

The strategic showdown that cranks the tension up a notch.

After the huge success of Roll’n Bump, it’s time for Roll’n Bump Duel — a version designed specifically for head-to-head battles.
Roll the dice, claim the best cards, and place them cleverly in your 4×4 grid. Every decision matters: each completed row scores points… but only if you skillfully diversify the three available card colors.

But beware — nothing is ever guaranteed. Bonus cards can turn the tables at any moment.
Play them at the perfect time to trigger a game-changing special action, or save them to score surprise points at the end of the game. Bluffing, timing, and bold choices will often be the difference between victory… and defeat.

Strategy, luck, and rivalry collide in this tense duel where every roll can change everything.
Are you ready to take on the challenge of Roll’n Bump Duel and claim final victory?

—description from the publisher

Monopoly: Prince is a Monopoly game with a theme around the music artist Prince. The board is customized with themed properties inspired by Prince’s tours, songs, and milestones, while the center displays his emblem. The game has custom tokens, fully redesigned game board, the purple and gold theme, and the tribute-based property names that immerse players in Prince’s world. Specially crafted player tokens include miniature replicas of Prince’s symbol, guitar, microphone, high-heeled boot, keyboard, and motorcycle.

Soar is a bidding game for 2-5 players set in the Fractured Sky universe. Players will commit bidding tokens to quests, balancing risk and reward, timing and deception.

Each turn, place two bids: one for yourself and one that empowers the mysterious Skyborne. Win quests with your bids to gain victory points and powerful special tokens, but beware: the player in last place doesn’t lose… necessarily. Instead, they claim the Skyborne’s final score.

It’s a quick, tense battle with simple rules and heart-racing reveals. Will you fight desperately for the lead or quietly align yourself with the Skyborne for the unexpected climactic reversal?

—description from the publisher

Unlock New Scoring Strategies. In the ever-shifting realm of Dreamland, the influence of the Dreamscape Weavers knows no bounds. Now, with the Dream Cards expansion, your mastery of the territories takes on new dimensions.

These Nine Special Cards introduce fresh scoring rules for the terrains. Each card presents a unique goal that players must pursue to earn additional points. You will draw two Dream Cards at the start of the game. These cards reveal specific tasks that grant points. Fulfill them strategically to enhance your influence.

Combine the Dream Cards with the Moon Cards(another expansion) for an enchanting blend of scoring possibilities.
Embrace the dream, chase the points, and become the most influential Weaver in this captivating expansion!

Adept Recipes is an expansion for Porion Crafter - roll and color, print and play alchemy game.

Play five card sets to score points. Strategically discard smaller sets to change up your cards. You may save cards for future hands but they will be visible to all players.

Each player has a hand of 4 cards. There is a community potato field of 3 cards. Additionally players can use their turn to save up to 4 cards in their own potato field.

Players will score by building 5 card sets using cards from their hand, their potato field, and the community potato field. Scoring sets can be Five of a Kind, Flush, Straight, All Suits, or Full House.

Play cards to add to your mountain range. Try to have the biggest range of numbers when nighttime falls and the game unexpectedly ends. You'll need to be clever to steal from others and play the right numbers at the right time to win.

Players will all have a hand of 3 cards. On their turn each player will draw one card, optionally steal a card from another player, replacing it with one of their own, and finally playing a card at either end of their own range.

Other players can steal from you but only a maximum of twice. After that you are safe from having your cards stolen.

Snacksquatch is a light family concealed bidding game where adventurous kids run outside with makeshift traps to catch all the (imaginary?) monsters in their neighborhood! Bait your traps with your favorite sugary snacks, but be careful – these are monsters! Too much sugar and they’ll get feisty and swat away the higher bidder! Adorable monsters and different scoring rules chalked on the sidewalk every game!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tales from the Adventurer's Guild: A supplement for Four Against Darkness
description:
This supplement for Four Against Darkness adds plenty of optional rules: Guidelines for Troupe play; Rules for stealth, streetwise, banking, magic lockers, gambling and hidden treasure troves; New equipment and spells; New monster tables (riff-raff, ambushers, weird monsters, chaos cultists); Rumors, Scenes and Red Herrings; Nine new thematic dungeons; Rules for joining an Adventurers Guild; Tables for Guild Jobs and unique minor quests; Rules allowing you to play with 1 to 3 characters; Trinkets – six new minor magic items; A ready-to-Run hex-crawl campaign. This book requires only Four Against Darkness but also provides many interactions with other titles.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ways of Wiles and Wits: Narrative and unusual missions for Four Against Darkness
description:
This supplement for Four Against Darkness features several short missions — outside-the-box, narrative, epic, tragic or comedic — for all levels and over 18 non-violent ways to interact with encounters. Leave your sword home… or not! This book also includes guidelines to reskin the mini-adventures for different narrative purposes, to bring your Four Against Darkness games into unexpected directions. This book requires only the core Four Against Darkness book, but it has plenty of optional interactions with many other titles in the line.

—description from the publisher

Your mission is to retrieve the briefcase full of money and get out. There's just one problem: enemy agents with identical bomb-rigged cases have compromised your contact and now there's no telling which one holds the money.

Agents take turns going clockwise, playing cards from their hand. They must outsmart the others, rely on their memory, and use every trick in the book—bluffing, deduction, deception—as the cases begin changing hands and the timer ticks down towards zero.

When the timer reaches zero the mission is over and all agents must reveal their briefcase. The agent holding the case of money is alive, the winner, and the best we've ever seen!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Monster of Loch Lomond: Wild Haggis Promo
description:
In the game Monsters of Loch Lomond, Wild Haggis is a fun extra card that allows you to influence the game in your favor.

Ability: 
Search the open Highland discard pile for a card of your choice, show it, and add it face down to an opponent’s [clan].

Publisher Note's:
What is the Wild Haggis card?
It is an exclusive card that was available during the crowdfunding campaign for the card game Monsters of Loch Lomond. It is not sold in stores, so if you’ve found one, you have a very special card in your hands! The only thing you still need is the game itself.

Wild Haggis:
In Scotland, it is said that the Wild Haggis was originally the basis for the dish “haggis.” There are two types of Wild Haggis: one with shorter legs on the left side, and one with shorter legs on the right side. The first can only run clockwise around the hills, the other counterclockwise. They also cannot mate with each other because if they tried to turn around, they would roll down the hills due to the difference in the length of their legs.

Every year, Scots still manage to convince tourists that you can hunt the Wild Haggis, and some people go into the Highlands in search of this mythical creature.

description:
ByteShift is a competitive, single-page Roll & Write where you draft and combine program elements to build the most efficient hacking routine. 
You roll dice to draft program elements from randomized tables, then write your “code” onto virtual floppy disks, card-crafting your way to the cleanest, smartest hack while trying to outsmart your opponent (or the system in solo mode).

Once you’ve crafted your full routine, you execute it from back to front, chaining effects for maximum efficiency and scoring —aiming to rack up more points than your opponent by the time the run is complete.

—description from the publisher

A Print & Play game of zombies to explore, try a new fighting system against Zombie hunters, and enjoy some juicy brains.

In Zombie Snack, you take on the role of a friendly but starving zombie in a solo 'Print & Play' dungeon crawling adventure. The core gameplay revolves around exploring a modular environment to find 'juicy brains' while avoiding relentless monster hunters. A standout feature is its unique combat system where players physically toss tokens into the game box, with the difficulty determined by the distance and obstacles involved. It is a quick, 15-minute tactical experience that blends resource management with a fun, dexterity-based mechanic.

—description from the designer

Crisis within Time is a fast-paced action card & dice game set in a chaotic world born from a time fracture.

The story takes place in a world born from the fracture of time itself called Terrana. Creatures thought long extinct, civilizations erased, futures that never came to be... suddenly appeared upon Terrana. These new arrivals, the Elseborn are thrust into this broken world.

Players will choose one of the Elseborn from different eras with their own strengths, survive shifting loyalties of the table and be the last person standing. In the game every card in your hand represents an attack & defense move,after you play your action fights resolves with dices and sometimes with a special rules.

Crisis within Time is a fast & brutal game which is easy to learn and can be played with 2 to 4 players. But because it came with different game modes, player numbers can be more according to the mode. There is 3 type of modes in core game; "Duel", "Arena" and "Alliance".

Since each Elseborn represents a different time and world, all the characters were created by a different artist. In the future, as the game develops new characters and new game modes, it will support all artwork being done by artists.

—description from the designer

A two-player peg and hole strategy game with a unique board layout.  Each player is attempting to create a line of four out of their five pegs using the connection lines on the board including the outer ring.

4x Cthulhu (P4/2025)
Strength 8

Hunter, Tough
While this creature is exhausted, it has sneaky.
