In this brutal and unforgiving environment, you must fight for your survival, facing wave after wave of deadly creatures, cunning traps, and ancient guardians. As you battle through the arena, you will uncover hidden treasures and gain the strength needed to confront the mage's final challenge.

Mechanics: Push your luck, open drafting, Melding and Splaying.

Gameplay:
This game has five levels of Encounters. You must complete all five Encounters to win. Each Encounter consists of a number of rounds, the enemy and the player take turns to gather
Energy and execute powerful attacks.

—description from the designer

Exalt is a tactical skirmish game set in a world where ancient spirits have nearly faded from existence as people’s belief in them has waned. To reclaim their influence, the spirits have challenged heroes across the land to battle in tournaments for their blessing.

Each player commands one of 10 Heroes and their Minions, moving around a hex-grid arena to outmaneuvre and defeat their opponent. A central part of the arena and the strategy is the Temple Door. Players use this gateway to the spirit realm to gain temporary blessings that can sway the tides of battle.

The game blends position-based combat, resourceful action management, and opportunistic use of powers to control the board. Players are rewarded for using tactical foresight and adaptability, as rounds are quick and each Hero brings their own strengths and weaknesses.

—description from the publisher

Tricked Out is a card game played simultaneously alongside an existing trick-taker, where as you win or lose tricks in the trick-taker you collect cards in Tricked Out.

These cards then give you points in Tricked Out, and unlock special abilities that change how you play the base trick-taker. You can easily find yourself with additional won tricks, the ability to play off-suit, or swapping cards with an opponent.

Tricked Out works with almost every trick-taker, and adds both strategy and chaos to your game of choice.

—description from the publisher

In Botanimagic, players take on the role of magic apprentices at the School of Botania, strategically growing magical plants across 3–4 shared biomes.
 Each turn, players have options of actions: Plant, Take Seed, or Harvest. Plant allows players to place seeds in a biome, Take Seed lets players add a seed to their hand, Harvest allows players to collect points from a biome and trigger magical effects. Whenever a player Harvests a seed, they must replant it, either to upgrade their spellbook or to reserve turn priority for the next round.
 The game lasts for 8 rounds. A Biome Event is triggered at the start of each round, and at the end of the round, each biome regenerates points based on the number of players present. When the final round ends, the player with the most harvested points becomes the most prized magic apprentice.
 Plant seeds to occupy biomes, recruit companion plants to aid you during the game, cast magic to control the biomes, and strategize to influence the harvest yield.

You love books, but your shelf is stuffed? With the card game No Shelf Control, you can tame the chaos in a playful way! Stack your bookshelf cards by matching colors and collect decorative items for bonus points. But be careful: you can’t place a book on top of a decoration item, and if you end up with an unread stack, you lose points. Each round, you draw a card — if you're lucky, it fits your stack, your shelf grows taller, and you earn more points. But if it doesn’t fit, your shelf collapses. Fortunately, you have a token that lets you repel a card or draw an extra one. Do you want to push your luck?
With a clever mix of strategy and humor, this game brings together book lovers who know the eternal space problem all too well.

-description from publisher

Travel to Japan during the Sengoku period and bring your city to life! Everyone simultaneously plans their own Japanese town — tile by tile. With each turn, new buildings take shape, defining your cityscape. You roll a dice to determine where to place the next tile. If the field is already occupied, an effect is triggered. These effects can increase or decrease your income or provide other benefits. The player who rolls the dice for everyone gets to choose first which building to place — but this building is then blocked for all other players. Who will combine their tiles most cleverly and build an engine for success? The player with the most money wins.
A tactical tile‑laying game of chance and clever combinations.

-description from publisher

Our adventurers stand at the gates of the Dungeon of Doom!
Your goal: be the one to deliver the final blow to the dungeon boss.

To play, you’ll need darts and a space to throw them.

Objective
Collect items and XP to defeat monsters and make your way to the final boss.
The player who defeats the boss wins the game!

How to Play
On your turn, you have 3 darts:
Head → gain 20 XP
Sword → gain 5 XP and block other players (they cannot collect XP on their turn)
Shield → removes the sword’s restriction
Treasure → collect an item

Defeating Monsters

Each monster has an XP threshold that must be reached to be defeated.
Once exceeded, the monster is defeated and you move to the next one.
You must defeat 3 monsters to reach the final boss.
Each monster has a unique ability that influences your adventure in the dungeon.

Ready to embark on your adventure?
Grab your darts and dive into the dungeon of Dart Quest!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Wars: Unlimited – A Lawless Time
description:
The A Lawless Time set features characters and content from across multiple eras of the Star Wars galaxy, with a particular focus on daring heists, thrilling raids, fearless outlaws, and criminal masterminds.

—description from the publisher

The Last Council is a board game for 3-6 players.

Each round, players secretly vote and bid power on difficult dilemmas, using influence, timing, and unique hero abilities to shape the outcome. Easy to learn yet rich in consequence, the game thrives on hidden intent, shifting alliances, and unforgettable moral choices.

The city is divided. The king grows weaker with each passing dawn, and whispers say his strength will not last another full moon. In the shadow of the throne, the council has gathered power piece by piece, its influence seeping through every hall, every gate, every prayer.

With no heir to claim the crown, the question has become inevitable: which of you will rule when the last royal breath fades? Some rise on the faith of the people; others on gold, leverage, and secrets. Some bind themselves to law and order; others learn to rule from the dark.

Once, the city ruled the realm through unity and will. Now it fractures—forced to rely on uneasy alliances, desperate bargains, and aid that never comes without cost. Each passing day brings a new trial, and every choice reshapes not only the city’s future, but the fate of the kingdom it commands.

In this realm, crisis is constant—and within every disaster lies an opportunity for greatness.

POINTLESS MINI 2023 EDITION: This travel size game based on the increasingly popular, witty and compelling BBC1 quiz show “Pointless” with new questions.

TV QUIZ FUN: Replicate the witty and compelling BBC TV quiz show, hosted by Alexander Armstrong, where the most obscure right answer, is the best answer!

TRAVEL GAME: This fun-packed, fast-paced, travel-size version is a great game to play with friends and family anywhere!

BECOME THE POINTLESS CHAMPION: Put you trivia knowledge to the test to find out if you have what it takes, to become the Pointless champion?

FAMILY GAME: A great game for 2-4 players or teams, age 12 years and over

—description from the publisher

Guided by the King’s vision, you must raise towering structures, cultivate the land, and weave terrain into harmony. The island awaits your hand, and the King awaits his masterpiece.

Royal Architect is a roll-and-write game for one to any number of players. As royal architect, you have been summoned to shape the kingdom itself - raising six structure types (castles, villages, towers, towns, mines and crop fields) and cultivating the land with four distinct terrains. Each turn brings new opportunities, but the King’s vision looms over all. He will not settle for a kingdom built on whim; his wishes are etched upon the structure cards, guiding and constraining your designs. Only those who can balance creativity with obedience to the crown will earn lasting glory in the annals of the kingdom.

—description the publisher

Battle Of Biomes is a fun, quick-paced mini game, featuring some of the mechanics from the standard edition of Dino Dynasty.

Each player represents a dinosaur species during the Cretaceous period.  The players face various Challenges, and their importance varies during the game.  The map is created gradually by the players, who place and match cards with Biomes.  Players move their Dino meeples across the map, expanding their territory, enlarging their herd and competing with opponents, collecting Challenge tokens.  The player who gets the most points from the Challenges wins!

Treasure Depths is a solo dungeon scrawler where you dive for sunken treasure while your oxygen supply ticks down. Form interconnected words to navigate deeper through a flooded cavern, collecting treasure scattered along the way. But every word you form uses precious oxygen, and if you run out before reaching the surface, you lose everything.

On Your Turn:
Place a word on the map by writing letters along your path. Each word must connect to your previous word by sharing or touching a letter horizontally or vertically. Every word you place costs oxygen from your limited supply. As you navigate, collect treasure chests scattered across the ocean floor by passing through or adjacent to them.

The Challenge:
You begin at the surface with a fixed amount of oxygen. Descend to the seabed, grab as much treasure as you can carry, then plot your route back to the surface before your air runs out. Each word placement drains your oxygen supply, forcing you to balance greed against survival. Do you push deeper for one more chest, or play it safe and return with what you have?

Winning and Losing:
The game ends when you return to the surface or run out of oxygen. If you surface successfully, your score combines the value of collected treasure with any remaining oxygen (rewarding efficient route planning). If you run out of oxygen before reaching the surface, you lose everything—the treasure, the game, and your life.

Integration with Word Dungeon:
Treasure Depths uses Word Dungeon's core mechanic of spelling interconnected words to navigate a hand-drawn map, but replaces monster battles with resource management. Instead of fighting enemies, you race against your dwindling oxygen supply in a push-your-luck challenge where every move matters.

—description from the designer

The Labyrinth is a solo dungeon scrawler where you race to escape a maze while a minotaur hunts you through the corridors. Form interconnected words to navigate towards the exit, but every turn you take, the minotaur moves closer. Find the way out before it catches you, or become another victim lost in the endless passages.

On Your Turn:
Place a word on the map by writing letters along your path. Each word must connect to your previous word by sharing or touching a letter horizontally or vertically. Your first three words are safe, but after that, the minotaur awakens.

The Hunt:
Once awakened, the minotaur moves after each of your words. Roll a die to determine its movement, and it crosses off that many letters from your path, making them unavailable for future words. When it reaches a branch, it destroys all letters along shorter paths before continuing down the longest route. Rolling a 6 triggers a charge where the minotaur rests, but you cannot reroll that turn.

You start with 5 rerolls for the entire game. Use them wisely to avoid disaster, but remember that crossed-off letters cannot be used for word placement (though they don't block adjacency between remaining letters).

Winning and Losing:
The game ends when you reach one of four exits or when the minotaur catches you. Each exit offers different bonus points, tempting you to push deeper into the maze for higher rewards. Your final score combines treasure collected during your escape with your chosen exit bonus. Victory means successfully escaping with your treasure intact. Failure means the minotaur destroys your path before you can reach safety.

—description from the designer

"Essence - The Grim Expeditions" is a solo or cooperative Tabletop Roguelite.
What makes Essence different is that you level up your attribute bands and these are your key to transform die rolls into APs (action points). You use these to perform abilities, cast spells, or solve obstacles. This dynamic system also allows for you being attacked while still dealing with an obstacle. A cascade of penalties, joining monsters etc may slowly drag you into demise.
Essence is a short legacy dice crawler in which you can level your heroes from level 1 to 8.
An XP shop lets you spend XP to level up abilities, expand your attribute bands. With each level you gain a new D8 (starting with 3 at level 1), so prepare to chuck a lot of dice with your max level heroes!
If you go solo you play 2+ heroes to be able to fight what's ahead of you.

-description from publisher
Imperium Sphere is an extension with 54 new cards to upgrade your Ultrium Sphere's deck.

Spell Track is one of the 8 games in Dr. Finn's Book of Two-Player Strategy and Word Games. The game consists of one game sheet and requires no other components -- just a pencil for each player.

Players race to score 12 points by spelling words. In each round, a random throw of fingers determines a row or column of letters (from a grid of letter) in which players will choose from.  Players use the letters to slowly build words. You score bonuses for longer words, but also for being the first to spell words, so you have to balance trying to score bigger valued words than your opponent, but also being first to do so.

-description from the publisher

Play as carnivores or the herbivores in this two-player tile flipping board game!
Search the pre-historic land for your teams Dinosaurs and rescue them before the comet crashes down to earth!

In your turn you use your one action to move one of your three dino meeples one space. If the tile underneath is face down then reveal it. If you have 3 of your meeples on tiles matching your team colour teams colour then score the points value shown, if not then leave them where they are and try again next turn.

Keep playing until the meteor card is revealed which immediately ends the game.  You then total up the points you have earned.

Begin the railroad revolution in the land of soccer and tango at the foot of the Andes. The new expansion for Dice of Steam welcomes you in Morocco! Prepare to embark on a captivating journey as you lay your tracks across North Africa.

Morocco’s landscape is a striking tapestry of natural contrasts. In the north, the Rif Mountains roll gently toward the Mediterranean, while the Atlas Mountains cut across the country with snow-capped peaks and hidden valleys. The central plains are fertile and green, supporting olive groves and farmlands. To the east and south, the land transforms into the vast Sahara Desert, where golden dunes, rocky plateaus, and oases shape a timeless horizon. Along the Atlantic coast, rugged cliffs and sweeping beaches meet the ocean’s rhythm. From mountain trails to desert caravans, Morocco’s terrain offers beauty, mystery, and adventure at every turn.

Claim your ticket, unleash your entrepreneurial spirit, master the art of steam, and leave your mark on the history of the 1900s Morocco.

Begin the railroad revolution in the land of roses and folklore nestled in the heart of the Balkans. The new expansion for Dice of Steam welcomes you to Bulgaria! Prepare to embark on a captivating journey as you lay your tracks across this historic and mountainous crossroads of Europe.

Bulgaria’s landscape is a breathtaking mosaic of natural beauty and geographic contrasts. To the south and west, the majestic Rila and Pirin Mountains rise with snow-capped peaks and deep forested valleys, while the Balkan Mountains stretch proudly across the heart of the country. Fertile plains in the north and south provide rich soil for vineyards, sunflower fields, and farmlands. Along the Black Sea coast, golden beaches and dramatic cliffs meet the waves in a dance of light and salt. From ancient Thracian ruins to alpine passes, Bulgaria’s terrain offers a journey steeped in history, wonder, and adventure.

Claim your ticket, unleash your entrepreneurial spirit, master the art of steam, and leave your mark on the history of 1900s Bulgaria. - from publisher

Begin the railroad revolution in the land of rolling pampas and rich traditions on the southern edge of South America. The new expansion for Dice of Steam welcomes you to Uruguay! Prepare to embark on a captivating journey as you lay your tracks across this historic and scenic jewel of the Río de la Plata.

Uruguay’s landscape is a harmonious blend of gentle hills, fertile plains, and sweeping coastlines. To the north, lush grasslands stretch toward the horizon, while the south cradles the rolling pampas that have long nourished the nation’s agricultural heart. The Río de la Plata shimmers along the southern edge, where charming coastal towns meet wide, windswept beaches and rugged cliffs. Vineyards, estancias, and quiet woodlands dot the countryside, echoing a legacy of both rural tradition and quiet elegance. From colonial towns steeped in history to the bustling heart of Montevideo, Uruguay offers a journey rich in culture, beauty, and timeless charm.

Claim your ticket, unleash your entrepreneurial spirit, master the art of steam, and leave your mark on the history of 1900s Uruguay. - from publisher

The elites of the City of Angels rely on their android servants for everything: running errands, menial labor, and organizing their lives. But what happens when the programming that holds their peaceful servants bound goes awry?

Contents:
    1 Case Folder
    42 Numbered Case Cards
    4 Darkness Cards
    2 Character Cards and Standees
    2 Character Skill Decks
    21 Android Tokens

Travel through the sunny Egypt. Make your way through desert dunes creating your own path. This Expansion for The Wayfarer contains map of Egypt.

Egypt isn’t just ancient ruins - its natural beauty is equally stunning. Along the Red Sea, places like Hurghada and Sharm El-Sheikh offer crystal-clear waters and world-class coral reefs for diving and snorkeling. In the Western Desert, the White Desert stuns with surreal chalk formations, while the nearby Black Desert features volcanic hills. Mount Sinai offers breathtaking sunrise hikes and spiritual history, while the remote Siwa Oasis charms with palm groves, salt lakes, and natural springs. A Nile cruise between Luxor and Aswan provides a peaceful way to enjoy lush river scenery and timeless landscapes.

Pick up a bundle and create your own path.

-description from publisher's website

Travel through the sun-kissed landscapes of Ecuador. Forge your own path through lush highlands, dense jungles, and volcanic peaks. This expansion for The Wayfarer includes a detailed map of Ecuador.

Ecuador isn’t just about the equator line – its natural beauty is just as captivating. Along the Pacific Coast, destinations like Montañita and Puerto López boast golden beaches, clear waters, and excellent opportunities for surfing and whale watching. In the Andes, the Avenue of the Volcanoes showcases dramatic peaks like Cotopaxi and Chimborazo, perfect for hiking and mountaineering. The Amazon Rainforest invites exploration with its rich biodiversity, winding rivers, and remote eco-lodges. The Galápagos Islands offer otherworldly landscapes and close encounters with unique wildlife found nowhere else on Earth. Whether you're wandering colonial streets in Quito or cruising through the jungle, Ecuador promises unforgettable scenery and adventure.

Grab a bundle and forge your own journey.

-description from publisher's website

Journey through the sun-drenched beauty of Croatia and the lush, alpine charm of Slovenia. Forge your own path along rugged coastlines, ancient forests, and timeless hilltop villages. This expansion for The Wayfarer includes a detailed map of both countries.

Croatia and Slovenia together offer a stunning blend of natural wonders and cultural treasures. Along Croatia’s Dalmatian Coast, towns like Split and Dubrovnik captivate with historic architecture, sun-soaked beaches, and the sparkling Adriatic Sea. Inland, the Dinaric Alps and Plitvice Lakes National Park dazzle with cascading waterfalls and dense forests. Meanwhile, Slovenia enchants with alpine lakes like Bled and Bohinj, the underground marvels of Postojna Cave, and the fairytale charm of Ljubljana. From the rolling hills and culinary delights of Istria to the dramatic peaks of the Julian Alps, every step is a discovery.

Grab a bundle and forge your own journey through these unforgettable lands.

-description from publisher's website

Venture into the soaring Andes and the otherworldly salt flats of Bolivia. Discover bustling highland markets, emerald jungles, and timeless villages nestled between mountains and valleys. This expansion for The Wayfarer includes a detailed map of Bolivia.

Bolivia isn’t just about the altitude – its natural beauty is equally captivating. In the Andes, Lake Titicaca shimmers with sacred islands and traditional villages, while the Cordillera Real offers dramatic peaks for trekking and mountaineering. The vast Uyuni Salt Flats create surreal, mirror-like landscapes unlike anywhere else on Earth. To the east, the Amazon basin beckons with its rich biodiversity, winding rivers, and hidden eco-lodges. Cities like Sucre and La Paz enchant with colonial architecture, vibrant markets, and cultural traditions that blend ancient and modern. Whether you’re wandering high-altitude streets or venturing deep into the wild, Bolivia promises unforgettable scenery and adventure.

Grab a bundle and forge your own journey.

-description from publisher's website

Journey through Angola’s dramatic landscapes, from the golden dunes of the Namib Desert to the thundering cascades of Kalandula Falls. Explore vibrant coastal cities, lush highland plateaus, and remote villages steeped in tradition. This expansion for The Wayfarer includes a detailed map of Angola.

Angola captivates with its stunning and diverse landscapes. To the north, the mighty Kalandula Falls plunge into deep gorges, while the highland plateaus around Huambo and Lubango reveal sweeping vistas and cool mountain air. Along the Atlantic, the Namibe Desert stretches in golden waves, home to the rare Welwitschia plant and striking rock formations. Wildlife-rich expanses like Kissama National Park and the winding Cuanza River showcase a land teeming with life. In cities such as Luanda and Benguela, colonial architecture, vibrant markets, and lively musical traditions weave together past and present. Whether standing before roaring waterfalls or exploring desert horizons, Angola offers unforgettable scenery and adventure.

Grab a bundle and forge your own journey.

-description from publisher's website

Journey through Ireland’s enchanting landscapes, from the rugged cliffs of Moher to the misty peaks of the Wicklow Mountains. Explore vibrant coastal towns, emerald-green valleys, and ancient villages rich with legend and lore.
This expansion for The Wayfarer includes a detailed map of Ireland.

Ireland captivates with its breathtaking and varied landscapes. To the west, the Cliffs of Moher rise dramatically above the Atlantic, while the rolling hills and misty peaks of Connemara and Wicklow reveal sweeping vistas and tranquil beauty. Along the rugged coastline, golden beaches and wild peninsulas meet the restless sea, home to ancient stone forts and windswept headlands. Verdant woodlands, serene lakes, and the winding River Shannon showcase a land rich in life and legend. In cities such as Dublin, Galway, and Cork, Georgian architecture, lively markets, and the sounds of traditional music blend history with vibrant modern culture. Whether standing atop a cliff edge or wandering a cobblestone street, Ireland offers timeless beauty and adventure.

Grab your pack and set out on your own journey.

—description from the publisher

Meat on Swords is a push your luck card game from Friedey's Restaurant, home of the Cheapass classic, Lord of the Fries.

Most Lord of the Fries games use a menu-based system to combine ingredients into recipes and combo meals, but here at Carnespadas Brazilian Steakhouse, there's no such thing as a menu. We just bring you meat on swords until you give up or explode.

In Meat on Swords, players declare before each round whether they are in or out for the round. Then the dealer asks each "in" player whether they want the next card on the deck. If they refuse the first card, they must take the next. Collecting points is good, but if you ever collect two of the same type of meat, you're knocked out.

Meat on Swords is a simple, silly press-your-luck game that just happens to use a nine-suited deck: it's nine kinds of meat, each numbered 1 through 6.

boardgame: Second World War at Sea Coral Sea: First Air Fleet
description:
“ In April 1942, the Japanese Combined Fleet offered Fourth Fleet the carrier Kaga (then completing repairs to the hull damage that kept her out of the Indian Ocean operations) for the upcoming Operation MO, the capture of Port Moresby. Fourth Fleet’s commander, Shigeyoshi Inoue, had been the pre-war head of naval aviation and recognized that one carrier would not be enough. Combined Fleet gave him two (Shokaku and Zuikaku), and these ships fought the Battle of the Coral Sea.

First Air Fleet is a Campaign Study based on Inoue’s recommendations, that in obedience to the classic principle of concentration of force, all six carriers of First Air Fleet should be deployed for the operation. We’ll study how that might have played out, and also Combined Fleet commander Isoroku Yamamoto’s initial idea to use just one heavy carrier (Kaga) and then to use three (Kaga, Shokaku and Zuikaku). And of course, possible American responses. With ten new scenarios for Coral Sea, using additional pieces from Midway.

You’ll need Midway Deluxe Edition and Coral Sea to play the scenarios.”

-description from publisher's website
family: Series: Second World War at Sea (Avalanche Press)

description:
What starts as an ordinary trick-taking game can suddenly propel you toward victory thanks to hidden cards that grant psychic powers (color and number) as special win conditions.

If you manage to use a psychic power, the points you earn are doubled.
Expose your opponent’s psychic power (their hidden card), and your score is doubled again!

Round 1 uses must-follow rules.

But—

Round 2 switches to may-follow.

Reveal your opponent’s power, steal it, and dodge their plays so they can’t use it—
an over-the-top, psychic-power battle trick-taking game!

Tank Commander: The Pacific is a tactical-level, narrative-style 1-2 player wargame using a tables-based game engine where the player commands a United States Marine Corps Sherman M4 tank fighting enemy Japanese forces on Saipan Island during 1944. As the commander of a Sherman M4 tank, your ultimate goal is to survive the battle for Saipan, while at the same time killing as many enemy Japanese units as possible.


     Abstracted USMC Forces. You are never alone on the battlefield.
     One D6, two D10, one D12, and one D20 dice are required to play.
     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.
     Total play time to complete a Short campaign game takes approximately 2.5 hours, with a typical battle averaging 30 minutes.
     Total play time to complete a Long campaign game takes approximately 5 hours, with a typical battle averaging 30 minutes.
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with detailed damage modeling, numerous crewmen skills, 8 types of enemy Japanese Army units, battle weather effects, terrain types, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Tank Commander: Pacific offers memorable experiences and high replay value.

Are you up to the challenge of surviving the battle for Saipan?

—description from the publisher

The aim of the game is to “solve” your side of the Cube by drawing color cards and strategically keeping and discarding them to fill your Rubik’s board with just one color. Special action cards allow you to really twist up the game – steal cards from your opponents’ boards, draw from the discard pile, trade hands, and more. The first person to successfully fill their board with one color has solved the Cube and wins.

Did you hear about Pluto? It'll always be a planet in my heart.

Did You Hear About Pluto is a funky trick taking game for four players, played with a traditional set of double-9 dominoes. By winning tricks you will strive to capture and collect as many planets/celestial bodies as possible, each represented by a suit. After three hands, the player who scored best wins!

Your dominoes are partially locked-suit! During tricks, dominoes may be rotated to signify which side is the suit, and which is the number. But when at the end of the day only the heavy end of the domino will count! (call it a consequence of gravity!) However, to keep trick play dynamic, 9 of the 10 suits have the potential to be trump when played off-suit.

boardgame: Heiho: The Mass Battle Samurai Game
description:
THE STRONG EAT, THE WEAK ARE MEAT!

'Heihō - The Art of War is a mass battle game that lets you recreate the vast, spectacular battles that shook the length and breadth of Japan at the end of the sixteenth century.

Recreate famous battles like Kawanakajima and Sekigahara, control armies that are truly massive in scale, and unleash the sophisticated tactical systems that were unique to Japan in this period as you dominate your opponent!

Sequence of Play:
1- Zanshin (Command Points) Phase: receive Zanshin Points
2- Determine initiative: players bid zanshin for initiative
3- First player's movement: move and fight (opponent can interrupt)
4- Second player's movement: move and fight (opponent can interrupt)
5- Army morale: army retreats if it loses 1/3 of its sonae and enemy has more sonae

Contains Heiho: The Art of War – Wargames rules for large battles of Sengoku period Japan, 1560-1615.

Dive into the exhilarating medieval battle where only one king can rise to glory! You begin with 4 cards and must use clever strategies to outsmart your opponents, forcing them to discard their cards. Eliminate all rivals to seize victory, but stay alert—running out of cards means you're out of the game! Each turn is packed with excitement and betrayal. Are you ready to become the last king standing?

—description from the publisher

In The Flower of Evil, you'll search the nearby countryside for Alien Artifacts, bringing them to your operative base for translation. Face off with a Cult who wants to see the Flower bloom, whilst also keeping the whole operation secret from your parents. Gather enough artifacts to prevent the World's destruction.

Your character is nothing more than a teen, almost a young adult. However, their past has made them able to see the Flower, as only people who suffered too much can. Each character has 3 basic stats: Endurance, Strength, and Wits. Each game, you'll draw a Childhood Trauma card, which determines the backstory of the character and gives them special abilities and restrictions.

The members of the Cult of Pure want to see the Flower bloom, as they believe it will cure the World of rot instead of destroying it. Their pawn will move on the various countryside locations like you, making progress on their own and setting yours back. If their uncover an artifact, the Flower Token will advance on its track. If it reaches the last space, you lose.

—description from the designer

‘Awaken, my creations!’ you hear, as you come into consciousness for the first time. You immediately realise you are a character from a song by pop princess Wendy Miasma, brought to life by some sick spell she has woven. She cackles for effect.

Wendy has created the world’s first necrocurrency, which she has dubbed ‘WENDYBUXXX’. She tasks you with becoming lifestyle influencers to shill her new brand. To help you spread the word, she has set up her own reality TV show, also named ‘WENDYBUXXX’, in which you will be the contestants.

In WENDYBUXXX, you'll manage a hand of cards, each of which has a value and a lifestyle. By drawing, playing and discarding cards, you'll be creating both positive and negative content about lifestyles, while also aiming to have the right amount of money left over to fit into the lifestyle that'll gain you the most followers at the end of each round. Discarded cards go into your offshore account, and the players with the highest and lowest total value in their offshore account at the end of each episode will gain/lose controversy. Having too much or too little controversy will lead to being unfollowed at the end of the game!

Your sole purpose for existing is to gain as many followers as you can before the show gets cancelled three episodes in and goes straight to VHS.

—description from the designer

Detective Box is a game that lets you experience a realistic and immersive murder mystery at home. You play as a detective who has to solve a complex case by examining clues, interrogating suspects, and following leads.
The game is divided into three episodes, each one delivered in a box that contains various elements of the investigation, such as police reports, evidence, testimonies, etc. You also have access to an app that helps you navigate the case and interact with the characters.
The goal of the game is to find out who killed the victim, why, and how, by using your logic, deduction, and intuition.

Detective Box is a game for one to four players, and each episode takes about three hours to complete. The game is suitable for anyone who loves crime stories, puzzles, and mysteries.
The investigation is structured via a web-based app that guides your progress, offer you tools and help if needed.
Research on the Internet might also be required during play.

In Woodlock University, you investigate the murder of Henry Coleman, a student.
A cryptic message, no clear path, three episodes to  distiguish truth from lies. Find the logic through the illusion.

Ciao Bella is a standalone short story (about 2h30):
"The Horde" has struck again. 5 million euros have been stolen from one of Monaco's largest casinos. Your mission: catch the gang members before they disappear.

Madeleine and Pierre Valentin have lost all contact with their daughter. Our only lead: her whereabouts in the Alps. Find her before it's too late.

A package has been delivered to the Agency: an enigmatic box, newspaper clippings from 1947, and the notebook of a magician accused of murdering his assistant. Why send this to us now?

Starry Eyed is one of the 8 games in Dr. Finn's Book of Two-Player Strategy and Word Games. The game consists of one game sheet and requires no other components -- just a pencil for each player.

In Starry Eyed, players are seeking to chart out the night sky.  On each turn, a player places a "tile" (by drawing it) that has multiple celestial body icons into the grid and then picks one of the available scoring conditions. Players take turns filling the grid and picking scoring conditions until the grid is filled.  Players score points based on how well they completed their scoring conditions.

-description from designer

boardgame: Kryptothera: The Cryptid Pursuit – Solo & Co-op Mode
description:
Kryptothera: The Cryptid Pursuit – Solo & Co-op Mode

Kryptothera: The Cryptid Pursuit – Solo & Co-op Mode expands the world of Kryptothera: The Cryptid Pursuit by introducing two alternative ways to play the game: a fully cooperative mode for four players and a dedicated solo mode for one player. Both modes are included in a single expansion box and use the core systems, components, and map of the base game.

The Kryptothera: The Cryptid Pursuit base game is required to play.


Solo Mode: The Descent (1 Player)

Solo Mode is designed as a complete single-player experience. One hunter faces the world alone, competing directly against the Order as it hunts cryptids, triggers events, and tightens its control over the map.

A dedicated solo event deck, influence system, and supporting components simulate an active opposing force, forcing the player to balance personal progress with containing the Order’s advance. Strategic choices become sharper, as every action carries greater weight and mistakes are harder to recover from.

Solo Mode focuses on efficiency, route planning, and threat prioritisation, offering a challenging and replayable way to experience Kryptothera without additional players.


Co-op Mode: United We Hunt (4 Players)

In Co-op Mode, four players join forces as a single expedition, working together to resist the spread of the Order’s influence. Players coordinate movement, share information, and plan actions collectively, balancing cryptid hunts against the need to contain escalating threats.

New co-operative event cards, systems, and components introduce global pressures that affect all players simultaneously. Success depends on careful timing, efficient use of shared resources, and deciding when to push deeper into dangerous territory versus when to regroup and stabilize the situation. Failure to manage the Order’s influence can bring the hunt to a premature end.

Co-op Mode emphasizes shared decision-making, long-term planning, and group risk management while preserving the exploration and tension of the base game.

-description from designer

Escape Detention with the Rebecca Zamolo's Detention Escape! Outsmart the principal, work through the hallways, and solve puzzles in this exciting group game designed for ages 4+ (with help) and 8+ on their own. Perfect for family game nights and playdates, this thrilling escape challenge promotes teamwork, critical thinking, and strategy! In this game, players will match, discover, and escape detention by overcoming fun and random challenges. Be the first to match both pieces on your detention card to claim victory! Get Ready for an Adventure! Whether you’re escaping detention or winning mini-games, Rebecca Zamolo's Detention Escape promises hours of fun.

—description from the publisher

Je Di Bo is a strategic board game for two players that blends logical, chess-like planning with elements of unpredictability. Players take turns moving their pieces on a 12×12 grid, using three types of pieces, each with distinct movement rules that require foresight and tactical thinking.

In addition to standard movements, a deck of 21 “karma” cards introduces unexpected events that can either help or hinder players, creating dynamic situations and forcing adaptation to changing conditions. Players must carefully balance long-term strategy with the opportunities and risks presented by the karma cards.

The primary objective is to eliminate all of the opponent’s pieces from the board. Strategic positioning, anticipating the opponent’s moves, and clever use of karma cards are essential to outmaneuver the other player and gain the advantage.

Victory is achieved by systematically reducing the opponent’s pieces while preserving your own, employing a combination of tactical skill, adaptability, and careful planning. Each game unfolds differently, providing replayability and depth with every match.

boardgame: The Flooded Forest: An Adventure for Four Against Darkness
description:
The Flooded Forest: Frogmen, swamp dragons, spellcasting giant moths... the flooded forest is not a nice place. This is a 20-page zine for Four Against Darkness. This is a setting for Four Against Darkness with new tiles, monsters and loot, describing a flooded forest.

—description from the publisher's website

The players are investors who compete in a series of auctions to purchase the most effective sustainable energy technologies in order to come out on top at the end of an intense game of energy poker with the highest number of terawatts produced.

(this prototype is currently tested by a potential publisher)

Welcome to The Cats of New Orleans. The year is 1868, and the city has acquired a modicum of peace in the aftermath of the Civil War. Cats — and even dogs — lick their wounds and look toward building a better life. However, the discontent of the city’s vermin bubbles like a cauldron full of crawfish that’s about to boil over.
 The Cats of New Orleans. is a miniatures solo/cooperative campaign game set in a turbulent time in the Crescent City. The game plays out over a series of linked scenarios that form a campaign and ongoing story. Each scenario in the campaign affects the next. In fact, many end with choices the players must make that lead them to the next. You will be playing directly on the coil bound scenario book and using 12 sided dice for combat and collecting cards for loot, skills and powers.

As you play each scenario, the heroes learn who’s behind the recent troubles in New Orleans and come closer to putting a permanent stop to their awful ambitions. Along the way, they must defeat swarms of mosquitos, rats, snakes, and other foul creatures. The heroes also gain loot and experience to unlock skills they can use to face stronger and tougher foes.

The Cats of New Orleans takes several sittings to play all the way through, but you can save your progress easily, especially between scenarios. This rulebook helps you learn to play the game by guiding you through the first three scenarios. Just play the game as you read through the text.

The first three scenarios are meant to be played until thoroughly understood. If you fail to finish a scenario or feel like you need a bit more time to master the rules they introduce, play them again.

Once you proceed past these first few scenarios, the game often offers you choices as to which scenario you can play next. The path you choose can change events in the campaign, so pick carefully. Once you’re done with one play through the campaign, though, be sure to come back and try different options to see how they pan out for you.

In the first scenario we teach you how to set up a scenario, move your heroes on the map, search for essential loot, and pick up items. In addition, you learn how to move the Vermin Army’s figures, starting with the vicious mosquitos.

-description from designer

boardgame: This Is Not a Test: It Came From the Wastes
description:
It Came from the Wastes is an expansion for This Is Not a Test, a 28mm wargame set in a post-apocalyptic United States where warbands fight for survival, power, and greed.  Chock full of the most dangerous of creatures the wasteland has to offer, this expansion rulebook features:

Big Behemoths – Equal to any single warband, behemoths are giant monsters that can throw cars, shoot powerful eye beams, or crush puny humans and mutants with claws twice the size of a man. This book is totally about them!

Build Your Own Behemoth – Full rules are included for designing behemoths using a point-buy system.  You can build whatever you want: a giant robot that can shoot missiles, a massive radioactive dragon, building sized insects, a 50 foot tall woman!  If you can dream it, you can make it.

'Scalable Destruction – The rules take into account different sizes of behemoths.  While a small warband might challenge a behemoth with 10 wounds, only the truly best warbands can take on the 20 wound mega-monsters!

Behemoth Fighting Time – Not content with behemoths smashing your puny warbands?  Want to pit your giant monstrosity against other behemoths? We totally have rules for that.

New Scenarios – Three brand new scenarios are included.  In The Trouble Between Us two warbands try to fight each other while a behemoth rampages across the battlefield While in the aptly named Dishonorable Duty, your warbands must collect valuable behemoth “droppings”.  Finally, a brand new solo scenario where your warband must recover valuable relics while avoiding a behemoth running amok.

Behemoth Bestiary – Seven pre-built sample behemoths are included.  Designed using the point-buy system, these behemoths will give you an idea of what you can make.  Fight Crabulous the Mighty, contend with the always angry Dave the Giant, and survive the buzz saw-filled gullet of the robotic Scrap Wyrm!
family: Game: This Is Not a Test

boardgame: This Is Not a Test: Broken Vaults
description:
Broken Vaults is an expansion for This Is Not a Test, a 28mm wargame set in a post-apocalyptic United States, where warbands fight for survival, power, and greed. This expansion rulebook features:

Explore Beneath the Wastes – For the first time, this supplement explores the ruins of the wasteland’s shelters, vaults, and bunkers, featuring new rules and fiction tied to this unique environment. Now you can learn how to set up underground bunkers for your warbands to fight over. The Last Americans thought they were safe, but their faith in steel and reinforced concrete failed them, maybe you can do better.

Four Brand New Scenarios – In Give Me Shelter your warband must race to the safety of a vault before a calamitous storm hits and they plunge through the cold and dark depths of a flooded vault in Dark Waters. In Property Rights you warband must fight for possession of a newly discovered vault, ripe for the picking, and finally in Rats in the Walls, there are rats. Lots of rats. Including rats that make other rats. Rats!

It’s All Your Vault – A new six scenario campaign that will have warbands run a gauntlet of scenarios where they are stuck underground and cannot resupply or replenish their losses. This campaign features new exploration tables for finding relics and a Wasteland Deck unique to underground encounters.

Unique Vault Hazards – What happens to your hapless warbands when the lights go out, their section of the vault floods, the roof over their heads collapses, or they encounter strange glowing mushrooms in the deep dark?

New Creatures – Can your warband stand strong against the bizarre and physics defying eye tyrant? How about blinder bats, stalkshrooms, and something called the Ewwze!? Lots of new creatures to kill and be killed by.

Brand New Fiction – Explore the world of This Is Not a Test with awesome stories by Gaven Conrad and Kyle Nixon.
family: Game: This Is Not a Test

boardgame: This Is Not a Test: The Wasteland Companion
description:
The Wasteland Companion is an expansion for This Is Not a Test, a 28mm wargame set in a post-apocalyptic United States where warbands fight for survival, power, and greed.

This expansion rulebook features:

New Background Material – Further explore the Tri-State Wasteland. Learn all about the locations, history, and factions that call the Tri-State Wasteland home.

New Skills, Mutations, and Special Rules – Two new skills for each skillset, 30+ new mutations, and even random skill tables for that old school feel! There is also a new way to buy off negative traits like Soft-belly and Ragtag.

New Warbands – Two new warbands have arrived; the Settlers and the Renegade Reclaimers. The Settlers are far from the most deadly combatants, but they have numbers and access to healthcare, technical expertise, and the enigmatic Mysterious Stranger. The Renegade Reclaimers are all about the recovery of old world relics no matter the cost. Though always out-numbered, they make up for this buy having more access to more power armor than any other warband.

Warband Variants – The Totem Tribals, a variant of the tribal warband, revere certain wasteland creatures. Players with a tribal totem can take wasteland creatures such as wastewolves or a juvenile Landsnapper into battle and gain access to special skills. Be careful though, this comes at the cost of rejecting technology even more than their tribal kin.

New Specialists – From the explosive Fire Bug for the Raiders to the Peacekeeper Blood Hound, who never lets a bounty go unclaimed, each warband gains new specialist options.

New Wasteland Deck – A whole new deck for your adventuring pleasure. Match wits with the rambling trader, raid a giant ant hill, duel with tribals, and even put on a play worthy of Shakespeare.

Warband Ranks – Gain new abilities that gives the warband unique skills and options.  Want to add a rule to a model that it could not access before? Want your leader to have a cool mutant pet? Now you can. There are even options for adding more elite models to your warband and increasing the total amount of relics your warband can carry.

Buying Off Penalties – Tired of your models stuck being poor or cowardly?  Now you can buy off general abilities like Softbelly or Rag-Tag instead of taking advancements.

Healing Your Warband – Injuries got you down?  Now you can take your warband to the local saw bones for a bit of a tender care.  Of course, this is not cheap and there’s a chance they could always make things worse.

Two New Scenarios – Just ‘Cause uses random mission requirements to allow for quick campaign or club play; while Ashes to Ashes challenges your warband to keep together while battling both the elements and your opponent.

All this and more can be found inside!
family: Game: This Is Not a Test

description:
LIGA the board game is based on the popular P3 radio sports entertainment program, LIGA, it has equal parts sports news, results, reportage, guests, music and quiz & competitions.

You have to get all the scarves in your club colour to win. You do this by answering correctly or doing fun tasks.

boardgame: Brave & Bold: Bag Building Combat Game
description:
For over 65 years, the Justice League has stood as Earth’s last line of defense against impossible threats. In Brave & Bold, that legacy becomes a head-to-head test of skill, nerve, and mastery.

Brave & Bold is a competitive bag-building combat game where every turn is a calculated gamble. Players draw tokens from their personal arsenal—deciding whether to stop safely or push deeper for devastating combos. Push too far, and the game pushes back.

Unlike traditional deck-builders, Brave & Bold replaces cards with tactile tokens drawn blindly from a personal bag, creating constant uncertainty and dramatic momentum swings. Players must manage not only what goes into their bag, but when and how much to draw from it—turning every action into a calculated risk.

Each turn plays out as a tense push-your-luck skirmish between rivals, where you draw tokens from your arsenal (bag)— combat, agility, focus, resources, character boosts, and crises deciding whether to keep digging or stop and trigger your character’s ultimate combos.

Surviving the draw lets you spend tokens to unleash character specific abilities, trigger jaw-dropping combos, and acquire stronger tech from the Hall of Justice. Quick turns, escalating
bags, and cinematic powers create a taut rhythm of risk, reward, and a spectacle worthy of your favourite superhero in every matchup.

Combat is fast, interactive, and highly asymmetric. Each player develops a unique engine over the course of the game, unlocking abilities, chaining effects, and building toward explosive finishing moments. Multiple game formats—from tense duels to large team battles—offer distinct strategic challenges while maintaining a tight competitive core.

Your goal is to be the last hero (or villain) standing. Your starting damage limit is defined by the game format. You win when you have defeated all other opponents first by injuring them, and then by reducing an injured opponent's damage limit to zero.

Designed with organized and competitive play in mind, Brave & Bold rewards skill, foresight, and adaptability. No two matches play the same, and victory belongs to the player who best balances aggression, restraint, and timing when it matters most.

Games unfold quickly but reward mastery. As bags evolve, strategies sharpen, and tension escalates, Brave & Bold delivers cinematic moments worthy of the DC Universe—whether in a tight 1v1 duel or a chaotic 3v3 team showdown.

About the game:
2-6 players in 1v1, 2v2 or 3v3 game formats.
45-minute playtime.
First of its kind bag-building combat game focused on character skirmishes.
2-character sets and 8-character combat vault configurations with new character expansions coming out regularly.
Push-your-luck style skirmishes where you draw tokens, allocate them to abilities, trigger damage and special combos. Build your bag by purchasing additional tokens each turn.
Every character wins in a different way in how fast they move, how they apply pressure and how they recover when things go wrong.

The game is easy to learn, deep to master and stable for organized play.

—description from the publisher

Counteroffensive Victory Chess (光復勝利棋, also known as Fǎngōng Shènglì Qí / 反攻勝利棋) is a 1960s Taiwanese board game produced by the KMT government during the Cold War. It was designed as a propaganda tool to teach children Chinese geography while gamifying the hypothetical retaking of mainland China from the Communist Party.

Gameplay & Rules
The game functions similarly to modern board games like The Game of Life or Snakes and Ladders, relying heavily on luck rather than traditional chess strategy.

Objective: Be the first player to reach Nanjing (the capital of the Republic of China at the time).

Starting Point: All players begin on the island of Taiwan.

Movement: Players roll dice to move through various Chinese provinces.

The Route: A typical path might start in Taipei, move through Kinmen or Matsu, then Fuzhou, and loop through various major cities (e.g., Guangzhou, Chengdu, Lhasa, Shenyang) before finishing in Nanjing.

Unique Historical Features
The board map reflects the geopolitical worldview and predictions of the 1960s Taiwanese government:

Unified Neighbors: Both Korea and Vietnam are depicted as unified, non-communist nations.

Independent Territories: Kashmir is shown as an independent nation, and Bangladesh is still labeled as Pakistan.

Mongolia: The map includes Outer Mongolia as part of the territory to be "retaken," as the ROC government did not recognize Mongolian independence at the time.

A Partners game.

Previously, you could get help from a partner, but in the new Partners Trio you are completely on your own and surrounded by enemies on all sides!

Partners Trio is a strategic and entertaining game for 3 players, where everyone competes against everyone else. The goal is to get your own pieces home as quickly as possible, while also trying to prevent your opponents from doing the same. After each card deal, you pass a card to one opponent and receive a card from another, which often requires quick thinking and short-term planning. The game features completely new card variations and possibilities never seen before — and you’ll need them, as you are surrounded by “enemies” on all sides!

In Dead Letters you play as a 1915 Dead Letter Office employee who has fallen asleep at your desk. The game is a dreamy landscape of "postage punk" aesthetics and early 20th century mail motifs. But at its heart, it's a simple word game.

Contribute letters to a central word to change it. Some letters give you special abilities. Figure out how you can outsmart your opponents with chain effects and Red Letters in order to rid yourself of all your cards and win! Or play cooperatively, placing cards on two central boards to make them both read the same word, without getting stuck (every card you play has to make a new word).

-description from designer

Compete in the World Cup Downhill Skiing competition through the 2018 season.  Women and Men skiers are rated, along with all-time-great skiers of the past.  Play through the 2018 season, or do fantasy match ups with the greats of the past.

Each ski course is graded into 4 sections.  Each skier is graded into three attributes.  Each run is quick t resolve, taking just a couple of minutes from gate to finish line.

A campaign system and tactical rules for pre-dreadnought naval warfare.

A mini campaign covering the Sino Japanese War, 1894 to 1895.

Also included is a brief history of the naval campaign and the Broadside and Salvo fast play pre-dreadnought rules and a complete set of ship data for the rules allowing the campaign to be fought out over a weekend or a few club nights.
family: Game: Broadside and Salvo (Long Face Games)

description:
Steppe Born is a brutal, fast-paced tactical skirmish game for 1–4 players set in a frozen prehistoric wilderness. 
In this 3' x 3' tabletop experience, players take command of a hunting party of four specialized Paleolithic warriors on a desperate mission to harvest enough meat to sustain their tribe through the winter. But the hunters are not the only predators on the tundra—starvation, fatigue, and prehistoric terrors are always one step behind.

The Hunt is On

Unlike traditional wargames where you simply aim to wipe out the enemy, Steppe Born is a game of objective-based survival. You have a limited window of opportunity to harvest Meat and survive.

Key Features

     Tactical Fatigue System: Pushing your hunters to do more in a turn increases their Fatigue. High fatigue can "stall" your hunters, forcing them to spend precious actions recovering while the wilds close in.
     Dynamic AI Instincts: No game master required. Both the Mammoth and roaming Predators (like Sabre-Tooth Tigers and Rival Hunters) operate on an Instinct Logic system. They react to their health and the proximity of the hunters, ensuring every game is a unique challenge.
     Specialized Hunter Classes: Choose your party from 6 distinct roles, including the reliable Spear Hunter, the savage Chieftain, the beast-master Tamer (with their hunting dogs), and the Shaman.
     High Stakes Survival: The game features a "Downed" state. If a hunter falls, their allies must rush to revive them before the tundra claims them forever—but spending time on first aid means less time for the harvest.


Inside the Manifest

     Core Rulebook: Streamlined rules designed for quick play and easy reference.
     The Wilds Bestiary: Stats and AI behaviors for Mammoths, Sabre-Tooths, Wolf Packs, Rival Hunter tribes and more
     Scenario Scaling: Rules for playing solo or cooperatively with up to 4 players.
     Steppe Born Manager (App): A digital App for playing the game.



     Required: D6 dice, 4 Hunter miniatures, 1 Mammoth miniature, various predator miniatures, and a 3' x 3' play surface.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The MORE FUN! CONFERENCE NORTH card pack contains player and scouting report cards for all 4 teams in the division for a total of 72 cards. Teams include the KITTENS, GREENBACKS, MONSTERS, and RAGNAROK. You can add a custom tuck box to your order at checkout. There's a unique one for each division!

KITTENS
Don’t let the name or cute [but slightly sinister?] logo fool you. The KITTENS are nothing to [yarn?] yawn at! They sport one of the most impressive running games in the league thanks to their superstar running back. Interestingly enough, he wasn’t drafted for his running ability but due to his name meshing perfectly with the team image and marketing plan. Meow!

     #44 GAVIN LOVER: team player
     #7 HARRY DANDERS: juke, squeeze play, stiff arm


GREENBACKS
The GREENBACKS like to ‘make it rain’ touchdowns with the pure pocket passing of superstar Roger Dodgers. It helps that impact player Ed Lazy keeps defenses honest with the threat of the running game which ‘buys’ Dodgers more time in the pocket. Lazy may not be the fastest [or most energetic] runner in the league, but his spin move has left more than a few defenders wondering what the heck just happened.

     #6 ROGER DODGERS: pocket passer, dodge, QB read
     #66 ED LAZY: spin


The GREENBACKS love their money as is evidenced by the logo and the fact that they are the only team in the MFL that is not owned by a single owner in partnership with the league. Instead, the team is owned by the players, and they share all profits.

League rules have since been changed to stop teams from being structured in such a way that it takes money away from the ‘deserving’ owners and league itself, but the GREENBACKS are grandfathered in.

If it’s not obvious by now, the GREENBACKS are all about that ‘cheddar’!

RAGNAROK
The RAGNAROK spread defenses wide and thin thanks to Randy Lichen’s skill at working the sidelines and Thor’s ability to drop the hammer on the secondary while running the ball.    

     #6 THOR ODINSON: run block, block +1
     #7 RANDY LICHEN: catch, sideliner


MONSTERS
The MONSTERS are known for their ability to control the center of the field on both sides of the ball, and this has earned them the nickname ‘Monsters of the Midfield’. The rushing attack of star running back Payday and solo blitzes of Solitary are made that much better by the blocking of Freezer.

     #33 PERRY FREEZER: block party
     #55 MIKE SOLITARY: blitz
     #7 WALT PAYDAY: juke, run block

You've battled each other over the streets of Metro City for years. But after all the criminal schemes and heroic feats, the question remains: what happens when you take your relationship to the next level?

Date Knights is a card game in which each player takes the role of a hero or villain out on a date. A player has a hand full of Event cards and is represented by a double-sided Character card and three Power cards, which are laid on the table in front of them.

During their turn, the player chooses which side of their Character card to be on: the Date side or the Super side. This represents their priorities at the moment -- is the character embracing romance or trying do heroic/villainous things?

The player then plays an Event and/or activates a special ability. They could be giving the other character a gift of chocolate, or robbing the charity gala the characters are attending. The player's actions earn Hearts, which represent their success on both the romantic and super fronts. When the pile of Hearts on the table is exhausted, the player with the most Hearts wins!

—description from the publisher

In Ramen Rush, players are rival chefs competing to cook the best ramen dishes using limited shared ingredients. Each round, everyone secretly bids coins to claim ingredients, bluffing, denying opponents, and negotiating deals along the way.

After bidding, players cook simultaneously, managing ingredients, buying sauces, and pushing their luck with the cooking die to complete ramen recipes. Finished bowls score points and grant optional one-time abilities.

The game ends when a chef cooks their fifth bowl, giving everyone one final turn. Smart timing and clever spending matter just as much as great cooking.

—description from the designer

The players try to win the bet by collecting the highest possible points by adding cards to their mines at the end of each "Round" aiming to be the first player to reach 100 points
at the end of the "Game".

​The game CONTINUES until someone reaches 100 points (it may last 2-3 rounds). If more than one player exceeds 100 at the same time, the player with the higher score wins the game.

​Before starting the game, 16 cards from the "Mine Deck" are dealt to everyone; the remaining cards are placed in the center of the play area. "4 coins" are placed in each player's coin pouch and every player chooses a "Mining Bonus Card" they deem suitable after looking at the cards in their hand.

​The "Merchant Deck" is placed in a separate location.

​Afterward, the player whose turn it is:
Draws 2 cards from the center(mine) deck, takes one into their hand and throws the other into a discard pile of their choice or takes the top card from one of the two discard piles ("Excavation and Coin Cards" CANNOT be taken). [Card Drawing Phase]
Then, they perform their moves as they wish—within the framework of the rules. [Action Phase]
Finally, they discard one of the cards in their hand into the other discard pile and the turn passes to the next player.
[Card Discarding Phase]

​Action Phase:
The player can play the "Mine Cards" they will add to their mine in sets of at least 3 (except for certain cards).
They can play "Excavation Cards" directly.

FIERCE COMPETITION
Designed for 2-5 players, making it the ultimate addition to any game night.

EASY TO LEARN A BIT HARD TO MASTER IT
Start playing in 5 minutes, but spend hours mastering your mining tactics.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Doodlebugs!: The First Week of Operation Eisbär, 1944
description:
Doodlebugs! is a solitaire game in which the player controls the British response to the V-1 rocket (nicknamed Doodlebugs, also Buzz Bombs) launches of Flak Regiment 155 located along the coast of France across the channel. Their target...London!

The game covers the first week of Operation Eisbär, which began on June 13, 1944. The game concludes after the last phase of the game is completed for June 20. The actual campaign continued until March 1945. At its peak, more than one hundred V-1s a day were fired at southeast England, 9,521 in total.

The goal of the player (playing the British side) is to prevent the Germans from gaining a victory, which would bring a tremendous propaganda coup for Nazi Germany.  This done by shooting down the V-1 rockets as they cross the channel heading for London, as well as suppressing  fires started as a result of V-1 rockets hitting their target.

—description from the publisher

In Entgleiste Wahrheiten (“Truths Derailed”), players step into the roles of investigators who must work together to solve a complex case.
A train has derailed in the middle of nowhere. Screams cut through the silence as chaos and panic seize the passengers. Was it a tragic accident—or part of a darker plan? You and your team are thrown into a web of lies, hidden motives, and deadly secrets. Every suspect has something to conceal, but how does it all connect? Will you bring the truth to light before it’s too late? Intuition—and nerves of steel—are your only weapons. Welcome to Entgleiste Wahrheiten, the crime case that might just knock you off the rails.
Each case features richly developed characters and clues that must be deciphered as the story unfolds. Navigating suspicious evidence and convincing false trails, only precise teamwork and sharp deduction will let you identify the culprit and bring the case to a close.
This is a one-time play-through case: the solution (including the culprit) is fixed, so once you’ve completed the investigation, the mystery is solved.

—description from the publisher

In „Entgleiste Wahrheiten“ („Truths Derailed“) schlüpfen die Spieler in die Rollen von Ermittlern, die gemeinsam einen komplexen Fall lösen müssen.
Ein Zug ist mitten im Nirgendwo entgleist. Schreie durchschneiden die Stille, während Chaos und Panik die Reisenden erfassen. War es ein tragischer Unfall – oder Teil eines düsteren Plans? Du und dein Team geraten in ein Netz aus Lügen, verborgenen Motiven und tödlichen Geheimnissen. Jeder Verdächtige hat etwas zu verbergen – doch wie hängt alles zusammen? Werdet ihr die Wahrheit ans Licht bringen, bevor es zu spät ist? Intuition – und Nerven aus Stahl – sind eure einzigen Waffen. Willkommen bei „Entgleiste Wahrheiten“, dem Krimi, der euch beinahe aus der Bahn werfen wird.

Jeder Fall bietet reich ausgearbeitete Charaktere und Hinweise, die im Verlauf der Geschichte entschlüsselt werden müssen. Zwischen verdächtigen Spuren und überzeugenden falschen Fährten bringt euch nur präzise Teamarbeit und scharfer Verstand ans Ziel: den Täter zu entlarven und den Fall abzuschließen.

Dies ist ein Einmal-Spiel (One-Time-Play-Through): Die Lösung (inklusive Täter) ist fest vorgegeben – sobald ihr die Ermittlungen abgeschlossen habt, ist das Geheimnis gelöst.

—description from the publisher (German)

Get ready to hit the beat with HITSTER Urban & Hip-Hop. This edition pays homage to the biggest names in urban music, from classic hip-hop to modern rap anthems. Scan a music card with the free app, guess the year of release, artist, and song title, and build your ultimate hip-hop timeline. From the hits of the golden era to today's chart-toppers, challenge your friends and show who's got the game down. The first player to arrange 10 tracks in the correct order will be crowned the Hip Hop Hitster Champion.

—description from the publisher

Annihil-8 is a game for a standard 52 card deck. In Annihil-8 you are a hacker decrypting and destroying a cybersecurity system. The system is protecting 16 Caches. With the help of 8 bits of your own Rogue Data, you must manipulate the system itself to annihilate its own security to unlock all Caches in order to win. The game ends when there are no more valid moves to make. If you have unlocked all Caches and you have injected all of your Rogue Data into the system, then you win.

On a turn, you must choose a Stack to move Data from. Take all faceup cards in the Stack and drop them off one by one onto adjacent Stacks in orthogonal sequence (similar to games like Five Tribes and Mancala). The Final card placed must be either on a Stack with no Data, or onto a Stack that will Annihilate all Data.

Data Annihilates Data if all Data in the Stack matches suit, rank, or if it forms a run of ascending/descending ranks.

Before moving Data, you may place one of your Rogue Data cards from your hand.

—description from the designer

Aur is a family card game set in the kingdom of Aur, where players take part in a tournament to earn prestige and prove their worth. Each player represents a contender seeking to rise above the others and become the new leader of the Royal Guard.

During the game, players play cards to compete in different challenges, manage their resources, and adapt their strategy based on the actions of their opponents. Timing and decision-making are key, as each round offers new opportunities and risks.

The game ends after a set number of rounds, and the player who has accumulated the most prestige points is declared the winner and earns the title of Head of the Royal Guard.

November 11, 1923.

On a wind-lashed island, a former French infantryman—shattered by his return from the trenches accepts a position as lighthouse keeper. In this austere exile, he seeks a measure of peace, the promise of eternal silence… a hope of redemption.

But the storm is rising, and when night falls, something prowls in the freezing darkness. Something that bides its time. Something ancient, unnameable… and that feeds on nightmares.

Le Phare ("The Lighthouse") is a narrative gamebook that closely intertwines a twilight novel with a survival game.

During the daytime phases, you must manage your resources, explore the island, and ensure that each evening the Beg ar Vran lighthouse can be lit.

At night, you must survive…

—description from the publisher

11 novembre 1923.

Sur une île battue par les vents, un ancien poilu, revenu brisé des tranchées de la Somme et de Verdun, accepte un poste de gardien de phare. Il vient chercher dans cet exil austère un peu de paix, la promesse d'un éternel silence... Un espoir de rédemption.

Mais la tempête gronde, et la nuit venue, quelque chose rôde dans les ténèbres glacées. Quelque chose qui attend son heure. Quelque chose d’ancien, d’innommable… et qui se nourrit des cauchemars.

Le Phare est un livre-jeu narratif qui mêle intimement roman crépusculaire et jeu de survie.

Durant les phases de jours vous devrez gérer vos ressources, explorer l’île et veiller à ce que chaque soir le phare de Beg ar vran puisse s’allumer.

La nuit, vous devrez survivre…

—description from the publisher (French)

Les Âmes Seules will immerse you in an investigation in the Haut-Doubs region in 1906. You will play the role of Inspector Lantillet, sent to the area to solve a sordid case of grave desecration. Your investigation will last four days, and each day you will have five actions to carry out: visiting a location, questioning a suspect, following a lead… Choose your actions carefully and don’t waste time! Especially since, depending on the day you visit a location, the same events may not occur.

—description from the publisher (Translated)

Les âmes seules vous fera vivre une enquête dans le Haut Doubs en 1906. Vous jouerez le personnage de l'inspecteur Lantillet, envoyé sur place pour résoudre une sordide affaire de profanation de tombe. Votre enquête durera 4 jours et vous aurez 5 actions à réaliser par jour : visiter un lieu, interroger un suspect, suivre une piste... Choisissez bien ce que vous souhaitez faire et ne perdez pas de temps ! D'autant plus que suivant le jour où vous vous rendez sur un lieu, il ne s'y passera pas forcément la même chose !

—description from the publisher

Players are wannabe influencers who try to increase their popularity catching the interest of the followers performing short speeches about miscellaneous topics linked together.

Topics are indicated on the cards: every turn the player draws new cards to link with the precedent ones. This way the speech will be more and more tricky to invent and hilarious to hear.

Other players listen to the speech and then starts a vote, but differently from other similar games, points (follower) are directly spotted in the cards (harder topics give more followers). In Guru the vote involve just the the topics nominated and the formal correctness of the speech.

The game comes with a free web page with a timer for the speech and constantly updated bonus topics.

—description from the designer

Detectorists is one of the 8 games in Dr. Finn's Book of Two-Player Strategy and Word Games. The game consists of one game sheet and requires no other components -- just a pencil for each player.

In this game, players deploy detectorists on the beach in search of coins, watches, and rings.  On a turn, a player draws a surveyor line along the beach area to create smaller areas.  Players then deploy detectorists in an effort to control the areas that are more valuable to them.  Each player has a secret scoring condition that is revealed at game end, so you are never quite sure who will win until the end.

-description from designer

The Devourers: A new threat for the MutantSlayers.

This expansion will introduce you to a new enemy.

Will you have the courage to face it?

—description from the publisher

Each player has a row of 8 face-down cards, and there’s a draw deck with 3 face-up cards in front of it. The cards show a landscape of mountains, forests, temples, lakes and Kondors, all of which are scoring criteria. Each card contains one or more of these criteria, some of them also show a special coin.

On your turn you can either take one of the face-up cards from the display or draw one from the draw deck. Then choose one of your own face-down cards and decide which of the two you want to keep and put in that spot in your row.

Play continues clockwise until all players have their 8 cards face-up in front of them. Score for each chain of mountains, for at least 4 lakes, for each forest, the most Kondors, for temples and special coins. The player with the most points wins.

Fish Mongers is one of the 8 games in Dr. Finn's Book of Two-Player Strategy and Word Games. The game consists of one game sheet and requires no other components -- just a pencil for each player.

Players buy fish wholesale at a market to resell to their clients.  The game lasts 4 rounds ("hours") with each round broken into 2  periods. At the start of each hour, players secretly make a contract to sell fish, and then bid on fish in the market. After 4 hours, players reveal the contracts made and sell their fish according to their contract prices.  The player who collected the most money wins.

-description from designer

Wonky Doodles is the laugh-out-loud drawing game where players use a bendy, wobbly pen to sketch wild prompts while their team races to guess what it is. It's chaotic, creative, and one of the best family games for adults!

Choose from easy, medium, or hard prompts and sketch fast while your team guesses before time runs out! With 12 rapid-fire rounds and only one chance to steal, this chaotic party games for adults and family guarantees nonstop laughs on game night.

1. Divide into teams. 2. Choose a difficulty level (Easy, Medium, or Hard). 3. One player from each team takes turns drawing a word from the word cards or challenge cards using the Wonky Doodler. 4. The player's team has 30 seconds to guess what the drawing is. 5. The team that guesses correctly scores a point. 6. The game continues with 12 rapid-fire rounds, with only one chance to steal a point from the opposing team. 7. The team with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Comes with 1 Wonky Doodler, 150 Word Cards, 18 Challenge Cards, 1 Whiteboard, 2 Pens, 1 Eraser, and a Sand Timer.

The Movie Set is a strategy card game about collecting and combining movie elements to create valid films. Players take on the role of producers assembling movies using Celebrity, Plot, and Location cards, aiming to score the most stars by building convincing and coherent movie sets.

On a turn, players draw cards from a shared display or the deck to build their hand, managing hand limits and competing for valuable combinations. To score, a player reveals one Celebrity, one Plot, and one Location card and explains how they come together as a movie. Other players may challenge the logic of the set, and validity is determined by group consensus or majority vote.

Wild cards can substitute for any card type but do not score points, adding flexibility at the cost of efficiency. Special cards allow players to refresh the shared display, creating moments of tactical disruption and opportunity.

The game ends after a set number of movie sets have been completed, and the player with the highest total star value wins. The Movie Set blends set collection, shared drafting, and social validation, encouraging both strategic play and discussion around film logic and creativity.

—description from the designer

Lạc Chẩn (English title: Misdiagnosis) reimagines the journey of women's health as a mythological battle between light and darkness. In this vibrant world, the body is a kingdom under siege by "Anomalies"—dark monsters representing real conditions like Endometriosis, Fibroids, and PCOS. To restore balance, players must reveal "Guardians"—magical avatars of real-world solutions like the Knight (Vaccine), the Guardian Angel (Doctor), and the Spirit of Strength (Exercise).

The game breathes new life into classic elimination mechanics with a story of bravery and selflessness. In competitive mode, every player conceals a secret curse (a Problem card) that they are personally enduring. However, victory is not about saving yourself first. You must become a guardian for your companions, racing to identify and purify the monsters haunting them.
