The winner is the last player standing—the hero who altruistically healed all their friends before themselves.

Played on a face-down 6x3 grid with only 18 cards, gameplay blends memory with tactical formation. Revealing matching suits allows players to cast "spells" (swap card positions), skillfully maneuvering Guardians into the perfect alignment to vanquish adjacent Anomalies.

Whether playing solo, cooperatively, or in the hidden-role mode, Misdiagnosis transforms dry medical concepts into a living fantasy realm. It turns the silent pain of women's health into tangible monsters that we can fight—and defeat—through the power of friendship.

—description from the designer

A long time ago, in a galaxy far, far away The rapid-fire trivia game "What Am I?" looms over the Galactic Empire.

Players wear one of six headbands featuring classic characters as they ask yes-or-no questions.

All Star Wars fans can play with 69 words spread across 3 difficulty levels. They must figure out which Star Wars character is on their headband before time runs out.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Slammer!

The Slammer rules are usable for a range of Hollywood-inspired science fiction skirmish action, complete with big heroes, cool space troopers and bug eyed aliens.

Featuring a clever reaction table, the morale and well being of your troops will affect what actions they can perform each turn but gameplay still moves quite quickly.

A starting game can play well with a handful of characters on each side, while you can easily play with a platoon once you understand how the game works.

The rulebook currently clocks in at under 40 pages, giving all the basic stuff needed to play a game without a lot of waffling about. You are here to shoot bug eyed monsters right?

---

The game was created by Chris Nicole in the late 90s, but with old internet sites disappearing into the void the game became unavailable.

This Legacy edition makes it available again in exactly the same it originally existed. It is our intention to publish an updated version in the near future. If you enjoy this game, please do not hesitate to be in touch and we will be happy to hear from you and take your experiences into account.

There will be minor updates to the legacy version as well, as I tweak wording and explanations and tidy up the rules a little.

---

Slammer has a unusual Activation and Turn Sequence that means it can be played solo or by multiple players. Units are activated in order of their Activation level. This reflects a units training, confidence, and approach to warfare. Activation Level may be affected by the unit's tactical situation, casualties or the presence of leaders.

Slammer Army Lists (available on Chris Nicole blog)
Various army lists for popular settings and guidance on how to use them, were offered for Slammer on the web site. These included:

     Colonial Marines and Xenomorph Aliens from the Ridley Scott movie.
     Mobile Infantry and Bugs! from the Starship Troopers movie.
     Zulus and Colonial British from the Zulu movie.

This was the "Mystery Game #2" offered during the "Don't Feed the Aliens, Deranged Dialogue, Bugaboo" Kickstarter campaign. The designer has stated he'll never publish it again, and due to the limited number of backers, there may be only 28 copies.

Exploding Solitaire is a version of Solitaire using a five-suited (all identical) deck, numbered 1-13, with four Bomb cards. First six columns are created, each with five face-down cards, with a sixth card on the bottom face-up.

The rest of the game involves either drawing one card and playing it to any column, or moving face-up cards between columns if they are of lower rank. If a Bomb card is revealed it removes all non-sequential cards from a column which are then redistributed. The goal is to get each suit back in numerical order, from 13-1.

Skullport28 is a card-driven skirmish game set in the City of Shadows — the lawless port beneath Waterdeep where Drow, Duergar, and worse compete for contracts in the dark.

				
				
					The HookCapture > Kill. Dead enemies give you nothing. Captives have value — for ransom, leverage, or settling old scores. The emotional payoff isn't the kill; it's finally getting revenge on that bastard dwarf who left your lieutenant in the Darklake.

				
				
					Core MechanicBoth players simultaneously reveal one Prep card (black) and one Nerve card (red). Your Execution = MIN(Prep, Nerve) — you need both planning and courage to succeed. Taking damage permanently degrades your Nerve cards, representing your crew's mounting exhaustion and desperation. When a Nerve card hits zero, it becomes a Joker — dead weight clogging your hand. Three Jokers and your crew breaks.

				
				
					The ToneThis isn't grimdark misery tourism. It's heist-movie energy: schemes, reversals, narrow escapes. Casablanca meets Blade Runner meets your favorite caper film. Everyone's running a scheme, trust is expensive, and violence is just business.

"The Contract is Sacred. Everything Else is Negotiable."

				
				
					Campaign PlayGrudges carry between games. The only way to improve your models is to settle grudges — capture or kill the specific enemy who wronged you. Ransom negotiations, debt collection, and revenge arcs create emergent narrative.

				
				
					Writing the ShameWhen a model goes down, you write on a note card how they were shamed — what the enemy said, what they took, how they left your soldier bleeding in the dark. These moments become the seeds for new grudges. The game's best stories come from settling old scores that started as scribbled notes on index cards.

—description from the designer

Discovered by accident, Isla Lagarto is home to the last living examples of prehistoric life on earth. A private consortium took control of the island, modified it for people's entertainment, and The Raptor Games were born. Thrill seekers from around the globe join the event in hopes of being the sole survivor and taking home the billion dollar prize. The Raptors who hunt as if with a shared brain, are the most formidable foes for these contestants. Their speed is blinding and many consider them the real stars of the show. As long as the Raptors stay together, not even highly trained soldiers stand a chance. Some contestants join the event to end the games for good, by clearing the island of every Raptor - several have tried but no one has succeeded.

—description from the publisher

The loudest edition of the cult game Stadt Land Vollpfosten, all about music, hits, and the best sound. The ultimate game pad with 26 creative and fun categories to sing along and dance to with family and friends. Each player receives one page of the DIN A4 pad and places it in front of them. A time limit is set for each round (we recommend 2 minutes). The innovative letter bar helps to determine the letter. Then you have to enter all the terms as quickly as possible. There are 8 terms to find per round.

—description from the publisher

Die lauteste Edition des Kultspiels Stadt Land Vollpfosten rund um Musik, Hits und den besten Sound. Der ultimative Spielblock mit 26 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien zum Mitsingen und Lostanzen mit Familie und Freunden. Jeder Spieler erhält eine Seite des DIN-A4 Blockes und legt diese vor sich hin. Es wird eine Zeit pro Durchgang ausgemacht (wir empfehlen 2 Minuten als Begrenzung). Die innovative Buchstabenleiste hilft bei der Ermittlung des Buchstabens. Dann gilt es so schnell wie möglich alle Begriffe einzutragen. Pro Runde sind 8 Begriffe gesucht.

—description from the publisher (German)

Fins up! Bring your scaly friends right in front of the laughing Buddha—before the others do or he moves to another shore. Only those who trade cleverly and keep an eye on their fish will win. Can you get your fish in line the fastest? Your task is to be the first to gather your group of fish in front of the laughing Buddha by trading cleverly and planning ahead. But be careful: the competition never sleeps, and Buddha is always on the move!

—description from the publisher

Flossen nach vorn! Bring deine schuppigen Freunde direkt vor den lachenden Buddha – bevor es die anderen tun oder er an ein anderes Ufer wandert. Nur wer clever tauscht und seine Fische immer im Blick behält, gewinnt. Kriegst du deine Fische am schnellsten auf die Reihe?Deine Aufgabe ist es, als erstes deine Fischgruppe durch kluges Tauschen und vorausschauendes Planen rechtzeitig vor dem lachenden Buddha zu versammeln. Doch Vorsicht: Die Konkurrenz schläft nicht und Buddha ist ständig unterwegs!

—description from the publisher (German)

In Endstation Unbekannt (“Terminus Unknown”), players step into the roles of investigators who must work together to solve a high-stakes case.
A silent strike at the heart of the republic. Stefan Severin, CEO of Deutsche Railway, is gone—erased. No witnesses. No traces. Only wiped servers and a city slipping into panic. Berlin teeters on the edge of chaos as you hunt a phantom through a web of lies, industrial espionage, and carefully planted misdirection. But beware: your opponents are always one step ahead. This isn’t a game—it’s a race against death. Will you find Severin before his trail goes cold forever?
Each case features richly developed characters and clues that must be deciphered as the story unfolds. Navigating suspicious evidence and convincing false trails, only precise teamwork and sharp deduction will let you uncover the truth and bring the case to a close.
This is a one-time play-through case: the solution (including the culprit) is fixed, so once you’ve completed the investigation, the mystery is solved.

—description from the publisher

In „Endstation Unbekannt“ („Terminus Unknown“) schlüpfen die Spieler in die Rollen von Ermittlern, die gemeinsam einen hochbrisanten Fall lösen müssen.
Ein lautloser Schlag ins Herz der Republik. Stefan Severin, CEO der Deutschen Railway, ist weg – einfach ausradiert. Keine Zeugen, keine Spuren, nur gelöschte Server. Berlin versinkt im Chaos. Ihr jagt einem Phantom durch ein Netz aus Lügen, Industriespionage und gezielt gelegten falschen Fährten hinterher. Doch Vorsicht: Eure Gegner sind euch immer einen Schritt voraus. Das ist kein Spiel – es ist ein Wettlauf gegen den Tod. Findet ihr Severin, bevor seine Spur für immer erkaltet?
Jeder Fall bietet reich ausgearbeitete Charaktere und Hinweise, die im Verlauf der Geschichte entschlüsselt werden müssen. Zwischen verdächtigen Spuren und überzeugenden falschen Fährten bringt euch nur präzise Teamarbeit und scharfer Verstand ans Ziel: die Wahrheit aufzudecken und den Fall abzuschließen.
Dies ist ein Einmal-Spiel (One-Time-Play-Through): Die Lösung (inklusive Täter) ist fest vorgegeben – sobald ihr die Ermittlungen abgeschlossen habt, ist das Geheimnis gelöst.

—description from the publisher (German)

In Wildnis ohne Wiederkehr (“No Way Back”), players step into the roles of investigators who must work together to solve a high-stakes case.
Paradise turns into a trap. In the isolated wilderness of Malaysia, the reality show Infinity 8 was meant to make history. But while the world stares at the glittering façade, something unthinkable happens in the shadows of the palms: a contestant dies—no witnesses. The cameras keep rolling. Live and relentless. As the jungle closes in, one truth becomes terrifyingly clear: the killer is still on the island. Masks slip. Paranoia spreads. Ratings soar. Who will survive the finale?

Each case features richly developed characters and clues that must be deciphered as the story unfolds. Navigating suspicious evidence and convincing false trails, only precise teamwork and sharp deduction will let you uncover the truth and bring the case to a close.

This is a one-time play-through case: the solution (including the culprit) is fixed, so once you’ve completed the investigation, the mystery is solved.

—description from the publisher

In „Wildnis ohne Wiederkehr“ („No Way Back“) schlüpfen die Spieler in die Rollen von Ermittlern, die gemeinsam einen hochbrisanten Fall lösen müssen.
Das Paradies wird zur Falle. Mitten in der Isolation Malaysias sollte die Reality-Show Infinity 8 Geschichte schreiben. Doch während die Welt auf die glitzernde Fassade starrt, geschieht im Schatten der Palmen das Unfassbare: Ein Teilnehmer stirbt – keine Zeugen. Doch die Kameras laufen weiter. Live und unerbittlich. Während der Dschungel euch umschließt, wird klar: Der Killer ist noch auf der Insel. Die Masken fallen. Die Paranoia wächst. Die Quoten steigen. Wer überlebt das Finale?

Jeder Fall bietet reich ausgearbeitete Charaktere und Hinweise, die im Verlauf der Geschichte entschlüsselt werden müssen. Zwischen verdächtigen Spuren und überzeugenden falschen Fährten bringt euch nur präzise Teamarbeit und scharfer Verstand ans Ziel: die Wahrheit aufzudecken und den Fall abzuschließen.

Dies ist ein Einmal-Spiel (One-Time-Play-Through): Die Lösung (inklusive Täter) ist fest vorgegeben – sobald ihr die Ermittlungen abgeschlossen habt, ist das Geheimnis gelöst.

—description from the publisher (German)

The Hedgies is a quick and light family board game where players help hungry hedgehogs gather food before winter returns. On each turn, players explore the wilderness, perform three actions such as moving their hedgehog, collecting food, fulfilling objectives, or interacting with other hedgehogs. As the deck of food cards diminishes, resource management and tactical choices become essential.

Players score points from collected food, completed objectives, and other bonuses. The game ends when all food cards have been revealed and resolved, and the player with the highest score wins. The rules include both a simple version for young children and extended rules that add depth for older players.

•••

Ежковците е бърза и лека семейна игра, в която играчите помагат на гладни таралежчета да съберат достатъчно храна преди да настъпи отново зимата. Всеки ход играчите извършват три действия като преместване на таралежа, събиране на храна, изпълнение на мисии или взаимодействие с другите играчи. С напредването на играта и изчерпването на картите с храна, тактическите решения и управлението на ресурси стават все по-важни.

В края на играта се събират точки за събраната храна, изпълнените мисии и други бонуси, а победител е играчът с най-много точки. Играта предлага както основни лесни правила за най-малките, така и разширени правила за по-големи деца и възрастни.

boardgame: Race to Stupid: The Expansion Volume 1
description:
150 new trivia cards to use with the base game.

Requires the base game to play.

Players answer trivia questions with an attempt to be correct and an attempt to be the most creatively and willfully wrong.  Answer correctly for points.  The best incorrect answer gets even more points.

Four additional answer boards and dry erase markers to add more minds to the think tank of creative truth fabrication.

Requires base game of Race to Stupid to play.

Add the album card "Asterix in Lusitania" to the base game.

Worth 15 sesterces.

Power: You can move Boulquiès on this card if he's in play or in the discard pile.

Add the neutral (purple) character card "Boulquiès" to the base game.

Strength: 3

Power: Get 1 centurion helmet from the stock pile.

boardgame: WICCA: El juego de las pociones
description:
Witches and wizards who fight to win the dimond of the Wicca tournament.

Using the cards in your hand, you can buy items in the market and/or attack your opponents, but you can only use one special potion or one amulet per turn. Once used, the cards are discarded (and the market cards are returned to their place. After completing your actions, take a card from the central deck (which you cannot use until your next turn). Play then passes to the next player clockwise.

When a player loses a life, they must turn their card face down so that only their character's remaining lives are visible. If they lose all four lives, they are eliminated.

The player who gets the diamond first or who eliminates all other opponents wins.

The playing pieces are placed on their colored starting spaces, and the horses on space 9 (the stable). With 2 players, 4 horses are placed on the stable; with 3 players, 5; with 4 players, 6; and with 5 players, all 7.

The players' objective is to get as many of their mounted pieces as possible to reach the finish line.

Players take turns moving a piece after rolling a die. Between the start and the stable, pieces can only move forward. From there, pieces (without horses) can move backward, but not beyond the stable. Riders (pieces on horses) can only move forward. Any number of pieces can occupy a single space without interfering with each other.

Pieces that land exactly at the stable on their way to the finish line occupy one of the empty horses and become riders. As long as they remain on that space, they are safe from attack.

If a piece lands on a rider between the stable and the finish line, it knocks the rider off their horse and places itself on the horse; the knocked-off piece is removed from the game. Conversely, if a rider lands on a space occupied by one or more pieces, they capture all of them. The finish is only accessible to riders. Arriving riders occupy the marked circles in turn, starting from number 1. In the endgame, riders may only move if the points they have thrown do not exceed the number of spaces to the finish. Pieces must move backward if there are not enough spaces forward to move their pieces.

The game ends when all horses have reached the finish. If two or three colors have reached the finish with the same number of pieces (as riders), the color whose occupied spaces yield the lower point value wins—that is, the color that arrived first.

—user summary

The players position their playing pieces on the corresponding corners of the game board. The player who rolls the highest number goes first.

If a player lands on a space numbered 1-12 that is currently unoccupied, they receive a rental agreement for that space from the market office and must purchase the stall for that number and five units of the corresponding item for 5 DM. The item is depicted on five small cards, each showing the corresponding stall number. The player places these five cards in front of them next to their rental agreement.

If a player lands on a space already rented by another player, they must purchase one of the five items for 2 DM. The special "Beautiful Fountain" space grants the player a 2 DM subsidy from the market office. The special space, "Frauenkirche" (Church of Our Lady), means that the player donates 2 DM towards the purchase of Christmas gifts for orphans.
If a player has sold all 5 items from one of their stalls, the stall is closed (returned to the market office), and they receive a 2 DM bonus from the market office for good sales performance.

There are two ways to end the game:

     A certain amount of time is agreed upon, and then the scores are tallied. If a player becomes insolvent before the end of this time, they are eliminated.
     The game continues until one player becomes insolvent; then the scores are tallied.


Score: The money retains its value. Each item purchased by another player is valued at 1 DM, as is each unsold item from a player's own stall. The player with the most capital after this settlement wins.

—user summary

Slaget vid Nissan ("The Battle of Niså") was a naval battle fought on 9 August 1062 between the forces of Norwegian king Harald Hardrada and king Sweyn II of Denmark. The battle was won clearly by the Norwegians, but since many Danes managed to escape, including Sweyn, it proved indecisive in Harald's attempt to conquer Denmark.

This tabletop miniatures game recreates the night of the battle; how it could have looked, and also if it could have ended in another way. It includes 200 miniature vikings, more than 25 ships, and a model of the coastal area outside of present-day city of Halmstad, Sweden.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Abejas: Las ordenes de la reina
description:
Every morning the meadow fills with color… and competition. Bees search for the sweetest nectar while hummingbirds, raccoons, and a hungry spectacled bear constantly disrupt the balance of the meadow.

In Abejas: Las ordenes de la reina, players compete to build stacks of flowers in order to produce honey and complete the Queen’s Orders. Throughout the game, players manage their cards, create efficient combinations, and adapt to changes caused by animals and events that can disrupt even the best-laid plans.

The game ends when a player completes three Queen’s Orders or when the Queen’s Orders deck is depleted. At that point, the player with the most victory points—earned mainly by producing honey and fulfilling the Queen’s demands—wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Cada mañana el prado se llena de color… y de competencia. Las abejas buscan el néctar más dulce mientras colibríes, mapaches y un hambriento oso de anteojos alteran constantemente el equilibrio del lugar.

En Abejas: Las ordenes de la reina, los jugadores compiten por construir pilas de flores para producir miel y completar las Órdenes de la Reina. A lo largo de la partida deberán gestionar sus cartas, crear combinaciones eficientes y adaptarse a los cambios provocados por animales y eventos que pueden afectar incluso los planes mejor preparados.

La partida finaliza cuando un jugador completa tres Órdenes de la Reina o cuando el mazo de Órdenes se agota. En ese momento, el jugador con más puntos de victoria, obtenidos principalmente al producir miel y cumplir las exigencias de la Reina, gana la partida.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Battle across the Solar System through a branching narrative campaign in this deck-building game for 2 or 4 players.

Asteroid belt – RareMetal Ventures mining facility, 2205 CE. For five years, conflict between the Lunar Frontier Authority and a resistance movement known as the Breakers has destroyed peace across the solar system. Their war has drawn in the Galilean Federation, and now threatens settlements and bases on Mars, the Moon, and even on Earth. The epic battle is about to begin: it’s time to choose your side.

Decide the fate of our solar system in Undaunted 2200: Revolution, a standalone deck-building game. Play through branching, four-scenario-long narrative arcs, or pick up any of the 15 scenarios for a quick battle. Master the skies and control the planet surface, in this innovative integration of aerial and ground combat. Varied terrain types affect line of sight and manoeuvrability, offering both challenges and opportunities.

Arm your mechs and launch your fighters. It's time to finish this.

—description from the publisher

Each player receives two product cards and the free ticket corresponding to their playing color. The remaining product cards stay face down on the table. Each player also receives a penalty chart explaining the meaning of the red numbered spaces.

Each player places their game piece on the Caramba space of their colored space and may begin moving immediately after rolling the die. A player may roll a second time on a 6, unless this 6 landed them on a red space.

If a player lands on a red numbered space requiring a specific Caramba product, they first check if they possess the necessary product card. If they do, they are in luck, as they may continue their journey without penalty in the next round. At the same time, they must return this card to the remaining cards on the table and draw the bottom one, as each player must always have two product cards in hand. The "table cards" are shuffled after each exchange.

If a player doesn't have the necessary product card, they have two options to potentially obtain it:


     Exchange one of their own product cards for the bottom "table card" or
     "Buy" the necessary product card from another player using a "free ticket." 


A third card acquired through "purchase" is immediately returned to the table cards after use. The "seller," however, must draw a card to replace their missing one.

Free tickets: If a player acquires a free ticket by "selling" a product card, they may use it as a "pass" on any numbered space. After one use, the ticket is removed from circulation for the current game. Naturally, each player may only use their opponents' free tickets as "passes." Their own free ticket is only for purchasing. No player is obligated to sell. If a player refuses, or if the required product card is not in play, the player who landed on a red numbered space must remain there and may exchange another product card after each round until they can advance. Of course, they still have the option of selling a product card to obtain a free ticket. 
If the player still fails to obtain the required product card, they may only continue their journey after skipping five turns.

If a player lands on a space already occupied by another player, they must move back 10 spaces.

If a player lands on a red junction space, they must continue in the direction of the red arrow. However, if they roll a higher number, they cross this space in the direction of the black arrow.

The game is won when a player reaches exactly the Caramba point located in their colored space.

—user summary

The land of Gea has been thrown into chaos with the sudden arrival of Poligon, the King of Hades, and ancient heroes now clash to control his mysterious power. King of Hades: The Arrival is an asymmetric competitive strategy board game for 2–6 players that unfolds across three distinct chapters, each with unique objectives and shifting alliances.

Players take on the roles of legendary heroes from rival races—Legurians, Ge-Ants, and Dragonrace—each with their own abilities and strategic strengths. Turns are driven by action choices and tactical movement across a richly interconnected map, where positioning, combat, and resourceful card play are essential. Players will explore sacred spots, enhance their heroes, trade or craft powerful equipment, and engage in strategic combat as they pursue chapter-specific victory conditions.

Combat is resolved through a blend of movement, attack and defence cards, and careful use of hero abilities, while special map locations open paths to upgrades, treasures, and card manipulation. Throughout the three chapters—The Arrival, The Sacred War, and The Betrayal—players will adapt their strategies as objectives shift from chase and capture to team conflict and free-for-all supremacy.

With deep strategic choices, variable player powers, and campaign progression between modes, King of Hades: The Arrival offers a rich competitive experience that rewards tactical planning and opportunistic play.

—description from the designer

Do you have a Filthy Dumper or a Trim Beaver? In Come Again, you'll ask outrageous questions, swap cheeky cards, and laugh your way through a deck full of innuendos. The more sets you collect, the closer you are to victory!


     Funny party matching game for adults: A quick, easy-to-learn matching card game built for laughs, reactions, and nonstop “did you just say that?” moments.



     Suggestive puns: Players request cards using hilarious innuendo-style phrases like “Do you have fuzzy balls?” that sound way dirtier than they look.



     Innocent artwork, dirty minds humor: Each phrase is paired with playful graphics that make the innuendos look totally harmless—until everyone hears them out loud.



     Travel size for parties and road trips: Compact and easy to pack, making it perfect for bringing to parties, game night, vacations, and anywhere you want instant adult humor.


Game includes 76 Game Cards with 19 Suggestive Phrases

—description from the publisher

Al & Denta add song and dance to your heroic Nomnisaur party! Take control of their slightly undercooked antics to (annoy) inspire your friends! The Nomster Manual also adds a powered up Dungeon, featuring a cost of all new villians inspired by your favorite RPG, like tiacat, the ferocious five-headed feline!

As our newest employees, it is your job to take care of our mythic guests; gathering ingredients for their baths, completing treatments, giving nourishment, and cleaning the baths to welcome new and potentially troublesome guests.

Players compete to earn the most amber tokens (victory points) by treating the mythic guests that visit the baths. Guests arrive with a set of ingredient requirements that must be met to complete their treatment. Over the course of the game players will gather ingredients needed to treat guests, nourish them to earn their favour, and clean dirty baths left after their treatment.

Treating guests and cleaning baths earns players amber. More complicated treatments will earn you more, but if you complete any treatment exactly, you will be rewarded with valuable tips from our guests!

Each turn, you take 1 main action, and possibly 1 bonus action, in any order of your choice.

Main actions:

     Gather 1 ingredient card used to treat guests.
     Nourish a regular guest to ensure only you can treat them and earn their favour.
     Treat a guest with ingredients.
     Clean dirty baths vacated by treated guests to earn amber and welcome new guests.


Players may use active favour tokens, earned throughout the game by nourishing then treating guests, to add minor bonuses to their main action.

The end of the game is triggered by a player treating a certain number of guests, scaled to the player count. The player with the most amber wins.

—description from the publisher

Skyward Republic picks up where Skyward left off. In just four short years, Skyward has become a bustling metropolis of humans and robots living and working side by side. The Wardens have successfully launched their city into the sky, but what do you do once you’ve achieved the impossible?

The Airborne City is now a culture in its own right, and the cultural luminaries have declared the city a Republic. To mark their independence from the old ways, the Wardens will construct an entirely new district showcasing the technological, cultural and economic achievements of Skyward. The four factions will cooperate to show off everything they’ve achieved so far, and together, look to the future of the Republic.

In Skyward Republic, players build a sky city together. As they do, they aim to score the most victory points (VPs) from Buildings, Extensions, Vehicles and Critters launched into their airspace, and from Benefactors whose requirements they can meet by building Buildings and Extensions to match Benefactors' resource requirement icons during the game. Benefactors also grant useful bonus abilities.

Players gain cards to launch through a ‘flip or pick’ open draft method that incrementally adds cards to the card piles on offer to the players, building the pressure to pick them early. They then use Faction (resource) cards they draft and cog tokens they earn or produce to pay for cards and launch (build) them into their airspaces.

Play continues over multiple rounds, until one player has 8 Buildings in their airspace.

After adding end game bonus points from their leftover cogs, and some cards, the player with the most VPs at the end of the game wins.

Skyward Republic reimplements Skyward with twice as many cards, an all new draft method suited to multiplayer drafting, larger tableaus of combinational card abilities and a race to gain Benefactors bonus points and abilities as you play.

—description from the publisher

There were some close calls, but the Roman Empire survived. The Ancient Fathers’ commitment to their honor and traditions kept them in the fight. Now, millennia later, it’s your turn to make your mark on history. It’s time the Eternal Empire gave up these weak aspirations of regional power. You will lead Rome to become what it was always meant to be: a global empire.

As Rome’s leader, you will need to manage the Empire’s resources wisely, build and fine-tune the economy, and lead your armies into glorious conquest. And, most importantly, you will construct magnificent monuments to honor the Ancients. But you must not become careless. Your enemies scheme at your borders, looking for an opportunity to invade and destroy what you’ve built. Can you lead Rome to victory and global dominance, all while devoting resources to honoring your ancestors?

But why stop there? Our destiny reaches beyond the firmament. One day, Rome will be an interplanetary empire. One day, she’ll take control of the stars.

Astra Imperium is a fast-playing resource management game for one player.

Draw cards and manage tough decisions about what to use them for each turn. Collect industry, science and military resources. Spend those resources to conquer regions or build buildings to improve your engine. Or, work towards constructing monuments with powerful abilities and scoring potential. Be sure to save enough military resources to defend from increasingly menacing enemy invasions at the end of each round!

After round 3, compare your score to the history books to gauge your success.

Instead of one-off games, you can play through 5 multi-stage campaigns that drastically change up gameplay and help you discover new strategies. Play the campaigns in order to guide Rome through an epic saga of rising to power, struggling through decline, traveling through space to find a new home planet, fighting off unique invading forces, and even terraforming.

—description from the publisher

Pacifist Raid is an asymmetric psychological chase game for 3 to 4 players. One player takes on the role of a fleeing "Pacifist Demon King," while the others form a team of Heroes dedicated to hunting him down.

The game unfolds over a series of rounds where all players take their turns simultaneously. In each round, players secretly program a sequence of three actions using cards. These actions are then revealed and resolved sequentially. Success depends heavily on reading your opponents; the Hero team must coordinate their movements and communicate effectively to trap the agile Demon King, while the Demon King must outwit them to survive.

The victory conditions are distinct for each side. The Heroes win by dealing enough damage to break through all three phases of the raid and ultimately defeating the Demon King. Conversely, the Demon King wins immediately if they survive the time limit (7 rounds) of just a single phase. It is a tense battle of endurance versus aggression.

—description from the designer

Brace yourselves! Le Temps des Braves plunges you into the heart of the epic battles of the Napoleonic and American Civil Wars!

Forget complex rules and endless games. Le Temps des Braves offers a skirmish game experience that is simple, intuitive, and incredibly dynamic. Take command of a team of 13 brave soldiers and engage in thrilling skirmishes where every decision counts.

The key to victory? Tactics! The realistic reloading system adds a unique strategic dimension. Anticipate, plan your moves, and exploit the enemy's weaknesses to triumph.

But that's not all!

Varied scenarios: Diverse missions for ever-renewed challenges.
Troop progression: Advance your soldiers through the games, unlock new skills, and forge a legend!
Le Temps des Braves: More than a game, a historical adventure at your fingertips. Prepare to experience intense moments and write your own chapter of history!

Why choose Le Temps des Braves?

     Easy-to-learn rules, difficult to master.
     Fast-paced and exciting games.
     Total immersion in the heart of the conflicts.
     Infinite replayability thanks to character progression and numerous scenarios.
     Don't wait any longer, join Le Temps des Braves, and become a legendary tactician!


—description from the publisher

An abstracted, strategic game at army and fleet level from July 1940 to Summer 1944, from The Libya/Egypt Wire to Rome.
Sudden death, if the Allies fall seriously behind schedule or take Rome sooner.
3 turns per year for 13 turns. Simple air-sea-land combat mechanics consuming supply counters transported to the front by sea and then land. Both Axis and Allied convoys, Italian In late 1941, the U-boats arrived joining British and Italian submarines, and Italian MTBs (motor torpedo boats) and Chariot sub-marine sabotage vehicles. Chances to paradrop on both Crete and Malta.
Force H from Gibraltar, Mediterranean Fleet from Alexandria, 2 Italian fleet units based in Taranto or wherever.
US Task Force 34 with Massachusetts.
Allied warship task forces have carrier strike capability.
Desert Air Force, USAAFME, US 15th Air Force, Regia Aeronautica and a couple Luftflotte 2 units, sometimes.
Gen. O'Connor, Adm. Cunningham, and Rommel have special, if risk-taking, powers.
Plays in 90-120 minutes.
Unpredictable enough to have solitaire potential.
Could be 2 players each side - one commanding land and air the other naval (and naval air) assets.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Cæris is a skirmish-style miniatures game for 2 to 4 players, in which each player takes control of a warband of 5 warriors battling for city domination.
Designed to be simple, intuitive, fast-paced, and highly tactical, Cæris relies on innovative yet familiar gameplay mechanics to satisfy all types of players.

Cæris also features a unique medieval fantasy world. 
Here, there are no Orcs, Elves, or Trolls, but magic is nonetheless very much present.
Shapeshifters, Ewins, Familiars, and Changelings will cross your path.
Each guild offers a very different gameplay from the other.

Scenarios are created by randomly drawing 3 cards, and apply to both players.

A game of Cæris plays in 3 turns each divided in 5 phases.

Finally, Cæris offers a range of miniatures. High-quality resin sculptures and casts are available for those who love to paint.

Tiny Tactix is a fast and accessible tactical skirmish wargame for two players. Each player commands a small team of specialized soldiers and engages in intense clashes on very compact battlefields.

The game uses unconventional and minimalist components: beer coasters form the battlefield, while individual soldiers are represented by bottle caps. This makes the game highly portable, quick to set up, and focused primarily on tactical decision-making.

Tiny Tactix can be played purely as a tabletop game or with an optional companion app that can expand the overall experience. With its pace and emphasis on short, high-intensity firefights, the game is strongly inspired by iconic tactical video games such as Call of Duty and Counter-Strike.

—description from the designer

In each round, the group chooses one friend to play the role of the irresistible single that everyone wants to date (they don’t have to be single in real life).

The rest of the players create potential suitors using cards filled with red flags — but don’t worry, there are also green flags and random traits to keep things balanced and unpredictable.

Each suitor must then introduce their character using the cards they’ve drawn, role-playing with as much creativity and attitude as possible to win over the single.

At the end of the round, the single player decides:

who they would go on a date with,

who they would absolutely avoid,

and who delivered the best roleplay performance.

Detective Charlie needs your help again to investigate the case!

A strange letter came up on Detective Charlie’s desk: someone is threatening the long-awaited concert!
Team up with her to prevent this malicious person to ruin the show.

Meet the people on the island and interrogate them to learn more about what happens here and what everyone is up to.

Cooperate to cross-check the clues, draw the right conclusions and put an end to the culprit’s mischievous plan!

An immersive cooperative deduction game with colorful illustrations and easy-to-handle components.

—description from the publisher

Challenge your music knowledge with Lyrical Genius, the ultimate trivia game for song lovers. Featuring 100 cards packed with lyrical clues, artist facts, and musical hints. 
2+ players with a playtime of 25+ minutes, Lyrical Genius is perfect for friendly competitions, parties, or game nights with fellow music enthusiasts.

How to play:
1. Gather 2 or more players. 
2. Shuffle the 100 trivia cards. 
3. The first player draws a card and reads the lyrical clue, artist fact, or musical hint aloud. 
4. Other players take turns guessing the song title and artist. 
5. The first player to correctly guess both the song title and artist earns a point. 
6. The game continues with the next player drawing a card. 
7. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Whether you're an experienced golfer or a complete beginner, enjoy a round with friends, family, and acquaintances... anytime, in any weather.
Whether it's 9, 18, or even 36 holes – you decide the length of the round and the game...
Take the golf course home with you and discover the magic of Golfville within your own four walls... The clubhouse guarantees a cozy get-together after your round...

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Ganz gleich, ob Sie bereits Erfahrung im Golfspielen haben oder auch nicht, gehen Sie mit Freunden, Familie und Bekannten auf die Runde... jederzeit und bei jedem Wetter.
Ob 9, 18 oder vielleicht sogar 36 Loch - die Länge der Runde und des Spiels entscheiden Sie...
Nehmen Sie sich den Golfplatz mit nach Hause und entdecken Sie die Faszination von Golfville in den eigenen vier Wänden...
Das heimische Clubhaus garantiert auch nach der Runde ein gemütliches Beisammensein...

—description from the back of the box

On the board, the players will stand.
Each one in a corner with a different color from the others, and each player must place their heart at the farthest point from the BIIIR.
which is located at the center of the board.

First step: the player must determine the direction of the light, meaning they throw the dice, which is the light cube.

After that, they randomly draw a dark monster from the bag and place it next to the BIIIR in the direction of the light.

Immediately after, they must draw a card which has an effect depending on the type of card drawn.
Among these effects is placing barriers that help block the dark monsters so they don’t reach the player’s personal area and cut their heart.

Game rule: the player who stays alive until the end is the one who wins the game.

—description from the publisher

The kitchen is in a frenzy! Can you keep up with the pace? In this game of observation and speed, there’s no waiting around: everyone plays at the same time. Be the quickest to play your ingredients, empty your deck, and become the ultimate Maestro!

Why You’ll Love It:
NON-STOP SPEED CHALLENGE: Forget waiting for your turn! All players act simultaneously to empty their draw piles as fast as possible.

TWO EVOLVING GAME MODES: Discover two "recipes" for play. Start with Margherita to master the basics, then move up to Romana, adding special cards (Scooter, Truck, Chef) for an extra kick of spice.

THE "CAT" JOKER TO SHAKE THINGS UP: Did a Cat card appear? It’s your wildcard! Shout "CAT!" and cover any pile to seize the advantage.

INTERACTIVE & STRATEGIC: Don’t just stare at your own cards. Keep an eye on the 12 piles on the table—feel free to play over your opponents' piles to slow them down!

FUN FOR THE WHOLE FAMILY: An incredibly accessible game for ages 6 and up, perfect for quick and lively rounds.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: King of the Middle East: Before the Armageddon
description:
It is 1950. World War II is over, but its memory still lingers. The great European empires are withdrawing their remaining troops from the Middle East, leaving behind a power vacuum. In their place, new leaders have clawed their way to power, each ruling over an emerging empire as military tensions once again begin to rise. Now it’s your turn to rise, wield your power, and claim victory in a game where only the strong survive.

In King of the Middle East, each player takes control of a country in the Middle East. A game starter is chosen, and players take turns in clockwise order after the previous player has completed their turn.

The objective is to conquer cities controlled by your opponents while defending your own. The game ends when one player controls all six capital cities and declares total victory.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Top Toopy: The one-of-a-kind family skill game—fun, acrobatic, and all about spinning tops!

ADAPTIVE & ACCESSIBLE: With 3 difficulty levels, everyone can join the fun. Whether you’re a total beginner or a seasoned pro, there’s always a challenge perfectly suited to your skill level.

40 CHALLENGES TO CONQUER: From basic moves to spectacular stunts, progress step-by-step through a wide variety of 40 different challenges!

BUILD TEAM SKILLS: Sharpen your fine motor skills, master synchronization with your teammates, and practice patience—all while having a blast. Top Toopy is the perfect blend of a party game and a game of skill.

ONLINE HELP: Stuck on a trick? Access our video channel to learn master techniques and blow your friends and family away.

With Top Toopy, defy the laws of gravity, push your limits, and lead your team to victory!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Codenames: Pictures – Cute Critters Expansion Pack
description:
Flip through a frog family photo album, tapdance with a tardigrade, or join a bee family dinner—and do all that without leaving your gaming table with the Cute Critters picture pack.

Codenames: Cute Critters Expansion Pack is a playful pack of 40 adorable animal-themed images. Simply mix the new cards into your base game of Codenames: Pictures and let these Cute Critters run wild around the main set. Or, if you wish, you can lay out a full 20-card grid with the new cards and play a game of Codenames: Pictures only with Cute Critters.

—description from the publisher

Do you dare attempt the ultimate heist? In The Safe, you and your team face the greatest challenge of your careers: cracking five heavily secured safes. But there is a catch, communication is strictly forbidden. Without speaking a single word, players must cooperate perfectly to uncover the hidden codes.

—description from the publisher

Once upon a time, Red Riding Hood and Captain Hook wandered into Oz… Find out how the story continues in the Codenames Fairy Tales pack!

Codenames: Fairy Tales Expansion Pack features a thematic set of 50 words, carefully chosen to spice up your game of Codenames or Codenames: Duet. Simply mix the new cards into the basic set of words, randomly or in whatever ratio you like, or even play two full games using only the 25 cards in this pack.

Aside from new words, the pack also includes 3 new agent tiles (2 for Codenames and 1 for Duet), and 4 fairy-tale-themed pictures for Codenames: Pictures to customize your games even more.

Codenames Expansion Packs are bite-sized mini expansions that let you customize your Codenames experience with new thematic words or images. A copy of Codenames or Codenames: Duet is required to play this Expansion Pack.

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
In Hop ’n’ Roll, every jump counts! Skilfully place your figure next to a dice and write down the number on your playsheet. There are different coloured areas – each colour has different rules! With up to 6 players, you can experience an exciting mix of luck and tactics.
Two different playsheets guarantee variety.

—description from the publisher

What happens after a mad scientist rides a sandworm into an asteroid belt? Explore the universe of possible outcomes in the Codenames Sci-Fi Expansion Pack!

Codenames: Sci-Fi Expansion Pack features a themed set of 50 words, carefully chosen to spice up your game of Codenames or Codenames: Duet. Simply mix the new cards into the basic set of words, randomly or in whatever ratio you like, or even play two full games using only the 25 cards in this pack.

Aside from new words, the pack also includes 3 new agent tiles (2 for Codenames and 1 for Duet), and and 4 sci-fi-themed pictures for Codenames: Pictures to customize your games even more.

Codenames Expansion Packs are bite-sized mini expansions that let you customize your Codenames experience with new thematic words or images. A copy of Codenames or Codenames: Duet is required to play this Expansion Pack.

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

boardgame: VITA: Born in 1902 – Dawn of Hollywood
description:
In VITA - Born in 1902 - Dawn of Hollywood players explore the life of characters in a historical period.

Your life begins at the dawn of the American century. Fight your way into the glorious film industry, make a name for yourself as a gangster in turbulent times, or fulfill the American dream in the City of Angels in your own unique way.

In this thematic strategy game, 2-4 players build up the biographies of their characters using events they obtain from a common "Tree of Life". The goal of the game is to gather the most happiness.

VITA is played throughout 3 Chapters: 
In the YOUTH, characters are customized by their first life events and obtain an Ambition - a goal for their life.

In the ADULTHOOD, characters compete with each other over valuable events and resources like wealth and fame.

In the OLD AGE, previously gathered score cards are scored for everyone together - every player tries to gain more bonuses than the others.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Winter Offensive Bonus Pack #17: ASL Scenario Pack for Winter Offensive 2026
description:
ASL Winter Offensive Bonus Pack #17 (2026) features three new scenario designs by Pete Shelling and a new board (99).

-description from the publisher's website

Herbcraft is a fast-paced card game with an addictive mix of push-your-luck, deck-building and pick-and-pass that plays in just 25 minutes. Set in the same fantastical setting as Mercurial: Alchemia, players are floramancers, cultivating magical herbs while trying to avoid growing Blightweeds.

Each round, players simultaneously reveal Herb cards from their decks and stack them left-to-right, increasing the rewards they’ll gain from harvesting later. Card positioning matters, as do collecting sets of materials — but many Herbs grant powers that let players reorder cards or control what they play. With Blightweeds lurking in your deck, you must push your luck carefully, for revealing too much Blight forces you to discard half your card stack!

After harvesting, players gain victory points and Shells, then draft up to 3 new cards through pick-and-pass to further build their decks. Powerful Herb cards cost Shells to keep, so timing is important; do you spend early to buy that perfect card, but risk not being able to buy any more later?

With fully simultaneous turns, Herbcraft stays quick and exciting throughout. After six rounds, the player with the most victory points wins.

-description from publisher

Blockade is a tactical, team-based board game designed for 4 players.  Teams of 2 work together to advance their Counters towards the Border and fill in their Tower squares to claim victory.

Each player has a distinctive role on their team either building a formidable Offense or an impenetrable Defense.  The game combines clever tactics, timely dice rolls and shifting strategies, with players swapping roles during gameplay to adapt to the evolving battle.

Its Wit, Walls, and War!

—description from the designer

Become the talk of the town with your stunningly stylish outfits or your crazy looks to be the only boutique that gathers interest.

In this 2 player game, players will collect clothing items by allocating material to the shared design rail. Place followers to completed outfits to score points. Game ends when a player has created 3 complete outfits. The player with the most points, is the winner.

—description from the designer

ACOR is a fantasy-themed game in which we will be an adventurer in search of the magical gems hidden in the forest of ACOR.

ACOR is a tile placement and test solving game, where your ability to place the tiles correctly will be as important as your luck with the dice to win the biggest amount of gems and rise to victory.

On their turn, players must choose a tile from the crossroads (market), place it in the forest they will create together, trying to collect as many gems as possible, and move their adventurer along the paths to the placed tile, overcoming the challenges they will encounter along the way by rolling dice.

The final round will begin when the crossroads can no longer be replenished. In the final round, players must place the city tile they received at the beginning of the game, which presented them with an individual mission.

The player who accumulates the most points from the value of collected gems, found equipment pieces, and the individual mission will win the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Say It First: After Dark Extra Cards
description: Introducing the After Dark Extra Cards for "Say It First," designed to spice up your game nights with adult-themed prompts. Perfect for those looking to add a twist of humor and intrigue to their gatherings.

boardgame: Say It First: Facts Extra Cards
description: Enhance your "Say It First" experience with the Facts Extra Cards expansion. These cards introduce exciting new prompts that spark creativity and laughter, making your game night unforgettable.

Panoply is a two-player tactical wargame of positioning, bluffing, and decisive duels, set against a medieval battlefield.

Players command opposing armies fighting for control of key positions on a shared grid. While movement and positioning are fully open and deterministic, combat is resolved through simultaneous card play, introducing hidden information, psychological tension, and calculated risk.

Victory is achieved by controlling all control posts for the duration of one round. Holding enough of these positions rewards players with additional cards, creating momentum shifts and forcing difficult choices between offensive and defensive play.

—description from the publisher

Build the city of the future as it grows from a small town to a big city.

The game is played over a variable number of rounds, determined by when the Achievements are claimed.

Players use policy cards to generate resources, plan urban growth, build infrastructure, and attract residents. Each round, players will draw, prioritize, and execute cards from policy cards. These cards can also be turned into laws that improve your action efficiency and boost your future actions.  Policy cards and zoning tiles are purchased from a shared market. Blueprint tiles allow players to expand their cities, which may affect their short and long term goals. Whenever players expand their cities, they will be attracting residents. Burgeoning cities generate resources, but also create new management puzzles.

—description from the publisher

PolyNimaux is a children's board game where players become farmers and arrange their own farmyards by placing various animals. The main goal is to create the happiest and most well-organized farm by correctly positioning all the animals, which you'll do using wooden stencils with paw prints of the farm's inhabitants.

Depending on the chosen difficulty level, take the required sheets from the notepad—this is your farm game board.
The game consists of 8 rounds. At the start of a round, the youngest player reveals a task card and takes the stencil shown on it. The player to their left takes the stencil with the next sequential number, and so on, until each player has one stencil.
All players simultaneously place their stencils on their boards, following the placement rules, and color in the animal paw prints. The paw prints will earn you victory points if they are located in specific areas. For example, a hoof print scores points if drawn in a pen, a goose footprint must be in the pond, and a chicken track in spaces next to the chicken coop. Therefore, it's important to choose the most advantageous spots for the prints.
After drawing the prints, players return the stencils to the center of the table, and a new round begins.

At the end of the game, tally the victory points. The player with the most points is declared the winner and the best farmer.

At more advanced difficulty levels, additional conditions and rules are added, requiring even more careful planning of your actions.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Please Don't Burn My Village!: Trash To Treasure Promo Card
description:
Card text for TRASH TO TREASURE
"When this card is added to a Bribe, draw the top 2 cards from the Discard pile.
Choose 1 to add to your hand. Return the other to the Discard pile.
At the end of the game, score this card as Wild."

This promo card was distributed to local game stores. It compliments the main game. 
In Please Don't Burn My Village!, which is set in the world of Castle Panic, you want to bribe with the right treasure at the right time to keep the dragon's attention — while buying treasures at the black market and cursing the other villagers' treasures. When no treasure remains, the dragon will burn all of the villages except one. Will yours be the one that survives?

Tricksy Rabbits is a trick taking game with no bidding featuring tricksy rabbits that can help you score points or cost you points.  This is a must follow, may trump game where players follow the suit of the lead card and the highest numbered card of that suit wins the trick and two points.  However, tricksy rabbits can be played at any time and a trick won with a tricksy rabbit scores the sum of the cards!

Each player starts with a -3 and a -5 tricksy rabbit so playing them at the right time matters.  If there is more than one tricksy rabbit in a trick, the last tricksy rabbit played wins the trick.  Play one round per player and the highest score wins!  Tricksy rabbits is a great way to introduce people to the trick taking mechanism.  It also has a surprising amount of depth and strategy for such a welcoming game.

-description from designer

boardgame: Este no es un juego de trivia
description:
A tricky trivia game where winning depends on your speed and wit.
Each round everyone draws card, and choose from several questions (with their answers) on it. Then each player writes down the possible answers for everyone to see, and starts reading the question aloud. The first person to touch the correct answer wins the turn, and is one point closer to winning the match.
Beware! The questions are designed with tricks and deceptions to confuse you.

---

Un juego de trivia lleno de trampas donde ganar depende de tu rapidez.
Sacá una carta de pregunta, elegí una y escribí las posibles respuestas para que todos las vean.
Leé la pregunta en voz alta: el primero en tocar la respuesta correcta se lleva la ronda.
¡Atención! Las preguntas están diseñadas con trucos y engaños para confundirte.
Un desafío familiar que combina conocimiento, sentido común y velocidad mental.

Complementary Produce is a pocket-sized spin on a classic trick-taking game.

Each player is dealt 5 cards. Play one or multiple cards face up in front of you and have the highest shown number to win the hand. Cards can be combined to create a higher number if they are the same color or complementary colors. win a round by having won the most hands or tying for the most hands won. Play to a mutually agreed upon final score.

Easy to learn, but hard to master. Know when to combine your cards or risk running out of cards while others still have more to play.

For children, the cards can be used for early learning like numbers, colors, produce, etc. As children grow, they can learn matching exercises, sorting things by category, addition, subtraction, or complementary colors.

—description from the designer

The Father's Day edition of the cult game STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN. The ultimate game pad with 30 creative and fun categories for unforgettable hours with fathers and the whole family. Each player receives one page of the A4 pad and places it in front of them. A time limit is set for each round (we recommend 2 minutes). The innovative letter bar helps to determine the letter. Then you have to enter all the terms as quickly as possible. There are 8 terms to find per round.

—description from the publisher

Die Papa-Edition des Kultspiels STADT LAND VOLLPFOSTEN rund um den Vatertag. Der ultimative Spielblock mit 30 kreativen und lustigen Kategorien für unvergessliche Stunden mit Vätern und der gesamten Familie. Jeder Spieler erhält eine Seite des DIN-A4 Blockes und legt diese vor sich hin. Es wird eine Zeit pro Durchgang ausgemacht (wir empfehlen 2 Minuten als Begrenzung). Die innovative Buchstabenleiste hilft bei der Ermittlung des Buchstabens. Dann gilt es so schnell wie möglich alle Begriffe einzutragen. Pro Runde sind 8 Begriffe gesucht.

—description from the publisher (German)

boardgame: Star Trek: Into the Unknown – Glory and Zeal Expansion
description:
Recruit new officers and requisition unique equipment for your task forces, and deploy detailed ships to help you attain your goals. Take on a new campaign arc and incident missions designed to test each faction’s political strategy, exploratory innovation, and resolve to fight!

The Cardassian Union is eager to work for either side, as long as they further their own agendas. With new officers, titles, directives, and equipment, this exciting new faction offers a unique way to play!

The Klingon Empire is bolstered by new named officers, titles, and equipment–providing several new tactical assets for each mission.

As for new starships, this expansion brings the Negh’Var-class, a powerful flagship, to the Klingons, solidifying them as a standalone faction in the quadrant. The Cardassians present a new and uncertain threat, as they deploy Galor-class cruisers and Groumall Type military freighters to the front lines. These ships provide new and varied assets to deploy either as mercenaries or as their own faction in the game.

—description from the publisher

The players divide into two teams. Each team receives the same number of playing pieces, which must also be distributed equally within the team. The number of pieces for each team can range from 10 to 16. Taking more or fewer is not advisable. One team receives red pieces and plays from point A, the other team receives blue pieces and plays from point B. In addition to these blue and red playing pieces, a single piece of a different color, the target piece, is needed.

After the order within each team is determined by lot, player #1 of the red team starts. In subsequent games, player #1 of the team that just lost starts. Player #1 throws the target piece first. They roll 5 dice and move the target piece forward on the center track the number of spaces equal to the total number rolled. However, if this roll totals less than 9, player #1 of the opposing team tries to place the target piece, and so on, alternating until a roll of at least 9 successfully places the target piece.

Player #1 of the red team then rolls three dice and moves their playing piece forward the number of spaces equal to the total number rolled, this time onto one of the two side lanes. Player #1 of the opposing team then rolls and moves their piece in the same way, followed by player #2 of the first team, and so on, alternating until all pieces have been placed. Only the side lanes are occupied.

However, if a player lands on the spaces occupied by the goal, they place their piece on the center lane in place of the goal, roll another die, and move the goal forward the number of spaces equal to the number rolled. This is usually disadvantageous for their team, as they are essentially pushing the goal towards the opposing team.

If a player rolls a number that is already occupied on both equal spaces on the two side lanes, they remove a playing piece from one of these spaces, place their own piece on that space, and move the piece previously occupying that space to the nearest available space. But always on the same track! The player is free to choose which stone they want to dislodge; they can move a stone of their own team more favorably or place an opponent's stone less favorably.

If a player moves a stone onto a space that also contains the goal, this displaced stone also moves to the center track in place of the goal. The owner of the stone, or someone from the same team, then rolls a die and moves the goal forward (towards the opponent) by the number of spaces shown. In this way, a player can dislodge an opponent's stone and force the opposing team to move the goal to their advantage.

Once all stones have been placed, the stones on the eight spaces surrounding the goal are counted. The team with the most stones on these eight spaces receives points equal to the excess of stones. The spaces on the center track are included in the count, and any stones on these spaces count double. If these 8 spaces are evenly occupied by both sides, another 6 spaces are added and counted. If there is still an even score, the game remains a draw.

Games are played until one side has 24 (or a predetermined number of) marks. That side then wins. If the game continues, the sides must be reassigned.

—user summary

This is a very interesting game, because it’s a platoon-sized infantry game that requires no measuring tape and has no fixed round structure.

Instead the game relies on an action/reaction system, where players keep activating their units until something happens that ends their turn, such as missing shots, having their units suppressed, or failing a morale test.

As an infantry game focussed on rifle and mg squads all units are always assumed to be in range, and movement is done in a straight line from cover to cover, so no measuring is required at all, making for a super fast experience.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
To Defy a King are tabletop war games rules for the English Civil War. The Rules are designed to give a flavour of the period whilst giving a quick and easy game. Games can be played with anything from a few units up to a full scale battle lasting all day. The rules have been designed with games lasting 3 to 4 hours using around 10 units per side.

There is a unique game set up using unit cards and army lists that use cards for each year of the war. The main unit within these rules is the regiment and each regiment is made up of a number of stands. Units can be as small as 2 stands going up to as large as 10 stands. All dice required in these rules are D6.

Included in the rulebook are the rules, army lists along with a battle report. You are also provided with a fast sheet and 10 A4 cards that provide the unit cards that make up all of the army lists.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Texas Bingo is a strategic bingo game inspired by Texas Hold’em poker.
Players simultaneously place revealed cards onto a shared 5×5 grid, competing across twelve distinct scoring lines—rows, columns, and diagonals—to build the strongest possible poker hands.
With hidden information, event-driven twists, and optional mission objectives, Texas Bingo blends familiar poker logic with the tension of spatial puzzle play.

-description from publisher

Who will be the first to reach the 26th floor to enjoy the breathtaking view?
The goal oh the game is to be the first to reach the rooftop using your elevator, but watch out for those who try to stop you!
The cards will allow you to move your elevator or the opponent’s elevators up or down, and move your token on your card with the elevator buttons to see which floor you are on!
 A fun, fast-paced, unpredictable game with a bit of strategy!
The Rooftop, the elevator game that raises the stakes!

A set of free skirmish rules compatible with any modern figures (soldiers, gangsters, what have you). The rules are extremely simple an fit on a single page, with a second for weapons data etc.

—user summary
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: The Monster Mask: A Four Against Darkness Adventure
description:
You are walking on a forest path. It is late afternoon, and you plan to set up camp soon, when a girl in a lilac gown, her long brown hair flowing and adorned with a ribbon, appears on your path. Her arms and legs bear deep scratches, and she is breathing heavily. In her hands, she holds a monstrous mask made of thick red paper. As she sees you, she halts, her eyes filled with hope. She reaches out, asking for help. “Please, help me,” she pleads. “My family is trapped in our old mansion. Our garden is infested with dangerous plants and creatures.”

—description from the publisher

Get ready for the world’s most ridiculous debates!

In Pigeon for President, players go head-to-head in hilarious showdowns where outrageous characters face off in even crazier scenarios. Each round, players pick two characters from their hand to answer a bizarre question, then vote until one champion remains. Will your pick survive the chaos of Dictatorship (one Judge rules all) or the unpredictability of Democracy (everyone votes)? Who would make the worst therapist—Batman or a potato? Which celebrity would start the strangest cult? You’ll argue, defend, and laugh your way through every absurd matchup as you battle for the pigeon’s ultimate respect.

Includes 250 character cards, 50 double-sided question cards (100 total), 12 voting tokens, 1 judge token, and rules.

boardgame: A Mushroom Mystery: An Adventure for Four Against Darkness
description:
This zine is a Four Against Darkness adventure for characters of all levels, written and Illustrated by Andrea Sfiligoi. You have been hired by the village of Cadvion to hunt down a Matanagar, an ogre-fungus that has been ravaging their crops and eating their cattle. But soon you will realize this is not just another monster hunt.

—description from the publisher

Brute Fort is an 18 card dungeon delving game for a single player. It takes 10-15 minutes to play.

In this game, your courageous (or foolish) heroes venture into the shadowy recesses of the fort. Inside they'll discover a jumble of fearsome foes and alluring loot. Over the course of play you'll place cards from your hand to build up the explored rooms of the cursed building.

Brute Fort takes the familiar format of card duelling games, like Magic The Gathering and Marvel Snap, and turns it into a lightweight solo experience. Instead of battling against an opponent, this game sees the player battling the eponymous fort itself, racing to reach the top and come out on top.

—description from the designer

Thunder Road: Ignition is a standalone game that uses the core game system from Thunder Road: Vendetta with tweaks to provide a familiar yet new, streamlined play experience.

Ignition features shorter road boards, new rubble terrain, barricades, and curved roads!

Ignition can also be used as an expansion to Thunder Road: Vendetta.

Stack The Deck™ is a party card game where players are dealt five face up cards numbered 1-10 and different colors.  Players arrange cards, in no particular order, in a row of five stacks in front of them.  Play begins with the first player drawing two cards from a draw pile (there is no discard pile).

The goal is to complete these stacks by creating either pairs (same or different color) and 3-card RUNS (same or different colors).  Once a stack is completed it is turned over to lock in a score & prevent stealing.  The first player to complete and turn over all their stacks wins the round and is first to tally their points.  The player with the highest score after ten rounds wins the game.

After a player draws their two cards, one of them must be played on one of their stacks first, if possible.  If one of the cards can be added to their own stack then they have the option to use the second card to steal any other players unturned stack by creating a pair with one of their cards OR completing one of their 2-card runs with the 3rd card.  Once a stack is stolen, it is handed over to the stealing player and it now adds to their end of round score.  If one or both drawn cards are unplayable, they are turned back over and inserted strategically back into the draw pile, to Stack The Deck.  Stack cards needed by other players deeper into the deck.  That is where Stack The Deck™ got its highly recognizable name.

There are WILD cards which can be substituted for any card # or color.  Smart use of these cards is one key to winning.  And stacks which include a WILD card are safe and protected from being stolen.  And a player cannot steal another players stack using a drawn WILD card.  WILDS can only be played in the player's stacks in front of them.

Once a player successfully turns over and locks in all their stacks a round ends for everyone.  Points are totaled:  5 Points for a different color pair, 10 Points for a same color pair, 10 points for a different color 3-card run and 20 points for a same color run.  If a player goes out and leaves other players with unturned stacks they are penalized -5 points for each unturned stack and -20 points for any unturned WILD card(s).

For a game that is simple to learn, there is a great balance of strategy and luck. And since all cards are visible during game play, all players are watching & anticipating what cards are drawn, how they will be played and if they will be victim of a steal.

Piquet is a pirate-themed board game that combines poker and strategic board play for 3–5 players. Play your cards right to win pots and spend wisely to outrun your opponents to the center island!

The objective of the game is to sail your ship to a center island on the board. You do so by gathering resources giving you the ability to sail and buy other items like cards. You win those resources by playing poker.

The poker style is standard Texas Hold'em with a few simple rule changes. Player win chips and gems in poker and use them to buy cards and advancement spots on the board.

Neutral is an easy-to-learn card game about climate with engaging gameplay. Mechanics work independently of the climate theme. It is enjoyable by players of all ages.

Players must gradually reduce their carbon footprint (sum of the cards in their play area). Values of the cards are their daily greenhouse gas emissions from food, transport, heating, shopping and travel. They range from 0 (Low) to 10. The emissions were calculated for France. Gameplay hinges on habit cards and temporary cards. Tentations cards let gamers sabotage other players.

At the beginning of the game, you are a major big emitter. Your emissions from food, transport and heating are 30 kg.CO2.eq per day (sum of the cards in front of you). Players start the game with three "10 cards" and four "Earth cards" in their play area. They have a hand of 7 cards.

One round of the game is one year. Adapt your lifestyle every year. Play 2 cards (one color on one color) in your play area or on the discard pile. If you go over the maximum limit of the round, you will lose an "Earth card". During the game, the objective of the round decreases (from 25 to 5). If you lose all your "Earth cards", you are eliminated. Manage to spend a carbon-neutral year to win the game. If no one reaches carbon neutrality, the last player is the winner.

—description from the designer

Selene – The Fantasy is a Living Card Battle Arena (a card and miniatures game played on a board for combat), set in arena-style battles where players face off against each other.

The initial game mode is tactical competitive PvP (Player vs Player), supporting up to 3v3 matches. It features RPG elements, strategic depth, and highly dynamic combat with simple and very fun mechanics.

Which characters from Greek, Egyptian, and Norse mythology will you be lucky enough to draw?

Will you be able to combine them with your special cards to win as many duels as possible?

Arm yourself with a little luck, a lot of strategy, and some knowledge, and emerge victorious from the battle of heroes!

—description from the back of the box (translated)
Diga 5 puts your vocabulary to the test is here! Can you name 5 African countries, or 5 child actors? Be quick and answer before time runs out. Gather your team and get ready for the challenge.

Train your musicians to play the master pieces of classical music and attract spectator to listen to them.

Each turn all the players will choose their actions simultaneously on a partition and using musical notes.

Your actions can be :

     train a musician in the music academy for one of the three families of instruments (Percussions, Strings and Winds) ; each musician can progress on three levels and win each time a bonus
     Learn a new piece of classical music to add it in your collection ; each piece is represented by a card with a unique illustration
     Sell ticket to spectators to make them listen the concert and applaud for you
     Make an audition to transfer your musicians from the conservatory to the orchestra ; the more different pieces you play during your audition the better place you will get in this orchestra.


At the end of the games each musician in the orchestra and each spectators will earn you applauses and the player with the most applauses win the game.

—description from the designer

Can you tell the difference between a quesadilla and a hamburger? In this little game you try to recognize different foods from around the world. Each card has a picture of a famous food, a question about that food and a short fact about it. No-one needs to wait for their turn as everyone is answering the questions at the same time. This handy-sized game contains 50 cards and fits both a suitcase and a game night!

—description from the publisher
family: Series: Of the World (Tactic small square box)

description:
The gods of Olympus continue their age-old argument. Zeus, Poseidon, Hades, and Thetis each claim their mortal child (demigod) is the most glorious of the Greek heroes. To settle the dispute, they summon their heirs to a divine trial of strength, cunning, and fate. Only one Hero will ascend to a seat among the Gods on Mt. Olympus.

Heirs to Olympus is a tactical skirmish game that places 2–4 players in a divine trial of Greek legends. Players take on the role of Hercules, Theseus, Macaria, or Achilles, pitted in a match to prove which of them is worthy of ascending to Mt. Olympus.  Lead your Hero, Companion, and four Acolytes across a shifting battlefield as you race to defend your sacred Relic while breaking that of your rival(s).

Each round you’ll allocate movement, play action cards, apply special abilities, and time decisive attacks. The Phoenix Rising System keeps the action fast and relentless: fallen units return through a reinforcement queue, positioning and timing matter intensely, and every clash is shaped by dice, powers, and faction-specific Action Cards. Unpredictable twists can also emerge from Pandora’s Box, adding moments of divine chaos to the trial.
