Only Acolytes can damage Relics, forcing bold advances and desperate defenses. And when the game enters Sudden Death, defeats become permanent—and one well-timed breakthrough can decide Olympus itself. A mythic contest of asymmetric heroes, decisive battles, and legendary stakes.

—description from the publisher

Dragon Ships & Iron Knight is set of three, two-player, tactical games covering the battles of 1066 that shaped the future for centuries to come.  The game includes:

The Battle of Hastings
The Battle of Stamford Bridge
The Battle of Gate Fulford

Each battle includes its own

17" x 22" map
Individual rulebook (all battles use the same core rule system)
Set of unit counters/markers
Player aids

—description from the publisher

Extinction Point is a thrilling cooperative card game that uniquely blends the classic strategy of trick-taking with the desperate creativity of base-building.

You and your fellow leaders must win tricks to take actions - using the very cards you played in the tricks to build structures, recruit people, and create weapons and shields to survive against the Mothership's attacks, all while outsmarting an Alien Infiltrator sent to sabotage your plans!

Every trick is a tense decision. Every round brings you closer to victory... Or extinction!

—description from the designer

In Fábrica de Heróis - O Cardgame, various superheroes must face each other and overcome challenges, whether saving people, preventing accidents, or fighting super villains, to discover which is the best team in the city of Nova Capital. For 1 to 6 players aged 14 and up, in this card game you must assemble a team of superheroes to complete your missions. Each mission is worth points, and whoever reaches the highest score first wins. But be careful! Other players will do everything they can to stop you.

Players place bets using chips on poker hands from a standard deck of cards with jokers.  The player to collect the highest point values in chips is the winner.  Another game in the  "According to Hoyle..." series.

Project Risk teaches project management skills with three different ways to play!  Facilitated, Individual or Virtual.  Players run business projects and assess risks.  Players share and transfer risks by negotiating and reporting on their status depending on their pawn position in the game.  The game ends at the end of 12 reporting periods or if the last competitor goes out-whichever comes first.

In Busão, players take on the role of bus drivers managing crowded buses full of passengers. Your mission? Drive through the streets, pick up passengers at stops and along the way, and transport them while completing your route with good passengers and avoiding chaos and discomfort on board.

Each round, you must manage passenger boarding and deal with unpredictable situations like fights, confusion, and the dreaded "squeeze" during the trip. Each card represents a passenger with unique characteristics, preferences, and demands. Use your strategy to ensure harmony on the bus and optimize your transport. But be careful! If tensions rise or the bus gets overcrowded, passengers may disembark early.

A strip down real estate trading game featuring exotic properties you may buy, sell, trade or rent.  Landlords may take rent money out in trade or impose pleasant penalties.  A real estate trading game where the winner wins a fortune or even the shirt off your back.

boardgame: Castelo Rá-Tim-Bum: A Aventura do Ratinho
description:
Castelo Rá-Tim-Bum: A Aventura do Ratinho is a cooperative board game for all ages. This time, Mau has played another prank and borrowed objects from the Castle's residents, hiding them away. Several characters are missing their favorite belongings: Pedro lost his top hat, Dr. Vitor is without his umbrella, and Morgana needs her favorite broom back.

Only Ratinho can navigate through the pipes and recover the items Mau scattered along the way. The pipes shift position and direction, seeming to have a mind of their own, and end in narrow passages with different exits. With his incredible shape-shifting ability, Ratinho can squeeze through the pipe connections and traverse the winding paths.

Abracadabra is a magical and fun card game where you and your friends compete to see who can pull the most rabbits out of the hat! But be careful, the rabbits have personalities and can either help or hinder your trick. Combine strategy, memory, and a touch of luck to get rid of rabbits and accumulate the lowest score. When you think you have the advantage, shout "Abracadabra!" and challenge your opponents - but do you really have the fewest rabbits?

Ask a question, pick your answer, and see who really gets you!
Be amazed by what you don't know about your friends & family, and what they DO know about you!
Get teens and adults talking about themselves with hundreds of cool questions
3 different ways to play
Mix & match cards from different packs
Play for fun or to win! A simple card game for 2-8 players, all ages 14+

boardgame: Primal: The Awakening – Woltyar and Frozen Wastes
description:
Face the heat of volcanic slopes and the frost of the northern wastes. This expansion includes a double-sided neoprene playmat that represents two of Thyrea’s biomes, adding both visual variety and unique gameplay elements.

The Woltyar biome causes the players to deal with rising temperatures, causing them to discard an increasing number of cards from the top of their deck as the battle wages on. You must also be careful while moving, as the rivers of lava can cause burning!

The Frozen Wastes saps the players precision and focus. At various rounds all mastery cards are considered blank, and from round 8 on this becomes permanent.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Primal: The Awakening – Crystal Caves and Flooded Wilds
description:
Battle in crystal caverns where shards rain from above, or in the tide-shifting Riverlands where deep-water terrain reshapes your strategy. This expansion includes a double-sided neoprene playmat that represents two of Thyrea’s biomes, adding both visual variety and unique gameplay elements.

In the Crystal Caves, glowing crystals fall from the ceiling, scattering across the battlefield. Their energy resonates with the Hunters, making it easier to play cards of the matching color. But the cave is collapsing, and only a few rounds remain before it all comes down.

In the Flooded Wilds, the riverlands are submerged by the rising tide. As the waters swell and recede, Hunters must adapt to unstable footing. This biome introduces the Deep Water terrain, which reduces your hand-size limit and pushes you to fight against the tide itself.

—description from the publisher

YAY! ALLOWANCE DAY has arrived. Money in your pocket, smile on your face... But what will happen to your money during the month? Will it be spent at the Shopping Center? Used to pay for Swimming School or that treehouse construction by Cupim Carpentry? Uh-oh, nothing will be left! But you can get a loan from the bank... But you have to pay interest! Maybe you'll win more money in the Sports Pool or the Rock Band Contest!

And when the end of the month comes, you'll have to pay all your bills and loans... How much money will be left? Find out in the most fun and exciting way with Jogo da Mesada! A challenge to see who can stretch their allowance best until the end of the month! Whoever has the most money left at the end of the month is the big winner!

Objective: Accumulate the largest pile of cards.

One player distributes 4 cards to each participant and turns 4 cards face up on the table. The first player must check if any card in hand matches any card on the table. If it matches, they take both cards and add them to their pile. If the card matches the top card of an opponent's pile, they can steal that pile, taking all the cards. If they don't have a matching card, they must discard one card face up in the center of the table.

When all players run out of cards in hand, 4 more cards are distributed to each, until the deck runs out. The game ends when there are no more cards to distribute, and the player with the largest pile wins.

The joker can be placed on top of a player's pile to protect it from being stolen, lasting until another card is placed on top.

boardgame: Primal: The Awakening – Endless Swamp and Nightmare
description:
Step into the toxic swamps of Thyrea or descend into the Nightmare Realm, where fear and truth merge into one. This expansion includes a double-sided neoprene playmat that represents two of Thyrea’s biomes, adding both visual variety and unique gameplay elements.

In the Endless Swamp, thick, toxic fumes rise from the stagnant waters, clouding your senses with every breath. Inhale its mists for too long, and the swamp will claim you as one of its own, dragging you down into its dark, suffocating depths.

In the Nightmare Realm, the most perilous and mysterious region of Thyrea is the Nightmare realm. Drawn by a haunting call toward the mountain’s peak, hunters march into the heart of fear itself, where truth and terror await. As the battle prolongs, players must choose to transform into monsters to restore their base damage, but lose the ability to perform or receive assists.

—description from the publisher

Sigilo is a cooperative word-guessing party game for teams. One player from each team gives clues while their teammates try to guess the target word in 1 minute.

The key rule is that the clue-giver cannot say any of the 5 forbidden words that the opposing team has chosen. Teams must make their clues difficult while earning points ranging from 1 to 5.

When all players run out of cards, 4 more cards are dealt until the deck runs out. The game ends when there are no more cards to distribute.

If you master the vocabulary, you can help your team guess the target word and score 10 points in the round.

Features over 1,800 words!

boardgame: Primal: The Awakening – Goldarks and Thunder Mountains
description:
Survive the fury of the Everlasting Storm or follow the Dragon’s Voice echoing through the golden mountains of the Goldarks. This expansion includes a double-sided neoprene playmat that represents two of Thyrea’s biomes, adding both visual variety and unique gameplay elements.

In the Goldarks, the Dragon’s Voice echoes through the golden valleys. As its power intensifies, the metallic veins running through the mountains begin to glow, while hunters and monsters are consumed by a shared hunger for blood and fury. Players and monsters suffer double damage in various rounds.

In the Thunder Mountains, bolts of lightning crash across the mountains, splitting the sky with blinding fury. Hunters must stay light on their feet, moving swiftly across the shifting ground to evade the relentless wrath of the Everlasting Storm.

—description from the publisher

Table games had been missing for some time, but now they are coming back, and with them the meeples, as everyone started believing in them again and playing table games. Help your meeple escape oblivion and return to Meepleland, the fantastic land of the game table.

In this game, each player has their own colored meeple, different from the others, and no one knows which color belongs to other players. Your meeple must advance along the paths towards "Meepleland." Using cards, players can control one, some, or all meeples, moving them along the path, swapping their positions, or turning them around, thus altering their positions on the paths.

The winner is the one who makes their meeple reach the end first.

boardgame: Primal: The Awakening – Niz-Maraga and Sunset Plains
description:
Endure the sandstorms of the Sunset Plains and the unpredictable wilderness of Niz-Maraga, where every leaf hides danger. This expansion includes a double-sided neoprene playmat that represents two of Thyrea’s biomes, adding both visual variety and unique gameplay elements.

In Niz-Maraga, the jungle dazzles with colors but hides dangers behind every leaf. Hunters must adapt constantly, changing their tactics and recycling cards as the living wilderness twists and turns around them.

In the Sunset Plains, when the desert wind rises, a sandstorm sweeps across the battlefield, making every action futile. Only those who master the rhythm of the storm can endure the fury of the Sunset Plains.

—description from the publisher

In this deduction game, players take on the role of detectives trying to solve mysteries. Players move around the board, gather clues, and use deduction to identify who committed the crime, where it happened, and with what weapon. Using memory and note-taking, players must piece together the puzzle before their opponents. The game features player elimination as suspects are ruled out throughout the investigation.

EKRUL: Le Roi Maudit ("EKRUL the Cursed King") is a miniatures game for one player.

Through several scenarios, linked together in a campaign, you will face creatures controlled by the game itself, aiming for the most heroic end possible and developing your characters along a captivating narrative. 
The game's simplicity, thanks to a unique and ingenious system, maintains the tactical depth of this type of game while reinventing the style with a highly original action sequence for even more strategic depth. 
Managing activation cards and power tokens offers enjoyable tactical choices. 
The goal is to combine easy access with great gameplay depth.

Let the adventure begin!

—description from the publisher (translated)
Be quick speaking words about the chosen theme. There are 24 different themes. The more themes you conquer, the better your chances of winning.

In Cuca Legal you must demonstrate your knowledge on various subjects! To win, you must be the best at everything, answering questions about Current Events, Geography, Portuguese, Sports, and other topics. By answering correctly, you can advance spaces on the board toward the finish. If you answer wrong, you must move back the number of spaces shown on the die, moving farther from victory. Over 600 questions to test your knowledge and get your brain working! Only those with a smart mind can reach the end.

A chaotic and fun party game where players are transported into a wizard battle without magic, but with the help of a Magic Hat. Now they must summon all kinds of crazy and random artifacts to attack their opponents, trying to survive until the end.

In Não Pode Rir UTC, players compete in teams to perform puns and wordplay challenges based on the popular Brazilian YouTube show 'Castro Brothers UTC'. One player draws a card with a theme and must create and deliver trocadilhos (puns/wordplay) to make the opposing team laugh. The opposing team must keep a straight face while the performing player tries various approaches within a time limit. Teams score points when they successfully make opponents laugh. The game includes challenge cards, a timer, and scoring tokens. The team with the most points after all players have performed wins the game.

TPR (table phone racing) formula is a formula type car racing board game, which has two unique particularities in the market:


     Track construction modules: while other board games have a fixed and static board, TPR formula incorporates hexagonal tiles with track segments, each game can be played on a different track, this feature gives the game enormous replayability.
     Instead of a physical die, TPR formula incorporates a mobile application that acts as a virtual dice, this application does not deliver simple random numbers, responds to a car configuration, in addition to calculating race dynamics such as: weather, fuel consumption and tire wear.


Each track path (proposed by the players), will present priorities for its optimal transit, so each player must evaluate the track and apply the most convenient configuration for their car.

There are random events such as climate changes, punctures and car failures that add a layer of luck, in addition the player must make decisions such as: risk taking curves to the limit and improving the speed of his car in critical moments.

The objective of the game is to win the race by crossing the finish line first in the number of laps agreed, in essence the player who best configures his car for the track layout and better circumvents random events will have more options to take the victory.

—description from the designer

Slaget om Køge is a local Danish board game from the "Slaget Om" series. Where the goal is to dominate Køge by owning famous places, buying pieces like scooters or caravans, and taking a loving piss on the locals.

Theme: The game focuses on nostalgia and local Køge references, and it is developed to be a fun game for the whole family.
Mechanics: It is reminiscent of classic board games, where you buy properties and try to beat your opponents through tactical choices on the board.

The game is known for containing fun and nostalgic cards that take a piss on local dialect and manners.

Venture into a realm where myth and history collide, forging epic stories, confronting fantastical threats and carving your legacy into the chronicles of Kyiv.

In the box, you can find:
1x Rulebook
5x Monster Boards
8x Miniatures (5x Monster, 3x Hero)
28x Cards (20x Fable, 3x Hero, 5x Rebel Faction)
16x Oath meeples
Punchboards.

—description from the publisher

Stone and Blade introduces new modules, a detailed Rurik Statue miniature, and the full Intrigue deck designed around it. You will also find advanced combat Strategy cards, a set of Epic Structures, upgraded Rebel interactions, and more.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Rovinia, a sun-drenched Mediterranean village where the sea sparkles, coffee lasts for hours, and politics is the only true national sport.

The locals are warm, welcoming, and relentlessly demanding.

As a mayoral candidate, you walk a razor’s edge. Every alleyway, every neighborhood, every handshake builds influence—but every promise, every maneuver, every scandal pushes your party closer to collapse. Spread your power too thin and you disappear. Push too hard and tension explodes.

Watch the city’s Thermometer carefully. When it rises, patience evaporates. At boiling point, the streets fill, tempers flare, and citizens don’t throw arguments—they throw tomatoes.
New Candidates emerge from the city’s factions. You recruit them carefully to your party—some bring influence, others bring trouble—and every choice reshapes your political future.

In Rovinia, control is subtle. Influence is fragile. And political glory is always one step away from public disgrace.

Are you ready to simmer in your own political pot?


In this game, you lead a political party in a small Mediterranean city where Influence is power and Tension is a trap.

Your goal is to accumulate the highest total Influence by the end of the game, carefully building your network through candidates, neighborhoods, and different social groups (factions). Every choice grants influence points, but it also increases your party’s tension.
As the city “heats up,” your options become limited. If you push too hard, you lose control. If you move too conservatively, you fall behind.

You win only if you manage to balance the expansion of influence, political diversity, and crisis control—while your opponents try to drive you to the boiling point.

—description from the designer

You sail your ship along Scotland’s coast, exploring the rugged cliffs. Skillfully place island tiles and collect valuable resources such as fabric, stone, and wood. With these, you build farms, villages, and even mighty castles. You can connect those buildings with trade routes that earn you valuable points. Or will you focus on gathering resources, fishing, and fulfilling your secret mission? Only those who keep an overview, build cleverly, and create the right connections can leave the competition behind.

—description from the designer

Ever folded a time machine out of a sheet of paper? Drawn a leaf blower with your eyes closed? Or guessed “district court” based only on a sound?

In Trallafitti, two teams compete to guess as many terms as possible. The twist: The same set of terms is used in all four rounds — but the way they must be presented changes each round.

With 19 different Trallafitti cards, the game offers a wide variety of presentation styles, ranging from classic verbal descriptions to creative, physical, or heavily restricted challenges. Featuring over 500 terms, Trallafitti provides strong replay value and constant variety.

As the terms become more familiar with each round, the presentation methods grow increasingly absurd. The result is a fast-paced, highly interactive party game focused on creativity, team spirit, and — above all — fun and laughter.

—description from the publisher

Schon einmal eine Zeitmaschine aus einem Blatt Papier gefaltet? Mit geschlossenen Augen einen Laubbläser gemalt? Oder „Amtsgericht“ nur anhand eines Geräuschs erraten?

In Trallafitti spielt ihr in zwei Teams und versucht, möglichst viele Begriffe zu erraten. Der Clou: In allen vier Runden werden dieselben Begriffe verwendet – doch die Art der Darstellung ändert sich von Runde zu Runde.

Neunzehn unterschiedliche Trallafitti-Karten sorgen für immer neue Erklärweisen – von klassischem Umschreiben bis hin zu kreativen, körperlichen oder besonders eingeschränkten Varianten. Mit 600 Begriffen bietet das Spiel große Abwechslung und hohen Wiederspielwert.

Mit jeder Wiederholung werden die Begriffe vertrauter, während die Darstellungsarten immer verrückter werden. So entsteht ein schnelles, interaktives Partyspiel, bei dem Kreativität, Teamgefühl und vor allem Spaß und Lachen im Mittelpunkt stehen.

—description from the publisher (German)

Slaget om Aarhus is a local Danish board game from the "Slaget Om" series. Where the goal is to dominate Aarhus by owning famous places, buying pieces like scooters or caravans, and taking a loving piss on the locals.

Theme: The game focuses on nostalgia and local Aarhus references, and it is developed to be a fun game for the whole family.
Mechanics: It is reminiscent of classic board games, where you buy properties and try to beat your opponents through tactical choices on the board.

The game is known for containing fun and nostalgic cards that take a piss on local dialect and manners.

In Máfia Submundo, players are divided into two rival mafia families battling for control of a crime-ridden city, while police officers infiltrate to save the population. Each player receives a secret role card determining their faction and special abilities. During the game, players use their cards strategically to eliminate opponents, protect allies, and discover who is friend or foe. Bluffing and deduction are essential as players must figure out the identities of others while keeping their own allegiance hidden. The game progresses through rounds of card play and elimination until one faction achieves victory by eliminating all opponents or fulfilling their faction's win condition.

Dinosaurs have taken over the pool and every turn you try to grow the best collections.

Play all cards of one dinosaur type from your hand, take any matching cards from the center into your display, and resolve the played card’s action, which can draw cards, steal dinos, or force opponents to discard. Reach six of a kind to claim a Dinoparty and make everyone else remove that type from their displays. Pool star cards mix things up by acting like dinos and triggering actions.

Celebrate three Dinoparties to win the game and earn the title Supersaurus! Quick turns and lots of player interaction make this a laugh‑filled choice for families and friends.

You are a group of gentle witches intent on helping the townspeople and the magical creatures of the Wood alike.

In this light eurogame, you will move between the 10 Locations on a beautifully illustrated map, distributing Magic to improve Roads and gathering resources to slot in your 3D Cauldron. In towns, you can search Libraries to gather arcane lore or brew potions for the citizens. In the Wood, you can stop at your cottage or at other players' residences to tend to the corresponding Garden, or you can pay a visit to the Venerable Witches, who can help you and give you tasks to accomplish.

Thanks to your Magic, Roads will become safe for humans and the resource they grant will improve during the game - and arcane animals will appear, looking for a safe Garden to live in. The game ends when the two cities are connected by safe roads, a semi-cooperative effort for every player to undertake. The witch who will have contributed the most to the wellbeing of the region and the advancement of the study of magic will be declared the winner!

-description from publisher

boardgame: Black Cat Files: Fall 001 – UFOs über Eastview
description:
A crime investigation game with a paranormal twist.
A mysterious death. An eccentric podcaster obsessed with aliens. Military, crop circles, and an entire small town on the verge of panic.
In the first case of the Black Cat Files, you take on the role of agents in a top-secret organization who must investigate undercover: What happened to Ethan Moore? What truth lies behind his final research?
This gripping mystery crime game combines physical evidence with digital investigation tools. Blend meticulous detective work with eerie phenomena, sift through red herrings, and stumble upon things that were never meant for your eyes.
But be warned: not everything you find can be rationally explained.
Can you pierce through the shadows and bring the truth to light?
Then welcome to the Black Cats.

Mead Mayhem is a fast-paced, creative card game where strategy and surprise collide. Designed for two to four players where you play as a medieval mead maker, competing to brew the finest mead. Players lay down Mead Recipes, add ingredients, and use clever action cards to outwit friends. Each round is shaped by Event cards that can help or hinder your plans. Complete recipes to earn Mead Points, and be the first to reach 10 to claim victory!

-description from designer

ARQUIMOTIM is a card game about two parallel societies seeking social well-being. Each player commands a society, its characters, and the social phenomena that affect them.

Players manage their society by playing character cards and responding to social events. The goal is to achieve the best social outcomes while navigating the complex dynamics between the two societies.

The game combines strategic card management with thematic elements of social policy, politics, and community building. Players must balance short-term actions with long-term societal goals.

It is a set of six Gamefound-exclusive miniatures which add new units depicting 4 Bishops (1 per player) and 2 Berserkers (both for Danes, played with the Danelag Expansion). The Bishops provide heavenly inspiration to your soldiers, allowing them to re-roll their battle dice.

The Berserkers provide Danes with 2 beige dice (per miniature) in combat. However, their survivability is rather poor (just 1 health point), and they must first be unlocked by an appropriate Kingdom Tile.

With two gameplay modes, Magnetic Stone Board is a blend of Jishaku and Kluster.

The magnetic stones are divided among the players, who must place them one by one inside the designated area. The first player to get rid of all their magnets wins. However, if stones snap together during a player’s turn, that player must collect them!

The designated area is chosen at the start of the game: the stones are placed either inside a cord circle or on a foam board.

Investigato is a logic and deduction game in which players are cat enthusiasts and lovers. They meet at a large feline fair, but something goes wrong — all the kitties get lost. Now, players must figure out which cat belongs to each person.

In Brasília: Um Jogo Politicamente Incorreto, players represent powerful figures who control the Brazilian political scene indirectly. To achieve their secret objectives, players must recruit politicians and guide their actions: influence elections, approve laws, manipulate media and public opinion. At the same time, they must deal with interference from other players and the ever-watchful eye of the media, which can be silenced or redirected with proper incentive. The game supports 1-6 players and combines worker placement, negotiation, bluffing, and simulation mechanics.

FangFrage is a strategic board game about competition, cooperation, and scarce resources. You manage your own fishing operations in a shared marine area – but the catch is limited, the competition is fierce, and every decision has consequences.

The game is played over three rounds. The goal is to catch as many fish as possible. In each round, the players decide how many fish they want to catch. The game ends after the seventh fishing season or when there are no more fish left.

In the first round, players are not allowed to communicate with each other. In the second round, they can make informal agreements. In the third round, these agreements are formalized by establishing a North Sea Council. Within the North Sea Council, concrete agreements, such as catch quotas, inspections, or penalties, can be negotiated and decided upon.

—description from the publisher

Corker is a competitive strategy game set on the working coast of Maine. Players take on the role of lobstermen, fishing in one the most iconic lobstering locales, Penobscot bay, filled with harbors, islands, ledges and deep water. They choose when to drop and when to haul traps across a map divided into over 60 locations. Competing for the best fishing spots while weighing the increased risk to do so.

Each round, players:

Roll for 1-6 action points
Turn over a +/- incidence card that can modify the productivity of their turn in a variety of ways or give them cards to play on other players
Place traps in different zones as they travel freely around the bay
Roll for catch based on zone strength and multipliers.
Return to one of 7 iconic harbors to sell their lobster catch which drives the market up and down.
Zones vary in productivity and risk. Weather events and interference can impact trap placement, retrieval timing, and overall catch, both globally for all players and for individual players. Market prices shift throughout the game, forcing players to decide when to sell and when to fish.

Players must balance:

More rewarding zones vs. overexposure to weather events
Timing vs. competition
High Risk/Rewards vs. Safer zones with decreased potential
The game lasts a finite number of turns that represent a single season and the lobsterman with the most cash at the end of the season is the winner.

—description from the designer

Set on the shifting shores of Kaelora, Kindred is a spatial tile-placement game for 2–4 players in which explorers document newly revealed species whose behaviors feel strangely familiar. As the island expands with the tide, players seek meaningful patterns in the wildlife—moments of recognition where observer and observed seem unexpectedly connected.

On your turn, you must place one of your uniquely shaped, double-sided Species tiles onto the shared modular board, fitting it cleanly within the grid. Over the course of the game, you may claim up to four rows using Explorer markers, each tied to a specific species. Once claimed, you score for every matching species in that row—no matter who placed it—while a glowing wild species adapts to count wherever it appears.

As space tightens, every placement matters, shaping both your strategy and your opponents’ opportunities. When no more legal placements remain, players score their four claimed rows—earning bonus points for their strongest collection and losing points for empty spaces. The explorer who forges the most meaningful connections wins.

boardgame: Aufbruch in Brighthelm: Spieleschmiede promo paupers
description:
The German Spieleschmiede crowdfunding campaign offered four additional characters / paupers with different baseline abilities or equipment, which increase the replayability.

Player tableau with two characters in blue/white:
Doris Heshner: baker from Bluevale
Dratt Kennan: reporter from Greyhaven

Player tableau with two characters in pink/grey:
Sterm Thrang: trumpeter from Lilacsville
Kathy Spelbis: wheelwright from Blacksand
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
Spend time with those you love most, playing and laughing. Whether preparing for a party or a more casual gathering, Faz ou Bebe is ideal to liven up your night.

You'll laugh a lot with all the game's challenges. The box contains 200 cards with many different activities. Perfect for all audiences who want to enjoy and make the most of their gathering.

Drinks and laughter. Want to drink a lot? Want a funny time with your friends? Faz ou Bebe is for you!

Turn boring nights into epic gatherings. Make your weekend worthwhile. Invite your friends and get ready because your night will never be the same.

Looking for a hilarious game that will make you and your friends have fun all night long? Faz ou Bebe has 200 different challenges that will make you laugh and have a different time. Whether playing at home or at pre-parties, Faz ou Bebe is the perfect game for any gathering with friends.

How to Play:

     Shuffle all the cards in the game.
     The first player draws a card and reads the challenge out loud.
     If the player completes the challenge correctly, they earn points. If not, they have to drink.
     At the end, the player with the most points wins.
Taco Escobar is a pocket card and dice game where your main objective is to collect cards to score points. Draw, trade and steal cards from your opponents to be the big winner!

Duelo de Dados is a collectible dice game where you become a powerful wizard, casting spells from your mystical medallions. Collect and trade 19 different dice that provide the magical power flowing through your veins, and 60 medallions with powerful spells to defeat your opponents. Each turn, roll your dice, absorb the magical powers from the dice, and use them to cast your spells against your opponents.

Terra à Vista is a strategy game where each player is a pirate searching for the location of a lost treasure! By moving the board pieces, the first player to form the pattern on their objective card wins!

Get ready to hoist the sails and set off on an exciting treasure hunt with Land Ho! This engaging strategy game turns each player into a fearless pirate, ready to decipher maps and be the first to find the lost treasure. Ideal for 2 to 4 players aged 10 and up, with quick 20-minute sessions, it's the perfect choice to challenge your crew's logic and agility!

Can you reach Earth with the Infinity Stones before Thanos? As a team, explore the galaxy to collect all the Infinity Stones, but beware—Thanos is also after them and can appear at any moment. Who will gather them first? The future of the universe is in your hands!

In this cooperative racing game, players work together controlling Marvel heroes, rolling dice to move across the board and collect the precious Infinity Stones. With custom miniatures and simple mechanics, the game delivers an exciting experience for the whole family as everyone races against time to save the universe from Thanos.

Eloquacious is one of the 8 games in Dr. Finn's Book of Two-Player Strategy and Word Games. The game consists of one game sheet and requires no other components -- just a pencil for each player.

Eloquacious is a cooperative game in which players are tying to complete a number of goals by filling out a crossword grid.  However, only the clue-giver can see the grid.

In each round, the clue-giver, hoping for a specific response that will help fill the crossword grid, gives a clue to the other player.  Each response given must be put into a crossword grid, even if it's not the response the clue-giver was hoping for.

In Dinheiro do mes, players receive a salary and must manage their finances throughout the month. Each day brings new opportunities for business deals, debts, bills, and unexpected commitments.

Players roll dice to move through the days of the month, facing various financial decisions and challenges. The game combines economic planning with the randomness of daily events, teaching financial literacy through gameplay. Success requires balancing income and expenses, making smart investments, and avoiding debt traps.

The Second Franco-Trarza War in what is now Mauritania reached a critical turning point during the Battle for Leybar Bridge on April 21, 1855. While the main French forces were away, Emir al-Habib launched a surprise assault on the colonial capital of Saint-Louis with over 1,000 warriors, only to be heroically repelled by a tiny garrison of 14 French marines holding a fortified tower.

The Battle for Leybar Bridge is an engaging dice manipulation and placement puzzle for 1 player. Take the role of Sgt. Henri Brunier and his garrison of 13 soldiers as they hold their outpost against the thousand-strong Trarza army. Can you repeat history and stop the Trarza army from reaching Saint-Louis?

Each round four enemies approach. Use your small dice pool and tactical abilities to defeat enemies, bolster morale, and protect the tower. When the moment is right, use your cannon die as a multiplier to even the odds. If you survive to the end of the deck, the Trarza army retreats. Manage your limited resources wisely to face overwhelming odds.

Simple rules and straightforward mechanics combine with tricky decisions to make a challenging puzzle playable that fits in your pocket and can be completed over a cup of coffee.

KRON is a dynamic game and its main mechanic is based on the LUCK factor of dice.

Gather up to six players for a match in a card battle arena and survive until the end with the most cards in hand. Use weapons and items that enhance your actions during the game and add more points to the final victory count.

KRON was developed to play with the whole family, for children aged 8 and above who are already familiar with simple mathematical calculations (addition and subtraction) and with 4 different mechanics to play and vary the game format.

The basic box contains 120 cards, including characters, weapons, and action items. 6 colored pawns, 4 stylized dice, and an instruction manual.

This booster pack for Pop Art expands the game to 9 players.

It includes:
• 6 Foreground Cards
• 6 Background Cards
• 4 Critics' Choice Cards
• 4 Artist Tool Cards
• 9 Paint Bucket Tokens

Vamphiros do Firmamentum is a cooperative board game. Players take on the roles of Vamphiros from the Court of Kharmadale, a fictional city within the Firmamentum mythology. They must traverse Castle Tormenta gathering vampiric relics to break the Mystiko seal that locked the castle and escape the building. Inside the castle, Daemonius and Diabretes are constantly being summoned, making the vampire group's quest much more difficult, as claiming the relics requires defeating the Daemonius and Diabretes as well as the traps in the rooms where the relics are found.

An electrifying word game! Explore the universe of words with Zax!, a vibrant and fast-paced game that challenges your knowledge and creativity. Players race against time to write words based on revealed themes and images, using their personal boards to write and score points. The dynamic gameplay keeps everyone engaged as card combinations change and new challenges emerge each round.

Danish for 'The Beer Game'. A simple roll and move game, where the players take turns rolling and moving. Chance cards and certain fields on the boards lets people drink beer, a small beer is a shotglaas, a large is a whole bottle. Points hare scored by drinking beer.  After an agreed upon time the game ends and the winner is the one who have scored the most points.

Answer before time runs out! In Trava Letras, you must answer and lock the first letter of the word you used! So be quick—as answers come in, available letters run out!

Game objective: Be the first to collect 3 category cards.

It's time for you to participate in a duel of elemental magic!

NEZEN is a light strategy game where your ability to adapt will be fundamental. Players move their characters across the game map, swapping cards from their hand with the board when casting spells. Always seeking to choose the best spell to use at each moment.

The first player to win 2 rounds will be declared the winner of this dynamic duel!

boardgame: Lost on the Yellow Brick Road
description:
A murder mystery inspired by The Wonderful Wizard of Oz (1900).

Difficulty Rating – 6.5/10

Originally released in July 2023. This mystery was not a part of any subscription service and was available only as a One-Time Box.

There are QR codes that will need to be scanned with a phone or tablet. Internet access is required.

You are Anna, an experienced medium and member of Valley Falls' paranormal investigative team, S.T.A.L.K. Lately, you find yourself drawn to Fratesi's Oddities and Curiosities. The locals know it was built on the location of an old Prohibition-era speakeasy that burned down in 1922. You and the other members of S.T.A.L.K. decide to conduct an on-site investigation to find out what really happened all those years ago. Can you uncover what lies underneath Fratesi's Oddities and Curiosities?

Difficulty Rating - 6/10

There are QR codes that will need to be scanned with a phone or tablet. Internet access is required.

This mini-mystery is half the size of the normal Deadbolt Mystery Society boxes. It was not part of any subscription plan and was available only as a one-time box.

Laboratory: Glory or the Gulag places 3–5 players in the absurd world of 1960s Eastern Bloc science, where disposable researchers in a backwater puppet republic are conscripted into “revolutionary” projects and struggle to remain in favor by presenting Progress Reports credible enough to avoid political consequences.

Each player leads a laboratory assigned an ambitious Project such as Pig Manure Distillation Vodka or Potato Peel Textile Substitute. Players compete to achieve the highest Status, which determines the winner at the end of the game—unless a scientist executes the most ambitious project of all: slipping away to the West and winning instantly.

Status equals the lower of a player’s Authority and Loyalty. Authority rises only if Progress Reports withstand scrutiny, but without Loyalty to the Party, credibility offers little protection.

Each round, players conduct Lab Tests on a shoestring budget using limited Resources, Catalysts, and Assistants, including equipment, funding, and test subjects supplied by political reeducation facilities. Assistants range from the occasionally competent to incompetent hires, Party appointees, secret police moles, opportunists, and alcoholics. Lab Test results determine the credibility of the Progress Report, from Laughably Fake to Vaguely Credible.
Players will of course attempt to Discredit and invalidate their rivals' reports. In Laboratory, weakening a competitor’s Authority or Loyalty can be as decisive as strengthening one’s own.

Players may also pursue Uncivil Enrichment through Embezzlement of State funds or through Black Market dealings in scarce goods—such as Real Chocolate or West German Erotic Magazines—valuable currency in the numerous opportunities for bribery. These Crimes are recorded in a personal Sedition deck, creating leverage for opponents. Through Snooping, Blackmail, and Denunciation, players attempt to expose one another’s transgressions, while Assistants may occasionally be sacrificed as scapegoats to absorb political consequences.

In Laboratory, the surest advancement rests on one expertise: concealing your crimes while exposing your rivals’.

—description from the publisher

The year is 1938, and the headline reads, "THE VANISHER STRIKES AGAIN!” Betty Collins, the wife of the latest Vanisher victim, comes to you seeking help. It’s time to bring a criminal to justice and make a name for yourself as a private investigator. So put on your fedora and trench coat and hit the pavement, Gumshoe. You've got a mystery to solve!

Difficulty Rating – 6.5/10

This mystery was not part of any subscription service. This was available only as a Printable Mystery via One-Time Boxes.

There are QR codes that will need to be scanned with a phone or tablet. Internet access is required.

More Brains! is a 1 – 4 player competitive board game (with options for cooperative and solitaire play) where each player represents a small group of "Alpha" zombies. These Alphas are much smarter than your average zombie and, being more advanced and exceedingly clever, they have shambled forward to organize and lead hordes of regular (i.e., dumb) zombies in an assault on the city of Romero Bay. Why Romero Bay? Inside the city's medical facilities, human scientists are working on the dreaded "Z-Kill" drug - a substance specifically created to permanently eradicate the zombie hordes. And the Alphas only hope of overwhelming the humans is …. get more brains!  The more brains they consume, the smarter the Alphas become. Players must push-their-luck by driving their Alphas and accompanying zombie hordes forward against the “wall of fire” emitted by the defending humans in the city before Z-Kill is perfected and deployed. The player that can accomplish this task the best and visit the largest zombie nightmare onto the city will be declared the winner of the game and the title of “Head Zed” - the supreme leader of all zombies!

—description from the publisher

Super Mombo Quest Board Game is a tabletop adaptation of the digital game Super Mombo Quest.

Each player chooses a colorful Mombo miniature and moves around the board, collecting crystals, defeating bosses, and gaining special abilities. The game emphasizes player interaction and character progression within the vibrant Mombo universe.

On a player’s turn, they move their miniature across the board and pursue core objectives such as collecting crystals, confronting bosses, and strengthening their character through new abilities.

The game centers on progressing through the board, overcoming bosses, and developing your character. Victory comes through successfully advancing in the adventure and defeating its challenges.

When you discover that you have a missed call from an old friend, Officer Ryan Finch, you almost don’t check the voicemail. When you miss another call from him a few minutes later, you know it must be urgent and decide to listen to the message he left for you. Much to your surprise, Finch is being framed for murder, and, somehow, a shark attack is involved. A road trip to Cape Anna doesn’t seem like the worst idea, and you decide to head out to the picturesque New England town to help a friend and catch a killer.

—description from the publisher

Difficulty Rating – 6/10

Invocation is an expansion for Emmerlaüs: The Duel of the Magicians that adds a new deck of 50 cards centered on the art of summoning creatures and supernatural powers to support your attacks. It increases the game’s variety by introducing new spell and die combinations, offering apprentice wizards additional ways to control the battlefield and surprise their opponents. This expansion requires the base game Emmerlaüs: The Duel of the Magicians and integrates directly into the main draw pile to enrich the diversity of games and strategic possibilities.

L’Abondance is an expansion for Emmerlaüs: The Duel of the Magicians that introduces 50 cards focused on resource generation and abundance mechanics, allowing players to amass mana, items, and buffs more efficiently. It enhances gameplay by adding strategies around wealth accumulation and overflow effects, enabling powerful combos through excess resources that can overwhelm opponents. This expansion requires the base game and mixes into the central deck for greater tactical depth and replayability.

This is a Deadbolt Mystery Society mini mystery, which is about half the size of the publisher's regular mysteries.

You and some of your friends decide to explore Ash River Cemetery the weekend before Halloween. It’s home to the final resting place of Kristofer Grimm, a man who was rumored to be rich, a serial killer, an evil toymaker, one of the fathers of modern cryptography, and a practitioner of the dark arts. The facts are likely an amalgamation of all those things, featuring bits and pieces of the truth as they were gleaned from all the various stories about him. What isn’t in dispute, however, is that, in life, he lived in the creepiest house in Valley Falls, and, in death, he resides in the most enigmatic, mystery-filled crypt in the entire graveyard. There are rumors that the mausoleum holds an immense fortune, a vast secret, or possibly even the bodies of his victims, depending on which story you believe. Treasure seekers, adventurers, puzzle enthusiasts, and true crime junkies have been going over every inch of his crypt for years, searching for clues about the truth behind what locals have named, “The Graveyard Game.” Now, you and your group of mystery-solvers are here in front of the crypt, prepared to investigate. Will you succeed where so many others have failed? Will you discover the truth about Kristofer Grimm?

Stories tell of a Mad Knight killing castle inhabitants with an axe more than 500 years ago, and now one of the guests is found in his room, murdered by an axe. Is that particular murder weapon just a simple coincidence or is there more to it than that? All evidence points to one of the other guests as the murderer, but no one claims to know the dead man. Uncover the secrets of both the guests and the castle itself as you attempt to solve a murder by spending a Night at the Castle.

When you arrive and hand your overcoat to Doyle, the doorman, he hands you a shoebox in return, stating that someone left it for you. You open it, expecting a welcome gift of some sort. Horrified, Doyle apologizes profusely for the contents of the box and swears he has no idea who left it for you. You believe him and input the coordinates into your phone to see where this gruesome keepsake leads. Le Destin is an expansion for Emmerlaüs: Duel of Mages that brings fate-altering cards, including prophecy spells and destiny-manipulating effects to influence dice rolls and future turns. It deepens strategic planning with mechanics that let players peek at or rewrite outcomes, creating tense moments of risk and reward. Requiring the base game, it integrates seamlessly into the draw pile to expand narrative and combo possibilities.

Magicians are known for making people disappear, but in most cases, a skilled master of illusion can also make them reappear with no harm done. The man known as The Magician who has suddenly burst on to the scene in Valley Falls has been making people disappear one day and then reappear the next day as pale, lifeless corpses. It's not enough to simply pull a rabbit out of a hat anymore. This magician is pulling life from unwilling victims, and it's up to you to catch this mysterious figure before they perform their next trick. Perhaps you can perform a trick of your own, investigate the case, wave your hands wildly, and say the word, "Presto!" right before unveiling The Magician, handcuffed and behind bars!

Viral on TikTok: Inspired by the popular TikTok trend, ChainLink is the ultimate battle of quick thinking and word association, where players race to connect words before their minds go blank.

Whether you're linking "Coffee" to "Date" or "Dog" to "Bite," the challenge gets trickier with each turn and the pressure is on to keep up!

Race to Complete the Chain: Players take turns guessing the missing links in the word chain. Grab your friends and your family and begin your race to the bottom, ChainLink is ideal for anyone who loves word games, fast paced games, and board games.

WHAT'S IN THE BOX: 450 Cards With 900 Unique Words, Rulebook

Why You'll Love It: Easy to play, fast paced party game, great for big groups and family game nights

boardgame: Nova Tactics: A Modular Space Wargame
description:
Nova Tactics: Fleet Operations

Nova Tactics is a tactical tabletop wargame of starship combat designed for 1-2 players. 
Utilizing a unique Comparative Roll-Off system, the game eliminates static "to-hit" numbers in favor of a dynamic technology-vs-defense engine. 
Whether you are leading a lone Corvette on a blockade run or commanding a massive Dreadnought at the head of a Warfare-scale fleet, every decision in the shipyard and on the bridge matters.

The Comparative Engine
In Nova Tactics, you compare your ship’s Tracking against the enemy’s Avoidance. The gap determines how many dice you roll. If your sensors outclass their thrusters, you gain the advantage; if they are faster than you can lock on, the pressure is on you. This same logic applies to Power vs. Defense, making every exchange of fire a tense, high-stakes calculation.

Total Fleet Customization
Inside this digital package, you'll find the Nova Forge, an interactive, browser-based fleet designer. 
Commission your ships, install modular systems like Blink-Drives, Adaptive Shields, or Hangar Bays, and assign powerful Admiral Traits to your flagship. 
The Forge calculates your points in real-time and generates printable manifest cards for your tabletop.

Solo & Cooperative Play
The included Solo Operations Protocol features a "Unified Logic Flow" for an Artificial Enemy (AE). 
Use the interactive Solo Decision Engine to automate enemy activations, allowing you to focus on your own strategy while the AI makes aggressive, context-aware tactical moves based on the state of the battlefield.

What’s Included:

     Core Operations Manual: The complete ruleset, including ship anatomy, movement protocols, and the brutal "Ramming" mechanics.
     The Nova Forge (Interactive Tool): A HTML-based fleet builder that works in any browser. Design, save, and print your custom battle groups.
     Solo Operations Protocol & Engine: Full rules for solitaire play, plus an interactive web-tool to run the AE (Artificial Enemy) during your games.
     Void Operations (Scenario Expansion): 4 unique tactical scenarios (from Event Horizon singularities to Nebula Ambushes) and 3 narrative campaign tracks.
     Sector War (Campaign System): A persistent 10-cycle campaign mode featuring ship XP, crew perks, permanent hull damage, and fleet requisition economy.

Ever since the Egyptian exhibit arrived, the museum has had a series of accidents and mishaps that have led some to think the whole thing is cursed. Yet, when you show up at the museum to meet Henderson based on the time you both agreed upon, you discover that he has disappeared. La Lumière is an award-winning restaurant known for its delicious food, beautiful place settings, and excellent service. The food is expensive. The dishes are complicated and eclectic. The clientele is elite. It's nearly impossible to get a reservation unless you know someone or have the right amount of money. It's definitely a place that is exclusive to those who enjoy the finer things in life. Which is why it's completely unexpected when a guest dies during their meal. Although the death is ruled as natural causes, there are serious doubts that is actually the case. Can you peel back La Lumière’s golden veneer and expose the rot and darkness beneath? El Colegio Encantado belongs to the line of educational Serious Games by AULA EN JUEGO.

An educational game specially designed to work on various curricular contents:

Types of words according to the number of syllables

El Colegio Encantado es un juego educativo englobado en el grupo de los Serius Games, esto es juegos para usar como herramienta educativa en el aula.

“I can’t believe it, no sooner had we received word that the force of Orcs from the North had been destroyed, than we received word that a village on our Western border had been sacked by Gnolls, and the sails of a Reptile fleet had been spotted off the East coast. I guess we had better hope that the Undead in the South don’t rise up too!”

This is a skirmish level wargaming system for playing a solo campaign of twelve battles where you have to select which forces will fight each battle, deal with the aftermath of each result, and hope your kingdom can hold out.

While there are many wargames that can be played solo, these have been specifically designed to be played that way. You take full control over your own forces, but for the Adversaries the force composition, positioning, and reactions are all determined by rolls of the dice.
  
The combat rules are designed to be miniature, scale and basing agnostic, so you can use what you already have. While the whole campaign assumes a much larger force, any actual battle is likely to involve around 30-50 figures per side. You can play with more or less of course, as forces are built around all units having a standard size (that you select based on the figures you have) and force matching being done by number of units not numbers of figures. The campaign will involve five different forces in all, if you can field that many.
 A 3 foot by 3 foot table is good and the scenarios can be played with the kind of terrain any fantasy gamer is likely to have on hand, and if not you can easily represent them with shaped objects or even two dimensional drawings to indicate where a hill, building, wood, etc. is.

You will also need ten Markers that are about playing card size for placing Adversary units and moving ones that have not been revealed yet. They should be blank (or all the same) on one side and numbered 1 to 10 on the other, so actually using playing cards from one suit without “face cards” would work. You could also do with some tokens to help you remember what is going on, like small counters numbered 1 to 10 to remind you of the unit numbers after the markers have been flipped, something to show when a unit has acted, and a few that show if a unit is disrupted.

—description from the designer

Avanti un Altro! The official game... Challenge your friends and have fun with the most famous game show on TV!!
Raise the "pidicozzi" and watch out for the special stakes. In the final round, the one who gets it wrong wins!
2,700 questions on a wide variety of topics. High replayability.
Test yourself and challenge your friends to become the new champion!
Do you have what it takes to win?

In Onsen play as the owner of your very own onsen, a Japanese-style hot spring resort or bathhouse.  Welcome quirky guests and fill your pools with their desired flavor of water! But at each strike of the shishi-odoshi (bamboo deer scare), make sure your pools are ready when your guests wet their feet, in this cozy puzzle about timing.

Onsen is a 2 to 4 player tile laying game. During the gameplay, players draft a set of a guest and a water tile, place scented water tiles to fill hot spring pools, and then situate guests around the outside, each of which scores based on sets of the scents in their row or column. Each guest and water tile only becomes active on certain turns, challenging you to time when and where you place what!

—description from the publisher

Flip Chess is a tactical card-flipping strategy game inspired by chess.

The game is played on a grid of face-down character cards. Each player controls a set of meeples and begins from different sides of the board.

On a player’s turn:

Move one meeple.

Flip one face-down card.

When a meeple enters a revealed card, that card’s chess-inspired ability must be resolved immediately. As more cards are revealed, the board state evolves, creating shifting tactical possibilities.

Victory can be achieved in three ways:

All cards are revealed and you control the majority of King cards.

You capture all of an opponent’s meeples.

An opponent can no longer make a legal move.

A player controls a King card when their meeple is on it. If no majority exists when all cards are revealed, play continues until one player gains one.

Flip Chess supports 2–4 players, with the core experience designed primarily for two.

—description from the designer
Demo case (Hot Chips, in English) for MicroMacro: Crime City that was originally playable on the publisher's website, but later offered in some retail stores in Spain as a physical product.

Compete for glory in the ultimate crab claw-down. Seize control of arenas, forge alliances, backstab combatants, and unleash your rage to win.

Every year, millions of salivating crustaceans from all over the world tune in for the wildest showdown on the planet! Five of the most legendary CRAB RAGE! teams will compete for the ultimate crown of decapod glory: THE GOLDEN CLAW.
 CRAB RAGE! is a quick, cut-throat, zany area control game. Use your pent-up rage to perform devastating actions and execute stylish signature moves. Build, break, and leverage alliances in a fierce tug-of-war for control. Keep the action fresh and the crowd hyped to become the most adored crab crew this side of the archipelago.
 Snip snap. It’s rage time!

—description from the publisher

In a world where primal forces flow like invisible rivers, will you master the currents or be consumed by the madness?

Runecall: Battle for Runes is a highly tactical, competitive strategy game for 2 or 4 players. You play as a Summoner navigating a shifting 6×6 grid, collecting elemental Runes, and weaving powerful spells to complete the ultimate Ritual of Ascension. But beware: every manipulation of the elements leaves a mark, and the line between power and insanity is razor-thin.
