Alfonso (or Alfonz - the male cricket; orange pawn), Bubble (or Gömbi - the baby beetle; yellow pawn), Berry (or Bogyó - a male snail; blue pawn) and Dolly (or Babóca - a female ladybug; red pawn) are rushing together to the train. Will they make it on time, or will the train driver (the Ant queen; green pawn)beat them to it and the train leaves without them? The essence of this simple cooperative game, easily understood even by the youngest players, is that participants don't play against each other but for one another, defeating the train driver as a team.

The game also appears in the July story of the Bogyó és Babóca month-by-month book (Bogyó és Babóca - Hónapok meséi).

—description from the publisher (translated, with clarifications in brackets)

In Ordem e Regresso, you become Brazil's next president! In this card game, you and your friends become presidential candidates competing for votes from the electorate. Do you have what it takes to win the hearts of the people?

During the debate, you present a proposal and other players use cards in their hands to make a rebuttal and modify that proposal to win votes from the electorate.

Brabbelkrabben is a chaotic family and party game combining tongue twisters and castanets. Each player gets two castagnets in differnent colors. The starting player tries to say a tongue twister containing the colors of the own castagnets and the two colors of the castagnets of another player. Every time a color  of one of your castagnets is mentioned, you need to rattle it. If you make a mistake you need to clip a small crown in your hair or on your shirt. And don't forget the amount of crowns that you or the other players have, because you need to include it in the next tongue twister. The player with the fewest crowns wins Brabbelkrabben.

Thirty3 is a high-energy, time-based trivia game where each round, a player must give clues for their team to guess as many words as possible on the Guess Card within 30 seconds. What makes it unique is a special deck of Action Cards that add twists each round, changing rules like board position, number of players, or scoring to keep the game unpredictable and exciting! The objective is to accumulate points and be the first team to complete a full circuit around the board.

—description from the designer

Do you believe in Meritocracy? How sweet.

In Mentirocracia, you will discover what really happens behind the scenes of the corporate world. Start from the bottom, dream big, and pull as many rugs as necessary to achieve the much-desired promotion. But beware: your coworkers will do the same to you! To "help" you on this journey, you will receive valuable advice from the famous Lino Silveira: open relationship manager, celebrity baker, DJ, model, and COACH!

Mentirocracia is a satirical game about the professional advancement process. A biting and humorous critique of the idealized concept of meritocracy, exposing, through playful practice, the absurdities and inconsistencies of a system that lures its prey by selling a supposed social justice. Each player takes on the role of a newly hired professional whose only goal is to reach the top... by any means necessary.

boardgame: Hora da Aventura: Jogo Caminho da Aventura
description: Adventure Time - Adventure Path Game Build your own adventure by constructing the path through the kingdom of Ooo and help Finn, Jake, Princess Bubblegum, and Ice King traverse the entire route. Face various challenges and discover who has the most luck!

Campaign: Bagration is a solitaire wargame that takes place during WW2 and puts the player in charge of the Soviet summer offensive of 1944 against Army Group Center, code named operation Bagration. Pick your three generals and use your resources wisely in order to obtain your campaign's objectives.

One month turns. Decide which card (objective) to go after, each with a unique set of Soviet defenses. Manage supplies required for each offensive, or choose to take an operational pause. Receive random event cards that are mostly beneficial but a few are Soviet counterattacks that can throw a serious monkey-wrench into your plans.

Play continues until fall begins, and you must report to Stalin with your success or failure.

—description from the designer

Safari no Eden is a card game for 2 to 8 players in which participants compete to score points by forming animal combinations such as pairs, families, same-species sets, or special groupings.

At the start of each round, players receive 9 cards. On their turn, they draw a card, perform actions (such as laying down combinations or adding a card to another player's set), and then discard one card. A round ends when a player empties their hand; all players score points based on the combinations they have laid down, with a 1-point bonus awarded to the player who ended the round. The game continues through multiple rounds until a player reaches 30 or more points during scoring, winning the game.
Adds 48 Ukiyo-e paintings by Katsushika Hokusai to Haiku.

The Long White Walk is a solo print-and-play adventure that puts you in the shoes of a survivor struggling through a frozen land. After your plane crashes in the desolate area, your only goal is to survive and find a way to get rescued.  Each step forward represents a grueling day of travel where the environment is as much of an enemy as the hunger of wild beast.

The gameplay centers on a tactical dice-allocation system. Each turn, you roll a pool of six-sided dice and must decide how to distribute them across various actions: Moving forward, Searching for food, or Fighting for survival. Higher dice results yield better outcomes, but the treacherous terrain often imposes penalties or requires specific rolls to overcome obstacles. You must constantly weigh the risk of pushing further, all while the "White Walk" track monitors your grueling progress toward the final destination.

To win the game, you must successfully reach the last chapter of the game and get a minimum score of 66 victory points.

—description from the designer

OLOMOC is a skirmish-style miniatures game set in the NOC role-playing game universe.

The game is based on the tried-and-tested rules of CAERIS: simple, fast-paced, and tactical. CAERIS players will enjoy exploring this new universe with confidence. But beware, the NOC universe is full of unpredictable events.

Beyond your opponent, the game environment itself will be formidable and unpredictable, and you will need to learn to be wary of it.

Features:

     Accessible: 3 minis per player are enough to start playing.
     Practical: Can be played on a 16"/40 cm table.
     Fluid: a movement system with innovative, precise and intuitive measuring strips.
     Fast: a game can be played in 1 hour.


How to win:
Score the most victory points

     by studying the 5 secrets on the table (2VP per secret)
     eliminate opponents, the higher the rank of the character, the higher the score.


The NOC Universe:
YOU ARE HUMANITY'S LAST HOPE
Night has fallen on the world. Earth has been engulfed by the Artifact, an impossible object that emerged from the depths of space and time. It has folded in on the world in a cosmic embrace, replacing the sky and the stars.
Condemned to regression, humanity has fragmented into islands of light. The population is entrenched behind the fortified walls of autonomous city-states: the Blocks. They obey the dictates of the Administration, an authoritarian supranational structure that works officially to preserve the human race in collaboration with the enigmatic Artifact.
Crushed by the constraints of this new order, men and women nevertheless continue their lives. They work, fight, suffer, love, believe, hope.

Some doubt.

For these dissidents, these nonconformists, it's not just about breaking their chains.

It's about dispelling the night and the lies, delving into the Artifact to unlock the secret of its origins, accomplishing the Promethean feat that will offer a new dawn to humanity.

BECOME A HERO OF THE NIGHT

Poranduba - Cartas de Cultura is a strategic card game inspired by Brazilian popular culture. The game has its own rules and includes game modes for two to four players, plus a solo mode. Recommended for ages 12 and up. In each match, you must use the cunning of sacis (Brazilian folklore tricksters), the wisdom of folk healers, the strength of werewolves, or the cleverness of a broom behind the door to win three challenges before your opponent. Just be careful: one mistake and your cards may go to Beleléu!

boardgame: Dice Throne Outcasts: Bleiche Dame v. Kopfloser Reiter
description:
Dice Throne is a game of intriguing dice, tactical card play, powerful heroes, and unique abilities.

It's a fast-paced 2-6 player combat game (1v1, 2v2, 3v3, 2v2v2, or free-for-all). Select from a variety of heroes that play and feel completely distinct from one another. Attack opponents and activate abilities by rolling your hero's unique set of five dice. Accumulate combat points and spend them on cards that have a large range of effects, such as granting permanent hero upgrades, applying status effects, and manipulating dice directly (yours, your teammate's, or even your opponent's).

A stand alone game supporting 2 players. Additional heroes required to play with more than 2 players.

The game includes the Pale Lady and the Headless Horseman.

Don't mistake the irritable Pale Lady for a delicate flower. Her cunning manipulations will corner her opponents. Then, transforming into her fearsome werewolf form, she becomes a ferocious adversary.

The Headless Horseman terrorizes his enemies with his gruesome pumpkin head! Charge into battle with thunderous hooves, swung axes, and high-speed action alongside this classic slasher!

boardgame: Dice Throne Outcasts: Rabenfürstin v. Totenbeschwörer
description:
Dice Throne is a game of intriguing dice, tactical card play, powerful heroes, and unique abilities.

It's a fast-paced 2-6 player combat game (1v1, 2v2, 3v3, 2v2v2, or free-for-all). Select from a variety of heroes that play and feel completely distinct from one another. Attack opponents and activate abilities by rolling your hero's unique set of five dice. Accumulate combat points and spend them on cards that have a large range of effects, such as granting permanent hero upgrades, applying status effects, and manipulating dice directly (yours, your teammate's, or even your opponent's).

A stand alone game supporting 2 players. Additional heroes required to play with more than 2 players.

The game includes the Raveness and the Necromancer.

Defeat knocks at the door of its foes when Raveness and her companion, the raven Nevermore, charge into battle. Their treacherous, tormenting tactics will set your heart racing in the fray!

Summon skeleton warriors, skeleton mages, and bone golems as a ruthless necromancer! Death knows no fear when it obeys your commands in heated, fast-paced battles!

A trivia game for children and youth up to 14 years old.

Master Junior is a trivia game that challenges childrens knowledge across a wide range of topics, featuring 1,395 questions designed to help them learn while having fun. The game includes three difficulty levels easy, medium, and challenge and players advance by answering correctly while losing a life token for each wrong answer. After answering six easy questions correctly, a player moves to the medium level, and after three correct answers there, advances to the challenge level. There are two ways to win: by correctly answering a challenge-level question, or if all life tokens run outby being the player furthest ahead on the board.

Jogo de Mimica is a lively charades-style party game where players act out words and phrases while others try to guess them before time runs out. Cards are divided into categories such as I Am (people, animals, characteristics), I Do (actions and expressions), Entertainment (movies, characters, music), and Objects. On a player turn, they select a card and a category, flip the timer, and begin miming while others attempt to guess the correct word. Points are awarded based on difficulty, and the game can be played individually or in teams. After a set number of rounds, the player or team with the highest score wins.

Zodiacus Intrépidos is a board game that blends the dynamics of cards with the interactivity of a game board, combined with the theme of zodiac signs and their mythology. The game unfolds in a classic Medieval Fantasy world, where your objective is to destroy the enemy castle, facing the heroes and strategies of your opponents.

In modes for 2 to 4 players, each player controls a trio of heroes that represent one of the twelve zodiac signs. Through each hero, the player controls a specific deck and board for the scenario, with its strengths and weaknesses.

The goal of Zodiacus is not just to be another board game, but also a way to introduce the world of tabletop games to a larger and younger audience, who often don't know this universe because they encounter overly complicated and hermetic systems. A great advantage of Zodiacus is its simplified and dynamic gameplay, along with an evolution system that prioritizes combat and fun.

With a strong narrative appeal, Zodiacus presents a universe filled with fantastic characters and legends. The game will bring in each box a bit more of the history of the heroes and creatures of this world.

Developed independently by the Rio de Janeiro duo of game designers Marcos Barreto and Victor Borba, the game was the winner of the contest held by Seven Game – Centro/RJ at TableTopDay 2014.

A treaty was forged among the mystical Castes.

Vampires, Werewolves, Demons, Angels, Humans, and Hybrids. Six courts clash within the short span of 1 Lunar Cycle (5 rounds), in a battle waged among the Supernatural Castes of Firmamentum. The game features a breakneck pace, constant twists, making the dynamics intense and engaging with easy mechanics. "Firmamentum: Supernatural War" brings simple and strategic movement, exciting combats, and high tension in the best "sudden death" style, where anything can happen at any moment.

You will strive to resist the creature's dominion and, who knows, even overpower it and keep it dormant for decades or even centuries. But be certain: the creature that shares your body will not gladly accept being subjugated!

Journey Around the World features a world map board with 132 cities from around the globe. Each player draws 9 cities to visit and plans a travel itinerary to reach each one as quickly as possible. During the journey, there may be some setbacks, such as needing to stay longer in a city or having to travel to a distant location, disrupting your route.

Often, taking a plane is the best choice. To win, you must return to your starting point.

Call your friends and embark on this fantastic journey!

A card game for people who enjoy having fun with good banter and know how fun it is to troll their friends. The objective of the game is to mock your friends and reduce their Morale Points to zero. Using cards to provoke, bluff, and mess around, players eliminate each other until the one who had the most Morale to withstand all the Banter is left standing. The deck is composed of cards that allow you to attack and defend, but the Banter doesn't stop there: with Challenge Cards, no one will have limits anymore, not even the most reserved players will be left out.

The party card game where the wildest wins and the most uptight gets flustered! The game to save the hangout. Pescotapa na Pantera is a card game with questions, answers, and challenges. The starting player draws a question card (red) from the deck, the other players choose an answer card (black), and the starting player decides the best and worst. The best answer receives one point and the question card as a marker. The worst answer gets a challenge card (gray) and must pay the penalty. Whoever gets three points by the end of the match wins.

Punhalada is a totally outrageous question-and-answer card game. Each play creates a joke more ridiculous than the last, and whoever is the most foolish, awkward, clever, or offensive wins, depending on the asker's mood. Punhalada is a game made for laughing and making life lighter, as well as learning things you'd rather not have learned. Punhalada is perfect with beer, but don't forget: if you drink, use sleeves.

In an adventure full of dangers and twists, Piratas do Firmamentum will take you sailing through the seas of Terrânia! Bringing you all aspects of piracy in an immersive experience that will transport you aboard the most famous pirate ship in the seas of Terrânia. Join the crew of the Notable Pirates, visit Tortuga and Port Royal, explore Treasure Island, plunder enemy vessels and fight the Corsairs. But beware, the Impetrian Armada has sworn to end the scourge of piracy and bring every outlaw to justice. The objective is to sink the rival ship within 5 rounds, which is the time the Impetrian Armada will take until its patrol arrives. If the objective is not met, there will be a sixth round where you must also break Raul de Cervantes' curse by defeating him, or leave the enemy behind for the Armada to destroy. Time is against the players, as the natural wear of both ship and crew puts them in worse condition to escape. Resources must be managed strategically and prudently, and actions against opponents must be precise.
A game for pointing fingers at others! Use your hands, throw your cards at the targets and be the only one left with just the pair of ROCK!!!! cards in your hands.

Use all your intelligence and cunning before BOOM!

A chip game that will test your nerves! With each card played, a chance to win or... to explode!

In Boom, the goal of the round is to have cards that add up to the maximum point number. If the total exceeds this value, it's boom!

At the beginning of the turn, players receive one card. One participant plays at a time, being able to choose to receive another card or continue with just that one. At the end of the play, they must bet 1 to 5 chips that they win the turn.

The player with the most chips at the end wins the game.
So, are you ready to challenge your friends in this strategic card game? Play, have fun and have a blast of joy!

The animals of the Arctic have caught an unknown virus and now scientists have been called to find the cure and heal them. Cure animals, earn artifacts found in the arctic, negotiate and go to the market to try new artifacts. The one who cures the most animals and gets the best artifacts will be the most recognized scientist and winner of the game.
Feel the emotions of a football coach in this Manager Game. Take control of your team, hire the best players and the best sponsors, and try to win the championship.

O Segredo da Pirâmide is a game for two participants who will face the dangers of the board to find the pharaoh's jewel! In this game, players alternate between adventurer and pharaoh. The adventurer must face all the traps set by the pharaoh and try to find the lost Jewel of Egypt! To move on the board, the adventurer will use cardinal directions, training and reinforcing these concepts.

Histórias ao Cubo is a storytelling game that uses 9 dice with 6 images each and 19 scenario cards. The game can be played in 5 different ways with up to 4 players.

The goal is to tell stories based on the images shown on the dice after they are rolled. The scenario card determines the location where the story takes place.

Players take turns rolling dice and creating narratives using the images, exercising creativity, improvisation, and memory. The game can be used in various settings: casual gatherings with friends, therapy sessions, corporate training, or even recruitment and selection group dynamics.

boardgame: Imperium Card: The Second Punic War
description:
Imperium Card: The Second Punic War is a historical strategy card game set during the conflict between Rome and Carthage (218–201 BCE). Players take command of opposing powers, deploying historical leaders, armies, and cities to contest control of the Mediterranean world.

Each player builds a strategic formation using a curated set of unit and command cards representing real historical forces. Tactical positioning, timing of deployments, and resource management determine the outcome of battles and territorial control. The game emphasizes strategic planning over randomness, rewarding careful sequencing and long-term military objectives.

Victory is achieved by securing decisive military superiority, capturing key territories, or fulfilling scenario-based campaign objectives inspired by historical events of the Second Punic War.

—description from the publisher

Every storyteller adds a point! To stimulate creativity, Quem Conta Um Conto is a different kind of game. Players must come together and use the cards as guides to create new stories, completely original fairy tales.

The goal of the game is to score the most points by drawing dogs from the dogpile or deck and strategically arranging them in your rescue in groups (called packs) to maximize points. You can build up to 4 packs in your rescue. Dogs at the top of each pack are called pack leader. Every dog has the potential to be a pack leader and they give you scoring bonuses if you meet their conditions..

Each dog has a particular personality and size (but you might run into some wild dogs along the way). Most dogs also have abilities that activate when you place them in your rescue, or special tricks you can perform during your turn.

Dogs who don't have a place in your rescue can be sent to the Foster Family, where they’ll also get bonus points depending on their personality.

Along the way, three Adoption Event cards will be revealed, which allows players to adopt out dogs from their rescue, foster family, or hand to score additional points. The game ends after the third Adoption Event is completed, and the player with the most points wins.

Adultos em Ação is an adult party game centered on charades, where players take turns drawing cards and performing actions without speaking while others try to guess within a time limit.

The game goes on successive performance and guessing rounds, and the cards include point values, allowing successful guesses to score points individually or by team. The player or team with the most points at the end of play wins.

boardgame: 怒れる森のクマさん (Angry Forest Bear)
description:
You've unfortunately become lost in the forest. Even more unfortunate, it seems there's an "Angry Bear" in the forest. The only way to appease his anger is to sacrifice someone. Make sure you don't become a victim and return safely from the forest.

This is a game where you use card actions to turn over forest cards without angering the bear. If you draw a "Noise" or "Crash" card when you turn a Forest card face up, the bear will get angry and you will take damage. It's also dangerous if an angry Forest Bear is in front of you! Push it onto the player next to you!

In 十王坂の審理 (Trial of Juozaka), you’ve died and must stand before the Ten Kings of the underworld. This competitive card game challenges players to accumulate virtue while navigating shifting judgments that threaten to drag them into hell. Earn the most virtue points and survive the trials to claim victory.

On your turn, choose one of four actions: draw a virtue card, accumulate matching virtue cards to score, clear a judgment by discarding cards that meet its conditions and earning powerful lucky card effects, or exchange cards with another player. As each judgment is cleared, the next King’s trial begins, raising the stakes and changing the requirements.

The game ends when pp0090.King Enma’s sixth judgment is cleared or when the virtue deck runs out. Players total their accumulated virtue, but any karma cards left in hand subtract from the score. Only the most virtuous soul will pass judgment unscathed.

Memory is everything! Use your Kung Fu knowledge to hold on to your cards!

功夫衰弱 (Kung Fu Suijaku) is a fast paced memory game for 2 to 5 players ages 6 and up, playing in about 15 to 20 minutes. Players flip cards in classic concentration style, matching identical kung fu techniques to claim them. Keep flipping as long as you make matches, but once you complete a pair and take it, your turn ends.

Winning is all about focus. At the end of the game, players score based on the color in which they collected the most cards, not the total number of cards overall. Secret Technique cards and Secret Circles add interaction, letting players interrupt opponents, steal key cards, or disrupt a turn at the perfect moment. When all cards are claimed, the player with the strongest single color set wins.
designers: game no dendo (ゲームの殿堂)

description:
Forge qui peut! is an offbeat exploration game packed with references, in which players venture into the heart of a mine fiercely guarded by a dragon. With the wedding of the dwarf king and queen fast approaching, only the most magnificent Gifts will do — and the precious gems hidden in the mine are the key to forging them.

Each turn, players reveal Mine cards to uncover what lies within: gems essential for crafting Gifts and earning victory points… or encounters in the dark Tunnels that trigger various events. The game is built around a push‑your‑luck mechanic: every card flipped generates Noise or Movement, gradually drawing the dragon’s attention. If the Noise threshold is exceeded, or if the dragon catches up to the adventurers, the player’s turn ends and the collected gems are lost. The cards are then turned face‑down again, adding a subtle memory element for future turns.

At the end of the game, the player who has earned the most victory points by forging Gifts is declared the winner.

—description from the publisher

Game setup: Each player places their 4 pieces on the starting circles of their color marked A.

Game objective: Each player's objective is to move their 4 pieces across the large brown main circle and over the blue barrier circles to the barrier symbol - E - of their color. There, they turn and occupy the 4 destination spaces of their color marked F. The first player to do this wins.

The first player rolls the die and moves their piece the number rolled as follows:
If they roll a 1, they place one of their pieces on the letter - B.
If they roll a 2, they occupy the ring - C, from where they can continue moving on the brown main circle.
If he rolls a 3, 4, 5, or 6, he moves into the blue restricted circle in the direction of the arrow.The other players then move their pieces according to the numbers rolled.

A piece that has moved into a blue restricted circle must continue moving within that circle until it is free again. This can happen in three ways:
a) If a piece lands on a brown ring -C- or D-, it must continue moving on the large, brown main circle until it reaches the next blue restricted circle, into which it must also move in the direction of the arrow. If it lands directly on the brown ring -C-, then it does not need to move into the blue restricted circle.
b) If a piece lands on a green lucky ring (with a clover), it jumps from there to the green lucky ring of the next blue restricted circle
c) Whoever lands on a red lucky ring (with a horseshoe) jumps from there to the red lucky ring of the next blue barrier circle.

Whoever can reach an opponent's piece with their roll moves it back to its starting circle A-. When jumping from one lucky ring to the next, only the opponent's piece on the second lucky ring may be removed. However, if the jump cannot be made because either the second lucky ring is occupied by one of your own pieces or because your turn has ended, then you occupy the first lucky ring and remove any opponent's piece that may be there.

Whoever rolls 6 gets a free throw. Whoever reaches a green or red lucky ring gets a free throw, regardless of whether they can make the jump to the next lucky ring or not. If a player lands on a brown ring C, they may exceptionally move into the blue exclusion zone if they roll a 3. This lands them on a green lucky ring, and they may jump.

Each player may bring their pieces standing on - A into play whenever they wish. If a move cannot be made, the dice may not be rolled again.

In this team game, players must perform the charade challenge so their team can guess the word written on the card.

You'll need to call your friends to try to guess the 240 possible words.

Show all your knowledge about celebrities spread across nine different categories. Each player will have to impersonate the celebrity drawn from the deck and answer questions asked by opponents! The fewer questions needed for someone to guess which celebrity it is, the more spaces both the player-celebrity and the one who guessed move on the board. The first to reach the end of the board becomes the great celebrity of the group! Will you win this fame?

Ybyrá is Living Nature — in movement, in balance, in transformation. In this game, you take on the role of a guardian of the land, exploring Brazil's natural riches. Connect biomes, animals, and landscapes to preserve what makes us unique. Each game is a journey of care, discovery, and enchantment.

Ybyrá is a strategy and set collection game, with bidding mechanics, hand management, and tile placement. Players combine animal and biome cards through colors and icons (pattern recognition), seeking to maximize points with a layered scoring system (variable scoring). It includes high social interaction (player interaction) and strategic decision-making (risk vs. reward).

The Al Capone Expansion introduces a brand-new hero to "Pacifist Raid". But don't let his legendary name fool you—at the start of the raid, Al Capone is a shadow of his former self.

He is the ultimate "Late-game Powerhouse" character. He begins the gmae entirely powerless, lacking any special abilities, and even starts by draining a portion of your initial resources. Choosing Al Capone means embracing a grueling early game where the team must sustain him.

However, for those who survive the struggle, the reward is absolute. As the game progresses and Al Capone reclaims his power, he evolves into an unstoppable force. His growth culminates in the pinnacle of his strength: Field Teleportation. No longer bound by distance, he can blink across the battlefield to strike with devastating precision.

A game with high replay value, new game mechanics, driven by special dice, tactics and a little bit of luck.

Who has the best strategy and tactics to gain the most prestige, attract enough visitors, and earn enough money to build their theme park with the best attractions and infrastructure ? All this in just 20 rounds against the competition or against their own high The goal of the game: Build your own theme park with infrastructure, shops, and attractions.

Achieve the highest prestige, coupled with the highest visitor numbers and potentially the highest income. The game lasts 20 rounds.

Game preparation:

     Each player receives a game board from page 1 or 2 and a pen.
     The dice are placed in a safe place for everyone. Each player starts with the 5 coins marked on the game board, which are immediately available to all players.


The game ends after 20 rounds. At the end, the player must have built an exit at the edge of the playing field, and this exit must be connected to a path leading back to the entrance.
Otherwise, he loses the game and his score is not included. Whoever has the most prestige and the most visitors to their park wins the game. In case of a tie, any remaining unused money is also taken into account.

—description from the designer

Who has the best strategy and tactics to attract the most tourists to the island ?

Objective of the game: The game lasts 20 rounds and the winner is the player who has achieved the highest value of
the rolled target.

Game Setup: Each player receives pages 1 and 2 of the player pad, 6 wooden markers (the round marker can also be used separately on an extra board for everyone), and a pen. Everyone places
their Money, Prestige, and Round markers at 0. The player who was last on vacation starts the game.
The starting player rolls the objective die to determine the game goal, which applies to everyone for the entire game. Everyone then marks their goal on the objective die.

End of game: The game ends after the 20th round and the winner is the one with the highest
target score.
Solo-Variant:
In the solo version, you play against your own high score for each game objective.

Witch Hunter is a solo card game where you step into a land choked by curses, crawling with monsters, and abandoned by hope. As a Witch Hunter, driven by grim resolve, you take on contracts no one else survives. Trading pain, preparation, and willpower to hold the darkness at bay. Every hunt is brutal and personal, where steel, alchemy, and hard choices decide whether the land is cleansed or claimed by terror.

Each Monster you face is a living threat, fueled by dread and corruption that spreads across the world as Doom. Left unchecked, the Monster’s influence grows stronger, warping the battlefield with curses, minions, and escalating horrors. The longer a hunt drags on, the more desperate it becomes—forcing you to decide when to press the attack, when to endure, and when to sacrifice safety for victory.

In Witch Hunter, you begin by choosing a Witch Hunter and an Aspect that defines your approach, then shuffling those two decks together to form your personal deck. Next, select a Monster to hunt, each with its own deck, mechanics, and escalating terror. Each round, you play cards by discarding others to pay their cost, balancing offense, defense, and threat management as the Monster attacks, terrorizes, and unleashes minions. Win by slaying the Monster and breaking its grip on the land. Fail, and the world falls one hunt closer to oblivion.

-description from designer

Who can achieve the maximum score in which landscape? Can you play the right cards at the right time of day and in the right landscape without incurring too many penalty points or having to discard cards?

Expansion options include nighttime, increased difficulty, and additional cards!

The goal of the game:

Beat your own high score in Solo Mode, as well as in the cooperative 2 player mode, or play against your opponent in the
competitive 2 player mode.

Game preparation: In solo play, four placeholder cards are removed from the game.
All landscape cards with the morning symbol are placed face down in the center of the table. Leave enough space to the right for
additional cards to be added. All other cards, including the remaining landscape cards, are shuffled together and placed face
down to form a draw pile to the left of the landscape cards. This is the starting deck, both in solo and 2-player modes. In solo
mode, each player starts with 4 placeholders; in 2-player mode, each player starts with 2. In 2-player mode, the player who last
took a walk in the woods becomes the starting player.

Gameplay:
The starting player (or solo player) draws a card and tries to place it adjacent to one of the laid-out landscape cards. Only cards with a morning symbol can be placed adjacent to the morning cards to score points. The card's value determines the score. If
the player places an incompatible card, they receive -2 points, as indicated on the card.

The game ends when the deck of cards is depleted. Then, the points for each landscape are tallied. In a competitive two-player game, the points earned must be recorded immediately during the game. In a solo game and a cooperative two-player game, the scoring takes place at the end.

—description from the designer

Go on an incredible, fun, jungle adventure where you’ll be digging for fossils in the hard soil, carrying said fossils to a mad doctor so DNA can be extracted from them, and running away in terror from an enormous monster all while your fellow competitors work against you at every conceivable moment. Did I say fun? Ha ha ha…

—description from the publisher

Players each have a role with a special power, and a hidden goal (to collect dinosaurs of a specific colour). Players move around a hex grid on a roll of two dice and pick up fossils from dig sites. They then return the fossils to base camp and roll to see which dinosaurs they have discovered. Decks of good and bad cards can affect gameplay. Meanwhile, an angry tyrannosaur is either hunting a particular player or controlled by the player in last place. If the dinosaur catches you you drop your fossils.

—user summary

boardgame: Of Light and Darkness: the Expansion
description:
This adds five new villains (Mr. Stabby, Creepling, Ruthless, Maximus, and Chemtrail), and five new heroes (Insider, Systemic, Simulator, Portal Boy, and Sombrero) to the deck.

Note: The card backings of this game have undergone a design revision. As such, the expansion deck will not match prior versions of the game.
